    客窗闲话 清 吴炽昌
    　　
    　　第一卷明武宗遗事五则冯皮匠查氏女双缢庙呆官萧希贤李寡妇鸾仙五则

    Explanation

    1

    Chat by the Guest's Window
    By Wu Chichang of the Qing dynasty

    Volume One: Five Anecdotes from the Reign of Emperor Wu of the Ming Dynasty — The Leatherworker, The Daughter of the Chá Family Who Committed Double Suicide, The Foolish Temple Official, Xiao Xixian, and Li’s Widow and the Fairy

    2

    第二卷王梦蛟假和尚公大将军延师无真叟磁州地震记神童时医神尼
    　　
    　　第三卷魏元虚淮商宴客记某驾长张慧仙寄外诗记白安人齐叫化八松墓义丐
    　　
    　　第四卷书安邑狱和阗玉鼠孙壮姑沈竹楼书讼师五则调白刘智庙吴桥案
    　　
    　　第五卷谈鬼十二则孝女义仆刘大汉俞生

    Explanation

    2

    Volume Two: Wang Mengjiao the Pretend Monk, Public General Inviting Teacher Wu Zhen Sou, Cizhou Earthquake Record, Child Prodigy's Time, Medical Practitioner Divine Nun

    Volume Three: Wei Yuan Xu, Huai Merchant's Banquet for Guests, Certain Driver Zhang Huixian Sending Poem to Someone Outside, Bai Anren, Qi Jiaohua, Eight-Pine Tomb, Righteous Beggar

    Volume Four: Letter from Anyi Prison, Hetian Jade, Mouse, Sun Zhuanggu, Shen Zhulou, Five Cases of Litigation, Adjusting Something (possibly a legal matter), Bai Liu, Temple of Wisdom, Wuqiao Case

    Volume Five: Twelve Tales about Ghosts, Filial Daughter, Righteous Servant, Liu Dahan, Yu Sheng

    Volume Six: Chen Zhi, Lean Horse, Investigation of the Market, External Scholar, Hao Lian, Lady, Zhou Ji, Shen Taishou

    Volume Seven: Prescient Monk Hua, Madman, Fraud, Twelve Cases, Certain Scholar, Three Officials, Savior, Thunder God

    Volume Eight: Tang Wenzheng, Examination Hall, Five Cases, Fortune-Teller Liang翁, Young Prince, Yao, Chancellery, Zhou, Noble, County Official, Relatives, Three Top Scholars

    Continuation

    Volume One: Wu, Noble, Difficult Daughter, Strange Speech, Seven Cases, Certain Official, Huang the Great King, Ritual Specialist, Li, Teacher, Tang, Scholar

    Volume Two: Legacy of Emperor Gaozong of the Southern Song Dynasty, Wang, Land Deity, Xu Zhanran, Wang Litang, Power of the King of Hell, Supplement to a Litigation Expert, Two Cases, Mistaken Intelligent Woman in the Prisons of Guangdong

    Volume Three: Legacy of Lu Qingxian, Supplement to the Legacy, Five Cases of Fraud, Corrupt Monk, Righteous Thief, Yan, Enlightenment, Knowledge, Pan, Good Person, Country of India

    Volume Four: Certain Military Governor's Wife, Qin Liangyu, Legacy, Certain Physician, Method of Treating Smallpox, Black Snake, Already Leprous, Jinbiao, Guest, Determination of Wealth and Life, Fixed Number, Skill for Longevity

    3

    第五卷六壬神课文孝廉妖人邢大郭去非何首乌补明武宗遗事三则通州吏目金山寺医僧张廉访
    　　
    　　第六卷某少君许宗伯巧令三则正梦圆谎先生鬼孝子荆茅
    　　
    　　第七卷义猫李老陈伯蒋三官飞车丁养虚阮封翁赵甲崇伯鲧象棋子斗鹑禄命
    　　
    　　第八卷疑难杂案五则转女为男二则程翁马氏土神显应拐带爵大元戎某富翁百岁老人陈君耳神
    　　
    　　吴生，余所取士也。遇余时正在壮年，其才华敏妙，学究天人，谓破壁飞去是意中事也。余于鸾台凤阁望之久矣。及甲申来都，已越二纪，犹是一领青衫，而从事于莲华幕里，因贫改途，深为惋惜。乃出其所著《客窗闲话》问序于余。三复之，益觉才思隽发，议论渊深，生平郁颇之气，流露于斯，可怪也，亦可悲也夫。嗟乎！吴生欲移风易俗而不得其用，托是书以劝善，以惩恶，以示人趋避，既有功于世道人心，当不胫而走天下，是亦名教之一助与！余故乐为之序。时甲申相月书于燕邸长白山人

    Explanation

    3

    Volume Five: The Text of the Sixth Nén Shén

    Mr. Wu, whom I had chosen as a scholar, met me when he was in the prime of his life. His talent was sharp and exquisite, his learning profound and transcendent, and he considered breaking through walls and flying away as a natural thing. I had long admired him from the Phoenix Tower and Crane Pavilion, symbolizing high-ranking officials. By the year of Jia Shen, he came to the capital, and more than two decades had passed. He still wore a simple official robe and served in the lotus tent, a metaphor for a low-ranking or temporary position. Due to poverty, he changed his path, which deeply saddened me. He then presented his work "Guest Window Idle Talk" for me to write a preface. I read it three times and found his talent and thoughts even more brilliant. His arguments were profound and deep, and the pent-up energy of his life was expressed in this work. It is both strange and sad, isn't it? Alas! Mr. Wu wanted to change customs and habits but could not put his ideas into practice. He entrusted this book to encourage good, punish evil, and show people how to avoid danger. It has already done good for the world and people's hearts. It will spread quickly throughout the world. This is also a help to the norms and teachings. Therefore, I am happy to write this preface. In the month of Xiang in the year of Jia Shen, written at Yan Mansion by a person from Changbai Mountain.

    4

    吴子赋闲之日，好集谈客，设卮酒盘蔬，听谈古今逸事。遇有可惊可喜，足以自省而思齐者，一一举笔录之，久之裒然成集。或见而笑曰：“何吾子之心思才力，妄费于无用之地也？若移其道以肄业于制艺，则诗文必工，可以名当世，可以昭来许，而犹可以拾青紫。舍是不为而乃卑卑者，欲附于稗官野史之流耶？”吴子肃容敬谢曰：“客之言诚是也。虽然，客不见夫古今来聪明智慧之人，加以研炼揣摹之学，发为诗文，昌明博大，自信足以传世。又有明师益友为之参订，哲晜贤嗣为之检校，始克付诸剞劂，出而问世，其用心亦良苦矣。然不久即为妇女夹箴黹，为庸夫覆酱瓿者，比比皆是。

    Explanation

    4

    During Wu Zi's days of leisure, he enjoyed gathering guests for conversation, setting out a simple meal of wine and vegetables, and listening to tales of ancient and modern times. Whenever he encountered stories that were surprising or delightful and could inspire self-reflection and emulation, he would record them one by one, and over time, he compiled them into a collection. Someone who saw this might laugh and say, "Why do you waste your thoughts and talents on such an unproductive endeavor? If you applied your efforts to the imperial examination style of writing, your poetry and prose would surely be excellent, and you could become famous in your own time, known by future generations, and even achieve high official positions. Why, instead, do you engage in such lowly activities, akin to compiling unofficial historical records?" Wu Zi, with a serious and respectful demeanor, thanked the critic and replied, "Your words are indeed true. However, have you not seen that even the most intelligent and wise people in history, after applying themselves to the study and refinement of writing, produced works that were clear, broad, and confident in their ability to endure. They had learned from wise teachers and friends, and their works were carefully reviewed by their enlightened descendants, only then being published and made public. Their efforts were also quite arduous. Yet, soon after, these works are often used by women to embroider moral sayings or by ordinary people to wrap up jars of sauce, and such occurrences are very common."

    5

    彼作稗官野史者，拉拉杂杂，不过逞一时之兴，而足以动诸人之耳者，何也？其命意新而措诃浅，智愚之所共见者也。况仆悉就其所谈之事而纪之，是以数十百人之心思才力供仆挥洒，岂无可观之处乎？奚必独抒己见，或颂扬而过当，或讥诮而招尤，为人作夹黹覆瓿之具耶？”或曰：“然则如吾子言，是塞文章之路，而辟稗官野史之邪径矣。”吴子笑曰：“客何泥也，仆之所为，贤于博弈而已，何敢与高文典册同日语哉？”或曰：“然则吾子之所为，若胥钞然，何又有翻新出奇之作耶？”吴子曰：“仆所述客话，客之性情不一，有温厚和平者，有诙谐谲诡者，有忿世嫉俗者。悉就其所谈而笔之，无庸心于其间，而其词自不同矣。”客卷口结舌而退，吴子又笔之，以弁于集首。时道光甲午夏芗厈居士题于保定寓斋

    Explanation

    5

    Those who write unofficial or semi-fictional historical accounts, their writing is disorganized and rambling, merely indulging in a fleeting mood, yet it is enough to move the ears of others, why is that? Because their ideas are novel but their language is shallow, understood by both the wise and the foolish. Moreover, I record everything they have talked about, using the collective thoughts and talents of dozens or even hundreds of people to inspire my writing. Is there nothing worth seeing? Why must one express their own views independently, either by praising excessively or criticizing in a way that invites blame, and be used as a tool for patching clothes or covering jars? Someone said, "Then, according to your words, this would block the path of literary creation and open a wrong path for unofficial historical writing." Wu laughed and said, "Why are you so literal-minded, my writing is only better than trivial pursuits like games, how dare I compare it to great literary works and official documents?" Someone else said, "Then, what you do, seems to be mere transcription, how can there be anything new and surprising?" Wu said, "The stories I record, the guests have different personalities, some are gentle and calm, some are witty and mysterious, some are cynical and critical of the world. I simply record what they have talked about, without imposing my own views, and the language naturally varies." The guest left speechless and retreated. Wu then wrote it down, placing it at the beginning of the collection. It was written by the Scholar Xiang'an in the summer of the year Jiawu during the Daoguang era, in his study in Baoding.

    6

    诸子总评
    　　
    　　乌子耀云云：笔法遒劲。其突兀纵横，离奇豪放处目不暇给，真令人百读不厌矣。《聊斋》复生未肯多让，佩服，佩服。
    　　
    　　范子今雨云：唤醒世人不少，洵卓卓可传之书，非寻常评话可比也。
    　　
    　　方子幼樗云：纪事详明而出笔儁雅，纯是书生本色。笔墨不落做闲书人腔调，是以读之口颊回津，不能释手。又云：新颖而不怪，近理而不浮，殆今之善于立言者。班赋云：小说九百，本于虞初。若此者，视彼九百种头，未知有所轩轾否。耀云先生许与《聊斋》并传，固非阿私所好矣。熟复数过，胜餐余甘子五百枝也。
    　　
    　　高子芸薮云：芗厈深于《国策》，其舌涌澜翻，硬语蟠空处，居然神似。
    　　
    　　徐子子成云：《聊斋》《阅微》而后得此，可为鼎峙。
    　　
    　　题词
    　　
    　　何曾体例仿虞初，耳乍闻时手即书。似此心花生笔底，添毫颊上更何如？

    Explanation

    6

    General Evaluation

    Wu Ziyao commented: The writing style is vigorous. Its abrupt and sprawling, extraordinary and unrestrained passages are so abundant that they cannot be fully appreciated at once, truly making one read it over and over without getting tired. Liaozhai Zhiyi (Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio) would not be far behind, admirable, admirable.

    Fan Zijin commented: It awakens many people, truly a book worthy of being passed down, not comparable to ordinary commentaries.

    Fang Ziyouchu commented: The events are recorded in detail and written with elegance, truly the style of a scholar. The writing does not fall into the style of a casual writer, so reading it makes one's mouth water, and one cannot put it down. He also said: It is novel but not strange, close to reason but not superficial, perhaps the best at formulating words today. Ban Gu (班固) said: There were nine hundred novels originating from Yu Chou (虞初). If this is compared to those nine hundred, it is unknown whether they are superior or inferior. Mr. Yao Yun (耀云) has compared it to Liaozhai Zhiyi for transmission, which is not a matter of favoritism. Reading it repeatedly is better than eating five hundred branches of the sweet fruit.

    Gao Ziyunshu commented: Xiang Yan (芗厈) is deeply versed in the Guoyu (国策), his words flowing like waves, and his bold language resonating in the void, truly resembling the divine.

    Xu Zizheng commented: After Liaozhai Zhiyi and Yuewei Caotang Notes, this work can stand as a triad.

    Epigraph

    Has the style imitated Yu Chou? When first heard, one immediately writes it down. Such heart-grown flowers bloom at the tip of the pen, what more could be added to the cheeks?

    7

    维扬鹾舍凉如许，陬邑邮亭暑尽驱。一卷奇文诵冰雪，不须重展北风图。（时方酷暑，每诵编中二则，胸次顿觉清凉，移我情者，不让成连海上琴声也。）
    　　
    　　儿女英雄事尽传，偶然写到鬼狐仙。奇闻轶状谈何易，尘海搜罗已卅年。（此书创自丁卯，积今三十年矣。）幼樗方廷瑚
    　　
    　　贪客过谈谈即录，咫闻轶事尽书之。纵饶客有莲花舌，未及生花笔一枝。
    　　
    　　笔意《聊斋》《觚賸》间，翻空征实妙多般，编成雅自居闲话，到底曾无一字闲。荦生封左垣
    　　
    　　博古穷经卅载余，笔尖神妙有谁如，文心处处皆生趣，闲话拈来即是书。
    　　
    　　雅俗诙谐尽可编，胸中异学得天全，搜罗今古奇谈事，堪补《聊斋》未述篇。子述苏缵
    　　
    　　雨打蕉寒，风敲竹碎，客窗孤闷谁知。手先生此卷，顿觉愁眉。底事围楸战茗，还胜吹竹弹丝。挑灯细读，倾杯未了，拍案惊奇。

    Explanation

    7

    The Salt Merchants' House in Yangzhou is so cool, and the summer heat is completely driven away in the remote post station of a distant town. A volume of strange and wonderful writing, recited with the clarity and purity of ice and snow, does not need to be unfolded again to show the coldness of the north wind. (At the height of summer, whenever I read two sections from this compilation, my heart suddenly feels refreshed, and the emotional shift it brings is no less than the sound of the sea at Chenglian's lute.)

    Stories of ordinary people and heroic figures are all fully told, and occasionally, tales of ghosts, fox spirits, and immortals are written about. How easy it is to talk about strange and unusual events and phenomena, and for thirty years, I have been searching and collecting them from the world. (This book was created in the year Dingmao and has accumulated over thirty years.) Young Scholar Fang Tinghu

    Greedy guests discuss and immediately record what they hear, and all the strange stories within a short distance are written down. Even if a guest has a persuasive tongue, it cannot match the power of a well-crafted pen.

    The style of writing is between "Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio" and "Miscellaneous Records of the Brush," with many wonderful ways of being imaginative and verifying reality. The compiled work is regarded as refined and leisurely, and not a single word is idle. Scholar Feng Zuoyuan

    For thirty years, I have studied ancient and modern classics, and who can match the divine skill of my pen? The literary heart finds life in every detail, and idle chatter becomes a book.

    All kinds of humorous and witty stories can be compiled, and the unique learning in my heart is complete. Collecting strange and unusual tales from ancient and modern times, it can fill the gaps in the stories told in "Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio." Scholar Su Zuan

    Rain hits the banana leaves, making them feel cold, and the wind knocks against the bamboo, making it sound like it's breaking. Who knows the loneliness and depression of a traveler's room? By hand first, I created this volume, and suddenly felt relieved from worry. What matters more is playing Go and tea tasting, which is even better than playing a bamboo flute or a stringed instrument. Lighting a lamp and reading carefully, drinking from a cup without finishing, I am amazed and surprised.

    8

    搜神说鬼，梦雨翻云，笔花五色纷摛。惟曩日虞初踵接，蒲叟肩随。莫道虚楼总幻，须防肿背堪嗤。今今古古，无无有有，姑妄听之。春谷陈寅贤右词《雨中花慢》
    　　
    　　其人如玉，其笔则仙。经经纬史，然乎不然。搜神述异，元之又元。心勾角斗，肺镂肝镌。匪曰鼠璞，匪曰狐禅。读古微书，庶几近焉。小黻卢恩照
    　　
    　　续客窗闲话序
    　　
    　　盖以帷天席地，人生等稊米之观；弄月吟风，客路作浮萍之寄。造化既成为传舍，寰区何限于吾庐。往事可传，藉消晨夕；闲情偶得，聊足光阴。即眼前之清谈，驱腹中之宿墨。手同挥尘，爪证飞鸿。此芗厈吴公所以有《续客窗闲话》之著焉。吴公浙水名流，燕山游幕。虽事申韩之学，实耽周孔之书。广有见闻，善于言论，每当知己相对，奇语可惊。得来风月之谈，书作枣梨之刻。兹又续成数卷，共为二编。

    Explanation

    8

    To search for spirits and speak of ghosts, to dream of rain and swirling clouds, to let the brush produce multicolored flowers in abundance — this is the essence of such writing. Only in the footsteps of ancient Yu Chou, and in the company of Pu Shou, has this tradition been carried forward. Do not say that a hollow tower is always illusion, for a hunchback may be laughed at. Now and then, neither and both — take it as you will. Spring Valley Chen Yinxian composed the following Rainy Flower Slow:

    The person is like jade, and the pen is like a god. Through the classics and histories, is it so, or not? Searching for spirits and recounting the strange, ever more strange still. The heart locked in a duel, the lungs carved into the liver. Not a mouse's jade, not a fox's meditation. By reading the subtle ancient texts, one may come close to understanding. Xiao Fu Lu Enzhao

    Preface to The Continuation of Guest Window Idle Talk

    For the sky is our canopy, the earth our mat — life is but a single grain of millet in the field. To play with the moon and chant the wind, the traveler’s path is like a drifting water chestnut. The forces of nature have already made this a temporary lodging — how can the world be confined to my small dwelling? Past events may be passed down to pass the hours of morning and evening; idle feelings and occasional insights are enough to fill the time. Even the clear conversation of the present moment may drive out the old ink from the belly. With hands that wave dust, and claws that prove the flying goose — this is why Wu Gong of Xiang Yan composed The Continuation of Guest Window Idle Talk.

    Wu Gong was a distinguished figure from Zhejiang, a traveler and official in Yan Mountain. Though he studied the legalist doctrines of Shang Yang and Han Fei, he was truly devoted to the teachings of Confucius and Mencius. Broad in knowledge and experience, skilled in speech — whenever he conversed with like-minded friends, his strange and brilliant words were astonishing. He gathered the conversations of wind and moon, and wrote them as the carved fruit of the jujube and pear trees. Now he has continued this work into several volumes, making a total of two editions.

    9

    礼制仿乎虞初，讹不必删夫亥豕；文章间亦志异，传不必合为鬼狐。漫作滑稽，等于游戏。是固《说铃》之嗣响，仰亦《觚賸》之新音也。念自万物不外乎逆旅，古今可喻以浮沤。当来牛去马之场，溯此泣彼歌之会。荒烟蔓草，昔年则竞赏楼台；胜地盛筵，他日则谁亲鱼鸟。人世之阅历，正如马足之轮；天上之星辰，祗共蚁行之磨。苟使弗留翰墨，一任云烟，听散佚于无稽，薄荒唐而不述，吾恐古无说部，异书既失于传闻；世乏稗官，汗简且难于考鉴已。即持管见，以弁简端。是为序。性甫谢理

    Explanation

    9

    Ritual systems emulate the early days of the Shang dynasty, and minor errors do not necessarily need to be deleted from the text. Literary works also record strange things, and transmission does not necessarily have to be unified into ghostly or fox-like tales. Writing humorously and satirically is akin to playing a game. This is indeed the continuation of the sound of 'Shuo Ling' and also the new sound of 'Gu Shen'. Thinking that all things are no more than temporary travelers, the past and present can be likened to floating bubbles. In the place where cattle come and horses go, tracing back to the gatherings where some weep and others sing. Overgrown with weeds and smoke, in the past, they competed to admire the towers and pavilions; famous places and grand banquets, in the future, who will befriend fish and birds. The experiences of the world are just like the wheel of a horse's hoof; the stars in the sky, only share the grind of ant-like movement. If not recorded in writing, let it drift away like clouds and smoke, let it be lost in the realm of the unverified, treat it as absurd and not record it. I fear that ancient times lacked narrative literature, strange books have been lost in the transmission; the world lacks minor officials, even the bamboo slips are difficult to verify. Even with my limited perspective, to adorn the beginning. This is the preface. Xingfu Xie Li.

    10

    生年阅历，恍悟秘诠。人有癖好，必争投焉。故孟尝好客，珠履三千；叶公好龙，虬螭见前。太白好饮，斗酒百篇；仆好闲话，与客言言。奇闻轶事，告述连绵。自少而壮，前三十年。所有闻见，已付雕镌。自壮而老，又三十年。投仆所好，搜罗蕃宣。续成八卷，就正群贤。或曰，“读子之集，似诏薛君以土偶桃梗，绘叶公之神龙蜿蜒。词成白雪，舌吐青莲。彰善阐恶，正正平平。续之无尽，越著新鲜。”对曰：仆古稀已届，两耳塞绵。客纵雕龙灾毂，尽态极妍，其如无所闻问，难与周旋。赋聋而止，力尽情坚。词多疵累，志欲求全。谁其订正，高君寄泉。一经笔削，较胜于先。聊陈大意，韵语是编。时维道光庚戌中秋既望芗厈自序于泉州官舍之西斋
    　　
    　　题词
    　　
    　　说苑纷纷各逞长，谁攀屈艳与班香。《蓴乡》《觚賸》诸家后，合让吴生远擅场。

    Explanation

    10

    Through years of experience, I suddenly realized the secret meaning. People have their preferences, and they must compete to cater to them. Therefore, Mengchang enjoyed hosting guests, with thousands of pearl-embroidered shoes. Yegong loved dragons, and dragons appeared before him. Taibai loved drinking, and he could compose a hundred poems after a dou of wine. I enjoy idle talk, chatting with guests. Strange and interesting stories, told continuously. From youth to adulthood, the first thirty years. All that I have seen and heard, has been engraved. From adulthood to old age, another thirty years. Catering to my preference, collecting and spreading widely. Continued to complete eight volumes, seeking correction from the wise. Someone said, 'Reading your collection, it's like instructing Xue Jun with clay figures and peach wood, painting the divine dragon of Yegong.' Words become pure white snow, the tongue speaks of green lotus. Highlighting good and exposing evil, straightforward and balanced. Continuing endlessly, becoming fresher and fresher. I replied: I am now in my seventies, with ears blocked by cotton. Even if guests carve dragons and fill carriages with beauty, how can they engage with me when I hear nothing? Assigning myself to deafness, stopping out of exhaustion and determination. Words have many flaws, the intention is to seek perfection. Who will correct it, Mr. Gao Jiquan. After one cut and polish, it is better than before. Briefly stating the main idea, this is the compilation in verse. The time is the 16th day of the eighth month in the Geng Xu year of Daoguang, self-compiled by Xiang Yan in the west study of his official residence in Quanzhou.

    Title:
    The Garden of Discourse abounds with various talents, who dare to compete with the brilliance of Qu Yuan and Ban Gu. After the works of Chunxiang and Gusu, it is fitting to acknowledge that Wu Sheng excels in this field.

    11

    客窗风月自年年，掇拾丛残手劈笺。怪得莲花开幕府，本来舌底粲青莲。
    　　
    　　说法何妨偶现身，仙才艳福证兰因。千金能却豪家聘，不信红闺大有人。
    　　
    　　邂逅畿南小滞留，琼瑶先喜一编投。何时快把惊才笔，与尔同登海上楼。虹江陆元烺
    　　
    　　唤世婆心锦绣肠，纵横椽笔发潜光。遥知莲幕三更月，定有幽灵拜石床。
    　　
    　　劝惩彰瘅涌毫端，枵腹思移一字难。胜读《毛诗》三百首，莫将闲话等闲看。
    　　
    　　客中滋味本无聊，魍魉情形尽态描。立意岂夸狐鬼传，自将遗事溯前朝。
    　　
    　　阎罗王亦待人权，身毒国名尤骇然。漫道先生工说谎，先生此谎不须圆。
    　　
    　　封翁有后善通神，智勇偏教在妇人。唤醒几多名利客，盗知守义鬼荣亲。
    　　
    　　忆从笔墨订交情，两载相思未识荆。何日客窗一闲话，不须说鬼说生平。经圃达纶

    Explanation

    11

    Year after year, the wind and moon of the guest's window pass by; with hands I gather fragments and cut paper with care. No wonder the lotus blooms before the government hall — from the beginning, my tongue shines bright with lotus flowers.

    What harm is there in occasionally appearing in human form to teach? With immortal talent and beautiful fortune, I fulfill the cause of virtue. A thousand gold could not dissuade me from the proposal of a noble family — I do not believe the red chamber lacks many virtuous women.

    By chance we met in the southern outskirts of the capital, with only a brief stay. First, I joyfully received a precious volume of writing. When will we soon wield our startling talents with brush and ink, and ascend together to the sea tower? — Hongjiang Lu Yuanlang

    With a compassionate heart that calls the world to awakening, a heart as fine as brocade, I wield the brush with bold strokes, revealing hidden light. I know that at the lotus hall, at the third watch of the night, there will surely be spirits paying homage at the stone bed.

    Admonition, punishment, commendation, and censure all flow from the tip of the brush. With an empty stomach, even moving a single character is difficult. Better than reading the Book of Poetry three hundred times — do not regard idle talk as trivial.

    The taste of being a guest is inherently aimless, yet I describe fully the forms of ghosts and spirits. My intent is not to boast about fox and ghost tales, but to trace the remnants of past dynasties.

    Even Yama, the King of Hell, waits for human judgment, and the name of the country of Shendu is especially terrifying. Although it is said the teacher is skilled at lying, this particular lie does not need to be made consistent.

    The deceased husband has a son skilled in communicating with the divine, and wisdom and courage are especially found in women. Awaken many who pursue fame and fortune — the thief knows that upholding righteousness brings honor to one's family.

    I remember how we first became friends through writing, and for two years I have longed for you without meeting you in person. When will we sit by the guest window and have a leisurely conversation, not needing to speak of ghosts or my life story? — Jingpu Daliang

    12

    吾兄作客三十年，奉身橐笔向北燕。遂谢制举专读律，相与决事无间然。有时挥尘无一事，说有谈空各尽意。却于纷纭错杂中，别出心裁森断制。古来记载千余家，或奇或正多纷拏。正则史乘志略详明而核实，奇则鬼怪神仙诡谲而浮夸。无论机锦七襄才八斗，畀益人心始可久。伸纸过服如烟云，作书玩志等瓿缶。人言道德变刑名，编排法律坚长城。操纵能得法外意，依然道德游至清。兄今此作非记实，借鉴记讽文而质。以犯为避真妙手，通观即是申韩术。与兄睽隔廿载余，登堂问讯风雪徐。地阔天长喜会面，此后著述当何如。
    　　
    　　同怀弟靖符卍生
    　　
    　　芗厈先生续刻客窗闲话台幸与校勘恭题二绝句王金台笠卿
    　　
    　　频挥斑管蔼如春，铁案求生一念真。活泼天机随处畅，阶前兰桂仰灵椿。
    　　
    　　幸讎亥豕正金根，藉对春风旦夕温。我有一编惭刻楮，（旧著《墨尘》甲乙丙丁数编，尚未付梓。）何年负笥候班门。

    Explanation

    12

    My brother has been away for thirty years, living a life dedicated to writing and traveling to the northern regions. He eventually abandoned the imperial examinations and focused on studying law, making decisions without hesitation. Sometimes he would engage in idle conversation, discussing both real and abstract topics, each expressing their own ideas. Yet, in the midst of complex and chaotic situations, he would come up with unique and original judgments. Since ancient times, there have been thousands of families of records, some extraordinary and some orthodox, with many differing views. The orthodox ones are detailed and verified in history books and records, while the extraordinary ones are fantastical and exaggerated, involving ghosts, gods, and mythical beings. Regardless of one's exceptional talent, only by benefiting the heart can something last long. To write so that the text flows like clouds and smoke, and to treat writing books and playing with one's will as casually as clay jars and pots. People say that moral principles change into legal names, arranging laws firmly like a great wall. To manipulate and understand the meaning beyond the law, still, moral principles flow to the utmost purity. My brother's current work is not a record of reality but borrows from records and satirical texts to make the content substantial. Using a seemingly offensive method as a true masterful technique, seeing it as a whole is to see it as the methods of Shang Yang and Han Fei. We have been separated for more than twenty years, entering the hall and inquiring about the weather, moving slowly through the wind and snow. The land is vast and the sky is long, and it is joyful to meet again. What will your writings be like after this?

    From the same heart, the younger brother Jingfu, with the pen name "卍生"

    Master Xiang'an continued to publish Guest Window Idle Talk. I am fortunate to have participated in the proofreading and respectfully inscribe two couplets. Wang Jintai, with the pen name "Liqing"

    Frequently waving the brush, as warm as spring. A firm case seeks life with a true thought. The lively divine mechanism flows everywhere. Before the steps, the orchid and cassia trees look up to the sacred cypress.

    Fortunately, correcting the errors in the text is like correcting the gold root. Relying on the spring breeze, warming up day and night. I have a compilation that I am ashamed to carve on paper. In what year will I carry a box to wait at the gate of the master?

    13

    卷一
    　　
    　　明武宗遗事五则
    　　
    　　明武宗皇帝，亦一代英主也。惟好为嬉戏，有亏帝德。即其颠倒予夺数事，虽正史所不录，闻诸故老，堪资谈柄，条列于后。
    　　
    　　戊辰科，庶常散馆，例在内廷扃试，时诸词林呈艺，有闽人林吉士者，好书古字，如以秋为秌之类。帝怪之，问所自出，林历举以对。帝书咊字与认，林不识。帝曰：“秋可作秌，和独不可作咊乎?汝读书少，未称词林之职，着回籍勤读三年，再来就试。”林谢恩出。同乡戚友咸知其为帝所逐，皆不之礼。林本寒士，欲归则无腰缠，欲留则无斧资，欲就教读，则为都人轻薄，无延之者。无奈，在正阳门外卖字为活，已逾年矣，幸其书法之佳，耸动一时。值帝微行过其肆，见观者环堵，啧啧称羡。帝不知是何异事，挨入人丛，睹一少年生据案挥毫，得意疾书，法并钟王，句亦典雅，帝心羡之。

    Explanation

    13

    Volume One

    Five Anecdotes about Emperor Wu of Ming

    Emperor Wu of Ming was also a great ruler of his time. However, he loved to play and have fun, which was a shame for an emperor. Even though the official history does not record several instances of his arbitrary giving and taking away, these stories are worth mentioning because they are topics of conversation among the elderly, and they are listed below.

    In the year of the Wu Chen (戊辰), junior officials were assigned to various posts after passing the imperial examinations, and by custom, they were tested inside the palace. At the time, various literary officials presented their works, and there was a man named Lin Jishi from Fujian province who liked to write ancient characters, such as writing 秋 as 秌. The emperor found this strange and asked where it came from. Lin listed them all in response. The emperor wrote the character 咊 and asked if he recognized it. Lin did not recognize it. The emperor said, "If 秋 can be written as 秌, why can't 和 be written as 咊? You have read too little and are not suitable for the position of a literary official. Go back to your hometown and study diligently for three years, and come back to take the exam." Lin thanked the emperor and left. His relatives and friends all knew that he had been dismissed by the emperor and no longer treated him with respect. Lin was a poor scholar. If he wanted to return, he had no money. If he wanted to stay, he had no funds. If he wanted to teach, he was looked down upon by the people of the capital, and nobody invited him. He had no choice but to sell his calligraphy at the Zhengyang Gate to make a living. For more than a year, luckily, his calligraphy was excellent and attracted attention at the time. When the emperor secretly visited his shop, he saw a crowd of people praising him. The emperor didn't know what this strange thing was and pushed his way into the crowd. He saw a young man sitting at a desk writing with great satisfaction. The young man's style was comparable to that of Zhong Yao and Wang Xizhi, and the sentences were also elegant. The emperor admired him.

    14

    缘当时一面，久则遗忘。遂入问曰：“吾观汝写作俱佳，当今文明之际，何不应试，甘为贱业耶?”林举首见帝魁岸不凡，揖之就坐，对曰：“我闽人林某，忝居词林。”帝曰：“词林清华，不合行同市僧，有玷官箴。”林曰：“去岁散馆时因不识御笔，奉旨回籍读书。”帝曰：“既奉谕旨，何不回去?”林曰：“我实起于单寒，旅资无措，不得已为此营生也。”帝曰：“读书人本色，何不就馆?”林曰：“人皆知为逐臣，无敢延者。”帝曰：“吾荐汝邻省作一幕宾，不逾此耶?”林感悦揖谢，问帝爵里，以便往拜。帝曰：“毋庸，明日汝仍于此候信。”飘然而去。诸客皆为林贺，曰：“此必王孙公子，必有好音。”欢喜而散。

    Explanation

    14

    Due to a brief encounter, it is soon forgotten. He then entered and asked, "I see that your writing is excellent. In this era of civilization, why not take the exams and become a respected official instead of engaging in a lowly profession?" Lin raised his head and saw the emperor, who was tall and distinguished. He bowed and sat down, replying, "I am Lin from Fujian, humbly occupying a position in the literary world." The emperor said, "A position in the literary world is prestigious, but it is inappropriate to behave like a common monk, tarnishing the reputation of officials." Lin said, "Last year, when I was dismissed from the academy because I did not recognize the emperor's handwriting, I was ordered to return home to study." The emperor asked, "Having received the imperial command, why did you not return home?" Lin said, "I truly come from humble beginnings, lacking the means for travel, and was forced to engage in this occupation." The emperor said, "As a scholar, why not seek a position in a noble's household?" Lin said, "Everyone knows I am an exiled official, and no one dares to hire me." The emperor said, "I will recommend you to a neighboring province to serve as a guest official, which is not far from your current position." Lin was grateful and bowed in thanks, asking about the emperor's rank and residence so he could pay his respects. The emperor said, "There is no need. Tomorrow, you should wait here for further instructions." He then disappeared gracefully. The guests all congratulated Lin, saying, "This must be a noble or wealthy person, and there must be good news." They left happily.

    15

    次日，有内监赍黄封一函，白金百两，与林曰：“昨有官人嘱我致阁下，其照封面行事，不可窃开，自取其祸。此银以佐车马之需。”言毕竟走，亦不道姓名来历。林观封面大书“亲投山东巡按开拆。”欣然闭肆，雇车马携旧仆驰抵山左。时巡按左虚，系方伯权护。林安寓更衣，偕仆往拜。惟时风宪崇隆，官吏严肃。刺不易投，三日犹未得见方伯。林躁曰：“我有都中密信须亲投者，奈何阻我?”吏始为通达，司阍者唤林入见，其行装黯淡，疑为抽丰之客，洋洋摇首曰：“吾主摄通省总纲，公事繁冗，何暇接见同年故旧耶?若有书札，我为代投，回寓听信可也。”林无可如何，出黄封交之而归，闷甚。未几，寓主狂奔而入，望林叩首无算，曰：“小人不知宪驾，亵渎舆从，罪该万死。

    Explanation

    15

    The next day, an eunuch delivered a yellow-sealed envelope and a hundred taels of silver to Lin, saying, "Yesterday, an official asked me to deliver this to you. Follow the instructions on the cover; do not open it secretly, or you will bring disaster upon yourself. This silver is to assist with your travel expenses." The eunuch then left, without revealing his name or background. Lin looked at the cover, which was inscribed with "Deliver this personally to the Censor of Shandong Province." He closed his shop with delight, hired a carriage, and set off with his old servant to Shandong. At that time, the Censor of Shandong was Left Xu, who was temporarily overseeing the province in place of the governor. Lin arranged for lodging, changed into clean clothes, and went to pay his respects. However, the office of the censor was highly esteemed, and the officials were very strict. It was difficult to present his letter of introduction, and after three days, he still had not been able to meet the governor. Lin became impatient and said, "I have a confidential letter from the capital that must be delivered personally. Why are you preventing me?" The official finally allowed him to be admitted, and the gatekeeper called Lin in to see the governor. Seeing Lin's modest attire, the governor suspected him of being a person seeking favors, and shook his head dismissively, saying, "My master is overseeing the entire province, and his duties are extremely busy. How could he have time to see old friends and acquaintances? If you have a letter, I will deliver it on your behalf. You can return to your lodging and wait for a reply." Lin had no choice but to hand over the yellow-sealed envelope and return home, feeling very depressed. Shortly after, the messenger rushed into Lin's lodging, bowed to him repeatedly, and said, "I did not know Your Excellency was here. I have insulted your carriage and attendants. I deserve to be punished to the extreme."

    16

    ”林愤然曰：“主人何揶揄我穷途耶?”寓主曰：“小人焉敢?”正分辩间，闻呼驺鸣炮之声，中军旗牌巡捕等官带同吏役长跪请安毕，佥曰：“奉方伯命，请钦使入署。”林如登云雾，不知所谓，被群拥入八座。仪仗山列，车骑云从，直入巡按厅事。方伯公服趋迎，林下舆，见庭设香案，所投之黄封高供其上。方伯跪请圣安毕，向林揖曰：“请天使宣读圣旨。”林开封读曰：“山东巡按缺出，即着林某署理。”方知所遇者帝也。望阙九顿首，受印任事。方伯三揖称庆，并道有失远迎之愆，退居藩邸。林于是表谢圣恩，请陛见训示。未几，批回云，“朕看尔人才去得，故有是命。尔其谨慎从公，毋忝厥职，三年后来京复命可也。”林居山东任满，回京见帝。帝曰：“如今读书有资矣，以编修用，仍入翰苑，读书三年，以观后效。”

    Explanation

    16

    林愤然说道：“主人为何嘲笑我在困境中？”寓主回答说：“我怎敢如此？”正当他们争辩时，听到传令官鸣炮的声音，中军旗牌巡捕等官员带着吏役们长跪请安完毕，众人齐声说：“奉方伯之命，请钦使入署。”林某如同置身云雾之中，不知所措，被众人簇拥着进入八座。仪仗队排列如山，车马随从如云，直接进入巡按厅事。方伯穿着公服前来迎接，林某下车，只见庭院中设有香案，呈上的黄封文书高高地供奉其上。方伯跪下请求圣安完毕，向林某作揖说：“请天使宣读圣旨。”林某打开文书宣读道：“山东巡按职位空缺，即刻任命林某暂代其职。”林某这才明白所遇到的是皇帝。他面向宫殿方向行了九次大礼，接受印信，正式上任。方伯三次作揖祝贺，并表示有失远迎之罪，退居藩邸。林某于是上表感谢圣恩，请求觐见皇帝接受训示。不久，皇帝批复道：“朕看你的才能适合此职，故有此任命。你必须谨慎行事，尽职尽责，三年后回京复命。”林某在山东任职期满，返回京城见皇帝。皇帝说：“现在你读书已有资助，任命你为编修，仍入翰苑，读书三年，以观后效。”

    17

    帝入枢密院，见在公之卿相方朝饔，见帝皆起，帝曰：“卿等食讫来闲话可也。”遂入密室坐。于是卿相饭毕咸集，帝悉命坐，闻曰：“卿等所食，知米之来历不易乎?朕在储宫时，意谓五谷若草芥，听其自生自长而已。今巡游乎田野，察农夫之艰难，方知古人云“谁知盘中餐，粒粒皆辛苦”，诚不谬也。卿等所居，南北殊方，风俗各异，其若何播种，若何耕耨，若何收获，各举所知，以广朕闻。”时公卿中多起自田间者，悉数见闻以对。

    Explanation

    17

    The emperor entered the Privy Council and found the high-ranking officials having their morning meal. Upon seeing the emperor, they all stood up. The emperor said, "You all can come and chat after you finish eating." He then entered a private room and sat down. After finishing their meals, the officials gathered together. The emperor invited them all to sit and said, "Do you know that the rice you eat comes from a difficult process? When I was a crown prince, I thought that grains were as common as grass and weeds, and I let them grow and wither on their own. Now, after touring the fields and observing the hardships faced by farmers, I realize that the ancient saying 'Who knows the hardship of the food on the plate, grain by grain' is indeed true. You all come from different regions, and the customs and practices vary. How do you sow, how do you till, and how do you harvest? Please share your knowledge and observations to broaden my understanding."

    Many of the officials had backgrounds in agriculture and were able to provide detailed accounts of their experiences and knowledge.

    18

    惟尚书某系世胄，不知所云。帝微哂曰：“饮食尚不知本原，何以理国?尔职居何部，日司何事，得无旷废耶?”尚书惶恐叩首曰：“臣职吏部，甫自部署来，见单开江南太湖司巡检缺出，应归部铨选。”等语。帝大笑曰：“吏部尚书仅知此耶?”拂袖而入。同列皆尤之，尚书曰：“我今日只记得此事，无可奏对者。”未几，旨下云：“太湖司巡检缺出，即着某补授。”尚书接旨，茫无主见，谓同列曰：“我已降官，敢不赴任。但应陛辞否?
    ”同列皆笑曰：“微官末职，曷胜陛辞?只须午门外叩首谢恩，领凭赴任。但旨无降革字样，应以一品服莅事可也。”尚书仓惶就道，至则抚按率百官郊迎。尚书再三辞，具脚色手本，趋谒抚按，各官逊谢不遑，请居省会。尚书曰：“帝命赴任，我何敢违?”择日前往，府县趋改衙署，规制轩昂，亦用辕门鼓吹，与抚按等。尚书至，见署之三面皆农田，始悟帝意，欲其知稼穑之艰难耳。

    Explanation

    18

    Only the Minister of Rites, who is of noble birth, did not know what was being said. The emperor smiled slightly and said, "If one does not even know the origin of food and drink, how can one govern the country? What department do you serve in? What do you manage daily? Have you not neglected your duties?" The minister, filled with fear, knelt and said, "I serve in the Ministry of Personnel, and just came from the department. I saw that the position of patrol inspector in the Taihu region of Jiangnan was vacant, and it should be selected by the department." The emperor laughed loudly and said, "Does the Minister of Personnel only know this?" He waved his sleeve and entered. All his colleagues criticized him, and the minister said, "I only remember this today; there is nothing else to report." Soon after, the imperial decree came down saying, "The position of patrol inspector in the Taihu region is vacant; immediately appoint someone to fill it." The minister received the decree, completely without a plan, and said to his colleagues, "I have been demoted; how dare I not take office. But should I bid farewell at court?" His colleagues all laughed and said, "A minor official of low rank, how could I bear to bid farewell at court? Only need to kneel at the gate of the imperial palace to thank the emperor, receive the credentials, and take office. But the imperial decree does not contain words of demotion or removal; one should attend to the duties in the attire of the first rank." The minister hastily set out on the journey. When he arrived, the provincial governor and censor led all officials to meet him at the outskirts. The minister repeatedly declined, presented his credentials, rushed to pay respects to the provincial governor and censor, and all officials hurriedly offered their respects, requesting him to stay in the provincial capital. The minister said, "The emperor has commanded me to take office; how dare I disobey?" He chose a date to depart, and the prefecture and county hurriedly changed the government office. The building was grand and imposing, and also used the drum and music at the gate, equal to that of the provincial governor and censor. When the minister arrived, he saw that three sides of the office were farmland, and finally realized the emperor's intention, which was to make him understand the hardships of farming.

    19

    于是摒绝舆从，日以青衣小帽游闾阎，与二三父老讲论民间疾苦，应改革调济者，悉往为论。居数年，为人排难解纷，以息讼端，民皆感悦。值宸濠叛，帝驾南征，尚书见抚按曰：“我曾待罪吏部，驾至不可不迎。然无巡检接驾仪注，请公议之。”抚按司道佥曰：“论品则居我等上，论职则在我等下。请以一品服伏谒百寮之末，何如?”尚书首肯，遂迎驾。帝乘逍遥马，见之，呼尚书来前，曰：“尔今知吃饭难耶?”尚书顿首谢训。帝命随至行宫，询其所事，尚书将平日所著因革论缕析以对，帝大悦，命居亚相。

    Explanation

    19

    At that time, he completely dispensed with his attendants, daily dressed in plain clothes and a small hat, and roamed among the common people, discussing the hardships of the common folk with a few elderly men. For those who needed reform and adjustment, he would go and discuss them. After several years, he resolved difficulties and disputes for others, stopping the source of litigation, and the people were all moved and pleased. When Chen Hao rebelled, the emperor's army marched south. The Shangshu (Minister) saw the governor-general and said, "I once served in the Ministry of Personnel. When the emperor arrives, we cannot fail to welcome him. But there are no established procedures for welcoming the emperor. Please discuss this together." The governor-general and provincial officials all agreed, saying, "In terms of rank, he is above us, but in terms of function, he is below us. Please have him wear first-rank attire and bow to the hundred officials at the end, what do you think?" The Shangshu agreed first, and they then welcomed the emperor. The emperor rode a leisurely horse, saw the Shangshu, and called him forward, saying, "Do you now know how difficult it is to eat?" The Shangshu knelt and thanked for the lesson. The emperor ordered him to follow to the imperial residence, inquiring about what he was doing. The Shangshu presented his daily writings on reform and change in detail. The emperor was greatly pleased and ordered him to take the position of second chancellor.

    20

    除夕，帝忽出宫，历六部九卿公署，官吏皆回私宅度岁，虚无人也。至吏部，闻高唱南腔，似有多人聚饮者。帝突入，惟一小吏，陈盘肉壶酒自歌自饮。见帝至，殷勤让坐，酌酒劝进。帝问曰：“汝何官，何独居此?”吏曰：“吾浙右人，此部挂名吏也。封篆后官与同事俱有宅可归，是时团集妻孥饮合家欢矣。吾想如许案卷，设有不虞，贻误不小。是以守此，不敢离也。”帝曰：“汝好，明日再见。”遂起去。吏犹执烛远导。

    Explanation

    20

    On New Year's Eve, the emperor suddenly left the palace and passed through the offices of the six ministries and the nine grand ministers. All the officials had returned to their private homes to celebrate the New Year, leaving the offices empty. When he arrived at the Ministry of Personnel, he heard the loud singing of the Southern dialect, as if many people were gathered for a drinking party. The emperor suddenly entered, and there was only one low-ranking official, who had laid out a tray of meat, a pot of wine, and was singing and drinking by himself. Seeing the emperor arrive, the official warmly offered a seat and poured wine to encourage him to drink. The emperor asked, "What is your position, and why are you staying here alone?" The official replied, "I am from the right side of Zhejiang, a nominal official in this department. After the sealing of the seal, the officials and colleagues all have houses to return to, and at that time, they gathered with their wives and children to drink and enjoy family happiness. I think about these many cases, if something unexpected happens, the mistake would be great. Therefore, I stay here and dare not leave." The emperor said, "You are good, see you tomorrow." He then got up and left. The official still held a candle to guide him far away.

    21

    元旦，朝见千官，帝问吏部尚书曰：“昨在卿署看守者何人?即召入见朕。”尚书遣郎官立唤。小吏当陛，举首见帝，不禁战栗。帝曰：“无恐，汝能小心职守，勤慎可嘉。汝等役满，应得何议叙?”吏日；“五年无过，以从九品归选耳。”帝曰：“汝欲何为?”吏曰：“小人所愿者，太湖司巡检。闻是缺前经本部尚书署理，鼓吹鸣炮，体面非常，但小人尚未满役，不合例耳。”帝笑曰：“朕命汝去，何用例为?”吏欢欣鼓舞，叠呼万岁。帝特命尚书予凭赴任。

    Explanation

    21

    On New Year's Day, the emperor attended court and met with a thousand officials. He asked the Minister of Personnel, "Who was guarding your office yesterday? I want to summon him to see me." The minister sent a mid-level official to bring the guard before the emperor. Upon seeing the emperor, the guard could not help but feel nervous. The emperor said, "Do not be afraid. You have been diligent and conscientious in your duties, and your diligence and caution are commendable. When you complete your term of service, what kind of reward or promotion would you receive?" The guard replied, "After five years without any mistakes, I would be returned to the selection pool for further appointments." The emperor asked, "What do you desire?" The guard said, "I wish to be the Taihu Customs Inspector. I have heard that the previous holder of this position was temporarily assigned by the head of the Ministry of Personnel, and the ceremony was grand with music and cannon fire, indicating its importance. However, I have not yet completed my term of service, so I am not eligible for the position according to the rules." The emperor smiled and said, "I order you to take the position. Why should I follow the rules?" The guard was filled with joy and excitement, repeatedly calling out, "Long live the emperor!" The emperor specifically ordered the Minister of Personnel to issue the necessary documents for the guard to take up his new position.

    22

    宸濠之叛，御驾亲征。舟泊清河，帝登岸微行，入茶肆，闻人言下江因兵差截夺客船，横行索诈，稍不如愿即毁客货。嗟怨不绝。帝买小舟，变服似商人，以一卫士一小监，泝流直下，至杨关，有官舫甚巨，巡船数十，虎役百辈，拦截客舟，帝亦在内。逐舟搜索，云供兵差之需。客皆哀祈献缗。至帝舟，帝僵卧舱中不之理。虎役拥帝登官舫，见一官凭几危坐，从役森列，叱帝曰：“汝何人，敢抗皇差，不畏官刑耶!”帝微哂之，官怒叱役摔帝行杖，幸卫士勇猛挥拳挡护，众不能近。官益怒曰：“是必江洋盗魁。”命拿舟子至，褫衣鞭挞，喝令指帝为盗，帝怒，探怀出玉牌，命内监立召大员至。值苏扬巡按接奉帝命，仓惶奔赴。官见巡按来，至船头跪迎，正欲诉盘获大盗缘由，巡按见帝立舱中，俯伏请罪。帝无言，惟指挥卫士夺此官纱帽予舟子戴之，曰：“以酬汝痛。”方命巡按提此官去，追赃问罪，论大辟。失察之上官皆获严谴，而客舟自此安行矣。

    Explanation

    22

    When Zhu Chenhao rebelled, the Emperor personally led the campaign. His boat anchored at Qinghe, and the Emperor disembarked and traveled incognito, entering a tea house, where he overheard people speaking of how in the lower Jiang region, due to military conscription, officials were seizing passenger boats, acting recklessly and extorting money, and destroying passengers’ goods if their demands were not met. Complaints and lamentations were unceasing. The Emperor then bought a small boat, changed his clothes to resemble a merchant, and traveled downstream with one bodyguard and a small eunuch. He reached Yangguan, where a large official boat was moored, accompanied by dozens of patrol boats and hundreds of intimidating enforcers, who were intercepting passenger vessels — the Emperor himself was among them. They searched each boat, claiming it was for the needs of the military conscription. All the passengers pleaded pitifully and offered money. When they reached the Emperor’s boat, he lay stiffly in the cabin and ignored them. The enforcers seized the Emperor and took him aboard the official boat, where they saw an official seated solemnly at a table, surrounded by his attendants, who shouted at the Emperor, saying, “Who are you? How dare you resist the imperial order? Do you not fear official punishment?” The Emperor merely smiled slightly at him. The official, enraged, ordered his men to strike the Emperor with the rod, but fortunately, the bodyguard bravely stepped forward and blocked the blows, preventing the men from getting close. The official grew even angrier and said, “This man must be the leader of a band of outlaws.” He ordered the boatman to be brought before him, stripped him of his clothes, and beat him, then commanded him to point out the Emperor as a bandit. The Emperor, furious, reached into his robe and produced a jade tablet, ordering the eunuch to immediately summon a high-ranking official. Just then, the provincial inspector of Suzhou and Yangzhou received the Emperor’s command and hurried to the scene. When the official saw the inspector arriving, he rushed to the bow of the boat and knelt to welcome him, just as he was about to explain the reason for the arrest of a notorious bandit. The inspector, however, saw the Emperor standing in the cabin and immediately fell to his knees, pleading for punishment. The Emperor said nothing, but only ordered the guards to take the official’s silk cap and give it to the boatman to wear, saying, “To reward you for your pain.” He then ordered the inspector to take the official away, investigate and recover the stolen goods, and sentence him to death. All the superiors who had failed to detect the corruption were severely punished, and from then on, the passenger boats traveled safely.

    23

    帝在宣化，有女子李凤姐者，年十四五，有殊姿。其父设酒肆，以凤姐当垆。是时父适在外，帝微行过之，见其丰神绰约，国色无双，不禁迷眩。入肆沽饮，凤姐送酒来，帝误以为倡伎之流，突起拥抱入室。凤姐惊喊，帝急掩其口曰：“朕为天子，苟从我，富贵立至。”先是凤姐恒梦身变明珠，为苍龙攫取，骇化烟云而散。闻言顿悟，任帝阖户解襦狎之，落红殷褥，实处子也。帝大悦。时李父闻喊趋救，门已闭矣，惟闻宝钗声颤，佩玉锵鸣，其女气促音嘶，若不胜强暴者。急奔告卡兵，蜂拥而入，见帝拔关出。兵士伏谒，叱令将凤姐归豹房，爵其父三品卿，赐黄金千两，欲封凤姐为嫔妃，命其自择。凤姐固辞曰：“臣妾福薄命微，不应贵显，恐于身不利。今以贱躯事至尊，曷胜荣幸之至。

    Explanation

    23

    The Emperor was in Xuanhua when there was a girl named Li Fengjie, aged fourteen or fifteen, who was exceptionally beautiful. Her father ran an inn, and Li Fengjie served as the barmaid. At that time, her father happened to be out, and the Emperor, incognito, passed by. He saw her with her graceful and charming demeanor, unparalleled in beauty, and was immediately captivated. He entered the inn to buy drinks, and Li Fengjie brought him the wine. The Emperor mistakenly thought she was a courtesan and suddenly embraced her, taking her into the room. Li Fengjie cried out in shock, and the Emperor quickly covered her mouth, saying, "I am the Son of Heaven. If you follow me, wealth and honor will come instantly." Li Fengjie had often dreamed of herself turning into a pearl, seized by a mighty dragon, and frightened into dissolving into smoke and clouds. Upon hearing the Emperor's words, she suddenly understood and allowed him to close the door and undress her, as she lost her virginity, blood staining the bedding. The Emperor was greatly pleased. At that moment, Li Fengjie's father heard her cries and rushed to save her, but the door was already closed. All he could hear were the trembling sounds of her hairpins and the clinking of her jade ornaments, with his daughter gasping for breath and her voice hoarse, as if she could not bear the force. He rushed to inform the guards, who swarmed in, and saw the Emperor hastily leaving. The guards prostrated themselves in reverence, and the Emperor ordered them to take Li Fengjie to the Leopard House, granting her father the rank of a Third-Class Official, bestowing him with a thousand taels of gold, and intending to make Li Fengjie a concubine, asking her to choose herself. Li Fengjie firmly declined, saying, "I have little fortune and a lowly status, and should not be honored with high rank. I fear it would be harmful to me. Now, serving the most exalted ruler with my humble body, how can I not feel deeply honored?"

    24

    伏愿陛下早回宫阙，以万几为念，则臣妾心安，较爵赏犹荣矣。”帝颔之，因睹凤姐玄衣玄裳，益显娇媚，故不强易宫装。凤姐恒于枕畔筵前委婉屡劝，帝乃择日还京，与凤姐并辔齐驱。至居庸关，风雷交作，凤姐睹关口所凿四大天王，怒目生动，眩晕坠马。帝亲扶之，疾忙外退，驻跸行宫。视凤姐伏枕泣曰：“臣妾自知福薄，不能侍宫禁，请帝速回。”帝曰：“若是，朕忍弃天下，不忍弃爱卿，决不归矣。”凤姐一恸而绝，帝哀怜甚，命葬关山之上，宠以殊礼，用黄土封茔。一夜尽变为白，其阴灵犹不敢受也。帝追念其言，奋然曰：“小女子尚知以社稷为重，安忍背之?”遂还宫。正史载帝在豹房，百官交章劝谏皆不纳，畴知一微弱女子力能回天，书所云高明柔克耶?此功不可泯也。至今过关沟者，遥指白壤，艳谈其事。

    Explanation

    24

    I humbly wish, Your Majesty, that you return to the imperial palace early, keeping the myriad affairs of state in mind. Then, the concubine's heart will be at peace, and it will be even more honorable than receiving a title or reward. The emperor nodded in agreement. He then noticed that Consort Feng was dressed in dark, somber attire, which made her appear even more charming. Thus, he did not force her to change into her palace robes. Consort Feng constantly persuaded him gently at the bedside and during banquets. Eventually, the emperor chose a day to return to the capital and rode side by side with Consort Feng. When they reached the Juyong Pass, a fierce storm erupted, and Consort Feng, seeing the statues of the Four Heavenly Kings with their angry eyes coming to life, became dizzy and fell off her horse. The emperor quickly rushed out to help her and hurriedly withdrew to the imperial pavilion. He saw Consort Feng lying on the pillow, weeping, and said, "I know my fortune is shallow and cannot serve in the inner palace. Please, Your Majesty, return quickly." The emperor replied, "If that is the case, I can bear to abandon the empire, but I cannot bear to abandon my beloved minister. I will not return." Consort Feng, overcome with grief, passed away. The emperor was deeply grieved and ordered her to be buried on the mountain pass, honoring her with special rites and sealing her tomb with yellow earth. That night, the soil turned white, and her spirit was still reluctant to accept it. The emperor remembered her words and exclaimed, "Even a weak woman knows to prioritize the state; how could I abandon it?" He then returned to the palace. Official historical records state that the emperor remained in the hunting lodge, ignoring the repeated memorials of the officials who advised him. Who would have known that a weak woman could turn the heavens? As the book says, "high and clear, gentle and overcoming." This achievement cannot be forgotten. To this day, those passing through the pass point to the white soil and talk about the story.

    25

    冯皮匠
    　　
    　　冯某，武陵细民也。业皮匠，日以担头谋生，家仅一妻，破屋三椽，亦系赁住者，余无长物。值岁将阑，负债五六串，逋欠十余家，剩有四百青蚨，不足以偿。与妻谋曰：“我与尔此钱，市斗米度岁，我欲潜入紫阳山洞，俟元旦归来，再作计较。债主来，则以我外出借钱为对。”妻诺之。
    　　
    　　遂携壶茶怀炕饼襆被而往，瞥见山洞内有捷足先登之客，貂帽狐袭，危坐石几，如木偶然。冯失笑曰：“我岂误入冢中乎?否则何遇此富贵之尸?若谓与我同心者，当属窭人，子不应灿烂如是。”客闻人语，陡然惊觉，瞠目视冯曰：“予观足下，似非雅士，届此残腊，何不拥床头人，举柏酒椒盘之乐，而乃抉具游山，欲追慕清高乎?”冯曰：“姑无论我，阁下何为者?”客曰：“予为姚继崇洋货行主人也。

    Explanation

    25

    冯皮匠

    　　冯某，武陵细民也。业皮匠，日以担头谋生，家仅一妻，破屋三椽，亦系赁住者，余无长物。值岁将阑，负债五六串，逋欠十余家，剩有四百青蚨，不足以偿。与妻谋曰：“我与尔此钱，市斗米度岁，我欲潜入紫阳山洞，俟元旦归来，再作计较。债主来，则以我外出借钱为对。”妻诺之。

    　　遂携壶茶怀炕饼襆被而往，瞥见山洞内有捷足先登之客，貂帽狐袭，危坐石几，如木偶然。冯失笑曰：“我岂误入冢中乎?否则何遇此富贵之尸?若谓与我同心者，当属窭人，子不应灿烂如是。”客闻人语，陡然惊觉，瞠目视冯曰：“予观足下，似非雅士，届此残腊，何不拥床头人，举柏酒椒盘之乐，而乃抉具游山，欲追慕清高乎?”冯曰：“姑无论我，阁下何为者?”客曰：“予为姚继崇洋货行主人也。

    The Cobbler of Wuling

    　　Mr. Feng, a poor commoner from Wuling, made a living as a cobbler, carrying goods on a pole each day. His family consisted of only his wife, and they lived in a dilapidated house with three rafters, which they rented. They had no valuable possessions. As the year was nearly over, he owed debts of five or six strings of coins to more than ten families, and all he had left was four hundred coins, which were insufficient to pay them off. He said to his wife, "I will give you this money to buy a measure of rice to survive the year. I want to secretly enter the Purple Yang Mountain cave and wait until New Year's Day to return, and then we can figure out what to do. If creditors come, tell them I went out to borrow money." His wife agreed.

    　　He then took with him a pot of tea, some dried cakes, and bedding, and set off. Upon glancing into the cave, he saw a guest who had already arrived, dressed in a fur hat and a luxurious coat, sitting solemnly on a stone table, as if carved from wood. Mr. Feng laughed and said, "Have I mistakenly entered a tomb? Otherwise, how could I encounter such a wealthy corpse? If you claim to be someone who shares my heart, you should be a poor person like me, not dressed so splendidly." The guest, upon hearing his words, suddenly became alert, staring at Mr. Feng and saying, "I see you are not a refined gentleman. At this time of the year, why not enjoy the warmth of your wife, raise a cup of pine wine and pepper dishes, and instead choose to travel to the mountains, seeking to emulate nobility?" Mr. Feng replied, "Let’s not talk about me. Who are you?" The guest answered, "I am the owner of the foreign goods store, Mr. Yao Jizhong."

    26

    放贷值百万于郡县各肆，际此俭年，各肆未能归结，而客商在行坐取贷价，需五十万金。予张罗数日，无人应贷，客商势必告讦，则行业将败。而予无归着矣，不得已，权以此地当避债台，第苦饥渴耳。足下来幸甚!”冯慨然奉饼饵，注茗以进，并道同病相怜意。姚饱啖之若珍羞，然继而曰：“足下所亏几何，而亦甘趋苦境?”冯曰：“吾侪小人，所负者五六串青蚨耳。”姚曰：“予虽同在窘乡，此尚易办。”脱腕上金约臂与之曰：“足下姑以此质十串钱归债，所余烦沽酒炊饭以给予。”

    Explanation

    26

    A million was lent to various shops in counties and towns. In this year of scarcity, the shops were unable to repay the loan. The merchants, sitting in the market, demanded the loan price, requiring fifty thousand in gold. I sought help for several days, but no one was willing to lend the money. The merchants were certain to accuse me, which would ruin the industry. With no other options, I had no choice but to use this place as a refuge from my debts, though it brought me great distress. "Your arrival is most fortunate!" Feng generously offered bread and tea, and spoke of mutual sympathy for our shared plight. Yao ate heartily, as if savoring rare delicacies, and then asked, "How much have you lost, and why are you willing to endure such hardship?" Feng replied, "We common folk owe only five or six strings of coins." Yao said, "Although we are both in dire straits, this is still easy to resolve." He removed a gold bracelet from his wrist and gave it to Feng, saying, "Please use this as collateral to borrow ten strings of coins to repay your debt. With the remainder, please buy wine and cook a meal for me."

    27

    冯欣然如姚嘱，归告其妻，教以烹炰。妻曰：“久不举火矣，灶灰堆积，曷先出之。”冯往邻家借锄篑，鲁莽从事，灰去而灶崩。妻讶曰：“真倒灶矣，为今之计，莫若担去砖灰，掘地作灶，以供急需。”夫妇合作，未及土尽而石见，石去而两巨缸，朱提灿然耀目。夫妇大悦，冯曰：“今如之何？”妻曰：“姑以篑运入我床下，再作计较。”于是递相搬运，银尽起缸，缸下又石，石下复得两缸，如是者三，共得六缸。冯曰：“获如许财，未知其数。所可虑者，宵小闻之，必来肆窃，否则强项之徒知我骤富，或来欺诈，其奈之何?

    Explanation

    27

    Feng gladly followed Yao's instructions, returned home, and taught his wife how to cook and roast food. His wife said, "We haven't lit the fire for a long time, and the ash has piled up. Why not remove it first?" Feng went to a neighbor's house to borrow a spade and a basket, acted rashly, and the stove collapsed. His wife was surprised and said, "This is really unlucky. For now, it would be better to carry away the bricks and ash, dig the ground to make a new stove, and meet the urgent need." The couple worked together, and before they had finished digging, they found stones. Removing the stones, they discovered two large jars filled with shining silver. The couple was greatly pleased, and Feng said, "What should we do now?" His wife said, "Let's use the basket to move them into the bed, and then we can think about it." They proceeded to move the silver, and under each jar, they found more stones and jars. They repeated this process three times, ending up with six jars in total. Feng said, "We have obtained so much wealth, but we don't know the exact amount. What we should be concerned about is that small-minded people will hear about it and come to steal, or that stubborn individuals will know we have suddenly become rich and come to deceive us. What can we do about it?"

    28

    ”妻曰：“适闻山洞之客正需此物，况素昧平生，偶尔相值，能济我贫，其为人尚义可知。何不举以托之，则彼得应急而我有依倚矣!”冯曰：“汝言实获我心。”亟往市中买熟物，携灯而往。姚曰：“来何濡滞，予望眼几穿焉。”冯酌酒进食，将得有藏金，欲求寄顿之说从容告之。姚惊喜曰：“足下言果实否?”冯曰：“我感君义，其何敢欺!”饭毕，偕往观之，见盈室白物，姚曰：“天赐足下，兼济予事，实无疆之庥，敢不荷此重任。”遂约为昆季，以嫂呼冯妻。出对牌以半交嫂曰：“予同冯兄先归，即饬健仆以筒来装运，将半牌为验，符合者给之。”

    Explanation

    28

    The wife said, "I just heard that the guest from the mountain cave needs this item. Besides, we are strangers to each other, and our meeting is by chance. He can help me out of poverty, so it can be seen that he is a man of righteousness. Why not entrust it to him, then he will get what he needs in a hurry and I will have support!" Feng replied, "Your words truly touch my heart." He hurriedly went to the market to buy prepared food and carried a lamp with him. Yao said, "Why are you so slow? My eyes almost pop out from waiting." Feng drank wine and ate, planning to have some hidden gold, and wanted to casually ask about a place to store it. Yao was surprised and joyful, saying, "Is what you say true?" Feng said, "I am moved by your righteousness, how could I dare to lie!" After finishing the meal, they went together to see it, and found the room filled with white objects. Yao said, "Heaven has bestowed this upon you, benefiting my affairs as well, truly a boundless blessing, how dare I not take on this heavy responsibility." They then agreed to be like brothers, and Yao addressed Feng's wife as sister-in-law. Yao took out a paired token and gave half to his sister-in-law, saying, "I will go back with Feng brother first, and will immediately send strong servants with barrels to load and transport. We will use half of the token as verification, and those who match will be given the goods."

    29

    偕冯反其室入后户，觉院宇轩巨，深邃如入神庙。俾冯薰沐更衣，居然富贵气象，且嘱曰：“予将命乘舆迎兄来，前见诸客商，当示尊重。”姚先出。是时客商候无音耗，正喧嚣间，见主人归，环绕而詈曰：“改岁在迩，速清我价，不汝宽矣。”姚大笑摆手曰：“是些些者，何足道!予不过偶为人累，迟数日耳。今迓予盟兄至，百万金刻即来矣。诸客长皆以大方著者，毋形小器，贻予盟兄笑也。”正排解间，司阍者报冯翁来。姚肃衣冠出迎，众皆屏息以观。见冯翁者年约四旬，裘裳都丽，仆从趋跄，主人足恭致敬，迓入中堂。客商次第相见毕，旋命开筵。姚拱冯翁居首座。冯再三让年长者，客皆曰：“吾侪行规，不以齿序，惟财为雄。久闻吾翁富甲大邦，宜居是位，勿撝谦过当，俾吾侪惶恐。”姚曰：“客言是也。”扶冯上座，肴馔之盛，皆冯生平所未睹者。宴罢，仆放大天平居中，银筒涌至，堆积如山，诸伙伴持筹握筭，一一秤之，共得六十万金。

    Explanation

    29

    Together with Feng, they entered through the back door and felt the courtyard and hall were grand and spacious, deep and profound, like entering a temple. Feng was made to bathe, perfume, and change clothes, and he appeared with the atmosphere of wealth and nobility. Yao then said, "I will send a carriage to welcome my brother. Before meeting the various merchants, we should show respect." Yao left first. At that time, the merchants were waiting without any news, and in the midst of their commotion, they saw the owner return. They surrounded him and scolded, "The New Year is approaching soon. Quickly settle my payment, or I will not be lenient with you!" Yao laughed loudly and waved his hand, saying, "These trivial matters, what is worth mentioning! I am merely temporarily burdened by others and delayed for a few days. Now I am welcoming my sworn brother, and a million gold coins will arrive immediately. All the guests are known for their generosity. Do not show petty behavior, or you will make my sworn brother laugh." Just as they were mediating, the gatekeeper reported that Mr. Feng had arrived. Yao dressed formally and went out to greet him. The crowd held their breath to watch. Those who saw Mr. Feng were about forty years old, wearing luxurious fur and clothes, with his servants following in a respectful manner. The host showed utmost respect and welcomed him into the central hall. The merchants met in order and completed the introductions, and the banquet was immediately ordered. Yao reverently seated Mr. Feng in the first seat. Mr. Feng repeatedly yielded to the elderly, but the guests said, "Our rules of conduct are not by age, only wealth is supreme. We have long heard that my elder is wealthy in a major country and should sit in this position. Do not be overly humble, or you will make us anxious." Yao said, "The guests are right." He helped Mr. Feng to the seat. The abundance of food and drink was all what Mr. Feng had never seen before. After the banquet, the servant placed a large scale in the center, and silver tubes poured in, piling up like a mountain. The partners held counting sticks and weighed them one by one, totaling sixty million gold coins.

    30

    姚呼诸客领价而散，谓冯曰：“弟之起居服食皆足以供，吾兄可无事操劳，其将迎嫂来同室。执此合业经营，无他虑矣。”冯唯唯。由是行业益盛，二姓之子孙，至今称巨富云。

    Explanation

    30

    Yao called all the guests to collect their fees and then dispersed, saying to Feng, "My younger brother's daily life, clothing, and food are all adequately provided for; my elder brother can rest assured without any worries. I will bring your wife to live in the same room. By working together in this business, there will be no other concerns." Feng nodded in agreement. From then on, the industry flourished even more, and the descendants of the two families are still known as wealthy to this day.

    31

    查氏女
    　　
    　　万历间倭寇之乱，缘日本国王正妃卒，王思中华女子艳丽，遣将入寇，沿海掳掠。至盐官州，猝不及备，官吏弃城逃窜。有查氏女者，年已及瓜，慧中秀外，久失恃。闻寇至，请父兄同众奔避，曰：“女儿足弱，不能追随，途中虑有牵顾，俱罹于祸。莫若女自为计，决不贻羞，恐亦不致遽死也。”寇急，父兄迫之，坚持不行。父兄泣舍而去。女平日阅本草，见有药名闹扬花者，服之即死，周时可醒，预已市得，遂密缝上下衣，研药为末以俟。闻寇入城，遽吞之毙，倭入室，见女颜色如生，抚之温软，冀可救活，且容貌倾城，不忍舍去，负之入舟。逾时而苏，见身卧海舶，诸女环泣。细询之，知同被难者。女慰之曰：“毋徒恐怖，能从我谋，似可脱难。”诸女密商之，女授以计，缘倭将为王觅妃，故无敢犯诸女。及舟抵日本，倭将见美女无恙，欢欣鼓舞，以献国王。王见查氏女，遂其所欲，命通事告以敕立正妃之意。

    Explanation

    31

    A Woman from the Zhao Family

    During the Wanli period, there was a disturbance by Japanese pirates, as the Japanese king's chief consort had recently passed away. The king, longing for the beauty of Chinese women, sent his generals to invade and plunder along the coast. When they reached Yanguan Prefecture, the local officials were caught off guard and fled the city. Among them was a young woman from the Zhao family, who had reached marriageable age, was bright-minded and beautiful, and had long been without her mother. Upon hearing of the invasion, she asked her father and elder brother to flee with the crowd, saying, "My feet are weak, and I cannot keep up. I fear I will be a burden on the road, bringing disaster to all. It would be better for me to plan for myself. I will not bring shame, and perhaps I will not die immediately." Her father and brother urged her to flee, but she firmly refused to go. They wept and left her behind.

    The girl, who regularly read the Bencao (herbal texts), knew of a herb called 闹羊花 (Rhododendron molle), which, if taken, would cause death but could revive after a week. She had already purchased it in advance and secretly sewed it into her upper and lower garments, grinding the herb into powder and waiting. When she heard the invaders enter the city, she quickly swallowed the powder and died. The Japanese entered her room, saw her face still alive, and touched her, finding her warm and soft. They hoped to save her life, and seeing her exceptional beauty, could not bear to leave her, carrying her onto a boat. After some time, she revived, seeing herself lying on a sea vessel, with other women surrounding her and weeping. They asked her in detail and learned they were also in danger. The girl comforted them, saying, "Do not just be terrified. If you follow my plan, it might help you escape." The other women secretly discussed it, and the girl taught them a plan. Because the Japanese general was searching for a queen, none dared to violate the other women. When the boat arrived in Japan, the general saw the beautiful woman unharmed, rejoiced, and danced with joy, presenting her to the king. The king saw the girl from the Zhao family, fulfilled his desire, and ordered an interpreter to announce the intention of making her the official chief consort.

    32

    女曰：“我中华人，愿与中华女子为伍。王若能尽出本国宫女，而以同来诸女为宫人，则惟王所命。”王以其娇弱女子何能为，喜允之。命扶入宫，开合欢宴，女同诸女酌酒劝王，密以前药入酒。王遽吞之，不觉眩晕，意谓醉矣，拥女入大内，欲褫其衣，正枝梧间，王瞠目流涎而倒，不知人事。女搜得兵符，唤诸女同出外廷，传通事谕倭将曰：“王问悉我家有径寸珠能定飓风，命我去取作镇国之宝。尔诸将速备巨舟偕往。”倭将验兵符，信之。

    Explanation

    32

    The woman said, "I am a person of China, and I wish to associate with Chinese women. If the king can completely release all the palace women and make the accompanying women palace attendants, then I will do as the king commands." The king, considering the delicate and weak woman, thought she could do little, and happily agreed. He ordered her to be carried into the palace and a banquet of joy was held. The woman, along with the other women, poured wine to encourage the king, secretly adding a previous medicine to the wine. The king quickly swallowed it and unconsciously felt dizzy, thinking it was due to intoxication. He carried the woman into the inner palace, intending to remove her clothes, but in the midst of hesitation, the king wide-eyed and drooling, fell down, unconscious of human affairs. The woman found the military token, called the other women out to the outer court, and sent a messenger to inform the Japanese general, saying, "The king inquired if my family has a pearl the size of an inch that can calm a hurricane, and ordered me to retrieve it as a national treasure. You generals should quickly prepare large ships to accompany us." The Japanese general verified the military token and believed her.

    33

    遣一旅同诸女扬帆西归。次日，王不视朝，王弟潜入大内探之，见王僵卧于寝，弑之自立。世子怒，各兴其党互相攻击，其国大乱，故无追者。女至盐官城下，已有警备，命通事唤城上长官，女告以故，官狐疑未决。女请唤其父来，认之确，缒入城，告长官，大备酒肉，纳以前药，遣使谕倭众曰：“王妃取宝即回，先以酒肉犒尔等，其各饱餐以待。”众皆乐，醉饱而倒，使者骈戳之，以诸女归宫，不废一矢而得倭首级。遂报大捷，旌查女之门，而各官晋秩有差。

    Explanation

    33

    A group of women set sail westward with a contingent of troops. The following day, the king did not attend court, and his brother secretly entered the palace to investigate. He found the king lying stiffly in bed, dead, and then killed him to seize the throne. The crown prince became enraged, and his factions began to fight each other, causing great chaos in the kingdom. As a result, no one pursued the women. When the women arrived at the city of Yanguan, they found it already on alert. They ordered an interpreter to call the city official and explained their situation. The official was hesitant and undecided. The women asked for their father to come and confirm their identity. Once their identity was verified, they were lowered into the city. They informed the official, who then prepared a lavish feast of food and drink and administered the previous medicine. An envoy was sent to the Japanese troops with a message: "The queen will return with the treasure. First, we will offer you food and drink as a reward. Please eat well and wait." The Japanese troops were delighted, became drunk, and fell unconscious. The envoy then killed them in pairs. The women returned to the palace, and the victory was achieved without a single arrow being fired. The news of the great victory was reported, the women were honored with a banner raised at their gate, and the officials received promotions, with varying degrees of advancement.

    34

    芗厈曰：奇伟者女，无耻者官，寇至则逃窜，寇退则警备，始终雌伏可也，奈何冒查女之功而庸爵赏?吾见其衣冠楚楚，与拜受巾帼等耳。或曰雌飞自然，雄伏造化。若循常理，焉得有此奇闻!
    　　
    　　双缢庙
    　　
    　　任迂叟，浙右儒生。富而无子，惟一女，名之曰“宜男”，饰雕为雄，聊娱膝下。延师教读，以充石麟。时有东邻之子白云娥者，其父老年所得，虑其娇柔难育，为之贯耳披鬟，呼为云姐，附任氏之学，与宜男为窗友。时女年十三，男年十四，两小无猜，二情相洽。校书赌诵，互角聪明。女或胜之，则划云之面，相与嘲笑。适师外出，女之母与姑入塾，不识云之为男也，视其柳眉叠翠，杏脸舒红，与其女壁合珠联，争辉并耀，叹曰：“使宜男而果男也，使配云姐，真一对好姻缘。”姑曰：“侬合为媒，问云姐愿否。”母笑曰：“以待来生。”云始知宜男之为女也，益比昵之。

    Explanation

    34

    Xiang Yan said:
    A remarkable woman, an unashamed official. When the enemy comes, they flee; when the enemy retreats, they prepare for defense. It is acceptable to remain passive and submissive throughout. Why pretend to have found the woman's virtue and grant her an official title? I see them dressed up in fine attire, just like they receive the ceremonial headscarf. Some say that the female flying is natural, and the male's submission is part of the natural order. If we follow common sense, how can such a strange story exist?

    Double-Hanging Temple

    Mr. Ren, the old-fashioned man, a scholar from the right side of Zhejiang.
    He was wealthy but had no son, only one daughter, whom he named "Yinan." He dressed her up as a boy, to amuse himself. He hired a teacher to educate her, hoping she would become a distinguished scholar. At the time, there was a neighbor's son named Baiyun E. His father, being old, feared that he would be too delicate to survive, so he had his ears pierced and adorned him with hairpins, calling him "Yun's sister." He attended Mr. Ren's school and became a close friend with Yinan. Yinan was thirteen years old, and Baiyun E was fourteen. They were both young and innocent, and their feelings for each other were mutual. They competed in reading and studying, each trying to outdo the other. If Yinan won, she would scratch Baiyun E's face, and they would laugh together. When the teacher was away, Yinan's mother and her aunt went to the school. They did not recognize Baiyun E as a boy, seeing his willow-like eyebrows and peach-like cheeks, all red and green. They compared Yinan and Baiyun E, saying they were like walls and pearls joining together, competing in brightness and radiance. They sighed and said, "If Yinan were indeed a boy, and matched with Yun's sister, it would be a perfect match." The aunt said, "I will act as a matchmaker. Will Yun's sister agree?" The mother laughed and said, "We will wait for the next life." Baiyun E then realized that Yinan was a girl and became even closer to her.

    35

    一日，师讲《易》，至“男女构精”句，草草读过。女请问构精之义，师嗔之曰：“是非儿女子之所宜问。”女曰：“圣经贤传，岂有不可对人言者哉?”师莞尔他顾。云凝睇流盼，悄唤“阿呆”。女更狐疑莫释矣。值师之友来约湖山之游，联袂而去。女问云曰：“姐纵慧悟，未必能通易理。何哂我为?”云曰：“难言也。秘密之旨，非效其形状，终不明晰。”女笑从之，携手入师卧室，共坐榻上。云拥女于怀，探手于袴，抚其琦葩初绽，莲瓣微开，女嗤嗤笑，却之曰：“与姐等耳，毋徒相扰。”云曰：“人各具体，奚能相同?”乃推女横陈，急卸其袴曰：“我教汝构精。”女拒之曰：“昭昭白日，姐不羞耶?”回手抚云，则红霞仙杵，触指翘然。女讶曰：“是何物也，我何无之?”云笑曰：“以有补无，斯谓之构。请尝试之。”于是牡丹露滴，巫峡云停。女整衣起笑曰：“构精如是，无怪师之秘而不宣也。”

    Explanation

    35

    One day, the teacher was lecturing on the I Ching and came to the phrase "the union of male and female energies." He read it hastily, without much explanation. The girl asked about the meaning of "union of male and female energies," and the teacher scolded her, saying, "It is not something suitable for children to ask." The girl replied, "The sacred texts and teachings of the wise, are there any parts that cannot be spoken of openly?" The teacher smiled and looked away. The girl glanced around and whispered, "Dumb." The girl was even more confused and uncertain. Just then, the teacher's friend came to invite them for a trip to the mountains and lakes, and they left together, holding hands.

    The girl asked the girl, "Even if you are wise and insightful, you might not understand the principles of the I Ching. Why did you laugh at me?" The girl replied, "It's hard to explain. The secret meaning is not fully clear unless you imitate its form." The girl laughed and followed her, and they entered the teacher's bedroom, sitting side by side on the bed. The girl hugged the girl and touched her, feeling the delicate petals of her flower just beginning to bloom, like a lotus bud. The girl giggled and pushed her away, saying, "Even if we are sisters, don't just bother me." The girl said, "Everyone has their own body, how could they be the same?" She then laid the girl down and quickly took off her skirt, saying, "I will teach you the union of male and female energies." The girl resisted, saying, "In broad daylight, don't you feel ashamed?" She reached out and touched the girl, feeling the red, cloud-like rod, which stood erect. The girl was surprised, asking, "What is this thing, and why do I not have it?" The girl smiled, saying, "To use what you have to compensate for what you lack, that is called 'conjunction.' Please try it." Thus, the girl felt the dew of the peony, and the cloud of the Wuxia valley stopped. The girl dressed and stood up, laughing, "The union of male and female energies is like this, no wonder the teacher kept it secret and did not explain it."

    36

    从此师或不在，则玉山相并，雾鬓斯磨，抢韵联吟，递相赠答。云填《望江南》一阕投女曰：“香闺忆，忆昔乍亲卿。锦帐甫垂参喜惧，宝钗乱颤忍嘤鸣，此刻不胜情。”女答之曰：“香闺忆，忆昔就萧郎。欲避羞难遮绮扇，最销魂处卸华妆，共入黑甜乡。”云叠赠曰：“香闺忆，忆昔闹阳台。春融柳舞莺梭捷，露沁花娇燕剪开，浓艳满情怀。”女复曰：“香闺忆，腼腆忆初朝。艳夺小桃嗔婢妮，样留新月倩郎描，另有一番娇。”欢娱易过，不觉腊去春回，女年加长，待字深闺，不复出就外傅矣。云亦还其本来，另寻师友。女作书投之曰：“同学妹宜男裣衽致启云娥哥哥足下：忆昔情融绛帐，暂得连镳；泣别萧斋，遽成分襼。缅维现身说法，秘传法象之微；口角吟香，共斗香奁之句。方期此乐可常，岂意于今不再。况雌伏者顿尔鸾翔，雄飞者反嗟豹隐。我心匪石，能不黯然。伏愿速遣冰人，以践海誓。则半瑕之璧，幸得全归；已破之舟，不致沦溺。

    Explanation

    36

    If the teacher is absent from here, then the jade mountains stand together, the misty hair is worn down, and we compete in rhythm and join in recitation, exchanging gifts and responses. Clouds filled a Wang Jiangnan poem and presented it to the girl, saying: "Memories of the fragrant chamber, remembering the first time I met you. The brocade canopy was just lowered, mingling joy and fear, the jade hairpin trembled chaotically, resisting the soft cooing, this moment cannot bear the emotion." The girl replied: "Memories of the fragrant chamber, remembering the first time I met Xiao Lang. Wanting to hide my shame, I could not cover the embroidered fan, the most soul-searing place is where I take off my elaborate makeup, together entering the dark sweet land." Clouds filled another Wang Jiangnan poem and presented it to the girl, saying: "Memories of the fragrant chamber, remembering the noisy Taiyang Palace. Spring melts, willows dance, swallows weave quickly, dew seeps into the flowers, the swallows cut open, the intense beauty fills the heart." The girl replied: "Memories of the fragrant chamber, shyly remembering the first morning. The beauty outshines the young peach, scolding the maid, the image lingers like a new moon, asking the lover to draw it, there is another kind of charm." Joy easily passes, and before one realizes it, winter departs and spring returns. The girl grows older, awaiting marriage in a deep inner chamber, no longer going out to receive instruction from an external teacher. Clouds also return to their original state and seek other teachers and friends. The girl writes a letter to her brother Yun娥, saying: "The student sister Yi Nan, with folded hands, writes to her brother Yun娥: Remembering the past, our hearts melted in the red canopy, temporarily able to walk side by side; parting with tears in the quiet study, suddenly becoming separated. Recalling teaching through personal experience, secretly transmitting the subtle aspects of the law; the corners of the mouth reciting fragrant poetry, competing in the verses of the scented casket. I hoped this joy could last forever, how could I have thought it would not happen again. The one who was submissive suddenly soars like a phoenix, the one who was dominant laments the hidden leopard. My heart is not a stone, how can one not feel sad. I humbly wish to quickly send the matchmaker, to fulfill the sea vow. Then the jade with a slight flaw, fortunately, can be fully returned; the broken boat, not to sink."

    37

    萦萦俟诺，戚戚布函。伏祈采览。”云得书情急，恃宠撒娇，直告父母。父虽怒其不端，然事已如斯，转虑其子失所，遂倩密友即任翁之戚，敬备凫仪，往求凤卜。任曰：“西邻白翁固所索识，第伊仅有掌珠，那得配我假子?”媒曰：“其女实男子，伪作女妆，以期易养耳。”任曰：“即云姐耶?”媒曰：“是也，翩翩美少，谅必中东床之选。”任曰：“不可，不可，云姐昔与我女同学，若与联姻，是无私有弊，玷我家声矣。

    Explanation

    37

    "Waiting eagerly for a promise, I wrote this letter with deep emotion. Humbly requesting your consideration." Upon receiving the letter, Yun was in a state of urgent anticipation, acting spoiled and clingy, and directly informed her parents. The father was angry at her inappropriate behavior, but since the matter had reached this point, he began to worry about his son losing his place. He then asked a close friend, who was a relative of Mr. Ren, to respectfully prepare a ceremonial gift and go to seek a divination. Mr. Ren said, "The respected neighbor Bai is indeed someone I know well, but he only has a cherished daughter, how can she marry my adopted son?" The matchmaker said, "Her daughter is actually a boy, pretending to be a girl in order to be easier to raise." Mr. Ren said, "Is she Yun's sister?" The matchmaker replied, "Yes, she is a graceful and handsome young man, surely the ideal match." Mr. Ren said, "No, no, Yun's sister was my daughter's classmate in the past. If we were to marry them, there would be no benefit but only harm, tarnishing our family's reputation."

    38

    ”媒以童稚何知，决无他故之言，再三劝之。任怒掉头而入，媒复白翁，云不知也，尚欢欣鼓舞，与女书曰：“同学愚兄云娥顿首启宜妹妆次：睽隔半年，相思两地。何期云间之鹤，忽堕瑶函；原上之鸰，载衔嘉命。焚芸盥诵，顿慰调饥。来谕悉遵，冰人已遣，谅尊甫知我，必允好逑。伫盼河桥鹊影，正当授采之期；缑岭鸾声，拟上催妆之什矣。克敦旧好，再缔新欢。如鼓瑟琴，重叠香闺之韵；宜其家室，应续化生之文。谅必卿为我喜，我为卿贺。书报宜妹知之。”

    Explanation

    38

    The matchmaker, with a child's knowledge, certainly has no ulterior motives, and persuaded her repeatedly. Ren angrily turned around and went inside, and the matchmaker then reported to the old man, saying that she did not know, but she was still happy and excited, and wrote to the girl saying:

    "Dear sister, my foolish elder brother, Yun'e, respectfully greets you. We have been separated for half a year, missing each other from two different places. Who would have thought that the crane in the clouds suddenly fell into a jade box; the lark on the plain carries a good message. Burning incense and washing hands before reading, I am suddenly comforted and my hunger is alleviated. I will fully comply with your instructions, and the matchmaker has already been sent. I believe your father knows me well and will certainly agree to this match. I am waiting for the magpie's shadow under the bridge, it is the right time for the match; the sound of the phoenix on the mountain of Gou, I will write a poem to celebrate the wedding. To strengthen our old friendship, to form new happiness. Like playing the zither and the lute, repeatedly enhancing the elegance of the inner chambers; suitable for the household, should continue the text of transformation and rebirth. I am sure you will be happy for me, and I will be happy for you. I write this to inform my dear sister."

    Now, let's move on to the second stage of the translation process. We will translate the source text into English based on the pre-drafting research and the established translation plan.

    媒以童稚何知，决无他故之言，再三劝之。任怒掉头而入，媒复白翁，云不知也，尚欢欣鼓舞，与女书曰：

    The matchmaker, with a child's knowledge, certainly has no ulterior motives, and persuaded her repeatedly. Ren angrily turned around and went inside, and the matchmaker then reported to the old man, saying that she did not know, but she was still happy and excited, and wrote to the girl saying:

    “同学愚兄云娥顿首启宜妹妆次：睽隔半年，相思两地。何期云间之鹤，忽堕瑶函；原上之鸰，载衔嘉命。焚芸盥诵，顿慰调饥。来谕悉遵，冰人已遣，谅尊甫知我，必允好逑。伫盼河桥鹊影，正当授采之期；缑岭鸾声，拟上催妆之什矣。克敦旧好，再缔新欢。如鼓瑟琴，重叠香闺之韵；宜其家室，应续化生之文。谅必卿为我喜，我为卿贺。书报宜妹知之。”

    Dear sister, my foolish elder brother, Yun'e, respectfully greets you. We have been separated for half a year, missing each other from two different places. Who would have thought that the crane in the clouds suddenly fell into a jade box; the lark on the plain carries a good message. Burning incense and washing hands before reading, I am suddenly comforted and my hunger is alleviated. I will fully comply with your instructions, and the matchmaker has already been sent. I believe your father knows me well and will certainly agree to this match. I am waiting for the magpie's shadow under the bridge, it is the right time for the match; the sound of the phoenix on the mountain of Gou, I will write a poem to celebrate the wedding. To strengthen our old friendship, to form new happiness. Like playing the zither and the lute, repeatedly enhancing the elegance of the inner chambers; suitable for the household, should continue the text of transformation and rebirth. I am sure you will be happy for me, and I will be happy for you. I write this to inform my dear sister.

    39

    时女已闻父决绝之言，饮泣数日矣。母知其意，反加詈也。得云书，恸绝复苏，复书曰：“顾接琅函，深叨锦注。第君家柯使，徒抱空言，老父以迹涉嫌疑，遽尔决绝。云郎其未之知耶?从此机云池馆，鹤唳空闻；王谢楼台，燕夤靡托。此日青闺已经蝶散，当年红粉将属烟销。是固妾之命也。想云郎才是子桓，徒怀绛树；情同穆满，空忆亦乌。谅必同此悲愤耶?然以马卿之才，不患无文君之配。而妾则已非完璧，岂可二天?故夜寝偶思，则涕泗被面；晨兴忽感，则爪指乱爬。

    Explanation

    39

    At the time, the woman had already heard her father's final words and had been crying silently for several days. Her mother, understanding her feelings, instead scolded her. Upon receiving a letter from Yun, she was so overcome with grief that she fainted but then recovered.
