The high officials all set up a farewell feast; when traveling, guards led the way, and when stopping, lodgings were well-prepared. All the cities and towns passed through welcomed and saw him off with great respect, from the provincial governors and supervisors downward. Upon reaching the riverside, there were more than ten large ships. Shen thought that a single shoulder of luggage required so many boats and carriages, and began to ask the servant, who presented a single sheet of paper. Counting it silently, it turned out to be all the clothes and boxes prepared over the years, as well as the items displayed in the study, regardless of books and antiques, all of which were brought along.

    81

    沈笑谓苍头曰：“误矣，是皆大将军之具，何可携归？”苍头曰：“大将军命，恐先生思念旧物，故悉举以赠。”沈感甚，意谓虽不得财，诸物犹值万计，半生无虑饥寒矣。及抵苏，则都督率百官迎于舟中，沈再拜以辞，曰：“予小子其敢劳上官？”众曰：“夫子为大将军上宾，我等其何敢亵？”辞众归，卫士拥护至第，则旧宅全非，门第轩昂，居然巨室。沈徘徊不敢入，是时观者如堵，邻叟谓曰：“封翁望郎君久矣，何不入室？”沈曰：“我家何在？”叟笑曰：“郎君开创大宅，而诿为不知也？”沈始敢入，迎于门者皆干仆，登堂则诰命辉煌，入室则父母衣四品服，俨然命卿。

    Explanation

    81

    Shen smiled and said to the servant, "You've made a mistake; these are all the belongings of the Grand General, how can they be taken back?" The servant replied, "The Grand General's order, fearing that you might miss your old belongings, so he has given them all as a gift." Shen was very moved, thinking that although he did not gain wealth, the various items were still worth tens of thousands, enough to ensure he would not worry about hunger or cold for half his life. When he arrived in Su, the Inspector and all the officials met him on the boat. Shen bowed twice to decline, saying, "How dare I trouble the high officials?" The officials responded, "You are the Grand General's honored guest, how dare we be disrespectful?" After declining, Shen returned home, escorted by guards. When he reached his house, the old house was completely transformed, with grand doors and a dignified appearance, indeed a large mansion. Shen hesitated to enter, as spectators gathered like a wall. A neighbor elder said, "The old master has been waiting for you for a long time, why don't you enter the room?" Shen asked, "Where is my home?" The elder smiled and said, "You have created a grand house, yet you claim not to know about it?" Shen then dared to enter, and those who welcomed him at the door were all servants. When he entered the hall, the imperial edict was splendid, and when he entered the room, his parents were dressed in the robes of the fourth rank, truly like a high-ranking official.

    82

    沈趋魂膝下，问所由来，其父母诧曰：“汝自为之，何不自知？”沈实对以不觉之故，其父北向揖曰：“大将军成全汝，可谓再造之天矣。”因云：“某年方伯来，谓老夫曰奉大将军命，为翁改宅。遂去旧而廓充之。老夫曰宅大人稀，得毋寥寂？方伯即送仆婢若干人，并良田质库，以及陈设之物，无不毕具。旋得捷报日，汝从大将军西征，以军功得县令，屡次荐升，今为观察，老夫妇皆膺封典，惟虑汝在军前，悬悬而望，今汝归来，举宅大庆矣。”沈感极涕零，出见诸仆，数十人以次叩谒。一老苍头捧椟跪陈数籍，以告曰：“是皆田宅人丁契券之属，大将军命置者。老奴已经理数载矣，请郎君捡收，以便老奴带卫士等回都覆命也。”沈慰劳之，受籍计点，其值百万。乃启谢大将军，自此寂然，亦无回音。沈思大将军权势过甚，虑罹党祸，不敢出仕，称疾家居。

    Explanation

    82

    沈趋魂膝下，问所由来，其父母诧曰：“汝自为之，何不自知？”沈实对以不觉之故，其父北向揖曰：“大将军成全汝，可谓再造之天矣。”因云：“某年方伯来，谓老夫曰奉大将军命，为翁改宅。遂去旧而廓充之。老夫曰宅大人稀，得毋寥寂？方伯即送仆婢若干人，并良田质库，以及陈设之物，无不毕具。旋得捷报日，汝从大将军西征，以军功得县令，屡次荐升，今为观察，老夫妇皆膺封典，惟虑汝在军前，悬悬而望，今汝归来，举宅大庆矣。”沈感极涕零，出见诸仆，数十人以次叩谒。一老苍头捧椟跪陈数籍，以告曰：“是皆田宅人丁契券之属，大将军命置者。老奴已经理数载矣，请郎君捡收，以便老奴带卫士等回都覆命也。”沈慰劳之，受籍计点，其值百万。乃启谢大将军，自此寂然，亦无回音。沈思大将军权势过甚，虑罹党祸，不敢出仕，称疾家居。

    Shen approached his parents with respect, asking where he had come from. His parents were surprised and said, "You did it yourself, why didn't you know?" Shen honestly replied that he had not been aware. His father bowed northward and said, "The Grand General has saved you, which can be considered a second life." He continued, "One year, the Regional Governor came and told me that he was following the Grand General's orders to renovate your father's house. The old house was torn down and expanded. I said that the house was large but there were few people, would it not be lonely? The Regional Governor then sent several servants and maids, along with good farmland, a pawnshop, and all sorts of household items, everything was fully provided. Soon after, I received news of a victory, and you went west with the Grand General, gaining the position of county magistrate through military merit, and were repeatedly recommended for promotion, now serving as an Inspector. My wife and I both received official honors, but we were worried about you being at the front lines, anxiously waiting, now that you have returned, the whole household is celebrating!" Shen was deeply moved to tears, and went out to see the servants, dozens of them kneeling in order to pay their respects. An elderly servant held a box and presented several documents, saying, "These are all records of land, property, and household members, as ordered by the Grand General. I have been managing these for several years, please have the young master inspect and take them, so that I may return to the capital with the guards to report." Shen comforted him, took the documents and counted them, and found their value to be a million. He then wrote a letter of thanks to the Grand General, after which there was no further word. Shen thought that the Grand General's power was too great, fearing he might be implicated in political persecution, so he did not take up official duties, claiming illness and staying at home.

    83

    不数载，闻帝愠大将军，迁谪吴地，百官交章劾其肆横状，帝震怒，命削职拿问。过苏郡，沈贿通缇骑，潜入舟中，抚大将军而泣。大将军笑曰：“大丈夫视死如归，予即不法，实无悖逆。第上怒不解，予固不望生还。况予以儒生起家，权势倾百寮，享用逾万乘，得无盈满之诛乎？惟幼子托先生青目。”沈唯唯，纳赆千缗不受。入都，帝廷鞫之，皆承赐缳首，籍其家，诸子弟皆遣戍远方，为怨家所灭。其幼子因无职名得以脱漏。沈闻之，不胜悲感。是夜，突有北来流丐二人，入宅求见。阍者与之钱不受，丐曰：“但得一晤主人，死亦无憾。”沈出视之，即老苍头与公子也，相对恸绝。遂匿以为子，以存大将军之后。

    Explanation

    83

    In a few years, the emperor heard that he was angry with the Grand General and had exiled him to Wu. The officials submitted multiple memorials accusing him of outrageous behavior, and the emperor was furious, ordering his position to be stripped and him to be arrested for questioning. Passing through Suzhou, Shen bribed the imperial guards and secretly entered the boat, comforting the Grand General and weeping. The Grand General laughed and said, "A true man regards death as returning home. I may be lawless, but I have no intention of rebellion. But the emperor's anger is not resolved, so I naturally do not expect to return alive. Especially since I rose to prominence as a scholar, my power and influence overshadowed all officials, and my enjoyment and possessions exceeded those of a king. Could I not face punishment for being too full of myself? I only entrust my young son to your care." Shen nodded in agreement, accepted a thousand strings of cash as a gift but refused it. Arriving in the capital, the emperor interrogated him in court, and all accepted the gift of a noose for their necks, confiscated his property, and all his sons and younger brothers were sent to distant border garrisons, where they were destroyed by their enemies. His young son escaped because he had no official title. Shen, upon hearing this, could not bear the sorrow. That night, two beggars from the north suddenly arrived at his house and asked to see him. The gatekeeper gave them money, but they refused it, saying, "If only we could meet the master, even dying would not be regrettable." Shen went out to see them, and they were the old servant and the young master, and they wept bitterly together. He then hid them and treated them as his own children to preserve the Grand General's lineage.

    84

    无真叟
    　　
    　　浙人章生，在闽游幕，应台湾县之聘，司征收，偕渡重洋。方其入署时，同事者欺其木讷。凡宽房畅室可以栖止者，皆为占去，章生几无息肩地。主人悯之，让居二堂左个，喧嚣甚，独步后圃，以散其心。见岑楼三间，左右有厢，环以短垣，地极幽雅，而寂无人居。叩其关，则锁闭坚固，莫知其故。徘徊间，有汲井入至，咨询其缘，始知楼为狐仙供奉之所，无敢擅入者，惟主人行香，一开即闭。

    Explanation

    84

    无真叟

    Zhejiang native Zhang Sheng traveled to Fujian as a retainer and was invited by Taiwan County to serve as the collector of taxes. He crossed the vast ocean with his colleagues. When he first entered the office, his colleagues mocked his taciturnity. All the spacious and well-ventilated rooms suitable for residence were taken up, leaving Zhang Sheng with almost no place to rest. The master pitied him and allowed him to live in the left wing of the second hall, which was very noisy. To calm his mind, Zhang Sheng walked alone in the rear garden. He saw a three-story tower with wings on both sides, surrounded by a low wall. The place was extremely elegant but quiet and uninhabited. When he knocked on its gate, it was tightly locked, and no one knew the reason. While wandering around, a well-digger came up and asked about the reason. Zhang Sheng then learned that the tower was a place for the fox spirit to be worshipped, and no one dared to enter it擅自. Only the master could open it for incense offerings, and it would be closed immediately afterward.

    85

    章生年已六旬，向习庄老，好谈清静，亦无所畏忌，今得其地，岂肯舍旃，急与主人谋迁，许之。遂启关粪除，以安卧榻于层楼之下，寓仆于厢。肃具衣冠，焚香登梯，见楼上清洁无尘，中设一几，一牌书“无真仙人之位”。章生再拜，致敬而祷曰：“弟子某游幕至此，与仙有缘，寄托宇下，伏祈庇佑，如蒙不弃，下愚某亦好道，维冀开其茅塞，诏以自新，则感受慈恩，益无既极。”自此安居楼下，从公无扰。朔望，则具鸡子清酒再申前祝，积诚不懈。虽无形迹，而风清月朗，则闻履声橐橐，自楼而下，或散步中庭，或推门径去。章生益朝夕致虔，哀祝前词。

    Explanation

    85

    Chang Sheng, now in his sixties, had previously studied the works of Zhuangzi and Laozi, enjoying discussions of tranquility and free from fear or hesitation. Now that he had found this place, how could he abandon it? He hastily sought advice from the host to move, and the host agreed. Thus, he opened the gate and cleaned up the excrement, setting up a sleeping mat under the multi-story building and housing his servant in the wing. Dressed in formal attire, he burned incense and climbed the ladder, seeing that the upstairs was clean and dust-free, with a table in the middle and a sign reading "No True Immortal's Seat." Chang Sheng kneeled twice, paid respect, and prayed, saying, "Disciple [name] traveled here as a guest, having a connection with the immortals, depending on the shelter. I pray for protection. If not rejected, this humble disciple also likes the Way, hoping to open his blocked mind, command to reform oneself, then feel the kindness, becoming increasingly boundless." From then on, he lived peacefully downstairs, not disturbing public affairs. On the first and fifteenth days of the month, he prepared eggs and clear wine to repeat the previous blessing, accumulating sincerity without slackening. Although there were no visible signs, but the wind was clear and the moon was bright, he heard the sound of footsteps from the upstairs to downstairs, or walking in the courtyard, or pushing the door and going directly away. Chang Sheng increased his daily devotion, repeating the previous prayer words.

    86

    半载余，时值中秋，天朗气清。是夜设几席于院落，樽酒盘飧，将欲独酌。忽一杯旋转，自移至对座而定，章生见而喜，急具衣冠拜请曰：“仙人果怜下愚，来格来飨，某敢侍坐。”亦举一杯敬谨酬酢，则无音响。又夜，独坐房中，忽一座自移相并。章生益喜曰：“仙人来矣。”亟向座拜迎，亦无他异。章生悟曰：“仙人屡顾而不接洽者，必因有仆在侧，恐泄其机耳。”次夜，托故遣仆他室卧，闭门而敦请，忽座上有人言曰：“先生可谓诚矣，老朽久鉴贤衷，惟不敢稍示异迹，以贻人口实耳。今吾两人相对，可无他虑。请言其志。”章生耸然曰：“下愚久幕仙道而无所遇，意谓黄庭道德诸经，皆古之寓言耳。今坐对仙人，是必真实无妄，请明以教我。

    Explanation

    86

    Half a year later, during the Mid-Autumn Festival, the sky was clear and the air was fresh. That night, Mr. Zhang set up a table and chairs in the courtyard, with wine and food, ready to drink alone. Suddenly, a cup began to spin and moved itself to the opposite seat, where it came to rest. Mr. Zhang saw this and was delighted. He quickly put on his formal attire and bowed to invite, saying, "Immortal, you truly pity my foolishness and have come to grace and enjoy the feast. I dare to sit beside you." He also raised a cup to respectfully reciprocate, but there was no sound. Another night, sitting alone in the room, a seat suddenly moved itself next to him. Mr. Zhang was even more delighted and said, "The immortal has come." He hurriedly bowed to welcome the seat, but there was no other unusual occurrence. Mr. Zhang realized and said, "The immortal repeatedly looked but did not interact, surely because there was a servant nearby, fearing the secret would be revealed." The next night, he made an excuse to send the servant to sleep in another room, closed the door, and earnestly invited. Suddenly, someone on the seat spoke and said, "Master, you can truly be called sincere. I, an old man, have long observed your heart, but dared not show any unusual signs to give others a pretext. Now that we are facing each other, there is nothing to worry about. Please speak of your aspirations." Mr. Zhang was startled and said, "A foolish person like me has long admired the way of immortals but has never met one. I thought the Huangting Jing and other moral texts were just ancient allegories. Now sitting before an immortal, it must be truly real and not false. Please clearly teach me."

    87

    ”对座笑曰：“老朽道号无真，而先生欲真之，奈何？”章生曰：“请问无真之义？”对座曰：“即如目前之房屋器具真矣，百年后安在耶？父子夫妇真矣，瞑目后何有耶？世人惟其认真即为真，累六欲得以牵之，三毒得以制之，老朽无此，故无牵制，不过一闲散汉。尊之曰仙人，则吾岂敢？请以无真叟唤吾可也。”章生曰：“叟为何朝人，以何成道，因何不居山林而溷迹坐寰？请言其故。”叟曰：“吾曾见宋高宗南渡，至今五百余年矣。自天台得道后，奉天狐命派司是邑印信，故不得不居此耳。”章生曰：“凡有衙门，即有印信，其尽仙人司之耶？”曳曰：“然。”章生曰：“何衙署中有彰彰供奉者，有默默无闻者？”叟曰：“道蕴有浅深，故灵应亦有隐显。如世之官长有能有拙，其能者赫赫争先，其拙者事事退后，亦由此理。”自是遂成相与，无夕不聚。凡章生有忧喜事，必预报之；有过失处，必规劝之。俨然哲友。

    Explanation

    87

    The person sitting across smiled and said, "I am an old and worn-out Daoist with the name 'No True,' and you wish to call me 'True.' How can that be?" Zhang asked, "May I ask the meaning of 'No True'?" The person sitting across replied, "For example, the houses and utensils we see now are real, but where will they be in a hundred years? Father, son, husband, and wife are real, but what remains after death? People make things true by taking them seriously, but they are burdened by the six desires and thus led astray, and controlled by the three poisons. I have none of these, so I have no such constraints, I am just an idle man. To call me an immortal, I dare not; please call me Old Man No True." Zhang asked, "Who was the old man in which dynasty? By what means did he attain enlightenment? Why did he not live in the mountains and forests but instead mingle in the world? Please tell me the reason." The old man said, "I have seen Emperor Gaozong of Song's southern migration, and it has been more than five hundred years since then. After attaining enlightenment at Tian Tai Mountain, I was appointed by a fox spirit to manage the seals of this town, so I have no choice but to stay here." Zhang asked, "Wherever there is an office, there is a seal, are they all managed by immortals?" The old man replied, "Yes." Zhang asked, "Why are some offices openly revered, while others are silent and unknown?" The old man said, "The depth of Dao varies, so its manifestations are sometimes hidden and sometimes revealed. Like officials in the world, some are capable and some are inept; the capable ones stand out prominently, while the inept ones lag behind in everything, and this is also the same principle." From then on, they became close friends, meeting every night. Whenever Zhang had joyful or sorrowful events, the old man would always foretell them; whenever Zhang made mistakes, the old man would always advise him. They were like wise friends.

    88

    时因主人酬神演剧，优伶数十辈争媚幕中人，有亚禄者，年已冠，虽色艺未衰，而人皆嬖宠幼稚，禄竞无颜，郁郁不得志。叟劝章生厚结之，章生虽非所好，因叟意，特爱怜之。自愧缠头不丰，而亚禄时有请乞，姑漫应之。方无所设措，而探诸床头，则必随意得物，以与亚禄。禄竟与诸稚伶争胜，有过之无不及。禄感章生甚，未几剧散，亚禄不知所往，而章生亦不置意。越三载，叟忽谓章生曰：“吾将告别，卜居贵乡西湖之隩。”章生曰：“典守者不得辞其责，印既无恙，奚可舍去？”叟曰：“印将舍吾，非吾离印。

    Explanation

    88

    At the time, the host was entertaining the gods with a performance, and dozens of actors competed to flatter those in the audience. Among them was one named Yalu, who, though already an adult, was still attractive and skilled. However, everyone favored the younger and more innocent performers, leaving Yalu feeling embarrassed and frustrated, unfulfilled in his ambitions. An old man advised Zhang Sheng to treat Yalu well. Although Zhang Sheng was not particularly fond of him, he agreed to the old man’s suggestion and showed special care for Yalu. He felt ashamed that his tips were not generous, yet Yalu often made requests, so he reluctantly complied. When he had no idea what to do, he would look around his bedside and find something to give to Yalu. Yalu, in turn, competed with the other young performers, often surpassing them. Yalu was deeply grateful to Zhang Sheng. Soon after, the performance ended, and Yalu disappeared, while Zhang Sheng paid little attention. Three years later, the old man suddenly told Zhang Sheng, “I am about to leave and choose a place to live in the secluded area of West Lake in the noble district.” Zhang Sheng replied, “As the custodian, I cannot shirk my responsibility. The seal is fine, why should I abandon it?” The old man said, “The seal will leave me, not that I am leaving the seal.”

    89

    ”章生讶曰：“何谓也？”叟曰：“足下日暮途穷，不思归故乡，而追问他人闲事？”章生曰：“予非不愿归，奈官场习惯，觉家乡之饮食起居，皆不能如意。迟迟吾行，职是故也。”叟叹曰：“数耶，数耶，老朽竟无可如何耶？今有一物相赠，务必随带勿离，亦可幸免。”飞一纸下，章生拾视，乃绍兴客之贩酒票也。章生莫知所以，以敬叟故，作囊佩诸内体，因请叟一显其形，以志别，叟诺。次夜，设果席于楼上，遣从闭户，闻叟唤入，则睹一老者，圆面大耳，碧眼方瞳，髯发皓白，着古衣冠，笑容相迎。于是举杯欢饮，章生醉卧而叟逝矣。

    Explanation

    89

    "Zhang Sheng was astonished and asked, 'What do you mean?' The old man replied, 'You are at the end of your journey, yet you do not think of returning to your hometown, and instead ask about others' trivial matters?' Zhang Sheng said, 'I am not unwilling to return, but the customs of the bureaucracy make me feel that the daily life and routines of my hometown cannot be to my liking. I have delayed my return, for this very reason.' The old man sighed, 'Is it fate? Is it fate? Even an old man like me has no recourse? Now I have something to give you; you must carry it with you and not lose it, and it may bring you good fortune.' A piece of paper flew down, and Zhang Sheng picked it up and saw that it was a ticket for wine sold by a guest from Shaoxing. Zhang Sheng did not know what to make of it, but out of respect for the old man, he put it in a pouch and wore it close to his body. He then asked the old man to show his true form as a parting gift, and the old man agreed. The next night, a fruit banquet was set up on the upper floor, and someone was sent to close the door. When the old man called him in, Zhang Sheng saw an old man with a round face and large ears, blue eyes with square pupils, a full beard and white hair, wearing ancient clothing, and smiling warmly. They then raised their cups and drank joyfully. Zhang Sheng fell asleep drunk, and the old man passed away."

    90

    未几林逆起事，攻城将殆，章生易厮役之衣履，随难中人遁，被林之党匪擒得，献俘堂下。林审之曰：“汝非土著，必官之亲故，伪装而逃者。”章生闻道破其情，陡然失色，一词莫措。林叱斩之，左右应声如雷，方欲趋缚，忽林逆后有俊俏后生急步下观，半跪启林曰：“勿斩好人，此章长者，小人素识，系浙产而在台贸易者，被官负其资，本困守于此，不识大王天威，故吓禁不能言耳。”林命搜其身，左右索得酒票一纸以献，林曰：“是沽客也。宥之，命截其发，以随吾军。”于是章生得命，为卒伍之厮养，不能自脱。

    Explanation

    90

    Not long after, Lin, the rebel leader, rose up in rebellion, attacking the city to the point of near destruction. Zhang, disguised in the clothes and shoes of a low-ranking servant, fled with others in the midst of the chaos, but was captured by Lin's followers and presented to Lin in the courtyard. Lin questioned him, saying, "You are not a native; you must be a relative or acquaintance of an official, having disguised yourself to escape." Upon hearing this, Zhang's true identity was revealed, and he suddenly turned pale, unable to respond. Lin ordered his execution, and those around him responded with a sound like thunder. Just as they were about to bind him, a handsome young man suddenly hurried down from behind Lin, half-kneeling to address him: "Do not execute this good man. This is Zhang, the elder, whom I have known for a long time. He is from Zhejiang and a trader in Taiwan. He was owed money by an official and was originally trapped here, unaware of Your Majesty's divine authority, hence he was too scared to speak." Lin ordered a search of Zhang's body, and those around him found a receipt for alcohol and presented it to Lin. Lin said, "He is a buyer of alcohol. Forgive him, and order his hair to be cut, so that he may follow my army." Thus, Zhang received his order, becoming a servant to the soldiers, unable to escape on his own.

    91

    数月，忽闻传呼曰：“亚将军阅兵至。”卒伍皆跪迎，章亦随跪，见骑从如云，马上一年少将军，戎装而妩媚。见章生，叱从人缚去。章又惶急将死，至辕门，掷而入。将军叱退从人，免胄而下，曰：“章先生识亚禄否？禄受先生惠，欲图报者久矣。今两遇先生，始得伸禄之愿，亦属天幸，请先生居禄帐下，觅便遣送。”章乃询禄何以至是，曰：“禄与林王有旧，别后相招，追随左右，于今三年，颇邀宠眷。”章以林必无成，劝禄同逸。禄曰：“是亦知之，但林王顾我厚，不忍舍耳。”未几拔章为亲军，与之旅资，遣赴闽城为细作，遂得渡台而归。

    Explanation

    91

    Several months later, Zhang suddenly heard a call: "General Yalu is here for the military inspection." All the soldiers knelt to welcome him, and Zhang also knelt. He saw a cloud of followers, and on a horse was a young general, clad in military attire but with a graceful demeanor. Upon seeing Zhang, the general ordered his attendants to bind him. Zhang was so terrified that he nearly fainted. They brought him to the gate and threw him inside. The general then ordered his attendants to leave, removed his helmet, and descended, saying, "Do you know Yalu, Zhang? Yalu has been grateful to you for a long time and has wanted to repay you. Now that I have met you twice, I can finally fulfill my wish, which is also a matter of fate. I ask you to stay under my command, and I will find a way to send you home." Zhang then asked Yalu how he came to be there. Yalu replied, "I have known Lin Wang for a long time. After we parted, he invited me to follow him, and I have been with him for three years, enjoying his favor." Zhang believed that Lin would not succeed and advised Yalu to escape. Yalu said, "I know that, but Lin Wang has treated me well, and I cannot bear to leave him." Soon, Yalu appointed Zhang as a personal guard, gave him travel funds, and sent him to Fujian City as a spy. Thus, he managed to cross the sea and return home.

    92

    芗厈曰：章生之所交者，狐也，兔也，皆兽其体而人其心，故藉以免于大难。吾不知世之与人其体兽其心者相处，不必假林逆之刀而杀之矣。噫，交游者其慎诸。
    　　
    　　磁州地震记
    　　
    　　维道光岁在庚寅，闰四月二十有二日戌刻，磁之人或甫晚餐，或已宴息，忽大声雷吼，从东南来，莫测其自天自地，如人在鼓中，逢逢四击。方骇愕间，有若千军涌溃，万马奔腾，而地皆震荡矣。人咸争先恐后，扶老携幼，走避空旷之区。亦如驾轻舟、涉江海而遇飓风，上下簸扬浮沉。倏忽俄顷间，屋字倾颓，砖瓦雨下，木石飘舞，飞灰蔽空。惟闻男嚎女啼，呼父母唤妻孥之声，与夫牛马惊嘶，鸡犬叫号，喧哗嘈杂，莫辨谁何。夜半稍息，复哀声四起，相传覆屋之内、颓垣之下，裂首破腹、折骨残支者比比皆是。以是内外抢呼，遐迩悲恸也。黎明，睹城郭庙宇及官私房舍，无一存者。

    Explanation

    92

    Xiang'an said: The people with whom Zhang Sheng associated were foxes and rabbits, all of whom had animal bodies but human minds, and thus relied on this to avoid great calamity. I do not know that in the world, those who associate with people who have human bodies but animal minds, do not need to rely on the sword of Lin Nv to kill them. Alas, those who associate should be cautious.

    Magnetic State Earthquake Record

    In the year of Gengyin during the Daoguang era, on the twenty-second day of the fourth month of the leap year, at the hour of Xu, the people of Cizhou were either just having dinner or had already retired to rest when suddenly a loud thunderclap came from the southeast, and it was impossible to tell whether it came from the sky or the earth. It was like being inside a drum, being struck four times. Just as they were in a state of shock, it was as if thousands of troops were breaking through, ten thousand horses were galloping, and the ground was shaking. People rushed out in a panic, supporting the elderly and carrying the young, fleeing to open areas. It was also like being in a small boat crossing rivers and seas and encountering a hurricane, being tossed up and down. In an instant, houses collapsed, bricks and tiles fell like rain, wood and stones flew, and dust and ashes blocked the sky. Only the cries of men and women, calling out to their parents and children, and the neighing of cattle and horses, the barking of chickens and dogs, the clamor and confusion, could not be distinguished. At midnight, it slightly subsided, but then sorrowful cries arose again, and it was said that inside the collapsed houses and under the ruined walls, those with broken heads and bodies, fractured bones and limbs, were everywhere. Therefore, both inside and outside, there were cries of distress, and everywhere, there was grief and sorrow. At dawn, looking at the city walls, temples, and official and private buildings, none were left standing.

    93

    地多坼裂，方圆长阔寻丈不等，均涌黑水、挟细砂，泛滥于道，而井泉反涸。于是山陵分崩，河渠翻凸，桥梁尽折，茔墓皆平，村庄道路不复可辨。二十三日戌刻，复大动，人皆野处，依树为栖。树拔则人物佥滚，男妇互撞，衣裳颠倒，疏戚溷淆。惟有架席作庐，掘地为灶，聊以食息，然而骨肉莫能顾，朝夕不相保，凄凄戚戚，惛惛懞懞，无复人寰气象矣。旬月间，犹或时动时止，其地蹈之皆作空声，甚有软如绵浮如沙者，其人则心胆俱碎，面目尽黑，稍一动摇，无不相抱恸泣，俯伏待毙。所谓民不聊生者，莫此为甚。钦惟圣天子视民如伤，恩纶叠沛，恤死赈生，葺城建宅，而群黎于是乎大定。惟坤土坚刚之气未复，间或震动，于今三年云。
    　　
    　　黄湘筠云：笔如环转，备极形容，披读一过，宛如目睹情形，使我心胆俱碎，所谓绘风有色，绘水有声者。

    Explanation

    93

    The earth frequently cracks, in varying sizes from several feet to several yards, all spewing black water that carries fine sand, flooding the roads while wells and springs dry up. Consequently, mountains and hills collapse, rivers and canals become uneven, bridges all break, tombs are flattened, and villages and roads can no longer be distinguished. On the twenty-third day at sunset, there was another major upheaval; people all lived in the wild, using trees as shelter. When trees were uprooted, people and objects rolled together, men and women collided, clothes turned inside out, and distant and close relatives were mixed together. They could only build shelters with mats and dig holes for cooking, just to eat and rest, yet they could not even look after family members, uncertain about survival from morning to night, gloomy and sorrowful, dazed and confused, with no longer any human world atmosphere. For weeks or months, it still moved and stopped intermittently; when stepped on, the ground made an empty sound, some parts being soft like cotton and floating like sand. The people had both heart and courage shattered, faces blackened, and even a slight movement caused everyone to embrace and weep, lying down waiting to die. Such dire living conditions were most severe. The emperor views the people as his own, bestowing many favors, comforting the dead and helping the living, rebuilding cities and houses, thus greatly stabilizing the common people. However, the earth's firm and strong energy has not yet returned, with occasional tremors lasting for three years.

    As Huang Xiangyun commented: "The writing is like a continuous loop, vividly describing everything, so that after reading it once, one feels as if they have witnessed the scene, shattering my heart and courage, which is what is meant by painting wind with color and painting water with sound."

    94

    神童
    　　
    　　神童某，不知何许人。其父为鹾商伙，负商千金，家贫无偿，托疾卧。商频来索，不过缓延而已。然积久难复，郁郁真病矣。神童时年七岁，母卒，仅依其父。每见父忧愁郁结，童亦咄咄书空，忽大笑曰：“阿翁无忧，儿有法退此债，且能使之助我家。”其父曰：“童子何知，大言不惭？”童曰：“无法可施之际，请尝试之，如其不效，不过儿戏而已，人不深责也。请与我床头钱一贯以图之，其法效，方敢告翁知也。”其父因爱怜故，姑听之。童携钱去，邻舍有为优者，予之钱曰：“后日汝装魁星像，潜我房中，有客推门，则一显其形，隐我身畔。客去，则我遣汝卸装归。”优诺之。童谓其父曰：“后日商主来，翁告以昨遣儿告贷亲友，有允诺者，不知何日送来，应问儿。翁即呼儿。儿高声读，若不闻也者。商必亲过儿舍，则遣之之法行矣。”

    Explanation

    94

    There was a child prodigy, whose origins are unknown. His father was a partner in a salt merchant's business, and he owed the merchant a thousand gold pieces. Due to poverty, he was unable to repay the debt and pretended to be ill, lying down to avoid repayment. The merchant frequently came to demand payment, but the father only delayed him. However, after a long time, the father became truly ill from the stress. The child prodigy was seven years old at the time, and his mother had recently passed away, leaving him to depend solely on his father. Whenever he saw his father's distress, the child would gesture in the air, and then suddenly burst out laughing, saying, "Father, do not worry. I have a way to repay this debt, and it will even help our family." The father replied, "How could a child know such things? You are speaking grandly without shame!" The child said, "If there is no other way, please let me try. If it does not work, it is just a child's play, and no one will blame you too much. Please give me one string of coins from the bedhead to attempt it. If the method works, I will then tell you, Father." The father, moved by his love and pity, reluctantly agreed. The child took the money and went to a neighbor who was a performer. He gave the performer money and said, "Tomorrow, you will dress up as the God of Literature and hide in my room. When there is a guest who knocks on the door, you will show your form, and I will hide by your side. When the guest leaves, I will send you to undress and return." The performer agreed. The child then told his father, "Tomorrow, when the merchant comes, Father, you should tell him that I was sent to borrow from relatives and friends yesterday, and someone has promised to send it, but I do not know when it will arrive. You should ask me. When the merchant comes, you should call for me. I will read aloud as if I do not hear him. The merchant will surely come to my house, and then the method of dismissing him will be carried out."

    95

    后日，商果来，其父一如儿言，大声呼儿不应，书声朗朗读益健。商笑曰：“肯读书，好儿子。我往见之。”至儿舍，甫推门，则有青面獠牙，虎裳绣甲之神，执斗笔当门而立，神光照耀。商大惊而退，思此童室有魁星，后必大发，欲以女妻之。乃谓其父曰：“令郎非凡品也，我不敢动扰，曷请来一见？”父又呼之，童始应声出。商见其貌既英俊，执礼甚恭，凡应对进退，无不动人敬爱者。商大悦，乃曰：“足下有宁馨儿，不患无振兴之日。我愿与结婚姻，肯为我婿，不但债可免，且亲翁养赡与令郎读书之资，我为备具。”其父喜出望外，扶床起拜，童亦拜。商欢喜而去。翌日遣媒纳彩，商即邀其父子去，重聘延名师，使童入学，而父仍司会计。于是神童之名退迩传布，采访使者闻之，贡入太学，后果大魁天下。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：七岁儿能如是乎，已具元魁之才。彼大腹贾，直为其气焰所取，非优伶之能为力也。

    Explanation

    95

    The next day, the merchant indeed arrived. The father followed the child's instructions exactly, calling out loudly for the child, but there was no response. Instead, the sound of reading aloud grew even more vigorous. The merchant smiled and said, "If he is willing to read, he is a good son. I will go to see him." Upon arriving at the child's room, as soon as he pushed open the door, he saw a fearsome figure with a green face, fang-like teeth, and a tiger-patterned robe and embroidered armor, holding a brush in front of the door. The divine light shone brightly. The merchant was greatly shocked and retreated. He thought that this child's room had the star of the top scholar and that the child would surely become great in the future. He wished to marry his daughter to the child. Turning to the father, he said, "Your son is no ordinary person. I dare not disturb him, but why don't you invite him to meet me?" The father called out again, and the child finally responded. The merchant saw that the child was handsome and observed etiquette with great respect. In all his responses and actions, nothing failed to move people to admiration and affection. The merchant was greatly pleased and said, "You have a fine son, and you need not worry about the day of revival. I wish to form a marital alliance with you. If he is willing to be my son-in-law, not only can the debt be forgiven, but I will also provide the expenses for supporting your father-in-law and funding your son's education." The father was overjoyed beyond expectation, helping the child up from the bed and bowing. The child also bowed. The merchant left happily. The next day, he sent a matchmaker with a dowry, and the merchant immediately invited the father and son to come. He offered a generous dowry and invited a distinguished teacher, sending the child to school while the father still managed the finances. Thus, the reputation of the child prodigy spread far and wide. An envoy for investigation heard about it and offered the child to the Imperial Academy. As expected, he eventually became the top scholar in the empire.

    Xiang Yan remarked: Can a seven-year-old be like this? He already possesses the talent of the top scholar. That wealthy merchant is directly taken by his influence, not something that actors can achieve.

    96

    时医
    　　
    　　吴某，槜李郡人也，业医兼设药肆。其邑宰有爱女，膺感冒之疾，延之诊视，用防风散服之，卒。宰怒，命缚治之，幸素识吏，知风而遁，越境乃免，依妻父家。逾岁，宰迁他邑，遂携眷归。妻弟送之，复整旧业。其邻里乡党集金贺之，开宴招饮，时已昏暮，客皆据席哄饮，兴正豪，有叩门求痧子药者。主人不得闲，嘱其妻弟曰：“笥内几隔几瓶，红色者是，汝往给之。”其妻弟酒已醺，随手撤一瓶，开视皆红面，包与其人而入。宴毕客散，吴医捡点器具，见一瓶在外，所盛者信石末也。问何人动此，其妻弟骇曰，“我以为痧子药，给来人矣。”医愕然曰：“来者何人，与药几许？”曰：“其人似营伍中者，得钱十余文，与药二三钱也。”医曰：“殆矣，我之不得业是者命矣。夫明日必兴大狱，身家其能保乎？”其妻弟曰：“无已，姑乘夜潜逃，以观其变。”乃嘱其姊居守，偕医乃遁。

    Explanation

    96

    The Physician of the Time

    Wu, a man from Zui Li County, practiced medicine and also ran a pharmacy. The magistrate of his county had a beloved daughter who fell ill with a cold and summoned Wu to diagnose her. Wu prescribed Fangfeng San for her to take, but she died as a result. The magistrate was furious and ordered Wu to be bound and punished. Fortunately, Wu had known the official before and, sensing the danger, fled. He crossed the county border and escaped, then took refuge with his father-in-law's family.

    After a year, the magistrate was transferred to another county, and Wu was able to return home with his family. His sister-in-law saw him off, and he resumed his former profession. His neighbors and fellow villagers collected money to congratulate him, and they held a banquet in his honor. By then, it was already late in the evening, and the guests were all seated, drinking merrily and in high spirits. Suddenly, someone knocked on the door, asking for shazǐ medicine.

    The host was too busy to attend, so he instructed his sister-in-law: "Inside the chest, there are several rows of bottles. The red ones are the correct ones. Go and give one to the person." His sister-in-law was already somewhat drunk and, without thinking, took a bottle at random. When she opened it, she saw that it was red. She wrapped it up and gave it to the visitor, then returned inside.

    After the banquet, when the guests had all left, Wu checked the utensils and noticed that one bottle was missing. He saw it outside, and upon inspecting it, realized that it contained xingshi powder. He asked who had taken it, and his sister-in-law, startled, said, "I thought it was shazǐ medicine and gave it to the person who came."

    Wu was stunned and asked, "Who was the person, and how much medicine did you give him?" She replied, "He looked like a soldier, and he gave me more than ten coins for two or three measures of medicine."

    Wu said, "This is doomed. My fate is sealed—my life as a physician is over. Tomorrow, a great legal case will be launched, and can my life or family be safe?" His sister-in-law said, "If there is no other choice, let us flee tonight and see what happens." So she entrusted her sister to stay behind, and together with Wu, they fled into the night.

    97

    当是时，有都督某大将军驻是邑，得眩疾，发即晕绝，惟以痧药灌之，周时斯醒。忽疾作将毙，其夫人命卒求药，卒因吴医新设铺药，必认真，故买之而归。夫人莫辨，急以水调药末灌之，大将军腹中如雷鸣，须臾起坐，大呼，“妙药，妙药！”夫人告以故，呼卒入询，得其处，曰：“良医也，差弁往聘之，以除吾疾。”翌日命中军参将率其卒伍，奉袍服冠履，白金五十，往延先生。中军至，则药肆已闭。叩门不应，唤两邻问之，佥曰：“昨甫开张，今日又闭，必近乡之人延去治疗也。然其妻在，我等往后户问明，以应台命。”中军颔之。邻人疾呼吴嫂。

    Explanation

    97

    At that time, there was a certain General stationed in the city who had a dizzy illness. Whenever it flared up, he would faint and lose consciousness, and could only be revived by administering sha medicine. After a period of time, he would awaken. Suddenly, the illness flared up again, and he was on the verge of death. His wife ordered the soldiers to seek medicine. The soldiers, because the Wu physician had just set up his shop and was very careful, bought the medicine and returned. The wife, unfamiliar with the medicine, hurriedly mixed the powdered medicine with water and administered it to the General. The General's abdomen made a sound like thunder, and in a moment, he sat up, exclaiming, "Wonderful medicine! Wonderful medicine!" The wife explained the reason, and the General called the soldiers in to ask about it. They found out where the physician was and said, "He is a good doctor. Send a messenger to invite him to treat my illness." The next day, the General ordered the middle-ranking general to lead his troops, carrying official robes, hats, and shoes, and fifty taels of silver, to invite the master physician. When the middle-ranking general arrived, the pharmacy was already closed. He knocked on the door but no one answered. He called two neighbors to ask, and they all said, "It opened yesterday and closed today. It must be that someone from the nearby area has invited him to treat an illness. But his wife is still there. We will go to the back door and find out clearly to respond to your command." The middle-ranking general nodded in agreement. The neighbors loudly called out to Mrs. Wu.

    98

    其妻因弟误其夫，惴惴尤栗，初闻门外传呼声，潜窥之，见马上长官同健卒十余人盘诘邻人，知夜来事发矣，惶恐饮泣，何敢应门。邻老不得已，越墙而语之曰：“官奉有礼仪，必无恶意。嫂姑纳之，无祸及妇人者。”其妻不得已启门，中军入室，陈其聘物，笑问，“先生何往？吾奉军门令，其速赴召也。”妻心始安，以下乡对。中军呼里长入，使往寻之。邻老作函招吴医回，往见大将军，开闾廷入，怡然请教。医诊之，实不知其疾之由来，谬以虚寒对云，“以葠苓、桂附为丸，厥疾可瘳。”大将军诺诺，遽以百金与之曰：“聊为药资，有不敷者，痊日重酬。”

    Explanation

    98

    The wife, due to her brother's mistake, was wronged by her husband, and she was filled with extreme fear and trembling. When she first heard the calls outside the door, she secretly peered out and saw the official on horseback accompanied by more than ten strong soldiers interrogating the neighbors. She realized that the matter from last night had come to light, and she was terrified, weeping silently, unsure how to respond. The elderly neighbor, unable to help otherwise, climbed over the wall and spoke to her, saying, "The officials are following proper protocol; they have no ill intentions. Please let them in, and there will be no harm to women." The wife, reluctantly, opened the door. The middle officer entered the room, presented his gifts, and asked with a smile, "Where is the gentleman going? I am here on the orders of the general, and he must come quickly." The wife's heart began to calm, and she answered, "He is in the village." The middle officer then called the village headman in, instructing him to go and find the man. The elderly neighbor wrote a letter to summon the doctor from Wu back, and the doctor went to see the great general. The general opened the gate and entered, pleased to seek advice. The doctor examined him but actually did not know the cause of the illness. He falsely claimed that the illness was due to cold deficiency and said, "Take ginseng, poria, cinnamon, and aconite to make pills, and the illness can be cured." The general nodded, immediately gave him a hundred gold pieces, and said, "This is just as a payment for the medicine. If it is not enough, I will pay again when he recovers."

    99

    医唯唯而退，谓其妻曰：“愈疾以信，既重且多，天下之大奇事也，然不可再。其将何药以应命耶？”其妻曰：“曷不延其亲随之人，而探其疾源。”医大悟，以酒食交欢于随官，始悉大将军以卒伍起家，方其戍守边微时，冬无裘衣，朝夕以酒御寒。酒家用信石入酿，饮之遍体遽暖，故寒士多从之。及大将军贵，开府南方，无此等恶劣酒，随成眩疾。医会其意，以滋补之味参以信石作引，为丸以进。终一料而体竟矍然，疾不复发。大将军深感之，凡所辖四营八哨九十余汛，属下将弁无论男女有疾，必使延吴先生，愈则强之重酬，若不瘳则曰：“吴先生尚不能治，其命尽矣。”于是造请者盈门，不数年成巨业，起大宅，自为门联曰：“运退防风杀命，时来信石活人。”

    Explanation

    99

    The doctor bowed and withdrew, saying to his wife, "To cure a disease through trust, it is both highly valued and numerous, a great marvel in the world, but it cannot be repeated. What medicine will he use to fulfill the order?" His wife replied, "Why not invite his close attendant to investigate the root cause of the disease?" The doctor had a great realization and entertained the attendant with wine and food. He then learned that the great general rose from the ranks of common soldiers, when he was guarding the frontier in his humble position, he had no fur clothing in winter and drank wine day and night to keep warm. The tavern owner added arsenic to the wine, and when he drank it, his entire body warmed up immediately, so many poor scholars and soldiers followed suit. When the great general became prominent and established his headquarters in the south, there were no such inferior wines, and as a result, he developed dizziness. The doctor understood the situation and mixed tonic flavors with arsenic as a base, making pills to administer. After finishing the course, his body suddenly regained vigor, and the disease did not recur. The great general was deeply grateful and, for all the subordinates under his command, including the four camps, eight outposts, and more than ninety garrisons, if they were ill regardless of gender, he would definitely send for Mr. Wu. If cured, he would insist on a generous reward; if not cured, he would say, "Mr. Wu cannot treat it, their life is over." Thus, visitors seeking treatment filled the door, and within a few years, he became wealthy, building a large mansion and writing a couplet for the gate: "When fortune wanes, fang-feng (a herb) can be deadly; when times change, xin-shi (arsenic) can save lives."

    100

    芗厈曰：胜者所用，即败者之兵。惟其时而已矣。故当世医无定评，忽贤忽不肖，有幸有不幸，畴能入岐黄之奥，而使苍生无夭札耶？噫，抱恙者实可危矣。
    　　
    　　神尼
    　　
    　　仆妇粱氏，江右人。夫早故，仅遗一子，衣食无资，送庙为僧。而身为巨室佣。是邦风俗，凡附近僧尼庙宇者，日给以饭，月施之米。梁氏之在其主家也，命与布施，升斗恒满，故僧尼皆德之。粱氏年逾六旬，双目瞽，主家资而遣之。怅怅无依，有观音堂之老尼者，素受其满施，频念之，今既见为逐妇，怜而收养，为之祝发，相依度日。未几老尼卒，其徒主持庙事，嫌瞽尼坐食，驱令他适。又无所依皈，哀泣于途，父老见而怜之。

    Explanation

    100

    Xiang Yan said: The weapons used by the victors are those abandoned by the defeated. It all depends on the timing. Therefore, in the present day, doctors have no fixed standard of evaluation; sometimes they are praised, sometimes they are criticized; sometimes they are fortunate, sometimes they are unfortunate. Who can master the profound mysteries of the Yellow Emperor and Qi Bo, and thus ensure that the common people do not suffer premature death? Alas, those who are ill are indeed in a dangerous situation.

    Divine Nun

    Liang, a woman from Western Jiang, whose husband died early, leaving her with only one son. They had no means of support, so he was sent to a temple to become a monk. She worked as a servant in a wealthy household. In this region, it is customary for households near temples to provide daily meals and monthly rice. While working in her master's house, she was given alms, and her contributions were always full, so the monks and nuns at the temple were grateful to her. Liang was over sixty years old and blind. Her master provided for her and sent her away. She felt desolate and had nowhere to go. There was an old nun at Guanyin Hall who had always received her full alms and often thought of her. Now that she saw Liang being expelled, she felt pity and took her in, making her take vows and shave her head, and they lived together. Soon after, the old nun died, and her disciple took over the temple affairs. The disciple disliked the blind nun for living off others and drove her away. Liang had nowhere to go and wept along the way. The elders saw her and felt pity for her.

    101

    时值有大五圣堂者，为恶丐所据，肆为小窃，扰居民，比户不得安。父老与绅士谋，俾瞽尼居是，藉以逐丐。是方之人，日施之食，尼于是始得安居。昕夕诵佛号，足迹不出外者十年，双目忽明，心胸了悟。始一游故主家，为陈因果，主家见其盲而复明，咸称异之，不独邻里亲串愿见，即遐迩闻之，莫不欲识其面者。乃与人言祸福，无不立应。于是五圣堂香火大盛，自命妇以及小家子女佥尊之曰“神师”，施舍无算。即其庙地创建大士阁，魏焕宏丽，士大夫亦来瞻仰问道，而尼但以诵佛为说。其子僧亦皈依其母，为之执爨灌器，殷勤奉侍。

    Explanation

    101

    During a certain period, there was a significant temple known as the Five Saints Temple, which was occupied by evil beggars who engaged in petty theft, disturbing the residents and making no household able to find peace. The elders and gentry plotted together to have a blind nun reside there, using her presence to drive out the beggars. The people of the area provided her with daily food, and thus she was able to find a place to live. She recited Buddhist mantras morning and evening, not stepping outside for ten years. Suddenly, her eyes became clear, and her mind and heart were enlightened. She first visited her former master's home, explaining the causes and effects of her transformation. The master's family saw her go from blind to seeing and all called it strange. Not only did neighbors and relatives wish to see her, but even those far and near who heard of her, all wished to meet her. She would speak of good and bad fortune with people, and all would immediately come true. Thus, the Five Saints Temple became very popular, and from noblewomen to children of ordinary families all revered her as a "spiritual teacher," receiving countless offerings. On the temple grounds, they built a Bodhisattva Pavilion, which was splendid and magnificent. Scholars and officials also came to pay respects and ask questions, but the nun only spoke of reciting Buddhist mantras. Her son, a monk, also took refuge in her, preparing meals and serving her with great care.

    102

    又十余年，寿八旬外，忽谓其子曰：“吾将西归，在此二十年，搅扰众居士，须有以鸣谢。期某月日，齐集阁前拜别也。”子诺，传布揭帖。至日来观神尼化升者，男妇不计其数。尼易新衣冠，参拜神佛天地四方，举手谢时，人似有所言，其时人声嘈杂，不能听辨。尼乃至阁前，以蒲团当陛趺坐，命其子进笔砚，以大幅硬黄纸布其前，运笔大书偈子一十八首，命粘于墙，以示大众。居然字画分明，多成句读，且协古韵。人皆知其向不识字，今作是举动，拜者崩角。尼乃执香朗宣佛号千声，香火及手，内外烈焰大作，顷刻坐化，异香匝地。大众泣拜，助其子聚骨而为之塔，士大夫铭之，至今存也。

    Explanation

    102

    After another ten years, when she was over eighty, the divine nun suddenly told her son, "I shall return to the west. Having stayed here for twenty years and caused much disturbance to the lay practitioners, I must find a way to express my gratitude. On a certain date, gather at the front of the hall to bid me farewell." The son agreed, and the announcement was widely circulated. On the appointed day, countless men and women came to witness the divine nun's miraculous ascension. The nun changed into new attire, paid homage to the deities, heaven, earth, and the four directions, and raised her hand to thank them. At that moment, it seemed as though she was saying something, though the crowd was so noisy that it was impossible to hear. She then went to the front of the hall, sat cross-legged on a mat as if on a throne, and ordered her son to bring writing implements. She placed a large sheet of yellow paper in front of her, took up her brush, and wrote eighteen verses in bold script. She instructed that they be pasted on the wall to be shown to the public. The characters were clearly legible, with proper punctuation, and harmonized with ancient rhythms. Everyone knew that she had never learned to write before, yet now she performed this remarkable act. Those who bowed fell to their knees in reverence. The nun then took incense and loudly recited the Buddha's name a thousand times. As the incense reached her hands, flames erupted inside and out. In an instant, she attained nirvana, and a fragrant aroma filled the air. The crowd wept and bowed, helping her son gather her remains and build a stupa in her honor. The scholars and officials inscribed her deeds, and her legacy still endures to this day.

    103

    或问曰：“奇哉，不读书而识字，已属难信，况能作韵语，其为荒诞也明矣。”芗序曰：“是不奇，吾子未之思耳，昔创字者苍颉，本是凭空结撰，至童谣葩经，更无传授，遂成千古绝调。可见人心本明，愚夫妇悉具圣贤之理。晦翁云：但为气禀所拘，物欲所蔽，则有时而昏然。其本体之明，则有未尝息者。故学者当因其所发而遂明之，以复其初也。老氏曰：人能常清静，天地悉皆归，岂仅识字能作诗而已？此尼二十年心无沾染，自然灵明来复，洞见本真十八首偈子，不过道其所道，何奇之有？”

    Explanation

    103

    Someone asked, "How remarkable! Not reading but recognizing characters is already hard to believe, let alone being able to compose rhymed poetry; it is clearly absurd." Xiang said, "This is not remarkable; you have not thought about it. The legendary creator of characters, Cangjie, was originally creating them out of nothing. As for children's rhymes and the Book of Songs, there was no formal transmission, yet they became timeless masterpieces. It can be seen that the human mind is inherently clear, and even ordinary people possess the wisdom of sages. Zhu Xi said: 'Though limited by nature and obscured by desires, the mind may sometimes be clouded. Yet the inherent clarity of the mind never ceases. Therefore, scholars should follow what is revealed and make it clear, to return to its original state.' Laozi said: 'If one can always remain calm and quiet, all things in heaven and earth will return to them. How could it be merely about recognizing characters and composing poetry?' This person, who has remained pure and untainted for twenty years, has seen their natural clarity return, and has composed eighteen verses that simply express what they express. What is there to find remarkable?"

    104

    第三卷
    　　
    　　魏元虚
    　　
    　　魏静，字元虚，江左诸生。游燕赵间，以记室谋食。寓保阳之古大士庵，间舍三楹，独居无偶。时已旅食半载，行李萧条，困顿之状，亲故白眼。值中秋节，月光皎洁，扃户出游，冀图一醉。行数家，咸辞以故。漏已三下，枵腹而回。入其寓，卧室之关已启，燃烛辉煌。魏疑居停逐之以延他客也，忿然入室，瞥见艳女披服鲜华，据榻而坐。见元虚，笑立相迎，魏愕然退步曰：“伊谁宅眷，此仆之卧榻，实僧舍也。

    Explanation

    104

    Chapter Three

    　　Wei Yuanxu

    　　Wei Jing, styled Yuanxu, was a scholar from the south. He traveled between Yan and Zhao, making a living as a secretary. He stayed at the ancient Great Master Temple in Baoyang, where he lived alone in a small house with three rooms. By then, he had been traveling and living in poverty for half a year, his belongings sparse, and he was treated with contempt by relatives and friends. Coinciding with the Mid-Autumn Festival, the moonlight was bright and clear. He closed the door and went out for a walk, hoping to get drunk. He visited several households, all of which refused due to various reasons. The water clock struck three times, indicating it was late at night, and he returned with an empty stomach. Upon entering his lodging, he found the door to his bedroom already open, with candles lit and shining brightly. Wei suspected that the host had kicked him out to make room for other guests, and he angrily entered the room. He suddenly saw a beautiful woman dressed in vibrant attire, sitting on the bed. Seeing Wei, she smiled and stood up to greet him. Wei, startled, stepped back and said, "Who is this person staying here? This is my bed, actually in a temple."

    105

    讵可误入？”女微哂曰：“亲朋拒子而无礼，妾悯厄穷来慰寂寥，何误之有？实告子勿惧，妾涂山氏之裔，前生藉子替劫，今已道成，欲图报耳。”魏闻益战果，女曰：“子何怯为，视妾岂祸子哉？”魏转念曰：“颠沛之人，即死于佳丽亦得。”随释然，问曰：“仙卿其将何以慰予？”女曰：“予亟阖外户，移席于月光之下，清谈雅酌，以舒子怀何如？”魏如命布设，女携一筐至中庭，无何，玉杯、象箸、珍馔、醇醪，皆探筐而出。女与魏对坐，举觞互酌，女曰：“妾系燕产，子前生为幽都少年，与妾有旧。

    Explanation

    105

    "How could it be mistaken?" the woman smiled faintly and said, "Your friends and relatives reject you without propriety, and I pity your plight, coming to comfort your loneliness. What could be wrong with that? I will tell you the truth, do not be afraid. I am a descendant of the Tu Shan clan. In my past life, I relied on you to take my place in the cycle of suffering. Now I have attained enlightenment, and I only wish to repay the favor." Hearing this, Wei became more resolute. The woman said, "Why are you so timid? Look at me, how could I harm you?" Wei then thought, "Even if I die, it is acceptable." He felt relieved and asked, "What will you do to comfort me?" The woman replied, "I will quickly close the outer door and move the table under the moonlight. We can engage in refined conversation and drink to soothe your heart. How does that sound?" Wei followed her instructions and arranged everything. The woman carried a basket to the courtyard, and soon, jade cups, ivory chopsticks, delicacies, and fine wine all emerged from the basket. The woman sat across from Wei, raised their cups, and took turns drinking. She said, "I was born in Yan, and in your past life, you were a young man from the northern capital. We had a past relationship."

    106

    同卧起时，值雷劫，妾避子身下而雷误杀子，妾得遁脱，入西山修炼二百余年，甫得正果。知子受困于此，来助子致身青云，以酬大德。”当其时，清辉朗照，睹女光彩焕发，冶容婉丽，国色无双。魏心动，微挑之曰：“仙卿即与仆有旧，愿修旧好，结两世之姻缘。”女曰：“不可。妾内丹告成，脱披毛之体，已得超凡，焉敢犯淫欲戒而堕落耶？”魏曰：“予前生以身替卿死尚甘心焉，卿为予一破戒乌得不可？”就逼之，女遥避曰：“不但于妾不利，将以盗子之元精而绝躯命，宁勿惧耶？”魏曰：“予观载籍，仙女下嫁者比比皆是，卿既成仙体，何损于予？幸勿深拒。

    Explanation

    106

    During the time when we slept and rose together, we encountered a thunderstorm. I sought shelter beneath the child, and the lightning mistakenly struck and killed him. I managed to escape and entered the Western Mountains for cultivation for over two hundred years, finally achieving the ultimate fruit. Knowing that the son was trapped here, I came to help him reach the heights of success, to repay the great virtue he showed me. At that time, the clear light shone brightly, and I saw the woman's radiance and vitality, her graceful and beautiful appearance, unparalleled in beauty. Wei's heart was moved, and he gently teased her, saying, "Divine lady, if you have an old acquaintance with me, I wish to renew our old friendship and form a marital bond for two lifetimes." The woman replied, "It is not possible. My internal alchemy is complete, and I have shed my animal form, already transcended the ordinary. How dare I violate the precept against lust and fall?" Wei said, "In my previous life, I willingly gave my life for you. You breaking the precept for me, how could it be wrong?" He approached her closely, and the woman stepped back and said, "Not only is it harmful to me, it will steal the son's primordial essence and end his life. Should I not fear?" Wei said, "I have seen in the records that there are many examples of fairy women marrying mortals. You have already become a fairy. How would it harm me? Please do not refuse deeply."

    107

    ”女曰：“好色者不在乎淫，子试思接狎之后，身颓气弱，情竭兴衰，有何意味？莫若缬其秀色，采其丰神，如对名花，可玩而不可亵，则意淡情长。况妾能与子清谈，作解语花观，不更胜耶？”魏不怿，直前抱之，觉有影无形，凛然而止。女笑曰：“痴人，仙体清虚，奚能满欲？即此可解仙女下嫁之诬也。”遂探筐出青精饭共食毕，敛器于筐，出朱提一笏，以授魏曰：“聊以资旅食备寒衣，后会有期，勿虚念也。”一转瞬间，女化清风去矣。魏若有所失，怅惘不已。

    Explanation

    107

    The woman said, "Those who love beauty do not indulge in debauchery. Reflect on the aftermath of intimate encounters: the body is weakened, the spirit diminished, emotions exhausted, and vitality wanes. What meaning or pleasure remains? It is better to appreciate her beauty, admire her graceful demeanor, like facing a famous flower, to admire but not to violate, thus the sentiment is mild but enduring. Moreover, I can engage in refined conversation with you, viewing me as a speaking flower, is this not more fulfilling?" Hui was displeased and rushed forward to embrace her, feeling as if there was a shadow but no substance, suddenly feeling a chill and stopping. The woman laughed and said, "Fool, the body of a fairy is pure and ethereal, how can it satisfy desire? This can dispel the false rumor that a fairy has descended to marry a mortal." She then reached into her basket and took out some sacred food to share with Hui. After they finished eating, she put the dishes back into the basket, took out a piece of red gold, and gave it to Hui, saying, "This is to provide for your travel expenses and winter clothing. We will meet again, do not have vain thoughts." In a moment, the woman transformed into a clear breeze and departed. Hui felt as if something was missing, feeling deeply melancholic and restless.

    108

    阅两月，资用告匮，正欷歔凝想间，女已遥立案前，低呼曰：“魏郎久阔，相思良苦。”魏惊喜相迎，责见疏之故。女曰：“为子筹御冬计，已足迹遍三辅矣。”出金置几上，皆细碎不成锭者。嘱曰：“以此卒岁，谅不致饥寒也。”魏揖谢曰：“屡蒙惠赐，感激难名。第予闻仙人致富不难，倘金穴可探，曷不偕予一游，益佩卿德？”女笑曰：“痴人，无论神仙，不当作贼。即地下藏镪，亦有主者。

    Explanation

    108

    After two months, his resources ran out, and as he sighed and pondered, the woman had already appeared at the desk, calling out softly: "Mr. Wei, it has been so long since we last met, and I miss you terribly." Mr. Wei was filled with surprise and joy, and he welcomed her, asking why they had not seen each other recently. The woman said, "I have been traveling throughout the Three Auxiliaries to plan for your winter." She then took out some gold and placed it on the table, all of which were small pieces that could not be formed into ingots. She instructed him, "Use this to make it through the year, and I am sure you will not suffer from hunger or cold." Mr. Wei bowed and thanked her, saying, "I have often received generous gifts from you, and I am deeply grateful. But I have heard that it is not difficult for immortals to become wealthy. If a gold mine can be explored, why not go with me on an adventure? I would appreciate your kindness even more." The woman replied with a smile, "Foolish person, whether or not one is an immortal, one should not steal. Even if there is gold hidden underground, it has an owner."

    109

    惟暴客劫掠之资与贪墨非义之财，乘其数目未明时，分润些微，斯不为造物所忌。安能暴富耶？且富者多累，今使子不饥不寒，不与神仙等耶？”魏曰：“是也，但予忝为丈夫，有室家而不能顾，有学术而不能展，仰赖仙卿，实抱惭衾影，后将若何？卿其为我决之。”女曰：“献岁后，子之命运亨矣。何忧一席地，显达随之，将膺一命。”魏曰：“何从而可？”女曰：“月正元日，子之旧居停来，已有位置，当入邢襄之幕，其署西有密室，子可托恶嚣之故而独居之，妾将为子谋入仕途也。”魏欲细诘之，女已倏忽不见。

    Explanation

    109

    Only the money obtained by robbers through plunder and the ill-gotten gains of corrupt officials, when shared in a small portion before the amounts are known, is not forbidden by the Creator. How could one suddenly become rich? Wealth often brings burdens. Now that you are not hungry or cold, is this not the same as being a god? Hui said, "Yes, but I am unworthy of being a man, having a family but unable to take care of it, having knowledge but unable to apply it. I rely on you, and I feel ashamed even in the presence of my own shadow. What will I do in the future? Please decide for me." The woman said, "After the New Year, your fortune will be favorable. There is no need to worry. Prominence and success will follow, and you will receive an official position. How can this be done?" The woman said, "On the first day of the first month, you will return to your old residence, where a position has already been arranged. You will enter the administrative office of the governor of Xingzhou. The office has a private room to the west, which you can claim as a reason for disliking noise and live in alone. I will help you enter the official career." Hui wanted to ask more questions, but the woman suddenly disappeared.

    110

    元旦，果有船居停访至，谓魏曰：“敬为先生推毂，今邢襄同寮某嘱迓文旌，希即命驾。”魏领诺，旋赴襄国。主人甚贤，惟先生命，果得密室居之。荏苒半载，盼女无音。夏之夜纳凉小院，瞥见天际微云下坠，女携囊橐而至，魏欢喜欲狂曰：“囊中何物，若是累累？”女曰：“子之终身在是。”魏急叩其说，女起而倾其囊，白镪盈几，笑谓魏曰：“子前欲致富，今且贵矣。

    Explanation

    110

    On the first day of the lunar New Year, a boat indeed arrived and stopped, and a visitor came to see Wei, saying, "I respectfully recommend the gentleman. A colleague from Xingxiang, named [someone], has entrusted me to invite you, please make haste and come." Wei accepted the invitation and immediately went to Xingxiang. The host was very virtuous, following only Wei's commands, and indeed provided him with a private room to stay in. Half a year passed, and Wei had no news of his daughter. On a summer night, while cooling off in a small courtyard, he saw a faint cloud descending from the sky. A woman carrying a bag arrived, and Wei was delighted to the point of ecstasy, asking, "What is in the bag, so much that it's piled up?" The woman said, "Your lifelong fortune is in here." Wei quickly asked about it, and the woman stood up and emptied the bag, revealing silver coins filling the table. She smiled and said to Wei, "You wanted to become wealthy before, now you are even more honored."

    111

    闻朝廷将开纳资之例，子数应得郡参军，而荐至令尹，俟命下之日，子作书借邮筒遍致旧好请助，必无应者，第伪称某某资益若干，以掩人耳目，则罗致有由，无突如之惑矣。冬仲得官荆湘间，妾在邯郸旅次相候。”魏不觉感深，膝曲跪拜，甫兴而女已杳矣。未几南河大工例开，魏如女言布置，是年冬十月，入都纳资，果得郡参军，分楚北，束装前往。至邯郸，忽遇美少年，策蹇追随，将投旅店，少年停骑问曰：“车中是魏郎否？家姊致命，请住大槐老店，在彼设饯也。”言毕，捷驰而去。

    Explanation

    111

    Hearing that the court is about to open a quota system, your son has been eligible for the position of military advisor in a county for some time, and has been recommended to the position of magistrate. On the day the order is issued, your son writes letters through the postal service to all his old friends asking for help, but there will certainly be no response. Instead, just pretend to say that someone has contributed a certain amount, to cover up the truth. Then there will be a reason to recruit him, without causing sudden confusion. In winter, he obtained an official position in the areas of Jing and Xiang, and I am waiting for you at a temporary residence in Handan. Wei felt deeply moved, knelt with his knees bent, and as soon as he stood up, the girl had vanished. Soon after, the example of opening a quota system for the Southern River project began, and Wei arranged things as the girl had said. In the tenth month of that year's winter, he went to the capital to contribute to the quota system, and indeed obtained the position of military advisor in a county, assigned to the northern part of Chu. He packed his belongings and went. Upon arriving in Handan, he suddenly met a handsome young man riding a slow horse and following him. As they were about to enter a temporary residence, the young man stopped his horse and asked, "Is the man in the carriage Wei?" "My sister gave me a message, please stay at the old inn called Dahui, where she has prepared a feast." After saying this, the young man quickly rode away.

    112

    魏知女所使，欣然前驰，果见槐阴之下，有旅寓甚清洁，门无他客，遂留寓后宅，而安置仆从于前。托疾早卧，刚月上槐梢，香风满院，女已至矣。魏相迎入室，女仍携筐设馔，珍错杂陈，相对而饮。女曰：“子至楚方伯署，东有宅一区，系妾亲串所居，久无人敢下榻。妾有一函，至彼祝而焚之，理宜相让，予赁之，价廉而宅精。”魏诺，祈请女偕往。女曰：“妾辈俱有界限，无故不敢逾越。将请命所司，再往相探可也。”魏已微醺，不觉情动，把杯至女前，跪告曰：“仆久旷之夫，望仙卿援我。”女跃登几而晒曰；“俗子真难与交，无已，请还子床头人。

    Explanation

    112

    Wei, knowing the woman's intentions, galloped forward gladly. Indeed, he found a guesthouse beneath the shade of a locust tree, which was very clean, and there were no other guests at the door. He then stayed in the rear courtyard and arranged his servants in front. Pretending illness, he went to bed early. Just as the moon rose above the locust tree branches, a fragrant breeze filled the courtyard, and the woman had already arrived. Wei welcomed her into the room, and the woman still carried a basket and set out food, with precious and varied dishes arranged. They drank facing each other. The woman said, "You have come to the office of the governor of Chu. To the east, there is a residence, which is where my relatives live. For a long time, nobody dares to stay there. I have a letter, and if you go there, recite it, and burn it, it is reasonable to yield. I will rent it, for it is cheap in price but exquisite in the house." Wei agreed and begged the woman to go together. The woman said, "We all have boundaries, and without reason, we dare not exceed. I will ask the authority in charge, and can go there to investigate again." Wei was slightly drunk and unconsciously moved by emotion. He held the cup in front of the woman and kneeled, saying, "I am a man who has been long without a wife. Hope the fairy lady helps me." The woman jumped onto the table and said with a smile, "Ordinary men are truly hard to deal with. If not, please return to your wife at home."

    113

    ”魏登几，女退缩依墙而立，魏突接其吻，不觉触鼻，审之墙间画美人耳。女已不知何往。魏哀祈不应，叹息而寝。至楚，觅得其宅，内外宽广，结构精致，依女言致祭，焚书敬祝，安居无恙。遂谒大寮及同官者。数月，忽家僮某来曰：“宅眷皆至，请往迎之。”魏方疑讶间，其子亦至，相将而往，有巨舫泊河滨，俨然妻在。魏登舟相晤，各道别情，研询来由，妻曰：“郎遣胡仆以资来迎，何自讳为？”魏辩无其事，妻曰：“胡仆偕来，今日停泊时，曰：已至矣，道郎寓所甚悉，请先往报。半日不回，因遣僮随儿寻之。”魏始悟仙女所为，遂详述其事，均大感悦。舍舟赴寓，设仙女之位而祀之。

    Explanation

    113

    Wei Dengji, the woman retreated and stood against the wall, Wei suddenly kissed her, unconsciously touching her nose, upon closer inspection, it was a painting of a beautiful woman on the wall. The woman had already gone somewhere unknown. Wei mourned and prayed, but received no response, sighed and went to sleep. When he arrived in Chu, he found her house, which was spacious inside and out, with intricate architecture. Following the woman's instructions, he performed a sacrifice, burned books, and made respectful wishes, living peacefully without harm. He then visited the high-ranking officials and his colleagues. After several months, suddenly, a servant named someone came and said, "All the family members have arrived, please go and welcome them." Wei was still in a state of doubt and surprise when his son also arrived, and they went together. There was a large boat docked by the river, and his wife was clearly there. Wei climbed aboard the boat to meet her, and they each recounted their separate experiences. After inquiring about the reasons, his wife said, "My husband sent a foreign servant with money to come and fetch me, why are you hiding this?" Wei denied the matter, and his wife said, "The foreign servant came with us, and when we docked today, he said: 'He has already arrived, and he knows your residence very well. Please go and report first.' After half a day, he did not return, so they sent a servant to follow their son to find him." Wei finally realized that this was the work of a fairy, so he detailed the story, and they were all greatly pleased. They left the boat and went to his residence, setting up an altar for the fairy and worshipping her.

    114

    载余，将致官某郡，魏洁诚斋宿，冀女下临，以问休咎。夜半，倏闻步履声，开帐而女在榻前。魏起拜曰：“大德成全，举宅被仙卿之惠，愧无以报。”女曰：“妾以报子，子何言报？从此致官，后五年必荐权某令，是时宦橐充裕，可以休矣。不然，某年月日祸且没家，子其志之。”魏悚然笔之，视女情甚依依，益觉妩媚超凡，复跽而请曰：“仙卿之惠我无穷，感且不休，复何他望？第不得一亲芳泽，终属歉事。

    Explanation

    114

    The source text describes an encounter between Wei Jie, a man of integrity, and a divine woman who appears to him in a dream. She offers him a prophecy about his future, including a promotion to an official post in five years, but also warns him of potential disaster if he does not heed her advice. Wei Jie is deeply grateful and writes down the prophecy with trepidation, feeling a strong emotional bond with the divine woman. The narrative emphasizes themes of divine intervention, gratitude, and the importance of official appointments in imperial China.

    I was being transported, and was about to be appointed to an official post in a certain prefecture. Wei Jie, known for his integrity and living in a clean, simple dwelling, hoped that a woman would descend to inquire about auspicious or inauspicious omens. At midnight, he suddenly heard the sound of footsteps. Opening the curtain, he found the woman standing by his bed. Wei Jie rose to bow and said, "Your great virtue has been completed, and the entire household has been blessed by the grace of the divine lady. I am ashamed that there is nothing to repay." The woman replied, "I will repay you, so why do you speak of repayment? From now on, you will be appointed to an official post, and in five years, you will be recommended for the position of magistrate in a certain prefecture. At that time, your official purse will be well-filled, and you can rest. Otherwise, on a certain day, disaster will befall your family. You should remember this." Wei Jie wrote it down with trepidation, looking at the woman with great affection, and became even more aware of her extraordinary charm. He knelt again and asked, "The grace of the divine lady is endless, and my gratitude is endless. What else could I hope for? But I cannot personally experience her fragrance, which is ultimately a regrettable matter."

    115

    ”女挽之曰：“子何惓惓于是？妾亦犹夫人耳，若如子意，不复得见，子其愿否？”魏曰：“且救目前之急。”女默然俯首，殊形羞涩。魏搂之不拒，于是代为解襦，登阳台涉鸿沟，悉臻实境，非复曩时空虚矣。东方既白，宛然在抱，谛审之，乃其妻也。魏愕然，推之醒，妻亦惊曰：“乌得在是？”魏询其故，妻曰：“妾安卧室中，漏三下，如被梦魇，至是方觉，不知若何出入也？”魏起视之，昨所解释者，皆其妻之衣服。自斋至室，重门尽辟，乃告以故。妻曰：“郎获咎仙女矣。”于是整装偕赴位前，焚香谢过，遂就官。三载考绩，得列保荐。

    Explanation

    115

    The woman held him and said, "Why are you so attached to this? I am also just like you. If it were as you wish, I would not be able to see you again. Would you be willing?" Wei replied, "Let's save the current emergency." The woman silently lowered her head, feeling very shy. Wei embraced her without resistance, and then helped him take off his robe. They climbed the terrace and crossed the moat, completely entering the real world, no longer the empty space of the past. The east was already white, and she was still in his arms. He carefully examined her, and it turned out to be his wife. Wei was startled, pushed her awake, and his wife also said in surprise, "How could I be here?" Wei asked her about it, and she said, "I was in my bedroom, and at the third drum strike, as if in a nightmare, until now, I only realized. I don't know how I entered or exited." Wei got up and looked around, and the clothes he had taken off yesterday were all his wife's. From the study to the bedroom, all the doors were open. He then told her the truth. His wife said, "You have incurred the wrath of the fairy." They then dressed properly and went together to the altar, burned incense to apologize, and then took office. After three years of evaluation, he was recommended for promotion.

    116

    又二年，权某县令。妻曰：“仙女所告之期至矣，郎无恋恋。”魏即日致仕，甫回江左，而白莲之变起，后任被难。魏闻之，为仙女立庙。从此优游林下十余载，年逾六旬。倏有北人问至，出书致魏曰：“予估客也，向在保阳，今来江左行贩，有同客少年胡姓者，嘱携此书以投明府，幸已达，即可归报。”魏挽之不住，开函别无片言，仅丧家之魂幡一具，上书魏元虚头衔。知大数将终，必女为预告，亟治后事而殁。殡之日，有女服缟索，长号而来，至丧前俯伏悲恸，魏孺人亟出抚，仅存白衣裳一袭，而其人杳矣。或曰：“是即仙女出吊也。”

    Explanation

    116

    Two years later, Hui became the magistrate of a certain county. His wife said, "The time foretold by the fairy has come, my dear. Do not linger." On the same day, Hui retired from office and returned to the Jiangnan region. Just as he arrived, the White Lotus Rebellion broke out, and his successor was in grave danger. Upon hearing this, Hui built a temple in honor of the fairy. From then on, he lived a leisurely life in the countryside for more than a decade, surpassing the age of sixty. Suddenly, a man from the north arrived, bringing a letter addressed to Hui. The letter read, "I am a merchant. I was previously in Baoyang and now come to the Jiangnan region to trade. A young man surnamed Hu, who is my companion, asked me to carry this letter to the magistrate. I hope it has reached you, and I can report back." Hui tried to detain him, but the man would not stay. When Hui opened the letter, there was no other writing inside, only a spirit banner from a funeral, with the title "Hui Yuanxu" inscribed on it. Realizing that his fate was coming to an end, Hui knew that his wife had forewarned him. He hastily arranged his funeral and passed away. On the day of his burial, a woman dressed in white came, crying loudly. She prostrated herself before the coffin, deeply grieved. Hui's wife hurriedly went out to comfort her, but only a single white robe remained, and the woman had vanished. Some said, "That was the fairy come to mourn."

    117

    淮商宴客记
    　　
    　　鹾客洪姓者，淮商之巨擘也，曾助饷百万，赐头衔二品。其起居服食，有王侯不逮者。戊辰之岁，予幕游淮上。仲夏，洪商投刺约消炎，会偕同事数友诣其宅。堂构爽垲，楼阁壮丽，姑无论矣。肃客入萧斋，委婉曲折，约历十数重门，入一院，小山玲珑，供素兰、茉莉、夜来香、西番莲数十种，以白石琢盆，梓楠为架，排列成行，咸有幽致。正南小阁三楹，前槐后竹，垂荫周匝。阁中窗户尽除，悬冰纹虾须帘箔，望之洞虚缥渺。卷帘入内，悬董思白雪景山水，配以赵子昂联句，下铺紫黄二竹互织“卍”字地簟，左右棕竹倚十六，磁凳二，磁榻一，以龙须草为枕褥，棕竹方几一，花栏细密，以锡作屉，面嵌水晶，中畜绿荇金鱼，游泳可玩。两壁皆以紫檀花板为之，雕镂山水人物，极其工致。空其隙以通两夹室，室中满贮香花，排五轮大扇，典守者运输转轴，风从隙入，阁中习习披香，忘其为夏。

    Explanation

    117

    A Banquet at the House of Hong, the Salt Merchant

    A salt merchant surnamed Hong, a leading figure among the Huai merchants, once contributed a million taels of silver to the state treasury and was granted the title of Second Rank. His daily life, clothing, and food surpassed even that of princes and dukes. In the year of Wu Chen, I was traveling in the Huai region as a guest in someone's retinue. In midsummer, Hong sent an invitation card, asking me to join him in a leisurely gathering, and I went with several colleagues to his residence. The main hall was spacious and well-ventilated, the towers and pavilions magnificent—these need not be described in detail. We were solemnly ushered into a quiet study, the entrance winding and twisting, passing through about ten or more gates before entering a courtyard. Within, a small, elegant hill was arranged, with dozens of varieties of flowers—white orchids, jasmine, night-blooming cereus, and passion flowers—planted in white stone basins, supported by frames of zhi and nan wood, arranged in rows, each possessing a quiet and refined charm.

    Facing south, there stood a small pavilion with three rooms. A locust tree in front and bamboo behind cast shade all around. All the windows of the pavilion had been removed, and hanging from the ceiling were curtain screens with ice-patterned, shrimp-whisker designs. From a distance, the pavilion appeared open and ethereal. Rolling up the curtain, one saw a snowy landscape painting by Dong Qichang, accompanied by a couplet by Zhao Mengfu. Beneath it lay a woven mat of purple and yellow bamboo arranged in the shape of the 卍 symbol. On either side stood sixteen leaning palms (a type of bamboo), two porcelain stools, and one porcelain bed, with dragon-veined grass used for the pillow and mattress. There was also one square bamboo table, with a finely crafted flower enclosure made of tin as the base, its surface inlaid with crystal, containing green algae and golden carp that swam about, a delight to watch.

    The two walls were made of rosewood panels, carved with landscapes and figures, the craftsmanship extremely exquisite. Gaps were left in the panels to connect to two side rooms, which were filled with fragrant flowers. Arranged within were large fans with five wheels, operated by attendants turning the axles. The wind entered through the gaps, filling the pavilion with a fragrant breeze, and one could forget that it was summer.

    118

    未几，肃客入苑囿，邱壑连环，亭台雅丽，日不暇给。于是绕山穿林，前有平池，碧玉清波，中满栽芙蕖，红白相间，灼灼亭亭，正含葩欲吐时矣。缘堤而东，千树垂杨之下，别有舫室。渡板桥而入，为头亭，为中舱，为梢棚，宛然太平艘。窗以铁线纱为屉，延入，荷香清芬扑鼻，其椅桌皆湘妃竹镶青花磁面为之。舱中两筵已具，肃客就坐，筵上安榴、福荔、哀梨、火枣、苹婆果、哈密瓜之属，半非时物。其器具皆铁底哥窑，沉静古穆。每客侍以娈童二，一执壶浆，一司供馔。馔则客各一器，常供之雪燕水参以外，驼峰、鹿臡、熊蹯、象白，珍错毕陈。妖鬟继至，妙舞清歌，追魂夺魄。酒数行，热甚，主命布雨，未几甘霖滂沛，烦暑顿消。从窗隙窥之，则池面龙首四出，环屋而喷，宴毕雨止。予潜察龙之所在，则洋夷制皮为之，掉入池中，一人坐其背，鼓水而上也。嗟乎！鹾侩耳，而享用逾王侯，何德堪此？今之疲乏，有以致之。执业者其戒之哉。

    Explanation

    118

    Not long after, the guests were invited into the garden, where hills and valleys intertwined, and pavilions and towers were elegantly beautiful, with no time to spare for all the activities. Following the mountain path through the forest, there was a flat pond ahead, with clear, emerald-green water, and lotus flowers of red and white, bright and upright, just about to burst forth in full bloom. Following the embankment to the east, under a thousand willow trees, there was a separate boat-like room. Crossing a board bridge to enter, it was a head cabin, a middle cabin, and a stern cabin, resembling a peaceful ship. The windows were made of iron-threaded gauze, and as they were invited in, the fragrance of lotus flowers was fresh and fragrant, filling the nose. The chairs and tables were all made of Xiangfei bamboo with blue-and-white porcelain inlays. In the cabin, two tables were already set, and the guests were seated. On the tables were placed pomegranates, lychees, pears, red dates, papayas, and Hami melons, among other things, half of which were out-of-season items. The tableware was all of iron-bottomed Ge kiln porcelain, serene and anciently dignified. Each guest was served by two young boys, one holding a pot of drink, and one serving the food. Each guest had their own dish, in addition to the usual offerings of snow bird's nest and water ginseng, camel hump, deer fat, bear paws, and elephant meat, all delicacies were displayed. Beautifully coiffed women followed, performing graceful dances and clear songs, captivating and mesmerizing. After a few rounds of wine, the heat was intense, and the host commanded rain to be brought. Soon, sweet rain poured down, and the oppressive heat disappeared instantly. Looking through the window crack, dragon heads emerged from the pool surface, circling the house and spouting water. The banquet ended and the rain stopped. I secretly observed where the dragon was, and it was made of foreign leather, dropping into the pool, with one person sitting on its back, pumping water up. Alas! A salt merchant, yet enjoying more than kings and dukes, what virtue can justify this? Today's fatigue is brought about by this. Those who practice should beware!

    119

    某驾长
    　　
    　　拳勇之技，旧推少林僧。今则散见诸方丛林中，往往有能者，然其道甚狭，稍有逾己者，必求胜而后已。惟遇超轶绝伦之辈，则甘拜下风矣。海昌有游方之医王姓者，奔走江河间，获利渐丰，遂止于家乡，设肆货药。时有游僧来市上，托紫石钵重百觔，入肆宣佛号，婪索不遂，辄以钵置柜上，张拱合掌拒门外，人无能出入者。市人恹苦之，不得已，满其愿。则又过一家，次第至药肆，索百钱，王仅与三枚。僧怒，将举钵压其柜，王接而掷之，抛出街心，石为之碎。观者哗然，僧恧颜拾破钵而遁。

    Explanation

    119

    Martial Arts and the Physician of Haichang

    The skills of martial arts, formerly attributed to Shaolin monks, are now scattered among various monasteries, and often there are skilled individuals. However, their way is very narrow, and if there is someone slightly better than themselves, they will seek to defeat them and then stop. Only when encountering someone who is truly extraordinary will they willingly yield to their superiority. In Haichang, there was a wandering physician named Wang who traveled between rivers and lakes, gradually accumulating wealth, and then settled in his hometown, setting up a shop to sell medicine. At times, a wandering monk would come to the market, holding a purple stone bowl weighing a hundred pounds, entering the shop to chant Buddha's name but failing to extort money. He would then place the bowl on the counter, with arms crossed and palms joined, blocking the door, preventing anyone from entering or leaving. The townspeople were annoyed by this, but had no choice but to fulfill his request. He would then go to another shop, proceeding in turn to the medicine shop, demanding a hundred coins, but Wang gave him only three. The monk became angry, intending to place the bowl on the counter, but Wang caught it and threw it, sending it flying into the street where it shattered. The onlookers were startled, and the monk, blushing with shame, picked up the broken bowl and fled.

    120

    逾载，王将赴吴下置药材，唤吴江小舟。舟子二，一为驾长，一为同伙。划舟之际，似驾长力猛，非橹脱即篙折，其伙怨詈之，恒忍受也。惟张帆时，则驾长右手执索，左手持柁，以足代篙，四体并用，无不恰当，其伙得卧而观之，故相安也。越三日，晚泊姑苏城外之寒山寺起爨。王睹月明如画，登岸闲玩，有僧立寺门外，审视王呼曰：“客非海昌药肆之王居士乎？”王曰：“唯，何以相识？”僧曰：“予前年托钵贵乡，领教已深，今幸至此，实有天缘。

    Explanation

    120

    After a year, the king was about to go to Wu to procure medicinal herbs and summoned a small boat from Wujiang. There were two boatmen: one was the helmsman, and the other was his partner. When rowing the boat, it seemed the helmsman had great strength, and either the oar fell off or the pole broke, causing the partner to blame him, though he always endured it. Only when raising the sail would the helmsman use his right hand to hold the rope, his left hand to hold the rudder, and his feet to replace the pole, using all four limbs appropriately. The partner could then lie down and watch, so they coexisted peacefully. After three days, in the evening, they anchored at the Cold Mountain Temple outside Suzhou and started a fire. The king saw the moonlight as bright as a painting and went ashore to stroll leisurely. There was a monk standing at the temple gate who examined the king and called out, "Are you not Wang Jushi of the pharmacy in Haichang?" The king replied, "Yes, how do you know me?" The monk said, "I was begging in your hometown last year and received much instruction. Now I am fortunate to be here; it is truly a matter of fate."

    121

    予师慕客久矣，请举玉趾方丈一叙。”王曰：“诺。姑俟我返舟饭毕，而后会尊师也。”僧喜，反奔入内。王归舟，潸然泣下。驾长见之曰：“客何悲为？”王语以故，曰：“入其玄中，必无生理。”驾长笑曰：“既能掷钵，何惧乎秃？吾技痒已久，今请助客。”王曰：“我既犯僧，死由自取，子何为哉？”驾长曰：“吾乐此，死亦无怨，恐僧不吾较耳。请假衣冠以师弟称，若角技时，必呼吾先，可以无事。”

    Explanation

    121

    "I have long admired your master," said the speaker, "please come and have a conversation." The monk replied, "Yes." The speaker then said, "I will wait until I return to my boat and have finished eating, and then meet with your master." The monk was pleased and hurried back inside. The king returned to his boat and tears welled up and fell. The driver saw this and asked, "Why are you sad?" The king explained the situation, saying, "If I enter their mysterious domain, I will certainly not survive." The driver laughed and said, "I have been eager to try my skills for a long time. Now, please let me assist you." The king said, "I have already offended the monk, and my death is self-inflicted. Why are you doing this?" The driver replied, "I enjoy this, and I will not regret it. I fear the monk will not challenge me. I will borrow clothes and claim to be a junior disciple. When it comes to competing skills, I will be called first, and thus avoid trouble."

    122

    王诺之。遂饱餐，偕往入门。群僧笑迎曰：“客信人也。”传报首座，出接中庭，视其人身高七足有余，脸横腰阔，气概粗豪，已望而生畏。肃客入方丈坐，乃曰：“前者小徒蒙赐教，老僧夙夜在心，冀图一遇。今既垂顾，请至艺圃，仰瞻绝技。”王唯唯。于是群拥入后圃，有地一区，高垣围绕，仅通一门，亦甚坚实。圃南包大殿之后檐，为阅武厅事，势甚雄伟。柱壮两围，础高三尺，隔以石栏，有椅二，首座与王对坐，寮僧十余，皆短褐持仗站围矣。群呼曰：“来，来，来。”王谓首座曰：“请徒与徒对，师与师对，我命弟子先戏，可乎？”首座目驾长体貌清癯，漫应曰：“何为不可？请贤弟子先。虑有伤触，毋自惭也。

    Explanation

    122

    Wang agreed. Then he had a full meal and, together with the others, entered through the gate. The group of monks greeted him with smiles, saying, “The guest is indeed a true person.” They reported to the head monk, who came out to meet him in the courtyard. Looking at the man, the head monk saw that he was over seven feet tall, with a broad face and thick waist, and a bold, coarse demeanor—already inspiring awe at first glance. The monks respectfully led Wang into the abbot’s chamber and seated him. The head monk then said, “Previously, your disciple received your instruction, and I have kept your words in mind day and night, hoping for an opportunity to meet you. Now that you have kindly visited, please come with me to the garden of skills, where I may look up to your extraordinary abilities.” Wang nodded in agreement. Thus, they led him into the rear garden, which was a secluded area surrounded by high walls, accessible only through a single gate, which was also very solid. To the south of the garden lay the back of the main hall, which served as a hall for reviewing military exercises, a structure of great grandeur. The pillars were two men wide, the bases three chi high, and the space was divided by stone railings.
