In a few more years, Commander Zhang died, and Zhu Lou arranged for his funeral, sending his subordinates back to Chu before returning to Huai. The merchants even more trusted his sincerity and honesty, planning to establish a salt business for him. Zhu Lou did not shirk hard work, and his accounting was meticulous, with everyone being close and harmonious. The business grew larger, allocating a million taels of silver, and he became a major merchant. There were hundreds of people managing affairs under him, and some advised him to take many concubines. However, Zhu Lou could not bear to betray his wife, sighed and shed tears, preparing to travel to welcome her.

    161

    值国家有川楚之变，助饷十万，获赏四品衔，遂置象服霞裳，明珰金翠之饰数十笥，若者奉父母，若者给妻弟。摒挡毕，泛画舫载珍宝，从者数十人，闯然南旋。见者仰望若巨公，皆侧目视，侧足立。未几，抵武陵，先使豪奴驰报，一乡皆惊，争来问询。竹楼至，父母迎门，弟亦伏谒，亲串趋承，酬应不绝。仰其气象光昌，莫不啧啧称叹曰：“大丈夫当如是也。”竹楼不见其妻，问诸父母，则曰：“儿已显贵，不患无佳妇，何惓惓于是？”竹楼疑焉，遂访妻母，李媪贫病无聊，见婿来声势赫耀，媪惊喜交集，不觉大恸曰：“孰谓郎君死耶，我女无福。”竹楼亟叩所以，媪曰：“郎君勿怪，是汝父母之过。自郎君去后，旋闻凶耗，我女欲殉者再，我以无确信止之。不意汝父母憎其坐食为累，亦欺我惛髦，不谋而嫁卖于某吏，去数年矣。

    Explanation

    161

    When the country was undergoing the upheaval in Sichuan and Hubei, Zhulou contributed ten million as military support and was awarded the rank of fourth-grade official. He then arranged dozens of boxes filled with splendid robes, colorful skirts, and ornaments of jade and gold, some for his parents, others for his sister-in-law. After everything was arranged, he sailed on a painted boat carrying treasures, accompanied by dozens of people, and boldly turned southward. Those who saw him looked up at him as if he were a great official, all looking at him sideways and standing on tiptoe. Shortly after, he arrived in Wuling, first sending a powerful servant to report, causing the entire village to be startled, and people rushed to inquire. When Zhulou arrived, his parents welcomed him at the door, and his younger brother also prostrated himself in greeting. Relatives and friends hurried to attend to him, and the exchanges continued non-stop. Admiring his splendid and radiant appearance, no one failed to exclaim in admiration, saying, "A true man should be like this!" Zhulou did not see his wife, so he asked his parents, who said, "Your son is now prominent and honored, not worried about lacking a good wife, why are you so concerned about this?" Puzzled, Zhulou went to visit his wife's mother, Mrs. Li, who was poor and ill. Seeing her son-in-law coming with such grandeur, she was overwhelmed with joy and sorrow, unable to hold back her tears, saying, "Who said my son-in-law had died? My daughter has no luck!" Zhulou urgently asked for the reason, and Mrs. Li said, "Do not blame your son-in-law, it is your parents' fault. After your son-in-law left, we soon heard of his death, and my daughter wanted to commit suicide twice, but I stopped her without concrete evidence. I did not expect your parents to dislike her for being a burden and deceive me, selling her to an official without my consent, which happened several years ago."

    162

    ”竹楼喟然长叹，既而曰：“十年夫妇，宁得忘情，兹虽别抱琵琶，非其本意，可俾予一见不？”媪曰：“一见何妨，即珠还，亦似不难。”遂唤舆迎之。妻见竹楼，不胜悔恨，曰：“郎何绝妾甚，不通一音，翁姑以凶耗为真，致妾不得安于其室。今既决绝矣，又邀妾何为！”竹楼诉其始末，深恨不早报也。映从者将衣服宝饰为赠，妻置不视，谓媪曰：“想郎君饥矣，母伴之，妾为治馔。”媪出沽酒，而妻入厨下自缢。媪回，惊唤邻人集，抚之已毙。某吏闻之，以威逼鸣官，官唤竹楼，讯得其故，笞吏而释之。竹楼厚葬其妻而回，尽出所带之物，尚值万金，以奉父母。曰：“是足为终养之资，儿无负亲恩，请暂归淮上，迎有日也。”其父母愧且悔，唯唯而已。竹楼回舟，至金山寺止宿，遣其仆从，将帑金缴纳，余资拥载以来，尽输禅室，以佐佛事，因披剃为僧以终。

    Explanation

    162

    Zhu Lou sighed deeply and then said, "After ten years of marriage, how could one forget feelings? Although I have now taken up a new life, it is not my original intention. May I be allowed to see you once more?" The old woman replied, "Why not? If the pearl returns, it seems not difficult." She then summoned a carriage to welcome him. When his wife saw Zhu Lou, she was overwhelmed with regret and sorrow, saying, "Why did you cut off our relationship so severely? You didn't communicate at all. Your in-laws believed the bad news, causing me to be unhappy in my home. Now that it is finally over, why did you invite me?" Zhu Lou explained the entire course of events and deeply regretted not reporting earlier. His attendants offered clothes and valuable ornaments as gifts, but his wife ignored them. She told the old woman, "I think my husband must be hungry. Mother, please accompany him. I will prepare the meal." The old woman went out to buy wine, while the wife hanged herself in the kitchen. The old woman returned, shocked, and called the neighbors to gather. When she touched the body, she found it already dead. An official heard of this and reported under duress. The official summoned Zhu Lou, investigated the matter, and caned the official before releasing him. Zhu Lou buried his wife with great ceremony and returned. He gave all his remaining wealth to his parents, which still amounted to ten thousand gold pieces. He said, "This is enough for supporting you in your old age. I will not betray your kindness. Please let me temporarily return to Huai River, and I will come to welcome you soon." His parents felt guilty and regretful, nodding in agreement but doing nothing. Zhu Lou returned by boat, stopping overnight at Jinshan Temple. He sent away his servants, paid the government funds, and donated his remaining wealth to the Buddhist temple, assisting in religious rituals. He then shaved his head and became a monk, ending his life in monastic practice.

    163

    书讼师五则
    　　
    　　江右士有所谓破靴党者，诪张为幻，无所不至。讼者咸师事之，坏法乱纪，此其极也。予记其变乱之尤者数则，魑魅魍魉，俾人人知所鉴而破之，幸甚。
    　　
    　　有父讼其子忤逆者。子大恐，持重金投师，师曰：“子无诉父理，奚以救为？”子出金跽请，师曰：“汝有妻乎？”子曰：“甚少艾。”曰：“汝能书乎？”子曰：“予曾应童子试，亦能书。”师受其金，曰：“得之矣，汝试作数字。”子书以示之，师熟视曰：“汝转背反手向予，试书符汝手，握之见官云云，则无患矣。第不得私视掌，则符泄不灵，且致大患，慎之慎之。”子诺，听其书毕，亟握而去，自投公堂。官果诘问，子痛哭不对，官怒呼杖，子如师教，膝行而前，舒掌向官，官视其左手，曰“妻有貂蝉之貌”，其右手曰“父生董卓之心。”官掷笔与之曰：“书来。”子书以献，官对其掌，字迹同，遂叱其父曰：“老而无耻，何讼子为？其速退，勿干责也。”

    Explanation

    163

    Five Cases of a Litigation Lawyer

    In Jiangxi, there are so-called "Broken Boot Party" lawyers who deceive and mislead through trickery, going to any length. Those who sue all treat them as masters, corrupting the law and disrupting the order, which is the extreme consequence. I have recorded several particularly egregious cases of their mischief, exposing the evil spirits and malevolent entities so that everyone may recognize and break through them, which is fortunate.

    There was a case where a father sued his son for being disobedient. The son was greatly frightened and brought heavy gold to seek the lawyer's help. The lawyer said, "You have no reason to sue your father, so how can you save yourself?" The son knelt and offered the gold, saying, "Do you have a wife?" The son replied, "She is very beautiful." The lawyer asked, "Can you write?" The son said, "I once passed the preliminary imperial examination and can also write." The lawyer accepted the gold and said, "I have it, try writing a few characters." The son wrote and showed it to the lawyer, who looked at it carefully and said, "Turn your back and extend your hand towards me, try writing a talisman on your hand, hold it and say something to the official, then there will be no harm. Just do not look at your palm secretly, otherwise the talisman will lose its power and cause great harm. Be very careful." The son agreed, listened to the lawyer finish writing, quickly held it and left, presenting himself in court. The official indeed questioned him, and the son cried bitterly without answering. The official angrily called for a beating. The son followed the lawyer's instructions, crawled forward on his knees, and extended his palm towards the official. The official looked at his left hand and said, "Your wife has the appearance of a beautiful woman like Diaochan," and at his right hand, "Your father has the heart of Dong Zhuo." The official threw the pen at him and said, "Write it down." The son wrote it down and presented it. The official looked at his palm, and the characters were the same. He then scolded the father, saying, "Old and shameless, why sue your son? Leave quickly, do not incur blame."

    164

    某甲者，家小康。有中表某乙，孑然一身，贫而无赖，屡屡借贷，亦小周之。时值冬季，乙又向甲贷百千偿债，甲怒其无厌，挥诸大门之外而闭也。乙始而叫骂，继思无以对债主，遂缢于檐椽之下。甲久不闻声息，出后户探之，见悬尸，恐甚，幸暮无知者，亟操巨金，往投讼师。时师方与数友为叶纸戏，甲备述来意，师曰：“予戏大负，无暇虑也。”甲出金献，师曰：“汝亟回解尸下，毋令外人觉，再来有说。”甲受计往，释尸又至，则命其观局，约三时许，甲屡屡祈请，师曰：“汝再回，悬尸故处。

    Explanation

    164

    A certain person named Jia, whose family had a moderate level of prosperity, had a cousin or relative named Yi, who was alone and poor and unprincipled. Yi frequently borrowed money from Jia and received some assistance. During the winter season, Yi again borrowed a substantial amount from Jia to pay off a debt. Jia became angry at Yi's greed and threw him out of the gate and closed it. Yi started shouting and cursing but then realized he had no way to face his creditors and hanged himself under the eaves. Jia did not hear any sound for a long time, so he went out the back door to investigate and saw the hanging body. He was very afraid, but fortunately, no one knew about it at dusk. Jia quickly took a large sum of money and sought out a lawyer. At the time, the lawyer was playing a card game with several friends. Jia explained his purpose in detail, and the lawyer said, "I have lost a lot in the game; I have no time to worry about it." Jia offered the money, and the lawyer said, "You should quickly return and remove the body, do not let anyone else know, and come back later for further instructions." Jia followed the lawyer's advice and returned to remove the body. When he came back, the lawyer told him to watch the game and waited for about three hours. Jia repeatedly pleaded with the lawyer, and the lawyer said, "You should return and hang the body in the same place."

    165

    ”甲曰：“仍害小人，何以释累？”师怒曰：“汝违吾教，看汝破家也。”甲惧而从之，又至，师笑曰：“何不惮烦耶？汝回高卧，明日有叩门者，不得应，俟官至，唤汝方出，若诘问，则求验而已，不必辩，自有脱汝计。”甲如教。次日，方保见尸，唤甲不应，即报官。官至呼甲出已，解尸审视曰：“汝识是人否？”甲伪睨之曰：“小人中表也，何以死小人门外？”官曰：“汝有仇乎？”对曰：“无之。”时方保隶役皆瞷甲财，告官曰：“死者既为某甲之戚，必威逼所致。”官怒曰：“予视尸领缢痕二，一浅一深，是移尸以图讹索者。汝等既诬甲威逼，必汝等为之。”叱杖保役，仅命某甲蠲棺以葬。

    Explanation

    165

    The teacher said, "Still harming the common people, how to relieve the burden?" The teacher became angry and said, "You have disobeyed my teachings, watch your family fall apart." Jia was afraid and followed his advice. Later, the teacher smiled and said, "Why don't you mind the trouble? You return and lie down, wait for the official to arrive, then call you out. If questioned, just ask for verification, there is no need to argue, there is a plan to save you." Jia followed his advice. The next day, Fangbao saw the body, called Jia but he did not respond, and immediately reported to the official. The official arrived and called Jia out, examined the body closely and asked, "Do you recognize this person?" Jia humbly replied, "I am a cousin of the deceased, why did he die outside my door?" The official asked, "Do you have any enemies?" Jia replied, "No." At this time, all the servants and laborers were eyeing Jia's wealth and reported to the official, saying, "The deceased is a relative of Jia, it must have been caused by coercion." The official became angry and said, "I see two rope marks on the neck, one shallow and one deep, this is moving the body to seek false compensation. You all have falsely accused Jia of coercion, it must have been done by you." The official scolded and beat the servants and laborers, and only ordered Jia to provide a coffin for burial.

    166

    某生者，与同村之富室某姓中表也，素为司会计。某富室夭亡，仅遗少妇而无子，富室之族争欲入继。妇曰：“未亡人年未二十，若继幼嗣，不善抚育。若继长者，恐贻口实，请俟数十年，得为老妇则惟命。”族人无词以答，然知其少艾，必不能安于其室，将乘隙以图之，贿仆婢以伺之。妇果与生通，始犹朝至暮归，继则与妇同寝处矣。族人得确耗，约仆婢启关，群哄入寝室，生与妇皆裸卧不及遁，连卧具卷而缚之进入城。喧传村落间，生之妻闻信大恐，亟叩讼师之门而求救，师曰：“奸已执双，何从置辩？能从我计，尚可为也。”妻曰：“生死惟命。”乃嘱其披发毁妆，唤健妇扶而去之。其时漏三下，晚衙已闭，巡罗之役见执奸至者，谕令姑停班馆，俟早衙呈报。于是安置生妇于密室，而群坐外室以待旦。师密持重金，偕生妻饮泣而来。

    Explanation

    166

    A certain young man, who was a cousin of a wealthy family in the same village and had always served as a manager of accounts, found himself in a precarious situation. A certain wealthy man from the family died young, leaving behind only a young widow and no children. The relatives of the wealthy man competed to adopt into the family. The widow said, "I, the widow, am not yet twenty years old. If I adopt a young heir, I would not be able to raise him properly. If I adopt an older heir, I fear it would provide grounds for criticism. Please wait several decades, and if I become an old woman, I will comply." The relatives had no words to respond, but they knew she was young and beautiful, and she would not remain content in her situation, so they planned to take advantage of the opportunity. They bribed servants and maids to spy on her. The widow indeed had relations with the young man, at first coming in the morning and leaving in the evening, but later staying with her in the same room. The relatives learned of this and, with the help of the servants, broke into the bedroom. The young man and the widow were both naked and unable to escape. They tied up the bedding and dragged them into the city. The news spread throughout the village. The young man's wife, upon hearing the news, was greatly frightened and immediately went to the lawyer's door to seek help. The lawyer said, "The adulterers have already been caught, how can you argue? If you follow my plan, there is still hope." The wife said, "Life and death are at your command." The lawyer then instructed her to cut her hair and ruin her makeup, and called strong women to carry her away. At that time, it was three in the morning, and the evening court had already closed. The patrol officers saw the adulterers being brought in and instructed them to temporarily stop at the station, waiting to report at the morning court. They then placed the young man and the widow in a secret room, while the others sat in the outer room waiting for dawn. The lawyer secretly held a large sum of money and came with the young man's wife, weeping.

    167

    役识讼师，佥曰：“先生何为暮夜至此？”师指生妻曰：“是为予外妹，所执之男子，其夫也，妹误谓杀奸，则夫已死，痛不欲生。予曰：‘执奸者为族人，焉敢杀？’妹不信，必欲一睹夫面，予故偕来。”语次以金授役，役笑曰：“既为先生妹，请至密室观之，无恙也。”健妇扶妻入，未几天曙，传呼放衙，师亟唤妹出，仍披发掩面，唤舆送归。无何，官升座，讼者入告，命役将生与妇入帏而给衣。生出，诘之曰：“儒者作奸犯科可乎？”生曰：“夫妇居室，人之大伦，何为不可？”官曰：“被执者是汝妻耶？”生曰：“然。”官曰：“乌得同宿某家？”曰：“生与某姓至戚，向为司事，戚某死，其妇少寡，生欲别嫌，是以偕妻同居，不意族人误执也。”遂唤生妻出，众见非妇，气馁而不敢辩，遂杖族人而释生夫妇。二人归，厚酬讼师。

    Explanation

    167

    The bailiff recognized the lawyer and all said, "Why are you here so late at night, sir?" The lawyer pointed to the man's wife and said, "She is my niece. The man being held, her husband, was mistakenly thought to have killed an adulterer, so her husband is already dead, and she is grief-stricken, almost unable to bear it. I said, 'The person who caught the adulterer is a relative, how could he dare to kill?' She did not believe me and insisted on seeing her husband's face, so I brought her along." As they spoke, the lawyer gave the bailiff money. The bailiff smiled and said, "Since she is your niece, please take her to a private room to see her; she is safe." The strong woman helped the woman enter, and before long, it was dawn. The bailiff announced the end of the session, and the lawyer quickly called his niece out. She still had her hair loose and covered her face, and the lawyer called a carriage to take her home. Soon after, the judge ascended the bench, and the plaintiff entered to report. The judge ordered the bailiff to bring the man and his wife into the curtain and provide them with clothing. The man was brought out, and the judge questioned him, "Is it possible for a scholar to commit crimes?" The man replied, "The relationship between husband and wife is the fundamental relationship of human beings; why would it not be possible?" The judge said, "Is the woman being held your wife?" The man said, "Yes." The judge asked, "How could you stay overnight at someone's house?" The man said, "I am closely related to the family of someone named Mr. Someone, and I previously served as an official. A relative of mine died, and his wife was widowed at a young age. I wanted to avoid suspicion, so I lived with my wife at the same house, but I did not expect to be mistakenly arrested by relatives." The judge then called the man's wife out, and everyone saw that she was not the woman, so they became discouraged and dared not argue. The judge then beat the relatives and released the man and his wife. The two returned home and rewarded the lawyer generously.

    168

    某甲者，在海昌城外业丝。其伙某乙，柔和而口给，能罗致客商，甲业藉以日隆。乙忽辞去，在城内自立丝肆，客商去甲而就乙，于是乙兴而甲将败矣。甲恨甚而无如何，恒对其戚友曰：“予必杀之。”是日，甲与诸友聚饮于城中之酒家，又论及乙夺业事，怨詈不已。一少年微哂曰：“庸奴焉敢杀人？徒喋喋乱人意。”甲已酒深，闻是言突然而起，窃携酒家之斧，狂奔出门。群友追持之，均怒少年。少年恐，谢过而笑慰之，遂息，咸护送回家，意犹忿忿，坐而不寐。

    Explanation

    168

    A certain person named A, engaged in the silk trade outside the city of Haichang, had a partner named B who was gentle and eloquent, capable of attracting merchants. Through B's efforts, A's business gradually prospered. Suddenly, B left and set up his own silk shop in the city. Merchants began to favor B over A, and thus B's business thrived while A's was on the verge of collapse. A hated B intensely but was powerless to do anything about it, often telling his relatives and friends, "I will surely kill him." On that day, A gathered with his friends to drink at an inn in the city, and they again discussed the matter of B seizing the business, continuing to complain and abuse him. A young man present slightly smiled and said, "A lowly slave dare kill someone? Just talking incessantly to disturb people." A, already drunk, suddenly stood up upon hearing these words, secretly took an axe from the inn, and rushed out of the door wildly. His friends chased and held him, all angry at the young man. The young man, fearing for his safety, apologized and comforted them with a smile, and the situation calmed down. They all escorted A home, but he still felt angry, sitting there without sleeping.

    169

    延至四鼓许，闻家人睡酣，携斧潜出，越城缺叩乙门，缘乙有心计，素必自起应门，甫拔关，甲突砍其头，弃斧而遁。家人闻声出视，乙脑裂而死。闻诸官，官往验，见斧柄书某号，某年月日置，遂执酒家至，诘之，酒家曰：“无论小人与乙无仇，且有杀人而不遁者乎？是斧被窃，遗害小人也。”官曰：“昨饮者有口角否？”酒家曰：“甲与某少年争。”遂唤少年至，陈其始末，拘甲而鞫，不认，严刑乃承。已置狱矣。甲之妻子遍求邑之名讼师而谋之，佥曰：“杀人者死，古今一律，虽诸葛复生，亦难更议。”有狂生戟手而笑曰：“虽然，汝有家业若干，如不恤赀，尚可为焉。

    Explanation

    169

    Until around the fourth watch of the night, A heard the family sleeping soundly and stealthily carried an axe out, leaping over the gap in the city wall to knock on B's door. Because B was shrewd, he usually would get up to answer the door. Just as B opened the door, A suddenly struck his head, dropped the axe, and fled. The family heard the noise and came out to see, only to find B with his brain split open and dead. The incident was reported to the officials, who went to investigate and found that the handle of the axe was inscribed with a certain number, placed on a certain date. They then took the tavern owner to court and questioned him. The tavern owner said, "Regardless of whether I had any grudge against B, is there anyone who kills and does not flee? This axe was stolen, causing harm to me." The official asked, "Did anyone argue last night?" The tavern owner replied, "A argued with a certain young man." They then summoned the young man, who explained the entire course of events. A was detained and interrogated, but refused to admit guilt, confessing only under severe punishment. He was already imprisoned. A's wife and children sought out famous lawyers in the county to consult them, and all said, "The killer must die; this principle has been the same throughout ancient and modern times. Even if Zhuge Liang were to be reborn, it would still be difficult to change the discussion." A wild scholar waved his hand and laughed, saying, "Even so, how much property do you have? If you do not spare your wealth, it can still be done."

    170

    ”妻子曰：“愿倾产听命，可得二千金。”生曰：“持金来，莫问出入。先教若夫忽认忽翻，以缓其狱，予入会垣谋之，半载可释。”妻子叩首去，众皆大笑而散。生携金赴省，觅廉访司之吏，询以近日盗案。吏曰：“汝邻邑语儿甫送大盗至，尚未过堂。”生大悦，即贿吏，愿一睹大盗。吏为转贿司狱者，引生见之。生笑问曰：“汝何为盗？”盗曰：“小人家无恒产，而有父母妻子，累谋业皆不遂，恃其旅力，掠以养家耳。

    Explanation

    170

    The wife said, "I am willing to exhaust all our assets to obey, and we can obtain two thousand gold." The husband replied, "Bring the gold, and do not inquire about its use. First, teach your husband to sometimes recognize and sometimes deny, to delay the trial. I will enter the official circle to plan it, and the thief can be released in half a year." The wife bowed and left, and everyone laughed and dispersed. The husband carried the gold to the provincial capital, seeking out an official from the Censorate, asking about recent theft cases. The official said, "Your neighbor's child just delivered the great thief, who has not yet been brought to trial." The husband was very pleased, immediately bribing the official, and wishing to see the great thief. The official then bribed the prison warden, leading the husband to see him. The husband smiled and asked, "What are you doing as a thief?" The thief replied, "My family has no stable means of livelihood, but we have parents, wife, and children. We have tried many trades, but none have succeeded. Relying on our strength, we steal to support our family."

    171

    ”生曰：“今家已富耶？”盗曰：“赃被官起，何富为？”生曰：“汝父母妻子今有养耶？”盗泣曰：“小人死在旦夕，何能再顾？”生曰：“有能为汝养家者，汝再承一杀人事，汝父母妻子得安饱而汝不加罪，愿乎？”盗曰：“斯世焉得有是好人？虽十死愿承也。”生曰：“予愿以千金俾汝家人为活计。汝过堂时，宪司必诘问他案，汝即承某年月日，夜在海昌城内某酒肆饮，窃得巨斧，启某乙丝肆户，有老者持灯出，被执衣裾，情急图脱，杀之而遁。如是而已。”盗允诺，亟召其家人至，予之金，盗感甚。未几过堂，复承是案，宪司查无申报者，即行文诘问，官吐舌曰：“有是哉，严刑之下，何求不得？几误是狱。”急破械释甲，而具盗案以报。

    Explanation

    171

    The person said, "Is your family wealthy now?" The thief replied, "The stolen goods were seized by the authorities; how can I be wealthy?" The person asked, "Do your parents, wife, and children have support now?" The thief wept and said, "I am dying soon; how can I take care of them anymore?" The person said, "If there is someone who can support your family, would you commit another murder? Your parents, wife, and children would be well-fed, and you would not be punished; would you be willing?" The thief replied, "In this world, how could there be such a good person? Even if I die ten times, I would be willing to do it." The person said, "I wish to give you a thousand gold pieces to support your family. When you go to court, the censorate will certainly question you about other cases. You would admit that on a certain date, you were drinking at a wine shop in Haimen City, stole a large axe, broke into a silk shop, and when an old man came out with a lamp, you grabbed his clothes in a panic to escape, killed him, and fled. That is all." The thief agreed, and quickly summoned his family. He gave them the gold, and the thief was very grateful. Soon after, he went to court and again confessed to this case. The censorate found no prior report and immediately issued a document to question him. The official exclaimed, "Is there such a thing? Under severe punishment, what cannot be obtained? Almost made a mistake in this case." He quickly broke his chains and released him, and prepared a report on the theft case.

    172

    调白
    　　
    　　盗之为道大矣，自响马老瓜，以至丢包绺窃，名目多端，神乎其技者，见诸载籍已不乏人。又有所谓调白者，益超出其俦而近乎神怪。有曰“下八洞”者，人之财物，一过其目，即能摄去。其最者曰“上八洞”，无须露财，与人同止息，即知其囊中物数，默运潜移而不觉也。闻有楚商胡某者，为其主收债，朱提盈箱，买舟而返。途遇一客，负箧襆被，呼舟共济。舟子辞之，胡悯其孤，招之同艇。其人谈甚豪，襟怀慨爽，胡甚乐之。饮食与共，谈数日，觉其非士非商，益畏而敬之，恒投其所嗜而助其不足，客甚德之。

    Explanation

    172

    Trickery and Deception

    The art of thievery is vast, ranging from horse thieves and bandits to pickpockets and burglars, with many names and forms, and those whose skills are so extraordinary as to seem almost supernatural. There are many examples of such individuals recorded in literature. There is also a type of trickster known as "调白" (diao bai), who surpasses others in their craft and approaches the realm of the supernatural. Among them, there are those called "下八洞" (xia ba dong), who can take away someone's possessions with just a glance. The most skilled are known as "上八洞" (shang ba dong), who do not need to reveal wealth; by sharing the same resting place with someone, they can instantly know the contents of their bag and silently transfer items without being noticed.

    There was a merchant from Chu named Hu, who collected debts for his master and returned home with boxes full of gold and silver, buying a boat to make the journey. On the way, he encountered a traveler carrying a box and wrapped in a blanket, who called for a boat to cross together. The boatman refused him, but Hu felt pity for his loneliness and invited him onto the same boat. The traveler talked very boldly, with a broad and refreshing spirit, and Hu enjoyed his company. They shared meals and conversations for several days, and Hu began to sense that the traveler was neither a scholar nor a merchant, but he grew to fear and respect him. Hu always gave him what he liked and helped where he lacked, and the traveler was very grateful to him.

    173

    将抵其处，乃谓胡曰，“别有日矣，余有所言，得无惊骇。因公长者，敢以实陈，誓不为公害也。”胡曰：“吾二人，虽邂逅之交，亦云睦矣，请直言毋隐。”对曰：“余上八洞也，始睹公舟，知为重载，既蒙容纳，益知有货若干，已移我箧中，请公验之。”胡骇其所言之数相符，亟视箱，则封识如故，已空诸所有。客启其箧还之，曰：“我不忍欺公，然前途有友短腰缠者，乞假五十缗，约某日必偿公舍。”胡曰：“囊中物皆客所赐，敢不惟命！”如数与之，客谢而起，一转瞬已登彼岸，离舟尚十余丈也。胡愕然，归告主人，疑信半之，即胡亦不计其偿否。至日，有衣冠华焕、仆从如云者投刺谒胡。出见，客即调白者，款之益恭，客喜而退。胡送出门，曰：“所借之资，已加子金，而纳诸主人之椟，且有以益公者，后如携金出外，必以稻米一撮实封中，则不受暗算，谨志之。”胡再拜谢教，客去，主人启椟而子母无亏，始信其伙，而知天下果有异人也。

    Explanation

    173

    As they approached the place, the guest said to Hu, "There is another day, I have something to say, you might be startled. Because you are a gentleman, I dare to tell you the truth, I swear I will not harm you." Hu replied, "The two of us, even though we are casual acquaintances, we are also friendly, please speak frankly without hiding anything." The guest responded, "I am from the Eight Immortal Caves. I first saw your boat, knowing it was heavily loaded. Having been kindly received, I came to know there were goods of a certain amount, which have already been moved into my box. Please check them." Hu was startled that the number he mentioned matched, and quickly looked at the box, but the seals were still intact, yet everything inside was gone. The guest opened his box and returned it, saying, "I could not bear to deceive you, but there is a friend ahead who has little money. I ask for a loan of fifty strings of coins, promising to repay you by a certain day." Hu said, "The contents of the bag are all given by the guest, how could I not follow your command!" He gave him the amount requested. The guest thanked him and stood up, and in an instant, he had crossed to the other side, still more than ten zhang away from the boat. Hu was astonished, returned to tell the host, and was half doubtful and half believing, even Hu did not consider whether he would repay. On that day, a man dressed in splendid attire, with a retinue like clouds, sent a card to visit Hu. Going out to meet him, the guest was the one who had spoken earlier, and treated him even more respectfully. The guest was pleased and left. Hu saw him out, saying, "The borrowed money, has been increased with your gold, and placed in the host's chest, and there is something that will benefit you. If you carry money outside, you must put a handful of rice in a sealed envelope, then you will not be harmed secretly. Please remember this." Hu bowed twice and thanked him for the advice. The guest left, and the host opened the chest and found the principal and interest intact, then believed the guest's words, and knew that there truly are extraordinary people in the world.

    174

    芗厈曰：此侠客也，古今恒有之。第茫茫宇宙，斯人安在耶？俾予遇之，愿再拜而投诸门下，习其术，抽取天下贪墨之财，以济四穷而助公举，不亦快哉！

    Explanation

    174

    Xiang'an said: These are the knight-errants, who have always existed throughout history. Yet, in this vast universe, where are these people? If I were to meet them, I would gladly kneel twice and submit myself to their tutelage, learning their methods, extracting the corrupt wealth of the world, to relieve the four kinds of poverty and aid public causes, wouldn't that be wonderful?

    175

    刘智庙
    　　
    　　刘智者，不知何许人。不事生产，而性好施舍。家资荡然，妻孥无存，流离失所。至山左德州，通衢之侧，有古刹存也，户毁垣倾，殿廷将圯，僧众皆散。智往托居而丐。但庙虽破落，香火犹存，每年四月八日，近村之人必大举庙会，商贾云集，百货俱陈，以席结棚，列分街道，居然闹市。而庙之三面，为不逞之徒大开赌局，银钱出入，盈千累万，童叟勿欺。智日游其间，不觉心动，乞得数钱，姑以压宝，随其意之所至，无不胜者。旬余，将前后左右所有赌局之资本，咸归智一人矣。计之得金钱数万。或劝之开业成家，以为富室，智慨然曰：“余丐也，而暴富不祥，当思有以禳之。且余孑然一身，与僧等耳，愿以资修复庙宇，而奉其香火，得保首领以没，神之祐也。”乃遍拜绅士，为之助力，鸠工庀材，拓疆启宇，顿改旧观。俾智主持其中，而四方士君子之道出其间者，咸游览也。至今相传百十年。凡历是境者，莫不呼之曰“刘智庙”。

    Explanation

    175

    Liu Zhi Temple

    Liu Zhi, whose origins are unknown, did not engage in productive work but had a nature that liked to give alms. His family wealth vanished, his wife and children were no longer alive, and he was displaced and homeless. He arrived at Shandong Province's Dezhou, beside a major road, where an ancient temple still stood, though its doors and walls were destroyed, its hall and courtyard were about to collapse, and the monks had all dispersed. Liu Zhi stayed there and begged. Although the temple was dilapidated, the incense and candles were still burning. Every year on the eighth day of the fourth month, people from nearby villages definitely held a temple fair, with merchants gathering in large numbers, all kinds of goods displayed, and temporary market stalls set up along the streets, turning it into a bustling market. On three sides of the temple, ruffians opened large gambling dens, with money flowing in and out in thousands and tens of thousands, and fair play was maintained. Liu Zhi wandered there daily, and his heart was moved. He begged for a few coins and tried his luck with the money, as his mind directed, and he won every time. After about ten days, all the capital from the gambling dens on all sides eventually belonged to Liu Zhi. He calculated and got tens of thousands of coins. Some advised him to start a business and establish a family to become a wealthy household. Liu Zhi responded with a sigh, "I am a beggar, and sudden wealth is not auspicious. One should think of ways to ward it off. Moreover, I am alone and destitute, just like a monk. I wish to use the money to repair the temple and maintain its religious activities, so that I can preserve my head until death, which would be the protection of the gods." He then visited all the gentry to seek their assistance. They gathered workers and materials, expanded the area, and built new structures, completely changing the old appearance. Liu Zhi oversaw the project, and scholars and gentlemen from all directions passing through came to visit. To this day, it has been passed down for over a hundred years. Those who pass through this place all call it "Liu Zhi Temple."

    176

    芗厈曰：富贵者造物所不惜，往往有无意得之，而其所最靳者，惟名也耳。历溯古来帝王卿相及文人学士，为野人妇竖之所知名者，能有几人？噫！生前赫赫，死后冥冥，如恒河沙数。彼刘智，丐也。一念之善，百世之贤愚悉知其名，超出于寻常帝王卿相、文人学士之上。且庙皆以神名，而是庙居然以刘智名之，纵日后庙有废时，而智之名千古不朽，缘此站地名即称刘智庙故也。是诚何福以享之哉！况天下之善男信女，施舍千万以开创庙宇，至今冥没无闻者，亦不知凡凡。噫！刘智何幸而得此名也耶？

    Explanation

    176

    The Master Xiang Yan said: Wealth and power are not sparing by the Creator; they are often obtained unintentionally. However, the most stingy thing is fame. Looking back through history, how many emperors, ministers, and scholars have been known by common people? Ah! There are countless people who are forgotten after death, like the sands of the Ganges. Liu Zhi, a beggar, had a single good deed that made his name known to all virtuous and foolish people for generations, surpassing ordinary emperors, ministers, and scholars. Even if the temple is eventually abandoned in the future, Liu Zhi's name will remain immortal through the ages because this place is named after Liu Zhi's temple. What blessing can enjoy it? Moreover, there are countless devout men and women who donate to establish temples, yet they are also forgotten after death. Ah! How fortunate Liu Zhi is to have this name!

    177

    吴桥案
    　　
    　　燕都南吴桥县之连镇，布市也。居是地者，半以贸布为业。有肩贩张乙，恒负布西方求售，出或两三月一归。年二十余，家仅老母，为之娶妇李氏，娇而荡，夫妇甚相得。弥月，张仍出贸易，妇不安于室，日游邻里，姑劝之不听，教戒之则怒目视，反唇稽矣。
    　　
    　　有武生许三者，城居，随父设肆于镇。父因老病，俾业其子，而养疴于家。许三恃衿无赖，好与恶少伍，而游猎于色，不逞之徒利其资而助为虐。一日，与李氏遇诸途，艳之，访诸恶少，或告之曰：“此吾邻张乙妇，其夫负贩外出，恒在我家游荡，可以利诱之。”许三喜诺，订约而去。其人归，与妻谋。妻曰：“是不难，使许君伪为吾弟也者。俟妇来亦来，吾夸其富饶，以欣动之，妇若不避，吾让之隙，则事成矣。”其人告许，盛服而往。妇适至，欲避，邻妻以其弟告，牵衣命坐。

    Explanation

    177

    The Wujiao Case

    In the southern part of Yan capital, Wujiao County, there was a town called Lianzhen, which was a market for cloth. Most of the people living there made a living by trading cloth. There was a shoulder-carrying peddler named Zhang Yi, who regularly carried cloth westward to sell and would be away for two or three months before returning home. He was in his twenties and had only an elderly mother at home. He married a woman named Li, who was flirtatious and promiscuous, but the couple got along very well. After the first month of marriage, Zhang Yi still went out to trade, and his wife was not content at home. She would wander around the neighborhood every day. Her mother-in-law tried to advise her, but she did not listen. When scolded, she would look at them angrily and argue back.

    There was a martial scholar named Xu San who lived in the city and followed his father to set up a shop in the town. His father, due to old age and illness, left the business to his son and stayed at home to rest. Xu San was arrogant and unprincipled, liked to associate with bad boys, and pursued women. Those who were not satisfied with their lives took advantage of his wealth and helped him commit misdeeds. One day, he met Li's wife on the road and found her attractive. He sought the help of bad boys, and one of them told him, "This is my neighbor Zhang Yi's wife. Her husband is a peddler who goes out, and he often wanders around our house. You can lure her with money." Xu San agreed happily and left after making an agreement. The person returned home and discussed it with his wife. She said, "It's not difficult. Let Xu San pretend to be my younger brother. When the wife comes, he will come too. I will praise his wealth and flatter her. If she does not avoid him, I will give her a chance to get close, and then the plan will succeed." The person informed Xu San, who then dressed up and went. The wife arrived, wanting to avoid him, but the neighbor's wife told her that Xu San was her younger brother and pulled her clothes, telling her to sit down.

    178

    妇斜睨许，许故卖弄姿首，渐与调笑，妇赧不言。邻妻曰：“吾弟非外人也，烦嫂相陪，吾具膳去。”妇口言归而身不动，邻妻出，反闭其户。许搂妇求欢，妇索服饰，许允之。相将入室，事甫毕，而邻妻至，妇羞惭无地，邻妻曰：“若欲不泄于人，必长与吾弟欢好，犹弟妇也。吾何言哉？若不常来，吾且播扬之，勿悔。”妇喜诺。由是许为之易新衣，备首饰，居然完好。姑诘其所自来，则以母家对。姑知其无父母兄弟，大疑之，访得其端倪，禁妇勿出，则骂鸡詈犬，搅扰不休。姑大不堪，俟子归告之故，立命休弃。张乙承母命，不得已与手书而逐之。

    Explanation

    178

    The woman glanced sideways at Xu, who deliberately displayed his good looks, gradually engaging in playful teasing. The woman blushed and remained silent. The neighbor’s wife said, “My brother is not a stranger; please keep company with him while I prepare the meal and leave.” The woman verbally agreed to return but did not move. After the neighbor’s wife left, she closed the door instead. Xu embraced the woman and sought intimacy, and the woman requested new clothes and jewelry, which Xu agreed to provide. They went into the room, and the matter was just finished when the neighbor’s wife arrived. The woman felt extremely ashamed. The neighbor’s wife said, “If you want to keep it secret, you must continue to have a good relationship with my brother, like a sister-in-law. What can I say? If you don’t come often, I will spread it around, so don’t regret it.” The woman happily agreed. Therefore, Xu provided her with new clothes and prepared jewelry, making her appear completely well. The mother-in-law questioned where she had come from, and she answered that she came from her mother’s family. The mother-in-law suspected that she had no parents or siblings and forbade her from going out. The woman then constantly complained and caused continuous disturbance. The mother-in-law was very annoyed and waited until her son returned to tell him the reason, immediately ordering her dismissal. Zhang Yi followed his mother’s command and, having no choice, wrote a letter to send her away.

    179

    妇泣去，无可归，乃投许而尤之，许曰：“今长为我妇矣，不得受恶姑气，尚不慊于心耶？”乃置宅相处，越数月，供亿不支。复与恶少谋，佥曰：“是非尔真妇也，可使之娼，征其夜合之资，不但衣食有藉，而致富不难矣。”许喜诺，逼妇接客，妇畏鞭笞，不敢不从也。张乙自出妇后，负气去，半载而回，与妇情犹未绝，访知为娼，潜往视之。妇见痛泣，且告之悔，牵留共宿，而还其手书。张乙归，不敢告母，正无可如何之际，许知是夜有客，而不知其为本夫。次日往索资，妇无以应，裸而挞之，妇以实告，许复与恶少谋曰：“殆矣，本夫已反其手书，若以占妻讼我，奈何？”佥曰：“彼经纪人一时计不及此，必将复来。俟其来时，吾等伏于左右，群起捕击，使惧而逃，似可绝迹。”许唯唯。未几，张心果不息，又至。甫扣门，伏发群殴之，诈死，伏不动。许曰：“殆矣，不过惧之而已，奈何置之死地？罪将在我。”众哄然散。

    Explanation

    179

    The woman wept and left, having nowhere to return. She then went to Xu and accused him. Xu said, "Now you are permanently my wife; should you not be free from the abuse of a cruel mother-in-law? Are you still unsatisfied in your heart?" He then provided her with a house to live with him. After several months, however, he could no longer support the household expenses. He then conspired again with some wicked youths, who all said, "This is not your true wife. You can make her a prostitute, demand her fees for entertaining clients, and not only will her food and clothing be provided, but wealth will not be hard to attain." Xu agreed happily, and forced the woman to entertain guests. The woman feared beatings and dared not disobey.

    After Zhang Yi sent his wife away, he left in anger and returned after half a year. His feelings for her had not yet faded, and upon learning she had become a prostitute, he secretly went to see her. The woman saw him and wept bitterly, confessing her regret. She persuaded him to stay and they spent the night together, and she returned his handwritten letter to him. Zhang Yi returned home, dared not tell his mother, and was at a loss as to what to do. Xu, however, knew that there was a guest that night, but did not know that the guest was her original husband. The next day, he went to demand payment, but the woman had no means to pay. He stripped her and beat her. The woman then told him the truth. Xu again conspired with the wicked youths, saying, "This is dangerous. The husband has already returned her letter. If he sues me for keeping your wife, what then?" They all said, "That broker did not consider this at the time. He will surely come back. When he arrives, we shall lie in wait on either side and attack him together, making him fear and flee. This may be enough to eliminate the problem." Xu nodded in agreement.

    Not long after, Zhang's heart was still restless, and he returned. As he was just about to knock on the door, they sprang from their hiding places and attacked him together. He faked his death and remained motionless. Xu said, "This is dangerous. It was only to scare him, but how could we put him to death? The guilt will be on us." The crowd then scattered in confusion.

    180

    张知众去，觉遍体受伤，不敢见母，匍匐至河干，趁舟入邻邑。夜扣行家，其主素识，惊问其故，张以酒后与人共殴，既被人伤，亦复伤人，求为调治，而谋避匿行。主为之延医，伤痊并为合伙，贩布于口外。当是时，连镇河干，芦苇中有浮尸。亭长报宰，宰验明遍体鳞伤，似群殴致毙而弃于河者。面目已败，莫辨谁何。宰为棺殓缉凶，标召尸亲而已。

    Explanation

    180

    Zhang Zhi Zong left, feeling his entire body injured, too ashamed to see his mother, and crawled to the riverbank, where he took a boat to the neighboring town. At night, he knocked on the door of a traveler's inn. The innkeeper, who was a long acquaintance, was startled and asked him what had happened. Zhang explained that after drinking, he had fought with someone, was injured by them, and in turn injured others, and he was seeking treatment while planning to hide and avoid pursuit. The innkeeper arranged for a doctor, and after his injuries healed, they became partners, trading cloth in the frontier areas. At that time, there was a floating corpse in the river near the town, among the reeds. The village constable reported this to the magistrate, who confirmed that the body was covered with cuts, suggesting it had been killed by a group beating and then thrown into the river. The face was already ruined, making it impossible to identify who it was. The magistrate ordered the body to be buried and the culprit to be investigated, and only marked the body and summoned the relatives of the deceased.

    181

    乃张乙之母，数日不见其子归，寻访无着，或告以河干之尸必其子也，母信以为然，即投宰，告许三谋妇杀子状。宰启棺使认，母亦难辨，因报仇心切，睹尸衣上右肩有补缀处，谬曰，“吾子布贩也，其肩负布易破，吾以旧布，补以白线缝，是否，请一验而定。”遂洗验，果然。即提许三与诸恶少一讯，皆伏辜。已解审会垣，许父哀其子，思有以援之，或告之曰：“此尸诈也。张乙年少身短，此尸年老身长，虽面目溃烂，而身旁有须一缕，其明证也。”父悟，急为上控，而使其子翻供。司发首郡复鞫，游移无定，已逾年矣。

    Explanation

    181

    Zhang Yi's mother, after several days of not seeing her son return, searched everywhere without success. Someone told her that the body found by the riverbank must be her son, and she believed it to be true. She immediately went to the magistrate and reported that Xu San's wife had killed her son. The magistrate opened the coffin to let her identify the body, but she also found it difficult to recognize. Driven by her intense desire for revenge, she noticed a patch on the right shoulder of the corpse and falsely said, "My son was a cloth dealer. His shoulder straps were prone to tearing, so I patched them with old cloth and sewed them with white thread. Is this correct? Please verify it." They washed and examined the body, and indeed it was so. They immediately arrested Xu San and the other ruffians and interrogated them, and they all confessed. The case was then submitted for review by the higher authorities. Xu San's father mourned his son and sought a way to help him. Someone told him, "This body is a fake. Zhang Yi was young and short, but this body was old and tall. Although the face was decayed, there was a strand of beard nearby, which is clear evidence." The father realized this and quickly appealed to higher authorities, and had his son recant. The higher authorities sent the case back to the prefecture for re-examination, and the verdict was indecisive. It has already been a year.

    182

    张乙贸易获利，归视其母，母见之，喜惧交作。张问故，母实告之，使仍避匿。张曰：“不可。我本无罪，若使许三同抵，则我咎不轻，且终身不得居故乡。不如自首。”使遂投宰而陈其始末，宰大惊，即偕往会垣，面告宪司。幸罪人未决，即出许，科以和奸罪，褫革荷校而已。张归安业，妇亦投回，哀求其姑，割指示志，改行为良，仍完聚也。

    Explanation

    182

    Zhang Yi gained profit from trade and returned home to see his mother. Upon seeing him, she experienced a mix of joy and fear. Zhang asked the reason, and his mother told him the truth, advising him to continue hiding. Zhang said, "It is not right. I am innocent, and if Xu San shares the same punishment, my guilt will be severe, and I will not be able to live in my hometown for life. It is better to confess." He then went to the local official and reported the entire course of events. The official was greatly surprised and immediately accompanied him to the city gate to report to the higher authority in person. Fortunately, the criminal had not yet been sentenced, so Xu was released and punished with the crime of adultery, stripped of office and put in stocks. Zhang returned to his normal life, and his wife also returned, pleading with her mother-in-law, cutting off a finger to show her resolve, reforming her behavior, and becoming virtuous, thus reuniting with the family.

    183

    芗厈曰：吾闻讯是案者，一老吏程姓，素以折狱为能。僚友佥尊之曰“老哥哥”。当许三翻供之际，历数问官，坚执不认，后值程尹谓之曰：“占妻斗殴之情既实，则死者是否本人，汝罪难逭。况承之未必即死，不承递相熬审，或喜用严刑者，或善于磨炼者，当此溽暑时，昼夜不息，得不瘐毙乎？与其速死，何如缓生，汝自度之。”许三感悟，痛哭承认，供招乃定。程尹傲于众曰：“诸君逾载所不能决者，老哥哥片言定之，鞫狱之能，不敢多让人矣。”正众口交相赞叹间，吏报吴桥令带领已死复生之张乙投首，太守命释许三，众乃粲然曰：“老哥哥竟是鞫狱神手，死人审活矣。”甚矣，定谳之难也。

    Explanation

    183

    Xiang Yan said: I heard that the person in charge of this case was an old official surnamed Cheng, who was renowned for his ability to resolve disputes. His colleagues all respectfully addressed him as "Elder Brother." When Xu San changed his confession several times, he steadfastly refused to admit guilt despite being questioned by multiple judges. Later, when Cheng the magistrate spoke to him, he said, "If the truth of the wife-beating and fighting is established, then whether the deceased was indeed the person you killed, your crime is difficult to escape. Moreover, if you confess, you might not necessarily die immediately. If you do not confess, you will be subjected to prolonged questioning by those who use severe punishment or persistent interrogation methods. During the hot and humid season, if you are questioned day and night without respite, you might not survive. Is it better to die quickly or to live slowly? Consider this for yourself." Xu San was moved and wept bitterly, finally confessing. The case was thus resolved. Cheng the magistrate boasted to the crowd, "The case that all of you could not resolve for over a year, I settled with just a few words. My ability to adjudicate cases is something I do not shy away from claiming." As the crowd was praising him, a clerk reported that the magistrate of Wuqiao had brought in Zhang Yi, who had been dead but was now alive, to confess. The governor ordered Xu San to be released. The crowd smiled brightly and said, "Elder Brother is indeed a master of adjudicating cases; he has brought the dead back to life." How difficult it is indeed to reach a final verdict!

    184

    第五卷
    　　
    　　谈鬼十二则
    　　
    　　客有善谈鬼者，怪怪奇奇，无不极情尽致，不胜录取。予采其可作笑谈者数则列后。
    　　
    　　吾邑有朱桥镇，布市也。贸布者五鼓毕集，黎明而散。忽途人相戒云。“桥左有一大鬼，高丈余，白衣冠，被发执扇，眉目下垂，口鼻流血，世所谓无常鬼是也。见者咸弃物奔逃，迟则惊毙。”以故市为改。时庄农王二者，家有急需，不得已，于寅刻携灯负布趋市。至桥左，遥见大鬼昂然来，王骇极，灭灯潜入桑林内，猱升树颠，藏丛叶间。

    Explanation

    184

    Fifth Chapter

    Twelve Tales of Ghosts

    There was a guest skilled at telling ghost stories, whose tales were strange and bizarre, each told with great emotion and detail, too numerous to recount. I have selected a few that can be turned into humorous anecdotes to present here.

    In my hometown, there is a town called Zhuqiao, which is a cloth market. Those who trade in cloth gather by the fifth drumbeat and disperse at dawn. Suddenly, people warned each other, saying, "To the left of the bridge, there is a large ghost, over ten feet tall, wearing a white cap and robe, with disheveled hair holding a fan, his eyebrows and eyes drooping, blood flowing from his mouth and nose. This is the ghost known as the 'Inconstant' in this world. Those who see him all abandon their belongings and flee; if they are slow, they will be frightened to death." Because of this, the market was changed. At that time, there was a farmer named Wang Er whose family had an urgent need. He had no choice but to carry a lamp and carry cloth to the market at the hour of the tiger. When he reached the left side of the bridge, he saw the large ghost approaching from afar. Wang was terrified to the extreme, extinguished the lamp, and sneaked into a mulberry grove, climbing the tree like a monkey, and hid among the leaves.

    185

    时月色朦胧，鬼不及睹，行至桑林外，喟然叹曰：“明明一人来，倏忽不见，妖耶？怪耶？”语未竟，又一大鬼来，服色面目相等，向前拱其手而过。前鬼曰：“噫，是矣。吾等费心计逐客至此，而为彼所得，当抽分其贷。”遂大唤后鬼回索分也。后鬼瞠目直视，忽扬大掌，拦腰一击，前鬼扑地，首与上下身及两臂跌分五截，俯捏之，得青烟二道。解佩囊纳讫，长啸而去。王二犹不敢下树。至旦，见行人结队来，始呼救，众集而后逼视，则鬼之首系纸糊者，两臂与手削木为之，上身一人、下身一人俱死。纸衣亦裂，始悟二贼顶接作长人，假鬼以行劫，而真鬼毙之，报亦巧哉。

    Explanation

    185

    At that time, the moonlight was hazy, and the ghost could not see. He walked to the outskirts of the mulberry forest and sighed, saying, "A person clearly came, but suddenly disappeared. Was it a demon? Was it a monster?" Before he had finished speaking, another large ghost appeared, with the same attire and appearance, and he bowed his hands forward and passed by. The front ghost said, "Ah, it is so. We have gone to great lengths to chase the guest here, but he was caught by him. We should divide his wealth." So he called out loudly to the rear ghost to return and claim his share. The rear ghost stared straight ahead with wide eyes, suddenly raised his big hand, and struck him across the waist. The front ghost fell to the ground, and his head, upper body, and lower body, along with both arms, fell apart into five pieces. He bent down to pick them up and found two trails of blue smoke. He opened his pouch and put them in, then let out a long howl and left. Wang Er still dared not come down from the tree. Until dawn, when he saw people coming in groups, he finally called for help. The crowd gathered and then closely examined it. They found that the head of the ghost was made of paper, and the arms and hands were made of wood. The upper body and lower body were both dead, and the paper clothes were also torn. He finally realized that the two thieves had connected their heads to make a long person, pretending to be ghosts to rob, but the real ghost killed them. The retribution was also clever indeed.

    186

    有钱、刘二役者，奉差勾摄人。知其人狡甚，夜往拘之。距城约二十里，一役持灯，一役执牌，行五六里许，钱谓刘曰：“吾有腹疾，予吾灯，将觅地大遗。尔前进某村，市尾有里保茶室，在彼俟吾。”刘诺而去，比及市尾，夜深户闭，无停留处。回原路，见市中一室，隙逗灯光，隐隐泣声甚悲，门外一人影贴身窥探。刘意为钱遗毕而来窃窥妇女耳，欲戏之，俾不敢作声，潜以中指挖其尻，其寒浸骨。突然回首，则眸出舌伸，发披血结，现缢鬼形。刘大惊，触板而倒。邻人闻声出视，识为县役，已痰涌气喘欲绝。邻人大呼，市众皆集，而钱亦至，正扶救间，室内亦大呼救人，众踹门而入，则少妇自悬于梁，其翁姑年老不能解脱，众为之卸救而苏。询之乃翁，知妇为姑虐，半夜轻生。缢鬼求代而窥之，为刘役冲散，此妇之命不应绝。而刘亦渐愈，惟右手全黑，经年始退。时人称之为“捣鬼手”。

    Explanation

    186

    Two役者, named Qian and Liu, were dispatched on an official mission to arrest a notorious individual known for his cunning. Knowing the person's shrewdness, they decided to carry out their task at night. The distance to the target's residence was approximately twenty li from the city. One役者carried a lamp, while the other held a summons, and they walked for about five or six li. Qian told Liu, "I have a stomach ailment. Give me your lamp, and I will find a place to relieve myself. You should proceed to a certain village ahead. At the end of the market, there is a communal tea house where you can wait for me." Liu agreed and continued on his way. By the time he reached the end of the market, it was late at night, and all the shops were closed, leaving no place for him to stay. Returning to the original path, he saw a room in the market with a faint light seeping through the cracks. Inside, he heard faint, mournful sobs. Outside the door, a shadowy figure was peering in closely. Liu thought Qian had finished relieving himself and was now secretly peeping at a woman inside. Wanting to play a prank on him, Liu made a gesture with his middle finger to make the figure keep quiet, but the figure suddenly turned around, revealing eyes bulging, tongue lolling out, and hair matted with blood—revealing itself as a hanging ghost. Liu was terrified and fell against the door, collapsing to the ground. Neighbors heard the commotion and came out to see what was happening. They recognized the役者as a county official, who was now choking and gasping for breath, on the verge of death. The neighbors shouted for help, and the townspeople gathered, including Qian, who arrived just in time to assist. Inside the room, another cry for help was heard, and the crowd kicked down the door to find a young woman hanging from the beam. Her elderly parents-in-law, too frail to remove her, were unable to save her. The crowd helped to untie and revive her. Upon questioning the old man, they learned that the woman had been mistreated by her mother-in-law and had taken her own life in the middle of the night. The hanging ghost had been seeking a replacement and had been spying on the woman, but was disrupted by Liu's actions. It seemed that the woman's fate was not meant to end. Liu gradually recovered, but his right hand turned completely black and remained that way for a year before fading. People of the time began to call him the "Ghost-Disrupting Hand."

    187

    陆都阃充卒伍时，出境迎上官至中途，闻上官遇雨而退。陆回，雨益大，衣履濡湿。道路泥泞，见旁有小楼一楹，趋避檐下。天将暮而雨不止，叩门借宿，少妇拔关出曰：“夫男入城未回，不便留客。”陆示之佩刀号衣曰：“吾行伍中人，正直自矢。今进退无从，只求楼下一席地耳。”妇哀其穷而纳之，且赡以粥，给之灯与薪。妇登楼阖户而纺，陆以薪为褥，趺坐啜粥，然火烘衣。约三鼓许，有老妇由门隙入，陆骇然禁声，以观其所为。老妇若未睹陆者，以一杖置门侧，望中溜再拜，匆匆登楼，闻与少妇言甚欢，未几悲声辛语，既而大笑。陆取其杖阅之，倏变为麻绳，甫坐于身下。而老妇下楼望中溜又拜，回身觅杖不得，始见陆，知为所收。告曰：“老妇恃杖而行，客须见还。”陆不答。老妇倏变为少艾，柔声怡色以哀之，陆仍不答。

    Explanation

    187

    When Lu, the commander, was enlisted as a common soldier, he went beyond the border to meet his superior officer halfway, but heard that the officer had turned back due to the rain. Lu returned, and the rain had grown even heavier, soaking his clothes and shoes. The road was muddy, and seeing a small pavilion nearby, he ran for shelter under its eaves. As evening approached and the rain still did not cease, he knocked on the door and asked for lodging. A young woman opened the door and said, “My husband has gone into town and has not yet returned; it would be inconvenient to entertain a guest.” Lu showed her his sword and military uniform and said, “I am a man of the ranks, and I have always been upright. Now I have no choice but to advance or retreat, and I only seek one place to sleep in this building.” Moved by his poverty, the woman accepted him, and gave him porridge to eat, along with a lamp and firewood. The woman went up to the floor, shut the door, and began to spin. Lu used the firewood as a mattress, sat cross-legged while eating his porridge, and lit a fire to dry his clothes.

    About the third watch of the night, an old woman entered through a crack in the door. Lu was startled and fell silent, watching what she was doing. The old woman acted as if she had not seen Lu, placing a staff by the side of the door, then looking toward the central beam and bowing twice before hurrying up the stairs. He heard her speaking very joyfully with the young woman, and soon after, her voice turned sorrowful and bitter, followed by loud laughter. Lu took the staff and looked at it, and suddenly it had turned into a piece of hemp rope, just as he sat beneath it. The old woman then came down the stairs, looked again toward the central beam, and bowed. Turning around, she could not find the staff, and only then saw Lu, realizing she had been caught. She said, “This old woman relies on this staff to walk, guest, you must return it.” Lu did not answer. The old woman suddenly transformed into a young and beautiful woman, speaking in a gentle voice and with a pleasant expression to beg him, but Lu still did not answer.

    188

    倏变为厉鬼，目凸口凹，耸肩鼓腹，望陆吹气，则冷风入骨，寒不可当。陆正襟御之。然老妇作气甚难，喘息片时，方能再吹，则不及前之冷甚，三吹则无力矣。陆笑曰：“汝能吹我，我宁不能吹汝？请还汝气。”亦鼓气吹之，则老妇胸腹皆洞，乃连吹之，化为浓血，转眼成灰，腥臭实甚。天已大明，陆不去。俟有叩门者，起应之。一少年入，睹陆而叱曰：“汝何人？敢入我室。”陆知为少妇之夫，语之故，曰：“与尔登楼，且救尔妇。”少年偕陆踏梯，推门不应，肩门而上，则妇悬于床，气犹未绝。共救之苏，问其故，妇曰：“夜有前村之某妪来，忘其为缢鬼。与我言终窭之惨，觉悲甚而泣，继又言伊证仙果，在极乐世界，可以同窥。遂以带作圈，望其中，飞楼画阔，金碧辉煌。不觉探首，则被悬系矣。”少年再拜谢陆曰：“若非足下藏其绳，则我妇作替代，而足下亦难明心迹矣。”自是陆得官渐起，至都阃府而止。

    Explanation

    188

    Suddenly, the old woman transformed into a fierce ghost, with bulging eyes and a sunken mouth, hunched shoulders, and a swollen belly. It looked at Lu and blew air at him, and a cold wind entered his bones, making him shiver uncontrollably. Lu straightened his robe to ward it off. However, it was very difficult for the old woman to blow, and she panted for a short while before being able to blow again. This time, the cold was not as intense, and after three blows, she became powerless. Lu smiled and said, "You can blow on me, but I can also blow on you. Please return your breath to you." He then blew air back at her, and her breast and abdomen both pierced. He continued to blow, and she transformed into a pool of thick blood, which turned to ash in an instant. The stench was truly intense. It was already dawn, but Lu did not leave. He waited for someone to knock on the door and got up to answer it. A young man entered and, upon seeing Lu, shouted, "Who are you? How dare you enter my house?" Lu knew he was the young woman's husband and told him the story, saying, "Come with me to the upper floor, and I will save your wife." The young man climbed the ladder with Lu. They pushed the door, but it did not open. They shouldered the door and climbed up, and there they found the woman hanging from the bed, her breath still not extinguished. They rescued her, and she regained consciousness. She explained that the previous night, an old woman from the neighboring village came, and she forgot that she was a hanged ghost. The old woman talked about her impoverished life, which made her cry. Then, she spoke of her attainment of the fruit of immortality in the Pure Land, where they could all enjoy eternal bliss together. The old woman then made a loop with a rope and showed her the flying palace, which was wide and splendid, with golden and jade decorations. Unconsciously, the young woman stuck her head in, and suddenly, she found herself hanging. The young man bowed twice to thank Lu, saying, "If it were not for you hiding the rope, my wife would have taken the place of the ghost, and you would have found it hard to clarify your intentions." From then on, Lu's career began to rise, and he eventually reached the position of Commandant of the Capital.

    189

    陈生年五十余，犹应童子试。遇刺史公之目不识丁者，开场取士，哄传为幕客所卖。刺史公无以自明，于给卷时留心孤寒者，得陈生，知其老且贫，拔之榜首。幕客以文理平庸争执，刺史公召陈生而谕之曰：“知予置汝前列之意乎？

    Explanation

    189

    Tchen Sheng, who was over fifty years old, still participated in the examination for young scholars. He encountered a governor who was illiterate and selected candidates, leading to rumors that he was influenced by his advisors. The governor, unable to clear his name, took special notice of poor and low-ranking candidates during the distribution of examination papers. He discovered Tchen Sheng, knowing he was old and poor, and selected him as the top candidate. The governor's advisors argued that Tchen Sheng's writing was mediocre. The governor summoned Tchen Sheng and said to him, "Do you understand my reason for placing you at the top?"

    190

    汝文不及人，面穷则独胜，藉汝以息货取之谤，然学使未必怜贫，约计试期，尚有半载，汝须摒一切事，专心文学，以博进取，勿负予一片苦心也。”乃出朱提一笏，以佐膏火。生感激无地，即赴省垣，觅幽静之区，作用功计。适有寄榇之庄，小楼三楹，人迹罕到，赁价甚廉。生得之，闭户奋志，吚唔昼夜已数月矣。一日将昏，闻屋内隔板上作爆声甚厉，生凝目注视，板隙处现人面如钱大，谛审之，则渐巨，以至额顶粱而颐着地。颧阔满板，两目如箕，视生而笑。生胆素壮，乃肃衣冠，再拜而祝曰：“予寒士也，见知于刺史，谬列案元，谅今科一芹可采，若从此连捷，当盛设以祭。请收尊容，勿以骇人是幸。”巨面笑口大开，惟无声，忽墙角伸一小手，未几盈丈，以巨指划大面作羞状，转瞬而没。陈是年入学寻卒，鬼预知其无禄，揶揄之耳。

    Explanation

    190

    Your writing is not up to par with others, but when faced with adversity, you stand out alone. Use you to stop the criticism about wealth, but the examiner may not sympathize with poverty. The estimated examination period is still six months away. You must set aside all other matters, focus on literature, and strive for advancement, do not辜负 my sincere efforts. He then gave a piece of gold to help with expenses. The student was deeply grateful and immediately went to the provincial capital, found a quiet place, and planned to study diligently. There happened to be a place where coffins were stored, a small three-room building, rarely visited by people, and the rent was very cheap. He got it, closed the door, and studied with great determination, reading day and night for several months. One day as dusk approached, he heard a loud explosion on the partition inside the house. He fixed his eyes on it, and a face as big as a coin appeared in the crack, upon closer inspection, it grew larger, until it reached the top of the beam and touched the ground. The cheeks were wide across the board, the eyes like baskets, and they looked at him and smiled. The student, who had always been brave, then put on his formal clothes, bowed twice, and said, "I am a poor scholar, known to the governor, mistakenly listed first in the exam, I believe I can pass this year, if I continue to succeed, I will set up a grand sacrifice. Please take your appearance back, do not scare people." The giant face opened its mouth wide in laughter, but made no sound, suddenly a small hand extended from the corner of the wall, soon it grew to a full foot, and with a large finger, it drew a blush on the big face, and disappeared instantly. Tian was admitted to school that year and soon died, the ghost had foreseen that he would not have a long life, so it mocked him.

    191

    葛生者，屡试不售，纳粟入监，以狂士自居。好饮酒使气，下元节随族众祀墓，食祭余而醉。众皆避去。星月已上，兴犹未尽，盘垣于丛冢间，忽睹西北茂林中，隐隐有三四人席地饮酒，生喜而前，见三男一女，皆沉吟构思，若有所作，然皆非文士。生呼曰：“公等豪兴，容狂生否？”一老者似曾相识，起迎曰：“葛先生来矣，汝等勿班门弄斧，贻笑方家。”众皆拱生入座，酌之，酒饭皆冷。生曰：“公等为诗耶？文耶？某愿领教。”众曰：“鄙人何以能文，欲凑酒令耳。”生请令式，老者指少女曰：“此红姑娘，吾乡名妓。渠所出令，要说一字，拆之则成姓名，合之则成事业。

    Explanation

    191

    A man named Ge Sheng, who had failed in many exams, paid grain to enter the imperial academy and considered himself a mad scholar. He enjoyed drinking and acting on his temper. On the Lower Yuan Festival, he followed his family to worship at their ancestors' graves, ate the leftover offerings, and got drunk. Everyone else left, but his enthusiasm was not yet spent. He lingered in the cluster of graves, and suddenly saw faintly in the northwest dense forest, three or four people drinking on the ground. Ge Sheng was delighted and approached them. He saw three men and one woman, all deep in thought, as if they were working on something, but they were not literary scholars. Ge Sheng called out, "You all seem to be in high spirits. Would you allow a mad scholar to join you?" An old man who seemed familiar stood up and welcomed him, saying, "Mr. Ge has arrived. You all should not show off your skills in front of an expert and make experts laugh." Everyone welcomed him to sit down and served him drinks. The wine and food were cold. Ge Sheng said, "Are you all writing poetry or prose? I wish to learn from you." They replied, "How could we write? We just want to play drinking games." Ge Sheng asked for the rules of the game. The old man pointed to the young woman and said, "This is Hong Guan Niang, a famous courtesan from my hometown. Her rule is to say a word that, when split, forms a name, and when combined, forms a career or achievement."

    192

    须切身分，泛则受罚，故难措耳。”一人曰：“予得之矣：林二小当禁卒。何如？”妓首肯。一人曰：“白七当皂隶。”一人曰：“丘八是兵。”妓皆颔之。生曰：“金同是铜匠。”妓笑曰：“铜臭则有之，匠则不切。”罚以巨觥，生苦思不得，争执前言不谬，妓曰：“敬为先生代。”倩笑曰：“牛一是监生耳。”生大怒挥拳，众皆长啸一声，冷风侵肌，毛发皆竖。生不禁自倒，作猪吼，觉口鼻间渐被填塞，而手足苦不得动。有笑者曰：“红姐给此等文人吃土，在所应得。吾恐填实心孔，将来连金同亦不能道矣。”哄然大笑。生正胀闷欲绝，遥闻火枪声，众始散，幸猎户来扶救之，生始得命，而狂气顿除。

    Explanation

    192

    "Must be closely related to one's status; if it is too general, one will be punished, and thus it is difficult to apply." One person said, "I have figured it out: Lin Er Xiao should be a jailer. How about that?" The courtesan nodded in agreement. Another person said, "Bai Qi should be a low-ranking official." A third person said, "Qiu Ba is a soldier." The courtesan nodded in agreement to all of them. The scholar said, "Jin Tong is a copper smith." The courtesan laughed and said, "There is a copper smell, but not in the sense of being a craftsman." He was punished with a large cup. The scholar struggled to think of it but couldn't, and argued that the previous statement was correct. The courtesan said, "I will respectfully take the place of the gentleman." The courtesan smiled and said, "Niu Yi is just a candidate for the imperial examination." The scholar flew into a rage and swung his fist, and everyone let out a long howl. Cold wind chilled the skin, and hair stood on end. The scholar couldn't help but fall down, making a pig-like sound. He felt his mouth and nose gradually being blocked, and his hands and feet were hard to move. Someone laughed and said, "Hong Jie is giving such literati dirt to eat, which is what they deserve. I fear that it will fill the heart's pores, and in the future, even Jin Tong won't be able to speak." They all burst out laughing. The scholar was swollen and suffocated to the point of death, when he heard the sound of gunfire in the distance. The crowd began to disperse, and fortunately, a hunter came to rescue him. The scholar regained his life, and the mad spirit was suddenly dispelled.

    193

    中表诸行泉者，胆识俱雄，为予家起租赴乡，舟抵其地，时已三鼓。凡收租者，须中夜至佃家，虑其藏匿米谷，使之不及防也。行泉登陆持炬，从者负器执械前行。道由小市，前徒奔回，骇相告曰：“市中有大鬼当道，不能越过。奈何？”行泉哂曰：“此又朱桥镇之伪鬼耳，予人伙矣，何足畏！当为地为除一害。”乃执炬前至其所。鬼身坐楼房，巨足踏地，首当在霄汉间。所衣白袍之前幅，披十余家门面。行泉以炬然之，火直上冲霄，率众急遁，伏于舟中，卒不闻有救焚之声。黎明复至其所，仅存纸灰一大堆而已。楼椽如故，无焦灼痕。或曰：“此魍魉也。”真耶，伪耶，使其真也，则鬼衣无质，乌得有灰？使其伪也，则纸然必及于屋，且此人之身体安在？是诚不可解矣。

    Explanation

    193

    Among those who travel and act as agents for the family, Xingquan is both courageous and insightful. He went to collect rent on behalf of my family and arrived at the countryside by boat, but it was already the third watch of the night. All those who collect rent must arrive at the tenant's house in the middle of the night, to prevent them from hiding grain and making it impossible to catch them unawares. Xingquan disembarked and carried a torch, while his followers carried tools and weapons and proceeded ahead. On the way through a small market, the front runners ran back in fright, telling each other, "There is a large ghost blocking the road in the market, and we cannot pass. What shall we do?" Xingquan smiled and said, "This is just a false ghost from Zhuqiao Town. We are many, what is there to fear! We should eliminate this harm for the place." He then took the torch and went forward to the place. The ghost's body sat on a building, with huge feet on the ground and its head reaching the sky. The front of the white robe covered the fronts of more than ten houses. Xingquan lit the robe with the torch, and the fire shot straight up to the sky. He led the group to flee quickly, hiding in the boat, and finally did not hear any sounds of rescue. At dawn, when they returned to the place, only a pile of paper ashes remained. The beams of the building remained as before, without any signs of burning. Some said, "This is a wailing ghost." Is it true or false? If it is true, then a ghost's robe has no substance, how could it have ashes? If it is false, then burning paper would surely spread to the house, and where is this person's body? This is indeed inexplicable.

    194

    吾乡俞少宰，少时有书呆之目。与同人集课某旷宅，其内室素有缢鬼，白昼现形，人不敢居。有顽仆窃窥之，则未申之间，梁上有绳垂下，悬宕久之，忽结为大圈，渐抽渐小，作爆声而灭。每日不爽。仆约其伙，缚鸡于竿头，由窗隙入其圈套，一收而鸡绝。众哗然奔跑。俞闻之，诘得其故，与同人约，欲以身试之，佥阻不听。次早，命仆粪除，就其梁下设一几一座，俞携文一册入室，闭门高声朗诵，同人伏于窗下，以观其变。

    Explanation

    194

    In my hometown, there was a local official named Yu, who was known in his youth as a "bookworm." He and his colleagues gathered to study in a certain abandoned house, which was said to be haunted by a hanged ghost that appeared during the day, making it too frightening for anyone to stay. One of his stubborn servants secretly observed the phenomenon and noticed that every day between noon and 3 PM, a rope would hang from the ceiling, dangling for a long time before suddenly forming a large circle, gradually shrinking smaller and smaller until it made a popping sound and disappeared. This happened every day without fail. The servant then devised a plan with his friends, tying a chicken to the end of a pole and slipping it through the window into the ghost's trap. Once the rope was pulled, the chicken died. The crowd ran in panic. Yu heard about this and inquired into the matter, discovering the cause. He then made a pact with his colleagues to test the phenomenon himself, but they all tried to stop him, though he would not listen. The next morning, he ordered the servant to clean the room and set up a table and a seat beneath the beam where the rope had appeared. Yu brought a book with him, closed the door, and began to recite aloud. His colleagues hid under the window, watching to see what would happen.

    195

    日晡，俞忽停诵，既而自诉其穷曰，“予为学已久，而不得高捷，家累又重，与其以措大困，不如死休。”遂大哭。同人恐其入迷，正欲相救，俞复大笑曰：“予得鬼矣。”众排闼入视，俞以左手执一灰色鸭，鼓翅不鸣，唤仆以刀来亲宰之，命速烹。谓众曰：“有不食鬼肉者，罚作东道。”乃酌酒共啖，昧不及常鸭而有泥土气。众叩其得鬼之由，俞曰：“予读文俟之不至，故作悲辛，一妇人自梁坠于怀中曰：［先生毋自苦也，肯偕入仙境，则世务不足道矣。］予问仙境何在，妇教以袜带作圈，探首可得。予遂作圈，以套妇人之首，而执持之，转瞬间化为鸭矣。”同人乃赠以号曰“赛锺馗”，又曰“白食鬼”。

    Explanation

    195

    At dusk, Yu suddenly stopped reading and then lamented his predicament, saying, "I have studied for a long time but have not achieved success, and my family burdens me heavily. It is better to die than to suffer as a poor scholar." He then burst into tears. His companions, fearing he might go mad, were about to intervene, but Yu suddenly laughed and said, "I have caught a ghost!" The crowd pushed open the door to see, and Yu held a gray duck in his left hand. The duck flapped its wings but did not make a sound. He called for a servant to bring a knife to personally slaughter it and ordered it to be cooked quickly. He told the crowd, "Anyone who refuses to eat the ghost meat will have to host the meal." They all drank together and ate the duck, which had a taste similar to regular duck but with a hint of earthy flavor. The crowd asked him how he had caught the ghost. Yu replied, "While reading, I waited for something to happen but nothing did, so I became sad. A woman suddenly fell from the beam into my arms and said, 'Do not trouble yourself, Master. If you agree to go to the fairyland with me, then worldly affairs are not worth mentioning.' I asked her where the fairyland was, and she instructed me to make a loop with my sock and tie, then look through it. I did so, put the loop around the woman's head, and held her. In an instant, she turned into a duck." His companions then gave him the nickname "Zhong Kui Rival" and also called him "White Food Ghost."

    196

    有山右孝廉，携其仆名邹仓者，体大而声洪。赴礼闱投邑馆，而人已满，孝廉贫不能他适，哀诸馆人。执事者曰：“后有一宅不利居者，如不得已，请权寓之。”孝廉欣然就宅，与其仆同卧起，然各有戒心，往往秉烛达旦，半月安然无恙。一夜漏三下，门忽自辟，阴气凛然，有美妇入宅，向孝廉流盼微笑。孝廉恐，大呼邹仓，仓于睡中惊觉，高声答应。美妇惶骇，退跌座上，色变气绝。是时，寓客闻声咸集，共睹美妇，作时世装，服饰富丽，俨然人也。公议连座迁于煤室，反关以俟之，如至日中不变，则闻诸官。明日启关入视，空有一座而已。自此宅竟平安。客笑曰：“邹将军之威大矣，其相似者尚足以吓鬼，无怪世之妄人依傍大人先生门户以吓人也。”

    Explanation

    196

    A scholar from Shanxi, holding the title of Xianlian, traveled with his large and loud servant named Zou Cang. They sought lodging at an inn for the metropolitan examination, but the inn was already full. Being poor, the scholar could not afford another place and pleaded with the innkeepers. The manager said, "There is a house that is not suitable for living, but if necessary, you can temporarily stay there." The scholar happily accepted the offer and shared the same room with his servant, though both were cautious. They often stayed up all night with candles, and for half a month, everything remained peaceful. One night, at the third watch, the door opened by itself, and a beautiful woman entered, looking at the scholar and smiling. The scholar was frightened and called out to Zou Cang, who woke up in his sleep and responded loudly. The beautiful woman was terrified, fell back onto the seat, and turned pale and breathless. At that moment, the other guests in the inn gathered and saw the beautiful woman, dressed in fashionable attire, richly adorned, and very human. They decided to move her to the coal room, lock it from the inside, and wait until noon. If she did not change by then, they would report to the officials. The next day, when they opened the door, they found only an empty seat. From then on, the house remained peaceful. The guests laughed and said, "The power of General Zou is great! Even those who resemble him can scare ghosts. It's no wonder that some people rely on powerful figures to intimidate others."

    197

    赵三官者，依缎行作伙，年轻胆壮，闻人言有鬼处则往骂之。其妻父诞期，假主人新袍而往，贺毕饮入醉乡。止宿不听，踏月而归。道出义塚，忽有墙阻路，左右旋转，四面皆然。赵醒悟曰：“旷野何得有墙？其为鬼作祟无疑。”遂拔身佩小刀，戳之则洞，觉墙软甚，闻墙外愀然曰：“壮哉，杀一鬼矣。”复戳之，曰：“勇哉，杀数鬼矣。”连戳无算，而墙竞无矣。踉跄奔回，向家人夸其杀鬼之能。家人曰：“汝何衣袍去而剩短褐归耶？”赵俯视之，其袍之下幅，戳无数孔，竟变为百结鹑衣，始悟鬼所为墙者，即以其袍幅蒙之，谬称勇壮，俾自毁其衣，无以对主人，赵悔恨欲绝，而无可如何也。

    Explanation

    197

    Zhao San Guan, who relied on the silk trade as his livelihood, was young and bold. If people said there was a ghost somewhere, he would go to curse it. On the day his father-in-law was born, he borrowed a new robe from the owner and went to congratulate him. After the celebration, he drank and fell into a drunken stupor. He stayed overnight and listened, then walked home under the moonlight. On the way, he passed by a public cemetery, and suddenly a wall blocked the path. He turned left and right, but the wall surrounded him on all sides. Zhao realized, "How can there be a wall in the open field? It must be the ghost causing mischief." He drew his dagger from his belt and stabbed the wall, making a hole, and felt the wall was very soft. He heard a mournful voice from outside the wall say, "Brave! You have killed one ghost!" He stabbed again, and the voice said, "Brave! You have killed several ghosts!" He stabbed repeatedly countless times, and the wall finally disappeared. He staggered back home and boasted to his family about his ability to kill ghosts. His family asked, "Why did you go with a robe and return with a coarse garment?" Zhao looked down and saw that the lower part of his robe had countless holes and had turned into a ragged bird's nest-like garment. He finally realized that the ghost used his robe to make the wall. He falsely claimed bravery, causing himself to destroy his own clothes. He had no way to face the owner, and Zhao was filled with regret and despair, but there was nothing he could do.

    198

    冀北某武举，好散凌善类，不信鬼神。中年仅有一子，爱同珍宝，暴殇，武举怒登城隍神之戏台，裸体辱骂，观者环堵。未几，超跃下台，狂奔至神前，俯伏如有扑之者，两臀现紫黑色，哀叫不敢。又曰：“罪该远戍，敢不领受。”
    　　
    　　遂起着衣，疾走至家，告其妻曰：“伯爷谓我不敬，予杖一百，充发烟瘴，有押解之鬼使在，速具馔焚帛，毋稍怠。”并取百金，摒挡行李，即日起程，直抵滇南，就有烟瘴处，为人佣工，至今未敢擅离。噫，世之获咎于神明，闻有在疾革时被谴责者，以肉身昭昭遣戍，直闻所未闻。噫，异哉！

    Explanation

    198

    A military scholar from the northern region of Ji, known for his tendency to mistreat good people and his disbelief in ghosts and spirits, had a single son whom he loved as dearly as a treasure. Tragically, the son died suddenly. Overcome with anger, the military scholar ascended the stage of the city god's temple, where he nakedly insulted the deity, drawing a crowd around the area. Soon after, he leaped down from the stage and rushed madly to the deity's presence, falling prostrate as if struck, with both buttocks turning purple-black. He cried out in pain, unable to continue. He then said, "I deserve to be exiled to a distant frontier, how dare I not accept it?"

    He quickly got dressed and hurried home, telling his wife, "The elder god says I am disrespectful. I am to be beaten one hundred times and exiled to a smoky, miasma-filled place. There are escorting spirit messengers here. Quickly prepare food and burn silk, do not slack off." He took a hundred gold pieces, packed his belongings, and set out immediately, traveling directly to the southern part of Yunnan, where he found work as a laborer. He has not dared to leave擅自 (without permission) since then. Alas, in the world, some people incur the wrath of the gods, and it is said that others are rebuked during their final moments of illness. Being punished by the gods with a clear and visible exile, this is indeed a rare and unheard-of occurrence. Alas, how strange!

    199

    道墟章雨北有二子，年甫五六岁，出入中庭，则必各持钱七八枚或十余枚而回。雨北意为伯叔所与，初无疑议，嗣与伯叔言及，皆无给钱事，始询二子。其长者曰：“中庭有乌帽绛袍人，每日早晚必在座，见则与钱。”是晚，雨北嘱其子出，潜窥之，果如所说。突前擒捕，绛袍人怆惶登几，扑入镜屏而灭。捧镜视之，始知即玻璃中所绘之天官像也。毁其镜，有血流出，而怪绝。
    　　
    　　又有蔡大官者，夜眠楼上，三鼓许，闻楼下有唤其名者，起视无人，如是者三。蔡乃卷纸作炬，以大火照之，始见楼梯下一乌帽绛袍人，持时钱二千，掷地而去。蔡惧不敢收。次日下梯，则钱仍在。蔡本博徒，自信为财神所赐，赍入博场，将以求大采也。适官司访拿，连蔡缚去，荷校月余，而其钱亦不知所往。或曰：“章雨北幸觉之早而绝其怪，蔡大官贪多而受祸。假使雨北亦贪得不问，则二子之祸必大。”噫，自来之财尚不可受，而况非分之求？君子于此可以鉴贪。

    Explanation

    199

    Chang Yubei of Daoxu had two sons, both just around five or six years old. Whenever they went in and out of the courtyard, they would each hold a few coins, sometimes seven or eight, or even ten or more, and return. Initially, Chang thought the coins were given to them by his uncles and aunts, and he had no doubts about it. Later, when he mentioned this to his uncles and aunts, they denied giving him any money. He then began to ask his sons. The older son said, "There is a person wearing a black hat and red robe in the courtyard, who appears every morning and evening. Whenever we see him, he gives us money." That evening, Chang instructed his son to go out and secretly observe. Sure enough, as his son had said, the person was there. Chang rushed forward to capture him. The person in the red robe was frightened and climbed onto the table, then plunged into the mirror screen and vanished. When Chang picked up the mirror to look at it, he realized that it was the image of a heavenly official painted on the glass. He broke the mirror, and blood flowed out, and the strange phenomenon ceased.

    There was also a man named Cai Daguang. One night, while sleeping upstairs, he heard someone calling his name from downstairs around the third drum beat. He got up to look, but there was no one there. This happened three times in a row. Cai then rolled up a piece of paper into a torch and lit it with a large flame to investigate. He saw a person wearing a black hat and red robe at the bottom of the stairs, holding several thousand coins, and threw them down before disappearing. Cai was afraid and did not dare to pick them up. The next day when he descended the stairs, the coins were still there. Cai was a gambling man and believed the coins were a gift from the god of wealth. He took them to the gambling place, intending to use them to win big. Just as he arrived, officials came to arrest him, and he was taken away along with others, carrying a枷 for over a month, and the coins also disappeared. Some said, "Chang Yubei was fortunate to discover the strange phenomenon early and stop it, while Cai Daguang, being greedy, suffered the consequences. If Chang had also been greedy and ignored the warning, the disaster for his two sons would have been great." Ah, unexpected wealth is already something one should not accept, let alone unreasonable desires! Gentlemen can learn from this to avoid greed.

    200

    会稽张孝廉三雨，寓京师。由扬淑胡同迁黄罴胡同，眷属下车之际，其婶氏忽作男子状，高视阔步而行，且三雨呼曰：“来，予与尔言，知尔丙子获售，予在阴曹亦增光彩，尚不忝为予子，其勉为勤学好善之士，毋自满及轻薄改行，则功名有在。”三雨闻之，居然其亡父声音笑貌，涕泣而拜，婶狂笑曰：“父子相聚，大喜事也，何悲为？予饥矣！”其具馔以进。索巨觥饮酒，大啖鱼肉，饭亦倍常食。毕，出后院似欲小遗而仆。众扶之入，渐苏，则为婶氏之言，问之，不自知矣。

    Explanation

    200

    Zhang San Yu, a scholar-official from Kuaiji, resided in the capital. When his family moved from Yangshu Hutong to Huangbi Hutong, as they were getting out of the carriage, his aunt suddenly appeared in a man's form, looking up high and walking with a broad stride. She called out to him, saying, "Come, I have something to tell you. I know you will succeed in the year Bingzi. Even in the underworld, I will gain more glory. Do not disgrace yourself as my son. Strive to be a diligent and virtuous person, do not become arrogant or change your ways lightly, and there will be success and fame." Zhang San Yu heard this and recognized it as the voice and appearance of his deceased father, and he cried and bowed. His aunt laughed wildly and said, "The joy of family reunion is a great event, why be sad? I am hungry!" She then prepared food and served it. She requested a large cup of wine, ate a lot of fish and meat, and consumed twice the usual amount of food. After finishing, she left the back courtyard as if to urinate and then fainted. The others helped her back inside, and she gradually regained consciousness. When asked, she did not remember what had happened.

    201

    又越人某，二十余岁而亡。逾年，其家婢出买物，忽大步登堂，对其主母揖曰：“嫂一载相别，身其康宁？”嫂骇然曰：“汝中邪耶？”笑曰：“毋恐，吾尔叔也。”据客位坐，语操南音，曰：“大哥好心，欲令五弟归吾榇。但五弟非人，不可托，不如附粮艘为便。”此事仅夫妇有私议，外人无知者。嫂问曰：“叔在阴司作何事业？”对曰：“吾生平无过，城隍神录吾为吏，职司考察，每闻土神报大哥作好事，则吾欢欣踊跃。报坏事，则吾寝食不安。

    Explanation

    201

    Another man from Yue, still in his twenties, died. A year later, one of his household servants went out to buy things, when suddenly she strode quickly into the hall and bowed to the mistress, saying, “Sister, have you been well these past twelve months?” The mistress was startled and asked, “Have you been bewitched?” The servant smiled and said, “Do not be afraid—I am your younger brother.” She sat in the guest’s seat and spoke with a southern accent, saying, “Big Brother has good intentions and wishes for Fifth Brother to return with my coffin. But Fifth Brother is no longer human, and cannot be entrusted with this matter. It would be better to attach the coffin to a grain boat for transport.” This matter was discussed only in private between the husband and wife; no one else knew of it. The mistress asked, “What do you do in the underworld?” He replied, “I have committed no wrongdoing in my lifetime, and the city god has recorded me as an official, responsible for examination. Every time the earth god reports that Big Brother has done good deeds, I am filled with joy. But if the earth god reports that he has done evil, I find myself unable to eat or sleep.”

    202

    嫂须劝大哥多积阴功，自然逢吉。”嫂指其幼子曰：“大哥仅有此儿，多病而弱，叔既神灵，祈默佑之。”笑曰：“天道无私，积德荫子孙，自然之理也。吾何能为？”又曰：“吾屋漏矣，凄风冷雨触骸甚苦，祈嫂修之。吾为此事恒欲相告，不得其便，今因公过门，附婢暂见，不可久留。”遂起，出门既跌，复起，则仍婢也。遣使视其棺，果有小孔，为补之加漆。是冬其兄在外，不知前事，以千金属其五弟回南贸易，便带灵柩。路由京师，为优伶所迷，挥金立尽，货其行囊而逸，榇则弃于旷野，未悉后能归否。

    Explanation

    202

    The sister-in-law should advise the elder brother to accumulate much merit, and naturally he will encounter good fortune.
