She pointed to her young son and said, "Elder brother has only this son, who is often ill and weak. Since the uncle is already a spirit, please silently protect him." He smiled and said, "Heaven's way is impartial, accumulating virtue benefits descendants, it is a natural principle. What can I do?" He then said, "My coffin is leaking, the cold wind and rain touch the bones painfully. Please sister-in-law repair it. I have always wanted to tell you about this matter, but never had the opportunity. Now because you are passing by, I will temporarily appear as a maid, but cannot stay long." He then got up, but fell when he went out, and then got up again, and it was still the maid. He sent someone to check the coffin, and there was indeed a small hole, which was repaired and painted. That winter, the elder brother was outside and did not know the previous events, he gave a thousand taels of silver to his fifth younger brother to return south for trade, and took the coffin with him. On the way through the capital, he was entranced by actors and spent all his money quickly, sold his belongings and fled, leaving the coffin abandoned in the wilderness, it is unknown whether it will ever return.

    203

    又沈咸，钱塘人，自少应童子试不售，郁郁至五十余岁而卒。送殓之客盈门，其妻抚尸号泣，仆而复起，大步出堂，遍拜宾客，慰劳再三，内有闻人孝廉者，是其生平好友，执手嘱曰：“我之后事，阁下任之。我之神主，阁下点之。”家资何存，妻子何度，絮絮不休。客始知为咸之魂附其妻也。孝廉曰：“阁下肉身尚在，何不附之？”而生乃手指孝廉而哂曰：“不通不通，人之所以得生者，气血耳。

    Explanation

    203

    Se Shen, a native of Qiantang, had taken the examination for young scholars since his childhood but failed to pass. He lived a life of dissatisfaction and died when he was over fifty years old. The hall was filled with guests coming to send him to his funeral. His wife touched his body and cried loudly, fell down and got up again, took large steps out of the hall, greeted everyone, and comforted and encouraged them repeatedly. Among the guests was Mr. Wenren, a respected scholar and a lifelong friend of Se Shen, who held her hand and said, "Take charge of my afterlife matters. Perform the rituals for my spirit tablet." The wife asked, "Where are our family assets? How will we live?" She spoke incessantly. The guests then realized that the spirit of Se Shen was attached to his wife. The scholar said, "Not understanding, not understanding, the reason why people can be alive is due to vital energy and blood."

    204

    此身气绝血凝，何以能生？”孝廉曰：“我闻死则魂入阴司，阁下何不一行？”又哂曰：“阴司何在？我自病革后，魂与体离，飘飘然无所归，只闻妻哭过悲，欲前慰之，不觉合而为一，然支持甚苦，刻刻欲分，想不能久住矣。”语毕而仆，扶之入帏，则仍妻也。传曰：“鬼有所归，乃不为厉。”三人者或远离庐墓，或未正首邱，或神魂失据，咸有为厉之端者。然宇宙之宽，群黎之众，死者日以千计，其中不得其所者不知几几，何绝无影响。或曰：“使死者人人附生者而言，亦属寻常事矣，奚烦足下记之？”对曰：“唯。”

    Explanation

    204

    This body, with its breath cut off and blood congealed, how could I possibly live?" The Scholar-official said, "I have heard that after death, the soul enters the underworld; why don't you go there?" Another one laughed and said, "Where is the underworld? Since my illness worsened, my soul has separated from my body, wandering aimlessly without a place to go. I only heard my wife weeping in sorrow, wanting to comfort her, and unconsciously merged back into one, but the strain is unbearable, and I am constantly on the verge of separating again. I suppose I cannot stay for long." After finishing his words, he fell down, and when he was helped into the bed, it was still his wife. The saying goes, "If a ghost has a place to return to, it will not become malevolent." These three people, either far from their ancestral graves or not properly settled in their original resting place, or their spirits and souls lost, all have the potential to become malevolent. Yet, the universe is vast, and the common people are numerous; every day, thousands die, and among them, those who do not find their proper place are countless. Why is there no trace of their presence? Someone said, "If the dead could all speak through the living, it would be an ordinary matter; why bother you to record it?" The response was, "Indeed."

    205

    孝女
    　　
    　　国家设官分职，佐朝廷以安养百姓者也。乃竟有不然者，莫如前朝仕宦之强争，以鱼肉乡愚为胜，故有宁使官家放火，不许百姓点灯之谣。即如江左某巨室，叔侄相继为宰辅，子弟之职居清要者数十人，天下名公巨乡半出其门下，雷令风行，极一时之盛。是应绵祖德报君恩，以长享富贵耳。畴知其弟某学士，因疾家居，伺应之强奴悍仆以千计，分班值日，统于老奴。其放班闲旷之时，成群结伙，占据于茶坊酒肆，搅扰不休。

    Explanation

    205

    孝女

    The state appoints officials and assigns duties to assist the court in ensuring the well-being of the people. Yet, there are those who do not follow this path. None are more so than the fierce competition among officials in the previous dynasty, who treated the ignorant villagers as fish and meat, and thus gave rise to the saying, "Rather let the government set fire, than allow the people to light lamps." For instance, a prominent family in the Jiangnan region had uncles and nephews successively become prime ministers, with dozens of their descendants holding important positions. Half of the eminent officials and influential families in the country came from their family, and their orders were enforced with the speed and force of thunder and wind, reaching the peak of prosperity in their time. This should have been a continuation of the virtue of ancestors and a repayment of the grace of the emperor, to enjoy long-lasting wealth and honor. Who knew that one of his scholars, his younger brother, because of illness, stayed at home, with thousands of strong and fierce servants and slaves assigned to serve him, divided into shifts and managed by an old servant. During their off-duty and idle times, they gathered in groups, occupying tea houses and taverns, disturbing continuously.

    206

    有何姓者，携其妻女在宦宅之左近开设酒接为业，其悍仆日来饮馔，不但逋欠者多，且一纵性逞兴，则号呼达旦，何厌苦之，以酒尽为辞。诸仆大怒，群搜其室，得酒数甕，叱问曰：“此非酒耶？奈何欺我？”何方强辩，一醉仆举甕以击何首，立碎之，漫骂而归。谋诸老奴，告主人以房佃何某不给赁值，使仆索取，伊触首于墙，思饰伤以抵赖，不意力猛自戕，请杂治之。主人从其言，使鸣诸县尹，甫闻何妻之报，又得宦词，草草验讯，竟以自戕定谳，反欲追租完案。何妻茹痛携女，遁于省垣，含冤莫诉。

    Explanation

    206

    A man surnamed He, along with his wife and daughter, opened a tavern near the residence of an official. His fierce servant came daily to drink and eat, not only owing him money but also, once he let his temper run wild, shouting and calling until dawn. He was very tired of it and used the excuse that the wine was finished. The other servants were very angry and all searched his house, finding several jars of wine. They shouted and asked, "Is this not wine? Why are you deceiving us?" He tried to argue strongly, but one drunk servant picked up a jar and hit He on the head, immediately breaking it, and cursed and went back. He consulted with the old servant, who reported to the landlord that tenant He did not pay the rent and sent the servants to collect. He hit his head against the wall, thinking to fake an injury to avoid responsibility, but unintentionally injured himself severely. He requested a mixed punishment. The landlord followed his advice and sent a complaint to the county magistrate. Just as he heard about He's wife's report, he also received a report from the official. He hastily examined and questioned, ultimately deciding it was a suicide, instead wanting to collect the rent to close the case. He's wife endured pain and took her daughter, fleeing to the provincial capital, harboring her grievance and unable to complain.

    207

    女渐长成，姿容艳丽，远近莫不知其美者。恒与母言及父仇，则恸不欲生。自名曰“仇”，志不忘也。及笄之年，媒媪造门，问字者踵相接。其母曰：“我女自幼矢志，无论小家巨族，为富为贫，为妻为妾，只须能击强宗，戮悍仆，与父复仇者，以身事之，毋需纳聘。”闻其言者皆咋舌。时有分镇大将军，宗室也，值社会之期偶出，见女艳羡之，谋诸从官，探得其故以告。将军曰：“孝女也，吾当成其志。”密陈于帝，帝怒，遣缇骑提学士问实，籍没其家，谪戍边陲，而置奴仆于大辟。女请得其仇首以祭，偕母投将军，愿长为侍婢以报。

    Explanation

    207

    The girl gradually grew up, with a beautiful appearance, and her beauty was known far and wide. Whenever she spoke with her mother about her father's grudge, she would be so grief-stricken as to not want to live. She named herself "Chou," determined not to forget. At the age of fifteen, matchmakers came to the door, and those asking for her hand in marriage followed one another. Her mother said, "My daughter has been resolute since childhood; regardless of whether they are from humble or noble families, rich or poor, as wife or concubine, only those who can strike down powerful clans, kill fierce servants, and avenge her father would be worthy of her service, without the need for a dowry." Those who heard her words were all speechless. At that time, there was a general who divided the command, a member of the imperial clan, who happened to see the girl on a social occasion and admired her. He consulted his attendants, who investigated and reported back. The general said, "She is a filial daughter; I will fulfill her wish." He secretly reported to the emperor, who was angry and sent imperial guards to investigate. The family's property was confiscated, and they were exiled to the frontier, while the servants were put to death. The girl requested to receive the head of her enemy for sacrifice, and together with her mother, she went to the general, willing to serve as a long-term servant in return.

    208

    义仆
    　　
    　　江左望族某氏，其父为太守，贿赂公行，纳资至数十万之富，犹孳孽搜求。有司阍之仆高禄者，恒劝谏其主曰：“非义之财，悖入者必悖出，况膝下仅有一公子，若恃其富有，则志气纵佚，恐不得上达矣。”太守心是之，然不能从也。未几太守夫妇相继卒，禄护其眷属回籍，少君骄甚，禄裁之以礼，少君怒，立逐之。
    　　
    　　自禄去后，益无忌惮，日挟千金，觅局纵博，不尽不归。于是为利之徒，从而诱引之，不数年家财罄尽，货及房产，万金之业，甫书契券，不及转手，一掷而去。渐及衣物，又数月而空诸所有。家人星散，即其相与之淫朋昵友，亦引避不遑，遂与匪徒为伍。登城席地之局，犹日为之。不觉囊空爨绝，仰屋而嗟。其少夫人，江南宦室之女，美而贤，惟拘以顺为正之义，从无反目事。

    Explanation

    208

    A Righteous Servant

    In the prominent Jiangnan family of a certain clan, the father served as a magistrate, openly engaging in bribery and accumulating wealth to the tune of hundreds of thousands. Despite his riches, he continued to seek more. There was a servant named Gao Lu, who served as a gatekeeper, and he often advised his master, saying, "Ill-gotten gains will eventually be lost. Moreover, you have only one son; if you rely on your wealth, his spirit will become reckless and dissolute, and he may not achieve great things." The magistrate agreed with his servant's words but could not act on them. Soon after, the magistrate and his wife passed away in succession. Gao Lu escorted their family back to their hometown. The young master was extremely arrogant, and Gao Lu tried to discipline him with propriety. The young master became angry and immediately dismissed him.

    After Gao Lu left, the young master grew even more reckless. He would carry thousands of gold pieces each day, looking for gambling venues and playing until he lost everything. Thus, those who sought profit led him astray, and within a few years, his family wealth was depleted. His movable and immovable property, worth tens of thousands of gold, was quickly transferred through contracts, but he could not hold onto it, and it was all lost in a single gamble. Gradually, even his clothes were gone, and within a few months, he had nothing left. His family scattered, and even his debauched friends and close associates fled without hesitation. He then associated with criminals. He still engaged in gambling sessions on the city walls every day. Unaware of his dire situation, he looked up at the roof and sighed. His young wife, a beautiful and virtuous woman from an official family in Jiangnan, adhered strictly to the principle of being compliant and correct, and there was never any conflict between them.

    209

    是时牛衣对泣，正无可如何间，有匪徒索逋登门，瞥见少夫人，谓少君曰：“尔家有如许大财，犹诈贫耶？”少君曰：“无之。”匪徒笑挽之出曰：“适所见之艳者，是尔妻耶？”少君曰：“是也。”曰：“是值千金，能舍之，则不患无资。”少君叱曰：“是何言欤！我纵贫窭，为一邑之臣族，非但不能卖妻，亦谁敢娶之哉？”匪哂曰：“呆哉，有此尤物，既不能自活，又不以与人，同为饿殍，奚济于事？若载往远方，则苏扬妓室，必重价相求，诓而货之，其谁得而闻哉？况尔得此身价，不仅宿逋顿清，依然裘马翩翩之佳公子矣。

    Explanation

    209

    At the time, the couple was weeping in their cowhide cloak, utterly helpless. Suddenly, a bandit came to demand payment of a debt, and upon seeing the young lady, he said to the young master, "Your family has such great wealth, yet you pretend to be poor?" The young master replied, "No, we do not." The bandit laughed and pulled him aside, saying, "The beautiful woman we just saw, is she your wife?" The young master answered, "Yes, she is." The bandit continued, "She is worth a thousand gold pieces. If you sell her, you won't have to worry about lacking money." The young master scolded, "What are you saying! Even if I am poor, as a member of a respected family in the county, I cannot sell my wife, nor would anyone dare to marry her!" The bandit sneered, "How foolish! With such a beautiful woman, you cannot live on your own, nor can you give her to others. You will both starve to death together—what good does that do? If you take her to a distant place, the brothels in Suzhou and Yangzhou will certainly offer a high price for her. You can deceive and sell her, and who would know? Moreover, you will gain this fortune, not only clearing all your debts at once, but still living the life of a handsome young man with a luxurious lifestyle."

    210

    方知我辈之深谋远虑也。”少君踌躇不忍，群匪力赞之，意遂决。归谓其妻曰：“升斗难谋，何以度日？闻汝兄弟有告养亲回者，我与汝投之，必能相济。”少夫人愿见其母，欣然从之。遂假资匪徒，买舟赴吴下，匪徒已先至妓室，宣扬少妇之色超前绝后，可冠古今。鸨姥乐从，匪曰：“是犹良家妇，不可直告以故，须设法觐之，诱入汝家，则惟命是从矣。

    Explanation

    210

    Now we understand the depth of our planning and foresight. The young man hesitated, reluctant to proceed, but the bandits strongly supported him, and the decision was finally made. Returning home, he told his wife, "It is hard to make a living with a meager income, how can we survive? I heard that your brothers have returned to support their parents. I will join you, and we will surely help each other." The young lady wished to see her mother and happily agreed. He then borrowed money from the bandits, bought a boat, and set off for Wu. The bandits had already arrived at the brothel and spread the news that the young lady's beauty surpassed all others, both past and present, and could be crowned the best of all ages. The madam was happy to comply. The bandit said, "She is still a good woman, we cannot tell her the truth directly. We must devise a way to meet her, lure her into your house, and then she will do as you say."

    211

    ”匪与鸨姥伪作仆妇，传太夫人命，遣来慰问者，见妇姿容果如所说，邀少君去，以五百金立券，备衣饰，遣婢女，以舆迎之。至则门第轩昂，居然大家。登堂入室，奉有白眉神像，命之拜。少夫人觉有异，问太夫人所在，鸨姥笑曰：“尔已为本夫所售作我家新妇，我即尔母，何来太夫人耶？”少夫人悟入骗局，忿不顾身，以首触墙，只求速毙，幸人众拦拒之，得勿伤。鸨姥怒喝健妇五六人，拥之入后楼，尽褫其衣裳，以巨索反缚手足，悬于梁，鞭挞无算，体无完肤，训之曰：“从则锦衣玉食，惟尔所欲。否则日三百鞭，求死未得。”少夫人悲啼不答，日夜不绝声，勺饮不入口。鸨姥无可如何。

    Explanation

    211

    A woman, not a servant but pretending to be one, was sent by a madam posing as a messenger of the "太夫人" (a respected lady) to offer condolences. Upon seeing the woman's appearance, which matched the description, she was invited to leave with the promise of five hundred gold pieces, a contract, prepared clothing and ornaments, and a maid to accompany her in a carriage. Upon arrival, the family was found to be imposing and prominent. She was then brought into the innermost room, where a statue of a god with white eyebrows was presented, and she was ordered to worship it. The young wife, realizing something was amiss, asked where the "太夫人" was. The madam laughed and said, "You have already been sold by your husband to be my new wife; I am your mother. Where is the '太夫人'?" The young wife realized she had fallen into a fraud, and in her anger, she struck her head against the wall, seeking only a quick death. Fortunately, many people intervened and prevented her from injuring herself. The madam then shouted angrily at five or six strong women, who dragged her into the back building, stripped her of all her clothing, bound her hands and feet tightly with a large rope, and hung her from a beam. She was whipped countless times until her body had no intact skin. The madam instructed her, saying, "If you obey, you will have silk robes and delicate food, whatever you desire. Otherwise, you will be whipped three hundred times daily, and you will seek death but not obtain it." The young wife cried sadly and did not respond, her crying did not stop day and night, and she refused to drink even a spoonful of water. The madam was left with no choice.

    212

    适其楼之东有参业主人，闻其邻哭声哀恸，旦夕不得宁，心为不平。乃召鸨姥责之曰：“吾知汝家勒良妇为娼妓，不从则威胁之，事本不可，今更忍心害理，惨不可闻。若有伤人命，势必累及邻人，汝不改过，吾当预鸣诸官矣。”鸨姥谢过，且曰：“妇人水性杨花，未有不可教训者。今此妇铁石居心，实所罕觏。”主人悯其贞烈，愿见之。鸨姥引至楼上，望见颜色，大骇而退，曰：“吾给汝值，此妇归吾可也。”鸨姥敢不从命，使数人扶而去之。

    Explanation

    212

    A businessman living to the east of the building heard the mournful crying of his neighbor, who could not rest day or night, and his heart was filled with indignation. He then summoned the madam and scolded her, saying, "I know that you force virtuous women to become prostitutes, and if they do not comply, you threaten them. The matter itself is not right, and now you act with cruelty and violate reason, so sad that it is unbearable. If there is any harm to human life, it will inevitably affect the neighbors. If you do not repent, I will report to the officials in advance." The madam apologized and said, "Women are fickle, and there is none who cannot be taught. Now this woman has a heart of iron and stone, and it is rarely seen." The owner pitied her chastity and bravery and wanted to see her. The madam led her to the upper floor, and when he saw her appearance, he was greatly frightened and stepped back, saying, "I will give you money, and this woman can be given to me." The madam dare not disobey and sent several people to help and take her away.

    213

    是时少夫人手足痹弱，不能自主。比及门，主人使健婢负之入，设座于中庭，纳少夫人上座，主人具衣冠，率妻妾子女再拜，跪而告曰：“老奴数载睽违，讵知少夫人受此大辱，奴罪通天矣。”少夫人熟视之，始识主人为高禄。悲噎而语之故，禄亦挥泪曰：“奴早知少主之不能成立，不意其败类至此，奴闻大舅爷督学滇南，二四两舅爷供职词林，太夫人在都中康乐，讵知少夫人若是窘辱耶？奴当先遣儿辈入都报闻，然后奴夫妇亲进少夫人归宁可也。”遂供奉于高禄之家。

    Explanation

    213

    At that time, the young lady had paralysis or weakness in her limbs and could not move independently. By the time they reached the door, the host sent a strong servant to carry her in. He set up a seat in the courtyard and placed the young lady in the main seat. The host, dressed in formal attire, led his wives, concubines, and children to bow twice, then knelt and said, "This humble servant has been away for several years, how could I know that the young lady suffered such great humiliation; my sin is as high as the sky." The young lady looked at him closely and recognized the host as High禄. She spoke with sobs about the reason, and High禄 also shed tears and said, "I knew early on that the young master would not succeed, did not expect him to be so corrupt. I heard that the eldest uncle is supervising education in Yunnan, the second and fourth uncles are serving in the literary bureau, and the great lady is healthy and happy in the capital. How could I know that the young lady would suffer such humiliation? I should first send my sons to report to the capital, then my wife and I will personally escort the young lady back to her parents' home." And thus she was cared for in High禄's household.

    214

    惟少君卖妻之赀不久又罄，怨及匪徒，匪曰：“尔妻此际想必名压花街，宠专金屋矣，乘其盛时，我等偕往妓室，以买良为贱，诈彼假母，不惧不发财也。”从之。鸨姥正怒无可迁之际，闻若辈言，操杖逐之。匪挟少君鸣于县，其大令为太守之门人，见词骇然，正欲勘其事，适高禄闻之，夜见大令，达其情曰：“少主来，不可使知之，恐搅扰不已，则少主母与小人皆不得安其所矣。”遂出四百金，请以官项断与少主，劝令改过谋生，尚可为也。大令义之，从其言，传少君与匪至，重惩匪徒，与少君金而教之迁善。禄始送少夫人入都，依其兄弟以终。

    Explanation

    214

    The young master, having soon exhausted the money he earned from selling his wife, resented the bandits. The bandits said, "Your wife must now be famous in the brothel district, enjoying exclusive favor in a golden house. Let us take advantage of her peak popularity, go together to the brothel, and buy her as a common woman, deceiving her fake mother, without fear of not getting rich." He followed their plan. The madam of the brothel, just angry and with nowhere to vent her anger, heard what they said, took a stick, and chased them away. The bandits took the young master to report the matter to the county. The magistrate, a disciple of the governor, saw the complaint and was shocked. He was about to investigate the matter when High Salary, hearing about it, visited the magistrate at night and explained the situation, saying, "The young master is coming; we must not let him know, for fear of endless disturbances. Then the young master's mother and I would not be able to stay in our places." The magistrate then took out four hundred gold pieces, asked to use official funds to give to the young master, and advised him to reform and find a livelihood, which still could be done. The magistrate found it right and followed his advice, having the young master and the bandits brought, severely punishing the bandits, and giving the young master money to teach him to change for the better. High Salary then took the young lady to the capital, where she stayed with her brothers until the end.

    215

    芗厈曰：嗟乎，孝也而在女，义也而在仆。彼俨然人上者，反以不孝不义成其名。此古今之变局，亦运会之迁移，所以明季缙绅为闯贼拷掠者，自取之也。
    　　
    　　刘大汉
    　　
    　　越人陆客，道经山左，见有身长一十二尺之老叟，首大十围，腰圆两抱，拳如巨钵，腿若堂柱，驱牍十余头，以三犁并耨，往来甚驶。陆异之，值山雨欲来，趋避树下以观。见壮者担觔饼八枚，牛脯一甕来饷，叟亦止树下食之，顷刻一空。陆更骇异，询其姓氏年岁，叟曰：“我生之初，于今两周甲子又五年矣。刘姓，无名，人呼我大汉，我漫应之。祖居是邑，世为农夫，有地一十八顷，耕以自给。子六人，今存其半，饷我者为曾孙。我日必耕地十余亩，以舒筋骨，否则体使不快。惟衣食甚费，一短褐需大布五匹，中衣三匹，袜犹半匹也。两餐需面二百五十六两，牛肉及白酒各三百二十两，如是而已，无他能也。

    Explanation

    215

    Xiang Yan said: Alas, filial piety is expected from a woman, and righteousness from a servant. Those who appear to be above others, instead, gain their reputation through being unfilial and unrighteous. This is a change in the circumstances of the past and present, as well as the shifting of fate. Therefore, during the late Ming dynasty, officials were tortured by rebels, which was self-inflicted.

    Dong Da Han

    A traveler from the south, Lu Ke, passed through the eastern part of Shandong province and saw an elderly man who was twelve feet tall, with a head ten times the circumference of a normal person, a waist as thick as two embraces, hands as large as giant bowls, and legs like pillars. He drove ten oxen and used three plows simultaneously, moving very quickly. Lu Ke found this remarkable and sought shelter under a tree to observe. He saw a strong man carrying eight pounds of cake and a jar of beef to feed the elderly man, who also stopped under the tree to eat, finishing it all in an instant. Lu Ke was even more astonished and asked about the elderly man's name and age. The elderly man replied, "When I was born, it has been two cycles of sixty years and five more years. My surname is Liu, but I have no given name. People call me Da Han, and I casually respond. My ancestors lived in this town, and my family has been farmers for generations. We have eighteen hectares of land, which we cultivate for our own sustenance. I have six children, and half of them are still alive, with the one who feeds me being my great-grandson. Every day, I must till more than ten acres of land to stretch my muscles and bones; otherwise, my body will feel uncomfortable. My food and clothing are very expensive; one short robe requires five bolts of coarse cloth, three bolts for undergarments, and half a bolt for socks. For two meals, I need two hundred and fifty-six pounds of flour, three hundred and twenty pounds of beef, and three hundred and twenty pounds of white wine. That is all, and I have no other abilities."

    216

    ”问其力，则曰：“我不自知，曾忆壮时皇帝东巡过此，山下有人熊冲驾而来，身与我等。侍卫之官挡之者，被摘心裂腹而啖。驾在危急，我不觉直前擒之，倒摔于地，而熊首碎竟毙。皇帝甚喜，欲予我一官，我对日，［小人野田愚民，文不识诗书，武不知战阵，何敢滥膺爵禄以误朝廷？］皇帝益喜，给我一纸书，知之者称之曰诏谕，地方官免我地粮，故至今无催科之扰。”乃出其诏书与客观，信不诬也。遥指其村落曰：“此皆我子孙之宅，昔我以一手托梁，一手植柱，身自创者。”客望之，虽俱土室，亦甚高大。客计其寿，时已一百二十五岁，力则万夫莫敌矣。归而告人，或疑其妄，然客诚朴人也，言当不谬。
    　　
    　　芗厈日，防风之骨可专车，长狄之身横九亩，巨人自古有之，不足奇。所可异者，富寿而多裔，立功而不居，惟圣人之邦，斯有此贤隐士耳。

    Explanation

    216

    "Asked about his strength, he replied: 'I do not know myself. Once I recalled a time in my youth when the emperor passed through here on his eastern tour. A man-bear, the size of myself, rushed towards the carriage from the mountain. The guards who tried to stop it were torn apart and eaten. The carriage was in danger, and unconsciously, I rushed forward to capture it, throwing it down to the ground, and its head was crushed and it died. The emperor was very pleased and wanted to give me an official position. I said to him, "I am a lowly rustic fool, ignorant of poetry and books, and unversed in warfare. How dare I accept a title and salary that would mislead the court?" The emperor was even more pleased and gave me a document, which those who know it call an imperial decree. Local officials exempted me from land taxes, so I have no trouble with tax collectors to this day.' He then showed the decree to the observer, confirming its truth. Pointing to his village from afar, he said, 'These are all the homes of my descendants. In the past, I held up a beam with one hand and planted a pillar with the other, building it myself.' The guest looked at the village, noting that although they were all earthen houses, they were also very large. Calculating his age, the guest found he was already 125 years old, and his strength was unmatched by thousands of men. Returning and telling others, some doubted it was false, but the guest is a sincere and simple person, and his words should not be mistaken.

    In ancient times, the bones of Fangfeng could fill a cart, and the body of Changdi spanned nine acres; giants have existed since ancient times, and it is not surprising. What is remarkable is that he is wealthy, long-lived, and has many descendants, having achieved great deeds but not seeking recognition. Only in a land of sages can such a virtuous recluse be found."

    217

    方幼樗云：论语古音古节，绝妙文字，唐人说部不是过也。
    　　
    　　俞生
    　　
    　　俞某，庠生也，即少宰公之裔，训读为业，馆于乡。夏日因事回家，夜与伯叔兄弟纳凉门外，纵谈而倦，不觉昏睡。漏三下，众已散，家人唤之醒，当街小遗，朦胧间觉灯光一闪，察之实无。入室卧，见二役持符来，以铁索锁之行。俞怒曰：“予生员也，纵有事在官，亦不得擅加刑具。”役哂曰：“既属儒生，犹不自悟，与至死不悔之下愚等耳。姑不与尔较，尔其见堂上者辩之。”俞无奈随之行，入城隍庙，忽睹灯烛辉煌，两行吏役约数百人，肃然就列，无敢哗者。一役先入跪禀，传呼带俞生入。一役拧链报门，牵至阶下叱跪，俞怦怦然，莫知其故，窃窥上坐者，金冕龙衮，色甚怒，诘俞曰：“汝身列黉宫，作民表率，何得背违礼法，当道而溺，污吾仪仗？”俞曰：“阴阳间隔，焉知神来？不知而罪之，恐非圣意。

    Explanation

    217

    方幼樗云：The Analects of ancient pronunciation and rhythm are exquisite writing, and Tang Dynasty narrative literature is no exaggeration in its praise.

    俞生
    俞某, a scholar, is the descendant of a former vice-premier, and works as a private tutor in the countryside. One summer, he returned home for an errand and spent the night talking with his uncles and brothers outside the gate, relaxing in the cool evening air. They talked until they were tired and fell asleep. When the third watch bell rang, everyone had dispersed, and his family woke him up. He urinated in the street, and in his drowsy state, he saw a flash of light, but upon closer inspection, there was nothing there. He went back inside to sleep when he saw two officials with official documents come to take him away, chaining him and leading him away. "I am a scholar," he said angrily. "Even if there is something in court, I should not be subjected to such punishment." The official sneered and said, "As a scholar, you still do not understand, and you are like the foolish ones who never repent until their death. I will not argue with you, but you must go to the main hall to argue your case." Helpless, he followed the officials to the city god temple, where he suddenly saw the hall lit up with brilliant lights, and about a hundred officials and servants lined up solemnly, none daring to make a sound. One official went in and knelt to report, and another official unlocked the chain and led him into the hall. The official ordered him to kneel at the bottom of the stairs, and he felt his heart pounding, not knowing what had happened. He secretly looked at the person sitting on the throne, who was wearing a golden crown and a dragon robe, looking very angry. The person questioned him, "You are a scholar in the imperial academy, a role model for the people. How could you violate the laws and regulations by urinating in the street, desecrating my ceremonial procession?" "There is a boundary between the living and the dead," he replied. "How would I know that a deity would come? To punish me for something I did not know, I fear it is not the will of the sage."

    218

    ”神日。“姑无论神明，治夜随在有之。汝为儒士，暗室屋漏犹当谨慎，岂可放肆于大道之旁乎？假令有妇女来，将以汝为大恶不道矣。”俞曰：“亦无论半夜之中，妇女行走者罕，即或有之，必男子护送，持灯前导，生焉得不避？”神曰：“汝家对面隔河，周环水阔，炎暑之际，妇女坐卧其中者不少，汝不知避嫌，乌得无罪？”俞词穷谢过，神叱杖之，倏见其亡父踉跄而入，叩首无算，告曰：“乞衰老朽生前无过，仅有此不肖子，今获罪神明，杖所应得。第闻一经阴板，则终身运败，老朽将为若敖之鬼矣，敢求怜而恕之。”神曰：“罪不可恕，为尔发阳官责之。”乃举笔书判，命吏持与观之曰：“发该县典史责一板。”哄然而散。俞梦亦觉，自忖典史不能擅责生员，纵有意外之事，何仅责一板，意谓妖梦，置之度外，仍赴馆。

    Explanation

    218

    A divine being said, "Let us set aside the existence of deities, but there are always spirits present at night. As a scholar, you should still be cautious even in a dark, secluded room. How can you be reckless beside the great road? If a woman were to come, she would surely consider you a greatly evil and unprincipled man." Yu replied, "Let us also set aside the fact that women walking at night are rare. Even if there were such a case, it would be a man escorting her, holding a lamp in front, and you would have to avoid suspicion." The spirit said, "Your house is opposite the river, with a wide, surrounding water area. During the hot summer, many women sit or lie there. You do not know how to avoid suspicion, how can there be no sin?" Yu, at a loss for words, apologized. The spirit scolded him and struck him with a stick. Suddenly, Yu saw his deceased father staggering in, bowing repeatedly, and pleading, "I, an old man, lived a life free of wrongdoing, but I have only this unworthy son. Now he has offended the gods and deserves to be beaten. However, I have heard that if he is punished by the underworld, his entire life will be ruined, and I will become a spirit like the ghost of Ifao. I dare to beg for your mercy and forgiveness." The spirit said, "The crime is unforgivable. I will send him to a public official to be punished." He then picked up a pen and wrote a judgment, ordering the official to read it aloud, saying, "Send him to the county magistrate to be beaten with a stick." The spirits suddenly dispersed. Yu woke up from his dream, thinking that a county magistrate could not arbitrarily punish a scholar. Even if there were an unexpected event, why would he only be beaten once? He considered it a strange dream and disregarded it, continuing to go to school.

    219

    至中元节，解馆而归，值县尉王某，江南之望族也。屈为小官，未免负才肆志。是日太尊按临触其怒，欲黜之，幸大令为之营求稍解。尉送太尊登舟，求见谢过，太尊呼跪船头，严加训饬，久而释之。尉愧且忿，候太尊去，甫登舆，适俞生葛衣布履，持盖而来，为人所拥挤，以盖柄触肩舆之玻璃而碎之，尉大怒，叱役牵来，俞不禁战栗下跪，问之不能答，喝行杖，左右捉俞，去裤按之地。杖甫一下，俞忽醒，大呼曰：“打不得，打不得！”诘之，对曰：“吾生员也。”告以姓名，尉惶恐，下舆手扶之，再三谢罪。俞摇首结裤而起，曰：“与阁下无干，此其中盖有神明也。”仍执其盖而去。尉回衙，亦自悔卤莽。是对观者甚众，典史擅责生员之说，传作奇闻。同学诸生知之，佥来问询，俞曰：“是诚有之，然系神明所使，吾不见过。”遂述其梦。

    Explanation

    219

    Upon reaching the Zhongyuan Festival, I left the academy and returned home. Along the way, I encountered County Magistrate Wang, a prominent figure from the Jiangnan region. Despite his talents, he had been forced to take a low-ranking position, which inevitably led him to express his ambitions and frustrations. On that day, the Grand Magistrate's inspection provoked his anger, and he wanted to demote him. Fortunately, the County Magistrate intervened and somewhat alleviated the situation. The County Magistrate then sent the Grand Magistrate off on his boat, asking for an audience to apologize. The Grand Magistrate called him to kneel at the bow of the boat and severely reprimanded him, but after a long time, he was released. The County Magistrate felt ashamed and angry, and as soon as he got into the carriage, Scholar Yu, wearing a coarse robe and straw sandals, came, holding an umbrella. He was pushed by the crowd and accidentally hit the glass of the shoulder carriage with the handle of his umbrella, breaking it. The County Magistrate became very angry, ordered his servants to bring Yu, who could not help but tremble and kneel down. When asked, he could not answer, and the County Magistrate ordered the beating to begin. His attendants seized Yu, pulled off his pants, and forced him to lie down. As soon as the stick was about to strike, Yu suddenly woke up and shouted, "Do not beat me, do not beat me!" When questioned, he replied, "I am a scholar." When told his name, the County Magistrate became alarmed, got off the carriage, held him by the hand, and apologized repeatedly. Yu shook his head, put on his pants, and stood up, saying, "It has nothing to do with you; there must be some divine intervention here." He then took his umbrella and left. The County Magistrate returned to his office and also regretted his hasty action. The incident was witnessed by many people, and the story of the Chief Clerk beating a scholar spread as a strange tale. His fellow scholars learned about it and came to ask about it. Yu said, "It is true, but it was done by divine intervention; I did not do anything wrong." He then described his dream.

    220

    诸生曰：“梦虚而事实，一人受辱，辱及同侪，吾等能不与较耶？”拥俞入县署，鸣鼓声冤，大令亟召学师来议有以解释之法。师谓令曰：“兔死狐悲，物伤其类，众怒不可犯也，然猛虎项下铃，系者自能解之。”是时尉虑诸生攻击之，潜避县署，闻言而出，长跪师前日。“我之误挞俞先生，追悔莫及，然俞先生器局宏大，初未与我较，是皆诸生之教，请师召俞先生而慰解之，我愿对众负荆则冰释矣。”大令亦参赞之。师出问，俞摇首曰：“门人不愿与较，只求有以慰众心耳。”师曰：“罚之何如？”众曰：“愿闻罚之之法。”师曰：“当众令其拜俞生以谢过。”众目视俞，俞犹摇首曰：“不愿庭辱长官。”众谓师曰：“既俞生之盛德，应令其摒去从人，执香步行至俞府跪拜，吾等释之。”师曰：“诺。”俞同众回，具衣冠洒扫门庭，烹茗以俟。未几，尉公服持香而来，俞远出慰劳，辞香献茗，极其诚敬，即回拜之。众皆无可如何而散。

    Explanation

    220

    The students said, "The dream was unreal, but the facts were real. One person was humiliated, and the disgrace extended to their peers. How could we not respond?" They escorted Yu into the county office and beat the drum to appeal for justice. The magistrate urgently summoned the schoolmaster to discuss a way to resolve the matter. The teacher said to the magistrate, "When the rabbit dies, the fox mourns; things grieve for those of their kind. The anger of the crowd cannot be provoked, but the bell on the tiger's neck can only be removed by the one who tied it." At that time, the military officer, fearing attack from the students, secretly avoided the county office. Hearing the news, he came out and knelt long before the teacher, saying, "I mistakenly beat Mr. Yu; I deeply regret it. However, Mr. Yu has a broad and magnanimous character; he did not initially hold a grudge against me. This is all due to the teachings of the students. Please summon Mr. Yu and comfort him. I am willing to apologize to the crowd and carry a bundle of thorns; then all will be resolved." The magistrate also advised. The teacher asked, and Yu shook his head, saying, "My students do not wish to confront them; they only seek to appease the crowd." The teacher said, "What about punishing them?" The crowd said, "We wish to hear the method of punishment." The teacher said, "Have them kneel before Yu in public to apologize." The crowd looked at Yu, and Yu still shook his head, saying, "I do not wish to humiliate the officials in court." The crowd said to the teacher, "Given Mr. Yu's great virtue, we should have them dismiss their followers, carry incense, and walk to Yu's residence to kneel and apologize; we will then release them." The teacher said, "Agreed." Yu returned with the crowd, dressed in formal attire, cleaned the courtyard, and prepared tea to wait. Soon, the military officer arrived in formal attire, carrying incense. Yu came out to comfort him, declined the incense, and offered tea with great sincerity and respect. He then bowed in return. The crowd had no choice but to disperse.

    221

    俞仍之馆，居停辞之曰：“昔吾以先生为英才也，故敢屈诸西席以为门庐光。今已受挞于市，为众讪笑，则吾子弟人皆目之为罪人之弟子也，何辱如之！敢辞。”俞无以自明，抱惭而归。然尉甚德之，中秋登门贺节，俞见之，色甚不怿。尉叩其情，俞告以失馆故，尉曰：“先生犯而不较，当今之亚圣也。为我而有屈贤才，我罪大矣，然馆谷不足虑，敬为先生推毂。”尉本名相后裔，为一族之长，先是族有公塾，其师老病卒，族人正请命延师，左虚数月矣。尉以二百金聘俞先生，遣仆送之。江南族人咸敬先生品淳而学优，岁有增修，俞阅十余年归家，积千金，称小康云。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：谦，福之基也。故《谦》卦六爻皆吉。俞生自受神诫之后，何其谦也。随责之而随富之，冥冥中假王尉之手。由是观之，为人处世，可不谦乎哉？

    Explanation

    221

    俞仍之馆，居停辞之曰：“昔吾以先生为英才也，故敢屈诸西席以为门庐光。今已受挞于市，为众讪笑，则吾子弟人皆目之为罪人之弟子也，何辱如之！敢辞。”俞无以自明，抱惭而归。然尉甚德之，中秋登门贺节，俞见之，色甚不怿。尉叩其情，俞告以失馆故，尉曰：“先生犯而不较，当今之亚圣也。为我而有屈贤才，我罪大矣，然馆谷不足虑，敬为先生推毂。”尉本名相后裔，为一族之长，先是族有公塾，其师老病卒，族人正请命延师，左虚数月矣。尉以二百金聘俞先生，遣仆送之。江南族人咸敬先生品淳而学优，岁有增修，俞阅十余年归家，积千金，称小康云。

    The Master still stayed at the host's house, but the host declined his stay, saying, "In the past, I considered you a talented person, so I dared to humble myself to you as a teacher to bring glory to my household. Now that you have been publicly humiliated in the market and ridiculed by the crowd, all my family members regard you as the disciple of criminals. How can there be a greater shame! I dare to decline." The Master was unable to defend himself and returned home with shame. However, the magistrate highly respected him and visited him during the Mid-Autumn Festival to congratulate him. The Master saw him and looked very unhappy. The magistrate inquired about his situation, and the Master told him about losing his position as a tutor. The magistrate said, "Sir, you offend without complaint; you are a near-sage in today's world. For me to have委屈a talented person, my sin is great. However, lodging and food are not to worry about; I respectfully recommend you." The magistrate was a descendant of a former prime minister and the head of the clan. Previously, the clan had a public school, and its teacher died of old age and illness. The clan members were asking for a new teacher, and it had been several months since. The magistrate hired the Master with two hundred gold pieces and sent a servant to accompany him. People from the Jiangnan region all respected the Master for his virtuous character and excellent scholarship. Yearly increases in salary, the Master spent more than ten years returning home, accumulating a thousand gold pieces, and was considered moderately wealthy.

    芗厈曰：谦，福之基也。故《谦》卦六爻皆吉。俞生自受神诫之后，何其谦也。随责之而随富之，冥冥中假王尉之手。由是观之，为人处世，可不谦乎哉？

    The commentator said, "Humility is the foundation of happiness. Therefore, all six lines of the Hexagram of Humility are auspicious. The Master, after receiving divine instruction, how humble he is. As he faced responsibilities and became wealthy, it was through the unseen hand of the magistrate. From this perspective, in dealing with people and affairs, how can one not be humble?"

    222

    第六卷
    　　
    　　陈制军
    　　
    　　两江陈制军，在金陵甄别书院亲临督课，与院长叙谈之际，忽闻人声汹涌，兼鼓乐花炮之音。遣官察问，回言生童不由拦阻，争拔关出外观迎娶者。制军有愠色，负手而出，阅历各斋房，考具咸在而虚无人也。惟后所有一人，若垂首构思者。制军甚异之，意为沉静好学之徒，欲与之言，至其前，系年轻后生。其人昂首，见制军来，起立歇侍。乃问之曰：“诸生皆出，汝独静坐作文耶？抑别有故耶？”其人失声大哭，急叩其故，对曰：“童生吴某，亡父为词林，今日所娶之女，系生原聘之妻。”制军笑曰：“家教何其严也，既系完姻，何必就试。汝去毕乃姻事，若母与师责问，以奉予命放假为词，谅无不可。”生益悲咽而对曰：“非也，此女今为人攘取矣。

    Explanation

    222

    Chapter 6

    Chen Zhi, the Military Commissioner

    Chen Zhi, the Military Commissioner of the Two Rivers, personally supervised the lessons at the Zhenbie Academy in Nanjing. While conversing with the academy's principal, he suddenly heard a loud commotion accompanied by the sounds of drum music and fireworks. He sent an official to investigate and was told that the students could not be stopped from rushing out to watch a wedding procession.

    Chen Zhi's face darkened with displeasure. He walked out, surveying each of the study rooms, where the desks and chairs were all present but empty of students. In the back of the academy, there was only one person, seemingly deep in thought. Chen Zhi found this unusual and suspected that the individual was a quiet and studious student. He decided to speak with him. As he approached, the person looked up and saw Chen Zhi coming. The person stood up and waited respectfully.

    Chen Zhi asked, "All the students have left, but you are sitting alone, writing. Is there another reason for your presence here?"

    The person suddenly burst into tears. Chen Zhi quickly inquired about the reason, and the person replied, "I am Wu, a student. My father was a scholar in the imperial court. Today, the woman I am marrying is the same woman I was originally betrothed to."

    Chen Zhi smiled and said, "Your family is indeed strict. Since you are already married, why do you need to take the exam? Go ahead and complete your wedding ceremony. If your mother or teacher questions you, tell them that I granted you a break. I am sure they will understand."

    Wu became even more distressed and said, "No, this woman has been seized by someone else."

    223

    ”制军详讯之，对曰：“妇翁系已致仕之豫省河工观察许姓，由卑官起家，值亡父为是省学政，攀援联姻，为之游扬，荐升大员。前岁父没于都中，一无所有，殡殓之具皆戚友所助。生随母扶枢归葬，闻妇翁素不恰人心，又失宪司欢，今失所依倚，知必不免，亦致仕归，闻生贫甚，萌悔婚意。召生予百金索庚帖，生不允，妇翁怒曰：［我女安富尊荣，岂能食贫居贱哉？汝纵不还庚帖，我另择嫁，汝奈我何！］生归，与母谋，母曰：［以势交者，势尽则交绝，明欺我死学士不能敌生观察耳。］然天道昭彰，听之而已。妇翁竟改婚与杨兵部家，闻今日迎娶，互斗奢华，故诸生出观之，谅此时往迎新妇耳。”制军曰：“汝能骑乎？”对曰：“能。”乃命备两马，召武弁随生归，迅取庚帖，俄倾而回。制军验收之，传谕中军，广带兵役往杨第称贺。

    Explanation

    223

    The military governor conducted a detailed inquiry, and the groom responded: "My father-in-law is Xu, a retired official in charge of river engineering in Henan province. He rose from a low-ranking position and, during my father's tenure as the provincial education commissioner, formed a marriage alliance, promoted him, and recommended him for a high-ranking post. Last year, my father passed away in the capital, leaving us with nothing. All the funeral equipment was provided by relatives and friends. I accompanied my mother back to bury him. I heard that my father-in-law was always disliked by people and had lost the favor of the censor. Now, without any support, he knows he will inevitably face trouble, so he also retired and returned home. He heard that I was very poor and began to regret the marriage. He summoned me, gave me one hundred gold pieces, and demanded the marriage contract. I refused, and my father-in-law angrily said: 'My daughter lives in wealth and honor; how could she live in poverty and low status? Even if you do not return the marriage contract, I will choose another to marry; what can you do to me?' I returned home and discussed it with my mother. She said: 'Those who form relationships based on power will have their relationships end when the power is gone; clearly, he is mocking that a deceased scholar cannot match a living official.' However, the ways of heaven are clear; we can only accept it. My father-in-law eventually changed the marriage to the family of an official in the Ministry of War. I heard that they are getting married today, competing in extravagance. That is why all the students are coming to watch; I suspect they are going to welcome the new bride now." The military governor asked, "Can you ride?" The groom replied, "Yes." The military governor then ordered two horses to be prepared and summoned a military officer to accompany the groom back. They quickly retrieved the marriage contract and returned shortly. The military governor inspected and accepted it, then issued orders to the deputy commander, with a large group of soldiers, to go to the Yang residence to congratulate.

    224

    其时兵部在京供职，仅有一子，与乃兄共之，乃兄系纳粟职员，闻制军来，大为荣幸，远迓乘舆及门。宾客中之显者共迎制军入坐。制军佥与为礼，询知媒妁为钟、徐二进士。茶三献毕，制军曰：“我闻天下风俗不同，吉礼为甚，素知此间与我省竟有大异者。”主人止观之。未几，爆竹震耳，则妆奁先至，媒妁相随。

    Explanation

    224

    At the time, the Minister of War was serving in the capital and had only one son, whom he shared with his elder brother, who was a government official appointed through the purchase of grain. Upon hearing that the governor-general was coming, they were greatly honored and went out to meet him at the gate. The prominent guests welcomed the governor-general together and escorted him to sit down. The governor-general greeted them all politely and inquired about the matchmaker, learning that she was the two Jinshi (imperial examination graduates) surnamed Zhong and Xu. After three rounds of tea were served, the governor-general said, "I have heard that customs vary across the country, especially in wedding ceremonies. I have always known that there are significant differences here compared to my province." The host listened quietly. Soon after, the sound of firecrackers filled the air, and then the dowry arrived first, followed by the matchmaker.

    225

    制军喝令立拿媒妁，谕兵役获运妆奁，命中军持令箭，邀截新妇彩舆俱送入书院，即其堂中设公案，提徐、钟二进士入跪，诘之曰：“尔等身列儒林，何竟败坏风俗，与有夫之妇为媒？”二进士骇曰：“某等系两家亲友，从不知许女订婚之事，是何凶徒竟敢妄告？”制军袖出庚帖，掷令观之，二进士识许某亲笔，共怒诉曰：“许某忝膺爵禄，而竟非人类，然彼在豫联姻，远隔数千里，渠居心瞒昧，某等何由知之？是陷人于罪也，请必究之。”杨亦跽告曰：“许某心若豺狼，行同鬼蜮，某若早知，决不与为婚媾。今此事惟某是主，弟在都中未之知也。某愿与许贼较，请严究之。”制军察众词色惊惧，谅不知情，饬发廉访质讯。遂借杨氏喜筵及新郎之冠服回书院，适诸生缴卷之际，谕令毋散，同陪筵席，迁院长于外，而以内宅作新房，命舆请生母公服而来，以观花烛。生乃衣杨婿之衣，与女合拜成礼。内外数十席，官衿吏役共饮，尽欢而散。

    Explanation

    225

    The military commander ordered immediately to seize the matchmaker, instructed the soldiers to bring the dowry, sent a mid-level officer with a command flag, intercepted the bride's sedan chair, and brought it to the academy. He set up a public desk in the hall, summoned the two Jinshi to kneel, and questioned them, saying, "You are listed among the scholars. Why have you ruined the customs by acting as a matchmaker for a married woman?" The two Jinshi were startled and replied, "We are relatives and friends of both families and were unaware of the engagement of the woman. Who is this villain to dare falsely accuse?" The military commander pulled out the birth chart from his sleeve and threw it for them to see. Recognizing Mr. Xu's handwriting, the two Jinshi angrily said, "Mr. Xu, who has been granted a title and salary, is not human. Yet he was married in Henan, separated by thousands of miles. He concealed his true intentions. How could we know about it? This is to frame someone for a crime. Please investigate thoroughly." Yang also knelt and said, "Mr. Xu's heart is like that of a wolf, and his actions are like those of a demon. If I had known earlier, I would never have married him. Now, this matter is entirely my responsibility. My younger brother in the capital did not know about it. I am willing to confront Mr. Xu. Please investigate strictly." Noticing the frightened expressions of the crowd, the military commander believed they were innocent and ordered a thorough investigation by the censor. Then, he borrowed the wedding feast and the groom's attire from Yang's family and returned to the academy. Just as the students were submitting their exam papers, he ordered them not to disperse, and together they attended the banquet. The head of the academy was moved outside, and the inner quarters were used as the bridal chamber. A carriage was ordered to bring the mother-in-law in official dress to witness the lighting of the wedding candles. The groom then wore the clothes of Yang's son-in-law and, together with the bride, completed the wedding ceremony. There were dozens of tables inside and outside, and officials, students, and servants all drank together, enjoying themselves and then dispersing.

    226

    次日，催廉访速究以报，制军濡笔待奏。许大怖，急奔书院见婿，叩首无算，自称死罪曰：“婿如能救我，所有家财，愿与婿共之。”生迟疑未决，母训之曰：“其父虽狂，其女甚谨，汝不见其闻言醒悟时则曰：［非制军力，几为父母诳矣！］痛哭不已，情可怜也，曷为之援解。”生敬诺，往见制军以情告。制军曰：“果尔，命其开报田宅，我为分判，以赎其辜，以为汝诵读之资。若稍纵之，吾去后老獠必变，汝非其敌。”遂命廉访提许至，依制军断以立案，吴生于是乎骤富，奋勉读书，是年入泮，旋登贤书。嗣制军因刚愎故，被仇家陷害，奉旨拏问，生追随入都，因乃父之门生故旧为之营救，竟得开释复用。而生亦铨得县令出仕矣。

    Explanation

    226

    The next day, the general governor urged the censor to investigate quickly and report back, while he prepared to write a memorial. Xu Da was terrified and rushed to the academy to see his son-in-law, prostrating himself countless times and admitting his guilt, saying, "If you can save me, I will share all my family wealth with you." The son-in-law hesitated and was undecided, but his mother advised him, saying, "Though his father is mad, his daughter is very cautious. Didn't you see her wake up when she heard the words and say, 'Without the general governor's power, I would have been deceived by my parents!' She cried bitterly, and her situation is pitiful. Why don't you help her?" The son-in-law respectfully agreed and went to see the general governor to explain the situation. The general governor said, "If that is the case, order him to report his lands and houses, and I will divide and judge them to redeem his guilt, to be your means for reading and studying. If you let him off lightly, after I leave, the old rascal will surely change, and you are not his match." He then ordered the censor to bring Xu Da, and according to the general governor's judgment, he was officially recorded. Wu Sheng thus became suddenly wealthy and worked hard at studying. That year, he entered the local Confucian school and soon was listed among the talented. Later, the general governor, due to his stubbornness, was framed by enemies and imprisoned by imperial decree. Wu Sheng followed him to the capital, and because of his father's former students and acquaintances, he was rescued and eventually released and reinstated. And Wu Sheng was also appointed as a county magistrate and began his official career.

    227

    瘦马
    　　
    　　金陵匪徒，有在四方贩买幼女，选其俊秀者，调理其肌肤，修饰其衣履，延师教之，凡书画琴棋，箫笛管弦之类，无一不能。及瓜，则重价售与宦室富商为妾，或竟入妓院，名之曰“养瘦马”。遇有贫家好女子，则百计诱之，有受其诳追悔莫及者，不知凡几。时有滇人徐邻哉，为上元令，因案墨误有亏帑项，忧郁以卒，亲友奴仆皆星散，惟遗孺人弱女，茕茕流寓。因逋负赁值已多，被寓主驱逐，惟求佣作女工。有人引入瘦马家，不知也。

    Explanation

    227

    Trained Courtesans

    In Nanjing, there were unscrupulous individuals who traveled throughout the land to buy young girls. They selected the most beautiful and graceful among them, beautifying their skin and dressing them in fine clothes. They hired teachers to instruct them in all manner of arts, including painting, calligraphy, music, and chess. When these girls reached maturity, they were sold at high prices to wealthy officials and merchants as concubines, or sometimes even taken into brothels, where they were known as "trained courtesans." If they encountered poor families with virtuous daughters, they would use every trick in the book to lure them away, and many were deceived and later regretted it bitterly, though it was too late. At one time, there was a man from Yunnan named Xu Linzai, who served as the magistrate of Shangyu. Due to a financial discrepancy in the official records, he became depressed and died. His friends, relatives, and servants all dispersed, leaving behind only his gentlewoman and frail daughter, who lived in exile. Because of unpaid debts, they were eventually evicted by their landlord and were left with no choice but to work as female laborers. Someone introduced them to a family that trained courtesans, but they did not know what was in store for them.

    228

    其家以老妪主政，家人婢仆数十人，咸尊之曰“老太太”。教师十余人，诸秀女各有所业，称妪为母、为祖母、为老老者，莫不娇容丽质，举止安娴。其家法，则三尺之童，非奉呼唤，不敢入中门，规矩井然，宛尔大家风范。所雇徐孺人，教诸女刺绣耳。见其带女来，年逋十三四，秀外慧中，超越诸女之上，妪甚怜之。因俾母女与己共食，易女以时服，嘱同诸女入塾读书，暇时调琴作画，以及吹弹歌舞之事，与女伴逞能竞敏，亦精绝无伦。

    Explanation

    228

    In the household, an elderly woman governed the affairs, and there were dozens of family members and servants who all respectfully called her "Grandmother." There were more than ten teachers, and each of the talented young women had their own duties. They addressed the elderly woman as mother, grandmother, or an elder, and none of them lacked beauty and grace in appearance or demeanor. The household rules were strict, and even the youngest children dared not enter the main gate unless called upon. The household was orderly and exhibited the demeanor of a noble family. The household had hired a woman named Xu Ruren to teach the young women embroidery. When she saw the young girl brought along, who was around thirteen or fourteen years old, she found her exceptionally beautiful and wise, surpassing all the other girls. The elderly woman deeply loved her. Therefore, she made the mother and daughter eat together with her, provided the girl with appropriate clothing, and instructed her to join the other girls in attending school. In her spare time, the girl played the lute, painted, and engaged in activities such as playing musical instruments, singing, and dancing, competing with her female companions in skill and agility, and she was also exceptionally skilled and unparalleled.

    229

    当徐孺人初至时，问妪门第，则以丈夫子皆外出服官，仅留诸女在家作伴伪答，孺人信之。荏苒三年，女已及笄，嘱妪为之择婿，欣然从之。未几报某公子欲相女，先命诸女次第出，皆不中选。遂华装徐女欲出，孺人止之曰：“此非大家所为。”妪曰：“金陵风俗皆如是，不能违也。”不得已而去。至中堂，见公子髯而伟，仆从甚众，有指点肥瘦者，有品评妍媸者。见孺人偕女来，公子傲不为礼，坐而观之。一仆指之曰：“此女骨秀神清，超前绝后，诚尤物也。”公子亦鼓掌赞叹曰：“不意粪壤中竟产灵芝。

    Explanation

    229

    When Madam Xu first arrived, she asked the old woman about her family background. The old woman falsely replied that her sons were all away serving in official posts, leaving only her daughters to keep her company. Madam Xu believed her. Three years passed, and the daughter had reached the age of coming of age. Madam Xu then instructed the old woman to find a husband for her, and the old woman readily agreed. Not long after, word came that a certain young gentleman wished to inspect the daughter. He first ordered the daughters to come forth one by one, but none were chosen. So the old woman dressed Madam Xu's daughter in fine attire and was about to send her out, but Madam Xu stopped her, saying, "This is not the way of a proper family." The old woman replied, "The customs of Jinling are all like this; one cannot disobey them." With no choice, they went. Upon arriving at the central hall, they saw the young gentleman, who had a beard and an imposing figure, accompanied by a large entourage of attendants. Some were commenting on the fat and thin, while others were evaluating beauty and ugliness. When they saw Madam Xu and her daughter arriving together, the young gentleman was arrogant and did not offer any courtesy, merely sitting and watching. One of his attendants pointed at her and said, "This girl has a refined bone structure and a clear, refined spirit, surpassing all who came before and after—truly a rare and exceptional beauty." The young gentleman also clapped in admiration and said, "I did not expect such a rare and precious mushroom to grow from the filth."

    230

    ”众仆见如公子意，皆曰：“千金值无多耳，不乘此时定之，佳人难再得矣。”女拉孺人俯首入，谓妪曰：“如此不堪，必非正配，我不愿也。”妪笑曰：“汝家一贫至此，谁与为婚？由小星而缓图之，宠荣之日，夺嫡何难？况此公子父官极品，家资巨万，若母随女嫁，则终身吃着不尽，而我亦藉沾余润，非一举而两得乎？”女闻之大啼，拔簪脱衣掷妪曰：“还我故袄，我愿与母丐而死，不愿辱而生也。”孺人亦怒曰，“如尔所言，直瘦马家所为耳。”妪闻道破其情，微哂曰：“汝母女纵有广大神通，亦跳不出我范围也。饮食教诲于今三年，不值数百金耶？汝女犹我家婢，能不我主也？”女益大啼觅死，妪叱群婢缚之空房，立遂孺人出。

    Explanation

    230

    The servants, seeing the young master's intent, all said, "A thousand gold coins are not much, and if we do not settle this now, a good woman will be hard to find again." The girl pulled the old woman into the room and said, "This is so unbearable, it must not be the right match for me, and I do not wish to go through with it." The old woman laughed and said, "Your family is so poor, who would marry you? By starting with a minor partner and planning slowly, on the day of honor and glory, how difficult is it to take over? Especially since this young master's father holds the highest rank in the imperial bureaucracy and has immense wealth. If the mother follows the daughter in marriage, they will have more than enough to eat and wear for life, and I will also benefit from the remnants, achieving two goals at once, is it not?" The girl, hearing this, cried bitterly, pulled out her hairpin, and threw off her clothes at the old woman, saying, "Give me back my old clothes, I would rather beg with my mother and die than live in shame." The old woman also became angry and said, "As you say, this is just what a poor man would do." The old woman, upon hearing the truth revealed, smiled slightly and said, "Even if you mother and daughter have great powers, you cannot escape my control. You have been fed and educated by me for three years, and is it not worth several hundred gold coins? Your daughter is still like my servant, and how can she not be under my authority?" The girl cried even more, seeking death, and the old woman ordered the maids to bind her and lock her in an empty room, then immediately escorted the old woman out.

    231

    孺人忿甚，问入县之途，欲鸣诸官。遇一媪，询得其情，笑曰：“汝一穷寡妇，思与瘦马为敌，祗自取辱耳。彼非金钱充塞，衙门吏役相与狼狈，焉敢公然作是业耶？”孺人曰：“依汝所言，有死之心，无生之路矣。”媪曰：“勿急，我系官媒，受瘦马之害不浅，与汝同仇。为今之计，汝既有针工之能，可觅一大衙门作活，日与夫人习熟，乘便诉苦衷，或有济也。”孺人不觉首肯，曰：“我何从入大衙门耶？”媪曰：“都堂陈大人谕觅绣娘，此其机也。且即日可进。

    Explanation

    231

    The woman was very angry and asked for the way to the county office, intending to lodge a complaint with the officials. She met an old woman who, upon hearing her story, laughed and said, "You, a poor widow, think you can challenge a rich man, and you will only bring shame upon yourself. He wouldn't dare to openly engage in such behavior if he weren't wealthy and had the collusion of officials and servants in the government office." The woman replied, "According to what you say, I have only a heart for death and no way to live." The old woman said, "Don't be in such a hurry. I am an official matchmaker and have suffered greatly from the rich man. I share your enmity. For now, since you have the skill of a seamstress, you can find a large government office to work in, become familiar with the official's wife over time, and take the opportunity to express your grievances. There might be a way out." The woman nodded in agreement without realizing it and asked, "How can I enter a large government office?" The old woman said, "The high-ranking official Mr. Chen has ordered the search for a seamstress. This is your chance, and you can enter immediately."

    232

    ”孺人求其引荐，偕入督署，夫人见其举止端方，喜而留之。其命伴女公子刺绣。孺人委宛教导，谨慎小心，女公子亦喜，愿与同卧起。孺人恒思忆其女，中夜涕泣，女公子诘得其故，转达其母，夫人为制军言之，制军怒，立召府县官至，面叱之曰：“地方容留人贩，失察之咎，已无可辞。甚至霸占宦室之女，为地方官者，昏愦无知，所司何事耶？”官皆失色，跽请其故。制军以孺人之事告之，勒令密缉严究，刻日申报，如有知风漏网，惟该府县是问。诺诺而退，即率役亲诣瘦马之门，合围而搜之，男妇及女咸获。缘出不意，无一脱逃者。

    Explanation

    232

    The gentlewoman sought an introduction and entered the governor's office together. The lady saw her manner was dignified and proper, and was pleased to keep her. She ordered her to accompany the governor's daughter in embroidery. The gentlewoman gently instructed her, being cautious and attentive, and the governor's daughter also liked her, wishing to share the same bed and rise with her. The gentlewoman constantly thought of her daughter, crying in the middle of the night. The governor's daughter inquired and found out the reason, passing it on to her mother. The lady spoke to the governor about it. The governor became angry, immediately summoned the officials of the prefecture and county, and reprimanded them face-to-face, saying, "The local area harbors human traffickers. The fault of failing to detect it cannot be excused. Even to the extent of seizing the daughter of a government official's household, as local officials, you are foolish and ignorant, what business do you have?" The officials all turned pale, knelt and asked for the reason. The governor informed them of the gentlewoman's situation, ordered them to secretly investigate and thoroughly examine the matter, to report within a specified period. If anyone lets wind of it leak out, only the prefecture and county officials will be held accountable. They nodded and left, then led their subordinates to the door of the brothel, surrounded it, and searched it. All men, women, and girls were captured. Caught by surprise, none managed to escape.

    233

    府县严刑鞫实，拟瘦马家男妇俱外遣，发诸女归其家。遂进徐女入院署与母团聚，感制军恩，相率叩谢。夫人曰：“未也，制军有后命，请孺人权为我女师，将为孺人女择配以完其事。”遂示期观风，集诸生而课之，得两生，文理并优，问一已有妻，一寒士赵生，壮而未娶。复面课之，无伪，以徐女娶之。籍没瘦马财产尽充徐令亏缺外，尚余数千金，并宅一所，为女妆奁。俾赵生安心肄业，是科高魁，连捷南宫，迎孺人而终也。当制军获咎时，值生在籍，亦追随入都，恐仇家有隐害制军者，起居必与共，饮食必先尝，防护之密，谨于亲人。故制军之得以安然无恙者，皆吴、赵二生报之也。

    Explanation

    233

    The local officials conducted a rigorous interrogation and investigation, proposing to exile all the men and women of the "thin horse" family and send the daughters back to their families. Xu's daughter was then admitted into the official residence to reunite with her mother, and they were grateful for the general's kindness, proceeding to kneel and thank him. The general's wife said, "Not yet, the general has a further order. He asked the lady to temporarily serve as my daughter's teacher to find a suitable match for the lady's daughter to complete the matter." She then set a date to observe the students' performance, gathered the students and tested them, and found two students, both of whom were excellent in both literature and reasoning. One was already married, the other was a poor scholar named Zhao, strong but unmarried. They further tested them in person, finding no deceit, and arranged for Xu's daughter to marry Zhao. The "thin horse" family's property was confiscated to cover the financial shortfall of Xu's order, with a surplus of several thousand gold pieces and a house, which were used as dowry for the daughter. Zhao was allowed to focus on his studies, excelling in the exams, passing the palace examination, and eventually bringing the lady home to live out her days. When the general faced trouble, Zhao, who was still a student, followed him to the capital, fearing that enemies might secretly harm the general, sharing his living quarters and tasting his food first, guarding him as closely as family. Thus, the general was able to remain safe and unharmed, all thanks to the loyalty of Wu and Zhao.

    234

    方幼樗云：吴赵二生事，俨然史公《管晏列传》笔法。而一则吴生详叙于前，一则赵生之妻胪陈于首，绝似天际神龙，总不令首尾并见，文心变幻笔墨间，大得烟云之助。
    　　
    　　查商
    　　
    　　查商，本江左诸生，善诗，以教读为业。家有水田数十亩，可以自食其力矣。忽作致富想，因族人有在津门以鹾务起家者，曰北查，凡乡里之亲故投之，获利甚厚。查核家乘，知在北之主者为侄辈行，乃货其产，半以安家，半作旅资，贸贸然北上，以土宜及谱牒馈主者。是时北查其盛，司总、司库、司宾之士为之奔走者数百人，恃以举火者千家。凡宾至，阍人报司总，或留或去，咸听其便。主者深居厚养如王公，非大事不预闻，人罕觏之。查来，不过族叔耳，既无显者之托，亦非期功之亲，司总推酌其间，反其土宜，留其宗谱，资助十金，命司宾者辞以主人事冗不及见，即见亦无益也。查大失所望，负气却其金，权寓旅邸，以俟他缘。

    Explanation

    234

    Fang Youchu commented: The stories of Wu and Zhao, young men, are written in a style reminiscent of Sima Qian's biography of Guan Zhong and Yan Ying. One part of the story is detailed by Wu, while another part is narrated by Zhao's wife at the beginning, much like a mythical dragon in the sky, always keeping its head and tail hidden. The literary spirit transforms within the brush and ink, greatly aided by the mist and clouds.

    Zhang Shang

    Zhang Shang was originally a student from the Jiangzuo region, skilled in poetry, and made a living as a teacher. He had several tens of acres of paddy fields, which allowed him to support himself. Suddenly, he developed a desire to become wealthy. Because there were clan members in Tianjin who had started a salt trade business and became prosperous, called North Zhang, anyone from the village who entrusted their affairs to him gained great profit. Zhang verified his family genealogy and learned that the head in the north was from his nephew's generation. Therefore, he sold his property, using half to settle down and the other half as travel expenses, recklessly heading north to present local specialties and genealogical records to the head. At that time, North Zhang was very prosperous, with hundreds of people serving as head of the household, treasurer, and host, and thousands of households relying on him for sustenance. Whenever guests arrived, the gatekeeper reported to the head of the household, and they either stayed or left as they pleased. The head lived in seclusion and was well cared for, like a king or prince, and was only informed about important matters; few people saw him. Zhang came, but he was just an uncle in the family, having neither the trust of prominent people nor being a close relative. The head considered the situation, returned the local specialties, kept the genealogical records, and provided ten gold pieces as financial assistance, ordering the host to decline the meeting, saying that the head was busy with affairs and could not meet, even if he did, it would be of no benefit. Zhang was greatly disappointed and, carrying a grudge, refused the money, temporarily staying at an inn, waiting for other opportunities.

    235

    居半载余，行囊渐罄，悲悔之忱，托诸咏歌。朝夕题写，墙为之满。旅主虽厌恶之，因其负欠已多，未敢逐。然津门为商贾云集之所，每岁底，各寓皆患人满，房资顿增，甚至三四人合寓一室。时有北客阿姓，系内府下屯取租者，觅寓至此，旅主曰：“已无独住处，若兼容并包，尚有查客之房可共也。”阿询知查为儒士，欣然乐从。阿乃遣其仆赴屯，而在寓静摄，见满壁诗词，遍读之，拍案叫绝曰：“天下有此通才而流落不偶乎？”遂与查纵谈今古，互相唱酬，阿词甚豪放，倜傥不群，恨相见之晚，订为莫逆交。盘桓旬余，其仆收租回，阿将言归，曰：“明年此间盐政有代者，予至戚也，敬为先容，付书一函，投之必遇。”唤旅主，为查偿其逋负，并留斧资而去。

    Explanation

    235

    After half a year, his traveling bag had gradually emptied, and his feelings of sorrow and regret were entrusted to songs. He wrote poems morning and night, filling the walls with his writings. The innkeeper, though he disliked it, did not dare to evict him because he owed a significant amount. However, Jinmen was a place where merchants gathered, and every year at the end of the year, all lodgings suffered from overcrowding, with rent suddenly increasing, even to the point where three or four people shared a room. At that time, there was a northern guest surnamed A, who was responsible for collecting rents from the inner court's estates. He was looking for lodging there, and the innkeeper said, "There are no more rooms for a single person, but if you are willing to accommodate others, there is still a room that 查 (the scholar) can share." A learned that 查 was a scholar and happily agreed. A then sent his servant to collect the rent, and while staying at the inn, he saw the walls filled with poetry and read them all, clapping his hands and exclaiming, "Is there such a talented person in the world who is wandering and not finding their match?" He then engaged in long discussions with 查 about the past and present, exchanging poems with each other. A's words were very bold and unrestrained, and he was unique and extraordinary, regretting that they had met so late, and they became sworn brothers. They stayed together for more than ten days, and when A's servant returned after collecting the rent, A was about to return, saying, "Next year, there will be a replacement for the salt administration here, and he is my close relative. I will introduce him respectfully, and I have given a letter, which is certain to be received." He called the innkeeper and paid 查's outstanding debt, and left some money for 查 to use before departing.

    236

    次年春，盐院果易，查持书往投，请相见者阿也。曰：“予与盐政一人交，业为先生介绍，岁奉修金二百，烦掌笔墨之事。现有微恙，未及见先生。”即饬隶役迁行李入院，拨二仆以伺之。曰：“予现司总务，先生若有所事问予，与居停无异也。”查安之。未几，纲商以名柬愿见，阍人请命，查不知所云，遣仆问阿，则曰：“见之可也。”遂见之。

    Explanation

    236

    The following spring, the salt administration indeed changed, and Zha carried a letter to present, asking to meet someone named Ah. Ah said, "I have a connection with one person in the salt administration, and I have already introduced Mr. to him. He annually receives a stipend of two hundred, and troubles him with the task of handling writing. He currently has a slight illness and has not yet met Mr. yet." Zha immediately ordered the servants to move the luggage into the residence and assigned two servants to wait on him. Ah said, "I currently oversee general affairs. If Mr. has any matters to ask me, it is the same as asking the host." Zha felt reassured. Soon after, a merchant of the salt industry offered a visiting card to meet, and the gatekeeper sought instructions. Zha did not understand what was meant, so he sent a servant to ask Ah, who replied, "It is acceptable to see him." Thus, they met.

    237

    商人足恭尽礼，再拜而告曰：“某等商总八人，其首推者为夫子之族侄，昨谒都宪，拒不与见。令侄惶恐之至，某等问诸司阍，始知前年曾开罪于夫子，都宪欲参之，夫子以一本之厚谊，谅可恕小子之无知。此后饮食教诲，惟夫子命。故某等为之先容，祈夫子原之，以达于都宪，则某等咸沐宏恩矣。”皆长跪不起。查亦跽告曰：“某于此不过与都宪之戚友善，居停实未之见也。何能为力？”众曰：“惟求夫子诺之，事之成与不成，某等何敢强勉？”查不得已应之。众欢谢而退。查乃请阿公而告之曰：“阔下命我延客，今误我矣。

    Explanation

    237

    The merchants, showing utmost respect and courtesy, bowed twice and then spoke: "We eight merchants in total, with one of us being your nephew, were unable to meet the Chief Censor yesterday. Our nephew is deeply anxious. We asked the gatekeeper and learned that last year he had offended you, and the Chief Censor intended to punish him. You, out of our deep bond of kinship, would surely forgive his ignorance. From now on, our diet and education are at your command. Thus, we have come to seek your help. If you agree, we will be grateful, but if not, we dare not force the matter." They all knelt and did not rise. 查 also knelt and replied: "I have no direct connection to the Chief Censor and have not met him. I can only ask you to agree. Whether the matter succeeds or fails, we dare not push it." 查 reluctantly agreed. The merchants thanked him and left. 查 then went to report to the elder figure: "You ordered me to invite guests, but I have been misled by this matter."

    238

    ”述为众所窘意，阿笑曰：“无碍，先生如可恕之，予为斡旋无难也。”查曰：“侄之过，我初憾之，嗣闻非其本意，则遥遥祖德，何敢忘诸。居停若推乌屋之爱，怜赐赦宥，我亦与有荣施矣。”阿曰：“先生既允之，可命令侄进见，报与同晤可也。”明日，其侄备重贽，谒见之际，长跪请罪，涕泗交流曰：“侄所用非人，致叔父下临，未能尽礼，昏愦无知，罪实不浅。今蒙原宥，其祖宗之灵，有以佑启我后人也。惟叔父严训之。

    Explanation

    238

    The person being addressed was troubled by the crowd, and Ah smiled and said, "No problem, if you can forgive him, I can mediate without difficulty." The elder said, "My nephew made a mistake, and I was initially resentful, but after hearing that it was not his true intention, I remember our distant ancestral virtue and dare not forget it. If the host extends the love of a humble dwelling and grants forgiveness, I too will share in the honor and favor." Ah said, "Since the elder has agreed, you can command your nephew to come and meet, and I will inform him to come together." The next day, the nephew prepared a valuable gift and, when he paid his visit, knelt for a long time to ask for forgiveness, with tears and snot flowing together. He said, "I used the wrong person, causing my uncle to come down, and I was unable to fulfill all the proper courtesies, being dazed and ignorant, and my fault is indeed serious. Now that I have received forgiveness, the spirits of my ancestors will protect and guide my descendants. Only my uncle's strict instruction will help me."

    239

    ”查曰：“我实无能，侄何尤耶？”命仆请阿公，则命服而来，始知即都宪也。谓其侄曰：“始予以汝不知叔侄之情，焉知君臣之义，昧昧从事，恐致亏帑项，故欲盘封，例不得私见。今闻先生言，知汝为家人所误，情尚可原。其速黜不肖者，以贤叔总其成，必能助汝经营矣。”其侄再拜，领命而退。阿谓查曰：“予初不以实告先生者，非敢诳也，恐先生易为之，则令侄无惧心，而谋无成矣。今必不敢轻率。”果岁以千金延叔，查乃出入盐院，阿益相得，惟命是从。众商佥仰之，为之公举，立鹾业，不数年而富甲宇内，据冀北豫东之半，是为南查。

    Explanation

    239

    Chá said, "I am truly incapable; why blame my nephew?" He ordered his servant to invite Uncle A, who came in official attire, and only then did Chá realize that he was the Censor-in-Chief. Addressing his nephew, he said, "At first, I thought you did not understand the affection between uncle and nephew, nor the duty between ruler and minister. Blindly engaged in matters, I feared it would damage the treasury, so I wanted to seal the accounts, but according to custom, I could not meet privately. Now hearing the words of Master, I know you were misled by your household staff. The situation is still pardonable. Quickly dismiss the incompetent, and have your honorable uncle oversee the completion. He will surely help you manage." His nephew bowed twice and withdrew with the command. Uncle A said to Chá, "At first, I did not tell Master the truth, not out of deceit, but I feared that if Master easily agreed, your nephew would have no fear, and the plan would not succeed. Now I will not act lightly." Indeed, he invited his uncle with a thousand gold coins annually, and Chá began to enter and exit the Salt Administration. Uncle A and Chá grew even closer, following each other's commands. All merchants unanimously respected him, and they publicly elected him to establish the salt industry. Within a few years, he became the richest in the land, controlling half of the northern Hebei and eastern Henan regions, and this was known as the Southern Chá.

    240

    方幼樗云：叙次简而明，详而练，最爱收处直截了当，亦深得史公笔意。
    　　
    　　场外孝廉
    　　
    　　吴人张某，幼业儒，屡小试不售。年已壮，父母使出外谋生。滞于三辅，以记室为业。性甚平和，善随人意，所主之牧令咸喜之。以是得不赋闲，其脩金除寄奉甘旨外，偶积余资，纳粟入北监。时或入闱，恒不终局，不过借此观光而已。至优觞博戏，妓馆酒楼，无一不好，惟不敢恋恋者，短于财耳。

    Explanation

    240

    方幼樗说：叙述简洁明了，详细而精练，最喜爱结尾处直截了当，也深得司马迁的笔法。

    场外孝廉

    吴地的张某，年轻时学习儒学，多次参加小试未中。年纪渐长，父母让他外出谋生。他滞留在三辅地区，以记室为业。性格非常平和，善于随人意，所服务的牧令都很喜欢他。因此得以不赋闲，他的薪金除了寄给父母外，偶尔有余钱，便捐粮入北监。有时参加科举，总是无法完成，不过只是借此观光而已。至于饮酒博戏，妓馆酒楼，无一不好，只是不敢过于沉迷，因为缺钱。

    241

    戊子夏，东人以事去官，有旧居停在都候铨者，昔曾以书招之，比至，则已铨得西粤行矣。张遂逗留京师，以图机会。是岁系科场，会馆皆为举子住，不得停留闲人。觅寓则房价倍长，行橐已空，力有不逮，几无存身处所。幸旧主者皆山右人，平日习惯西音，作太原语，无能辨者。因访得西馆尚有虚室，伪托西人赴试者，得托足地。旋有太原人十数辈来视馆舍，凡有闲宅，皆修饰一新，为上房，为下舍，为书斋，为客厅，为厨为厕，莫不布置井井，陈设焕然，似将寓显宦。位置已妥，分数人往迓，未几一少年来，马腾车涌，仆从如云，投止于内。次日以名柬拜同乡，知为太原王姓，父为大贾，家资百万，心羡科第，援例赴北闱者。拜客毕，下帏读书，吚唔不彻。其所带之伴客三人，闲暇无事，与张聚谈，竟成相与。遂入局斗叶子为戏，客问张所事，则以就试乏资以俟戚友伪对。

    Explanation

    241

    In the summer of the year of the Earth Rat, a man from the east resigned from his position due to certain matters. He had an old host in the capital who was in charge of administrative affairs. This host had previously invited him with a letter, but by the time he arrived, the host had already secured a position in the western regions of Guangdong. Zhang, therefore, stayed in the capital, hoping to find opportunities. That year, the imperial examinations were held, and the inns were occupied by candidates, leaving no room for idle people. Finding a place to stay was difficult, as the rental prices had doubled, and his travel funds were depleted, leaving him almost without a place to stay. Fortunately, the old host was from Shanxi and spoke the Taiyuan dialect, which was not widely understood. Zhang managed to find a vacant room in a western-style inn by pretending to be a Westerner going to take the exams. Soon, several people from Taiyuan came to inspect the inns, and they renovated all the vacant houses, decorating them with new furniture, bookshelves, living rooms, kitchens, and bathrooms, making them look like they belonged to high-ranking officials. Once everything was set up, they sent a few people to greet the guests. Shortly after, a young man arrived with a large entourage, riding in a grand procession. He stayed at the inn. The next day, he paid respects to his fellow townspeople with a visiting card and was introduced as Wang, a surname from Taiyuan. His father was a wealthy merchant with assets worth a million, and he had come to the capital hoping to succeed in the imperial examinations, following the precedent of taking the exams in the northern district. After paying respects to his guests, the young man retreated to study in seclusion, reading aloud but incomprehensibly. The three companions he brought with him, having nothing to do, gathered with Zhang and ended up chatting together. They then started playing cards for entertainment. When one of the guests asked Zhang what he was up to, Zhang falsely claimed that he was waiting for relatives and friends to help him with the expenses for the exams.

    242

    七月间，骤有一客来，与王密语，唤三客入，久之斯出，皆有张惶之色。诘张曰：“足下既来应试，贡乎？监乎？”张曰：“监也。”又曰：“真乎？伪乎？”张曰：“有照为凭，奚能伪也？”客故作不信，张启箧与观，三客咸欣然曰：“足下真读书人也，惜不及入场，功名误矣。曷不一纵游观，以解其闷乎？”张以穷乏对，客曰：“我辈相好，无不可通融者。”遂坚邀入妓室，纵酒肆博，客为给采，张乐而忘反，沉湎花丛，至八月七日，客曰：“我等须送主人入场矣。”张曰：“贵东人初次应试，恐有未谙者，我为公等助＿之。”偕至试场，张为之携具，护送至唱名处，遇素识者，误谓张亦入闱也。出则仍在妓室同乐，三场毕，与客接主人归。

    Explanation

    242

    In the seventh month, a guest suddenly arrived and spoke secretly with Wang. He called three guests in, and after a long time, they emerged, all with a look of panic. Zhang was then asked, "Since you have come to take the exam, are you a candidate for the imperial examination or a supervisor?" Zhang replied, "I am a supervisor." The guest then asked, "Is it genuine or fake?" Zhang said, "There is a certificate as proof, how could it be fake?" The guest pretended to doubt it, and Zhang opened his box to show the certificate. The three guests all said, "You are truly a person who has studied, but it is a pity that you did not enter the examination hall in time, and your career was ruined. Why not indulge in some sightseeing to relieve your boredom?" Zhang replied that he was poor, but the guest said, "We are friends, there is nothing we can't accommodate." So, he insisted on taking Zhang to a brothel, where they drank and played games, and the guest provided the funds. Zhang was so happy that he forgot to return, and he indulged in the pleasures of the brothel until the seventh day of the eighth month. The guest then said, "We must send the master into the examination hall." Zhang said, "Your esteemed master is taking the exam for the first time, and he might not be familiar with the procedures. I will assist you all." They accompanied Zhang to the examination hall, where Zhang carried the necessary items and escorted him to the place where names were called. They encountered someone he knew, who mistakenly thought Zhang had also entered the examination hall. After the three examinations were completed, they escorted Zhang back home with the guests.

    243

    张入己室，见箱箧似有启动者，然一物不失，置之不言。放榜之前一日，王盛席延宾，张亦与也。主人约通宵饮，以俟报捷者。半夜闻榜发，逃席而去者皆个中人也。张则心无所事，放怀大酌，忽人涌而入，报主人中矣。王大悦，贺客盈门，易席复饮。张醺醺然渐入醉乡。午刻，有噪而入者，阍人不能阻，直至筵前。有识张者曰：“此非汝等所觅之新孝廉耶？今果在是。”众皆贺曰：“奈何潜藏于此，俾吾等觅遍长安城矣。”张瞠目不知所谓，三客大恐，皆出席招众曰：“新贵人醉矣，勿扰之生怒。所需几何，我等代为分发可耳。”于是众随客出，张拍案曰：“异哉，异哉。”主人益惶急，呼三客速入，延张至他室，曰：“今日事，当实相告，足下凭空中式，其命也夫！谅亦欢喜无词。”张曰：“何为也？”

    Explanation

    243

    Zhang entered his own room and saw that the chests seemed to have been touched, but nothing was missing, so he ignored it. The day before the results were announced, Wang hosted a grand banquet and invited guests, and Zhang also attended. The host arranged for everyone to drink all night in order to await the announcement of good news. At midnight, they heard the announcement of the results, and those who left the banquet were all those who had a stake in the results. Zhang, however, had nothing to do with it and drank freely without concern. Suddenly, people rushed in, reporting that the host had passed. Wang was greatly pleased, and the house was filled with congratulatory guests. They changed the seating arrangement and continued to drink. Zhang became increasingly drunk. At midday, noisy people entered, and the gatekeeper could not stop them, and they reached the banquet. Those who recognized Zhang said, "Isn't this the new scholar-official you all were looking for? He is indeed here." Everyone congratulated and said, "How did he manage to hide here, causing us to search everywhere in Chang'an?" Zhang stared blankly and did not understand. The three guests were greatly frightened and stood up, calling out to the crowd, "The newly honored person is drunk. Do not disturb him and anger him. How much is needed? We can distribute it on his behalf." Therefore, everyone followed the guests out. Zhang thumped the table and said, "Strange, strange." The host became more anxious and called the three guests to quickly enter, leading Zhang to another room and saying, "Today's matter, I should tell you honestly. You passed the examination out of the blue, it is fate! I am sure you are happy and have no words." Zhang said, "What is this about?"

    244

    客曰：“我主人以数千金订某贡生代倩，不意某临场骤丁外艰，不能以正名入试，吾等先曾询得足下系赴试者，是以引入妓馆，以缚足下之身，而盗取监照，俾某顶名与试，不意其人学优心实，竟为足下完场，公然取中，其命也夫。”张始恍然大悟，继而曰：“科场大弊，性命相关，何先不谋诸我？欲我冒作孝廉，不可，不可。”主人闻之，与客佥长跪请命，张曰：“我寒士也，无力作举人。”王曰，“忝与足下同年，所需若干，惟命是从。”张直以万金为请，不敢不与，但曰：“愿足下能保此以终耳。”张曰：“噫，我寒士也，万金之资，不为少矣。科第之荣，不为贱矣。我之才力，何足以进取？与其悖入悖出，宁藏其拙，得以尽仰事俯育之心足矣。”再拜称谢，拥厚资而归故乡。此之谓富贵逼人来也。

    Explanation

    244

    The guest said, "My master paid thousands of gold pieces to have a certain recommended scholar take your place in the examination. Unexpectedly, that scholar encountered the death of his father on the day of the exam and could not register properly. We had previously found out that you were preparing to take the exam, so we brought you to a brothel, bound you, and stole your examination certificate to allow the scholar to take the exam under your name. Unintentionally, that person, who was both academically excellent and morally upright, completed the exam for you and was openly selected. It was fate, indeed." Zhang suddenly realized what had happened and then said, "The corruption in the examination system is of life-and-death importance. Why didn't you consult me first? To have me pretend to be a filial and upright official is unacceptable." Upon hearing this, the host and the guest both knelt down and begged for forgiveness. Zhang said, "I am a poor scholar and do not have the means to become a successful candidate." Wang replied, "We are fellow examinees who passed the same exam. Whatever amount you need, I will comply." Zhang requested a sum of ten thousand gold pieces and could not refuse, but he said, "I hope you can maintain this situation until the end." Zhang added, "Ah, I am a poor scholar. Ten thousand gold pieces is not a small sum. The honor of passing the exam is not insignificant. My abilities are not sufficient to achieve success. Rather than gain something unjustly and then lose it unjustly, I would prefer to keep my limitations hidden and be able to support my parents and nurture my children." He bowed twice and thanked them, then returned home with a substantial amount of wealth. This is what is meant by 'wealth and honor come unexpectedly and irresistibly.'

    245

    郝连大娘
