The innkeeper presented the best ginseng, selected forty ounces, and sent two staff members to carry the ginseng and exchange for payment, instructing them to deliver the sealed silver after the exchange was completed. The two staff members went together to the public hall, entered the room, and quickly reached the back hall.

    289

    其主以楼上为房，房内罗帐高悬，锦衾绣褥，洋表时钟之属，陈设焕然。其箱箧以四为式，自床东直至窗前数十号，乃命仆开第五排二十号贴地一箱，正拆银封秤兑，忽楼下有人操西音大呼而来曰：“今日虎邱之东奈何不赴？我寻将来也！”其主谓二伙曰：“客且坐，此我乡亲某刺史也，其向我借贷屡矣，不可使彼登楼，见如许物，则更扰累不清矣。”使仆以参及银皆归箱内锁之，匆匆下楼，闻被来客强拉之行。仆来送茶，传其主命曰：“烦客略坐，守去即来也。”乃反扣楼门而去。旋闻幼仆数人在楼下戏谑，始而喧哗，继而揪斗，有老苍头来，吆喝不应，鞭挞从之，幼仆不服，哭声震耳。

    Explanation

    289

    The master had converted the upper floor into a room, its interior adorned with high-suspended silk canopies, embroidered quilts and cushions, and Western-style watches and clocks, all arranged in splendid display. His boxes and chests were arranged in sets of four, stretching from the east side of the bed to the window, numbering in the dozens. He then ordered a servant to open the twenty-numbered box on the fifth row, which was placed on the floor; just as they were breaking the silver seal and weighing the silver, someone suddenly came running up from below, speaking in a Western accent and shouting, “Why did you not come to the east of Tiger Hill today? I am coming to look for you!”

    The master told his two attendants, “Please have the guest sit down. This is a relative of mine, a certain Magistrate, who has borrowed from me many times. We cannot let him go upstairs, for if he sees all these things, it will only cause more disturbances and confusion.” He ordered the servant to put the ginseng and silver back into the box and lock it, then hurried downstairs, hearing that the guest had been forcibly taken away. A servant came with tea and conveyed the master’s command: “Please the guest to sit for a moment; I will return shortly.” He then locked the door from the inside and left. Soon after, the sound of several young servants joking was heard downstairs; at first, there was noise, then a scuffle. An old servant came, shouted, but was not answered, and was then beaten. The young servant不服, and his cries could be heard clearly.

    290

    久之寂然。至晚无一人来，二伙馁甚，推窗望楼下，适行主同伙伴持灯唤入，二伙应曰：“毋庸着慌，人参与银俱在此。”行主登楼去扣，入门以火照之，二伙指此箱曰：“参银都在内也。”行主曰：“予自大门至楼，人物一空，似已迁去，不妨开其箱。”遂共观之，洞见楼下。细揣其箱，底与地板凿通，触机旋转，遍举各物，无甚贵重者。除罗帐外，其衾褥系高丽纸印洋花者。钟表仅有外面，中空无物。箱皆纸糊，中藏石块数包而已。始悟诸仆叫唤争斗时，正转运箱内之物，以人声嘈杂溷之，俾不觉。行主鸣诸官，且问与骗子往来之故，官曰：“以都中枢密信来，不能不答。”乃为缉捕，杳无踪影。

    Explanation

    290

    After a long while, it became silent. Until late, no one came. The two people were very hungry, so they pushed the window to look down at the floor below. Just as the innkeeper and his companions held lamps and called them in, the two people responded, "No need to panic, the ginseng and money are all here." The innkeeper went from the main door to the upstairs, and everything was gone, it seemed they had moved away. He said, "Let's open the box." They then opened it together and saw through to the downstairs. They carefully examined the box and found that the bottom of the box was drilled through to the floor. When touched, it rotated, lifting all the items inside. Nothing was very valuable, except for the silk canopy. The bedding was made of Korean paper with printed foreign flowers. The clock was only an outer shell, empty inside. The box was made of paper, and inside were several packages of stones. They finally realized that when the servants were shouting and fighting, they were actually moving the contents of the box, using the noisy human voices to mask it, so that they wouldn't notice. The innkeeper reported to the authorities and asked about the reason for dealing with fraudsters. The official said, "Because of a secret letter from the capital, I couldn't help but respond." Then they began to search for them, but they disappeared without a trace.

    291

    有耄而聋者，在武陵大关乞丐。关前来一官舫，扬旃鸣钲而泊，舱中有五品官，探首见丐，使从者扶之登舟。官细察之曰：“汝非某长者乎？前曾继我为义子，我因回籍求功名去，今幸选得是邦矣，不意义父一贫至此，儿之罪也。”丐知其误，姑应之曰：“我年老糊涂，前事如梦矣。”官曰：“虽系风尘面目，骨格犹存，儿识之无误。”饬从者请封翁先赴澡堂沐浴更衣，移舟至僻静处，颐养月余，为之栉沐，须发暗以胶粉染之，皤然一叟。谓之曰：“儿衣不称父身，将入市买金帛，为父修饰，以便同赴任所。但父曾在此行乞，恐城中有识者碍儿颜面，至铺内阅货时，合意只须摇首，不可多言。”丐允之。放舟入城，唤肩舆二乘，随带二仆，父子皆服五品衣冠，招摇过市。入银楼，换金约臂，每个重四两者两对，谓铺主曰：“我将赴缎局，偕往兑银可也。”铺主从之。入缎局，以单与局主观之，须三千余金货物。

    Explanation

    291

    There was an elderly and deaf beggar in the great checkpoint of Wuling, where he would often beg. One day, a government boat approached, its flags raised and gongs sounding as it docked. Inside the cabin was a fifth-rank official who, upon looking out, saw the beggar and ordered his attendants to help him onto the boat. The official examined him closely and said, "Are you not a certain elder? You were once adopted as my son. I left to return to my hometown to pursue official rank, and now I am fortunate to have been selected for this region. It is regrettable that my adoptive father is so poor; it is my fault." The beggar realized he had been mistaken and replied, "I am old and forgetful; the past is like a dream." The official said, "Despite your weathered appearance, your bones and structure still exist; I recognize you without doubt." He ordered his attendants to invite the elderly man to the bathhouse to bathe and change clothes, then moved the boat to a secluded place where they cared for him for over a month. They combed and washed him, darkening his hair and beard with glue and powder, transforming him into a white-haired old man. The official said, "My clothes do not fit my father's body; I will go into town to buy gold and silk to adorn him, so that we can travel together to my post. But since my father used to beg here, I fear that people in the city who recognize him might embarrass me. When we enter the shop, if you agree, just nod; do not speak much." The beggar agreed. They launched the boat into the city, called for two sedan chairs, and brought along two servants. Both father and son wore the clothing of a fifth-rank official and paraded through the city. They entered a silver shop and exchanged for gold bracelets, two pairs each weighing four taels. The official said to the shop owner, "I am going to the silk shop; we can exchange for silver together." The shop owner followed. They entered the silk shop, presented the list to the shop owner for review, and needed goods worth over three thousand taels.

    292

    邀入厅堂，殷勤款接，私叩其仆，知少者为严州二府，老者是其封翁，因二府之妹与首郡太尊之子结亲，送至会垣完姻，置办赠嫁物耳。局主分外趋承，设席宴之。官局邀金铺主同坐曰：“是我好友。”铺主唯唯听命。方自以为荣，局主乃出绉缎洋呢各物，先奉封翁阅之，封翁皆摇首，局主曰：“此皆上等货也，可以入贡，岂不堪服用耶？”官曰：“既不合父意，可与我妹观之。”饬舆夫扛抬货物，一仆押去，良久未回。

    Explanation

    292

    The official invited them into the main hall, received them with great courtesy and warmth, and privately inquired of his servant. The servant revealed that the younger man was the magistrate of Yanzhou and the older man was his father-in-law. The reason for their visit was that the younger man's sister was marrying the son of the chief magistrate of the capital, and they had come to the capital to finalize the wedding and prepare the gifts for the bride. The manager of the office went out of his way to serve them, setting up a banquet for them. The official invited the manager of the gold shop to sit with them, saying, "He is my good friend." The manager nodded and obeyed, feeling proud of this. The manager then brought out various silk and wool fabrics, first presenting them to the father-in-law to inspect. The father-in-law shook his head, indicating disapproval. The manager said, "These are all high-quality goods, suitable for tribute, how could they not be fit for use?" The official said, "Since it does not please the father, let us show it to my sister." He ordered the bearers to carry the goods, and one servant accompanied them, but they did not return for a long time.

    293

    又饬一仆往催，舆夫先回曰：“舟中人嘱我禀官，铀缎经姑娘目俱合意，不知应用何号平色银两，请官自去检点。”官谓局主曰：“烦侍父暂坐，我去兑银即回。”乃乘舆去，至舟中，多给舆夫钱文曰：“尔等往来劳苦，先吃饭去。”舆夫走而舟大行矣。丐坐局中，俟至更深不来。局主与金铺主皆惶急，不得不追问封翁，丐亦情虚，语言闪烁，群拥之鸣县，大令究得实情，亦无可如何，不过踩缉而已。释丐出，众褫其衣服，惟靴帽不合时宜，众皆不要。此丐尚藏五品冠，着朝靴，赤体叫化，见者大笑。

    Explanation

    293

    The official ordered another servant to hurry, but the driver returned first and said, "The people on the boat asked me to inform the official that the cloud brocade, after the young lady's inspection, is all satisfactory, but I do not know which grade of standard silver should be used. Please check for yourself." The official told the head of the office, "Please stay with your father for a moment; I will go to exchange the silver and return immediately." He then went by sedan chair to the boat, gave the driver extra money, and said, "You all have been working hard traveling back and forth; go and eat first." The driver left, and the boat set off immediately. The beggar sat in the office, but did not return until late at night. The head of the office and the owner of the gold shop were greatly alarmed and had to question the husband of the widow. The beggar also seemed to have something to hide, speaking hesitantly. The crowd surrounded him and reported to the county magistrate, who investigated and found the truth. However, there was nothing more to do but to investigate. The beggar was released, and the crowd stripped him of his clothes. Only his boots and hat were not in line with current fashion, and everyone refused to take them. This beggar still had hidden a hat of the fifth rank, wearing official court shoes, and was now begging naked, causing those who saw him to laugh.

    294

    蒋中丞抚浙时，察访官民之不法者重惩之。温郡某太守簠簋不饬，闻风而惧。突有外来三人操北音者，寓府廨侧，不言所事。凡太守升堂必往观之，暇则与馆人辩论太守之是非曲直。馆人怪之，密报府县。太守瞷俟三人出，遽搜其行李，得中丞访牌一道，凡太守私事俱在内，宛然紫印。又首县致永嘉令一面，尚未缄封，有云“蒋厅尊奉大宪命探事来治，诸祈照察”云云。太守益惧，归与永嘉令商酌，拟重赂之。

    Explanation

    294

    During the time Governor Jiang was governing Zhejiang, he investigated and severely punished officials and civilians who were lawless. The magistrate of Wenzhou, whose integrity was compromised, became afraid upon hearing the news. Suddenly, three men from the north, speaking northern dialects, arrived and lodged near the government office, without mentioning their business. Whenever the magistrate ascended the hall, they would always go to observe, and in their spare time, they would debate with the servants about the magistrate's right and wrong actions. The servants found this strange and secretly reported to the prefecture and county. The magistrate waited and watched, and when the three men left, he hastily searched their luggage, finding a visitation notice from the governor. All the magistrate's private matters were written on it, with a clear purple seal. Additionally, there was a letter from the county magistrate to the magistrate of Yongjia, which was still unsealed, stating, "Governor Jiang, in accordance with the orders of the higher authority, came to investigate matters, please pay attention and investigate." The magistrate became even more afraid, returned to discuss with the magistrate of Yongjia, and planned to heavily bribe them.

    295

    三人归，见箱箧俱乱，唤馆人诘之，馆人曰：“阁下去后，太尊来拜，必欲面晤。在房中坐俟半日方去，动阁下之行李者，其太尊乎？”三人默然，既而曰：“机事泄矣，盍去诸。”遂买舟行。馆人飞报太守，转令永嘉令往拜之。至舟中，仅有二人。令问蒋司马何在？二人曰：“我主驰回省垣去矣，留函奉呈。”令收阅，一系首县原函，一系司马自致，云公事匆促，不及谋面，深致抱歉之意。令白太守修书，馈柑橘四桶，中藏白物，因其仆追赠之。未几，太守至省晤蒋司马，讯无赴温事，知前物已入骗子手而不敢言。

    Explanation

    295

    Three people returned and found all their boxes in disarray. They called the innkeeper to question him, who said, "After you left, the high official came to pay respects and insisted on meeting face-to-face. He sat in the room for half a day before leaving, and it was likely the high official who disturbed your luggage." The three people fell silent, and then said, "Our secret matter has been revealed; why not leave?" So they hired a boat and set off. The innkeeper quickly reported to the governor, who then ordered the magistrate of Yongjia to go and pay respects. When they arrived at the boat, there were only two people. The magistrate asked where Jiang Sima was, and the two replied, "Our master hurried back to the provincial capital, and left a letter for you." The magistrate received and read the letter, which contained two documents: one from the chief county and one written by Jiang Sima himself. Jiang Sima apologized for the rush of official business and not having time to meet, expressing deep regret. The magistrate informed the governor and wrote a letter, sending four barrels of oranges as a gift, which contained valuable items hidden inside, delivered by his servant. Not long after, the governor went to the provincial capital to meet Jiang Sima, who inquired about any business in Wenzhou. The governor realized that the valuable items had already been taken by thieves but did not dare to speak of it.

    296

    有乘舟携仆访亲者，舟抵岸，谓其仆曰：“我先赴亲戚家，汝将行李收拾迁至头舱，我着人协同起去。”仆如命，坐于舟首俟之。有一人来，手携竹箩，内存大米数升。至河涘淘洗者，笑谓其仆曰：“此处泊舟，须要小心。昨日我目睹一舟亦如是停泊，一贼如是登舟。”其人即跃上曰：“舟中亦有如是一卷行李，贼如是窃取。”其人即背负之曰：“如是登岸竟去。”仆方笑曰：“此人何其呆也，自弃其米为人负重妆点何为耶？”舟子曰：“此人一去不顾，恐是骗子。”仆悟，急追，不知所之矣。

    Explanation

    296

    Someone was traveling by boat with a servant to visit relatives. When the boat reached the shore, he told his servant, "I will go to my relatives' house first. You will pack the luggage and move it to the bow of the boat. I will have someone help move it." The servant did as instructed and sat at the bow waiting. A person came, carrying a bamboo basket with several liters of rice. He came to the riverside to wash rice and smiled, telling his servant, "Be careful when mooring here. Yesterday, I saw a boat moored like this, and a thief boarded it in the same way." The person immediately jumped on and said, "There was also a bundle of luggage on the boat, which the thief stole." The person then carried it on his back and said, "He disembarked in the same way and left." The servant laughed and said, "How foolish this person is! Why would he abandon his rice and let someone carry his burden?" The boatman said, "This person left without a care, probably a scammer." The servant realized and rushed to follow, but lost sight of him.

    297

    有儒生赴岁试者，舟抵岸，行李迁起，尚未议定脚价，站坐行李上守之。忽有衣冠而来者，对之长揖曰：“兄台何自来耶？”生忙回揖，细认彼此皆不识。来人曰：“误矣。”揖谢不安而别。生退坐，仰跌倒地，方知行李已为人抽去。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：龙有嗜，可豢之；物先腐，虫生之。诸公皆有隙可乘，故入其玄中。然东坡云：“回视人间世，了无一事真。”诚哉是言也。无论癏阓中百货杂伪，试问医者充市，畴为张长沙、刘河间耶？星者盈街，畴为李虚中、袁天罡耶？当今入幕之宾，名为习申、韩者，不知申、韩是一是二，是何代人，何曾读不害、非子之书耶？或者谓九流杂技固不足道，甚至科甲遍宇内，何者为班、马、屈、宋之词华，何者为周、程、张、朱之理学，叩之茫然者居多，不过以八股时文骗功名耳。嗟乎！纷纷一大世界，人骗己，己骗人，有时己亦骗己，何怪乎骗子手耶！

    Explanation

    297

    A scholar was taking the annual examination, and his boat had just reached the shore. He moved his luggage and stood guard over it, waiting to agree on the price for carrying it. Suddenly, a man dressed in formal attire approached and bowed deeply, saying, "What brings you here, sir?" The scholar hurriedly returned the bow, but upon closer inspection, they realized they did not know each other. The stranger said, "It was a mistake," and bowed again before leaving in embarrassment. The scholar sat down, fell backward to the ground, and only then realized that his luggage had been taken away.

    The commentator said: Dragons have their preferences, and they can be tamed; objects decay first, and then pests infest them. All of you have weaknesses that can be exploited, hence they are penetrated into their hidden depths. Yet, as Su Dongpo once said, "Looking back at the human world, nothing is truly real." Indeed, that is true. Whether in the bustling market where goods are mixed with fakes, or among the doctors who fill the market, who are Zhang Zhongjing and Liu Jing? Or among the astrologers who fill the streets, who are Li Xuzhong and Yuan Tiangan? Today's inner-circle figures, who claim to study Shen and Han, do not know whether Shen and Han are one or two, or what dynasty they belonged to, or have ever read the books of Buhei and Feizi? Some might say that the nine schools of thought and various skills are not worth mentioning, but even the imperial examination system is widespread throughout the land. Who are the Ban, Ma, Qu, and Song who are known for their literary excellence? Who are the Zhou, Cheng, Zhang, and Zhu who are known for their rationalist philosophy? Most people are at a loss when asked, and they only use the eight-part essays to cheat for official positions. Alas! In this chaotic world, people deceive each other, and sometimes even deceive themselves. Is it surprising that there are so many deceivers?

    298

    方幼樗云：一片婆心，映醒世间多少自欺欺人之辈，笔亦古秀而健。
    　　
    　　某广文
    　　
    　　某学广文，髦而贪，诸生皆恶之。适有少年科甲之学使来，最恶白髯，见之辄曰，“汝已老大，好让后生矣。”必罢之。故斑白者皆闻声而惧。此广文须发皓然，遍求乌药，又不肯解囊，勒派诸生代觅，有谓之曰：“门生之戚宦于东粤，有好乌须药，名透骨丹。初染色红，三复则黑如明漆，泽润有光，真无价之宝也。门生感受师恩，仅分得少许，敬以奉赠。”广文大悦，谢而受之，如法试验，一染而红，再染而绛，三染而紫赤色，逾洗则逾鲜明，俨如道院中所塑之祝融像，见者大笑。寻其门人，不知所往，竟不敢赴试。致仕归去，生始告人，其药以龟溺熬紫草为之，即染鬃缨之法，岂能改色乎？此广文者，俾终生为红胡子矣。

    Explanation

    298

    The young scholar named Chū remarked: "A piece of maternal heart, illuminating the world and awakening so many who deceive themselves and others, the writing is also anciently elegant and robust."

    A Certain Scholar-Official

    A certain scholar-official, old and greedy, was hated by all the students. Just as a young scholar-official who had passed the imperial examinations came, he most disliked white beards. Upon seeing someone with white hair, he would always say, "You are already old, better let the younger ones take over." He would definitely dismiss them. Therefore, those with white hair were all afraid when they heard about it. This scholar-official had completely white hair and beard. He searched everywhere for black medicine, but he refused to pay for it, forcing the students to find it for him. One of his disciples said to him, "One of my relatives serves in Guangdong and has a good black hair medicine called Tougudan. It first dyed the hair red, but after three applications, it turned as black as polished lacquer, lustrous and shiny. It was truly a priceless treasure. Feeling the teacher's kindness, I managed to get a small amount and respectfully offered it as a gift." The scholar-official was very pleased and thanked him for accepting it. He tested it according to the method. After the first application, it turned red; after the second, it turned dark red; after the third, it turned purple-red. The more it was washed, the more vivid it became. It looked like the statue of Zhurong in a Taoist temple, and those who saw it laughed. He searched for his disciple, but did not know where he had gone. He finally dared not take the exam. He retired and returned home, and then told others. The medicine was made by boiling turtle urine with purple grass, the same method used to dye horsehair and ribbons. How could it change the color? Thus, this scholar-official was destined to have red hair for life.

    299

    三官救劫
    　　
    　　吾邑陈氏，有仆年逾六旬，职司田庄。夏月，随主收租回，至城外，仆渴，欲饮水，就岸滩伏河面吸之。其主在后，水中照见仆身影背插长旂，朱书“勒雷霆诛击恶犯一名某”，即其仆姓名。其主骇甚，问仆有所睹否，仆亦见之，起跪主前曰：“老奴自幼无欺心事，近又持经修善，更无为非，其为前世事耶？天命不可逃也，请主速回，将奴所司之簿籍检点收藏，二子不可信托，命其以榇来收殓奴尸。奴死此树下矣，不敢入城惊众也。”其主恋恋不忍，然疾风暴云旋起，不得不奔。

    Explanation

    299

    The Three Officials Save from Calamity

    In my hometown, there was a family named Chen. They had a servant who was over sixty years old and was responsible for managing the family's rural estates. One summer, the servant followed the master back to the city after collecting rent. Outside the city, the servant became thirsty and wanted to drink water, so he approached the shore and drank from the river surface. The master, who was behind him, saw in the water the servant's shadow with a long flag on his back. The flag was written in red ink, saying, "Summoning thunder to punish the evil offender named [specific name]," which was the servant's name. The master was very frightened and asked the servant if he had seen anything. The servant also saw it and got up, kneeling in front of the master and saying, "Old servant, since childhood, I have had no deceitful thoughts. Recently, I have been holding scriptures and practicing good deeds, and I have not done anything wrong. Is this a matter from a past life? Fate cannot be avoided. Please master, return quickly. Inspect and store the records that I am responsible for. The two sons cannot be trusted. Order them to bring a coffin to collect my body. I will die under this tree, and I dare not enter the city to alarm the people." The master was reluctant to leave, but a sudden storm with strong winds and dark clouds arose, forcing them to run away.

    300

    及家，雨至，闻轰击之声甚厉，立唤仆之妻子，告以故，皆号泣欲往护之，其主不允。未几晴霁，老仆欣然归，妻子见之，方讶其主之言谬妄，仆曰：“主去后雨即来，我跪树下，瞑目受死，心无杂念，惟口诵索习之三官经。闻雷声下击，及树而回。我张目视之，见一大道士高与树倍，身坐树颠，以袍幅垂护。我正探首仰观间，谅必雷神见我霹雳骤下，道士挥之以肱，雷始收声，道士亦不知所往。我幸免于难，将告主人虔诚祀之，以蒙其庥耳。”故吾邑崇奉三官菩萨者，至今甚盛。

    Explanation

    300

    Upon arriving home, rain came, and I heard the sound of thunder striking very loudly. I immediately called the servant's wife and children, told them the reason, and they all cried out, wanting to go protect him, but the master did not allow it. After a short while, the rain stopped and the sky cleared, and the old servant happily returned. His wife and children saw him and were just surprised that their master's words were absurd. The servant said, "The master left, and the rain immediately came. I knelt under the tree, closed my eyes and accepted my fate. My mind had no distractions, only reciting the Three Officials Sutra I had memorized. I heard the thunder strike, and it bounced back upon hitting the tree. I opened my eyes and looked at it, and I saw a great Taoist master who was twice as tall as the tree. He sat at the top of the tree, with his robe hanging down to protect me. I was just looking up when I assumed that the Thunder God saw me and the lightning struck down. The Taoist master waved his arm, and the thunder stopped. The Taoist master also disappeared. I was fortunate to escape, and I will inform the master to worship him sincerely, to receive his protection." Therefore, the worship of the Three Officials in our county is still very prevalent.

    301

    或曰：“扶灾危而救苦难，神之功德大矣。”芗厈曰：“然非也，亦视其人何如耳。以人道论，天君犹皇帝，三官犹宪司，雷神犹缇骑耳。帝使戳人，宪司历陈其人生平之善，德可掩眚，帝必赦之。使其人凶恶，宪司执奏以为能奉事，臣躬请曲宥之，则谴谪立加，能回天听耶？然则福善祸淫，彼苍之定理也，谄渎何为哉？”
    　　
    　　雷神
    　　
    　　《笔谈》载宋治平中，雷震天王寺，柱倒书十一字。《耳谈》载姑苏韩姓堂中，暴雷绕击砌石，上绘梅花一枝，纹理精妍。是雷神善书画，见于记载久矣。
    　　
    　　吾邻查氏宅，暑雨中暴雷绕垣奋击后，视垣面一砖去粉，琢磨朱书“令”字，径四寸余，秀健如赵文敏笔法，查氏抽换其砖以治邪疟，砖到即痊。吾邑有安国寺，夏月雷绕大殿，其左献往作旋螺文伽蓝神。后墙上绘一鸟迹，四趾带爪，阔大盈尺，作鹰拿状，入砖三分。僧以石灰补之，数日复现。

    Explanation

    301

    Someone said, "Helping those in danger and rescuing those in distress, the divine merit is great indeed." Xiang Yan replied, "That is not entirely true; it also depends on the person. From a human perspective, the supreme deity is like an emperor, the Three Officials are like constitutional officials, and the god of thunder is like a royal guard. If the emperor orders someone to be executed, the constitutional officials will list the person's good deeds throughout their life, and virtue can cover up faults, so the emperor will certainly pardon them. If the person is evil and cruel, and the constitutional officials present a case stating that the person can serve well, even if I personally request leniency, punishment will be immediately imposed, and can they change the emperor's mind? Therefore, blessing the virtuous and punishing the wicked is the fixed law of heaven, why flatter and show disrespect?"

    Thunder God

    According to the Pen Talk by Shen Kuo, during the reign of Emperor Shenzong of Song, a thunderstorm struck Tianwang Temple, causing a pillar to fall and reveal eleven characters written on it. The Ear Talk records that during a violent thunderstorm at a hall in Suzhou belonging to a family named Han, the lightning struck a stone step, and a branch of plum blossom was painted on it, with exquisite and beautiful details. It has been recorded for a long time that the god of thunder is skilled in painting and calligraphy.

    In my neighbor's house of the Zha family, during a summer rainstorm, a violent thunderstorm struck the wall, and upon inspection, a brick on the wall had lost its powder, revealing a red "command" character carved into it, about four inches in diameter, with a style as elegant and strong as that of Zhao Mengfu. The Zha family used this brick to treat a case of malaria, and the patient recovered immediately upon receiving it. In our county, there is An Guo Temple, and during the summer, a thunderstorm surrounded the main hall. On the left side of the hall, there is a spiral patterned guardian deity. On the back wall, a bird's footprint was painted, with four toes and claws, about a foot wide, resembling an eagle grasping something, and it was carved into the brick by three parts. The monk filled it with lime, but it reappeared after several days.

    302

    吾戚金氏楼居，炎暑之际，以幼孩睡床上已酣，其父母皆下楼去。大雨如注，霹雳一声，穿楼而过，在室之人莫不惊悸丧神，其父母忆及幼孩在床，谅必惊毙矣。登楼视之，前后窗俱辟，帐亦高卷，不见幼孩。觅之，以席卷横置于床下，取出观之，儿睡犹未醒也。席面朱书一字，非篆非符，无识之者。
    　　
    　　又郑孝廉暑月赴乡遇雨，避于房檐下，雷声甚厉，忽见云中堕一火球，后有数十神将体为云护，惟足着尖靴，相随电光疾下，大震一声，半里外田间击死一人，背有古篆，不知所云。然则雷书恒有之，不足异，所可异者，自古有方履无尖靴，神亦从时，其为今之神欤？

    Explanation

    302

    My relative, Mr. Jin, lives in a tower. During the hot summer, with the young child already asleep on the bed, his parents all went downstairs. Heavy rain poured down, and a thunderclap passed through the building. Everyone in the room was terrified and lost their composure. The parents remembered the child on the bed and thought they must have been terrified to death. They went upstairs to check. The front and back windows were open, and the canopy was fully raised, but the child was not seen. They searched for him and found him rolled up like a scroll and placed under the bed. They took him out and saw that he was still sleeping and hadn't woken up. A single character was written in red on the scroll, neither seal script nor a talisman, and no one could recognize it.

    Another time, Mr. Zheng, a scholar-official, encountered rain while traveling to his hometown during the hot summer month. He took shelter under the eaves of a house. The thunder was very loud. Suddenly, he saw a fireball fall from the clouds. Behind it were dozens of divine generals whose bodies were protected by clouds. Only their feet were in pointed boots, and they followed the lightning down quickly. A great thunderclap, and someone in the fields half a li away was struck and killed. The back had ancient script, which no one could understand. Therefore, such inscriptions from lightning are always present and are not unusual. What is unusual is that since ancient times there have been flat shoes, not pointed boots, and the gods also follow the times, perhaps they are gods of the present era.

    303

    第八卷
    　　
    　　汤文正
    　　
    　　苏郡社会，甲于全省。暮春之际，举国若狂。其会首绅耆咸集神庙，公议分坊敛资，置办彩衣，务极鲜艳。搬演故事，则翻新出奇，争奢斗富。即如寿星之袍，以珍珠满缀寿字。百蛮之宝，以径尺翡翠盘盛金叶火齐珠。钟进士送妹，以二尺余之白玉瓶，内插珊瑚枝，上站云拥美人，随小鬼执绣盖，此之谓抬阁。一座之价，使人不能估测。不仅以金玉镯结阑干而已，如是者数十座。又有所谓肩阁兜子，彩龙马道等类，不计其数。以是故，远近来观者千万人，凡临街之楼，招集妇女，皆凭栏俯瞩，粉白黛绿，迷眩一时。故少壮之徒，争赌目办，百十成群，随会来往，评定美人。今年以某处某人为状元，必复之四五日，众议佥同，则探其父母夫家，皆有垂涎之意。

    Explanation

    303

    Chapter 8**
    Tang Wenzheng

    Suzhou is the best in the entire province. At the onset of late spring, the whole country goes mad with excitement. The leaders and elders gather at the temple to publicly discuss and divide the neighborhoods to collect funds, prepare colorful costumes, and ensure they are extremely vivid. The performances of stories are innovative and novel, with competitions in luxury and wealth. For example, the robe of the God of Longevity is adorned with pearls spelling out the character for "longevity." The treasures of a hundred ethnic groups are placed in a jade dish measuring a foot in diameter, holding gold leaves and fire opals. In the story of Zhong Jinshi sending his sister away, a white jade vase over two feet tall is used, with coral branches inserted inside, and a cloud-like figure of a beautiful woman standing on top, accompanied by small demons holding embroidered parasols. This is called a "tai ge." The price of one such structure is beyond estimation. Not just adorned with gold and jade bracelets on the railings, there are dozens of such structures. There are also "shoulder pavilions" and "covered carriages," as well as colorful dragon and horse paths, innumerable in quantity. Therefore, thousands of people from far and near come to watch. All women on the streets gather at the windows to look down, their white and green makeup dazzling everyone at the time. Thus, young men compete in betting on who has the best eyesight, forming groups of tens or hundreds, following the procession, and evaluating and judging the beauty of women. This year, someone from a certain place is declared the "zhuangyuan" (the top beauty), and this must be repeated for four or five days. Everyone agrees unanimously, and then they investigate their parents and families, all having desires for them.

    304

    时有赵五官者，年十七八，已订婚孙姓，在钱局作伙。是日随众纵观，知众所定之元系及笄友，访之即孙姓，赵五官之未婚妻也。五官意乱神迷，惟欲速娶为快。第岁俸十余千钱，不敷奉母，何以为婚，与母谋之，母告以父在日，有钱会，应得一百余千，或可敷用，但须摇点。未能操必得之权。五官忆及大五圣堂其神甚灵，有求必应。至摇会之日，以香烛往告云，如得会完娶之后，夫妇偕来叩谢。祝毕赴会，举骰摇之，得三十六点，如愿而归。遣媒订期纳采迎娶，花烛之下，妇果艳丽倾城。

    Explanation

    304

    At that time, there was a man named Zhao Wuguan, who was seventeen or eighteen years old and had already arranged a marriage with a woman from the Sun family. He worked at the money bureau. On that day, he followed the crowd to watch and learned that the person chosen was his betrothed. Upon inquiry, it was confirmed that she was the Sun family woman, Zhao Wuguan's fiancée. Zhao Wuguan was in a state of confusion and excitement, eager to marry quickly. However, his annual salary of over ten thousand coins was insufficient to support his mother, making it difficult to afford the marriage. He consulted his mother, who told him that his father had a savings account that would yield over a hundred thousand coins, which might be sufficient. However, the outcome depended on a lottery, and he could not guarantee success. Zhao Wuguan remembered the Great Five Saints Temple, where the deities were very powerful and always granted requests. On the day of the lottery, he went to the temple with incense and candles and prayed, saying that if he won, he would come back with his wife to thank the deities. After praying, he attended the lottery, rolled the dice, and got thirty-six points, achieving his wish. He sent a matchmaker to set a date for the wedding and arrange the betrothal gifts. On the night of the wedding, his wife was indeed beautiful and captivating.

    305

    五官不禁狂喜，惟新妇似惜夫貌陋，然亦无词。三三朝后，具牲礼同妇往酬五圣而回，妇已卸妆矣。至晚忽又盛饰端坐，五官入房，妇正色拒之曰：“我非汝家人矣。五圣将迎我为夫人，法驾将临，汝其速退，勿干神怒也。”五官疑其有疯疾，即出延医，医至而妇已僵，异香满室，遥闻音乐之声。五官大恸，殡殓后，以神夺民妇讼于邑宰，官斥其诬妄，不准。奔告于府，太守视其人神色悲忿，姑纳其词。五官赴虎邱问卜。有某瞎子者，名重一时，为之卦成，骇曰：“异哉，汝欲与神讼耶？讼果胜，今夜子时听审，勿远离也。”五官欣然归寓。

    Explanation

    305

    The Five Officials could not help but wildly rejoice, but the new wife seemed to pity her husband's ugly appearance, though she had no words to express it. After three days, they prepared sacrificial offerings and went with the wife to offer them to the Five Saints and returned. The wife had already removed her makeup. By evening, she suddenly adorned herself beautifully and sat upright. When the Five Officials entered the room, the wife sternly refused them, saying, "I am no longer your family member. The Five Saints will welcome me as their lady, and the imperial carriage will soon arrive. You must retreat quickly, lest you offend the gods' anger." The Five Officials suspected she had gone mad and immediately called a doctor. When the doctor arrived, the wife was already stiff, and a strange fragrance filled the room. They could hear the distant sound of music. The Five Officials were greatly saddened. After burying and dressing her in her funeral attire, they reported to the magistrate of the county that the gods had taken a civilian wife. The official rebuked them, saying their claims were false and absurd, and disallowed their report. They rushed to report to the prefecture, where the prefect saw their sad and angry expressions and temporarily accepted their words. The Five Officials went to Tiger Hill to consult a fortune-teller. There was a famous blind man at the time, and after casting the divination, he was shocked and said, "Strange indeed, you want to litigate against the gods? If you win the lawsuit, listen to the judgment tonight at midnight, and do not leave far away." The Five Officials returned to their lodging with great joy.

    306

    是时汤文正公抚江南，正直之声震于遐迩，神亦畏之，暗随五官来。知太守已收呈状，夤夜入藩库，缘库内有三金宝，系守藏之物。五圣盗之，穿库楼而出，神光烛天，巡守者皆误为火起，鸣钲高叫，方伯亲临，吏役俱集，开库检点，惟金宝三不知所之矣。五圣以宝入抚院内宅，示梦于夫人曰：“予为吴江之五圣神，被顽民妄控于府，求为庇佑，愿以金宝为馈。”乃掷之于妆台。夫人闻震声而觉，则金宝三枚俨在。

    Explanation

    306

    At that time, Grand Secretary Tang was governing Jiangnan. His upright reputation was known far and wide, and even the gods feared him. They secretly followed the officials. Knowing that the governor had already received the complaint, they entered the provincial treasury at night because there were three golden treasures inside the treasury, which were the guarded items. The Five Saints stole them and escaped through the treasury building. Their divine light illuminated the sky, and the guards all mistakenly thought it was a fire. They sounded the gong and shouted loudly, and the governor personally arrived. All the officials and servants gathered. They opened the treasury to inspect, but only the three golden treasures were missing.

    The Five Saints brought the treasures into the governor's inner residence and appeared in a dream to the governor's wife and said, "I am the Five Saints of Wujiang. We were wrongfully accused by the stubborn people at the prefecture. We seek your protection. We offer the golden treasures as a gift." They threw them onto the dressing table. The governor's wife heard a loud noise and woke up, and the three golden treasures were clearly there.

    307

    爰命俾女请文正入，语以异事，言未毕而门外传点声，请公升座。文正出，则两司百官咸聚，方伯以失宝事告。文正笑曰：“盗易得，勿张惶也。”问首府曰：“昨有人控神夺民妇者乎？以其词来。”太守曰：“有之。”饬从者取到，立传赵五官，文正面鞠之得实，归宝于库。具狱牒正一真人府请殛之，真人复文曰：“神虽不正，妇亦淫邪。憎其夫而悦神貌，致启奸图，孽由自作。然阴阳道隔，虽和同强申，革圣神之号，遣发幽都，长为饿鬼，以正其罪可也。”文正命地方官扑五圣像，居民争毁之，今改为总官堂矣。

    Explanation

    307

    Upon being summoned, Wen Zheng was invited into the presence, and strange events were recounted to him. Before he had finished speaking, a sound of gongs was heard outside the door, and officials were asked to take their seats. Wen Zheng emerged, and the officials and hundred officials gathered. The provincial governor reported the loss of the treasure. Wen Zheng smiled and said, "The thief is easily caught; do not be alarmed." He asked the prefect, "Did someone report that a god took a woman from the people yesterday? Bring his statement." The prefect replied, "Yes." He ordered his attendants to bring the suspect, who was immediately summoned. Wen Zheng personally interrogated him and found the truth, and the treasure was returned to the treasury. A legal document was prepared and sent to the office of the Taoist master, requesting punishment. The Taoist master replied in writing: "Although the god is not righteous, the woman is also licentious. She hated her husband and admired the god's appearance, leading to evil plans, the evil is self-inflicted. Although the paths of yin and yang are separated, even if they try to force a union, remove the titles of saint and god, send them to the underworld, become a hungry ghost forever, to punish their crimes appropriately." Wen Zheng ordered local officials to destroy the images of the five gods, and the residents competed to destroy them. Today, it is known as the General's Hall.

    308

    科场五则
    　　
    　　吴生，浙之槜李人，儒而兼贾，不预科场久矣。是年梦其父祖催令入闱，生自知此道荒疏，无可侥幸之处，置之勿论。嗣又梦父祖厉色督责之曰：“汝若不去，场中缺一孝廉矣。是为违天，违天不祥，必有后祸。”生乃述其无文何遇，父笑曰：“易耳，今科头题为［乡人皆好之］一节，本家兰陵先生有此文，汝入闱时，访而录之可也。”生始欣然，温故入闱，访问兰院先生所在。夫吴兰陔者，时文中之名手也，其门下从学之徒数百人，发科甲入词林者甚众。惟先生落笔高古，屡困场屋。

    Explanation

    308

    Five Principles of the Examination Hall

    Wu Sheng, a native of Juzi, Zhejiang, was both a scholar and a merchant, and had not participated in the imperial examinations for a long time. In that year, he dreamed that his ancestors were urging him to enter the examination hall. He knew that he was unprepared and saw no chance of success, so he dismissed the idea. Later, he dreamed that his ancestors sternly scolded him, saying, "If you do not go, there will be a deficiency of a scholar-official in the examination hall. This would be a violation of heaven, and violating heaven is inauspicious, bringing sure misfortune." Wu then explained his lack of preparation, and his father laughed and said, "It's easy, the head theme of this year's examination is 'The People All Praise Him.' My family's Mr. Lanling has written on this topic. When you enter the examination hall, seek it out and copy it." Wu was then delighted and reviewed his old knowledge before entering the examination hall, searching for the location of Mr. Lanlan. Mr. Wu Langai was a renowned writer of his time, with hundreds of disciples studying under him, many of whom passed the examinations and entered the literary bureaucracy. However, Mr. Lanlan's writing was highly refined and ancient, and he had often failed in the examinations.

    309

    时年已五旬外矣，功名之念甚切。生访得之，致其景仰之意，曰：“闻先生窗下有［乡人皆好之］一节，题文为士林传诵，小子与先生居隔百里，未由亲炙，今日尚闲，务求赐教。”兰陔见其殷勤难却，录出与观，生曰：“容小子携回号舍，细细揣摹可乎？”兰陵颔之，生欢喜捧去。未几，兰陔亦往生号内答之，见其在卷上挥毫疾书，讶曰：“尚未出题，何得有文？”生笑曰：“小子读先生文，不忍释手，恭缮试卷，以志钦佩。即文不对题，不过被黜而已，亦所甘心。”兰陵曰：“我累足下矣。奈何！奈何！”遂别去。

    Explanation

    309

    At the age of over fifty, the desire for fame and achievement was very strong in him. The student sought out the scholar and expressed his admiration, saying, "I have heard that there is a section in your writings titled 'All the villagers admire him,' which has been widely praised in scholarly circles. I live a hundred miles away from the master and have not had the opportunity to learn directly from him. Today, I still have some free time and earnestly request your guidance." Lan Gai, seeing his sincere and persistent request, could not refuse and wrote out the passage for him to read. The student said, "May I take this back to my study and carefully ponder over it?" Lan Ling nodded in agreement, and the student happily took the passage away. Soon after, Lan Gai also went to the student's study to respond. He saw the student writing quickly on the paper and was surprised, asking, "The question hasn't even been given yet, how can you have written something?" The student smiled and said, "I couldn't put down your writings, so I respectfully prepared the examination paper to express my admiration. Even if the content does not match the question, I am only willing to be rejected." Lan Ling said, "I am troubling you too much. What shall I do? What shall I do?" And then he left.

    310

    逢相识者告之，一时传作新闻。是夜试题出果对，兰陔不胜悔恨曰：“得意之作既被人录去，谅天意终身不得售矣。”遂信笔一挥，交卷而出。二三场为门人苦劝讫事。是科竟中。兰陔以旧作入见座主曰：“门生薄有微名，闱中之作，聊以塞责，不堪为多士寓目，请以此文易之。”座主曰：“可，虽然此文若在场中，未必中式，盖阅卷如走马看花，气机流走者易于动目，此文非反复数过，不知其佳处。试官有此闲情乎？故无益也。”兰陔悟，随有《读墨一隅》之选。

    Explanation

    310

    A familiar acquaintance was told about it, and it quickly became a piece of news. That night, when the exam questions were revealed and the results announced, Lan Gai could not bear the regret and sorrow, saying, "My favorite work has already been recorded by someone else; I suspect that heaven's will is that I will never succeed." He then wrote casually and submitted his paper. He was persuaded by his disciples to continue the exams. He ultimately passed the exam. Lan Gai presented his old work to the examiner, saying, "I have a slight reputation as a student, but the work I did in the examination hall, just to fulfill the requirement, is not worthy of the eyes of many scholars. Please allow me to replace it with this one." The examiner said, "Yes, although this work, if it were in the examination hall, might not have passed, for reading the papers is like looking at flowers from a galloping horse. Works that flow smoothly are easier to catch the eye, but one must read it several times to appreciate its merits. Do the examiners have the leisure to do this? Therefore, it is of no benefit." Lan Gai realized, and he then compiled a selection called Readings in Calligraphy.

    311

    先是吴生归，不作第二人想，整顿衣冠，预备筵宴，思作新孝廉之乐若登天然。瞬过重阳，闻榜发，而好音竟绝，觅得题名录观之，兰陔高捷矣。怨恨之极，怒其父祖曰：“何为诓骗子孙耶？”欲毁木主。夜复梦父祖来，怒责之曰：“不肖子无知，此中自有天命，汝若不抄截兰陔之文，彼必自录，又不得中式矣。”生曰：“彼之中与不中，与我何千耶？”父曰：“闱中饭食皆出帑项，即为天禄，非生时注籍，岂易得哉？汝命中尚有一次，不完总不得安静也。”生悟，次科仍入闱，其友曰：“前此得极妙文章尚不入彀，今何为耶？”生曰：“公等皆抡元夺魁手，我自来领钦赐饭食以了公案耳。”

    Explanation

    311

    Before the examination results were announced, Wu Sheng returned home, not expecting to come in second. He prepared his attire ceremoniously and arranged a banquet, thinking of the joy of becoming a new xiao-lian as if it were ascending to heaven. Soon after the Double Ninth Festival, he heard that the results were posted, but there was no good news for him. Upon finding the list of successful candidates, he discovered that Lan Gai had passed the examination. Filled with extreme resentment, he angrily said to his ancestors, "Why did you deceive your descendants?" He wanted to destroy the ancestral tablet. That night, he dreamt that his ancestors came to him, angrily scolding him and saying, "Unworthy son, ignorant! There is a heavenly decree in this matter. If you had not plagiarized Lan Gai's work, he would have written it himself and would not have passed the examination." Wu Sheng replied, "Does his passing or failing have anything to do with me?" His ancestor said, "The food in the examination hall comes from the treasury, it is a heavenly salary. It is not something easily obtained by being registered at birth. You still have one chance in your fate. You will not be at peace until you complete it." Realizing this, Wu Sheng decided to participate in the next examination. His friend said, "Before this, you wrote an excellent article and still did not pass the examination. What are you doing now?" Wu Sheng replied, "You all are capable of winning the top honors. I am here to receive the imperial banquet and settle the matter."

    312

    北闱大学士某公典试，题为“回也闻一以知十”二句。所取文内有用《易经》天一、地二，及七日来复，八月有凶等语，不慊士心。好事者撰新戏云：玉帝巡狩，忽见怨气上冲阻驾，问于太白星官，奏曰：“此时人间乡试，士子有不才而遇，才而不遇者，不安义命，故有此怨毒之气，致干圣驾。”帝曰：“乡试取士，俱有定额，本属善法，若二教中仙佛漫无定数，致有弄法欺人，兴妖作怪之辈，朕甚虑之。亦将仿照人闱，举行乡试可乎？”太白曰：“善哉，善哉，不可缓矣。”爰命文昌历举文理优长之神仙，以充试官，如儒童菩萨、文殊菩萨及地下修文郎辈，皆命往洞天福地，纷纷去矣。惟玉京尚无典试者。帝问太白星官，太白曰：“此处应位尊爵显者为之。

    Explanation

    312

    In the northern examination district, a grand councilor presided over the examination, with the question being "Hui heard one and knew ten." The selected essays included phrases from the I Ching such as "Heaven One, Earth Two," "seven days to return," and "eight months with calamity," which did not satisfy the scholars. A well-wisher then wrote a new play: The Jade Emperor was on an inspection tour when he suddenly saw a cloud of resentment blocking his path. He asked the planet Venus, "At this time, there are scholars in the human world who are talented but fail to succeed, and those who lack talent but succeed. They are discontent with their fate, hence this aura of resentment and poison is obstructing Your Majesty." The Emperor replied, "The provincial examination selects scholars with fixed quotas, which is a good system. If immortals and Buddhas in the two religions have no fixed numbers, leading to those who manipulate rules to deceive people and create chaos, I am deeply concerned. Should I also imitate the human examination system and hold a provincial examination for them?" The planet Venus responded, "Excellent, excellent, it should not be delayed." He then ordered the God of Literature to list immortals who excel in literature and reason to serve as examiners, such as the Bodhisattva of Learning, the Bodhisattva of Wisdom, and scholars from the underworld. They were all sent to the paradises, leaving in droves. However, the heavenly capital still lacked an examiner. The Emperor asked the planet Venus, "This position should be filled by someone of high rank and prestige."

    313

    ”乃举齐天大圣孙悟空，帝曰：“尊矣，显矣，奈其不通文墨乎？”太白曰：“天下试官未必尽通，况猴子最灵，奉命之后，自能设法延请高明相助，可无虑矣。”爰召悟空，命之主试，不得推辞。悟空不敢违命，入文昌宫，请友为助。文昌曰：“我宫内天聋、地哑二童俱被人重聘去矣，焉有余人！”悟空退思，吕纯阳系大唐进士，必通文理，往商之。吕祖曰：“我已奉命典试琅环福地，何暇相助？无已，或访知命之士，以命取人，亦不为屈。

    Explanation

    313

    "Then the Great Sage Equal to Heaven, Sun Wukong, was selected. The emperor said, 'Respected and eminent, but he does not know how to read or write?' The White Rabbit replied, 'Officials who administer exams may not all be proficient,何况猴子最灵，奉命之后，自能设法延请高明相助，可无虑矣。' The emperor summoned Sun Wukong and ordered him to preside over the examination, and he must not refuse. Sun Wukong dared not disobey, so he went to the Palace of Literature to ask for friends to help. The god of literature said, 'The two children, Heaven's Deaf and Earth's Dumb, have already been heavily engaged by others, how can there be any others?' Sun Wukong then thought, Lü Chún'yáng, a Jinshi from the Tang Dynasty, must be well-versed in literature, so he went to consult with him. Lü Chún'yáng said, 'I have already been ordered to administer the examination in the Land of Immortals, how can I have time to help? If there is no other way, perhaps I can find someone fated to help, and it won't be a burden.'"

    314

    ”悟空往访鬼谷先生，行抵北天门，与玄天上帝晤，问知去意，上帝笑曰：“若须知命者，不必远求，我座下龟灵圣母为当今第一能手。”悟空悦，乃召圣母，见之曰：“蠢然一物，请入闱中，未免不雅。”圣母曰，“我之法身，能大能小，能现能隐，请缩为金钱龟，藏于大圣袖中，则人皆不觉。及阅文时，我知其命应中式者，以我八卦衣在大圣前显之，大圣取之无误也。”悟空从之。故是科多取八卦者。戏为皇上所闻，罚试官俸，而停用泛词者三科会试。

    314

    Wukong went to visit Master Guiguzi and arrived at the North Heavenly Gate, where he met with the Jade Emperor. After learning of Wukong’s intentions, the Jade Emperor smiled and said, “If you seek to know your destiny, you need not go far. My disciple, the Tortoise Spirit Holy Mother, is the foremost expert today.” Wukong was pleased and summoned her. Upon seeing her, he said, “A mere creature, please enter the enclosure, but it’s not very elegant.” The Tortoise Spirit Holy Mother replied, “My divine body can be large or small, visible or invisible. Please shrink into a turtle, hidden in the Great Sage’s sleeve, and no one will notice. When reading the texts, I will know who is destined to pass the exam. I will reveal it with my Eight Trigrams robe in front of the Great Sage, and the Great Sage will take it without error.” Wukong agreed to her plan. As a result, many candidates in that examination were selected based on the Eight Trigrams. The emperor heard about this and punished the examiners, banning the use of vague terms for three consecutive exams.

    315

    江右召贡生有三子，皆业儒。长为廪膳生，次为增广生，其三应童试十余年不售，长为老童生矣。其父厌恶之，谪在厨房司爨，故“烧火三相公”之名噪于戚里。值开科年，长次二子高列优等，将届入闱。父命三相公同往会垣，供奔走之役。三相公欣然应命，入见其妻呜咽悲泣，三相公叩其故，妻曰：“二伯人也，汝亦人也，何二伯若座上客，汝为灶下养耶？已属无耻，今乐为送考之下走，为汝妻者，何颜立于妯娌间耶？”三相公曰：“我岂乐为？父命难违耳。”妻曰：“我何敢教汝违父命？汝若有志，亦得进场，我与有荣施矣。”三相公曰：“童生焉得与大试，奈我命何？”妻曰：“汝此去必谒丈人，是为方伯管库之吏，捐纳省监，系属专司。我有金珠在，汝以质与丈人，纳一监生，亦可观光闱屋矣。”三相公欢然从之。偕父兄赴会垣，谒妻父纳监讫，归谓父曰：“丈人强与儿监，欲儿就试也。

    Explanation

    315

    A scholar from Jiangxi was summoned to take the imperial examination and had three sons, all of whom pursued the Confucian classics. The eldest was a recipient of a government stipend, the second had been promoted to a higher rank, and the third had taken the preliminary examination for over ten years without success, thus becoming a "long-time unsuccessful candidate." Disappointed by his youngest son's lack of progress, the father demoted him to the kitchen to cook, earning him the nickname "the third son who cooks." This name spread throughout the neighborhood.

    When the year of the imperial examination arrived, the two older sons performed exceptionally well and were about to enter the examination hall. The father ordered the third son to accompany them to the capital to assist with their travel. The third son happily accepted the command and went to see his wife, who was weeping bitterly. He asked her why she was crying, and she replied, "Your eldest brother is a man, and you are a man too. Why is he treated as a guest, while you are treated as a kitchen servant? This is already shameful. Now you are willing to be a mere servant for the exam, how can I face my sisters-in-law?" The third son said, "How could I be happy about it? I cannot disobey my father's command." His wife said, "How dare I teach you to disobey your father? If you have the will, you can also take the exam, and I will share in your glory." The third son said, "How can a long-time unsuccessful candidate like me participate in the major examination? What can I do about it?" His wife said, "When you go this time, you must visit your father-in-law, who is an official responsible for managing the treasury. You can purchase a position as a student in the provincial academy through financial contribution, which is his designated duty. I have some gold and jewels. You can pledge them to your father-in-law and purchase a position as a student in the academy, which will allow you to participate in the examination."

    The third son happily followed her advice. He accompanied his father and elder brothers to the capital, visited his father-in-law, and purchased the position as a student in the academy. Upon returning home, he told his father, "My father-in-law forced me to purchase a position as a student in the academy, and he wants me to take the exam."

    316

    ”父曰：“遗才不取，或贴出墙东，看汝何言对丈人耶？”及录遗，公然附取，得随两兄入闱。因三相公食量甚宏，其父为之备绍酒、金蹄、烧鸭、熏鹅之类，满足一挑。三相公领卷入号舍，见其左右邻皆武林寒士，三相公慷慨食之，邻士皆悦。是夜题来，“譬如为山”四句，至次日之下午，三相公仍大烹以延邻士，皆议论名人作法，三相公默然。

    Explanation

    316

    The father said, "If a talented person is not selected, they might be overlooked, like sticking out east of the wall. What will you say to your father-in-law then?" When the list of candidates was recorded, the third son openly attached himself to the list and was able to enter the examination hall with his two brothers. Because the third son had a very large appetite, his father prepared a grand feast for him, including Shao wine, golden trotters, roast duck, and smoked goose, filling a whole basket. The third son carried his exam papers into the examination room and saw that his neighbors on both sides were poor scholars from Wulin. He ate generously with them, and all his neighbors were pleased. That night, he wrote down four lines from the Analects: "It is like building a mountain." The next afternoon, the third son still held a grand feast to invite his neighbors, and they all discussed the methods of famous people. The third son remained silent.

    317

    邻士叩其故，三相公实告以初次观光，遇大题敷衍难成，奈何？邻士笑曰：“若欲完篇，何难之有？十三经中，不乏山字语头，莫管义理，填砌成文，则洋洋数千言，尚引用不完也。”三相公亦实告以腹内空虚之故，邻士争为写书，且教以运用之法。三相公大悟，挥洒自如，千言立就。试毕，其长次二兄皆录文呈父，父乃举酒阅文，恬吟密咏，推为必售之作。三相公亦技痒，以其稿恭呈父前，其父拍案大呼曰：“浮泛至此，亦可以见人乎？不知愧恧，至汝极矣。”其兄碎其文，喝令速退，毋触父怒也。三相公抱头鼠窜而泣。是科主试者，非邓奇即帅怪，此二公者，生性偏僻，好为诡异。十五日，例设抡元宴，隔帘相叙。

    Explanation

    317

    The neighbor asked the reason, and the three young men truthfully told him about their first time taking the imperial examination, encountering a difficult topic that was hard to develop, and what should be done? The neighbor laughed and said, "If you want to complete the essay, what difficulty is there? In the Thirteen Classics, there are no shortage of phrases starting with 'mountain.' Do not worry about the meaning or logic; fill and construct the text, then thousands of words will flow abundantly, and they cannot be fully cited." The three young men also truthfully told him about the reason for their empty minds, and the neighbor strove to write the book and taught them the method of application. The three young men had a great enlightenment, writing freely and effortlessly, completing a thousand words immediately. After the exam, the eldest and second brother both copied the text and presented it to their father. The father then lifted his wine and read the text, reading softly and intently, recommending it as a sure success. The youngest brother also itched to show off, respectfully presenting his draft to his father. The father banged the table and shouted, "So superficial, can it be shown to others? Does not know shame, you are the worst." The older brothers tore up the text, ordering him to leave quickly, do not provoke the father's anger. The youngest brother ran away like a rat and cried. The main examiner of this year is either Duke Deng or General Strange, these two gentlemen have eccentric personalities and like to do strange things. On the fifteenth day, as usual, the banquet for the top scholar was held, and they conversed through a curtain.

    318

    内则正、副二主试，带同十八房考官，外则监临、中丞相率提调各官合宴。此夜，公请主试宣明题义，应取何等文字以定元魁。大主试笑曰：“文无定法，惟真山真水者中。”此戏言也，中丞不觉失声耍笑，众官和之，哄堂一粲。大主试怒，拂袖而起曰：“我欲云云，谁敢尔尔。”竟罢宴入内，众官不欢而散，皆私议特觅此等浮泛之作以玩之。幸有三相公之文在，二房官得之，笑不可遏。众官闻声趋视，曰：“有此不通主试，即有此不通举子。可谓千古奇遇。

    Explanation

    318

    Inside, the chief examiner and deputy examiner were accompanied by eighteen assistant examiners; outside, the supervisor and vice-censor led the various officials in a banquet. On this night, the officials invited the chief examiner to explain the meaning of the examination questions and to determine what kind of writing should be selected to determine the top scholar. The chief examiner smiled and said, "There is no fixed method for writing; only those who write about true mountains and waters can succeed." This was a playful remark, and the vice-censor couldn't help but burst into laughter, followed by the other officials, who all laughed loudly. The chief examiner became angry, rose and left, saying, "I want to say what I want, and who dares to contradict me?" He finally ended the banquet and went inside, and the officials left in an unhappy mood, privately discussing that they had specifically sought such superficial works to mock them. Fortunately, there was an essay by the third scholar present, and the two assistant examiners found it and couldn't stop laughing. The officials heard the laughter and hurried to see what was happening, saying, "If there is such an incompetent examiner, then there must be such incompetent candidates. It can be called a rare encounter in a thousand years."

    319

    ”众曰：“何不荐之？”此房官曰：“无乃过谑？”众曰：“我等公荐如何？”皆首肯。于是十八官相率呈堂曰：“职等自奉命后，在九千六百余卷中仅搜求得真山真水者一本，用敢公呈电鉴。”大主试阅之，明知众官谑己，拍案而起曰：“如此典博之文，不合抡元耶？”举墨笔于填书之处密密圈之，标定第一名。众官面面相觑，不敢作声。幸副主试闻声而来，持此卷且读且笑，谓大主试曰：“博则博矣，无乃稍涉浮泛乎？”大主试曰：“此元我定，与阁下无干。
    放榜后，我自挂弹章听皇上处分可也。”咸知其固执之性，倔强难挽，皆无言而退。三粕公居然发解矣。是时召公率其三子移寓西湖之麓，至龙虎日，高会亲友，开宴以俟捷音，各出文互相赞诵。三相公举碗擎盘，往来应酬，或索其文阅，复为父兄诟谇，或怒曰：“渠既入场，何至不堪如此？”携三相公手，踏月湖堤以消不平之气。值报捷者飞舆而来，问之，有召姓。三相公喜曰：“我兄中矣。

    Explanation

    319

    The crowd said, "Why not recommend him?" This official replied, "Isn't this a bit too playful?" The crowd said, "How about we collectively recommend him?" They all nodded in agreement. Thus, the eighteen officials presented the scroll in the hall, saying, "We officials, upon receiving orders, searched through nearly 9,600 scrolls and found only one that truly depicts mountains and waters. We dare to present it publicly for examination." The chief examiner read it and, knowing the officials were joking with him, slammed his hand on the table and said, "Such a profound and classical piece of writing, isn't it worthy of the top prize?" He took his ink brush, circled it closely on the form, and marked it as first place. The officials looked at each other in silence, not daring to speak. Fortunately, the deputy examiner heard the noise and came, holding the scroll, reading it, and laughing. He told the chief examiner, "It is broad indeed, but isn't it a bit too superficial?" The chief examiner said, "I have already decided this; it has nothing to do with you. After the results are posted, I will submit a complaint and leave it to the emperor to decide." Everyone knew his stubborn nature, that he was stubborn and hard to persuade, and all left without saying anything. Mr. Sanmo was unexpectedly selected. At that time, Mr. Zhao moved with his three sons to the foot of West Lake, on the day of the Dragon and Tiger, gathering with friends and relatives, holding a banquet to await the good news, each presenting their own writing and praising each other. Mr. Sanxiang held the bowl and plate, interacting with others, sometimes asked to read his writing, and was criticized by his father and elder brothers, sometimes angrily saying, "If he has already entered the exam, how could he be so poor?" He took Mr. Sanxiang's hand, walked along the Moon Lake embankment to vent his dissatisfaction. A messenger came running to report the good news, and asked him, "His surname is Zhao." Mr. Sanxiang happily said, "My brother has passed."

    320

    ”共拥至父前，众称非常之喜，先索报资，不与名条阅视，召曰：“我大儿合中久矣，今发已迟，不足奇也。”众曰：“否。”召曰：“然则我次儿正应中式。”众亦曰：“否。”召曰：“否则误矣，岂有烧火三相公得中举人耶？”众曰：“然。”召曰，“果有之已属万幸，不过副车，好则榜尾耳。”众曰：“请定赏例，自观名条可知也。”召曰：“副车十金，正榜倍之。”众曰：“元魁如何？”召曰：“魁则五十，元可百金，决无此理。”众使书券讫，攫其百金一纸而与之报条，公然第一。召骇曰，“文风之变迁，至于此极，今而后，不敢复论文矣！”

    Explanation

    320

    The crowd gathered and brought the man to his father's presence, praising it as an extraordinary joy. They first demanded a reward, but were not given the list of names to review. The father called out, "My eldest son has been eligible for a long time, but now it is too late, and it is not surprising." The crowd replied, "No." The father said, "Then my second son should have passed." The crowd also replied, "No." The father said, "Otherwise, you are mistaken. How could the third son, the fire carrier, pass as a juren?" The crowd said, "Yes." The father said, "If it really happened, it would be extremely fortunate, but it would only be a secondary prize. At best, it would be the last place on the list." The crowd said, "Please set the reward. You can know by looking at the list of names." The father said, "A secondary prize is ten gold pieces, and a top scorer is double that." The crowd asked, "What about the top scorer?" The father said, "The top scorer would get fifty, and the first place could get a hundred, but that is absolutely impossible." The crowd had the contract written and signed, took a piece of paper worth a hundred gold, and gave it along with the list. The third son was clearly first. The father was shocked and said, "The change in literary style has reached this extreme. From now on, I will not dare to discuss literature again!"

    321

    有朱解元者，眇一目，时人呼为“朱瞎子”，亦曰“朱半仙”。时文中之能手也，名噪一时。其未发解之前一科，偕友赴试，八月初七日，夜梦见二青衣相邀，入一殿廷，有冕旒王者，降阶相迎曰：“闻先生文名藉甚，今有尔浙闱墨，请先生为之润饰。”朱唯唯，延入后殿，朱衣神以卷送阅，王者命置笔砚于几而退。朱见元作格法高超，惟稍有未圆融处，为之易数字，已尽善矣。王者复来谓朱曰：“先生且停笔墨。今科解元文才尚好，不意该县城隍神来奏，此生近有奸人室女事，阴德有亏，应削其籍。予已追取下科解元文到，请先生正之。”朱阅其文曰：“此必童子之作，质地虽佳，功夫未到，何以冠多士？”王者曰：“且请就文整顿，资格所限，不能易他人也。”朱大加删削，炼作老境笔路，以呈王者。王者许可，命梦神飞传与之，然后次第阅竟，亦大费经营矣。

    Explanation

    321

    There was a man named Zhu Jieyuan, who was blind in one eye. People of his time called him "Zhu Xiazǐ" (Blind Zhu) and also referred to him as "Zhu Banxian" (Half-Saint Zhu). He was a master of writing and was famous for a time. Before he was appointed as the top scorer in the provincial examination, he took the exam with his friends on the seventh day of the eighth month. That night, he had a dream where two green-clad attendants invited him into a grand hall. There was a ruler with a ceremonial headdress and veil, who descended the steps to greet him and said, "I have heard of your excellent literary reputation. Today, I have the examination papers from the Zhejiang provincial examination. Please polish them for me." Zhu replied respectfully, and the ruler led him into a rear hall. The God of Literature presented the papers for review, and the ruler ordered the brush and inkstone to be placed on the desk before retreating. Zhu saw that the original works were of high quality but had a few areas that were not fully harmonious. He made some changes, and the papers were then perfect. The ruler returned and said to Zhu, "Please stop writing. The top scorer in this year's examination has good literary talent, but unexpectedly, the city god has reported that this person recently had an affair with a married woman, which has damaged their good deeds. They should be removed from the register. I have already retrieved the papers of the next year's top scorer. Please revise them." Zhu read the papers and said, "This must be the work of a child. The quality is good, but the skill is not yet refined enough to be the best among many." The ruler replied, "Please revise the papers. Due to qualifications, I cannot replace them with someone else." Zhu made extensive revisions, refining the writing to a mature style, and presented the papers to the ruler. The ruler approved and ordered the dream spirit to deliver them. Then, he read through the papers in sequence, which also required considerable effort.

    322

    王者大悦曰：“先生再后科之元也，今以阅卷功拔补下科元缺，以酬劳瘁。至所黜之元，系山阴某生，奸其邻女，幸未破败，然而神目如电，已为所司执奏。先生归去，访其人劝之改过，将来尚可登科也。至新解元，诚如先生所云，系新进童子，其父兄皆为词林，仁和人，祖功宗德甚厚，子孙科第未有艾也。然此子拔早一科，阴律应减阳寿五年，先生亦为之劝勉，俾绳其祖武，不但寿可免减，禄且日增矣。”遂命青衣仍送朱回寓，而梦觉已高卧三日。其仆守之，见朱忽醒，曰：“何病耶？头场将毕，自误功名，奈何？”朱曰：“倦耳，无病。”遣仆往接其友，录出两元作，俟友回，出门访见山阴生及仁和童子，告以神语，以文为证。皆惶悚受教。朱归视亲友之文，一读破题，即知其中第几名，有佳文，曰：“惜哉不售，其伤阴隲耶！”初不之信，后皆不爽，遂有半仙之称。

    Explanation

    322

    The king greatly delighted and said, "The gentleman is again the top scholar of the next examination. Today, he is promoted to fill the vacancy of the top scholar of the next examination due to the merit of grading papers, as a reward for his hard work. As for the one who was demoted, it is a certain student from Shan Yin, who had an affair with his neighbor's daughter. Fortunately, it was not too serious, but the divine eye is as sharp as lightning, and it has already been reported to the relevant authorities. The gentleman should go back and visit the person, advising him to reform. In the future, he may still pass the examination. As for the new top scholar, it is indeed as the gentleman said. He is a new candidate, and his father and elder brother are all in the literary world. He is from Ren He, and his ancestors' achievements and virtues are very profound, and the descendants' success in the examinations is endless. However, this young man was promoted too early, and according to the rules of the underworld, his life span will be shortened by five years. The gentleman should also encourage him to follow in his ancestors' footsteps, not only can his life span be spared from reduction, but his fortune will also increase daily." The king then ordered the attendants to send Zhu back to his residence, and when he woke up from the dream, he had been lying down for three days. His servant guarded him and saw Zhu suddenly wake up, saying, "What illness do you have? The first exam is about to end, and you are losing your reputation for yourself, what should we do?" Zhu said, "I am just tired, I have no illness." He sent his servant to fetch his friends and recorded the two top scholars' works. Waiting for his friends to return, he went out to meet the student from Shan Yin and the child from Ren He, informing them of the divine words and using the texts as proof. They were all shocked and received the instruction. Zhu returned and read his relatives and friends' essays, and immediately knew their rankings. There were excellent essays, and he said, "Too bad they did not pass, did they harm the moral order?" At first, he did not believe it, but later, everything turned out to be accurate, and he thus gained the reputation of a half-god.

    323

    吾乡有刘君者，应童试不售，去而习申韩业，公然宪幕。丁卯岁，朝梦迎天榜，伊名列第一。不觉技痒，复理故业，纳监入闱。头场犯规被贴，君然第一。此鬼神揶揄之耶？抑若吴生之命，有天录耶？必居一于此矣。
    　　
    　　卜者梁翁
    　　
    　　海昌张端林，父为云南尉，卒于任。端林迎父榇归，道由湖广，米价甚廉，以宦囊所有籴八百石，舟运入豫。江值大风，望船多处收泊。至一村落，四面皆河，客舟环泊，中有一大家，高其闬闳，厚其墙垣，门前停舟更密。端林登岸散步，偶入酒肆，沽饮独酌，闻人议论卜者粱翁，知人过去未来事，言休咎，其应如响。端林就客问之，始知即大宅内之人。遇异传，以大六壬著名，问卜无须开口，即知所事，因此起家巨万。近以年老，每日只卖十二课，须黎明至其家，与挂号者清钱百文，课金一两，得列簿内，则得卦。迟则挂号不及，即不得预。

    Explanation

    323

    There was a man named Liu in my hometown. He failed the imperial examination for young scholars and turned to studying Legalism, eventually becoming an official in the provincial government. In the year of the Rabbit (丁卯), he dreamed of receiving a royal list of successful candidates, with himself listed as first. He felt an urge to engage in his studies again, resumed his previous career, registered for the imperial examination, and entered the examination hall. However, during the first session, he violated the rules and was punished, yet he was still ranked first. Is this a mockery by the gods? Or is it like Wu's fate, recorded in heaven? It must be one of these two.

    The Fortune-Teller Liang

    Zhang Duanlin from Hai Chang had a father who was a magistrate in Yunnan and died in office. Zhang Duanlin brought his father's coffin back home, passing through Huguang where the price of rice was very low. He bought 800 shi of rice with his official salary and transported it by boat to Henan. On the river, he encountered a strong wind and found a place with many boats to dock. He reached a village surrounded by rivers, with boats of travelers docked around. In the middle of the village, there was a large house with high gates and thick walls, and even more boats were docked in front of it. Zhang Duanlin went ashore for a walk and happened to enter a wine shop, where he bought and drank wine alone. He heard people talking about the fortune-teller Liang, who knew past and future events, spoke of good and bad omens, and his predictions came true immediately. Zhang Duanlin approached a guest to ask about it and learned that it was a person inside the large house. The fortune-teller was known for his expertise in the Da Liun system of divination, could tell what someone wanted to ask without them speaking, and became wealthy due to this ability. Recently, due to old age, he only sold twelve divinations per day. One had to arrive at his home at dawn, pay a hundred cash to register, and pay one tael of silver for the divination. If registered, he would receive a divination. If late, registration would not be possible, and one would miss the opportunity.

    324

    人争趋之，有不远千里而来候教者，故门前舟常满也。端林亦起意问卜，次日赍银钱入其家，门房内设柜，掌柜者系其亲戚，收仪登号。及端林去，十二数已满，强之增添，则曰，“非翁自主，不敢私加也。”乃特簿邀十二客入内，端林随入观之，登堂入室，室中陈设精雅。有老翁年近八旬，带四品冠，据案上坐，前列牙筹一筒，两旁设四小儿，各具笔砚，其徒四人伺应书单，前后坐椅环列。客入，翁起让坐，客各就位。掌柜人开簿唱号曰：“第一号某客，请抽筹。”客抽送翁前，翁观筹掐指，谓其徒曰：“某客得某时，因某事问，课主何吉凶。

    Explanation

    324

    People compete to seek him out, with some traveling from as far as a thousand miles to seek his instruction, hence the boats in front of his door are always full. Duan Lin also decided to consult a diviner, and the next day he carried silver and money into the diviner's home. Inside the entrance hall, there was a cabinet set up, and the person in charge was related to the diviner, who received the offering and recorded it. When Duan Lin left, the number twelve was already full, and when someone tried to add more, the diviner said, "If not the old man himself, I dare not add privately." Then a special register was used to invite twelve guests inside, and Duan Lin followed them in to observe. They went up to the hall and entered the inner room, which was elegantly furnished. There was an old man nearly eighty years old, wearing a fourth-rank cap, sitting on a desk. In front, there was a cylinder of bamboo sticks for divination, and on both sides, there were four children, each with a brush and inkstone. His four disciples waited to respond to the written requests, and chairs were arranged in a circle in front and behind. When the guests entered, the old man stood up to invite them to sit, and the guests took their seats. The person in charge opened the register and called out the numbers: "The first guest, please draw a stick." The guest drew and presented it to the old man, who looked at the stick and counted on his fingers, then told his disciples, "The guest drew a certain time, asked about a certain matter, and inquired about the main outcome."

    325

    ”徒举笔照录所断，皆合来意，无一爽者。十二课次第毕，客亦陆续退出。端林目注神疑，忘其进退。翁忽谓曰：“远客不及入号，老朽合送一课，以尽地主之谊。足下姓张，从滇南来耶？”端林曰：“然，何以知之？”翁曰：“足下坐于离宫，正时属午，度值张星，我故知之。今日乙卯三传，申、酉、戌为日之财官，值贵神虎常玄，夫白虎凶神同官，爻为有官之尊属，舟中合有父棺。戌为地狱，生前曾为司狱之官。太常为米麦，附酉金而兼连茹，当带有稻米。两金重四，其八百石乎？寅甲一冲，箕星动矣。

    Explanation

    325

    The visitor merely lifted his pen to transcribe the divination results, and all matched his intentions, not a single one was wrong. The twelve lessons were completed in order, and the guests gradually left. Endlin's eyes were fixed in a state of doubt, forgetting whether to advance or retreat. The old man suddenly said, "The distant guest did not have time to enter the number, I, an old man, should give you a lesson to fulfill the host's duty. Your name is Zhang, and you came from the south of Yunnan, isn't it?" Endlin replied, "Yes, how did you know?" The old man said, "You sit in the Li palace, the correct time belongs to the noon, and I judged by the position of the Zhan star, so I knew. Today is the day of Yi Mao, the three transmissions of Shen, You, and Xu are the day's wealth and officials, and they are accompanied by the noble spirit Hu Chang Xuan. The White Tiger, an ominous spirit, is associated with an official, and the line represents a respected official. There must be a coffin for a father on the boat. Xu represents hell, and before death, he was an official in charge of prisons. The Taichang represents rice and wheat, attached to the metal element of You and including continuous growth, so he should carry rice. Two metals weigh four, does it represent eight hundred stones? The branches Yin and Jia clash, and the Jixing is now moving."

    326

    明日寅时转西北风，大顺，未传为地，足与卯作合，应十二月之卯日到籍，其米不但不得价，且颗粒全无，缘财入玄武耗散之手，尽化为三传之鬼矣。足下其慎之。”端林得课回舟，果于五鼓得顺风，扬帆而进，于腊月二十六日己卯，抵邑之王家桥。败岁之际，无暇安葬，泊舟处有姊夫陆某，米客也。借其闲地权厝父棺，因思米运入家，宗族强借可虞，不如近就姊家，以寄于栈，端林奉母回城度岁，岁朝往贺姊家，叩关而入，其姊对之号泣曰：“尔姊夫亏客货千余金，上年尔所寄之米，被客强起去。姊夫无以对尔，今不知遁于何处。”端林忆及粱翁之课，慨然曰：“数已前定，果无可逃。姊毋惶急，忝在至亲，米价不必计论矣，我为寻访姊夫归家可也。”
    　　
    　　小王子

    Explanation

    326

    The next day, at the hour of Yin (3 AM to 5 AM), the wind shifted to the northwest, bringing favorable conditions. It was not yet firmly established, but it was compatible with the hour of Mao (5 AM to 7 AM). It was expected to reach the records on the day of Mao in the twelfth month. The rice not only failed to fetch a price, but it was completely lost, as the wealth was dissipated into the hands of the Black Warrior (symbolizing financial loss). It was entirely transformed into something negative. Be cautious," advised the teacher. Endlin received the lesson and returned to the boat. Indeed, at the fifth drumbeat (early morning), he caught a favorable wind, raised sail, and advanced. On the twenty-sixth day of the twelfth month, the day of Ji Mao, he arrived at Wangjiaqiao in the county. In the midst of the year's failure, there was no time for a proper burial. At the place where the boat was moored, there was his brother-in-law, Mr. Lu, a rice trader. He temporarily buried his father's coffin in an idle place. He thought that bringing the rice home might lead to the family taking advantage of the situation, so it was better to bring it closer to his sister's house and store it in a warehouse. Endlin, accompanied by his mother, returned to the city to celebrate the new year. On the first day of the new year, he went to greet his sister's house. He knocked on the door and entered. His sister greeted him with tears, saying, "Your brother-in-law has lost over a thousand gold pieces in trade. The rice you sent last year was taken by the customer. Your brother-in-law has no way to explain it to you, and I do not know where he has fled to." Endlin remembered the teacher's lesson and sighed, "Fate is predetermined, and it cannot be escaped. Do not be anxious. As a close relative, the price of rice is not worth discussing. I will go to find my brother-in-law and bring him home."

    327

    江左徐君，知医道，开设药肆为业。门临官河，栽柳成行，有美荫。一丐者小艇，恒泊于下，有二妇操异音，日与其夫荡桨行乞。盛署之际，忽闻二妇大放悲声，徐往视之，丐已欲毙，气存一息。发恻隐心，谓其妇曰：“尔夫当是痧症，我取药来，或可救也。”进与蟾酥丸，用童便灌之，大醒，越日大愈。丐登门叩首，谢活命恩。自此放舟，一去不复反矣。
    　　
    　　历十余年，徐积得百数金，与亲友合本渡海，赴关东贩豆。遇飓风飘入大洋，舟子不能为力，砍桅去帆，任其播扬。不知几日夜，亦不知几千百里，遇岛触矶而舟碎，财物俱沉，所幸人无伤损，聚哭于沙屿而已。此岛内系大镇市，朝鲜所辖，有瞭望者见舟破人存，咸来问讯，备录名单，飞报所司。值小王子巡边，闻报见单，亲临点验，至徐君，细审其行业，曰：“先生门前垂柳尚存乎？”徐不知所以，谨以实对，王子乃命司官，以诸客就养于馆舍，厚给其饩廪，曰：“遇便送还天朝可也。

    Explanation

    327

    In the east of the Yangtze River, there was a man named Xu who knew the art of medicine and ran a pharmacy as his profession. His door faced the official river, and he had planted willows in rows, providing beautiful shade. A beggar with a small boat would always dock below, accompanied by two women who spoke a different dialect, rowing and begging for alms with their husband. During the hot summer, suddenly, the two women let out loud cries of sorrow. Xu went to see them and found the beggar nearly dead, with only a breath left. Feeling compassion, he told the woman: "Your husband must be suffering from a heatstroke. I will fetch medicine, and perhaps he can be saved." He administered toad venom pills and gave him an enema with young boy's urine. The beggar awoke greatly and recovered significantly the next day. He came to the door and bowed deeply, thanking Xu for saving his life. From then on, he set sail and never returned.

    Over ten years passed, and Xu accumulated several hundred gold pieces. With relatives and friends, he combined capital to cross the sea, heading to the Guandong region to trade beans. He encountered a typhoon and drifted into the open ocean. The boatman could not help, so they cut down the mast and removed the sails, letting the boat drift. They did not know how many days and nights had passed, nor how many thousands of miles they had traveled, until they encountered an island and hit a reef, causing the boat to break. All property sank, but fortunately, no one was injured. They gathered to cry on the sandbar.

    This island was a large town under the jurisdiction of Korea. There were lookouts who saw the boat broken but people still alive, and they all came to inquire, recording the names and quickly reporting to the authorities. The young prince was on his border patrol and heard the report, seeing the list. He personally inspected and came to Xu, carefully examining his profession. He asked, "Does the willow by your door still stand?" Xu did not understand why he asked this, so he answered truthfully. The prince then ordered the official to accommodate the guests in a guesthouse, providing them with generous provisions and said, "When convenient, send them back to the Celestial Empire."

    328

    ”命备法驾，肃徐登辇，王自乘骑前导，白旄黄钺，禁尉传呼，行人跪道。徐怦怦然如梦如痴，听之而已。越两日，及大城，百官郊迎，添卤簿易銮舆，称跸而入城中。三市六街，人烟辐凄，至宫门，王子挽徐手升殿交拜，分宾主西东对坐，传命后宫，宣妃主夫人见客。乃有彩娥贵嫔翼二天人出，法衣象服，佩玉鸣珰，向徐盈盈再拜。徐答拜不迭，长跪于地，曰：“小人何德何能。敢受我王殊礼？请明以告我，以志异数。”王子亦跽告曰：“先生忘十年前柳荫下小艇中之病丐乎？即寡人也。

    Explanation

    328

    The king ordered the imperial carriage to be prepared, and Xu solemnly and slowly ascended the carriage. The king himself led the procession on horseback, with white banners and yellow axes, and guards shouting out commands, while people knelt on the road. Xu was dazed and confused, like being in a dream, just listening to it. After two days, they reached the great city, where officials welcomed them at the outskirts. The entourage was expanded, and the imperial carriage was changed, and the emperor's presence was announced as they entered the city. The three markets and six streets were bustling with people. Upon reaching the palace gate, the prince led Xu up to the hall, and they bowed to each other. They sat facing each other as host and guest. A message was sent to the inner palace, and the arrival of the guests was announced to the consorts and ladies. Then, beautiful consorts and noble ladies emerged, dressed in formal and ornate clothing, adorned with jade and bells, and bowed twice to Xu. Xu could not keep up with the bowing, and he knelt on the ground, saying, "What virtue or ability do I have? How dare I receive such special treatment from my king? Please clearly tell me, so that I may remember this unique treatment." The prince also knelt and said, "Do you remember the sick beggar in the small boat under the willow shade ten years ago? That was me."

    329

    我国法，凡贵者得异症，必远丐三年，以申忏悔。寡人二十余岁时，忽得眩疾，发则眩晕即死，过日而苏。前王命太史占之，须乞丐中华沿海之区，必有奇遇，疾以痊也。”乃指二妃曰：“寡人带渠行乞，得遇先生以仙丹救治，寡人自此疾愈，逮今十余年不复发矣。昔年王父崩，寡人接位，国服甫满，尚未朝贡请封，故国人犹以小王子称寡人耳。今天幸先生来此，俾寡人有以报德，先生欲贵乎？富乎？欲贵，则特设宾师之位以奉先生；欲富，则珍宝货财惟先生命。

    Explanation

    329

    According to our laws, whenever a noble person contracts a strange illness, they must go far away and beg for three years to express their repentance. When I was in my twenties, I suddenly contracted a dizziness disease, and when it struck, I would faint and almost die, but would recover after a few days. The previous king ordered the historian to divinate, and it was said that I must beg in the coastal regions of China, and there would be a strange encounter that would cure the disease. I took them with me on my begging journey, and I met the master who treated me with elixir, and from then on, my illness was cured, and I have not had it again for over ten years. Last year, when my father passed away, I ascended the throne, and the mourning period was just over, and we had not yet paid tribute and requested investiture, so the people still call me the young prince. Today, I am fortunate that the master has come here, allowing me to repay your kindness. Do you desire nobility or wealth? If you desire nobility, I will specially set up a position of guest teacher to honor you; if you desire wealth, I will give you treasures and wealth as you wish.

    330

    ”徐曰：“小人远方医士，无奇才异能，敢妄玷宾师以速官谤？无已，本为运豆而来，请假以资本，送之关东，则感戴无既矣。”王子笑曰：“关东豆不及小邦者，不意中华贵重至此。异日请先生观我仓贮何如？”乃开大宴毕，馆徐于天使行宫，命大臣之善华言者陪侍，娱之以本国杂乐。王子日往朝之，居数月，徐思家欲归，辞留数四，不得已，王子命开太仓，请往观豆。则一颗之大，约重四五分，果胜于关东数倍。徐欲之，王子举仓以赠，载海舶数十艘，奉徐与诸客，以王大船送之归国。徐货豆得十余万金，改药肆为质库矣。

    Explanation

    330

    Xu said, "I am but a humble physician from a distant land, lacking any extraordinary talent or unique abilities. How dare I rashly soil the position of guest and teacher and invite official slander? If there is no other option, I originally came to transport beans. Please lend me some capital to send them to Guandong, and I shall be eternally grateful." The prince smiled and said, "The beans of Guandong do not compare to those of our small kingdom. I did not expect the value of China to be so high. One day, I will ask you to see how well-stocked my granaries are." He then held a grand feast and accommodated Xu in the embassy's residence, ordering ministers skilled in Chinese to attend and entertaining him with our country's various music. The prince visited Xu daily, and after several months, Xu missed home and wanted to return, refusing to stay several times, but was unable to help. The prince then ordered the opening of the great granary and invited Xu to inspect the beans. Each grain was about four to five fen in weight, indeed surpassing those of Guandong several times. Xu desired them, and the prince gave the entire granary as a gift, loading them onto dozens of sea ships and accompanying Xu and the guests back to his country with the king's large ship. Xu sold the beans for over ten thousand gold pieces and changed his medicine shop into a pawnshop.

    331

    或曰：“惜哉徐君，不取珍宝而惓惓于豆。”芗厈曰：“倬哉徐君，不贪珍宝而仅仅以豆，不失我中华体统，其市侩中之豪杰耶！”
    　　
    　　姚幕府
    　　
    　　台逆之乱，制军已飞章入告，集多官议剿守计，连日未决。忽有严旨下，值制军筵宴时也。客皆退避，制军接读之间，目定口呆，神魂失据，颓乎坐上矣。仆皆惶急奔告公子，公子来，先捧谕旨，与幕友姚先生阅之，骇曰：“殆矣，上意切责其怠缓，限十日内平定。为今之计，惟有刻即进兵，以副庙谟。”公子曰：“父病垂危，焉能进剿？”姚曰：“我与尊公宾主相投，久而无间，今日之事，不得不出身赞襄。姑将尊公抬送大堂，我当代为发令。”公子不得已从之，乃命文武员弁，俱集辕门听令。

    Explanation

    331

    "Someone said, 'Alas, Master Xu, you did not take precious treasures but were deeply concerned about a bean dish.' Xiang Yan replied, 'How noble Master Xu is! He was not greedy for treasures but valued only the bean dish, preserving the dignity of our Chinese culture. Is he not a hero among ordinary people?'

    Graveyard of the Governor's Office

    During the rebellion, the Governor had already sent a rapid report to the court, gathering many officials to discuss strategies for suppression and defense, but no decision was reached for several days.
