The person who threw him into the water was a wealthy man who hired a good swimmer for a high price.

    412

    芗厈曰：人之阴谋诡计，惟图色为甚。然而天道昭彰，竟无不破之案，是以大盗亦戒采花。是案也，彼庸庸者流，竟谓新郎投河而死，众目观瞻，与妇女何尤？遂成疑案。其有心者，不过揣新郎之发狂也，或以药酒为之，疑女有故。然不能破其奸，敢讯诸乎？后令之勘访搜寻，可谓有胆有识。然使床下之男履不露，何从发其覆乎？我故曰：“天道也。”

    Explanation

    412

    Xiang Yan said: People's secret plots and deceitful schemes are especially driven by lust. However, the way of heaven is clear and evident, and there is no case that cannot be solved; thus, even great thieves are wary of kidnapping. In this case, the common folk assumed that the groom drowned himself in the river, under the watchful eyes of many, and what has it to do with women? Thus, it became a mystery. Those with insight suspected that the groom went mad, perhaps it was done with drugged wine, and they suspected that the woman had a reason. Yet, they could not uncover the deceit, dare to ask about it? Later, the magistrate's investigation and search, can be said to be both courageous and wise. Yet, if the man's shoes were not exposed from under the bed, how could they uncover the truth? I say, "It is the way of heaven."

    413

    职谬
    　　
    　　滇南米商某，列肆于市。值县之少尉出，四人肩舆，隶卒擎盖执仗，前拥后呼，辟人于道，遇坐者喝之起。商适与客核账，思索出神，不及起。尉见之怒，执之舆前，责掌二十。尉去，商大哭。客哂之曰：“父母官责尔不敬，能不顺受，徒泣何为？”商曰：“彼父母官者，犹夫人耳，何以尊严若是？何科甲出身耶？”客笑曰：“彼铜进士出身，汝何不知？”商曰：“吾实不知铜进士为几甲？”客曰：“是不过银子科第三甲耳。”商曰：“如客所云，吾亦有银，何难与埒，甘受其辱。不亦过乎？”决意止肆，携赀入都，报捐未入流，分发畿南。但未习仪注，难以谒宪。

    Explanation

    413

    A certain rice merchant in the southern part of Yunnan set up a shop in the market. When the junior county official emerged, four people carried his sedan chair, and attendants held ceremonial weapons, clearing the way and shouting at anyone to stand up. The merchant was just with a guest calculating accounts, deep in thought, and did not notice the official's command to stand up. The official saw this and was angry, taking the merchant to the front of the sedan chair and having him receive twenty lashes. After the official left, the merchant cried bitterly. The guest smiled at him and said, "The local official scolded you for not showing respect. Could you not accept it gracefully and just cry?" The merchant replied, "That local official is just like any other person, why is he so dignified? What rank did he achieve through the imperial examinations?" The guest laughed and said, "He is a copper scholar, how could you not know?" The merchant asked, "I really don't know what rank a copper scholar is?" The guest said, "It's just a third-rank scholar." The merchant said, "If that's the case, I also have silver, why can't I match him? Why should I suffer such humiliation?" He decided to close his shop, took his money to the capital, bought a low-ranking official position, and was assigned to the southern part of the capital region. However, he was not familiar with the etiquette, making it difficult for him to visit the governor.

    414

    幸都有干仆为之谋曰：“主所有者财耳，何不普拜乡亲，大开筵宴，既联桑梓之谊。藉学趋跄之礼，彼同仕者孰不愿为指教耶？”商是其言，遍拜同乡，饮之食之，乡人皆乐为教，肆习旬余，拜跪叩首，皆无谬误。众议先见府道，以熟其仪，如无错谬，则可谒宪司矣。乃往见郡伯，嘉庆二十五年六月也。商公服而纬帽和。郡伯者，人甚谦，凡属员叩首，必手自扶之，扶之不起，必回礼。时商趋入遽跪，郡伯俯扶之，商纬帽极滑泽，贯郡伯朝珠于首，猛然起，珠散而郡伯几跪。凡有珠者，深恶此兆，以为不祥，故郡伯拂袖而入。左右检珠，咸笑曰：“客可以退矣。”商乃失色出，同官传为笑柄。

    Explanation

    414

    A servant from the capital of Xingdu offered advice to the merchant, saying, "The only thing you have is wealth. Why not broadly pay respects to fellow townspeople and hold a grand banquet to form bonds of hometown friendship and learn the proper etiquette of bowing and walking? Who among those who serve in the same position would not be willing to offer guidance?" The merchant agreed with his words and visited all his fellow townspeople, entertaining them with food and drink. The fellow townspeople were all happy to teach him, and after practicing for over ten days, his bowing, kneeling, and kowtowing were all done without error. The group suggested seeing the governor first to become familiar with the rituals. If there were no mistakes, then one could visit the higher authorities. They went to see the prefect in June of the twenty-fifth year of the Jiaqing era. The merchant wore official attire and a hat. The prefect was very humble. Whenever subordinates kowtowed, he would personally help them up, and if they couldn't stand up, he would return the favor. The merchant rushed in and suddenly knelt down. The prefect bent down to help him up, but the merchant's hat was so slippery that it caught the prefect's pearl necklace. He suddenly stood up, causing the pearls to scatter and almost making the prefect kneel. Those who have pearls deeply dislike this omen, considering it inauspicious. So the prefect angrily entered. The attendants gathered the pearls and laughed, saying, "The guest can leave now." The merchant then left in a panic, and his fellow officials made him the subject of ridicule.

    415

    众尤商曰：“汝未能从容进退，不可以见上宪，尚须练习。”又逾月而国恤闻，官皆白衣冠，避舍二十七日后，素服顶帽见客。商之乡亲有与方伯司阍者善，谓之曰：“有敝乡里初出茅庐者，恐贻同官笑，能使之独见宪台否？”司阍曰：“是不难，我主素无拘束，随到随见，使早来，我有以位置之。”随告商。时商已谒观察无误，方欣欣自得。次日，入藩署投谒，彼宪司之门房，强于下寮之厅事，铺设斐然。司阍者盛服据上坐，问其仆曰：“某官已至乎？”仆乃引商入。商见陈设眩目，人物轩昂，意为即方伯，趋入拜脆，献履历。司阍者笑曰：“误矣，敝主人不在此，且请少憩无躁。”商知有误，赧然坐。

    Explanation

    415

    The crowd reproached Shang, saying, "You are unable to act with composure, and thus cannot be seen by the higher authorities; you still need to practice." After another month, news of national mourning arrived, and officials all wore white clothes and hats, avoiding their residences for twenty-seven days before meeting guests in plain clothes and hats. Shang's fellow townsman, who had a good relationship with the gatekeeper of the provincial governor, told him, "There is a fellow villager from my hometown who is inexperienced and may be ridiculed by his colleagues. Can you arrange for him to see the higher authorities alone?" The gatekeeper replied, "It is not difficult. My master is usually unbound by formalities and sees whoever arrives. If he comes early, I will find a place for him." He then informed Shang. At that time, Shang had already paid his respects to the governor without any mistake and was feeling very pleased and satisfied. The next day, he went to the provincial office to pay his respects. The reception room of the higher authorities' office was more impressive than the hall of the lower officials, with splendid furnishings. The gatekeeper, dressed in fine attire and seated in the upper seat, asked his servant, "Has the official arrived?" The servant then led Shang in. Shang saw the display and was dazzled by the dignified and impressive people, thinking it was the provincial governor. He rushed in to pay his respects and presented his resume. The gatekeeper laughed and said, "It was a mistake. My master is not here. Please rest for a moment and do not be anxious." Shang realized his mistake and sat down, blushing with embarrassment.

    416

    司阍嘱其仆人探方伯，而内随官者询知司阍之友，欲逞能而见好，命其仆引商先入客室，以候方伯。商见客堂一色纯白，无字画陈设，铺垫皆黑麻为之，不如门房远甚，意谓是必随官之室，不可自卑，再贻人笑，遂首座而趺坐。时方伯由内出，随官皆不及知，见上座有客，金其顶者，意谓幕中人，拱手让坐。商见方伯青衣，猝然问曰：“足下高姓？”方伯道其姓，商诧曰：“足下与大人同姓，可喜可贺。”时随官闻有人接谈，于门隙窥见主人已出，不觉皇然入，陈商之手版。方伯俯首阅，商甫见珊瑚其顶，惶恐之极，趋跪膝前曰：“大人该死！大人该死！”方伯拂然大怒，叱退之。后郡伯入见，方伯怒犹未息，语以故。郡伯曰：“是即散卑府之朝珠者，闻系市侩。”方伯曰：“商者无失其为商，何可乱我仕版？仍使归市可也。”遂休之。

    Explanation

    416

    The Gatekeeper instructed his servant to inquire about the Governor, and an attendant within learned that the Gatekeeper's friend wished to show off and make a good impression. The attendant ordered his servant to bring the merchant into the guest room ahead of the Governor. The merchant saw that the guest room was entirely white, with no paintings or decorations, and the floor was covered with black hemp mats, which were far simpler than the gatehouse. He thought it must be the attendant's room and decided not to lower himself, lest he be ridiculed, so he sat in the most prominent position with his legs crossed. At that moment, the Governor emerged from inside, and the attendants were unaware. Seeing someone sitting in the prominent seat with a golden topknot, the Governor assumed it was a subordinate official and bowed to offer the seat. The merchant saw the Governor in plain clothes and suddenly asked, "What is your honorable surname?" The Governor replied, and the merchant was surprised, saying, "We share the same surname, how fortunate!" The attendant heard someone talking and peeked through the door, seeing the host had already come out. He hurried in, presenting the merchant's credentials. The Governor looked down and read them. The merchant, upon seeing the coral topknot, was extremely frightened and hurriedly knelt before the Governor, saying, "My lord, I am at fault! My lord, I am at fault!" The Governor became extremely angry and shouted at him to leave. Later, the Prefect entered and saw the Governor was still angry. He explained the situation. The Prefect said, "This is the person who lost the beads of your ceremonial necklace, and I hear he is a merchant." The Governor replied, "A merchant should not lose his place as a merchant; how can he disrupt my official duties? Send him back to the market." And so, the matter was resolved.

    417

    芗厈曰：是躁人也，不第杂职中有之。闻有某尹，蜀人也。谒制军，问及峨眉山猴子究有多大，尹对曰：“同大人大。”制军哂之。尹自觉谬，惶然改口曰：“与卑职一般大。”一日与藩臬道会议，三官皆吸烟，命仆为尹点烟，尹曰：“卑职性不食烟，有三子均喜吸烟，恶之不暇，而身自犯耶？”皆传为笑柄。余故曰躁则妄，妄则言多谬，当以三愆为戒，为之徐徐云尔。或曰不然，昔有人戒友之躁者，其人衣适着火，友见之而不言。火焚及身，怒其仆不早为扑灭。友曰：“我早见之，君戒我躁，我姑徐徐云尔。”若遇是人，又将何说？余恍然悟曰：“然则举世皆谬，若欲不谬，何如出世？”

    Explanation

    417

    Xiang Yan said: This is a restless person, not only are there such people among the miscellaneous officials. There was once an official from Sichuan who visited the governor-general. When asked about the size of the monkeys on Mount Emei, the official replied, "The same size as a great person." The governor-general laughed at him. The official realized his mistake and hastily changed his answer to, "The same size as me." One day, he attended a meeting with the governor, the censor, and the provincial commissioner. All three officials were smoking, and they ordered a servant to light the official's pipe. The official said, "I do not smoke, and my three sons all enjoy smoking. I dislike it so much that I cannot bear to do it myself." Everyone laughed at him. I therefore say that restlessness leads to rashness, and rashness leads to many mistakes. One should take three faults as a warning and act slowly and carefully. Some say otherwise, recalling an incident where someone tried to warn a friend about their restlessness. The friend's clothes caught fire, and the friend saw it but did not speak. The fire burned him, and he was angry that his servant did not put it out earlier. The friend said, "I saw it early, you warned me about my restlessness, so I acted slowly." If one encounters such a person, what can one say? I suddenly realized, "Then the whole world is wrong. If one wants not to be wrong, how about leaving the world?"

    418

    智女
    　　
    　　嘉庆初年，白莲党之扰川楚也，贼帅掠良家妇女无算，内有楚女，英英特立，秀出冠群，帅爱而教留之。女曰：“得为将军之妻，妾之愿也。但妾诗礼旧家，虽乱杂中不得父母之命，媒妁之言，然花烛合卺之礼，不可废也。日后为王为相时，妾叨福庇，称王妃夫人，确系堂堂配偶，得自立于人前，无苟合之讥。亦将军之光。”贼帅悦其词令，不觉首肯，使入室改装成礼。于是择掠得衣饰之美者，及脂粉之类，送与添装。复使他妇数人，入供役使。女退之，传命曰：“今日初见良人，无须役，明日惟命。

    Explanation

    418

    In the early years of the Jiaqing era, during the White Lotus Rebellion that troubled Sichuan and Hubei, the rebel leader seized countless women from respectable families. Among them was a woman from Hubei, who was exceptionally intelligent, independent, and outstanding among her peers. The leader admired her and decided to keep her. She said, "To become the wife of a general is my wish. However, I come from a family with a tradition of poetry and propriety. Even in the midst of chaos, I cannot marry without the consent of my parents and the matchmaker. The traditional wedding ceremony involving the lighting of candles and the sharing of wine from a single cup cannot be abandoned. In the future, when you become a king or a prime minister, I will be honored as your princess or noble lady, a proper and honorable spouse, able to stand on my own, without the criticism of hasty or improper union. It will also be a source of pride for you." The leader was pleased with her words and agreed, allowing her to enter the room to prepare for the ceremony. He then selected beautiful clothing and makeup items and sent them to her. He also sent several other women to serve her. The woman dismissed them and conveyed his message: "Today is the first time I meet my good man, so there is no need for service. I will obey your orders tomorrow."

    419

    ”贼以其处子碍羞，允之。女乃盥沐凝妆，见有利刃，窃袖之。贼帅因久待不出，入室窃窥其新装，益增秀媚，不觉心动，突入拥抱。女以刃直刺其心，立死之，推尸床上。以己之装饰饰贼尸，傅粉涂朱，剪己鬓贴其首，加以钗钿，望之宛似女身。女乃衣贼帅衣冠佩剑，薄暮出，呼马执旂而遁。其亲信者候之，彻夜不归。次日午刻，大营有令，众议将军昨何往，谅夫人知之。不得已，入室请命，见夫人尚卧，不敢骤近，觉血腥出于床，呼众人捡之，方知装夫人者将军。将军装者，不知其何往也。嗣是贼营得妇女，不敢留，掠人之风，因之稍戢。

    Explanation

    419

    The bandit leader, considering the girl's virginity a matter of shame, allowed it. The girl then washed herself and carefully put on makeup, noticing a sharp blade, which she secretly tucked into her sleeve. Because the bandit leader had been waiting for a long time without seeing her, he entered the room to secretly peek at her new attire, finding her even more charming and beautiful, and was suddenly struck by desire. He rushed in to embrace her. The girl, however, thrust the blade straight into his heart, killing him instantly, and pushed his body onto the bed. She then dressed his body with her own clothes, applied powder and rouge, cut a piece of her own hair and attached it to his head, adding hairpins and ornaments, making it look like her own body. She then dressed herself in the bandit leader's attire, wore his sword, and left at dusk, calling for a horse and holding a flag as she fled. His trusted followers waited for him all night, but he did not return. The next day at noon, the main camp issued a command, and everyone wondered where the general had gone yesterday, assuming that his wife must know. Reluctantly, they entered the room to inquire, seeing the wife still lying in bed, they dared not approach her immediately. They noticed the smell of blood coming from the bed and called the others to investigate, discovering that the body dressed as the wife was actually the general. As for the one dressed as the general, no one knew where he had gone. Afterward, when the bandit camp captured women, they dared not keep them, and the practice of seizing people gradually subsided.

    420

    芗厈曰：以甘言悦贼帅，使不备反而刺之，此古之节烈妇女为之者，不乏人。所可异者，颠倒阴阳，悠然而逝，出人意外。使不及追，且能全他人之节，何胆智之精细而雄烈耶？同时有贼得美妇，拦入深林，欲奸之，妇坚拒，不能成，贼怒甚，断其首而去，妇尸自移就首，竟凑合而生，年逾八旬乃卒。时又有百十妇女，以带联结众身，各持刀作方阵而行，贼冲之不散，围之不克，无可奈何，以火枪轰击而并殪之。惜乎，不得智女为之帅，以成奇观也。

    Explanation

    420

    Xiang Yan said: To please the bandit leader with sweet words, making him unprepared and then stabbing him, this was done by many chaste and heroic women of ancient times. What is remarkable is that they reversed the natural order, drifting away effortlessly, beyond people's expectations. They were so swift that they could not be pursued, and yet they preserved the chastity of others—how courageous and intelligent were they? At the same time, there was a bandit who captured a beautiful woman, dragging her into a deep forest to rape her. The woman firmly resisted, and the bandit could not succeed. Enraged, he cut off her head and left. The woman's body moved on its own to join her head, and they eventually reunited and lived, dying only after she had lived past eighty years. There were also over a hundred women who linked their bodies together with a rope, each holding a knife and forming a square formation while moving. The bandits charged at them but could not disperse them, and when they tried to surround them, they failed. Unable to do anything, the bandits attacked with fire guns, killing them all at once. Alas, there was no wise woman to lead them, which would have made the scene even more extraordinary.

    421

    卷三
    　　
    　　陆清献公遗事
    　　
    　　公讳陇其，号稼书，淛之当湖人。以理学名家，配享圣庙。公以进士即用，宰直隶灵寿县，父老相传，遗风善政，有足录者。昔公之任，无幕友官亲，二仆与官，三人而已。早起盥沐后，冠带坐堂上，唤吏役告之曰：“凡莅民，以省事为第一关，我无多事，不须若干人祗候。或农或贾，各就所能，以养尔父母妻子。但已领工食，应为国家办事，不能尽去。每日酌留书吏二人，衙役四名，轮班听事，余各执业，勿远离也。”暖阁之内，左右二几，一具律，一具平码，完粮银者，当堂弹兑，余厘毫，必还之。投呈者，呼至案前，细为评点曰：“某一节，人错待汝；某一节，无此情理，谎词也。

    Explanation

    421

    Volume Three

    The Legacy of Lord Lu Qingxian

    Lord Lu Qingxian, also known as Jia Shu, was from Danghu in Zhejiang Province. He was renowned for his mastery of Neo-Confucianism and was honored in the Confucian temple. As a newly appointed official, he served as the magistrate of Lingshou County in Zhili Province. The elders of the county have passed down stories of his exemplary governance and virtuous policies, which are worth recording. When Lord Lu Qingxian took office, he did not rely on any staff or relatives in official positions; he had only two servants and himself, making a total of three people. After waking up and washing, he would dress formally and sit on the main hall, calling the officials and clerks to instruct them: "In governing the people, the first priority is to minimize affairs. I have few matters, so I do not need many people to wait on me. Whether farming or trading, each should do what they can to support their parents and children. Those who have received their salaries should serve the state, and cannot all be dismissed. Each day, two clerks and four clerical assistants are kept on duty in shifts, while the rest carry out their duties and should not stray far." Inside the warm pavilion, there were two desks, one with legal codes and one with weights and measures. Those who paid taxes were weighed and exchanged in person, and any excess was returned. Those who submitted petitions were called to the desk for detailed review, and he would evaluate them, saying, "In one part, you were wronged by someone; in another, there is no such reason, and it is a lie."

    422

    ”应讯者，即书票钤印过朱，差原告往传被告到案，曲为开导曰：“尔原被非亲即故，非故即邻，平日皆情之至密者，今不过为婚姻田土钱债细事，一时拂意，不能忍耐，致启讼端。殊不知一讼之兴，未见曲直，而吏有纸张之费，役有饮食之需，证佐之亲友，必需酬劳，往往所费多于所争。且守候公门，废时失业，一经官断，须有输赢，从此乡党变为讼仇，薄产亿为乌有，切齿数世，悔之晚矣。即如此案某人故薄待某人。”即检律例以指示之曰：“罪应笞杖，但国法不加有礼之人，某合与某叩首服理，即回去静思三日，倘彼此豁然，来授结可也。”往往感激涕零，情愿当堂具结，和好如初，因此互相劝勉勿讼。半年之后，门庭如水，堂可张罗。

    Explanation

    422

    For those summoned for interrogation, a summons is written, stamped, and passed through official channels, and the plaintiff is dispatched to bring the defendant before the court. The official then gently advises them, saying: “You and the other party are either kin, old acquaintances, or neighbors; in the past, you were bound by the closest of ties. Now, the matter at hand is no more than a trivial dispute over marriage, land, money, or debt, which has caused a momentary grievance and an inability to endure, leading to the initiation of a lawsuit. But you do not realize that once a lawsuit is begun, the truth of who is right or wrong is not yet known, yet officials must bear the cost of paper, and the attendants must have food. Friends and relatives who serve as witnesses must also be compensated, and often the expenses of the lawsuit exceed the value of the dispute itself. Moreover, waiting at the public gate wastes time and livelihood. Once the court delivers its judgment, there must be a winner and a loser, and from that point on, neighbors become litigants and enemies. A modest estate may be reduced to nothing, and generations may live in bitter enmity, with regret coming too late. This is exactly the case with the dispute between such-and-such individuals.”

    The official then consults the legal code and explains: “The offense would warrant a beating with the rod or stick, but national law does not punish those who are respectful and courteous. Therefore, the two of you should kneel and submit to the truth. Go home and reflect for three days; if both of you come to an understanding, you may then submit a reconciliation statement in court.” Often, the parties are moved to tears, willingly submitting a reconciliation statement in court and restoring their former harmony. Because of this, they encourage one another to avoid litigation. After half a year, the court is as quiet as a still pond, and the hall can once again be prepared for other matters.

    423

    公无事，带一书一役，挨村讲乡约，训以孝弟忠信，节义廉耻，必引经据典，参以时事，民皆乐听，浅直易从，无窒碍难行者。遇孝慈节义之人，立即表扬，且为之咨嗟叹赏曰：“我所不及。”凡获小偷到案，则曰：“汝亦有人心者也，何至为此？”偷曰：“小人为贫所迫耳。”公曰：“是不难。为利之最厚者，莫如纺织，且人人能为之。”随命仆市棉花斤余，使偷在堂右，教以纺花之法。曰：“能，即释汝；不能，即惰也，罪加倍。”偷愿速释，无不留心学习，数日间，即能为矣。公曰：“此赀本不过数百钱耳，今数日内，循环例换，已赢余若干，除汝饭食外，尚剩有数百钱，汝回去执业。倘再犯，不恕汝也。”偷泣而去，改过迁善者多。间有犯，则杖而后教之，在堂上纺花一月。三犯，则曰：“是不能改矣。”使二役挟之急行千步，以热醋灌之，一碗饮至半，使一人突拍其背，则嗽终身不愈，不能作贼矣，仍纺花以没世。

    Explanation

    423

    When the official had no urgent matters, he would take along a book and a servant, visiting villages to explain the village regulations and teach the people about filial piety, fraternal duty, loyalty, trustworthiness, righteousness, propriety, integrity, and shame. He would always cite classical texts and relate them to current events, and the people were all happy to listen, finding the teachings simple, direct, and easy to follow, with no obstacles or difficulties. When he encountered people who were filial, kind, righteous, and virtuous, he would immediately praise them, and he would sigh and admire, saying, "I am not as good as they are." Whenever a thief was caught, he would say, "You are also a human being, how could you do such a thing?" The thief would respond, "It's just that I was forced by poverty." The official would then say, "That is not difficult. The most profitable thing is weaving, and everyone can do it." He would then order his servant to buy several pounds of cotton and make the thief stand to the right of the hall, teaching him the method of spinning cotton. He would say, "If you can do it, I will release you; if you cannot, it is due to laziness, and the punishment will be doubled." The thief wanted to be released quickly and paid close attention to learning. Within a few days, he could do it. The official would say, "The capital required is only a few hundred coins. Within a few days, through repeated exchanges, he has already made a profit. After deducting your food expenses, there are still several hundred coins left. Go back and practice your trade. If you commit another crime, I will not forgive you." The thief would weep and leave, and many of them reformed and turned to good. If he occasionally committed another crime, he would be beaten and then taught, spinning cotton in the hall for a month. If he committed a third crime, the official would say, "He cannot be reformed." He would order two servants to forcibly make him run a thousand steps, then pour hot vinegar down his throat until he had drunk half a bowl. One person would suddenly slap his back, causing him to cough for the rest of his life, unable to be a thief anymore. He would continue spinning cotton until the end of his life.

    424

    时尚未设养廉。若奉文有摊捐款，必具文申请曰：“职奉银四十五两，仅敷自食，所需捐款，动钱粮耶？抑杂税耶？”道府寿，以挂面十束、筋烛一对，躬亲致祝。上游皆恶其执，甄别已之。后任甫至，即有报案者，系以斧杀人于旷野，不知凶手为谁。后官访于公曰：“老前辈任此数年，囹圄空虚，案无留牍，可见一邑之人，皆在春风化雨中矣。弟甫下车，即有疑难命案，若留疑以有待者，何也？”公曰：“方自愧虚糜，何敢当兄台盛誉？命案有无，偶然耳。案在何处？”曰：“某村。”公思之曰：“必某人所为也。”后官拘其人至，曰：“案无事由，杀无证佐，何所见而执吾？”后官曰：“此前官所教，必无谬误。”其人曰：“前官，圣贤也，岂肯冤人？”后官无词可答，曰：“带汝见前官，谅必有说。”于是押犯诣公。

    Explanation

    424

    At the time, there was no system of salaries or allowances for officials. If they received funds from localities to support their official duties, they would have to formally apply, saying: "I receive only forty-five taels of silver, which is barely enough for my own sustenance. Where does the additional donation come from? Is it from regular taxes or miscellaneous levies?" On the occasion of a superior's birthday, local officials would present gifts such as bundles of noodles and pairs of candles, personally offering their congratulations. Superiors disapproved of the official's strict adherence to rules and thus excluded him from promotion. When a new official arrived, immediately a report came in of a murder in a field, with no knowledge of the perpetrator. The new official asked the previous one, "For several years, you have governed this district with such benevolence that the prison is empty and there are no pending cases. How can it be that I, having just taken office, am faced with a difficult murder case, leaving doubts unresolved?" The previous official replied, "I am ashamed of my own inadequacies and how dare I accept your high praise? The occurrence of a murder case is random. Where did the case take place?" The new official said, "In a certain village." The previous official thought for a moment and said, "It must have been done by a certain person." The new official then arrested the suspect and said, "There are no details about the case, and there is no evidence or witnesses. How can you be so sure that it was this person?" The new official replied, "This is what the previous official taught me, and it must be correct." The suspect said, "The previous official was a sage, how could he wrongfully accuse someone?" The new official had no words to respond and said, "Take you to see the previous official, he must have a reason." Thus, the suspect was taken to the previous official.

    425

    其人见公，即有惭惧之色。公呼使前，曰：“某人，汝忘余谆戒之苦心耶？余初至汝村讲乡约时，见汝面有凶横气，历历开导，汝色渐和。逾今三载，前色顿变，其为不改凶心，致有此事。可知杀人者死，罪无可逃，与其受尽责扑而后陈，不如直陈无隐，身不受亏，而恩犹可冀。”其人叩首泣曰：“洞见小人肺腑矣！昔年我与被杀之某，共交一嫠妇，后某独占而屏我，是不平。嗣公至以善劝人，人皆革面洗心，其不能者，为众所不齿。皆偕妇出亡，我亦闻公言自悔。今公甫谢事，某与妇仍归旧村，且讪笑村众日；‘汝等不过畏陆青天耳，渠不能自保，今尚有此不近人情官耶？’小人恨其词，触前忿，潜杀之，意谓无人能破此寨，不意公竟先觉也，虽死奚辞！”后官怜其尚有良心，薄其罪而流之。国法，凡甄别官，例引见。公入都面君，见其岸然道貌，奏对从容不苟，圣祖大悦，卿相复保奏之，用为礼部主事，洊升侍御，致仕归。

    Explanation

    425

    Upon seeing the official, the person immediately showed a look of shame and fear. The official called him forward and said, "Mr. [Person's Name], have you forgotten my earnest and sincere warnings? When I first came to your village to teach the village rules, I saw a fierce and domineering look on your face. I patiently guided you, and your expression gradually became more gentle. More than three years later, your previous expression suddenly changed, indicating that you did not change your wicked heart, leading to this incident. It is known that those who kill will die, and there is no escape from the crime. It is better to speak frankly without hiding anything, so you do not suffer harm, and still have hope for mercy." The person knelt down and wept, saying, "You have seen into the depths of a small person's heart! In the past, I and the person who was killed both had relations with a widowed woman. Later, he took her exclusively and excluded me, which was unfair. After you came and encouraged people to be good, everyone reformed their ways and reformed their hearts, and those who could not were despised by the community. They both fled with their wives, and I also heard your words and repented. Now that you have just retired, he and his wife have returned to the old village and mock the villagers, saying, 'You all are only afraid of the upright official Mr. Lu; he cannot protect himself, so how can there still be such an inhumane official?' I hated his words, which triggered my previous anger, and I secretly killed him. I thought no one could break this stronghold, but I did not expect that you would discover it first, but even if I die, I have nothing to say!" Later, the official, moved by his still existing conscience, reduced his punishment and exiled him. According to national law, all officials who are evaluated are usually summoned for an audience. The official went to the capital to meet the emperor. He saw his dignified and upright demeanor, responded calmly and without carelessness, and Emperor Kangxi was very pleased. The ministers recommended him again, and he was appointed as a secretary of the Ministry of Rites, promoted to a higher position, and retired home.

    426

    芗厈曰：人之所以异于禽兽者，廉耻二字。若忘此，虽圣贤不能化也。故盗贼之三犯者，只好饮之以醋而已。至杀人者，虽因奸也，实缘讪笑公而顿激忿怒，斯人尚有良心。彼甄别公者，直斯人不若矣，当以醋灌之。
    　　
    　　补骗子五则
    　　
    　　京师布肆，大业也。有乡人来，阅布数十匹，约值京钱五十余贯。拣既，谓肆主曰：“我初学肩贩，须同伙来定，姑待之可乎？”应曰：“诺。”别为他人贸易。乡人坐半日，见买卖渐稀，谓肆主曰：“我尚未饭，伙又不来，身无余钱，所存赀本银两系合伙者，未便换钱，腹中馁甚，奈何？”肆主曰：“既有银两，无患无钱，若未便换，典之可也。”乡人欣然出银，灿然一提，揖肆主曰：“我不知质库在何处，此银二十两，请烦贵伙代为入典。但我不过饭食所需，质钱一缗足矣。”肆主交其伙典钱一串，并券交付，乡人感谢去。未几复来，肆主曰：“何速也？”曰：“吾侪小人，果腹而已，不求甚佳。

    Explanation

    426

    Xiang Yan said: "What distinguishes humans from animals is the sense of shame and honor. If one forgets this, even sages and virtuous men cannot transform them. Therefore, those who repeatedly commit theft or robbery can only be dealt with by 'drinking vinegar.' As for murderers, even if they kill due to adultery, it is actually because they were mocked in public and suddenly became enraged. Such a person still has a sense of conscience. Those who mock others in public are even worse than this person. They should be treated with the same harshness as 'drinking vinegar.'"

    Five Addenda on Scammers

    The Cloth Shop in the Capital

    The cloth shop in the capital is a major business. A rural person came in, examined dozens of bolts of cloth, and agreed to a price of over 50,000 coins. After selecting the cloth, he told the shop owner, "I am new to this trade, so I need to wait for my partner to arrive. Can I wait a moment?" The shop owner replied, "Of course." He then went to trade with others. The rural person sat for half an hour, seeing that trade was slowing down, and said to the shop owner, "I haven't had any food yet, and my partner hasn't arrived. I have no extra money, and the silver I have is for a partnership, so I can't exchange it. I am very hungry. What should I do?" The shop owner said, "Since you have silver, there is no need to worry about money. If you can't exchange it, you can pawn it." The rural person happily took out the silver, a full bag, and bowed to the shop owner, saying, "I don't know where the pawn shop is. This silver is twenty taels. Please ask your partner to pawn it for me. But I only need money for a meal, so pawning a thousand coins would be enough." The shop owner gave his partner a string of coins and the pawn ticket, and the rural person thanked him and left. Shortly after, he returned, and the shop owner said, "You came back so quickly?" He replied, "We small people, we only need to eat, and we don't seek the best."

    427

    ”余钱八百余文，掷柜上，坐守至晚，其伙竟不来。乡人惶然谓肆主曰：“天将暮，难出城，我伙或以要事不得来，盍以我二十金之券，抵运布去，尚有余钱，明日偕伙来算可乎？”应曰：“可。”乡人乃郑重出券，交之曰：“此即贵伙代当者，请留三日，我自赎取归账。”肆主验之无误，遂收执。乡人以余钱雇驴车，载布去。候之三日，竟不来，乃赴质库取银，仅有二两，肆主曰：“我所质者二十两大锭也，何以仅止此？”典主查簿示之曰：“是日贵伙以二十金质钱一串，我疑其所需过少，问之，曰：‘是客物也，只需此为饭食耳。’我予券去。随即有乡人持券加利取去，又有二人，以此小锭亦质钱一串，汝所收之券是也。”肆主始恍然悟为骗子掉换去矣。

    Explanation

    427

    A villager had eight hundred and some coins left, which he threw onto the counter. He sat there waiting until late, but his partner did not come at all. The villager, in a panic, told the shop owner, "It's getting late, it's hard to leave the city. My partner might have some important matter and cannot come. Why don't you accept my twenty taels of silver as a voucher to transport the cloth? There is still some extra money; can we settle it tomorrow when my partner comes?" The shop owner replied, "Yes." The villager then carefully produced the voucher and gave it to him, saying, "This is the voucher for your partner to take over. Please keep it for three days; I will come back to redeem it and settle the account." The shop owner checked it and found it correct, then took it. The villager used the remaining money to hire a donkey cart to transport the cloth. After waiting for three days, he did not come at all. The villager then went to the pawnshop to get the silver, but only two taels were there. The shop owner said, "What I pawned was twenty taels in two large ingots. Why is it only this much?" The pawnshop owner checked the records and showed him, "On that day, your partner pawns twenty taels for a string of cash. I suspected that he needed too little, so I asked him. He said, 'It's for a guest, just enough for food.' I gave him the voucher. Then a villager came with the voucher and took it with interest. There were two more people who also pawns the small ingots for a string of cash. The voucher you received is this one." The shop owner finally realized that it was a scam and the voucher had been switched.

    428

    京师骡马市，大集也。有贵官，戴五品冠，服色甚丽，气象雄伟，似武弁入朝者。至鞍鞯市，择一佳者，出大银一锭，谓肆主曰：“我仆因买他物，分遣开矣。烦汝伙肩此鞍至骡市，我欲试良马也。”主者即遣一人为负去。至市，择一大骡甚骏，价值数百金，命来人以鞍鞯备之曰：“汝在此姑待，我试骑之。”卖骡人见有仆在，任其鞭驰而去，久不返。谓其人曰：“汝主何往耶？”其人曰：“我鞍鞯铺之伙，孰为我主？”卖驴人骇曰：“是必骗子也。汝铺亦被诓矣。”其人曰：“幸有银在。”于是偕往铺中，出银公估，则铅心伪物也。共鸣诸官，海捕而已。

    Explanation

    428

    The Imperial City's Mule and Horse Market was a bustling hub. There was a high-ranking official, wearing a hat of the fifth rank, dressed in splendid attire, exuding a majestic presence, much like a military officer entering the court. Upon arriving at the saddle and bridle market, he selected a fine saddle and handed over a large silver ingot to the shop owner, saying, "My servant went to buy something else and has been sent away. Please have your assistant carry this saddle to the mule market; I wish to try out a good horse." The shop owner immediately dispatched an assistant to carry the saddle away. Upon reaching the market, they chose a very fine mule worth several hundred gold pieces, and the owner instructed the assistant to prepare the saddle for it, saying, "Stay here for now; I will try riding it." The mule seller, seeing the assistant present, allowed him to ride off, and the official did not return for a long time. The assistant asked, "Where did your master go?" The assistant replied, "I am just an assistant from the saddle shop. Who is your master?" The mule seller was startled and said, "This must be a scam. Your shop has also been deceived." The assistant said, "Fortunately, there is still silver here." They then went together to the shop, and upon presenting the silver for appraisal, it turned out to be a lead-core fake. The matter was reported to the authorities, and a nationwide search warrant was issued.

    429

    某观察，富而淫者也。姬妾满前，各自树党争宠，而正夫人不顾也。需次会垣时，恒有花婆出入公馆，忽带少妇，携珠翠求售。其为人也，轻盈软媚，妇女皆爱怜之。夫人乐于盘桓，曰：“那得若个好娘子，长为我伴，则我得所托矣。”妇笑曰：“妾何如人，敢当夫人青目耶！妾夫素跟官，若夫人不弃，言于大人，则夫妇皆有倚赖矣。”长跪而请。夫人曰：“是不难。尔能并事大人，与我一气，何事不可也？”妇再拜称谢。

    Explanation

    429

    A certain official, wealthy and licentious, had many concubines before him, each forming factions to compete for his favor, while his legitimate wife paid no heed. During his time in the capital, there was always a courtesan coming in and out of his official residence. Suddenly, she brought a young woman, carrying jewelry to sell. The young woman was graceful and soft in manner, and women all loved and pitied her. The wife enjoyed lingering with her and said, "How I wish there were such a good woman, to be my constant companion, then I would find someone to rely on." The young woman smiled and said, "What kind of person am I, to deserve the favor of the lady? My husband has always served as an official. If the lady does not disdain me, and speaks to the master, then both my husband and I will have support." She knelt for a long time to request. The wife said, "It is not difficult. If you can serve the master alongside me, and be in harmony with me, what cannot be done?" The young woman knelt twice and expressed her gratitude.

    430

    适观察入，夫人使妇递茶，即以眉目传情，观察大悦，问此妇何来？夫人以夫妇愿投靠对，观察曰：“其妇如是，其夫可知。我正少一知心青衣，收之可也。”妇即迎膝叩首，观察手援之，即扶观察之手而起，益悦之，于是妇留而花婆去。唤其夫来，果干仆也。其夫妇遂迁居邻院，朝出暮归，殷勤奉侍。观察虽与妇情密，调唇抚乳，无所不为，而未及于乱者，公馆无隙地也。一日，妇送茗至书室，观察置诸膝而恳曰：“能毕乃公心事，则衣珠财物惟汝所欲。”妇曰：“非不甘心，奈众目昭彰乎？无已，请遣我夫他出，晚至妾室，惟命是从矣。”言甫毕，报客至，妇脱身入。

    Explanation

    430

    When the magistrate entered, his wife sent the woman to serve tea. Immediately, she conveyed affection through her eyes and eyebrows, and the magistrate was greatly pleased. He asked where this woman came from, and the magistrate's wife explained that the couple wished to seek shelter. The magistrate said, "If the wife is like this, then the husband can be known. I just need a trusted maid. It is possible to take her." The woman immediately embraced his knees and bowed her head. The magistrate supported her with his hand, and she then supported his hand and stood up, even more pleased with her. Thus, the woman stayed, and the matchmaker left. He called her husband to come, and indeed, he was a low-ranking servant. The couple then moved to the neighboring courtyard, going out in the morning and returning in the evening, serving attentively. Although the magistrate had a close relationship with the woman, kissing and caressing her breasts, doing nothing but, he did not reach disorder, as the magistrate's residence had no room. One day, the woman served tea to the study. The magistrate placed it on his knees and earnestly said, "If she could complete my lord's desires, then clothes, jewels, and money as you wish." The woman said, "Not unwilling, but many eyes are clearly visible. If not, please send my husband elsewhere. In the evening to my room, only obey." Just finished speaking, reported that guests had arrived, and the woman escaped and entered.

    431

    观察乃以数十金，使其夫远出干事，当晚潜入妇室，妇亦早归，正好合时，其夫持刀排门突入，喝曰：“我知汝年轻。必不能安其室，果留男子，合杀却否？”妇泣跪曰：“非妾敢犯救，主人势逼，无奈相从。”夫曰：“必非主也，岂有身后贵官，而知法犯法哉？”妇泣唤主起，夫乃持刀视曰：“果是主人，将何以全奴颜面乎？”观察曰：“汝能周旋，将以汝为长班。得官之日，政由宁氏，祭则寡人，能容之否？”其夫半跪称谢，呼妇起曰：“汝好好伺候主人，我出差去矣。

    Explanation

    431

    The official used several dozen gold coins to send the husband far away on a business trip. That night, he secretly entered the woman's room. The woman had also returned early, making the timing perfect. The husband, upon entering, shouted, "I know you are young and cannot keep your husband satisfied. If indeed a man was left behind, should he be killed or not?" The woman wept and knelt, saying, "It was not I who dared to commit adultery; the master forced me by his power, and I had no choice but to comply." The husband said, "It must not be the master. How could a nobleman after death know the law and break it?" The woman called out to the master to rise. The husband then held a knife and said, "If it is indeed the master, how can I preserve my face?" The official said, "If you can handle the situation, I will make you the head servant. On the day I become an official, the administration will be handled by the Ning family, but I will perform the sacrifices. Can you accept this?" The husband bowed halfway and thanked him, calling the woman to rise and saying, "Serve the master well. I will go on a business trip."

    432

    ”观察色丧神颓，妇起闭门，投怀而慰解之，始得安于阳台。未几，观察得缺，竟以其司阍，妇则留于内室，不复出矣。其夫大权在握，任其播弄，观察无可如何，听之而已。然能劝其主勉事上官。未几权廉访，其夫招摇撞骗，无所不为。时有富室身犯大辟，许以数万金出罪，富室知为廉访信使，从之，得赃而遁，富室首于中丞。廉访始知其事，亟传妇严讯之。妇曰：“是非妾夫也，妾本妓女，彼以重聘雇妾来，认为夫妇，嘱妾勾引主人，若得满载而归，许以半分，妾故愿为之使。然不知其何许人，姓氏亦未确也。”廉访无可奈何，坐此败官。后妇之本夫来，又不得不重酬妇而遣之。

    Explanation

    432

    The observer, in a state of despair and mental exhaustion, was comforted by his wife, who closed the door and embraced him, soothing his troubled spirit. He finally found peace in a place of intimacy. Soon after, the observer lost his position, and the husband took over as the gatekeeper, while the wife remained in the inner room and did not leave. The husband, wielding absolute power, manipulated the situation as he pleased, leaving the observer helpless and resigned to accept it. However, the observer could still advise the master to serve his superiors diligently. Not long after, the husband rose to the position of a censor, and he swaggered and deceived, doing whatever he wished. At one point, a wealthy man was accused of a serious crime, and the husband offered him a bribe of tens of thousands of gold pieces to avoid punishment. The wealthy man, knowing the husband was a trusted messenger of the censor, accepted the bribe, fled with the money, and then reported the incident to a higher official. The censor, finally learning about the matter, urgently summoned the wife for a strict interrogation. The wife defended herself, saying, "This man is not my husband. I am a prostitute whom he hired with a large dowry, and he made me believe we were husband and wife. He asked me to seduce the master, promising me half of the proceeds if I succeeded. I willingly did as he asked, but I do not know who he really is, and his name and identity are unclear to me." The censor, powerless to stop the situation, was dismissed from office as a result. Later, when the wife's original husband came, the censor had no choice but to heavily reward her and send her away.

    433

    有徐大诳者，丝市中伙也。平日喜诳人取笑，以是得其美称。一日，随居停至吴下贸易，舟泊太子码头，居停谓徐曰：“予起货去，汝留此须慎防之，此处多骗子，勿为所诳。”徐曰：“吾不诳人，人其诳我乎？人果行其诳，我将诳谁矣？”居停曰：“咍！此处骗子，皆鲜衣盛服，充衣冠中人。汝若遇之，必逢迎不惶，恐小巫见大巫，堕其术中而不觉也。汝必慎之。”徐曰：“诺。请予洋银二牧，我将作虎邱之游。”居停给之而去。见肆有锡洋钱，为孩童所玩弄者，与真无异。

    Explanation

    433

    There was a man named Xu Da Kuang, a commoner from the silk market. He enjoyed deceiving people for amusement and thus gained a good reputation. One day, he accompanied his host to trade in Wu (southern region), and their boat docked at the Prince's Wharf. The host told Xu, "I will go to collect goods, you should stay here and be cautious. There are many scammers here, do not be deceived by them." Xu replied, "I do not deceive people, will people deceive me? If people indeed deceive others, who will I deceive then?" The host said, "Ha! The scammers here all wear fine clothes and pretend to be gentlemen. If you meet them, you will surely greet them with great respect, fearing that a lesser sorcerer will be overwhelmed by a greater one, and you will fall into their trap without realizing it. You must be cautious." Xu said, "Yes. Please give me two silver dollars, I will go on a trip to Tiger Hill." The host gave him the money and left. Xu saw a shop with tin foreign coins being played with by children, indistinguishable from real ones.

    434

    问其价，枚值五六文，徐置百枚，又以百余钱买一布囊，复大书日“某年月日某乡某人置”。以棉纸裹锡钱，作两封，纳囊中，负之归舟。易短褐毡冠，作乡人状。以红纸开绸缎账单，怀之，持雨盖负囊，游于市。见古玩铺前，坐一客，衣冠甚丽。时正隆冬，披火狐裘，貉冠皮鞋，手持玉嘴银头乌木烟管。徐有触于怀，乃坐对门巨室阶上，出其单与锡洋布目前，以真洋钱颠掷，而口作怨词。正自嗫嚅间，客见其多金，洋洋来前，喝问曰：“汝何等人？坐我府前，意欲何为？”徐忙收洋钱入囊，执账单拜曰：“不识尊府，动扰不当。

    Explanation

    434

    He asked for the price, and each piece was worth five or six coins. He carefully placed a hundred pieces, then bought a cloth bag with a hundred or so coins, and wrote in large characters on it, "On a certain day in a certain month in a certain village, a certain person placed it here." He wrapped the tin coins in cotton paper, made two bundles, and put them in the bag. He carried the bag back to his boat. He changed into a simple, coarse garment and a felt hat, appearing as a local villager. He opened a silk and satin invoice with red paper, kept it in his possession, carried an umbrella, and carried the bag, wandering in the market. He saw a customer sitting in front of an antique shop, dressed very elegantly. It was mid-winter, and the customer was wearing a red fox fur coat, a mink hat, and leather shoes, holding a jade-tipped, silver-headed ebony smoking pipe. Xu had a sudden thought, and he sat on the steps of a large house opposite. He took out the invoice and showed the tin coins and cloth to the customer, flipping the real coins, and muttered complaints. While he was muttering to himself, the customer saw his wealth and approached him arrogantly, shouting, "Who are you? Sitting in front of my house, what do you intend to do?" Xu quickly put the coins back in the bag, took the invoice, and bowed, saying, "I didn't recognize your house, and I shouldn't have caused trouble."

    435

    我某县乡人也，因侄女将嫁，兄与我百洋并账单，使置嫁衣。我至大邦，不识大绸缎局在何处，又不知买单中物敷用否，无人指点，以是怨恨耳。”客接其单视之，皆奁中急需物，信为真实，笑指之曰：“北去里许，高墙大门内有绸缎局，是我亲戚所设，两京十八省客，皆贩其贷，真大行市也。汝往必无欺。”徐曰：“我目不识丁，虽有招牌，奈不能认，请借贵步一引可乎？”客曰：“幸我无事，汝其从之。

    Explanation

    435

    I am a native of a certain county. Because my niece is about to be married, my elder brother gave me a hundred yamens and an invoice, asking me to buy wedding clothes. When I arrived in a major city, I did not know where the large silk and satin shop was, nor did I know if the items on the invoice were sufficient. There was no one to guide me, which made me resentful. The guest took the invoice and looked at it, noting that all the items were urgently needed for the dowry. He believed it to be true and laughed, pointing out, "Go about a mile north; inside a high wall and large gate, there is a silk and satin shop. It is set up by my relative. Customers from the two capitals and eighteen provinces all trade there; it is truly a major market. You will definitely not be deceived." I said, "I cannot read or write. Although there is a sign, I cannot recognize it. Please allow me to follow you." The guest replied, "Fortunately, I have nothing to do. You can follow me."

    436

    ”徐负囊随其后，行未几，徐曰：“我尚未餐，腹中馁甚，官人肯同一点心否？”客乃引入面肆上坐，徐坐其旁，面尚未至，徐促额曰：“我患疟痢未痊，不意又发作矣，中急而寒甚，奈何？”客曰：“去厕不远，汝去登之。”徐曰：“奈此累坠物乎？”举囊交客曰：“暂累官人看守，我非不放心，奈寒甚，乞假我短挂一披，烟管一吸。”客自思火狐短挂与烟管不过三十余金，诓其百洋，其利倍蓰，乃故作踌躇而后解语之曰：“此百金物，汝速往速来，慎勿污我衣。”徐乃郑重披裘执筒而逸。客待其出肆，携囊遁回，谓家人曰：“今日诓得洋银百元，当为我贺。”出囊审之，疑有伪，急请识者，方知皆锡物，不禁哑然失笑曰：“我一生骗人为业，反为人骗，数十年老阿婆，今竟倒绷孩儿矣。”

    Explanation

    436

    Xu carries a bag and follows behind, walking a short distance. After walking a short distance, Xu says, "I haven't eaten yet, and I'm very hungry. Will you share some snacks with me?" The guest then brings him to a noodle shop and they sit down. Xu sits beside him. The noodles haven't arrived yet, and Xu says, "I have malaria and diarrhea that haven't fully healed, and it seems to have flared up again. I feel extremely cold and urgent." The guest says, "The restroom is not far away, go there." Xu says, "What about these heavy items?" He hands over the bag to the guest and says, "Please take care of it temporarily. I'm not being careless, but I'm so cold, please lend me your short coat and let me smoke a pipe." The guest thinks that a short coat and a pipe are worth no more than thirty gold pieces, and he can trick him into giving him a hundred gold pieces, which would be very profitable. He pretends to hesitate and then says, "These are worth a hundred gold pieces, go quickly and come back quickly, be careful not to dirty my clothes." Xu puts on the coat and takes the pipe and leaves. The guest waits until Xu leaves the shop, then takes the bag and runs back, saying to his family, "Today I tricked him into giving me a hundred silver dollars, let's celebrate." He takes out the bag and examines it, suspecting it might be fake. He quickly asks someone knowledgeable, and finds out they are all tin. He can't help but laugh and says, "I have made a career of deceiving others, but I was deceived myself. After decades of being a seasoned trickster, I've been tricked by a child."

    437

    查禁倭烟之年，有伪符假役，在江河之间，挨船搜索，讹攫银钱，为害行旅。惟不掠贷物，不携衣装，是以告讦之案甚少。有黠客贩货入楚，舟泊江滨，突来壮役七八人銕索郎当，手持签票，口称官命，查抄鸦片，入舟搜检。见银钱皆取之，以充饭食。值客卧病，其伙拦阻不及，盘缠被攫尽矣，不觉垂泪。客徐起见之，笑曰：“毋作妇人态。从来悖入者亦悖出，被将十倍偿我，无忧也。”是时同泊之舟，无一免者，人皆切齿。

    Explanation

    437

    During the year when the government was cracking down on Japanese opium, there were false officials and fake enforcers who, in the rivers and streams, searched boats one by one, deceiving and seizing silver and money, causing harm to travelers. They only did not steal goods and did not take clothing, which is why there were very few cases of reporting and denunciation. There was a clever merchant transporting goods into Chu, and his boat docked at the riverbank. Suddenly, seven or eight strong officials came with iron chains and ropes, holding warrants, claiming to act under official orders, searching and seizing opium, and entering the boat to search. Seeing silver and money, they all took it to pay for their meals. When the guest was sick, his companions could not stop them, and all his travel expenses were taken away, causing him to unconsciously shed tears. The guest slowly got up and saw them, smiling and saying, "Do not act like a woman. What goes against the grain will also come out against the grain; I will be compensated tenfold, so there is no worry." At that time, none of the boats moored at the riverbank were spared, and everyone gnashed their teeth in anger.

    438

    黠客于是择舟子之强有力者十余人，饰以仆从之服，自乃冠水晶顶，造作令箭，急易快舟，尾追伪役，沿江而下。役掳掠满载，忽绕出其前，使仆截擒，即以其铁索锁之。登邮亭而讯曰：“本厅奉军门查拿伪役抄抢案，送省枭示。”先起其赃，彼舟载银钱已数千矣，伪授皆叩首乞命。于是运赃入己舟，将伪役交驿卒看守曰：“候本厅禀报后，来取若人，勿任逃脱，自干重罪。”竟扬帆逆流而归，以钱俵分舟子，银则归己，无不欢呼痛快。伪役忽解悟，告驿卒曰：“是官也，何以获赃不获犯？假可知也。”驿卒亦悟，曰：“无论真假，我辈岂白与人看待哉！”伪役已无一钱，乃各脱其衣履贿驿卒，始得纵归。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：骗子奇矣，然不过乘人所欲而中之，乃竞有骗骗子者，使之笑不得哭不得，大快人心。可见天下事，出奇无穷。纵有智士才人，只好一齐俯首。

    Explanation

    438

    The cunning guest then selected more than ten strong boatmen, dressed them in the attire of servants, and himself wore a crystal crown, forged command tokens, quickly exchanged for a fast boat, and chased after the fake officials downstream along the river. The officials, laden with loot, suddenly emerged before him, and he had his servants intercept and capture them, immediately locking them with an iron chain. Ascending to the post station, he interrogated them, saying, "This office, under the orders of the military governor, is investigating the case of fake officials involved in robbery and plunder, and they are to be sent to the province for public execution." First, he seized the stolen goods; the boat was already loaded with thousands of silver coins, and the fake officials all knelt and begged for mercy. He then transported the stolen goods into his own boat and handed over the fake officials to the postal guards for custody, instructing them, "After reporting to this office, come to retrieve them, do not let them escape, as they will bear the heavy penalty themselves." Finally, he raised the sails and returned against the current, distributing the money among the boatmen and keeping the silver for himself; all were cheering and delighted. The fake officials suddenly realized, telling the postal guards, "Is this official, why did he seize the goods but not the criminals? It must be a fake." The guards also realized, saying, "Regardless of whether true or false, we are not to be treated lightly!" The fake officials had no money left, and they then removed their clothes and shoes to bribe the postal guards, and were finally allowed to go free.

    The commentator said: The trickster is indeed extraordinary, yet he merely exploits people's desires to deceive them. But there are even those who deceive the tricksters, leaving them neither laughing nor crying, greatly pleasing the hearts of the people. It is evident that the world's affairs are endlessly surprising. Even the most intelligent and talented individuals can only bow their heads in the face of such cunning.

    439

    奸僧狱
    　　
    　　浙有嘉禾生，为邑之巨富，将入棘闱，偕其妻与姑母仆婢辈，同赴武陵，寓西湖山庄，以便游观。生妇少艾而佞佛，以天竺进香为请，生从之，命仆唤肩舆。仆体主人省约意，不投行家，散雇舆六乘，价甚廉。生妇艳装盛饰，罗绮满身，珠翠盈头，价值千金，乃与姑母仆妇婢女辈乘舆而行，生骑马在先，仆携香烛随行。至半途，生妇之舆杆折，五乘皆随骑而前，是舆落后。一夫抽取折杆回去，谋易新者，一夫守之，仆亦立候。舆夫笑曰：“主人将抵山门矣。汝不以香烛去，有不失误詈汝者乎？况五乘前行，仅一乘伙回易杆，即来，何须呆守耶？”仆信其言，追奔而去。一夫以杆来，共舁入乱山中。生妇惶惑诘问何以行僻径，舆夫曰：“为时已久，恐官人守候，出捷径，入庙之后户，不甚便耶？”妇亦信之。未几停舆，见墙缺朱门，舆夫轻叩其铜环，一沙弥提关出曰：“来耶？”至舆前，请夫人下舆。

    Explanation

    439

    Prison of the Wicked Monk

    In the region of Zhejiang, there was a wealthy man named Jiahe Sheng. He was a prominent figure in his hometown and was preparing to enter the imperial examination hall. Accompanied by his wife, mother-in-law, and servants and maidservants, he traveled to Wuling, where they stayed at West Lake Villa for the convenience of sightseeing. His wife was young and beautiful, and devoted to Buddhism. She requested to go to Tianzhu (India) to offer incense, and he agreed. He ordered his servant to call for a sedan chair. Understanding the host's frugal intention, the servant did not hire from a professional service but instead hired six sedan chairs individually at a very low price. The wife of the host was dressed in beautiful and elaborate attire, adorned with pearls and jade, worth a thousand gold coins. She and the others traveled in sedan chairs, while the host rode ahead on horseback, carrying incense and candles along.

    Reaching halfway, the pole of the sedan chair broke. The other five sedan chairs followed the horse, leaving this one behind. One man took the broken pole back to replace it with a new one, while another guarded it. The servant also waited. The sedan chair bearer laughed and said, "The master is about to reach the temple gate. You do not take the incense and candles with you. Is there anyone who will not scold you for making a mistake? Especially since the other five sedan chairs are already ahead, only one is being repaired. It will come soon, why wait foolishly?" The servant believed what he said and ran after and left. One man returned with the pole, and they carried the broken sedan chair into the mountains. The wife of the host was alarmed and asked why they were taking a shortcut. The sedan chair bearer said, "It has been a long time. Fearing the master is waiting, we took a shortcut and entered the temple through the back door. Isn't it inconvenient?" The wife also believed him. Soon the sedan chair stopped. They saw a red door with a broken wall. The sedan chair bearer lightly knocked on the copper ring. A novice monk opened the gate and said, "Come in?" He came to the sedan chair and asked the lady to get down.

    440

    生妇曰：“我家人安在耶？”沙弥曰：“多在前殿，候夫人久矣。”生妇视沙弥，年不过八九龄，谅无他，不得已，从之入门，曲折引入密室，请夫人少坐。沙弥去而健妇至，生妇益惶急，亟询官人安在，健妇笑曰：“休问官人，我辈皆香客，亦为大师掠至，无奈相从，汝若婉顺，则此间吃著不尽。且人尽夫也。何乐如之？否则暴虐相加，无从逃避。”妇知入危地，悲忿觅死。一僧突入曰：“何来泼妇，入我法门中，敢肆横耶？”喝令健妇并沙弥齐上。将生妇衣饰洗剥，以布带缚其手足于醉翁椅上强奸之。生妇痛骂，又一僧以麻桃塞其口而递淫之，妇亦昏沉如醉，听之而已。至夜，健妇以米粉汤执鼻而灌，不得不咽，仍劝其相从，自苦无益。生妇任以巧言，合目不答，健妇以衾覆之而去。次日来，除其失溺，为之洗沐，焚香于室，群僧复入，互相嬲也。如是六七日，忽见群僧变色相谋，变头接耳，不可闻。是夜健妇灌之以药，冥然如死。

    Explanation

    440

    The wife of the living person said, "Where are my family members?" The novice monk replied, "Most are in the front hall, having been waiting for Madam for a long time." The wife of the living person looked at the novice monk, who was no more than eight or nine years old, and believed there was nothing else. Reluctantly, she followed him inside, winding her way into a secret room, and politely asked Madam to sit for a while. The novice monk left, and the strong woman arrived. The wife of the living person became even more anxious, quickly asking where the official was. The strong woman laughed and said, "Stop asking about the official. We are all incense guests, dragged here by the master. We have no choice but to follow. If you are gentle and compliant, there is plenty to eat and enjoy here. Everyone is a husband. What could be more enjoyable? Otherwise, violence will be inflicted, and you cannot escape." The woman knew she was in danger, seeking death out of grief and anger. Suddenly, a monk entered and said, "What kind of scold came here? Into my religious domain, dared to act so recklessly?" He shouted at the strong woman and the novice monk to come up together. They stripped the wife of the living person of her clothes and decorations, bound her hands and feet with a cloth belt on the drunkard's chair, and raped her. The wife screamed in anger. Another monk plugged her mouth with a hemp peach and raped her. The woman was also dazed and drunk-like, just listening to it. At night, the strong woman forced a rice flour soup down her nose, and she could not but swallow. They still urged her to comply, but suffering alone is of no use. The wife allowed herself to be persuaded by clever words, closed her eyes, and did not answer. The strong woman covered her with a quilt and left. The next day, they came to clean up her urine and feces, washed and bathed her, and burned incense in the room. The group of monks entered again and played with each other. For six or seven days, suddenly they saw the monks change color and plot, whispering to each other, which could not be heard. That night, the strong woman gave her a drug, and she was as dead as a stone.

    441

    当生之入庙也，姑母与仆妇女婢俱至，惟不见其妻。未几，仆亦奔至，众问主母何在？曰：“随即到矣，我恐香烛有误，是以先来。”生曰：“今何在？”仆始以舆杆断折易换需时对。生惶急，命速追之，自亦乘马往寻。穷日之力，毫无影响。复使仆往接其姑婢人等，则舆亦不知去向矣。唤他舆舁归。生在寓殴仆骂婢，终宵躁急，无法可施。次日入县鸣冤，官为饬缉。

    Explanation

    441

    When the living person entered the temple, the aunt and the servants, women, and maids all arrived, but only his wife was not seen. Soon after, the servant also rushed to the scene. Everyone asked where the mistress was. The servant replied, "She will arrive shortly. I was afraid that the incense and candles might be wrong, so I came first." The host asked, "Where is she now?" The servant then said that the sedan chair's poles had broken and needed to be replaced, which would take some time. The host became anxious and ordered them to chase after her quickly, and he himself rode a horse to search for her. After searching all day, there was no sign of her. He then sent the servant to fetch his aunt and the maids, but the sedan chair was also nowhere to be found. He called another sedan chair to carry them back. At home, the host beat the servant and scolded the maids, remaining restless all night, unable to find a solution. The next day, he went to the county office to lodge a complaint, and the official ordered an investigation.

    442

    过三日，仍无踪影，生乃投省候试，与亲友商之，老成者曰：“今大中丞爱惜士子，君鸠同考者百余往诉之，较县官得力多矣。”生从之，集多士投辕门，巡官以公状入，中丞命传原告与老成者数人，谓之曰：“某生应试而带室人，且率之游荡，本属非礼，以致诲盗诲淫，皆未可定。然属有此巨案而不能破，亦大吏之责也。”即令中军率健卒五百，改装易服，散布西湖各庙访缉，限三日获犯有赏。旋传府县官至曰：“属有大憝，敢诱掠良妇，官不能破，朝廷何虚糜爵禄为也？期三日不获，必登白柬。”府县诺诺而退，命役数百人，假作香客，布散各庙，使生仆为眼目访之。

    Explanation

    442

    After three days, there was still no trace of the matter. The young man then submitted himself for examination and discussed the issue with his friends and relatives. An elderly and experienced person said, "Now the grand governor cherishes the scholars. You should gather over a hundred examinees to appeal to him; compared to the county official, this is much more effective." The young man followed the advice and gathered many scholars to approach the gate of the governor's office. The patrol officer submitted an official document, and the grand governor ordered to summon the plaintiff and several elderly people. He addressed them, saying, "A certain scholar brought his wife to the examination and led her to wander around, which is already against etiquette. This could lead to teaching theft and promiscuity, but all of these are not yet determined. However, if there is such a major case and it cannot be solved, it is also the responsibility of high officials." He immediately ordered the middle army to lead five hundred strong soldiers, who changed clothes and disguised themselves, to spread out to various temples in West Lake to investigate and search. There was a deadline of three days, and rewards for capturing the criminals. Then he summoned the officials of the prefecture and county and said, "There is a great villain who dares to entice and rob good women. The officials cannot solve it. Why does the court waste titles and salaries for this? If not captured within three days, they will be listed on a white notice." The prefecture and county officials nodded and left, ordering hundreds of officials to pretend to be incense burners and spread out to various temples, while having the young man's servant act as an informant to investigate.

    443

    逾二日无迹，文武员弁惶恐，共谋将挨搜僧之密室。僧大惧，以药迷生妇，夜使健者负置乞丐茅棚，丐者惊呼，兵役咸集，见一蓬首垢面著僧衣裤之妇，奄奄一息，势将毙矣。急唤生来认，确系其妻，呼仆妇来，舁之归寓。以开通小药灌之，穷日夜乃醒。见姑母在旁，泣诉所苦。时兵役因已得妇，皆回署禀报，生亦以前情补呈府县。各官聚谋曰：“虽有妇而不知其地其人，将何以定案？无已，尽拘西湖僧，使妇辨之，冤可白矣。”乃使生归而谋诸妇，妇愧怍难言，乘人不备而缢。生又鸣诸官，官亦无可如何矣。时诸僧已集千余人，无不呼冤者。即有奸僧在内，无可对证，良莠莫辨，以告中丞，乃命兵役遍抄静室，于兰若内搜得女舄，即以所住之僧锻炼成狱而斩之。冤乎不冤，未可知也。

    Explanation

    443

    After two days, there was no trace. The officials and military officers were anxious and fearful, and they all conspired to search the monk's secret room. The monk was greatly afraid and drugged the living woman. At night, he had strong men carry her to a beggar's hut. The beggars were startled and called out, and the soldiers gathered. They saw a woman with unkempt hair, dirty face, and wearing a monk's robe and pants, who was on the verge of death. They quickly called the husband to identify her, and he confirmed that she was his wife. They then called the maidservants to carry her back to the inn. They gave her a small dose of medicine to revive her, and after days and nights, she woke up. She saw her mother-in-law and wept, recounting her suffering. By then, the soldiers had already detained the woman and reported to their superiors. The husband also submitted a detailed account of the previous events to the prefecture and county officials. The officials gathered and discussed, saying, "Although we have the woman, we do not know where she was or who she was. How can we determine the case? There is no other way, so we must detain all the monks from West Lake and make the woman identify them. The injustice can be cleared." They then sent the husband back and instructed the woman. The woman was ashamed and could not speak, and she hanged herself when no one was looking. The husband reported this to the officials again, but the officials could do nothing about it. By then, over a thousand monks had been detained, and all of them cried out in innocence. Even if there were some evil monks among them, there was no way to prove it, and it was impossible to distinguish the good from the bad. They reported this to the governor, who then ordered the soldiers to thoroughly search the quiet rooms. Inside the temple, they found a woman's shoe, and they immediately accused the monk who lived there, tried him, and executed him. Whether it was unjust or not, it is uncertain.

    444

    芗厈曰：僧同不法，而生实自贻伊戚，冶容诲淫，何可厝崭于火？是以律禁妇女入庙烧香，此正本清源之良法也。至案已成，即使妇不死，赧颜辨认。余知此等奸僧，必不在千百众之内，送女出时，早已纠集远扬矣。然以僧室而有女舄，纵非此案奸僧，而平日所，谅必更甚于此，是以神泄其机，致被搜获，斩之亦不为冤。况杀一可以警百，亦大中丞力也。否则，此等案件，地方官只好置诸海捕而已。

    Explanation

    444

    The commentator said: Monks who act lawlessly bring trouble upon themselves through their own actions, adorning themselves in a way that invites lust, how can they place themselves on the fire? Therefore, the law prohibits women from entering temples to burn incense, which is a good method to address the root cause and clear the source. When the case is already formed, even if the woman does not die, she will blush and identify herself. I know that such deceitful monks are not among the thousands, and when they send the girl out, they have already gathered and fled far away. However, with a monk's room and women's shoes, even if these are not the monks involved in this case, their usual behavior is likely even worse, so the gods reveal their secrets, leading to their discovery and capture. Executing them would not be unjust. Moreover, killing one can warn a hundred, which is also the power of the high-ranking official. Otherwise, such cases would only be left to the local officials to issue general warrants for arrest.

    445

    义盗
    　　
    　　黄八子者，太湖大盗。奉盗魁往劫富室，明火执杖者一十八人。撞门入内，挨房搜劫财物。盗魁入，见事主之女卧室，其女年甫十五六，闻外间哄闹悲哭之声，惊骇战栗，蒙被坚卧而已。盗抽被，女坠，见姿色艳丽，强按于榻污之。诸盗掠得财物，齐聚外室，不见盗魁，黄八子复入搜寻，见盗魁拥女而卧，八子顿足曰：“噫！汝为此不义事，必遭诛戮，我等为汝误矣。”盗魁起笑曰：“好花不采，枉生人世，毋相诟也。我让汝一乐何如？”八子曰：“汝不记吾党规条乎？采花必败，天道昭彰，我将去汝不暇，尤而效之，非人也。”遂出告诸盗，辞别欲散。盗魁留之，不听，与之物，不取，脱然长往。

    Explanation

    445

    义盗

    　　黄八子，太湖一带的著名大盗。他奉盗魁之命前往抢劫富户，一行十八人，手持明晃晃的武器。他们撞开大门闯入屋内，逐屋搜寻劫掠财物。盗魁进入时，发现事主的女儿正在卧室中，她年仅十五六岁，听到外面的喧闹和悲哭声，吓得浑身发抖，只敢蒙头躲在被子里。盗贼抽开被子，女孩跌倒在地，见她姿色艳丽，便强行按倒在床上侵犯了她。其他盗贼劫掠了财物后，聚集在外屋，不见盗魁回来。黄八子再次进去寻找，发现盗魁正与女孩同床而卧。黄八子跺脚说道：“噫！你做这种不义之事，必定会遭到诛杀，我们都被你害了！”盗魁起身笑道：“好花不采，枉生人世，别互相责怪了。我让你也享一回乐，如何？”黄八子说：“你难道不记得我们团伙的规矩吗？采花必败，天道昭彰，我连离开的时间都没有，还跟着你学这种事，岂不是人？”说完，他走出房门，告知其他盗贼，辞别后打算离开。盗魁试图挽留他，但他不听，盗魁还拿出财物给他，他也不取，毅然决然地离开了。

    446

    至海昌，投县，自首丝肆被窃案。此案实非八子所窃，因与是夜同时，冒之以就轻也。羁禁日久，因无原赃，不能结。时有刑书某，以办漕亏欠收禁，八子深与结纳，问得若干银，可仍回刑房？书以四百金告，八子曰：“是不难，我为先生完此事。”书悦曰：“果能如是，我必有以报。”因此交更密。书家送饭食，恒与八子共之。一日晚膳，食炖羊腿，八子甘甚，问系自置乎？书曰：“买诸市肆。”八子曰：“何处有？”书曰：“熟食铺皆有。”八子骤欲饱啖，书曰：“门已封矣，明日嘱家人多备可也。”八子不能俟，取钱出院，但闻锁镣脱落声，禁子出视，只有刑具，而八子不知所矣。书嘱禁卒勿张皇，此人去必来，决无他虑也。未几，仍锁镣而入，肩羊腿，笑曰：“幸不辱命。”共切食之。

    Explanation

    446

    The protagonist traveled to Haichang and turned himself in to the county authorities, confessing to the theft case involving the silk shop. The case was not actually committed by the protagonist, but he falsely claimed responsibility to avoid a harsher sentence. He was imprisoned for a long time, but the case could not be resolved due to the lack of original evidence or stolen goods.

    At that time, there was a criminal clerk named Mr. X, who was imprisoned for failing to meet grain transport quotas. The protagonist deeply befriended Mr. X and asked how much silver would be needed to get back to the criminal records office. Mr. X replied that it would cost four hundred taels of silver. The protagonist said, "It's not difficult, I'll take care of this for you." Mr. X happily replied, "If you can do this, I will surely repay you." As a result, their relationship became even closer. Mr. X's family brought food, and they always shared it with the protagonist.

    One evening, they ate stewed lamb leg. The protagonist enjoyed it greatly and asked if it was homemade. Mr. X said, "I bought it from the market." The protagonist asked, "Where can I find it?" Mr. X replied, "It's available at all ready-to-eat food shops." The protagonist suddenly wanted to eat a lot and said, "The door is already closed, but I can ask my family to prepare more tomorrow." The protagonist could not wait and took money to leave the prison. He heard the sound of chains falling off, and the prison guard came out to check, but only saw the criminal tools, and the protagonist had disappeared. Mr. X instructed the prison guard not to panic, saying, "This person will surely return, there's no need to worry." Soon after, the protagonist returned with chains and a lamb leg, smiling, and said, "I didn't fail your expectations." They shared the meal together.

    447

    书曰：“君去来何其神速？”八子曰：“我今与先生交深，敢以实告。我因盗党采花必败，避重就轻，故认此案，惟原赃难得。先生出后，可托事主，不必深究，见赃即领，我事毕矣。不然，脱禁而去，亦有何难？所不肯为者，避前事耳。况我日在禁中，夜则游行自在，同盗多人，皆不及知。今已与先生言明，夜将往取财帛，以了先生事。”因复潜出，伏巨室屋上，系库书某家，方夜宴，为出脱一重囚，纳贿千金。八子俟夜深人静，窃取而回，次日，以四百金交书，完亏释放。值是案破，缘被奸之女羞忿自缢，事主上控，上峰奏参，奉旨严拿，盗魁与十六人全获，无一漏网者。供及黄八子，行文到县。刑书以八子是月日夜方在此行窃丝肆，岂能远往三百里外从盗乎？其为仇攀可知。启令移覆，彼处亦不深究。书为关说事主，以物之相似者，充原物领认，八子之案遂定，以其自首减等，城旦而已。

    Explanation

    447

    The clerk said, "How swift your departure and return!" Huang Baizi replied, "I have now become close to Master, and dare to speak frankly. I was part of a gang of thieves, and we were destined to fail. To avoid the heavy responsibility and seek the lighter one, I admitted to this case, but the original stolen goods are hard to recover. After Master leaves, you can entrust the matter to the complainant, and there is no need to investigate deeply; simply claim the goods upon seeing them, and my case will be resolved. Otherwise, escaping from confinement and leaving would be no difficulty at all. What I do not wish to do is to be implicated in past matters. Besides, I spend my days in confinement, but at night I roam freely. There are many thieves with me, and none of them know about my actions. I have now made it clear to Master that tonight I will go to retrieve the stolen goods to settle Master's matter." Huang Baizi then sneaked out again and hid on the roof of a grand house belonging to a family that maintained official records. At that time, the family was hosting a night banquet, and he accepted a bribe of a thousand gold coins to release a major prisoner. Huang Baizi waited until the night was deep and quiet, then stole the goods and returned. The next day, he handed over four hundred gold coins to the clerk to compensate for the loss and was released. Just as the case was about to be broken, a woman who had been violated by a thief was ashamed and angry, and hanged herself. The complainant filed a lawsuit, and the higher authorities reported the matter, and the imperial decree ordered a strict pursuit. The ringleader and sixteen others were all captured, none escaped. The confession mentioned Huang Baizi, and a document was issued to the county. The clerk argued that Huang Baizi was in the act of stealing silk at the time in question, how could he have traveled three hundred miles away to join the thieves? It is clear that this is an accusation by an enemy. The order was to transfer the case for review, and the other place did not investigate deeply either. The clerk interceded with the complainant, substituted similar items for the original stolen goods and claimed them, and Huang Baizi's case was settled. Because he confessed, his punishment was reduced, and he was sentenced to penal labor.

    448

    芗厈曰：好好色，人情之常，贤者不免。八子盗也，乃能力拒此关，非有大魄力不能。故其艺亦甚精，惜乎无有能荐之者，若得其人为将，国家何忧外侮哉？
    　　
    　　严氏
    　　
    　　金阊张子，率妻严氏，在都市开张杂货肆，即在后胡同作寓。其宅三上四厢，垣外客房三楹，寓内惟少妻幼子，雇一年轻嫠妇作伴而已。肆内一伙，年逾五旬，严作餐，其夫传食。伙悯主人之劳也，曰：“何不觅一仆，任其事乎？”张子首肯。其伙每早起，洒扫肆外，似已有人代为扫除洁净，留心察之，则一少年所为也。伙叩其意。少年曰：“我南人，落魄于此，日为人效奔走以糊口。夜寄庑下，君虽不知，我心不安，略效微劳。何足齿也？”伙悦其勤敏，荐与主人试用。其人不惜辛勤，不辞劳瘁，凡所作为，能先得主人意，内外皆爱怜之。但半载为仆，已暗与嫠妇通。

    Explanation

    448

    Xiang Yan said: It is natural for people to like beauty, even the virtuous cannot escape it. Eight sons who were thieves, yet they could resist this temptation strongly, which is not possible without great courage. Therefore, his skills are also very refined, but it is a pity that there is no one who can recommend him. If he could be appointed as a general, the country would have no worries about foreign aggression.

    Yan Shi

    Mr. Zhang from Jin Chang, with his wife Yan, opened a general store in the city and lived in a courtyard behind a lane. Their house had three upper rooms and four side rooms, with three guest rooms outside the wall. Inside the courtyard, there were only a young wife and young children, and they hired a young widowed woman as a companion. In the store, there was an assistant who was over fifty years old, and Yan prepared meals, while her husband served the food. The assistant felt sorry for the owner's hard work and said, "Why don't you find a servant to take care of these tasks?" Mr. Zhang agreed. Every morning, the assistant would clean the store, and it seemed as if someone had already swept it clean. Upon closer observation, it was done by a young man. The assistant asked him about his intentions. The young man replied, "I am from the south, and I am destitute here. I work for others to make a living. At night, I stay in the veranda, and although you may not know, I feel uneasy and offer a little help. What is there to boast about?" The assistant was pleased with his diligence and recommended him to the owner for a trial period. The young man spared no effort and did not complain about the hard work. Whatever he did, he always anticipated the owner's wishes, and everyone inside and outside the house loved and pitied him. However, after half a year as a servant, he had secretly become involved with the widowed woman.

    449

    一日，张子置货赴通州，嘱其仆住客房，守门户，惟严氏带十二岁子，与嫠妇同室。漏三下，子已沉睡，二妇尚作女工，忽有叩房门者。嫠妇拉关，仆操刀入室，突拥严氏曰：“汝必与我为欢，否则杀之。”严氏急呼嫠妇护救，嫠笑曰：“一家人，无伤也。主人不在，姑试尝之。”严氏知其同谋，料难力敌，转嗔作喜，谓仆曰：“汝果有情耶？此室有孺子在，恐被目见，事必泄漏，不能永欢矣。汝先入西厢俟我何如？”皆笑目：“真精细人也。”嫠先抱其衾枕而去，仆料其孤力难拒，亦携刀随往。严氏乃去大衣，紧束其身，潜入西厢。仆起拥抱曰：“果来耶？且解我渴。

    Explanation

    449

    One day, Zhang Zi went to Tongzhou with goods for sale and entrusted his servant to stay in a guest room, guarding the entrance, while only Yan's wife, with her twelve-year-old son, shared a room with a widow. At the third strike of the water clock, the child was already asleep, but the two women were still engaged in needlework. Suddenly, there was a knock on the door. The widow pulled the door shut, and the servant entered the room with a knife, suddenly grabbing Yan's wife and saying, "You must have sexual relations with me, otherwise I will kill you." Yan's wife urgently called out to the widow for help, and the widow laughed and said, "We are family, no harm will come. The master is not here, let's try it." Yan's wife realized they were in cahoots and knew it would be difficult to resist, so she changed her anger to joy and told the servant, "Do you really have feelings for me? There is a child in this room, fearing that he might see, the matter would surely be revealed, and we cannot enjoy this forever. Why don't you go to the west wing and wait for me?" They both laughed and nodded, "Truly a clever person." The widow first took her quilt and pillow and left, and the servant thought she would be alone and unable to resist, so he also took his knife and followed. Yan's wife then took off her outer garment and tightly bound her body, secretly entering the west wing. The servant got up and embraced her, saying, "You did come? And quench my thirst."

    450

    ”严氏笑却之曰：“我索爱汝精细，今何粗莽乃尔？此事也，我向不愿清醒相对，是以男子在家时，合有药酒，临时饮之，能坚战，身女交畅，不亦可乎？”嫠曰：“我亦知之，未得尝试，仰慕已久。”严氏曰：“今我三人同心，可尝试之。”乃指藏酒所，同嫠取一樽至，并携果食，共倾饮之，香美异常。严氏力劝二人尽量，且媚灌其仆，任其嘲戏抚摩，若甚甘心者。仆乃大醉，自脱上下衣，仰卧炕上，呢呢语曰：“速来试我具。

    Explanation

    450

    Yan Shi laughed and dismissed him, saying, "I long to love you for your refinement, but why are you so rough now? This matter, I previously did not want to be fully awake and present. Therefore, when the man is at home, there should be medicinal wine. Drink it temporarily, and it can help maintain strength, making the physical intimacy between a man and a woman smooth and enjoyable, wouldn't it be good?"

    The widow said, "I also know this, but I have not yet tried it; I have admired it for a long time."

    Yan Shi said, "Now we three are of one mind, so we can try it." She pointed to the place where the wine was stored, and together with the widow, they took a jar of wine. They also carried fruits and food, and together poured and drank it. The wine was unusually fragrant and delicious. Yan Shi strongly urged the two to drink to their heart's content and seductively poured the wine for the servant. She let him mock, play, and touch, as if he were very pleased. The servant became very drunk and undressed himself, laying on his back on the kang and murmuring, "Come quickly and test my equipment."

    451

    乘此酒兴，切勿再延。”严氏遣嫠睡去曰：“事毕，易汝何如？”嫠妇含笑去。严闭门，索其刀，犹在身畔，乃举刀力砍。已破其腹，仆跃起，复劈其脑，仰跌炕上，手掷足扬而毙。嫠闻声，披衣叩门，笑曰：“汝等如是猛战，邻舍闻之，不泄漏机关耶？”呼仆开门：“我欲作壁上观也。”严乃反臂隐刀身后，潜拔其关，嫠扑入，瞥见炕上血尸，正欲声喊，严从脑后力劈之，颠扑炕上，亦毙。乃解弃血衣，携灯出，反锁其门，回房与子同卧。

    Explanation

    451

    Taking advantage of this drunken mood, Yan commanded, "Do not delay further." He sent the widow to sleep and said, "When the matter is done, what will you do?" The widow smiled and left. Yan closed the door, took the knife, which was still at her side, and lifted it to strike with force. He had already cut open her abdomen; her body fell and leaped up, and he again struck her head. She fell backward onto the bed, her hands flung and feet raised, and she died. The widow heard the noise, put on her clothes, and knocked on the door, smiling and saying, "You all are fighting so fiercely; won't the neighbors hear and reveal the secret?" She called out for the door to be opened, saying, "I want to watch from the sidelines." Yan turned his arm and hid the knife behind his back, secretly unlocking the door. The widow rushed in and caught a glimpse of a bloody corpse on the bed. Just as she was about to shout, Yan struck her from behind, and she fell onto the bed, dying as well. He undressed and discarded the bloody clothes, carried a lamp out, locked the door, and returned to the room to sleep with his child.

    452

    次早，倩邻妇呼其伙来，告之曰：“昨夜仆与妇同逃，幸未窃物去，汝速遣人唤主人归，商其事，合报官否？”伙雇人唤张子回，询其事，严含糊答对。夜深人静，方以杀奸事白之，张子愕然。严曰：“君必首之，有罪妾任。若潜隐其事，日久必破，君亦不得辞其咎矣。”张子思其言，遂首诸司坊，官来验讯，严氏侃侃直陈，奏交秋部大司寇鞠之。严氏理直气壮，坚执不挠，乃释严氏夫妇而瘗二尸。案定后，乡人之宦游京师者，咸羡严之胆智，公悬额以旌之。

    Explanation

    452

    Early the next morning, Yan hired a neighbor woman to call her partner and told him, "Last night, the servant and the woman fled together, fortunately without stealing anything. You should quickly send someone to call the master back and discuss the matter—whether to report it to the authorities." The partner hired someone to call Zhang back, who inquired about the matter. Yan replied vaguely. Late at night, when people were quiet, Yan finally revealed the matter of killing the villain to Zhang, who was startled. Yan said, "You must report it; if there is a crime, I will take responsibility. If you conceal the matter, it will eventually be exposed, and you will also not be able to avoid blame." Zhang considered Yan's words and then reported the matter to the local authorities. The officials came to investigate, and Yan spoke frankly and directly, submitting the case to the Autumn Department and the Great Judge for judgment. Yan was reasonable and confident, firmly holding his position without wavering. They then released Yan and his wife and buried the two bodies. After the case was settled, people from the village who were officials in the capital all admired Yan's courage and wisdom, publicly hanging a plaque to honor him.

    453

    芗厈曰：智明者，胆必壮。严氏初心，亦犹恒人，未尝不呼嫠妇共拒。迨嫠妇同谋，知难免矣，乃速转关作荡妇态，诱其入我彀中。刀可得，仇可报也。然亦有天幸，若仆必欲先污之而后饮，我知仆之命，必不能脱其手。而严氏不得为完人，饮恨无穷矣。或曰：“必不被污也，仆视严氏犹几上肉，断不能脱我口，何妨姑且作乐？况以两情交畅之言动之，有不垂涎愿从者乎？从之，则必酩酊，二人皆自取杀身也。”我愿世之君子室无严氏，断不可使少妇壮仆在一处，即有严氏，多一事，不如少一事也。

    Explanation

    453

    Xiang Yan said: A wise and perceptive person must have a bold heart. Yan's initial intention was also like that of an ordinary person, and she never hesitated to call upon widows to resist. When the widows conspired together and she knew she could not avoid it, she quickly changed her tactics and acted in a promiscuous manner, luring the servant into her trap. The knife could be obtained, and the revenge could be avenged. However, there is also divine favor; if the servant must defile her first and then drink, I know his fate will not allow him to escape her grasp. And Yan cannot remain a virtuous woman, her hatred will be endless. Some say, "He will certainly not be defiled; I see Yan as meat on a platter, I cannot be separated from her mouth, why not indulge in pleasure for a while? Moreover, with words that stir mutual passion, who would not be tempted and willing to follow? If he follows, he will surely be drunk, and both will bring about their own destruction." I wish that the gentlemen of the world would have no Yan in their homes, and absolutely not allow a young wife and a strong servant to be in the same place, even if there is a Yan, better to have one less thing to deal with.

    454

    悟知子
    　　
    　　悟知子，不知何许人。初业儒，不得功名，弃而学贾，不得财帛，偃蹇半生，一无成就，幸无妻子，孑然游荡。遇江湖卜者，学推算五行法，昕宵研精，寒暑不辍者三年，恍然大悟曰：“道在是矣。”随立肆于市，垂帘卖卜。诣之者，所决事皆当，故得钱无虚日。正为人作卦，左右环观，猝有一道人来，著折角巾，褴襶不堪。坐椅上，椅顿折，人皆笑之。道人抱歉将行，悟知子婉留之。

    Explanation

    454

    Enlightened Child

    Enlightened Child, whose origins are unknown. Initially engaged in Confucian studies, he did not achieve fame or success. He gave up and learned commerce but did not acquire wealth. Struggling for half a life, he achieved nothing. Fortunately, he had no wife or children and wandered alone. He encountered a fortune-teller from the rivers and lakes and learned the method of calculating the Five Elements. Studying diligently day and night, without interruption through cold and heat, for three years, he suddenly had a great realization and said, "The truth is here." Then he set up a stall in the market and sold fortunes behind a curtain. Those who came to him found that all the matters he decided were correct, so he earned money every day without fail. While he was casting a hexagram for someone, surrounded by onlookers, suddenly a Taoist arrived, wearing a hat with a broken corner and ragged, worn-out clothing. He sat on a chair, which suddenly broke, and everyone laughed at him. The Taoist apologized and was about to leave, but Enlightened Child gently persuaded him to stay.

    455

    卜完人散，悟知子诣道人膝前，跪称仙师求教，那道人曰：“予游方之士耳，何所见而仙予？”悟知子曰：“弟子早推定数，请观坐坏之椅背。”道人观之，上书“某年月日，此椅为仙人坐折”。道人曰：“孺子可教哉！惜费聪明于无益之地。子既仙我，盍言尔志？”曰：“求赐仙丹，度脱尘凡，相随门下。”道人笑曰：“子犹俗见，误甚。子先业儒，曾作文投明师。道家丹药，犹儒家笔墨，子能吞墨举笔即成名家乎？”曰：“不能。”“能使师入尔心腹，代为著作乎？”曰：“不能。”“能求师一日间诲汝作通人乎？”曰：“不能。”“我知汝不能也。仙道犹儒道，须专心致志，历阶而进，断无饵一粒丹即飞升天上者，皆好事者托词以误世也。

    Explanation

    455

    After the divination and the people had dispersed, Wuzhi approached the Daoist master, knelt before him, and addressed him as "Immortal Teacher" to seek instruction.
