The Daoist master replied, "I am merely a wandering scholar. What have you seen that leads you to call me 'immortal'?" Wuzhi responded, "Your disciple has long foreseen your destiny. Please examine the back of the broken chair." The Daoist master looked and saw inscribed, "On a certain day in a certain month, this chair was broken by an immortal." He said, "The child is teachable! It is a pity to waste intelligence on something unproductive. Since you see me as an immortal, why don't you express your aspirations?" Wuzhi said, "I request the gift of elixir to transcend the mundane world and follow you under your guidance." The Daoist master smiled and said, "You still hold common views, which is a great mistake. You first studied Confucianism and once wrote essays to present to a wise teacher. Taoist elixirs are like Confucian writing tools. Can you swallow ink and pick up a pen to instantly become a master?" "No," Wuzhi replied. "Can you make the teacher enter your innermost being and write on your behalf?" "No," he said. "Can you ask the teacher to instruct you in one day to become a learned person?" "No," he answered. "I know you cannot. The way of immortality is like the way of Confucianism. One must concentrate and advance step by step. There is no such thing as eating a single pill and instantly ascending to heaven. All are false claims made by those who wish to deceive the world."

    456

    子善悟，我先示子丹头。”乃从袖中出一轴展之，中间大书“诚”字，上书“真”字，下书“妄”字，右书“实”字，左书“幻”字，曰：“此即大丹原委，成始成终，尽在于是。”悟知子曰：“弟子愚昧，敬请详示。”道人曰：“上求其真，下劈其妄，右依即实，左依即幻。如为人之道，孝悌忠信，礼义廉耻，八者无愧，是即诚也。天岂有弃此八字之仙乎？故入道之始，曰真人，言真能克尽人道也。一有不然，即流于妄。纲常既不明，则人且不成，何有仙乎？能右此八字，敬慎勿失即实，实则德明，无物不明矣。故曰‘至诚前知’。

    Explanation

    456

    Since you are wise, I will first show you the essence of the true elixir. He then took out a scroll from his sleeve and unfolded it. In the middle, it was written in large characters, "诚" (sincerity), above it "真" (true), below it "妄" (false), on the right "实" (real), and on the left "幻" (illusory). He said, "This is the essence of the great elixir, the beginning and end of it, all contained here." The wise one asked, "I am ignorant, I respectfully request a detailed explanation." The Daoist said, "Seek the true above, cut through the false below, follow the right to find reality, follow the left to find illusion. As for the way of being human, filial piety, fraternal respect, loyalty, trustworthiness, propriety, righteousness, integrity, and shame—these eight without fault, this is sincerity. Could heaven abandon those who embody these eight virtues? Therefore, at the beginning of entering the path, one is called a True Person, meaning that the true can fully fulfill the human way. If there is any deviation, one will fall into falsehood. If the fundamental principles are not clear, then one cannot become a person, how can one become a true person? If one follows these eight virtues to the right, with reverence and caution, one will not lose them, and thus achieve reality; reality leads to clarity of virtue, and nothing will remain unclear. Perfect sincerity has foreknowledge."

    457

    左此八字即幻，幻则狂妄，荒诞不经矣。故曰‘左道惑众’。子能一心守真实，去幻妄，诚在其中，自然入定，定则静，静则精气神三宝之先天自见，见而团聚之勿散，即是内丹。道在是矣。”悟知子曰：“师所云理学常谈也。道尽于此乎？”道人曰：“儒释道三教同源，其流则分。儒在明明德，释在渡罗密，道在清静头，彼圣佛无不从此入门也。即如子之神卜，亦从庸流所论五行入手，能劈去浮游而存精实，非由一诚得之乎？大小一理，子何信其小者而略其大者乎？”悟知子曰：“唯。”即失道人所在。三年后，悟知子亦不知去向。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：纯阳为仙，纯阴为鬼，人在阴阳之中，故可鬼可仙。惟刻求诚明，自有四通八达之日。任意荒怠，即中七情六欲之迷。道人所云，堂堂之阵，正正之旗也，明者自知。知而不行，行而不专，是自趋于鬼道也。

    Explanation

    457

    Left these eight characters are illusions; illusions are madness and absurdity, far-fetched and unreasonable. Therefore, it is called 'left path deceiving the masses.' If you can focus your mind on truth, discard illusions, sincerity will be within you, and naturally you will enter meditation. Meditation leads to stillness, stillness leads to the natural appearance of the three treasures of essence, energy, and spirit. Seeing and gathering them without dispersing is the inner elixir. The way is here. Wu Zhi Zi said, "What the teacher says is common talk in rational learning. Is the way exhausted here?" The Daoist said, "Confucianism, Buddhism, and Taoism have the same origin, but their streams diverge. Confucianism aims to illuminate virtue, Buddhism aims to cross the sea of suffering, Taoism aims to achieve purity and tranquility. Those saints and Buddhas all enter from this gate. Just as your divine divination also starts from the common understanding of the Five Elements. Being able to cut through the superficial and retain the substantial, is this not achieved through sincere effort? Great and small are one principle, why do you believe in the small and neglect the great?" Wu Zhi Zi said, "Indeed." Then he lost the place where the Daoist was. Three years later, Wu Zhi Zi also disappeared without a trace.

    Xiang Yan said: Pure yang becomes a deity, pure yin becomes a ghost. People are in the middle of yin and yang, so they can become either ghosts or deities. Only by constantly seeking sincerity and clarity, one will have the day of being all-round and open. If one acts carelessly and indulges in idleness, one will be lost in the seven emotions and six desires. What the Daoist said is a grand and proper array and a proper flag. The wise will know. Knowing but not acting, acting but not with dedication, is self-guiding to the path of ghosts.

    458

    潘善人
    　　
    　　潘翁，粤东香山人，谈者忘其名字，生在前明中叶，家赀千万，席丰履厚，有善必为，如刷印经文、创修庙宇、造桥铺路、掩骼埋胔，以及赠亲朋、济邻里，犹不足以尽其心。乃日食千百人，不使邑有乞丐，当博施济众时，有媪旦暮必至，索食饭之，索饮茗之，无倦色，如是者久，无如旱涝之年，闻善人之名者，远近就哺，日以万计。翁虽富，力渐不支，然不肯中止，犹去质库，卖田宅为之。迨时和年丰，灾黎散而善人贫矣，前媪尚日诣之。翁犹自减口粮以为之食，始缚不怠。媪不自安，谓翁曰：“我孤独无归之嫠也，今汝业已败，我何忍累汝？曷以住宅施我作大士阁，我愿为优婆夷，自主香火，藉以常得温饱，无求于汝矣。”人旨怒叱之，翁曰：“诺。”即谋迁妻孥，举宅以施。

    Explanation

    458

    Mr. Pan, a man of great virtue

    Mr. Pan, a resident of Xiangshan in Guangdong, is so revered that his name has been forgotten by those who speak of him. He was born during the middle period of the Ming Dynasty, and his family was extremely wealthy, enjoying a life of abundance. Whenever there was an opportunity to do good, he would seize it, such as printing scriptures, building and repairing temples, constructing bridges and roads, and burying the dead. He also gave to relatives and friends and helped neighbors, yet these acts were still not enough to fully express his benevolence. He fed thousands of people daily, ensuring that no beggars were found in the town. During times when he was widely distributing his charity, an old woman would come to him every morning and evening, asking for food and drink, never showing any signs of fatigue. This continued for a long time, but during years of drought and flood, when people heard of Mr. Pan's name, they came from far and wide to seek his aid, numbering in the tens of thousands each day. Despite his wealth, Mr. Pan's resources gradually became insufficient, but he refused to stop his charitable efforts. He pawned his property and sold his land and houses to continue helping others. When the weather improved and the harvest was bountiful, the disaster-stricken people dispersed, and Mr. Pan became poor. The old woman still came to him daily. Mr. Pan continued to reduce his own food rations to feed her, and he never grew tired of it. The old woman felt uneasy and said to Mr. Pan, "I am a widow, alone and without a home. Now that your fortunes have declined, how can I bear to burden you? Why don't you give me your house to build a Bodhisattva pavilion? I wish to become a female lay devotee, manage the incense and candles, and thus always have warmth and food, without needing anything from you." Mr. Pan was angry and scolded her, but he said, "Yes." He then planned to move his wife and children and donated the entire house.

    459

    媪曰：“汝舍去，将何所依？得无怨我乎？”翁曰：“否。北郭外有山地数顷，因历年旱涝，寸草不生，无人肯售去，结茅庐亦足栖止，何怨之有？”媪曰：“此地吾前见之，有大风水在，汝因历年为善，未及葬汝父母，今既已迁此，我当为卜宅。其山之阳，枯木之下，天生石穴一区，直佳城也，窀穸于此，不但旧业可复，将亿万斯年吃著不尽。汝信之乎？”翁曰：“谨受救。

    Explanation

    459

    The old woman said, "If you leave, where will you go? Will you not blame me?" The old man replied, "No. Outside the northern city wall, there is a piece of land with several acres, but because of long-term droughts and floods, not a blade of grass grows, and no one is willing to sell it. Building a thatched hut is enough for shelter. What have I to blame you for?" The old woman said, "I saw this place before. There is a great geomantic feature here. You have done good deeds over the years, but you did not get to bury your parents. Now that you have moved here, I will choose a place for you to settle. On the sunny side of the mountain, under a dead tree, there is a naturally formed stone cave, which is a good place for a tomb. Burying here, not only can your old property be restored, it will last for countless generations without end. Do you believe me?" The old man replied, "I will follow your advice."

    460

    ”媪执引送之，指牛眠穴，坎三离向，以罗浮为案，以大海为荫，曰：“此凡夫之所见不及者也，且使庐墓而居。”乃出异果数斗，给之曰：“以此遍撒荒田，俟其生长勿伐，三年乃成，三年后，必有蛇虺守此，汝当别迁。欲得此异物者，惟五月五日，可使健男子身涂雄黄，醉饱来伐取，行将独擅天下之妙业矣。”翁曰：“将为何物？必先教之。”媪笑曰：“汝将来观其形状，似何物即创为何物，何必先知？但我所与者，菩提果也，勿以予人。纵予人，无益。”媪乃去。翁于是安庐种果，半载方休。入城视己旧居，已成观音堂，香火甚盛，有尼住持，媪已不知去向矣。

    Explanation

    460

    The elderly woman led him to a specific burial site, pointing out a place where a cow had lain down, with the north-south orientation and the south-facing direction. She explained that Mount Luofu served as a focal point and the sea provided protective shade, saying, "This is a place that ordinary people cannot see, and you should live here by the tomb." She then gave him several baskets of unusual fruits, instructing him, "Scatter these fruits across the barren fields, and do not cut them down until they grow. After three years, they will be ready. Three years later, there will certainly be a snake guarding this place, and you should move elsewhere. To obtain this extraordinary object, only on the fifth day of the fifth month can you send a strong man to coat his body with realgar, drink and eat to his fill, and then cut it down. You will then achieve a marvelous accomplishment that is unique in the world." The old man asked, "What is this object? I must be taught about it first." The elderly woman smiled and said, "When you see its form, create whatever it resembles. Why must you know it beforehand? But what I am giving you is the fruit of enlightenment. Do not give it to others. Even if you give it to others, it will be of no benefit." The elderly woman then left. The old man built a house and planted the fruits, working for half a year before resting. When he returned to the city and looked at his old home, it had already become a Guanyin Temple, with a thriving incense offering and a nun in charge. The elderly woman was nowhere to be found.

    461

    翁归不数月，其物萌茁，形似芭蕉，遍山皆是。爱护之，勿敢伐。历三年，叶形成扇，翁乃剥其粗者，贱售之。人皆争市，一夏得数万钱。时已有巨蛇出没，毒气射人，翁不能存身，仍迁城市。次年，如媪嘱，以重价雇壮丁，于五月一日始，人各执斧持钻，腰带雄黄酒，绳索入山，砍其细者，捆载而出。五日毕事，拣其细心完善者，以玳瑁象牙作柄，绵绣其边，一柄值银数钱。商贩之徒，争来售置，其门如市，后遂广行宇内，自贵官长者、文人墨士及闺阃中，几至人执一柄，以为雅玩。以是复业，传至子孙，至今三百余年。家益富，他人欲窃效之，俱不能，盖蕉为虺蛇所守，平日入山者，受毒即毙，故无敢窃。亲友乞其种，亦未尝吝惜，但树他处，变为美人蕉，结花娇红可爱，叶小而脆，不堪作扇，然后知媪即观自在菩萨，传翁绝业，为天下后世为善者示劝。

    Explanation

    461

    Upon returning home, the old man found that within a few months, the plant had sprouted and grown vigorously, resembling a banana tree, covering the entire mountain. He took great care to protect the plants and refrained from cutting them down. After three years, the leaves grew large enough to be shaped into fans. The old man then harvested the larger leaves and sold them at a low price. People eagerly purchased the fans, and he earned tens of thousands of coins in one summer. However, large snakes began to appear, and their poisonous breath was lethal to anyone who ventured into the area. Unable to survive, the old man relocated to the city.

    The following year, he followed the old woman's instructions and hired strong men at a high price to begin cutting the plants on the first day of May. Each worker carried an axe, a drill, a belt of wine, and a rope, and they entered the mountain to cut the smaller plants, bundling them up and carrying them out. The task was completed in five days. They selected the finest and most carefully crafted fans, using tortoiseshell and ivory for the handles and embroidering the edges. Each fan was worth several silver coins. Merchants and traders competed to purchase the fans, and the old man's home became as busy as a market. The fans soon spread throughout the land, becoming popular among high-ranking officials, scholars, and even women in the household, to the point where almost everyone owned one as a refined pastime.

    Through this venture, the old man resumed his business, passing it down to his descendants, and it has continued for over three hundred years. His family grew wealthy, and others tried to imitate his success but failed. The reason was that the banana plants were guarded by venomous snakes, and anyone who ventured into the mountain would be poisoned and killed, so no one dared to steal the secret. Friends and relatives who asked for seeds were not denied, but when the plants were grown elsewhere, they became beauty bananas, producing small, delicate, and brittle leaves that were unsuitable for making fans. It was then that the old man realized the old woman was a manifestation of Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion, who had guided him to revive his lost business and demonstrated the importance of doing good deeds for the benefit of future generations.

    462

    芗厈曰：天之报施善人也，大善则大报之，小善则小报之。如潘翁之博施济众，直欲掠美唐虞，天乃报以万年不拔之基，以是知生物不测，造化无穷。
    　　
    　　身毒国
    　　
    　　无无子者，不知何许人，亦不详其姓氏。其为人也，愤世嫉俗，好作汗漫游。一日，附洋船随风飘至一国，见其人凹目深鼻，皆衣白，熙熙浩浩，无争竞心。见中国客到，争饭之。米大而香洁，无肉食，并无菜蔬。虽言语不通，而貌甚恭敬。走报一红衣老者来，自言曾出使中华数次，能华言，邀无无子至家曰：“先生欲知我国风土人情乎？请译而道之。我国分男女两部，王曰元首，男部统之。其下十二司，分三等。上司三，曰耳目司、口鼻司、喉舌司。中司四，曰左肱司、右肱司、心腹司、臂指司。下司五：曰前阳司、后阴司、左股司、右股司、皮毛司。

    Explanation

    462

    Xiang Yan said: Heaven rewards good people: great good is rewarded with great recompense, and small good with small recompense. Take Pan Gong, for instance, who extensively helped the masses, almost rivaling the deeds of Tang and Yu. Heaven rewarded him with a foundation that would last for millennia, thus proving that the workings of nature are unpredictable and creation is boundless.

    The Country of Shendu
    There was a man named Wu Wu Zi, whose origins and surname are unknown. He was a person who was furious against the world and disgusted with custom, fond of wandering aimlessly. One day, he boarded a foreign ship and was carried by the wind to a country. He saw that the people had sunken eyes and deep noses, all dressed in white, bustling and friendly, with no competitive spirit. Upon seeing Chinese guests, they competed to feed them. The rice was large, fragrant, and clean, with no meat or vegetables. Although they could not understand each other's language, they were very respectful. A red-clothed elderly man came running to report, saying that he had been sent to China several times and could speak Chinese. He invited Wu Wu Zi to his home and said, "Sir, do you want to know about our country's customs and people? Please allow me to translate and tell you. Our country is divided into male and female departments, with the king being the head of state, governed by the male department. Below the king, there are twelve departments, divided into three ranks. The upper departments are three: the Ear and Eye Department, the Mouth and Nose Department, and the Throat and Tongue Department. The middle departments are four: the Left Arm Department, the Right Arm Department, the Heart and Abdomen Department, and the Arm and Finger Department. The lower departments are five: the Front Yang Department, the Rear Yin Department, the Left Leg Department, the Right Leg Department, and the Skin and Hair Department."

    463

    十二司戴元首，合而为身毒国，毒译督，言一司总督诸司也。耳目口鼻喉舌最贵，比中华之宰相尚书卿贰，左右肱比都督将军，心腹比抚按，臂指比提镇。前阳主刑名，后阴主钱谷，比中华之有司。左右股比汛弁，皮毛司比佐贰杂职。自王以下，皆布衣，以色分品级。王衣黊，上司衣朱，中司衣蓝，下司衣青，民衣白。田地无主，亦无旷土，产香谷，大于中华之稻米，一岁三收。至春夏秋三时，人之有力者，不拘何地，尽量布种。自王以下，并耕而食，无分彼此。诸司督耕，能多得谷者，司举奏王，以下司爵之，有递迁至朱衣者。第非考较出身，仅虚职荣之耳。怠情者，发穷黎岛，日给一粥终其身。

    Explanation

    463

    The Twelve Departments and the Head Form the Shendu Kingdom

    The twelve departments, each with its own responsibilities, together form the Shendu Kingdom, a metaphorical state where "Shendu" is a transliteration of "India." The term "Shendu" here refers to a metaphorical state, and "毒译督" is a transliteration or phonetic rendering of a word meaning "supervise" or "oversee." This phrase explains that one department oversees all others, using the metaphor of a head overseeing the body.

    The most important organs—ear, eye, mouth, nose, throat, and tongue—are compared to high-ranking officials in the Chinese bureaucracy, such as the Chancellor, the Secretaries, and the Assistants. The arms are compared to generals, the heart and abdomen to provincial governors and censors, and the arm and finger to military commanders. The front (yang) is compared to the administration of justice, and the back (yin) to the management of finances and grain. The legs are compared to lower-ranking military officers, and the skin and hair to minor officials.

    In this society, everyone, including the king, wears plain clothes, with social status indicated by color. The king wears black, high-ranking officials wear red, middle-ranking officials wear blue, low-ranking officials wear green, and commoners wear white. There is no private ownership of land, and no idle land. The land produces a highly productive and aromatic crop, greater than rice in China, with three harvests per year. During the spring, summer, and autumn seasons, those who are physically capable, regardless of their location, plant seeds extensively. From the king down, everyone works the land and eats from it, without distinction.

    The officials oversee the farming, and those who produce the most grain are nominated by their department to the king, and are promoted in rank, with some even advancing to the level of red-robed officials. However, these promotions are not based on merit but are honorary. Those who are lazy are sent to the remote island of Qiongli, where they are given only one bowl of porridge per day for the rest of their lives.

    464

    年丰，则家藏一岁之用，余尽归元首，分贮十二司。若歉年，十二司积储，仍散于民，故无饥馑，亦无贫富。上下皆食谷，不知有菜，何来荤腥？虽产有金银珠玉宝贝，不足贵也，不过为布地饰屋之需，狁中华之砖瓦朱粉。所贵者米布，如中华之银钱，凡动用之物，皆以此交易。我王相传为净饭王分枝八十四世裔孙，其长枝，即阿弥陀佛，代传一那摩佛，即中华所称喇嘛大班禅活佛也，能参四禅八定。诸司选民之后，俊秀者送入学道，或三四年，或五六年，由那摩佛考较道行之浅深，送王补诸司缺，故皆慈祥，为国为民。后曰元后，女官统之，凡女皆习工作。

    Explanation

    464

    In years of abundant harvest, households store enough food for an entire year, and any surplus is returned to the central authority, to be distributed among the twelve administrative departments. In years of poor harvest, the stored resources in the twelve departments are redistributed to the people, thus there is no famine, nor extreme poverty or wealth. Both the upper and lower classes eat grain, and there is no knowledge of vegetables or meat, so where would such things come from? Although precious metals and jewels are produced, they are not valued, and are used only for decorating the ground and houses, akin to Chinese bricks, tiles, and paint. The valuable items are rice and cloth, comparable to Chinese silver coins, and all goods are traded using these items. Our king is said to be the 84th descendant of King Suddhodana, and the main branch of the lineage is the Buddha Amitabha, who passes down the lineage of the Buddha Namuo, known as the Dalai Lama in China, who can enter the four meditative absorptions and eight trances. After the various departments select the people, the talented ones are sent to study the path, for a period of three to six years, and are examined by the Dalai Lama for the depth of their spiritual practice. Those who pass are sent to fill positions in the various departments, and thus they are all kind and serve the country and the people. The queen is called the "Yuan Hou" and is governed by female officials, and all women learn to work.

    465

    自后以下，俱能种棉织布，成衣以易谷食。凡男年二十以上思妻者，投前阳司送女部，后配与年相若者一人，三宿而去。若再过之，则又另配人矣。生女则留，生子，抚养至五六岁时，发男部，任人子之，只许人领一子。有夭殇者，许再，故民无氏族，亦无绝后之人。凡男女年至六十，不耕织，所颁女子，力作养之，亦无委弃者。死则公殓以大瓮，亦无棺椁。邻邦皆尚繁华，以我国为鄙陋，故无侵犯，我国只求内治，亦不征伐。故自净饭王以来，数千年不易姓，如是而已，无他长也。”无无子大悦曰：“此极乐世界，我愿受一廛而为氓。”老者笑曰：“我国法，不留外人。子回中华，卧而思之自得，何必留此？”乃怅伥而返。

    Explanation

    465

    From the lowest to the highest, everyone is capable of growing cotton, weaving cloth, making clothes, and trading them for grain and food. Any man over twenty who wishes to marry goes to the designated office for women, is matched with a woman of similar age, and spends three nights together before parting. If he returns, he is matched with another woman. Girls are kept, and boys are raised until they are five or six years old, then sent to the designated office for men, where they are adopted by others, with the limit of one child per adopter. If a child dies, another can be adopted, so there are no surnames, and no one is left without an heir. Any man or woman who reaches sixty is exempt from farming and weaving, and is supported by the community through the labor of designated women. The deceased are publicly buried in large jars, with no coffins. Neighboring countries, which value sophistication, see our country as simple, so they do not invade us. Our country focuses solely on internal governance and does not engage in warfare. Since King Shuddhodana, our country has remained unchanged for thousands of years, with no notable achievements beyond this. "无无子" was delighted and said, "This is a paradise! I wish to accept a small plot of land and become a commoner here." The elderly person smiled and said, "Our country's law does not allow outsiders. Return to China, and reflect on what you have seen. Why stay here?" And so, he left with a sense of disappointment.

    466

    芗厈曰：身毒国十司皆妥，惟前阳、后阴二司尚可议。以前阳能刚能柔，能进能退，故主刑名耶？然少者浮躁，老者疲软，奈何？至后阴只出不入，何可司钱谷耶？或曰：“后世之能者，出糟粕而纳精华，君其未知之耶？”予笑应曰：“唯。”

    Explanation

    466

    Xiang Yan said: In the Shendu state, all ten departments are in order, except for the Qianyang and Houyin departments, which are still open to discussion. The Qianyang department is able to be strong and flexible, able to advance and retreat, so it is in charge of criminal matters? However, the younger ones are impulsive and restless, and the older ones are weak and tired, what to do about it? As for the Houyin department, it only goes out but does not come in, how can it be in charge of finances and grain? Someone said, "Those who are capable in later generations, discard the dross and retain the essence, you do not know this, do you?" I smiled and replied, "Yes."

    467

    第四卷
    　　
    　　某制军夫人
    　　
    　　某制军由科甲出身，好士爱才，门下皆一时誉髦也。制军风雅甚，如夫人甚众，琴棋诗画各擅一长。咸使名士传之。有某孝廉者，年逾弱冠，品貌俊逸，诗如其人。家屡空，投制军谋食。时制军新得宠姬某氏，诗笔清新，与孝廉相类，遂使姬作女学生。传递诗词，两情甚洽，有文君之约，而未得其便。值制军寿诞，内外优剧百戏并作，乃乘喧杂时，姬订孝廉卷其细软，逾花圃后垣同逸。次日，仆妇书童报二人夜不归室之事，制军大怒，传心腹武弁入内室，授之剑而谕之故曰：“迅往南追取二人首。认姬作汝妻，被孝廉奸拐，故追而斩之。投所在官司自首，汝既无罪，且不次超迁汝职。”

    Explanation

    467

    Chapter 4
    The Lady of a Certain Military Governor

    A certain military governor, who rose through the imperial examination system, was fond of scholars and talented individuals. His retinue consisted of the most distinguished and talented people of the time. The military governor was very cultured, and he had many concubines, each skilled in one of the four arts: music, chess, poetry, and painting. All of these were recorded by the literati. There was a certain scholar-official, over the age of twenty, who was handsome and talented, and his poetry reflected his character. His family was often in financial difficulty, so he sought support from the military governor. At the time, the military governor had recently acquired a favored concubine, whose poetry was fresh and clear, and who was similar to the scholar-official. He arranged for her to act as a female student. They exchanged poems, and their feelings for each other were very harmonious, and they had a romantic agreement like that of Zhuo Wenjun and Sima Xiangru, but they had not yet found the right opportunity. On the occasion of the military governor’s birthday, there were performances and entertainments inside and outside the compound. Taking advantage of the commotion, the concubine arranged with the scholar-official to pack their belongings and escape together by jumping over the rear wall of the flower garden. The next day, the servants and pages reported that the two had not returned to their rooms at night. The military governor became furious and summoned a trusted military officer into his inner chamber. He handed the officer a sword and explained the situation, saying, “Swiftly go south to pursue and take the heads of the two people. Acknowledge the concubine as your wife, who was kidnapped by the scholar-official, so pursue and kill them. Surrender to the local authorities and confess. You will be free of blame, and I will promote you beyond your current rank.”

    468

    弁领命将行，夫人遽出喝止。乃谏制军曰：“机事不密则害成。此等事想已飏播省垣矣。引盗入室，斩之非义。一顶绿头巾终脱不去，徒然掩耳盗铃，不贻笑士林耶？且孝廉才名甚著，爱之者不仅夫君一人。使士林抱不平，察实而发其覆，贻害无穷矣。夫君聪明阔达，岂不见及此？妾恐一时忿怒昧之耳。”制军不觉怒息，曰：“为之奈何？”夫人曰：“是不难，昔杨越、裴晋二公，皆人杰也。盗其侍妾者，不但不追，间有厚赠者。曷不仿而为之？请作札遣家人赠以行资，则孝廉有不感愧图报者乎？且以美姬赠名士，古人恒为之。不但士林不笑，且宽大之誉遍环区矣。孰得孰失，君试思之。”

    Explanation

    468

    The Military Commander was about to set out on a mission, but his wife hurriedly came out and shouted to stop him. She then advised the General: "If a secret is not kept confidential, it will lead to disaster. I suspect that this matter has already spread widely among the high officials. Bringing a thief into the house and killing him would be unjust. A green hat will never be removed, and it is merely self-deception to cover one's ears while stealing a bell; will you not be ridiculed by the scholarly community? Moreover, the Scholar-Official's talent and reputation are very prominent, and those who love him are not just my husband alone. If the scholarly community feels aggrieved and investigates to expose the truth, it will cause endless harm. My husband is wise and broad-minded; could he not see this? I fear that a moment of anger has clouded his judgment." The General's anger subsided, and he said, "What should I do?" The wife replied, "It is not difficult. In the past, Yang Yue and Pei Jin, two outstanding individuals, did not pursue those who stole their concubines, and sometimes even gave them generous gifts. Why not follow their example? Please write a letter and send family members to give him travel funds. Will the Scholar-Official not feel ashamed and seek to repay such kindness? And to give a beautiful concubine to a famous scholar is something that ancient people often did. Not only will the scholarly community not laugh, but the reputation for being magnanimous will spread throughout the region. What is gained and what is lost, you should think about it."

    469

    制军大悦曰：“非夫人之论，我几大误。”即作书以四百金遣奴子追赠，其书略曰：“仆年逾花甲，所以室有少女者，非自好也。诚以聪明俊秀之产，造物亦几费经营，使之误配莽夫，殊深惋惜，是以罗而致之，且随其姿质教成学术以配君子，岂非天下大快事耶？故某女遣随足下，仆早有作合之心。所不预言者，缘此女学未大成，欲其精进而后成之。不意巫山神女，竞自投楚王矣。其识见亦属非凡。仆当顺其情，以遂好逑佳话。但不别而行，妙手空空，腰缠何藉？未免累及君子。故遣下走馈赆，伏冀哂存。所有足下行囊，与某女衣饰，俱捡点附上。”

    Explanation

    469

    The general was greatly pleased and said, “Had it not been for your lady’s words, I would have made a grave mistake.” Immediately, he composed a letter and sent a servant with four hundred taels of silver to deliver it in pursuit. The letter read approximately as follows: “I am past the age of sixty, and yet I have a young daughter in my household—not out of personal desire, but because I believe that the clever and beautiful child, whom Heaven itself has labored to create, should not be carelessly matched with a coarse and unrefined man. This would be deeply regrettable. Therefore, I have gathered her and brought her to myself, and I intend to educate her in scholarship according to her natural talents, so that she may be a suitable match for a gentleman. Is this not a great joy for the world? Thus, I have sent this girl to be with you, for I have long harbored the intention of uniting us. I did not speak of it before because this girl’s learning has not yet reached full maturity, and I wished for her to advance further before completing her education. I did not expect, however, that the goddess of Mount Wu would herself have cast herself into the arms of King Chu. Her insight and understanding are indeed extraordinary. I shall follow her feelings and fulfill this tale of a good match. Yet if she departs without a farewell, empty-handed, what use is the money tied around one’s waist? It would only be a burden upon the gentleman. Hence, I have sent my subordinate to deliver this parting gift, and I humbly hope you will accept it with a smile. All your belongings and the girl’s attire have been carefully prepared and sent along.”

    470

    二人得书，惭愧悲感，望东叩首，曰：“夫子之德竟是二天。当作犬马图报。”再拜作禀，申意其词曰：“忆自亥子春秋，获游夫子门下。巴渝妄奏，得近云门；菉葹下材，顿亲琪树。乃康成雅度，欲听慧婢之诗；而小子何知，俾作丽娘之傅。于是银蝉入户，共赓隔巷之清歌；红杏出墙，愿借邻家以艳色。以致宓妃留枕，非关韩豫偷香。携手星奔，抱衾露宿，方虞飞洞宾之剑，何期添裴晋之装。夫子休休有容，勤勤乐育，使狗偷鼠窃，不作青磷；成凤侣鸾俦，同偕白首。且高以赆仪四百，行李增光；从此弱水三千，片帆稳渡。感周旋之仔细，益愧寸衷；期报答以辛勤，难酬尺泽。惟思砺顽钝之器，庶不贻冰鉴之羞。千载遭逢，万言莫罄。敢因臂指，敬布腹心”云云，后孝廉捷南宫，由词林入谏垣。有与制军为难者，多方营救之。与某姬偕老且多子云。

    Explanation

    470

    The two students, upon receiving the letter, felt deeply ashamed, moved, and grateful. They bowed eastward and said, "The virtue of our teacher is truly as vast as two heavens. We shall repay you with the loyalty of a dog or horse." They bowed again and wrote a letter, expressing their feelings: "Recalling the time when we were fortunate to study under your guidance during the years of the 'Hai Zi' era, we, like those who offer unworthy songs, were able to approach the lofty teachings of the 'Cloud Gate.' Like lowly grass, we suddenly found ourselves close to the precious tree of your wisdom. Your noble demeanor, like that of Kong Rong, was willing to listen to the poetry of a humble student; yet, how could we, with our limited knowledge, be worthy of becoming the teacher of 'Li Niang'? Thus, the silver cicada entered our home, and we joined in the clear songs of the neighboring lanes; the red apricot blossoms reached out to borrow the beauty of the neighboring house. It was as if the goddess of the Luo River left her pillow behind, unrelated to the illicit behavior of Han Yu. We held hands and rushed like stars, sharing a quilt under the moonlight, fearing the sudden appearance of Lu Dongbin's sword, but unexpectedly finding ourselves adorned with the sword of Pei Xun. Your broad-mindedness and diligence in nurturing us have transformed us from petty thieves into noble beings; we have become a harmonious pair, to be together until our white-haired old age. Moreover, you generously gifted us with four hundred units of travel funds, adding luster to our journey; from now on, we shall cross the vast ocean of knowledge with a single sail. We are deeply grateful for your careful guidance, which makes us even more ashamed of our own shortcomings; we hope to repay your hard work, though it is difficult to match the depth of your kindness. We resolve to sharpen our dull and blunt tools so as not to bring shame upon the clear mirror of your judgment. Through the ages, we have encountered such rare fortune, and no amount of words can fully express our gratitude. We dare to use our arms and fingers to sincerely convey our innermost feelings." After this, the student, who passed the imperial examination and entered the literary academy, went on to serve in the advisory council. Whenever someone was in trouble with the military governor, he would use various means to save them. He lived a long and fruitful life with his wife, and they had many children.

    471

    秦良玉遗事
    　　
    　　女帅秦良玉，石柱土司所属人也。生而警敏多机智，父母皆爱怜之。有兄莽夫也，良玉五六岁时，邻人被窃，多方构之不得，与其父咨嗟叹惜。良玉曰：“无事多求，此必米具所为也。”邻曰：“何以知之？”玉曰：“我本不识具，日者潜窥汝室。彼以我为幼，不之避。倏又一人来呼日，‘米具，汝何为耶？’具即与耳语而去，是夜被偷，非具而何？”缉之果得。

    Explanation

    471

    The Deeds of Qin Liangyu

    The female general Qin Liangyu was a native of the Shizhu Tusi. She was born with alertness, quick-wittedness, and great intelligence, and both her parents loved and cherished her. She had an older brother who was somewhat rough and unrefined. When Qin Liangyu was around five or six years old, a neighbor's belongings were stolen, and despite various efforts to find the culprit, nothing was discovered. Her father and the neighbor lamented and sighed over the matter. Qin Liangyu said, "There is no need to make such a fuss. This must be the work of Mi Ju." The neighbor asked, "How do you know this?" Qin Liangyu replied, "I do not know Mi Ju personally, but the day before, I saw someone secretly peeping into your room. He thought I was young and did not avoid me. Suddenly, another person came and called out, 'Mi Ju, what are you doing?' Mi Ju then whispered to him and left. That night, the theft occurred. It must have been him." The investigation indeed led to the discovery of the thief.

    472

    及笄时，土俗皆自择夫。春秋之际，纵少男女于山野，唱歌求配。有马生者，土司宗族也，年及冠，无父母昆季，贫而好学，美秀而文。玉一见即携手同归，父母及兄皆贫之。玉曰：“事在人为，我只求同心者耳。贫不足道也。”谓生曰：“君得我不忧不富贵，我得君不忧不多闻。君所感者家贫，我所感者腹贫。家贫易为力，我请任之；腹贫须好学，君其为我助之。”生曰诺，相得甚欢。良玉恒执女工伴读，辄有所悟，忽谓生曰：“君所读之书，以治身心则有余，非我辈救时之策也。曷求富强之学，以成我愿。”生乃购借《韬钤武备》及《三农致富》等书数十种，闭户讲论。

    Explanation

    472

    At the age of fifteen, when she came of age, it was a local custom for girls to choose their own husbands. During the spring and autumn seasons, young men and women would gather in the mountains and fields, singing songs to find a partner. There was a man named Ma Sheng, a member of the local chieftain's clan, who had reached the age of twenty but lacked parents and siblings. He was poor but loved learning, and he was beautiful, elegant, and cultured. When Yu first met him, they held hands and returned together, but her parents and brother were concerned about his poverty. Yu said, "Success depends on human effort. I only seek someone with whom I can share my heart. Poverty is not worth mentioning." She told Ma Sheng, "If you have me, you will not worry about not becoming wealthy or noble. If I have you, I will not worry about not gaining much knowledge. What you are troubled by is family poverty, and what I am troubled by is mental poverty. Family poverty is easier to help with, so I will take care of it; mental poverty requires good learning, so you should help me with that." Ma Sheng agreed, and they got along very well. Yu always did needlework while studying, and she often gained insights. Suddenly, she said to Ma Sheng, "The books you read are sufficient for cultivating your mind and body, but they are not the strategies we need to save our times. Why not seek learning about wealth and strength to fulfill my wishes?" Ma Sheng then bought and borrowed dozens of books such as Taoqian Wu Bei (Military Strategy) and San Nong Zhi Fu (Agricultural Prosperity), and they closed themselves in to discuss and study.

    473

    年余，玉曰：“得之矣。”乃出门遍历荒山，得无主之地数十顷，归而尽其所有皆易钱，不足，乞贷父兄亲族以益之。使生置芋粟，一名包谷，此贱而易成之物，遍撤山地。玉乃日游里闾，结好众瑶妇，得其爱戴心，谓之曰：“本年当大旱，救荒之计我已密布山间，将来成熟时可以周济汝等，但须为我照料耳。”众妇皆悦，为之挟刃巡逻。秀实时争为收割，不失一茎。时果夏秋无雨，禾苗枯槁，惟此独茂。玉乃计口授瑶妇粟，欢呼拜谢而去。尚余千钟，粜之得千余金。偿债之外，犹称小康。次年，瑶妇皆来请种，愿为耕耘。玉曰：“今年应涝，惟稷独成。”购种遍播之。夏秋果大雨，诸谷皆淹没，稷高丈余，不畏水，又获丰收。仍分给酬劳，外剩数千钟，粜之大富，起厦屋居之。

    Explanation

    473

    After a year, 玉 said, "I have found it." He then went out and traversed the wilderness mountains, acquiring unowned land for dozens of acres. He returned and exchanged all his possessions for money. When it was insufficient, he borrowed from his relatives to make up for it. He instructed his servants to plant sweet potatoes and corn, also known as corn. These are cheap and easy-to-grow crops. He cleared the mountain land. He daily roamed the village and befriended many瑶women, gaining their affection and respect. He told them, "This year will be a drought. My plan to alleviate the famine is already in place in the mountains. When they mature, they can help you, but you must take care of them." The women were all pleased and patrolled with weapons. When the crops were ready, they competed to harvest them, not missing a single stem. Indeed, there was no rain in summer and autumn, and the crops withered. Only these crops thrived. He distributed rice according to the number of people. They left after bowing and thanking him. There were still thousands of measures left. Selling them earned him over a thousand gold coins. After paying off debts, he still lived a comfortable life. The next year, the瑶women all came to request planting. They wanted to cultivate the land. This year should be a flood, but only millet will succeed. He bought seeds and sowed them everywhere. Indeed, there was heavy rain in summer and autumn. All other crops were submerged. Millet grew over ten feet tall and was not afraid of water. And had another bountiful harvest. He still distributed rewards. There were still thousands of measures left. Selling them made him very wealthy. He built large houses to live in.

    474

    他察官民皆乏食，流为盗贼，而石柱赖以无恙。男女咸敬佩良玉如神明，愿为服役者甚众。玉择其勤能者留之。生亦喜曰：“卿何以知天支，测之皆验？”玉笑曰：“《前汉书》曰‘巢居知风，穴居知雨’，我师蚁耳。凡旱年其穴必深，涝年必迁高处。以是卜之，百不失一，何须高谈天文，应奇惊异哉？”生乃服其读书之得闻也，曰：“卿言富，则果富矣。贵乌在？”良玉曰：“勿急，我后必貤封君，为天下妇人吐气。

    Explanation

    474

    He observed that both officials and the common people were suffering from food shortages, and as a result, many had become thieves and bandits. However, the Shizhu area remained unharmed. Both men and women revered Liang Yu as if he were a god, and many were willing to serve him. Liang Yu selected the diligent and capable ones to keep. The man also expressed his admiration, saying, "How do you know the heavenly signs, and all your predictions are accurate?" Liang Yu smiled and replied, "The History of the Former Han Dynasty states, 'Those who live in nests know the wind, and those who live in burrows know the rain.' I simply follow the ants. In dry years, their burrows are deeper, and in wet years, they move to higher ground. By observing these signs, I can predict the weather with perfect accuracy, so why bother with grand talk of astronomy or amaze people with strange predictions?" The man was impressed by Liang Yu's knowledge and said, "If you say you are rich, then you are indeed rich. But where is your nobility?" Liang Yu responded, "Do not be in a hurry; I will be ennobled in the future, and I will give voice to all women in the world."

    475

    但目下大忧将至，奈何？”生曰：“方安居乐业，何出此言？”玉曰：“富者盗之饵。且邻寨饥荒，有不觊觎我哉？我司官素懦弱，又不知训练，猝遭强暴，鲜不倾覆，是则可忧也。君速购铜铁木石，觅巧匠，我将仿诸葛法制连弩，傅以见血封喉之药，作救急计。”于是作劲弩千张伏垣外，夜则埋机当路。凡藏粟帛之处，半穴其地作窖，中布铁蒺藜，亦傅毒药，上以板覆之。内室财帛皆露，板皆作机，自行无碍。人踏之，触机翻转，颠入窖，着蒺藜立死。

    Explanation

    475

    But great worry is about to come, what shall we do?” The man replied, “We are currently living in peace and prosperity, why speak of such things?” The jade said, “Wealth is like bait for thieves. Moreover, neighboring regions are in famine; would they not envy or covet our resources? Our officials are weak and lack training; if we are suddenly attacked by a strong force, we will surely be overwhelmed. This is what I am worried about. You should quickly purchase copper, iron, wood, and stone, and find skilled craftsmen. I will imitate Zhuge Liang’s tactics to make crossbows, coated with poison that is lethal upon contact, as a plan for emergency defense.” Thus, they prepared a thousand crossbows hidden outside the walls, and buried traps on the roads at night. For all places where grain and silk were stored, they dug half of the ground to create hidden chambers, filled with iron spikes coated with poison, and covered them with boards. Inside the rooms, the valuables were left visible, with the boards rigged to flip automatically when stepped on, causing no harm. Anyone who stepped on them would trigger the mechanism, fall into the hidden chambers, and die instantly from the spikes and poison.

    476

    布置方罢，而邻寨果生心矣，使数十人行窃。知生家富，先攻其室，为连弩射死者半。破宅门入皆奔仓库，颠入窖死者又十之六七，仅剩十数贼。邻人救至，咸缚之送司，于是邻寨藉以为词，遍邀各寨，群起而攻之。土司驱市人而战，大败，遂杀土司。寨民大恐，公议良玉之夫系土司宗戚，应袭职。众曰：“不如其妻。”群入生家，坚请良玉掌土司印以御寇。玉曰：“御侮之道，须众齐心，如臂指之相使，乃克有功。若人各一心，前车可鉴，是死我矣。君辈能听我号令否？”众皆曰：“凡我寨民，所以得温饱者，皆出夫人之赐。谁不愿为效命？如有异志，众共戳之。”良玉察其情切，乃出视事。先点千人，各予连弩一张，一发十矢，命其兄统领，射退乌合之众。

    Explanation

    476

    As the arrangement was just completed, the neighboring villages indeed harbored ill intentions and sent dozens of people to steal. Knowing that the household was wealthy, they first attacked the house, and half of those shot with the repeating crossbow died. Breaking into the house, they rushed to the warehouse, and another six or seven out of ten tumbled into the cellar and died. Only a dozen or so thieves remained. Neighbors came to rescue, bound them all, and sent them to the authorities. Thus, the neighboring villages used this as an excuse, invited all the villages, and rose up to attack the chieftain. The chieftain led the townspeople into battle, suffered a major defeat, and was subsequently killed. The villagers were greatly afraid, and publicly discussed that Lady Liangyu's husband, being a relative of the chieftain, should inherit the position. The crowd said, "Better than his wife." The crowd entered the household and firmly requested Lady Liangyu to take control of the chieftain's seal to defend against the enemy. Lady Liangyu said, "The method of defending against insults or attacks, must have everyone united in heart, like the arm and finger working together, then can achieve success. If each person has their own mind, the past can serve as a warning, and this will lead to our destruction. Can you all listen to my commands?" All the villagers said, "All my villagers, the reason why we obtain warmth and food, all come from Lady's grace. Who does not wish to serve faithfully? If there are different intentions, we will together kill him/her/it." Lady Liangyu saw their sincere feelings and then went out to handle affairs. She first selected a thousand people, gave each a repeating crossbow, which could shoot ten arrows with one pull, and ordered her brother to lead them, shooting back the ragtag group.

    477

    于是，略其地方三百余里，料其民得十万余众。立为大寨居中，以亲信者同住。环作小寨，居其民。择其强壮者，训以兵法，坐作进退，井井有条。寨外掘长壕周匝，壕中皆置毒蒺藜，上覆机板，并如居室法，外伏毒弩。寨之四隅树长木，木颠以辘轳悬竹屋。穴孔向外，以老花近视二镜叠作筒安孔中，即千里镜也，能睹百里外人马。使能书者居其上，以长绳悬铃达内营，名曰天观，一有所见，即书条摇铃索报信。又掘地三四丈，埋瓮，使耳聪者卧其上，能闻百十里人言马嘶。亦以铃索达内营，名曰地听，一有所闻，亦如前录报。再远则广布细作，故远近巨细，无不周知。又作连翔阵，人各执喷筒，以毒药煮细沙，晾透纳筒中。每伍间弩手一排护之。战必抢上风，顺风扬沙，入人目即迷，疼不得开。弩手枪手继之，是杀瞽目也，故易胜。

    Explanation

    477

    Thus, he surveyed an area of three hundred miles and estimated the population at over one hundred thousand. He established a large camp in the center, with trusted individuals living alongside him. Around this central camp, he built smaller camps to house the people. He selected the strongest and most robust individuals to train in military tactics, teaching them to sit, stand, advance, and retreat in an orderly manner. Outside the camp, he dug a long trench all around, placing poisonous barbed wire in the trench, covering it with movable boards, and setting up poisoned crossbows outside. At the four corners of the camp, he erected tall poles with pulleys suspending bamboo houses, with holes facing outward, and two close-up lenses stacked inside the holes, forming a telescope that could see people and horses a hundred miles away. He stationed someone skilled in writing there, with a long rope and bell extending to the inner camp, called "Heavenly Observation," and to write down any sightings and shake the bell to report them. Additionally, he dug pits three to four zhang deep, burying jars, and had someone with keen hearing lying on top, able to hear people and horses talking and neighing a hundred li away. He also used a bell and rope system to report to the inner camp, called "Earthly Hearing," and to record and report any sounds heard. For even greater distances, he widely deployed spies, ensuring that far and near, large and small matters were all known. He also formed a flying formation, with each person holding a spray tube filled with finely ground sand cooked with poison, dried, and placed inside the tube. A row of crossbowmen guarded every five people. In battle, they would seize the upwind position, scatter sand in the wind, causing people to be blinded and in pain, unable to open their eyes. Crossbowmen and musketeers followed, effectively killing the blinded enemy, making victory easy.

    478

    时败去之贼复邀洞獠，大举而来。先驱牛羊驼马在前，土车随之，至寨前，触弩机中箭者畜牲耳。箭尽，即以死畜并土车填壕，一拥而入。则良玉已遁，遗有粮食甚广。众皆以为得计，居之不疑。未几，寨中地震，火炮直冲。顷刻寨地皆陷，粮食中火箭火球竟发，烟焰迷空，死者数千人。余贼争奔出寨，则围兵四合，众皆请降。良玉审其为恶者诛之，胁从皆释缚，赏以口粮，曰：“去留任汝。”佥感德畏威，皆曰：“愿从夫人，虽死不去。”又益数千人，旁寨咸服。乃教以屯田富强之法，遂雄据一方。

    Explanation

    478

    The defeated bandits rallied the local tribes and launched a large-scale attack. They led the way with livestock and carts, and when they reached the camp, only the animals were hit by the crossbow traps and arrows. Once their arrows were spent, they used the dead animals and carts to fill the moat and breached the defenses in a rush. Lady Liang had already fled, leaving behind a vast amount of food. The bandits thought they had succeeded and settled in without hesitation. Soon, the camp was shaken as if by an earthquake, and fireballs were launched straight at them. In an instant, the ground beneath the camp collapsed, and the food stores were hit by flaming arrows and fireballs, filling the sky with smoke and flames, killing thousands. The remaining bandits scrambled to flee the camp, only to find themselves surrounded by the encircling troops, and they all begged for mercy. Lady Liang executed those who were truly evil and released those who had been coerced, providing them with food and saying, "You may stay or go as you wish." All were moved by her kindness and awed by her authority, and they all said, "We wish to follow Lady Liang, even if it means death." She gained thousands more followers, and the surrounding camps all submitted. She then taught them the method of garrison farming to achieve wealth and strength, and thus she became a powerful ruler in that region.

    479

    生大悦曰：“方贼之劫寨，卿何预知之？”曰：“得天观地听力也。贼来时，牲畜在前，土车随后，早以见闻得报。我知此法前寨必破，故退伏于外，而以地雷火炮伏寨中。彼见寨多粮食，谅必停留，不知中藏火箭火球等物也。彼方住歇，我使人由地道燃火线轰击，贼已胆裂，外又促之，进退不得，有不降哉？今以降人居外屯田，有益无损，是以日见富强。”生曰：“今富贵全矣，卿诚天人也。”良玉曰：“富不过百万，贵不过土司。卑卑者何足道哉？行将建大功于国家，膺天朝之高爵厚禄，方吐英雄之气耳。君姑待之。”

    Explanation

    479

    The person greatly delighted and said, "Just as the bandits were attacking the camp, how did you know beforehand?" The other replied, "By observing the heavens, the earth, and listening to the sounds. When the bandits came, livestock was in front, and earth carts followed. Early on, through observation and hearing, we received the news. I knew that this method would surely break through the front camp, so I retreated and lay in ambush outside, and set up landmines and fire bombs inside the camp. They saw that the camp had plenty of grain and must have stayed, not knowing that it contained fire arrows and fireballs. They just settled down to rest, and I sent people through underground tunnels to ignite the fuse and attack. The bandits were already terrified, and from outside, they were pushed further, unable to advance or retreat. How could they not surrender? Now, by having the surrendered bandits farm outside the camp, there is benefit without harm, therefore, we see daily growth in wealth and strength." The person said, "Now, wealth and status are complete. You are truly a divine being." The other replied, "Wealth does not exceed a million, and status does not exceed that of a local chieftain. Such humble things are not worth mentioning. Soon, great achievements will be made for the country, receiving high rank and generous rewards from the imperial court, only then will one truly express heroic spirit. Please wait and see."

    480

    此前明崇祯间事也。时寇围京师甚急，檄四方勤王师。良玉偕其夫拔寨俱起，使其兄前驱，顺风扬沙，转战皆捷，京师解严。帝大悦，召良玉入觐，欲侯之。玉辞曰：“侯及妇人，古虽有之，非天朝体制。无已，请貤封夫主。”乃召马生，以为靖北侯，赐予无算。以良玉为勇烈夫人、石柱大元帅。谢恩出，阁部索贿，玉谓其夫曰：“朝纲紊矣。帝之左右皆谄媚贪谗之辈，势必不久，勿预其祸。”遂托故告退，振旅而归。秦良玉之子孙，至今世袭土司勿替。

    Explanation

    480

    This event took place during the reign of Emperor Chongzhen of the Ming dynasty. At that time, the enemy forces were besieging the capital city of Beijing with great urgency, and an edict was issued to mobilize the feudal lords to come to the aid of the king. Lady Liang and her husband marched their entire camp, sending her brother to lead the way. They used the wind to throw sand, obscuring the enemy's vision, and won all their battles, lifting the siege on the capital. The emperor was greatly pleased and summoned Lady Liang to the court, wishing to make her a marquis. Lady Liang declined, saying, "Marquises and women, though they exist in ancient times, are not part of the imperial system. If it cannot be avoided, please bestow the title on my husband." The emperor then summoned Ma Sheng and made him Marquis of Pacifying the North, bestowing countless gifts. Lady Liang was made the Brave and Valiant Lady and the Great Marshal of Shizhu. She thanked the emperor and left, but the cabinet ministers demanded bribes. Lady Liang told her husband, "The court is in disarray. The emperor's attendants are all sycophants and greedy slanderers, the situation will not last long, do not get involved in their troubles." She then used an excuse to withdraw and returned with her troops. The descendants of Lady Liang have continued to inherit the title of native chieftain.

    481

    芗厈曰：观制军夫人与秦良玉所筹画，古之伟丈夫不过如是。不意巾帼中有此人物，天开奇局也。予向读《易》，疑阴阳之说。阳盛于阴，何不曰阳阴？或曰《易》卦皆倒装，终不足以解惑。今观二夫人，方知阴之明者，直可盖阳，此阴阳二字之确解也。
    　　
    　　某郎中
    　　
    　　郎中某，武林人，能忆宿世事。云前生为苏郡庠生，六十无子，家寒教读度日，仅足夫妻糊口而已。自曾祖父母以下三代未葬，恒以为憾。乃发愿每夜焚香一枝，跪告天日，愿减己寿，俾进财百金以葬三代，死亦瞑目。积虔五六年，未能如愿而卒。

    Explanation

    481

    Xiang An said: Observing the planning of the military governor's wife and Qin Liangyu, one realizes that the great men of antiquity were no more than this. Who would have thought that among women there could be such a figure? It is a marvel of fate. I once read the I Ching, doubting the theory of yin and yang. Yang is dominant over yin, but why not say "yang-yin"? Some say that all the hexagrams in the I Ching are inverted, but ultimately, it is insufficient to resolve doubts. Now observing these two ladies, one realizes that the clear aspect of yin can indeed surpass yang. This is the true interpretation of the words "yin" and "yang."

    A Certain Medical Officer

    A certain medical officer from Wulin could remember past lives. He said that in his previous life he was a scholar in Suzhou, sixty years old and without a son, living in poverty by teaching for a living, just enough to make ends meet for the couple. From grandparents to great-grandparents, three generations had not been buried, always felt regret. He then made a vow to burn one incense stick every night, kneeling to ask heaven and earth, wishing to shorten his own lifespan, so that money would be provided to bury the three generations, to die with peace of mind. Accumulated this devotion for five or six years, failed to fulfill his wish and died.

    482

    其魂飘然入阴司见阎罗王，王曰：“此孝子也，愿减筭以葬父祖。不知汝父母生前恶业甚多，致汝绝后。汝虽一点诚心历年不懈，但生平不过安分，无大功德，不足挽回造化。然孝思可敬也。”赐之坐。左右报大孝子到，王命开正门，身出迎之。见一披发尺余，鸠形鹄面之丐，鹑衣百结，水湿淋漓，见王下跪，王扶之起，携手而入，设座生上。王奖不绝口，丐觳觫不安。生甚疑虑，王顾之谓生曰：“汝疑此君乎？此君镇江人，生于贫家，自幼失怙，其母携之乞食。至七八岁时，膝行隶乞，以养其母。又逾七八年，其母老病不能行，此君负之，哀号于市，人皆怜而济之，必饱其母而后敢自食。如是者十余年，其母病卒。此君求化一席，创土埋之，投江而死。是一生专行其孝，别无他志。孝为百行先，出于下愚之人，岂不足以感动神明哉？”正议论间，忽见红光照耀，瑞彩缤纷。

    Explanation

    482

    His soul drifted ethereally into the underworld to meet King Yama. The king said, "This is a filial son, who wishes to shorten his lifespan to bury his ancestors. However, your parents committed many evil deeds in life, leading to your family line ending. Although you have shown sincere filial piety for years without fail, your life has been nothing but content with your station, lacking great virtue or merit, which is insufficient to change fate. Yet, your filial thoughts are admirable." He granted him a seat. The attendants announced the arrival of the great filial son, and the king ordered the main gate to be opened, personally coming out to greet him. They saw a beggar with hair hanging down more than a foot, gaunt and emaciated, dressed in tattered clothes, drenched in water. Upon seeing the king, the beggar knelt down, and the king helped him up, taking his hand and leading him in, seating him above. The king praised him without ceasing, but the beggar trembled with unease. The son was very doubtful, and the king turned to him and said, "Do you doubt this man? This man is from Zhenjiang, born into a poor family, orphaned from a young age. His mother carried him around begging for food. By the time he was seven or eight years old, he crawled on his knees while begging to support his mother. After another seven or eight years, his mother grew old and ill, unable to walk, and this man carried her, wailing in the marketplace. People all pitied and helped him, feeding his mother first before daring to eat himself. This went on for over ten years until his mother passed away. This man sought a place to rest, dug a grave to bury her, and then drowned himself in the river. Throughout his life, he devoted himself solely to filial piety, without any other aspirations. Filial piety is the foremost virtue, even among the most ignorant people, how could it not move the gods and spirits?" Just as they were discussing this, suddenly they saw a red light shining, with auspicious colors swirling around.

    483

    堂上堂下吏卒俱俯伏，朱衣判报圣僧来矣。王亦下堂跪，见五色云自天降，中立一胖大和尚，以手援阎王起而笑曰：“老僧七世修行，尚须过台下。不知何岁西行耳？”王肃容致敬曰：“圣僧根蒂既深，证果不远矣。”和尚曰：“正恐坠落耳。”乃设座公案前。王侍立，和尚命之坐，然后隅坐鞠躬。时生与丐皆避殿下，和尚举目遍观，在群鬼中指曰：“此二孝子也，请来相见。”二人入拜。和尚合十答礼，皆命坐。左右献茶，和尚举杯谓二人曰：“此茶饮之味性，然不饮则违天条。老僧请以法解之。”命二人擎杯至座前，和尚默诵神咒，茶水自涸，各举空杯对阎王曰：“饮讫矣。”王唯唯。髯判执簿进王曰：“圣僧下降吉时到，请即飞升。”见数鬼卒扶长梯置殿前，王肃和尚登梯，和尚曰：“有缘者二子，可以同登。”王即命判注簿，使二人相随。僧前丐中生后，直上数百级，足力不济，乃坠，囡然一声已作孩矣。

    Explanation

    483

    The officials and soldiers in the hall and below all knelt, and the judge in the red robe announced that the holy monk had arrived. The king also descended from the hall to kneel, and he saw a five-colored cloud descend from the sky, in the center of which stood a stout and plump monk. The monk reached out his hand to help the king rise and smiled, saying, "This old monk has practiced for seven lifetimes, yet I still need to pass through the judgment hall. I wonder when I will make the western journey?" The king bowed respectfully and said, "The holy monk's roots are deep, and it is not far from achieving enlightenment." The monk replied, "I am afraid of falling short." He then sat on a seat in front of the table. The king stood by, and the monk told him to sit, then sat himself, bowing slightly. At that time, the living and the beggar were hiding below the hall, and the monk looked around and pointed to them in the crowd of ghosts, saying, "These two are filial sons. Please come forward to meet." The two entered and bowed. The monk returned the bow, and they were all told to sit. The attendants offered tea, and the monk raised his cup to the two and said, "This tea has a taste and nature, but not drinking it would violate the heavenly laws. The old monk will use the method to resolve it." He ordered the two to hold the cups to the front of the seat, and the monk silently recited a divine spell, and the tea water dried up. Each raised an empty cup to the king and said, "We have finished drinking." The king nodded in agreement. The bearded judge took a record book and presented it to the king, saying, "The time for the holy monk's descent has arrived. Please ascend immediately." Several ghosts set up a long ladder in front of the hall, and the king respectfully helped the monk climb the ladder. The monk said, "Those with a karmic connection, these two sons, can ascend together." The king then ordered the judge to note it in the record, allowing the two to follow. The monk went first, and the beggar followed, climbing hundreds of steps. When their strength was exhausted, they fell, and with a sound, they were reborn as children.

    484

    开目见本生父母皆喜曰男也。生觉顶心虚怯不能言，惟笑啼而已。得乳甘美果腹。至三岁，囱门合而能言，忆前事了了，如在目前。知鬼神敬孝子，是以一举一动无不曲体亲心。父母爱如珍宝。前生所读书不忘只字，五六岁即理前业，能诗文，称神童。八岁入学，十三举于乡，十七成进士，授礼部主事。父母相继殁，哀毁尽礼，守制三年。二十而娶，入都复职。元日大朝，随班礼毕，皇上退殿。一太监至主事前，问姓名官职，曰：“某亲王有命，请主政公今夜入府有话。”授以牙牌曰：“持此以达，门官即不敢阻，得速见也。”主事向不识王，心甚疑之，然不敢拒。

    Explanation

    484

    When he opened his eyes, his birth parents were all happy and said, "It's a boy!" At birth, he felt a void in his mind, unable to speak, only able to laugh and cry. He received sweet milk and was satisfied. By the age of three, his fontanelle closed, and he could speak. He remembered past events clearly, as if they were happening right now. He knew that ghosts and spirits revere filial sons, so every move and action was always in line with his parents' wishes. His parents loved him like a treasure. He remembered every word of what he had read in his past life. At the age of five or six, he could manage his previous studies, write poetry and prose, and was called a prodigy. At eight years old, he entered school; at thirteen, he was recommended by his hometown; at seventeen, he became a Jinshi (imperial scholar), and was appointed as an official in the Ministry of Rites. His parents died one after another, and he mourned deeply, observing all the rituals, and observed mourning for three years. At twenty, he married, and returned to the capital to resume his duties. On New Year's Day, during the grand court ceremony, he followed the ranks in the ritual, and the emperor left the throne. A eunuch approached the official and asked for his name and position. The eunuch said, "A certain prince has a message, asking the Minister of State to come to his mansion tonight for a conversation." He was given a token and told, "Hold this token to reach him; the gatekeeper will not dare to obstruct you, and you will be able to see him quickly." The official had never met the prince before and was very suspicious, but he dared not refuse.

    485

    归宅，晚膳后登王府，以牙牌手版交门官。引入见王，其状貌即大和尚也。王笑迎曰：“故人犹忆前事乎？”主事再拜曰：“唯不敢忘我王大德。”王赐之坐，曰：“余在殿上遥睹君貌犹昔，故使人招君话旧。君今虽登仕版，然世事纷扰，不若修行之乐也。余生不食荤，忆前生所作工课，惟默为之，恐骇圣父母听闻耳。然时时惧坠落，不敢稍生妄念，今将回首矣。君何不弃官入道？”主事曰：“固所愿也。但家贫子幼，难摆脱耳。”王曰：“事不难，君记孝丐否？余将使彼助君，不可为外人道，静候时运耳。”主事起告退，王曰：“君去不必再来，修心为要，无多嘱也。”主事叩谢而出。

    Explanation

    485

    Returning home, after dinner he went to the prince's residence, presenting the tooth card and tablet to the gatekeeper. He was led to see the prince, whose appearance was that of a great monk. The prince smiled and greeted, saying, "Do you still remember our past?" The official bowed twice and said, "I dare not forget Your Highness's great virtue." The prince offered him a seat and said, "I saw your appearance from the hall and it was still as before, so I sent someone to invite you to reminisce. Although you have now entered the ranks of officials, worldly affairs are so chaotic, not as enjoyable as the joy of cultivation. I have never eaten meat since birth, recalling the work I did in my previous life, I only silently do it, fearing to shock the ears of my parents. However, I am always afraid of falling, and dare not have any delusions, now I am about to return. Why don't you give up your official position and enter the path of cultivation?" The official said, "That is indeed what I wish. But my family is poor and my children are young, it is difficult to break away." The prince said, "It is not difficult, do you remember the filial beggar? I will have him help you, do not tell outsiders, just wait for good fortune." The official got up and took his leave, the prince said, "You don't need to come back, cultivating the mind is the most important, there is no need for further instructions." The official bowed and thanked him before leaving.

    486

    未几，琉球国入贡请封。内降特旨，以主事为正使，假一品服饰，乃谢恩领敕，会副使航海而去。至则国王于城上架天桥，鞠躬逢迎，主事见王披发削脸，犹有丐状。王行礼受封毕，执主事手作华言曰：“故人识贱丐否？”遂大笑。主事曰：“大王孝感动天，作一国主。如某卑卑者，不足道矣。”王笑曰：“天使何让也？王，人不大于诸侯乎？吾今有私情奉托，天使荣旋时，敢以万两黄金为嘱。若还至镇江，吾前生之母尚土葬江边白杨下，以一小半金为吾母买棺拾骨，迁葬高原。坟前立一庙，置地百亩，招僧主之，岁时祭扫，以毕我愿。余金赠君以表相识之情。至正副天使，别有常仪，君请勿辞。”主事因聆亲王先嘱之言，故直受不辞。

    Explanation

    486

    Not long after, the Ryukyu Kingdom presented tribute and requested investiture. The emperor issued a special decree, appointing the official-in-charge as the chief envoy and granting him the attire of the first rank. The envoy then expressed his gratitude and received the imperial edict, meeting the deputy envoy and setting sail. Upon arrival, the king raised a celestial bridge on the city walls and bowed to greet them. The chief envoy saw the king with disheveled hair and a gaunt face, appearing somewhat like a beggar. After the king performed the rituals and accepted the investiture, he took the chief envoy's hand and spoke in refined Chinese, saying, "Do you recognize this humble beggar?" and laughed heartily. The chief envoy replied, "The great king's filial piety has moved heaven, and he has become the ruler of a nation. As for me, a lowly individual, I am not worthy of mention." The king laughed and said, "Why do you humble yourself, envoy? A king is not less than a feudal lord, is he? I have a personal request to make. When you return in triumph, I dare to entrust you with ten thousand taels of gold. If you reach Jiangdu, my mother from a previous life is still buried in a simple grave by the riverbank under a poplar tree. With a small portion of the gold, you can buy a coffin, collect her bones, and relocate her to a highland. Erect a temple before her grave, set aside a hundred acres of land, and invite monks to manage it, performing seasonal sacrifices and cleaning the grave to fulfill my wishes. The remaining gold, I will give to you as a token of our acquaintance. As for the chief and deputy envoys, there are separate standard procedures, so please do not decline." The chief envoy, having heard the king's earlier words, readily accepted without hesitation.

    487

    事毕还朝，迁郎中，以酬其劳，访亲王则前月薨矣。乃悟世事虚浮，旋即辞官。归至镇江，为琉球王毕所事。因念前生之三代，至苏郡访其家，则老妻早故，陋室中八柩尚存，尽举而厚葬之。回家余朱提数万，交安人抚其子，己则附居庙宇之清静者，依高僧奉佛以终。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：人惟知目前，故昏昏然营营然以毕世。若知前生，则甘苦备尝，有何意味，不入空门，欲归何所？惜世皆俗僧耳，南无阿弥陀佛。

    Explanation

    487

    After completing his duties, he returned to court and was promoted to the position of Langzhong, as a reward for his efforts. When he visited the prince, he learned that the prince had passed away the previous month. Realizing the transience and insubstantiality of worldly affairs, he immediately resigned from his post. Upon returning to Zhenjiang, he served the last king of Ryukyu. Reflecting on his ancestors from three generations ago, he went to Suzhou to visit his family. He found that his old wife had passed away long ago, and in the humble house, eight coffins still remained. He had them all buried with full honors. After returning home, he had several ten thousand pieces of gold left, which he entrusted to his wife to take care of his son. For himself, he chose to live in a quiet temple, relying on a high-ranking monk to practice Buddhism until the end of his life.

    Xiang'an said: People only know about the present, so they are dazed and busy all their lives. If they knew about their past lives, they would have experienced all kinds of joys and sorrows, so what would be the meaning? They would not enter the Buddhist door, where else would they go? Alas, the world is full of ordinary monks, Namo Amitabha Buddha.

    488

    术芷治痘
    　　
    　　痘疮者，父母欲火所结，病在先天。天一生水，故人之五脏肾先成，火毒伏其中，感时气即发。其有平险者，父母入房时，清心寡欲，不茹荤酒，则毒轻易出。若食有毒之物，而又沉醉，所生子女其毒必重。不见富贵之儿，殇于痘者多，贫贱之子，伤于痘者少乎？若痘出幼年尚可，成丁婚配后出痘，成者甚鲜。盖先天巳伤，不能通达故也。凡医皆执此说。
    　　
    　　乡人黄大者，年二十，父母已故，娶有妻室。幼未出痘。是年春间痘症盛行，黄大忽患大热，一身尽疼，入于骨骸，求死不得。七八日间，面现红尖数点，其妻请邻翁视之，曰：“此痘疮也，甚险。”乃延痘科诊之，曰：“阴亏甚矣。必补托而后可，以六味地黄加减。”立方而去。其妻以百钱求邻翁取药，翁曰：“我家潮湿，甚思买苍术、白芷薰屋，正好乘便。”遂入市择兴旺药肆，投以钱与方并买术、芷二十钱。

    Explanation

    488

    Smallpox Treated with Cangzhu and Baizhi

    Smallpox, it is said, is caused by the sexual desires of the parents, and the illness is rooted in the inherent weakness of the child. According to the Book of Changes, water is the primordial element, and thus the kidneys form first among the five organs. Fire toxicity is hidden within, and the disease is triggered by seasonal changes. Those who are less affected by the disease are those whose parents, during intimate moments, maintain a clear mind and few desires, abstaining from meat and alcohol. In such cases, the toxins are easily expelled. However, if one consumes impure substances and indulges in excessive drinking, the toxins in the children born will be more severe. It is observed that wealthy children often succumb to smallpox, while poor children are less likely to be severely affected. If smallpox appears in childhood, it may still be survivable, but if it occurs after reaching adulthood and marriage, survival is rare. This is because the inherent weakness is too severe to overcome. All doctors adhere to this belief.

    In a certain village, there was a man named Huang Da, who was twenty years old, had lost his parents, and was married. He had not contracted smallpox as a child. That spring, smallpox was widespread. Huang Da suddenly developed a high fever, experiencing severe body pain that seemed to penetrate his bones, and he wished for death but could not find it. After several days, smallpox lesions appeared on his face. His wife asked a neighboring elder to examine him, who said, "This is smallpox, and it is very dangerous." They then called in a specialist for smallpox, who diagnosed, "There is a severe deficiency of yin. It must be treated with tonifying and supporting methods, using a modified version of the Six Flavor Rehmannia Pill." The specialist prescribed the medicine and left. The wife asked the neighbor to buy the medicine with 100 coins. The neighbor replied, "My house is damp, and I am thinking of buying Cangzhu (苍术) and Baizhi (白芷) to dry it out. This is a good opportunity." He then went to a well-established pharmacy and bought the medicine and herbs for 20 coins, along with the prescribed formula.

    489

    铺伙甚忙，包药之际，以药方误插术、芷包上。翁不知也，至家，交有方者与黄妻，嘱速煎而进之。黄大服后，即得安眠。翁夜生盆火，开包取术、芷，则皆药料矣。急往叩门问之，黄妻曰：“已煎服半日，睡甚安，似大见效。”翁不敢言而回。次早往视，一身痘花皆现，光明磊落，且索饭食，神气间如无病者。翁诧异，即代为延医复诊，医曰：“已反逆回顺，非我重用地黄，不能如是神速也。今可不药收功矣。”翁心中抱歉，朝夕在侧殷勤服侍。果十朝痘尽回，痂渐脱落，壮健如初。

    Explanation

    489

    The shop assistants were very busy, and during the preparation of the medicine, the prescription was mistakenly placed in a package containing herbs shu and zhi. The elderly man did not notice this mistake and, upon returning home, entrusted the knowledgeable person to give the medicine to Huang’s wife, instructing her to prepare and administer it quickly. After taking a large dose, Huang fell into a deep sleep. That night, the elderly man lit a small fire, opened the package, and found that instead of the herbs, it contained the medicinal ingredients. He hurriedly went to knock on the door and asked Huang’s wife, who replied, “He has been taking it for half a day, and he sleeps very soundly, as if it has had a great effect.” The elderly man dared not speak up and returned home. The next morning, he went to check on Huang, who now had smallpox spots all over his body, which appeared bright and clear, and he was asking for food, looking as if he were not ill. The elderly man was astonished and immediately arranged for a doctor to come for a follow-up visit. The doctor said, “The condition has reversed, and it would not have improved so swiftly without the heavy use of rehmannia. Now, he can recover without further medication.” The elderly man felt guilty and stayed by his side morning and evening, caring for him diligently. Indeed, after ten days, all the smallpox had receded, the scabs gradually fell off, and Huang regained his strength and health as before.

    490

    黄大感甚，乃杀鸡烹肉备谢礼请医，并酬邻翁之劳。医持杯大言曰：“非我妙手，恐此症不得回春。老翁阅历多矣，岂不见壮年出痘者十有九死乎？”翁曰：“不然。假如此症误服苍术、白芷，将何如？”医曰：“一滴入口，早已毙矣。”翁即回家取药曰：“请观此，非先生所开之药乎？”医检之曰：“是也。”翁曰：“是则误矣。以同市两包相混，而黄大所服者实术、芷也。”医曰：“莫与我争功。我阅书多矣，从无术、芷发痘之说。此必药肆匆忙，误包两料药，实未给术、芷也。”翁乃呼黄妻出药滓示之，固术、芷也。翁曰：“我非争功，若果术、芷所愈，有后患否？是以求教。”医摇首支吾，持谢礼而去。二人怀疑莫释，遍访时医，皆曰无此情理。或者痘为术、芷逼引入内，复发无药可疗矣。二人更急矣。

    Explanation

    490

    Huang Da felt very ill, and so he killed a chicken and cooked meat to prepare a gift for inviting a doctor, and to thank the elderly neighbor for his assistance. The doctor, holding a cup, spoke grandly, saying, "Without my skillful hands, this illness might not recover." The old man replied, "But I have seen many young people die from smallpox, haven't I?" The doctor said, "Not so. Suppose this illness was mistakenly given Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica, what would happen then?" The doctor answered, "Even a drop in the mouth would have already killed him." The old man then went home to fetch the medicine, saying, "Please look at this, is it not the medicine you prescribed?" The doctor examined it and said, "Yes, it is." The old man said, "Then it must be wrong. The pharmacy mixed up two batches of medicine, and what was actually given was Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica." The doctor said, "Do not share the credit with me. I have read many books, and there is no mention of Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica causing smallpox." The old man then called Huang Da's wife to show the remnants of the medicine, which were indeed Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica. The old man said, "I am not claiming credit; if the illness was truly cured by Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica, would there be no further complications? That is why I seek your advice." The doctor shook his head and hesitated, taking the gift and leaving. The two people remained doubtful and unable to resolve their doubts, seeking advice from many contemporary doctors, who all said that such a situation made no sense. Some suggested that the smallpox might have been forced into the body by Atractylodes lancea and Angelica dahurica, making it untreatable. The two people became even more anxious.

    491

    时巨室以千金延名医薛生白在邑，二人执香跪门求诊。生白悯其诚，使入诊之。谓黄大曰：“脉甚和平，无疾何治。”翁始以疑质之，生白大笑曰：“是矣，此必黄君祖功宗德，不应绝后，故有此误。若服地黄汤，闷死久矣。时医不知用药之活变，而执古方，冤死者不少。彼意谓有夫妇必有房事，亏损肾经。故壮年出痘，必先补托。不知夫妇之事天生王化，互相补益。若以为有房事者必损，则世无八九十岁夫妇偕老之人，而鳏寡孤独皆千万岁矣。惟好色过度则伤，黄君一夫一妇，人伦之常，复有何损？其痘之郁而不发者，必平日尽力农田，长浸水中，深受湿气，遏不得达，是以痛楚。老翁所市术、芷必有数两，大剂投之，水气立散，有不畅茂条达者乎？时医何能见及此？我故曰：祖功宗德，鬼神所使也。且黄君脉大而长，不但痘不复发，且享寿无疑。”二人心始释然，喜跃再拜而去。

    Explanation

    491

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a wealthy family in the county invited the famous physician Xue Shengbai. Two men, holding incense and kneeling at the door, requested a diagnosis. Xue Shengbai, moved by their sincerity, allowed them to enter and examine the patient. He told Mr. Huang, "The pulse is very smooth and balanced; there is no illness, so why treat?" The elderly man began to question him, and Xue Shengbai laughed and said, "This is it. This must be due to Mr. Huang's ancestors' merits and virtues, which should not have led to this mistake. If he had taken Rehmannia Decoction, he would have suffocated long ago. Modern physicians do not understand the living changes in medicine and stick to ancient formulas, causing many innocent deaths. They think that sexual intercourse between husband and wife must damage the kidney meridian. Therefore, a young adult breaking out with smallpox must first be supported. They do not know that the relationship between husband and wife is naturally part of royal governance and mutually benefits each other. If one thinks that sexual intercourse must cause damage, then there would be no couples who live together until they are in their eighties or nineties, and widowers, widows, orphans, and the lonely would all live for thousands of years. Only excessive indulgence in sex causes harm. Mr. Huang has one husband and one wife, which is the normal human relationship; what harm could there be? The smallpox that is suppressed and not breaking out must be because he works hard in the fields every day, long immersed in water, deeply affected by dampness, blocked and unable to reach, hence the pain. The old man must have bought several ounces of artemisia and sweet flag. A large dose is given, and the dampness immediately disperses; is there anyone who does not flourish and grow? Modern physicians cannot see this far. I therefore say: ancestral merits and virtues are influenced by ghosts and spirits. Furthermore, Mr. Huang's pulse is large and long; not only will the smallpox not recur, but he will surely enjoy a long life." The two men's hearts finally relaxed, and they happily and joyfully bowed twice and left.

    492

    乌蛇已癞
    　　
    　　蛇之种类伙矣，皆追风药也。内有乌稍蛇一种最毒。姑苏有曹吏部，由郎中出为粤东潮州府。是邑也，凡幼女皆蕴癞毒，故及笄须有人过癞去，方可婚配。女子年十五六，无论贫富，皆在大门外工作，诱外来浮浪子弟交。住弥月，女之父母张灯彩，设筵席，会亲友，以明女癞去可结亲矣。时浪子亦与宴事毕，富者酌赠医资送去。多则一年，必发癞死。且能过人，故亲人不敢近。官之好善者，设癞院收养之。
    　　
    　　曹太守有弟已冠，不好学，日事游荡。戚友知此间风俗者，恒诏戒之。介弟初亦不敢犯，但游观而已。一日至巨宅前见一女子，国色也，不粉饰而自然，既艳丽而庄重。不禁迷恋辗转，再三舍之不得。喟然曰：“人生几何，美色难遇，牡丹花下死，较老髦乐甚也。”意乃决。与女交谈，引之入室，两情相得，有终焉之志。

    Explanation

    492

    The Black Snake Has Leprosy

    The kinds of snakes are numerous, and all are medicines for wind disease. Among them, the Wushao snake is the most venomous. There was a Cao official from Suzhou, who was appointed as magistrate of Chaozhou in Guangdong from the position of Langzhong. In this county, all young girls are said to be afflicted with leprosy, so when they reach the age of marriage, they must be exposed to leprosy by someone in order to be eligible for marriage. Girls aged fifteen or sixteen, regardless of wealth or poverty, all work outside the front gate, luring outsiders and wayward young men to have relations with them. After a month of cohabitation, the girl's parents will hang lanterns and decorations, set up a banquet, and invite relatives and friends, to signify that the girl has undergone the leprosy process and is now eligible to be married. At this time, the wayward youth also participates in the banquet. Those who are wealthy give a gift of money or resources for her medical care. If the stay lasts more than a year, she will certainly fall ill with leprosy and die. Moreover, she can transmit the disease to others, so her relatives dare not get close to her. Officials who are kind-hearted establish leprosy hospitals to care for these women.

    The magistrate Cao had a younger brother who had already undergone the coming-of-age ceremony, but he did not like to study and spent his days wandering about. Relatives and friends who were aware of the local customs often warned him against this. At first, the younger brother also dared not transgress, only wandering and observing. One day, he came before a grand mansion and saw a woman of extraordinary beauty. She was naturally beautiful without any makeup, both radiant and dignified. Unable to resist, he became entranced, constantly returning to her side, unable to let her go. He sighed and said, "Life is short, and beautiful women are hard to find. To die beneath the peony flower is far more pleasant than to grow old in solitude." His mind was then resolved. He conversed with the woman, brought her into the room, and the two became mutually fond, with the intention of a lifelong union.

    493

    无如弥月后，例应分折。其父母见二人情重，不使女知。请介弟前堂大宴，询及家世，方知为太守亲弟。屡奉府县查访綦切，不胜惊骇。但事已如此，不能隐匿，赠以千金送之回。府太守以乃弟自作之孽，无可奈何，资送回籍，俟死而已。一路毛发脱落，日渐周身发痒。及家，其次兄收之，虑其蔓延，锁于酒房下榻。嫂氏哀之，使老媪给饮食。未几癞已匝身，奄奄一息，自知必死矣。
    　　
    　　先是介弟去后，女方知其事，乃与父母为难，誓不二夫，必欲同死。其父母婉劝教戒，矢志不回，不得已以实情告。太守敬其节义，允为作礼，遣送姑苏为弟守节，来投嫂氏。嫂谓女曰：“叔病癞已不起矣，莫如原舟遄返，以妹品貌，何患无好逑君子，何必恋此及泉人耶？”女泣曰：“妾故知之，不忍郎之独为癞鬼。且女身不可二夫，来就死耳，非效于飞之乐也。”嫂怜而敬之。送女入酒房，与介弟相抱而泣。女乃遣婢仆归复命。亲为其夫调养。

    Explanation

    493

    As the month passed, according to custom, the couple was to be separated. The parents, seeing the deep affection between the two, chose not to inform the daughter. They invited the younger brother to hold a grand banquet in the front hall, where they inquired about his family background, and learned he was the magistrate's younger brother. The local authorities had repeatedly conducted urgent investigations, causing great alarm. However, since the matter had come to this, they could no longer conceal it, and gave him a thousand gold pieces to send him back. The magistrate, seeing that his younger brother had brought this upon himself, could do nothing but provide provisions for his return and wait for his death. On the journey, the younger brother's hair began to fall out, and he gradually developed itching all over his body. Upon returning home, the second eldest brother took him in, fearing the disease might spread, and locked him in the wine room. The wife of the second eldest brother pitied him and had an elderly woman provide him with food and drink. Soon, the younger brother's entire body was covered in sores, and he lay on the verge of death, knowing that he would surely die.

    After the younger brother left, the daughter learned of the situation and became distressed, vowing never to have two husbands and determined to die with him. Her parents tried to persuade her gently, but she remained steadfast in her resolve and could not be swayed. Reluctantly, they told her the truth. The magistrate respected her chastity and righteousness, and allowed them to perform the necessary rites, sending her to Suzhou to remain chaste for her deceased husband. She came to stay with the wife of the second eldest brother. The wife said to the daughter, "Your brother-in-law is already beyond help with his illness. It would be better to return home soon. With your beauty and virtue, why worry about finding a good match? Why should you cling to this dead man?" The daughter wept and replied, "I know it well, but I cannot bear the thought of my husband being alone as a leper. Moreover, a woman's body cannot have two husbands. I have come only to die with him, not to imitate the joy of flying together." The wife pitied and respected her. She sent the daughter into the wine room, where she embraced her husband and cried together. The daughter then sent the maids and servants back to report to her parents and personally took care of her husband.

    494

    一日，介弟使女烹茶未至，渴甚，循墙而起，觅饮房中，惟酒缸十余。寻至室隅，尚有剩酒半缸，以碗饮至数四，渴解而人亦醉倒。女持茶来扶之卧。至次日，癞皆结痂，人亦精爽，谓女曰：“此酒大有益处，日与我冷饮之，当有效。”女顺其意，每饭必先以酒。半月癞痂寻脱，一身新肉滑腻非常，眉发复生，居然风流年少矣。夫妻快慰。及酒将完，见缸底一大黑蛇浸毙其中，盖乌梢也。出问家人，乃知前年注酒时，见有蛇在内，是以遗弃半缸，不意为贵介弟起病之祥。于是夫妇相将仍赴粤东，女之父母及曹太守皆大悦，共出财为谋功名，得河泊所官以终。此其有一命之荣，故不死耶。余曰：非也，粤女贞一之操，有以感召之耳。

    Explanation

    494

    One day, the younger brother sent the woman to brew tea, but she did not arrive, and he was very thirsty. He rose by the wall and searched for a place to drink, finding only more than ten wine barrels in the room. He searched until the corner of the room and found half a barrel of leftover wine. He drank several times with a bowl, quenching his thirst and falling drunk. The woman brought tea and helped him lie down. By the next day, the sores all formed scabs, and he also felt refreshed. He said to the woman, "This wine is very beneficial. Every day give me cold wine, and there will be an effect." The woman followed his wishes, and every meal must start with wine. After half a month, the scabs fell off, and the new flesh was very smooth and slippery. The eyebrows and hair grew back, and he became a handsome young man. The husband and wife were very happy. When the wine was almost finished, they saw a large black snake dead at the bottom of the barrel, which was a black snake. They asked the family and learned that last year when the wine was poured, they saw a snake inside, so they discarded half a barrel. Unintentionally, it became a sign of recovery for the noble younger brother. So the couple returned to Guangdong together. The woman's parents and Cao the magistrate were very pleased. They jointly contributed money to pursue official success, and he eventually became an official at the river office. This is why he had a chance of honor, so he did not die. I say: no, it was because of the purity and integrity of the woman from Guangdong, which attracted this outcome.

    495

    芗厈曰：观上二则，可知医无恒方，药若得当，实有起死回生之效。惜时医执陈方不知变通，以至危症不救，安得人人巧遇如黄、曹二君耶？
    　　
    　　金标客
