    　　标客金氏，籍隶嘉禾。其为人也好义，幼习武艺，能运气敌金刃。壮年出外保标，江湖赫赫有名，盗贼闻风而惧。及其老也，腰有千金，在江南之清河立船行为业，悠悠自得，不外出矣。

    Explanation

    495

    Xiang Yan said: By examining the two cases above, we can see that a doctor has no fixed formula, and if the medicine is appropriate, it truly has the miraculous effect of reviving the dead. Alas, many doctors cling to old formulas without adapting, leading to the inability to save critical cases. How often can one happen to meet someone like the Yellow Emperor or Cao?

    Gold Standard Guest

    The Gold Standard Guest, surnamed Jin, was registered in Jiaxing. He was known for valuing righteousness, having learned martial arts as a child and being able to use qi to resist metal weapons. In his youth, he went out to protect valuable items and became famous throughout the rivers and lakes, with thieves terrified by his reputation. As he grew older, he had a thousand gold coins in his belt and established himself in the shipbuilding business in Qinghe, Jiangnan. He was contentedly at ease and no longer went out.

    496

    夏日在水阁纳凉，见一舟扬帆而过，篷窗西启，中坐青年公子，外二仆人。回顾舟子，识皆劫贼。顿起怜悯心，疾卷行囊追及之。乞附舟南旋，舟子不纳。公子见其二毛，怜贫老而许之。舟子曰：“人各有心，知其无歹意耶？设有他虞，非我等不预言也。”公子曰：“此一老，我三人能备之。毋需尔过虑。”金闻言，一跃登舟，与公子为礼，遂问行踪。公子告以父宰山左，回原籍武林就婚也。与金联乡谊，谈甚洽，以长者呼之，每饭必共。家人心不谓然，见于词色。公子益尊敬之。舟行长淮时，金密谓公子曰：“舟子皆江湖积贼，今当停泊某所，若过此，必逗遛旷野，将行其谋矣。”公子怖甚，曰：“奈何？”金曰：“有老夫在，彼四五后生不足惧也。某今夜与公子易地而寝，看其如何下手！”公子甚疑惧。果舟至闹市竟过。金问之，舟子曰：“风利不得泊也。”公子益惧，夜乃与金潜易卧处。

    Explanation

    496

    In the summer, while cooling off in a waterside pavilion, I saw a boat sailing by with its awning window open to the west. Inside sat a young nobleman, accompanied by two servants. Looking back at the boatman, I recognized them all as bandits. Instantly filled with compassion, I quickly packed my bag and caught up with them. I begged to join the boat and sail south, but the boatman did not accept. The young nobleman, seeing my gray hair, felt pity for my poverty and old age and agreed to let me on board. The boatman said, "Everyone has their own mind. Do you know they have no bad intentions? If there is any other trouble, it is not for us to say." The young nobleman replied, "With this old man, the three of us can handle them. You need not worry too much." Upon hearing this, Jin leaped onto the boat, paid respects to the young nobleman, and then asked about their destination. The young nobleman told him that his father was the magistrate of Shandong and that they were returning to their hometown in Wulin for marriage. Jin formed a bond of hometown friendship with the young nobleman, and they talked very well. The young nobleman addressed Jin as an elder and ate every meal with him. The family was not convinced, and this showed in their words and expressions. The young nobleman respected Jin even more. When the boat was sailing along the Huai River, Jin whispered to the young nobleman, "The boatmen are all seasoned bandits from the rivers and lakes. Today, they will stop at a certain place. If they pass this place, they will surely linger in the wilderness and carry out their plan." The young nobleman was very frightened and asked, "What shall we do?" Jin said, "With me here, those four or five young men are not to be feared. I will switch places with you tonight and see how they will act." The young nobleman was very suspicious and fearful. Indeed, the boat passed through the bustling market, and the boatman said, "The wind was too strong to stop the boat." The young nobleman was even more frightened and, at night, he secretly switched places with Jin.

    497

    舟行至半夜，忽停，似闻舟子皆息灯而卧，驾长密谓其伙曰：“以我五人，了彼三人甚易，今添一客，多费手足矣。”伙曰：“客似强健，然老不足畏也。”驾长偕一伙，持利斧潜启舱门，系公子常卧处。抚首砍之不入。窃谓伙曰：“得无误砍其枕乎？不然，文弱少年安有如是之结实头皮也。”伙曰：“汝于星光下照囟门劈之。”二人听卧者犹有鼾声，乃双手举斧猛劈之。斧反激回，伤驾长首而跌。金乃扬声有贼。于是公子主仆举灯入，舱门大启。金起坐，呼舟子，伙四人咸集。

    Explanation

    497

    The boat traveled until midnight when it suddenly stopped. It seemed that the boatmen had extinguished their lamps and gone to sleep. The captain secretly told his companions, "With us five, it would be very easy to deal with them three, but now with the addition of one guest, it will take more effort." One companion replied, "The guest seems strong and healthy, but an old man is not to be feared." The captain and one companion, holding a sharp axe, secretly opened the cabin door where the young master usually lay. They struck the head without success. The captain whispered to his companion, "Perhaps we've mistakenly cut his pillow? Otherwise, how could a weak and scholarly young man have such a solid scalp?" The companion suggested, "You should strike the fontanel under the starlight." The two listened to the sleeper still snoring, then lifted the axe with both hands and struck fiercely. The axe rebounded, injuring the captain's head and causing him to fall. Jin then shouted that there was a thief. Then the young master and his servants lit the lamps and entered, the cabin door widely opened. Jin sat up, called the boatmen, and the four companions gathered.

    498

    金曰：“驾长何在？”曰睡久矣。金曰：“顷有贼斧劈我首，似言好结实头皮者，驾长声也。得无自伤乎？我标客金某也，在千万寇盗中，出入自如。汝五毛贼欲伤我得乎？恐自不能保。”驾长闻言，亦以布蒙首出，五人佥再拜请罪曰：“久闻长者大名，不意在此相见。长者既明此意，不必多言。我等愿革面洗心，送公等回籍，断不敢复萌异志。乞恕无知之罪。”金曰：“谅汝辈亦无能为，速与我开舟泊扬城，为公子压惊，不汝罪矣。”舟子叩首出，扬帆前行。公子及仆皆感甚，至扬州盛席宴客，不使舟子破钱，亦感甚。
    　　
    　　未几舟抵嘉禾，金客携囊告别，公子泣留。金曰：“此去皆官塘大路，不过二日抵省，无下手处。我嘱之，彼不敢违。”乃呼舟子告之曰：“汝等好进公子回府，取得平安信来复我，日后好相见。若稍有怠惰，公子莫给与信，汝五人命悬我手矣。”皆曰：“不敢，惟长者命。”遂送公子归，取札归报，金客回家。

    Explanation

    498

    Gold said, "Where is the captain?" The reply was, "He has been sleeping for a long time." Gold said, "Just now, a bandit struck my head with an axe, saying something about how solid my scalp is—that was the captain's voice. Could he have injured himself? I am Gold, a traveler among countless bandits, moving about freely. Could you five petty thieves harm me? I fear you cannot even protect yourselves." Upon hearing this, the captain also covered his head with a cloth and came out. The five men all knelt and bowed, asking for forgiveness: "We have long heard of your esteemed name, never expecting to meet you here. Since you understand this situation, there is no need for further words. We are willing to reform ourselves and send you all back to your hometowns. We swear we will not dare to harbor any other intentions. Please forgive our ignorance." Gold said, "I believe you are incapable of anything, so quickly open the boat and dock in Yangzhou to calm the young master. I will not hold you guilty." The boatmen bowed and departed, sailing on. The young master and his servants were very grateful, and upon reaching Yangzhou, they held a grand banquet for the guests, without making the boatmen spend money, which they also appreciated greatly.

    Not long after, the boat arrived at Jiahe, and Gold, carrying his bag, bid farewell. The young master wept and tried to persuade him to stay. Gold said, "The journey ahead is all on official roads, and it will take no more than two days to reach the province, with no opportunity for trouble. I have instructed them, and they dare not disobey." He then called out to the boatmen and told them, "You must safely escort the young master back to his residence. Once you receive a letter of safe return, bring it to me, so we can meet again in the future. If there is any negligence, the young master will not give you the letter, and your lives will be in my hands." They all replied, "We dare not, only following the elder's command." Thus, they escorted the young master back and retrieved the letter to report back, while Gold returned home.

    499

    时邑中有数十少年皆习拳勇，推一人为翘楚。其人亦诩诩自得，众皆曰：“我邑中老前辈，惟金某为最。汝能胜此人，则杰出矣。”其人曰：“近闻回家，姑试之。”或劝之曰：“金长者为人和霭，在乡党中犯而不较。汝以少壮而殴衰老，胜之不武。”其人曰：“我姑戏之，亦无伤也。”于是群伺于市。是日微雨，金持盖着屐出市。其人潜至身后，以右手挖其臀。金即运气至臀，夹其手使不得出。故作不知，徐徐而行。其人五指痛入心肺，不觉哀切求恕，金行自若。市人皆大笑。众见少年色变，群遣金客陪礼，金始松臀。其人跌出丈余，伏地不动。众视之，指臂皆青肿。金回顾微哂曰：“少年恃血气之刚，狎侮老夫，自作孽。但系子侄辈，何忍死之？公等扶至我家，治之可也。”乃共掖赴金宅，以三丸给之曰：“以酒服，当不至大损。”其人服之，下血升余，疾虽愈，而右臂从此无力。

    Explanation

    499

    At the time in the town, there were several dozen young men who all practiced martial arts. They all recommended one person as the best among them. The person was also boastful and self-satisfied. Everyone said, "In our town, the most senior elder is only Mr. Jin." If you can defeat this person, then you will be outstanding. He recently heard that he had returned home, so he decided to try. Someone advised him, "Mr. Jin is a kind and gentle old man who does not计较 (计较:计较,计较) in the乡党 (乡党:乡党,乡党) community." You are young and strong, but attacking an old man is not a fair fight. I will just play with him, it won't harm him. So they all waited in the market. On that day, there was a light rain, and Mr. Jin came out of the market holding an umbrella and wearing clogs. The person secretly approached behind him and dug his right hand into his buttocks. Mr. Jin immediately used his qi to his buttocks, gripping his hand so it couldn't be pulled out. He pretended not to know and walked slowly. The person's fingers were in such pain that they involuntarily begged for forgiveness. Mr. Jin continued walking as if nothing happened. The people in the market all laughed. Seeing the young man's face change, the crowd sent Mr. Jin's guests to apologize, and Mr. Jin finally released his grip on his buttocks. The person fell back several meters and lay on the ground motionless. The crowd looked at him, and his fingers and arms were bruised and swollen. Mr. Jin turned back and smiled slightly, saying, "The young man relies on his youthful vigor to mock an old man, bringing this upon himself. However, since he is a relative, how could I let him die? Please carry him to my house, and I will treat him." So they all carried him to Mr. Jin's house and gave him three pills, saying, "Take them with wine, and it won't cause great harm." The person took them, and after expelling blood, his illness was cured, but his right arm remained weak from then on.

    500

    或曰：“拳勇亦多矣，何至头能敌巨斧，臀能损人臂，无乃言过其实乎？”芗厈曰：“术有所谓金钟罩铁布衫者，皆运气之功。气之足也，至大至刚，虽以万觔石压身无碍。金老想必习此，拒斧夹臀犹其小戏耳。”
    　　
    　　富贵死生定数
    　　
    　　浙人召姓，在部充贴写。夫妇二人生一子，已六龄。家寒甚，赁小屋三椽以居。夜间邻宅火发，延及屋粱，夫妇排窗跃出院。其妇曰：“尚有子在室，奈何？”召曰：“吾中年只此一子，不可舍也。”复跃入，见一青衣卒手执青旗引烟下旋，一红衣卒执红旗引火欲下。青衣卒见召，即以旗挡红衣卒曰：“姑缓，尚有奔牛巡司未出。”于是烟火皆往上避，召得抱其子跃出。乃自信将来必为巡检也。逢南来者，即访问奔牛司出息。逾十余年，依然故我，郁郁不得志而殁。其子接业，司官怜其勤敏，拔为吏，役满，选得奔牛司巡检。

    Explanation

    500

    Someone said, "There are many kinds of martial arts, but how could it be that the head can withstand a huge axe, and the buttocks can injure someone's arm? Isn't this an exaggeration?" Xiang Yan replied, "There is a martial art technique known as 'Iron Shirt' or 'Iron Body,' all of which are the result of qi cultivation. The qi is extremely powerful and robust, so even if a thousand-pound stone is pressed onto the body, it would not be hindered. Old Jin must have practiced this, and resisting an axe and pinching the buttocks are just small tricks."

    Wealth, honor, life, and death are all predetermined.

    A man surnamed Zhao from Zhejiang, who worked as a clerk in the government, had a wife and a son who was already six years old. They were very poor and rented a small house with three rafters to live in. One night, a fire broke out in a neighboring house and spread to the roof beams. The couple pushed open the window and leaped out of the yard. The wife said, "There is still a son inside the house, what shall we do?" Zhao replied, "I am middle-aged and have only this one son; I cannot abandon him." He leaped back in and saw a man in blue uniform holding a blue flag leading the smoke downward in a spiral, and a man in red uniform holding a red flag leading the fire downward. The man in blue uniform saw Zhao and immediately used his flag to block the man in red uniform, saying, "Please wait, there is still the patrol officer at Buniu who has not yet arrived." Thus, the smoke and fire all moved upward. Zhao then picked up his son and leaped out. He then believed that he would surely become a patrol officer in the future. Whenever he met someone from the south, he would ask about the prospects of the Buniu patrol. After more than ten years, he was still the same, dejected and unfulfilled, and died. His son took over the business. The patrol officer felt sorry for his diligence and quickness, promoted him to an official position. After serving his term, he was selected to become the patrol officer at Buniu.

    501

    又山阴王生，在都训读。其戚以廉价得厦屋，因外宅有鬼甚厉，住此者十有九死，以致无人敢往。王生胆素壮，不信鬼神事，谓其戚曰：“饮我，为君住宿辟除不祥。”戚深知其强项，不可劝，乃饮食之。生择最凶之处下榻，燃巨烛读书，至三鼓，倦甚方寤。烛焰渐青，见人影往来幢幢。生意欲起击之，无奈四肢如缚，口禁不得言，惟心甚了了。微闻一鬼言：“何人大胆，敢来挠搅，我必毙之。”一鬼前，细阅生面目，摇手曰：“此太平父母，不可犯。

    Explanation

    501

    Wang from Shan Yin was studying in the capital. His relatives obtained a large house at a low price, but because there was a very fierce ghost in the outer house, nearly all who stayed there died, so no one dared to go there. Wang's courage was always strong, and he did not believe in ghosts and spirits. He told his relatives, "Let me drink, to stay here and ward off evil." His relatives knew he was stubborn and could not be persuaded, so they drank and ate with him. Wang chose the most dangerous place to stay, lit a large candle, and read until the third drum beat. He became very tired and fell asleep. The candle flame turned blue, and he saw people's shadows moving back and forth. Wang thought to get up and attack them, but could not move, as if his limbs were bound. His mouth was sealed, unable to speak, but only his mind was clear. He heard faintly a ghost say, "Who is so bold to come and disturb? I will kill him." One ghost came forward, carefully looked at Wang's face, shook his head, and said, "This is a peaceful parent. Cannot be harmed."

    502

    ”众鬼笑曰：“据汝言亦不过太平父母而已，何足畏？纵不毙之，戏之可也。”乃共举其榻，满室盘旋。生觉头晕身颠，烦懑欲绝，鸡初鸣始散。生方得安寝，日午未起。其戚虑有他故，使人逾墙拔关至榻前，见生酣睡，唤之醒，生张目曰：“公等来看太平父母耶？”述其故，共贺之。生亦自诩将为明府，且平安也。每谓所亲曰：“吾若得为大令时，某司刑钱，某司总务，吾家大兄只堪接待宾客而已。”无如每试被黜，年逾不惑，狁诸生也。偃蹇甚，舍去投亲，引入都中作吏，满日选得太平县典史。

    Explanation

    502

    The ghosts laughed and said, "According to your words, you are no more than a peaceful father and mother. What is there to fear? Even if we do not kill you, we can still play with you." They then all lifted his bed and circled the room. The protagonist felt dizzy and disoriented, filled with frustration and despair, and they only dispersed at the first crow of the rooster. He finally got to sleep peacefully and did not wake up until noon. His relatives were worried about some other reason and sent someone to climb over the wall and unlock the door to reach his bed. They saw him sound asleep and called him awake. The protagonist opened his eyes and said, "Are you all here to see the peaceful father and mother?" They recounted the story, and congratulated him together. The protagonist also boasted that he would become a prefect and that he was safe. He often told his close friends, "If I become a magistrate, I will be in charge of criminal affairs, and someone will be in charge of general administration. My eldest brother is only suitable for entertaining guests." But he was repeatedly disqualified in exams, and by the time he passed forty, he was still a student. He was very frustrated, so he left to visit relatives, who introduced him to a job in the capital, and after completing his term, he was appointed as the magistrate of Taiping County.

    503

    有扬州菜佣刘大者，负贩渡江。至江心风浪大作，渡船颠覆，与二十余人沉入江底。大于昏晕之中闻唱名查点声，不能开目视。未几点完，似有神问何多一人？有来提验者高声报曰：“此南部中人，不合死此。”神曰：“可送之登岸。”觉有推之者。张目，已在瓜州滩上卧矣。徐起走回，每问人南部系何官职。时前明万历间也，或曰：“南部自尚书以下郎官俱全，汝非读书人，那得至此？”又一人曰：“习武可作兵部差官，亦部中人也。”大闻甚喜，然无力应试，与戚友谋，共助之，得入营充伍。大甚奋勉，诸差向前，不稍退诿，武弁甚喜之。其时扬郡遭大火后，营师议设救火，兵弁举大为兵目，食双分粮，意谓官有望矣，不胜欢忭。一日，商家失火，刘大率众持挠钩往救，弁命升屋锯梁。梁断，刘大坠入火焚死。

    Explanation

    503

    A vegetable vendor named Liu Da from Yangzhou carried goods across the river. When he reached the middle of the river, a fierce storm arose, and the boat capsized, causing him and over twenty others to sink to the bottom of the river. In his state of unconsciousness, Liu Da heard the sound of names being called and checked, but he could not open his eyes to see. Before the roll call was completed, it seemed as if a deity asked why there was one extra person. A person came to investigate and loudly reported, "This is a person from the southern region, who should not have died here." The deity said, "You can send him back to the shore." Liu Da felt someone pushing him. He opened his eyes and found himself lying on the beach of Guazhou. He slowly got up and walked back, often asking others about the status of the person from the southern region. It was during the Wanli period of the Ming Dynasty. Someone said, "The southern region has officials from the level of Shangshu down to the rank of Langguan, but you are not a scholar, how did you get here?" Another person said, "One can become a military official through martial training, and you are also a person from the Ministry." Liu Da was very pleased to hear this, but lacking the means to take an exam, he sought help from relatives and friends, and together they helped him join the military. Liu Da was very diligent and took on all tasks without shirking. The military officers were very pleased with him. At that time, Yangzhou had suffered a great fire, and the military commander proposed setting up a firefighting team. The officers recommended Liu Da for a military position, and he received double rations, thinking that he might have a chance of becoming an official, which made him very happy. One day, a merchant's house caught fire, and Liu Da led a group with hooks to rescue the situation. The officer ordered him to climb onto the roof and cut the beam. The beam broke, and Liu Da fell into the fire and was burned to death.

    504

    芗厈曰：由上三则观之，可见定命之理竟无可动移也。乃前明袁了凡先生有《立命篇》曰：先生以皇极数推算，命该廪贡，得尹无子，寿止五十三。遇云谷禅师授以功过格、准提咒。一意行善，至一万六千余条。登科发甲，官县令，且有子，寿至七旬余。然则趋吉避凶，凿然有据。祸福自己求之，岂妄言哉？吾愿世之君子，不可如前三人之为数缚，当效了凡先生修身立命，超出数外，岂非豪杰之士乎？

    Explanation

    504

    Xiang Yan said: From observing the three cases above, it is clear that the principle of fate is ultimately unchangeable. Yet, during the Ming dynasty, Mr. Yuan Luofan wrote The Discourse on Self-Making One’s Fate, stating: Mr. Yuan used the Huajing (Imperial Astrology) to calculate his fate, which dictated that he should be a grain officer, have no sons, and live to fifty-three. He met Master Yun Gu, who taught him the Gongguo Ge (Book of Merits and Demerits) and the Dizhi Zou (Dizhi Mantra). He dedicated himself to doing good deeds, totaling over 16,000. He passed the imperial examinations, became a county magistrate, had a son, and lived to over seventy. Therefore, seeking good fortune and avoiding misfortune is clearly based on evidence. Good fortune and misfortune are sought by oneself; how can this be mere empty talk? I wish that the gentlemen of the world do not become bound by fate like the three people above, but should emulate Mr. Yuan Luofan in cultivating themselves and making their own fate, transcending the bounds of fate. Are they not true heroes?

    505

    一技养生
    　　
    　　吾乡有钟生者，业医。无人延请，偃蹇不堪。乃赴豫投亲戚之出仕者。其戚系闲曹，又未补缺，钟依之糊口而已。六月间，豫抚太夫人疾急，医皆束手。属员来见，必问有南医否，其戚以钟对。中丞命即召之，其戚谆嘱小心，钟唯唯而去。中丞延入卧室，见窗户皆闭，大生炉火，热不可耐。太夫人犹衣棉，且覆以棉衾。钟诊之，无脉。出谓中丞曰：“太夫人阳伏于内，阴见于外。当此大暑之时，必先去火开窗，并去棉衾，则脉必复。脉复乃可定方也。”中丞曰：“前医无言及此者，先生高明。”乃从之。未几，太夫人索饮，中丞大悦曰：“我母不言已二日矣，今依先生法，竟大苏醒。”复请诊视。钟诊之得脉，以藿香正气汤饮之，立痊。太夫人悦，命留在署，欲官之。为之报捐未入流，奏留豫省，充文巡捕。中丞言听计从，因此获财无算。

    Explanation

    505

    A Skill for Nourishing Life

    In my hometown, there was a man named Zhong who practiced medicine. Without anyone calling him to treat patients, he felt dejected and unable to bear it. So, he went to Henan to seek refuge with relatives who were in official positions. His relative held a low-ranking office and had not yet been appointed to fill a vacancy; Zhong relied on him for a living. In June, the mother of the governor of Henan fell seriously ill, and all the doctors were at a loss. When subordinates came to see, they always asked if there was a southern physician, and his relative mentioned Zhong. The governor ordered him to be summoned immediately. His relative earnestly instructed him to be careful, and Zhong nodded and left. The governor invited him into the bedroom and saw that all the windows were closed, with a large fire burning inside, making the room unbearably hot. The governor's mother was still wearing cotton clothing and covered with a cotton quilt. Zhong examined her and found no pulse. He went out and told the governor, "The governor's mother has yang energy suppressed internally and yin energy excessively present externally. At this time of great heat, it is essential to first remove the fire and open the windows, and to take off the cotton quilt. Only then will the pulse return, and only then can a proper prescription be determined." The governor said, "None of the previous doctors mentioned this, Mr. Zhong, you are truly wise." He followed Zhong's advice. Soon, the governor's mother asked for water, and the governor was greatly pleased, saying, "My mother hasn't spoken for two days now, but following Mr. Zhong's method, she has suddenly become much more awake." He then asked Zhong to examine her again. Zhong examined her and found the pulse, and he gave her the formula of Huoxiang Zhengqi Tang (Huo-fragrant Qi-Regulating Decoction) to drink, and she was immediately cured. The governor's mother was pleased and ordered Zhong to stay in the office, intending to appoint him to an official position. They arranged for him to donate money to purchase a low-ranking official title, and he was petitioned to remain in Henan Province, serving as a clerk or secretary. The governor followed his every word and decision without question, and thus Zhong amassed wealth without limit.

    506

    又张生系鹾商子，一无所长，惟好口腹。广搜古今食谱而准酌之，烹调甚精。未几父死业败，室人交谪，暂避武林，寓吕祖阁。阁前树木茂盛，有亭纳凉甚适。阁之左有大宅，系候补太守公馆，其仆从暇即来亭内坐谈，以是识张。数日间，闻太守盛怒，鞭扑家人，骂詈无已。其仆亦有时来，皆楚囚相对，非复从前畅快矣。张偶询之，一仆告曰：“主人蜀之富族，纳资得官。所好者精馔，有得意庖人偕来，日前中暑而死。主命我辈觅人，不如意则挞举荐者。连挞多人，皆不敢引进矣。又詈我等无良心，必欲饿死主人也，故嚷嚷不绝。不意偌大郡城，寻不出一善庖者。我辈命该绝矣。”同辈皆咨嗟叹息。

    Explanation

    506

    Ouyang Sheng, the son of a salt merchant, had no particular talents but loved food. He widely collected ancient and modern recipes and adjusted them according to his taste, making his cooking very skillful. Soon after, his father died and the business went bankrupt, and his wife and others criticized him. He temporarily took refuge in Wulin (Hangzhou), staying at the Lu Dongbin Hall. The trees in front of the hall were lush and abundant, and there was a pavilion that was very suitable for cooling off. To the left of the hall stood a large mansion, which was the residence of a governor-in-waiting. The servants of the governor would come to the pavilion to chat when they had free time, and thus Ouyang Sheng became acquainted with them. Within a few days, he heard that the governor was in a rage, beating his family members and continuously cursing and scolding them. The servants would sometimes come, all looking dejected and dispirited, no longer as carefree as before. Ouyang Sheng asked one of the servants about it, who replied, "Our master is a wealthy family from Shu who became an official by paying money. He loves fine cuisine and brought a skilled cook with him, who died of heatstroke the other day. The master ordered us to find someone, and if we were not satisfied, he would beat the recommender. He beat many people in succession, and none dared to recommend anyone. He also cursed us for being heartless, saying he must want to starve the master to death, so he continuously shouted and complained. Unexpectedly, in such a large city, we could not find one skilled cook. Our fate is sealed." His fellow servants all expressed sighs and lamentations.

    507

    张微晒曰：“我以为有大事也，故如此盛怒。区区者何地无之，君等诚不善觅耳。”仆曰：“然则先生能之乎？”张曰：“未识汝主果知味否？”众仆皆喜曰：“我等姑耐一顿皮鞭，请试为之。”乃具应用之物交张，成四簋。夕飧进之，其香始升，不觉触鼻。太守曰：“何来此味？”试尝而甘之，大啖饱食。呼仆问所从来。仆告之故，立命传见。张曰：“我非庖人，因图馆寓此，行将归矣。岂为人作厨下媪哉？”仆复命。太守曰：“客何能，欲修金若干？肯为我庖人指点，我必延之。汝等善为我词，不可失此人也。”仆又往商，张曰：“汝主必欲留我，岁修三百金，亲来聘请，为司帐房兼督庖厨可也。”

    Explanation

    507

    Zhang Wei smiled and said, "I thought there was a big matter, so I was so furious. Such things are everywhere; you all are really bad at finding them." The servant replied, "Then can you do it, sir?" Zhang said, "I don't know if your master really knows the taste." The servants all rejoiced and said, "We will endure a few lashes of the whip and try it." They prepared the necessary ingredients and handed them to Zhang, who made four dishes. For dinner, they served the dishes, and the aroma began to rise, unconsciously touching the nose. The magistrate said, "Where did this flavor come from?" He tried it and found it delicious, eating heartily and becoming full. He called for the servants to ask where it came from. The servants explained the reason, and the magistrate immediately ordered them to summon Zhang. Zhang said, "I am not a cook; I am staying here temporarily in the library and am about to leave. Would I work as a kitchen maid for someone?" The servant reported back. The magistrate said, "What are your skills, and how much money do you want? If you are willing to instruct my cooks, I will definitely hire you. You all should speak well for me, don't lose this person." The servant went back to negotiate, and Zhang said, "Your master must want to keep me; pay me 300 gold pieces annually, and I will personally oversee the accounts and supervise the kitchen."

    508

    众仆惟恐失之，急为办行李衣装复命。太守往拜，订交，一一如约，相得甚欢。未几，太守得缺，张为司总，加修至千金。因此起家。
    　　
    　　又有妇人善哭者，无端发声，闻者泪下。南俗富贵之家凡有丧，吊客来不绝，内帏须终日哭。主妇力不逮，或荐此妇代哭，能日夜不绝声。吊者闻其哀甚，佥称主妇孝。于是有丧之家争延之，通邑无二人。故鲜暇日，亦得小康。

    Explanation

    508

    The servants, fearing to miss the opportunity, hastily prepared the travel gear and clothing and reported back. The prefect went to pay respects, established a friendship, and everything went as agreed, and they got along very well. Soon after, the prefect obtained a vacancy, and Zhang became the head of the office, with his salary increased to a thousand gold. As a result, he rose to prominence.

    There is also a woman who is good at crying, who unprovokedly makes a sound, and those who hear her cry. In the southern customs, wealthy families, whenever there is a death, have mourning guests keep coming, and the women in the inner chamber must cry all day. The mistress cannot do it herself, so she recommends this woman to cry on her behalf, and she can cry day and night without stopping. The mourners hear her crying and find it very sad, and all say the mistress is filial. Therefore, families with a death compete to hire her, and there is no one else in the entire city. As a result, she has few days off, but she also becomes moderately well-off.

    509

    芗厈曰：人既不能上达，必习一长一技，以为仰事俯育之资。否则，妄想求财，我不知渠何所藉。或曰：前三人亦命应如此，不然，饱学秀才埋没在蒙馆中者不知凡几。一长一技，更何足道哉？余曰：如君言误人不浅，若无所凭藉而待命，天其雨金雨粟乎？非所以励中人也。
    　　
    　　昔有袜匠，业甚兴隆，晚年得子爱如珍宝。年将及冠，犹不使学事业。亲友皆劝之，匠曰：“有命存也。”故人皆戏呼之曰袜公子。时有推五星者曰张铁口，名卓卓。匠使推其子命，铁口曰：“此大富造也。行年三十，家资五百万。我在贵邑推命多矣，无出其右者。”书单与之。匠大悦，归使其妻以锦作囊，纳单其中，悬于其子胸前曰：“无忘发财之年。”且以夸示亲友，益任其怠惰矣。为其子完姻后，匠夫妇相继亡。其子不能自立，所遗袜肆为其伙抵盗净尽，渐至夫妇相携行乞。

    Explanation

    509

    Xiang Yan said: People cannot reach higher realms on their own, so they must learn one skill or trade to provide for the support of their parents and the nurturing of their children. Otherwise, to foolishly seek wealth, I do not know what he relies on. Someone said: The previous three people were also fated to be like this; otherwise, there are countless well-learned scholars who remain obscure in primary schools. One skill or trade, what is there to talk about? I say: Your words mislead people greatly. If one relies on fate without any support, will heaven rain gold and grain? It is not a way to encourage ordinary people.

    Once there was a sock maker whose business was very prosperous. In his old age, he had a son whom he loved like a treasure. When his son was nearing adulthood, he still did not allow him to learn a trade. Relatives and friends all advised him, but the sock maker said, "There is a destiny at work." Therefore, people all jokingly called him "Socks Prince." At that time, there was a fortune-teller known as Iron Mouth, who was very famous. The sock maker asked him to predict his son's fate. Iron Mouth said, "This is a chart indicating great wealth. At the age of thirty, with assets worth five million. I have predicted many fortunes in this town, and none surpass this one." He gave him the chart. The sock maker was very pleased and returned home, where he had his wife make a silk bag, put the chart inside, and hang it around his son's neck, saying, "Do not forget the year of wealth." And to show off to relatives and friends, he became even more complacent and lazy. After arranging his son's marriage, the sock maker and his wife died one after another. His son could not support himself, and the sock shop he left behind was completely stolen by his partners. Gradually, they began to beg together.

    510

    惟日盼而立之年作富翁矣。至二十九岁，适遇大饥，人人不能自给，谁肯济丐。其人饿病于枯庙中将毙，忿谓其妻曰：“我不济矣，我之不习一业，以至此者，皆张铁口误我也。汝年少艾，不患无温饱日。我死后，汝号于市日，有能棺殓前夫者嫁之。谅必有人承值，殓时必以命单纳棺中，我将控于冥司，为妄谈命者戒。”遂殁。其妻如言改嫁而葬埋之。其人之魂见阎罗王，诉其冤苦，王为追铁口至，究之，铁口曰：“小人推命从无谬误，恐其八字不准，非小人之过矣。”王使判查降生簿，则其命运与单符合。

    Explanation

    510

    Only by waiting until the age of thirty to become a wealthy man. By the age of twenty-nine, he happened to encounter a great famine. Everyone could not support themselves, who would help a beggar? The person became ill from hunger in a dilapidated temple and was about to die. He angrily told his wife, "I am done for, it is because I did not learn a profession, and all of this is due to the false predictions of Zhang Iron Mouth. You are young and beautiful, you will not lack for warmth and food. After my death, you should call out in the market, whoever can bury my former husband will marry you. Surely someone will agree to do it, and when burying me, they will place my life prediction document in the coffin. I will sue Zhang Iron Mouth in the underworld, to serve as a warning to those who falsely predict destiny." He then died. His wife followed his words and remarried, burying him. The soul of that person saw King Yama and complained about his grievances. The king ordered Zhang Iron Mouth to be brought before him and investigated it. Zhang Iron Mouth said, "I have never made mistakes in predicting fate. Perhaps his birth chart is incorrect. It is not my fault." The king ordered the judge to check the birth records, and found that his destiny matched the prediction.

    511

    王曰：“如此其财何在？”判又查应富簿，曰：“某应以贸易起家，已于降生之年交招财，利市二神矣。”王释铁口，使鬼卒押其人问二神，曰：“有之，某应以二十岁外渐成家业。吾神在三百六十行买卖中，查无其人，无从给付，恐其误习文学，则非吾所能主。已于某年月日送文帝去矣。”又押赴文昌宫，朱衣神曰：“有之，收财之日曾禀明帝君，请以数万金，准作科甲，出宰一方，以余财付之。乃历科以来，魁星在南北大小文场中查无其人，恐误习武，于某年月日送武帝去矣。”又押赴关帝庙，周将军曰：“有之，吾奉命巡武场，并无其人，恐误其发财之日，遂交转轮王处矣。

    Explanation

    511

    The king said, "Where is such wealth?" The judge then checked the register of wealth and said, "He should have started a family through trade, and on the day of his birth, he was handed over to the deities of wealth and prosperity." The king released the oracle and had the person escorted to ask the two deities. They said, "Indeed, he should have gradually built a family business after the age of twenty. We gods, in all trades and professions, found no one to pay, and we were concerned that he might mistakenly pursue literature, which is not within our jurisdiction. On a certain date, we sent him to the God of Literature." They then escorted him to the palace of the God of Literature, where the red-robed deity said, "Indeed, on the day he received wealth, we informed the God of Literature. We granted him a sum of several million gold coins as a reward for passing the imperial examinations and being appointed as an official in a region, with the remainder given to him. Since the beginning of the examinations, the God of Literature has found no one in all levels of literary examinations, and we were concerned that he might mistakenly pursue martial arts. On a certain date, we sent him to the God of War." They then escorted him to the temple of Guan Yu, where the general said, "Indeed, I was ordered to patrol the martial arts examinations, and I found no one. We were concerned that he might miss the day of his wealth, so we handed him over to the Wheel-Turning King."

    512

    ”又押至第十殿，王命判检簿曰：“有之，因其人既不习文武，又不习商贾，无从给发，不得已饬交当方土地，埋藏其家，使掘地得之甚易。今犹未得，是土地之过也，请追问之。”乃召土地，曰：“小神领有此银，知其人已流落枯庙，即以其银埋在庙阶之下，无奈其人从不动土，且未曾扫地，欲雨给之，恐其不知暂避，误伤性命。正无法可施，今既来此，原财奉缴，以脱小神之累也。”王曰：“嗟乎，天下竟有如此怠惰之人，神亦不能福之，使其为人也，实害之也。然某前世之福泽尚在，无已，判作富贵家猫，眠锦绣而食膏粱，毋庸自力。且所见之财，亦千百万也。”故人而无能，不如为畜。

    Explanation

    512

    The person was brought to the Tenth Court, where the King of Hell ordered the examination of the records. "There is a record of this person," he said. "The person neither studies literature nor martial arts, nor does he engage in commerce. Unable to earn a living, he was ordered to be handed over to the local land deity, who buried his wealth in his home, making it easy to obtain by digging. Yet, the wealth has not been found, which is the fault of the land deity. Please investigate further." The King then summoned the land deity, saying, "I have this wealth, and I know the person has fallen into a dilapidated temple. I buried the wealth under the temple steps, but the person never digs, nor does he sweep the floor. I wanted to give it to him in the rain, but I feared he would not know to take shelter temporarily and might be injured. With no other options, I am here now, returning the original wealth to free myself from trouble." The King sighed, "Alas, there are such idle people in the world that even the gods cannot bless them. However, the person's good fortune from a past life still exists. Let him be judged as a cat in a wealthy family, sleeping on brocade and eating rich food, without the need to exert himself. Moreover, the wealth he sees is also in the millions." Thus, a person who is incapable is better off as an animal.

    513

    第五卷
    　　
    　　六壬神课
    　　
    　　吾乡张君，鹾商子也。幼多病，故不甚读书，然敏甚，具慧解，书旨皆能领悟。及冠，自觉气禀甚弱，不愿婚娶。一日，在朋友案头见《大六壬》书，悦之求教。友曰：“我虽知此，不甚精。”遂以大略指示之。张携归学习，不忍释手，复购求他本以为揣摹。闻有秘本，不惜重价，不畏远道，务罗而致之。不售者，亲往手录。是以其书盈室，多人所不经见者。研精十载，忽大悟曰：“道不远人，非书所能该。”进束书不观，亦不肯为人决事。有时自露其机，则无不中。

    Explanation

    513

    Fifth Volume

    The Six Ren Divine Lesson

    My hometown's Mr. Zhang, the son of a salt merchant, was often ill as a child and did not read much. However, he was very intelligent and possessed wisdom and understanding, comprehending the essence of books. When he came of age, he felt his constitution was very weak and did not wish to marry. One day, while at a friend's desk, he saw the Da Liu Ren book and was pleased, seeking to learn. His friend said, "I know this, but not very well." Then he gave him a brief overview. Mr. Zhang took it home to study and could not put it down. He bought other versions to study and emulate. Hearing of secret versions, he spared no expense, feared no distance, and made sure to obtain them. If they were not for sale, he would personally go and transcribe them. So his room was filled with books, many of which were unseen by others. He studied intensely for ten years, suddenly having a great realization and saying, "The Way is not far from people; it cannot be fully encompassed by books." He stopped reading books and also refused to make decisions for others. Sometimes when he revealed his methods, they were always accurate.

    514

    忽谓兄嫂曰：“此宅住不得矣，速往某亲戚家借寓，犹可及也。”兄曰：“宅乃祖遗，居此百余年，丰衣足食，人口平安，有何不美，而欲依亲戚家，不为人非笑耶？且迁宅不易，汝勿多言。”再三恳之，兄嫂执不可，张乃哀求其母曰：“十日不迁，儿为大不孝子。必见责于天而受祸也。”其母夙爱怜之，见惶迫之状出于至诚，谕长子迁居。迫于母命，往觅其戚假宅。戚果非笑之。然其家宅广人稀，乐亲戚之情话，允之。张君迫促速移，上下皆有怨言，若不闻也，督催益急。至九日，母尚在旧宅督理，张突负而趋，言勿惊老母者再。甫至戚家，喘息未定，人报左邻火发，延及张宅，顷刻荡然。而张氏之器用财贿无伤也。然后怨者德之。
    　　
    　　母与兄曰：“何不先言？”张曰：“天机不可预泄，然与先言何异耶？”

    Explanation

    514

    Suddenly, Zhang said to his elder brother and sister-in-law, "This house can no longer be lived in. Hurry to a certain relative's home to temporarily stay; it's still in time." His brother replied, "The house is an inheritance from our ancestors. We've lived here for over a hundred years, with abundant clothing and food, and our family members are safe and healthy. What is not good about it? Why would we want to rely on relatives' homes and be ridiculed by others? Besides, moving is not easy. You should not talk too much." Zhang repeatedly urged them, but his brother and sister-in-law insisted on not agreeing. Zhang then pleaded with his mother, "If we do not move within ten days, I will be a very unfilial son. I will surely be blamed by heaven and suffer misfortune." His mother, who always loved and pitied him, saw his earnestness in his anxious and desperate state and ordered the eldest son to move. Compelled by his mother's command, Zhang went to find a relative's house to borrow. The relatives indeed laughed at him. However, the house was large but sparsely populated, and they enjoyed the intimate conversation with relatives, so they agreed. Zhang hurriedly urged a quick move, and everyone above and below had complaints, as if not hearing, he urged more urgently. By the ninth day, the mother was still at the old house overseeing. Zhang suddenly carried her and rushed, telling her not to be startled by the old mother twice. Just arrived at the relative's house, breathing was not yet steady, when someone reported that a fire broke out at the left neighbor's house, spreading to Zhang's house, in an instant, completely destroyed. But Zhang's household items and wealth were unharmed. Afterward, those who had complained praised him.

    The mother and brother said, "Why didn't you say it earlier?" Zhang replied, "The heavenly secret cannot be revealed in advance. But what is the difference from saying it earlier?"

    515

    一日，至其表兄王生家道贺。王曰：“无喜可贺。”张曰：“老兄长郎今科举孝廉，非大喜耶？”王曰：“弟言谅无谬。既来道贺，必得饮食。家中猝不及备，请往市肆可乎？”张曰可。同往至市，途遇一友，王邀偕往。入肆，饮毕送面来。张曰：“两碗足矣。何必三？”王曰：“三人也，岂可两？”张曰：“一人不得食也。”皆举箸笑曰：“今亦有错谬时乎？”言次，友家人来报，其母痰作几危，请速归。视友乃投箸而去。王曰：“弟毋乃仙乎？我等饭后速往友家探之。”张曰：“无伤，其母痧发，刻已愈矣。”食毕，余一碗，皆饱不能食。张曰：“卖之可也。”王曰：“何人肯买剩面？”张假笔书条曰：“为官事见官面，虎头人食此面。”王乃嘱肆主曰：“为我卖此面。”肆主笑诺之。果有一人头汗淋漓入座，急索凉面，肆主即以剩面与之，甚得。王徐问曰：“君高姓，何如此急急也？”其人曰：“我虞姓，为役所迫往见官，故需凉面。”速食而去。

    Explanation

    515

    One day, Zhang went to his cousin Wang Sheng's house to pay a visit and congratulate him. Wang said, "There is nothing to congratulate on." Zhang replied, "Isn't it a great joy that your eldest son has been selected as a xiao-lian in this year's examination?" Wang said, "My words are surely true. Since you have come to congratulate, you must be offered food. Our home is not prepared, so please allow us to go to the market, may we?" Zhang agreed. They went to the market together, and on the way, they met a friend. Wang invited him to join them. Upon entering the shop, after drinking, they were served noodles. Zhang said, "Two bowls are enough. Why three?" Wang said, "There are three of us, how can there be only two?" Zhang said, "One person cannot eat." They all picked up their chopsticks and laughed, "Even now, are there times when mistakes occur?" Just then, the friend's family came to report that his mother was having a severe cough and was almost in danger, asking him to return quickly. Seeing this, the friend put down his chopsticks and left. Wang said, "Is your brother not a bit of a magician? After we finish eating, let's go to your friend's house to check on him." Zhang said, "It's not a problem, his mother had a sudden illness, but she has already recovered." After finishing their meal, there was one bowl left, but they were all full and could not eat more. Zhang said, "Let's sell it." Wang said, "Who would buy leftover noodles?" Zhang wrote a note with a borrowed pen, saying, "For official matters, see the official's face; a tiger-headed person eats this noodle." Wang then instructed the shopkeeper, "Sell this noodle for me." The shopkeeper laughed and agreed. Soon, a person with sweat dripping from his head entered and hurriedly asked for cold noodles. The shopkeeper immediately gave him the leftover noodles, and he was very satisfied. Wang asked slowly, "What is your surname, and why are you so rushed?" The person said, "I am surnamed Yu, and I am being forced by duty to go see an official, so I need cold noodles." He quickly ate and left.

    516

    于是二人偕往友家。友出迎，问其母果发痧，绝而复苏。家人皆外出请医，二人渴甚，无人烹茶，张曰：“厨有大柿二，亦足解渴。”发寻之果得，分食二人而去。是年，王生子应大比归，往候。张君曰：“侄今果中式矣。我有一物遗君。”出匣，封志其固，曰：“捧归悬之，榜发后启视。不可预发，致我与君皆速祸也。”王敬谨携归，榜发拆视，内贮全榜一纸，报捷者来出录，比对无一误者。

    Explanation

    516

    At this time, the two of them went together to their friend's house. The friend came out to greet them and asked if the mother had indeed had a heat stroke, and she had fainted but then revived. The family members had all gone out to call a doctor, and the two of them were very thirsty, with no one to make tea. Zhang said, "There are two large persimmons in the kitchen, which should also quench our thirst." They found them, and the two of them shared and ate them before leaving. That year, Wang returned from taking the imperial examinations after having a son. Wang went to visit Zhang. Zhang said, "My nephew has indeed passed the exams. I have something to leave with you." He took out a box, sealed it securely, and said, "Take it back and hang it up. After the results are announced, open it and look inside. Do not open it prematurely, as it could bring misfortune to both of us." Wang respectfully carried it back, and after the results were announced, he opened it and looked inside. Inside was a complete list of the results, and the person who reported the success came out to record it. When compared, there was not a single error.

    517

    未几，张君疾作日甚，表兄卢翁虽市井中人，而朴诚方正，来视疾曰：“惜弟天生才智，不习正业，用心于无益之地，耗损心血成此危症，亦自悔乎？”张笑曰：“命之修短，天也。知数固死，不知亦死。与其昏昏，何如昭昭耶？”卢曰：“人虽传弟知未来事，是或可信，岂能洞见肺腑？”张曰：“弟请为兄决之，兄稍回避。”乃执笔操算，作单以匣封固，谓卢曰：“兄携回，晚开之。”卢如其言，归肆贸易，夜核帐后开封，则是日出入总帐一纸，厘毫不误，卢乃服，来谓张曰：“弟能如是，岂非仙乎？何以仙亦有疾，是所不解。”张曰：“仙则不能，惟六通已得其二，惜知之晚，而又自执其能，不得精进，以结内丹。天乎！假我数年，即成道矣。无如数尽于某月日。从此长别，不亦痛哉。”相向而泣，至日果卒。

    Explanation

    517

    Not long after, Zhang Jun's illness worsened day by day. His cousin, Lu Weng, though a common man from the marketplace, was honest, sincere, and upright. He came to visit and said, "It is a pity that you, with your natural intelligence, did not study the proper profession and devoted your heart to places of no benefit, wasting your vitality and falling into this serious condition. Do you regret it?" Zhang smiled and replied, "The length of one's life is determined by heaven. Whether one knows their fate or not, death is inevitable. Is it better to be ignorant or enlightened?" Lu said, "People have heard that you know future events, which may be true, but can you truly see deeply into the innermost parts?" Zhang said, "I will decide for you, brother. Please step aside for a moment." He took a pen and began to calculate, writing down figures and sealing them in a box. He told Lu, "Take this back and open it tonight." Lu did as he was told, returned to his shop to trade, and after closing the accounts at night, opened the box. Inside was a detailed ledger of that day's transactions, accurate to the smallest unit, with no errors. Lu was convinced and came back to Zhang, saying, "If you can do this, are you not a god? How can even gods have illnesses? This is what I cannot understand." Zhang said, "I am not a god, but I have attained two of the six supernatural abilities. It is a pity that I learned of them too late, and being overly confident in my abilities, I could not make further progress in cultivating the inner elixir. Heaven! If only I had a few more years, I would have achieved enlightenment. Alas, my days are numbered for a certain date. From now on, a long farewell, is it not painful?" They wept together, and on that day, Zhang indeed passed away.

    518

    芗厈曰：书自圣经至杂艺，载当然而不载所以然。惟博闻强记，则左右逢源，一旦豁然贯通矣，小道可观，其张君之谓乎。然由一艺而追其极，皆道也。所患者自执其能而止，此张君之所以悔乎？
    　　
    　　文孝廉
    　　
    　　有陈孝廉者，家甚寒。贷亲友数十金入都会试，独行至王家营，以三十金雇车。两骡甚膘壮，车夫目立眉扬，挺胸凸肚，亦甚勇健。陈喜，意谓能速驰也。不意车夫横甚，甫入山东境，车价支讫。宿第三站时，入陈君室坐，借十金，如不给不能前。陈曰：“此地距都千有余里，安得多金济汝？果不能前，还我原价，另雇可也。”车夫曰：“长装短卸，向有行规。休想毫厘也，我不惯与小人乘，请辞。”商声纷争，陈君泣下。

    Explanation

    518

    Xiang Tan said: Books range from sacred scriptures to miscellaneous arts, recording what should be done but not why. Only through extensive knowledge and strong memory can one find sources on both sides and suddenly gain a clear understanding. Even a small path has its value, is it not referring to Zhang Jun? However, pursuing the ultimate in any art, all lead to the Dao. The concern is that one stops at their own abilities, this is why Zhang Jun regrets it.

    As for the Scholar-Official Chen, his family was very poor. He borrowed tens of gold from relatives and friends to go to the capital for the imperial examinations, traveling alone to Wangjia Camp, and hired a cart with thirty gold. The two mules were very strong and healthy, and the cart driver had a proud demeanor, standing tall and broad-shouldered, also very brave and strong. Chen was pleased, thinking that he could travel quickly. Unfortunately, the cart driver was very arrogant, as soon as they entered Shandong, the fare was paid. At the third station, the driver entered Chen's room and demanded ten gold, threatening not to proceed if it was not given. Chen said, "This place is more than a thousand miles from the capital, how could I have so much gold to help you? If you cannot proceed, return my original payment, and I will hire another cart." The driver said, "There are established rules for long and short distances. Don't even think about it. I am not accustomed to riding with a small person, please excuse me." The argument became heated, and Chen cried.

    519

    是室逼近上房，所寓亦会试新孝廉，大车五六辆，主仆十余人，方唤妓雅歌倩酒，闻攘攘声使仆询问。车夫曰：“我等算帐，何预汝事？”陈乃实诉所苦。仆入主出，携陈君去，叙科分，同年也。同行三孝廉，此君文姓，为主，谓陈曰：“兄独行踽踽，宜为小人所侮。我闻车夫甚横，当教戒之。”立呼车夫入曰：“汝何敢与客争？”车夫曰：“小人贫困无聊，故为人仆，不过向客假数金耳，不意其肆咆哮也。”文孝廉曰：“果为是，我假汝十金，明日随帮早行，不许再有后言矣。”车夫唯唯。五鼓开行，使陈君车在文前。

    Explanation

    519

    This room was close to the main house, and its occupants were new candidates who had passed the provincial examination. There were five or six large carriages, and a group of more than ten people, including masters and servants, who were enjoying the company of courtesans, singing elegantly, and drinking wine. They heard a commotion and sent a servant to inquire. The carriage driver said, "We are settling accounts. What business is it of yours?" Chen then truthfully complained about his troubles. The servant entered, and the master came out, taking Chen with him. They discussed their examination ranks and discovered that they were colleagues from the same year. Three successful candidates were traveling together, and this gentleman, surnamed Wen, was the leader. He told Chen, "Brother, traveling alone and feeling lonely, you are likely to be bullied by common people. I heard that the carriage driver is very arrogant. I will teach him a lesson." He immediately called the carriage driver inside and said, "How dare you quarrel with the guests?" The driver replied, "I am poor and have nothing to do, so I serve as a driver. I only borrowed some money from the guests, and I did not expect them to shout at me." Wen said, "If that is the case, I will lend you ten gold coins. Tomorrow, you must leave early with the group, and I will not allow any further complaints." The driver agreed. At the fifth drumbeat, they departed, with Chen's carriage leading the way.

    520

    行不过二十里，车夫趋狭路疾驰，陈觉有异，始则喝问，继以哀求，车夫曰：“今日之事我为政，汝尚有同年友相助耶？”时文孝廉仆见陈车斜驰去，告主人。文目：“是不怀好意。”疾跃出车，解骖乘健骡，不鞍而驰之。车夫有觉追者，奋力鞭两骡。而文驰已及，以一足踹车辕，两骡寸步不能移，以一手提车夫掷地，飞身踏其背，夺鞭，鞭之数百。车夫哀号求恕，陈君下车为之缓颊，而后释之，使陈还坐。而己坐辕上，押送回大路。谓车夫曰：“我虽文士，而习武功，无论汝一人二骡，能止之不足奇。汝观我以两手倒挽五六套大车，可使逆行。”试之果然。车夫咋舌，始畏惧不敢荫异志。相随入都，还文孝廉所借之银而去。陈君感甚，曰：“兄神力无敌于天下，生成乎？抑练习乎？”文曰：“此《易筋经》法也，其书具在，勉习之无所不可，人自不为耳。”从此相得甚欢，遂成莫逆。

    Explanation

    520

    As they traveled no more than twenty li, the coachman rushed through a narrow road at high speed. Tchen noticed something amiss and first shouted a question, then begged. The coachman replied, "Today's matter is under my control. Do you still have your fellow students to help you?" At that moment, Wen, the Scholar-official, saw Tchen's carriage veering off course and informed the master. Wen remarked, "He has ill intentions." He quickly leaped out of the carriage, unharnessed a strong mule, and rode it without a saddle. The coachman, sensing someone was pursuing him, lashed the two mules with all his might. Wen, however, had already caught up and kicked the carriage pole with one foot, immobilizing the mules completely. With one hand, he lifted the coachman and threw him to the ground, then leaped onto his back, took the whip, and lashed him hundreds of times. The coachman cried out in pain and begged for mercy. Tchen, seeing the commotion, got out of the carriage to intercede on the coachman's behalf, and Wen eventually released him, allowing Tchen to return to his seat. Wen then sat on the carriage pole and escorted them back to the main road. He told the coachman, "Although I am a scholar, I am also skilled in martial arts. Whether it's you or the two mules, stopping them is not remarkable. Watch as I reverse a cart with five or six horses with both hands." He demonstrated it, and indeed, it worked. The coachman was tongue-tied and began to fear, no longer harboring any ill intentions. They accompanied each other to the capital, where Wen returned the silver he had borrowed from Wen, the Scholar-official. Tchen was very grateful and said, "Your divine strength is unmatched in the world. Is it innate or through practice?" Wen replied, "This is the method of Yi Jin Jing. The book is here. If you practice it, there is nothing you cannot do. People just don't do it." From then on, they became inseparable friends.

    521

    芗厈曰：此入道之大端也。夫道，无所不可。养气，则至大至刚；养志，则修齐治平；养身，则合精气神为正一。可以飞升，可以拔宅。即《易筋经》一书，达摩传为入道基，非仅习勇力也。人配天地曰三才，其才本广大无伦，故儒家之明德，道家之三宝，释家之本觉、真心，人人共具，专其一而修之，圣贤仙佛惟人所愿，岂仅勇力乎哉？
    　　
    　　妖人邢大
    　　
    　　燕人邢大，幼失怙恃。年十七，艳丽过好女，因无事业，偃蹇不堪。里有洪大者，家小康。有龙阳之癖，亦无父母妻子。途遇邢，目逆而送之曰：“此天下尤物，可遇而不可求者。”尾至其家，见隤垣败室，虚寂无人，入门唁之。邢见洪来，羞涩之态，亦若女子之初见良人者。洪讯得困苦状，不胜怜悯曰：“弟若肯随至家，我能温饱之。”邢本无能，腆然随去。洪为置鲜衣，给美食，抚养周至，邢实心感。

    Explanation

    521

    Xiang'an said: This is the great principle of entering the Dao. The Dao has no limitations. Nurturing qi leads to the greatest and most robust state; nurturing aspirations leads to self-cultivation, family order, state governance, and world peace; nurturing the body integrates essence, qi, and spirit into a unified whole. One can ascend to immortality or elevate one's entire household. The Yijin Jing (Muscle/Tendon Changing Classic), transmitted by Bodhidharma, is the foundation for entering the Dao, not just for practicing physical strength. Humans, when aligned with heaven and earth, form the Three Powers, whose talents are boundless and incomparable. Therefore, the bright virtue of Confucianism, the Three Treasures of Daoism, and the innate awakening and true mind of Buddhism are all inherent in every person. By focusing on one and cultivating it, one can become a sage, a virtuous person, a deity, or a Buddha, depending on one's wish, not just for physical strength.

    A Demon Person Xing Da

    Xing Da from Yan province, who lost his parents at a young age, was seventeen years old and more beautiful than a good-looking woman. Because he had no career, he was in a state of depression and dissatisfaction. There was a man named Hong Da in the village, who was moderately well-off. He had a fondness for men and also had no parents, wife, or children. He met Xing Da on the road, looked at him intently, and said to him, "This is a rare treasure of the world, one that can be encountered but not sought out." He followed him home, saw dilapidated walls and an empty, quiet house, and entered the house to console him. Xing Da, upon seeing Hong Da, showed a shy and bashful demeanor, as if a woman were meeting her true love for the first time. Hong Da learned of his plight and was deeply moved, saying, "If you will come to my home, I can provide you with warmth and food." Xing Da, who was naturally incapable, shyly followed him. Hong Da provided him with new clothes, good food, and took care of him thoroughly. Xing Da was truly grateful.

    522

    一日饮内室薄醉，邢颜色焕发，洪不能复忍，拥之求欢，邢曰：“弟受兄德泽无以加矣。身非草木，焉得无情？以身报之，固所愿也。但日后色衰爱弛，弟仍落魄无依，徒贻失身之诮，不如其已。”洪曰：“我只图好色，不分牝牡。弟若蓄发披鬟，终身相从，即我妻也。决不再娶，誓无异心。”邢遂与同宿，两情益密。邢从此养发贯耳作旗装，俨然国色，且习女工，针黹刺绣甚巧。洪嬖爱益甚，所欲无不顺从，服饰之珍，饮馔之腴，甲于贵胄。夫好男色者，必病股与目，况旦旦而伐之，有不速毙者乎？三年，洪业渐败，目既眊，而半身不遂矣。

    Explanation

    522

    One day, while Hong was slightly drunk in the inner chamber, Xing's complexion was radiant. Hong could no longer resist and embraced her to seek pleasure. Xing said, "Brother, your kindness has reached its limit. I am not made of wood or grass, how can I be without feelings? To repay you with my body, it is my wish. But if in the future, my beauty fades and your love wanes, I will be destitute and without support, only to be ridiculed for losing my virtue. It would be better to stop." Hong replied, "I only seek beauty, without regard for gender. If you grow your hair and wear a hairpin, to be together forever, you will be my wife. I will never marry again, I swear to have no other thoughts." Xing then slept with him, and their affection became even closer. From then on, she grew her hair and wore a hairpin in the style of a man, appearing to be a national beauty. She also learned needlework, being very skilled at embroidery. Hong's affection for her grew even more, and he desired nothing but to please her. Her clothing was precious, her food was rich, surpassing even the nobility. Those who love men will inevitably suffer from diseases of the hips and eyes, especially if they engage in frequent sexual activity, how can they avoid a quick death? After three years, Hong's business gradually declined, his eyesight became dim, and he became paralyzed on one side.

    523

    先有刘六者，亦美男子。洪与结为昆季，恒引至家与邢相见，则曰：“我妹也。”刘见其娟美，亦爱恋之。洪已有交易之心，而邢不许。故每见刘，则一礼而退，刘亦无可如何。值洪病革，刘愿以重聘婉求其妹为妻。洪与邢谋曰：“我病不能复起矣。今汝已习女装，声容举止宛然好女。本相订终身，不意半途抛撇。若恋我，则无男子守节理，若仍改男装，则已失本来面目，又未习丈夫事业，后作饿殍，皆我累汝矣。汝纵无怨，我在九泉亦不瞑目。不如因刘子之好嫁之。我得财礼可藉以饰终，汝亦得其所矣。”邢曰：“我非真女，彼娶而后觉之，能相容乎？”洪曰：“世无不好色者。彼若觉察，汝须善为调停。溺爱之人，决无偾事。况刘之为人与我相同，我故愿托之也。”邢诺。洪以告，刘遂转告父母，邀媒行聘，择吉娶之。父母亲戚见新妇婉娈柔顺，与其夫一对玉人，交相庆慰，刘更欣喜。

    Explanation

    523

    A Man Named Liu Liu

    There was a man named Liu Liu, who was also a handsome fellow. Hong and he became brothers, often bringing him home to meet with Xing, whom he introduced as his sister. Liu, upon seeing her grace and beauty, fell in love with her. Hong had already developed romantic intentions towards Xing, but Xing did not agree. Therefore, every time Hong saw Liu, he greeted him with a bow and then left, leaving Liu helpless. When Hong was gravely ill, Liu wished to offer a substantial dowry and respectfully request Xing as his wife. Hong and Xing discussed the matter and said, "I am too ill to recover. Now you have learned to dress like a woman, your voice, appearance, and mannerisms are just like a beautiful woman. We originally planned to be together forever, but unexpectedly you left me halfway through. If you love me, it would be unreasonable for a man to remain faithful. If you still change back to male clothes, you will lose your true appearance, and since you haven't learned any man's skills, you might end up starving to death, which would be my fault. Even if you have no resentment, I will not rest in peace in the afterlife. It would be better to marry you off to Liu. I can use the dowry to prepare for my death, and you will find a good place." Xing said, "I am not a real woman. If he marries me and later finds out, will he be able to tolerate me?" Hong replied, "No one in the world doesn't like beauty. If he finds out, you must handle it well. A person who is deeply in love will never cause trouble. Liu's character is similar to mine, so I trust him." Xing agreed. Hong told Liu, who then informed his parents, and they arranged a match, choosing a good day to marry Xing. The parents and relatives saw the new bride as gentle and obedient, and together with her husband, they were a pair of jade people, and they all celebrated and comforted each other, Liu was even more delighted.

    524

    至晚入房，曰：“妹何见我即避，今夜更避何处耶？”拥入衾中。邢早于兜肚下作袋，将肾囊前阳包起，仍曲举其股以臀窍受淫，故不觉也。然日久厮熟，时亦渐热，刘必欲尽去邢之上下衣，强赤其体，无从慢藏，厥物显露。刘不禁骇异，邢拥刘尽媚，而实告之曰：“尔若舍我，恐女子中未必有胜我者。

    Explanation

    524

    By evening, he entered the chamber and said, “Why do you see me and immediately turn away? Where will you hide tonight?” He embraced her into the bedding. Xing had already fashioned a pouch beneath her belly-wrap, wrapping her scrotum and penis, and still bent her legs so that her vaginal opening received the act of intimacy, and thus she remained unaware. However, after a long time together, they became familiar with one another, and at times the intimacy grew warmer. Liu insisted on removing all of Xing’s upper and lower garments, forcing her body into nakedness, leaving her no chance to conceal herself. Her genitals were now exposed. Liu could not help but be startled. Xing embraced Liu with all her charm and, in truth, told him, “If you were to leave me, I fear there may not be a woman in the world who surpasses me.”

    525

    ”刘曰：“我固不忍舍汝，但娶妻为子也，汝能生育乎？况我家不过仅可度日，无余资再娶，不误我后嗣耶？”邢曰：“毋恐，我有祖传符篆，能看香治病。尔倩人绘女仙像供养，我将有仙人附体，治病神效。传播人知，业必兴隆。得财后任置妾媵，不尔禁也。”刘曰：“为我谋则善矣。但汝以男子身而为此，何能忍乎？”邢益媚妩之曰：“此事始虽楚而后乐，恐天下男子知此味，人人欲嫁丈夫。世间甘为此者非我一人也。尔如不信，请尝试之。且闺中事外人不知，何妨互相为乐耶？”刘亦迷而顺之。从此，夫其夫而亦妇其夫，妇其妇而亦夫其妇。两美交融，眷恋之情益切。

    Explanation

    525

    Liu said, "I truly cannot bear to part with you, but I married you to have children. Can you bear them? Besides, my family can barely make ends meet, and we have no extra resources for another marriage. Won't this affect my descendants?" Xing replied, "Do not fear. I have inherited family talismans that can diagnose and cure illnesses. You can ask someone to paint an image of a female deity and worship it. I will have a deity inhabit my body, and my healing will be miraculous. If this becomes known to people, our business will surely flourish. After gaining wealth, you can take concubines without restriction." Liu said, "Your plan is good. But how can you endure doing this as a man?" Xing, growing more alluring, said, "At first, it may be painful, but later it will be pleasurable. I fear that if all men knew this, they would all want to marry women. There are others in the world who are willing to do this. If you do not believe me, please try it. And since these matters are private, what harm is there in enjoying them together?" Liu was also enchanted and agreed. From then on, he acted as a wife to his husband, and she acted as a husband to her wife. The two beauties merged, and their affection grew deeper.

    526

    刘发财心胜，告于父母，别居附近乡屯。传播仙姑治病之说，人见以美妇行医，争相延请，日得时钱数贯。一番役垂涎妇色，诈病唤邢去，入室突拥而抚其下体，出其不意，不及掩饰，居然伟男子也。役缚而讯之，邢哀求包容，愿任鸡奸而多与之贿。役曰：“村中不乏少艾妇女，非亲即故，容汝在此，皆不得作完人矣。且我获妖人，官赏必厚，岂贪汝贿自贻伊戚耶？”并获刘六送坊。转入秋部，鞠实，于左道惑人本罪上，加重问拟缳首，即行正法。刘六照为从例，刺配黑龙江，给索伦达呼尔为奴。此嘉庆十二年四月案，有友任刑曹者，录出原供如是。

    Explanation

    526

    Liu Fa Cai, having overcome his fears, informed his parents and moved to a nearby village. He spread the story of the fairy maiden curing diseases, and people saw a beautiful woman practicing medicine, competing to invite her, and she earned several strings of cash daily. One official lusted after the woman's beauty, fabricated an illness to call Xing over, entered the room and suddenly embraced and touched her lower body, catching her off guard, and revealing himself to be a well-built man. The official bound her and interrogated her, and Xing begged for leniency, offering to submit to sodomy and provide more bribes. The official said, "There are plenty of young and beautiful women in the village, either relatives or acquaintances. If I allow you to stay here, none of us can remain virtuous. And if I capture a witch, the official reward will be substantial. How could I accept your bribes and bring trouble upon myself?" Liu Liu was also captured and sent to the prison. The case was transferred to the autumn department for further investigation, and it was found that she had practiced witchcraft and deceived people, leading to an aggravated sentence of hanging and immediate execution. Liu Liu was punished according to the law for being an accomplice, exiled to Heilongjiang, and given to the Solon Dahu'er as a slave. This case occurred in April of the twelfth year of Jiaqing, and a friend who served in the Criminal Department recorded the original testimony as such.

    527

    芗厈戏判曰：“看得邢有宋朝之美，洪生卫灵之心。食我余桃，既若情谐合卺；报其断袖，何妨长与同衾。倘暂解弁冕以披髻鬟，时之所有；乃永谢衣冠而为巾帼，古之所稀，创新法于狂童，应遭冥殛；使旧宠为归妹，随肆奇情。
    　　
    　　彼刘六者，既经明辨雌雄，当发电闪雷轰之怒，何竟互为牝牡，反追云翻雨覆之能？彼丈夫，我丈夫，阴阳敌体；出乎尔，反乎尔，前后相偿。从此潜迹闺门，法犹可避；竟敢炫奇闻里，情无可原。立异者律以妖人，允宜缳首；为从者配充奴子，投彼索伦。此判。”

    Explanation

    527

    The Judge of Fragrant Hall said: "Upon reviewing, Xing has the beauty of the Song dynasty, and Hong has the heart of Wei Ling. Eating my leftover peach, it's already like a harmonious union; reporting his broken sleeve, why not share the same quilt? If temporarily removing the hat and crown to wear a hairpin, it's what is common at the time; to permanently give up the attire of a man and become a woman, rare in ancient times, to innovate a method for the mad child, should be punished by heaven; to make the former favorite a returning maiden, to indulge in strange emotions.

    　　As for Liu Liu, having already clearly distinguished between male and female, should emit lightning and thunderous anger, why ultimately become male and female, to turn back to the ability to change like clouds and rain? He is a man, I am a man, opposites are equal; what you give out, you receive back, to repay each other in turn. From now on, hiding in the inner chambers, the law can still be avoided; daring to show off strange things in the village, no reason for emotion. Those who stand out should be treated as demons, should be hanged; those who follow should be matched as slaves, sent to the Soren. This is the judgment."

    528

    郭去非
    　　
    　　吾乡郭君去非，世家子也。生好丝弦声，襁褓中闻为弹阮成琵琶者，即在母怀喜跃。至十四五岁时，见戚某有抚琴者，恋恋不舍。某为之指点手法，即专心致志。数十年无间寒暑，朝夕一琴。是以心平气和，恬淡寡欲，不但无求富贵心，即加以横逆，亦置若罔闻。人皆以痴讥之，莫有知其能者。
    　　
    　　寿辰，亲友集贺，郭君恶嚣，避居一室，使其子酬应而已。晚宴毕，有二三老人系外兄弟辈，曰：“主人以琴名有年，必有与凡手异者。平日不敢请，今特乞寿星赐闻法曲，勿以对牛却之是幸。”众皆踊跃附和，其子达意。郭君携琴出曰：“此圣贤之法器，天地之中声，可以通神明格鬼神者也。”其戚曰：“如是必能使我移情，但通神之说未敢遽信。”郭因命其子设炉焚香，多举灯烛，闭其窗户，嘱少年胆怯者毋惧。

    Explanation

    528

    Guo Qufei

    My fellow townsman, Master Guo Qufei, was the son of a noble family. From birth, he loved the sound of stringed instruments; even as an infant in his mother’s arms, he would leap with joy at the sound of someone playing the ruan or pipa. By the time he was fourteen or fifteen, he saw a relative playing the guqin and became deeply attached, reluctant to leave. That relative taught him the proper techniques, and from then on, he devoted himself entirely to the instrument. For several decades, without pause through cold or heat, he played the guqin morning and evening. As a result, his mind was calm and composed, his desires simple and few. He had no longing for wealth or rank, and even when faced with injustice or adversity, he treated it as if he had not heard it at all. People all mocked him as foolish, and no one knew of his skill.

    On his birthday, relatives and friends gathered to celebrate, but Master Guo, who disliked noise, retreated to a quiet room, entrusting his son to handle the reception. After the evening banquet, two or three elderly men, his cousins, said: "The master has been renowned for his guqin playing for many years; there must be something different about his skill from that of ordinary players. We dared not ask before, but today we specially beg the birthday celebrant to allow us to hear the sacred music. We would be most fortunate if you did not refuse to perform as if playing for a cow." The others eagerly agreed, and his son conveyed their request. Master Guo took his guqin and said: "This is the sacred instrument of sages and virtuous men, the central sound of heaven and earth, capable of communicating with the divine and appeasing the spirits." His relative said: "If that is true, it must be able to move my emotions, but I dare not believe so quickly in the claim of communicating with the divine." Master Guo then instructed his son to set up an incense burner, light incense, light many lamps and candles, close the windows, and told the younger, timid ones not to be afraid.

    529

    皆笑曰：“三四十人在此，即鬼神现形，何惧之有？”郭君调弦入弄，洋洋盈耳。未几，声渐悲戚，灯焰渐昏。觉幽风飒飒，鸣声呜呜。窃听之，妇女皆泣，忽似窗外来百十人抛掷泥沙，窗纸欲裂，逼人毛发皆竖。于是幼男童女扑入老者怀中，少年互相拥持。一老摇手曰：“曲不可终矣。恐胆怯者成悸疾奈何？”郭君微笑，改弦易操，灯烛骤明，觉阳和之气满室，悠然而止。客曰：“所弹何曲，而能如是？”郭曰：“普庵咒，第一转仅弹六段，君等畏惧不及再转，即收矣。”皆再拜曰：“神乎其技，虽有他乐不敢请矣。”肃然而退。

    Explanation

    529

    All laughed and said, "With thirty to forty people here, even if ghosts and gods were to appear, what would there be to fear?" Mr. Guo adjusted the strings and began to play, and the music filled the ears with a pleasant sound. Soon, the sound gradually became sad and mournful, and the candle flame dimmed. They felt a cold wind blowing, and the sound was mournful. They listened secretly, and all the women were weeping. Suddenly, it seemed like hundreds of people were throwing mud and sand from outside the window, the window paper was about to tear, and it made one's hair stand on end. The young boys and girls rushed into the arms of the elderly, and the young men held each other tightly. An old man shook his hand and said, "The music cannot be finished. What about those who are timid and might develop palpitations?" Mr. Guo smiled, changed the strings and switched to another piece, and the lamp and candle suddenly became bright. They felt the warm and harmonious atmosphere fill the room, and the music gradually and peacefully stopped. The guests asked, "What piece did you play that could do this?" Mr. Guo replied, "The Pǔ'ān Chú, the first part is only six sections long. You all were too scared to hear the second part, so I stopped." They all bowed twice and said, "The skill is divine. Even if there are other performances, we dare not ask for them." They left respectfully.

    530

    壬戌清明，郭君携本族兄弟子侄辈，买舟赴武林祀墓毕。遨游吴山，舟泊城内，大雨连日，山泉暴注，河水顿涨，平岸塞桥，舟不得行矣。且风狂浪急，颠簸不安，乃移舟藏于巨室水阁下。是日断炊，众皆惶迫。郭君笑曰：“无伤也，今在患难之中，不得不贬我声价，以琴求食似尚可得。”众曰：“然不能登陆，奈何？”郭曰：“毋庸。”乃横琴前舱，作《水调歌头》。甫毕，阁上有开窗问者，舟子以实告。既而水阁大开，请舟入厂内安泊。仆从数人，左右张盖，送一老者登舟。去其雨衣，则赫然四品冦带。入舱与郭君为礼曰：“甫闻先生琴音清妙，小女习此有年，未得明师指授。如不我弃，请过舍一叙。”乃使仆囊琴，扶郭君，并邀众入。其家门庭宏敞，陈饰纷华，大族也。

    Explanation

    530

    On the Qingming Festival of the year Renxu, Guo Jun led his family members, including brothers, uncles, and nephews, to buy a boat and travel to West Lake for ancestral worship. After completing the rites, they strolled around Wushan Mountain. The boat docked in the city, and continuous heavy rain fell, causing mountain springs to pour down and the river to suddenly rise. The riverbank was flooded, blocking the bridge, and the boat could not proceed. The wind was fierce, and the waves were turbulent, making the journey uncomfortable. They then moved the boat to hide under a large water pavilion. On that day, there was no food, and everyone was anxious. Guo Jun smiled and said, "No harm done. Now in trouble, I must lower my status and use my lute to seek food, which might still be possible." The others asked, "But we cannot disembark, what shall we do?" Guo Jun replied, "No need to worry." He then placed his lute in the front cabin and played the Shuidiaogoutou (Water Tune Head). Just as he finished, someone opened a window upstairs and asked; the boatman told the truth. The water pavilion was fully opened, inviting the boat to enter and anchor safely. Several servants, with umbrellas on both sides, escorted an elderly man onto the boat. After removing his raincoat, he was revealed to be a senior official with a fourth-rank military belt. He entered the cabin and greeted Guo Jun, saying, "Just heard your lute playing is clear and exquisite; my daughter has practiced this for years but has not received guidance from a master. If you do not reject me, please come to my home for a visit." He then had his servant pack the lute, helped Guo Jun, and invited everyone to enter. His family's courtyard was grand and spacious, with luxurious decorations, indicating a prominent family.

    531

    询知主人由内翰出为观察致仕，普称富有。父女皆好琴，其水阁即琴室也。女恒在是，忽闻琴声与众不同，故告其父而敦请。主人问客未炊，乃大开筵宴，以薪米给舟子，皆得饱餐，见郭君系年高有德之人，延之入内，使其女拜投门下。年约及笄，聪明俊秀，落落大方。郭君使试作一弄，曰：“大法亦似矣，只须拨正音节，可以入妙。”乃问其所欲，传以《汉宫秋》一阕，款留数日，天气睛明，水势亦退，众欲告辞。主人之意留翁而送客。郭曰：“老夫年届八旬，恐犯不宿于外之戒，只可暂作盘桓，不能留恋，况女公子慧甚，自具妙解，即以一阕推之，头头是道矣。”坚不能留，乃具币帛致赆仪，恭送登舟而别。自此，郭君之名，噪传遐迩。凡好此者纷纷从学。郭君恶其烦，遁于硖石山，不知所终，或曰仙去矣。

    Explanation

    531

    Inquiry revealed that the host was a former翰林 (Hanlin scholar) who retired as an observer (观察使) and was generally acknowledged as wealthy. Both the father and daughter had a fondness for the zither, and the waterside pavilion served as their zither room. The daughter was often there when, suddenly, she heard a zither sound that was different from the usual. She informed her father, and they earnestly invited the guest. The host asked if the guests had not yet eaten and then held a grand feast, providing firewood and rice to the boatmen so that all could eat their fill. Seeing that De Guo was a person of advanced age and virtue, they invited him inside and made his daughter bow and become a disciple. The daughter was around the age of marriage, intelligent, graceful, and poised. De Guo asked her to play a piece and said, "The general technique is also quite good, but only the correct pitch and rhythm need to be adjusted to reach excellence." He then asked what she desired and taught her one piece of "Autumn in the Han Palace." They kept him for several days, during which the weather was clear and bright, and the water level also receded. The guests wanted to take leave. The host intended to keep the old man while sending off the guests. De Guo said, "I am nearly eighty years old and fear violating the rule of not staying out overnight; I can only stay for a short while and cannot linger. Moreover, your daughter is very intelligent and naturally possesses a profound understanding. Even when tested with one piece, every point is well-argued." They firmly could not be persuaded to stay, so they prepared gifts and presents as farewell tokens and respectfully sent him off as he boarded the boat and took his leave. From then on, De Guo's name spread far and wide. Those who liked the zither flocked to learn from him. De Guo found it too troublesome and retreated to Xiashe Mountain, where he disappeared without a trace. Some say he became a spirit.

    532

    芗厈曰：郭君之琴，托门下者甚众。得其真传，惟金道士。金传丁先生养虚，余壮年在养虚先生家，学抚一二阕。是时，聆先生音节之妙，声声沁入心脾，已超出时手百倍矣。惜余生也晚，不得听郭君法曲，作成连海上游也。
    　　
    　　何首乌
    　　
    　　何首乌，一名能嗣，药中仙品。产山泽者固多，亦有在城市，而其根反得成人形者，以得人之精气多耳。然具人形者必通灵，隐现无恒，人不能得。若得而食之，即仙去，相传已久。
    　　
    　　吾邑有张氏姑妇者，夫与子皆诸生，以家贫教读外出，惟二妇在家操作女工度日，是以纺纱必夜午方休，每秋月皎洁，时闻院中似有幼孩征逐声。拔关视，则无有。妇与姑谋，后若有所闻，一人仍纺，一人穴窗隙窥之。于是轮流伺隙，妇果见两孩出自墙阴。长不满尺，一男一女，皆赤体，携手至院落中，对月再拜，互相扑跌为戏，妇潜告，姑虑曰：“恐系妖孽之子孙，犯之自肇衅矣。”皆不敢出，然心甚怀疑。

    Explanation

    532

    Xiang An said: Those who rely on Master Guo's patronage are numerous. Only the Daoist Jin received the true transmission. Jin passed it on to Master Yang Xu, and during my prime years, I was at the home of Master Yang Xu, where I learned to play one or two pieces. At that time, I listened to the wonderful rhythm and tone of Master Yang Xu, each note penetrating the heart and soul, far surpassing contemporary musicians by a hundredfold. Alas, I was born too late to hear Master Guo's ritual music and to make a journey together to the sea.

    What is He Shou Wu?

    He Shou Wu, also known as Neng Si, is a divine herb among medicines. Those produced in mountains and marshes are numerous, and there are also those found in cities whose roots have taken on human form, having absorbed more of human essence and energy. However, those with human form are spiritually aware, appearing and disappearing without a fixed pattern, and cannot be obtained by humans. If one were to eat it, they would become a god and ascend, a belief that has been passed down for a long time.

    In my hometown, there are two women from the Zhang family. Their husband and son are both scholars, and because of poverty, they teach and read outside the home. Only the two women stay at home, working on women's tasks to make a living, so they spin yarn until noon at night. Every autumn, when the moon is bright, they often hear sounds of children playing in the courtyard. When they look, there is nothing. The wife and grandmother discuss, and if they hear something again, one continues spinning while the other peeps through a window crack. They take turns watching for signs. The wife indeed sees two children emerging from the shadow of the wall. They are not more than a foot tall, one boy and one girl, both naked, walking together to the courtyard, bowing twice to the moon, and playfully chasing and falling on each other. The wife secretly reports, and the grandmother worries and says, "Fearing they are the descendants of demons, if we disturb them, we will bring trouble upon ourselves." They dare not go out, yet they are very suspicious.

    533

    一日，所亲至，知医博学士也。姑以所疑质之。戚曰：“宅若有妖，何能安居？此必灵药所变。得而蒸食之，当成地仙。”妇笑曰：“渠闻人声即遁，焉能攫取？”曰：“无难，吾闻稻米，天地正气所结，能压宝藏。若由窗隙掷之，得中其身，即不能遁矣。”戚去，妇度院中孩戏之处，至窗隙约丈余，谅掷米未必适当。乃截竹为筒，撒米其中，以箸卷布催送之。日练其手法至精熟，复何于窗隙，二孩来前，妇即以筒米弹之，果中，二孩皆仆，突出擒拿，入手僵直。呼姑举火烛之，类木雕者，眉目如画，气甚芳馥。姑妇相谋煮饭时于铁锅内蒸之，一次稍软，至五六次，香绵可食。姑妇各分食一枚，觉鲜美异常，腹果甚，一日不思饮食。

    Explanation

    533

    One day, a close acquaintance of hers, a learned scholar, came to visit. She asked him about her doubts. Her mother-in-law said, "If there is an evil spirit in the house, how can one live in peace? This must be a transformation by a magical medicine. If you can steam and eat it, you will become an immortal." The woman laughed and said, "It hears human voices and immediately flees. How can it be caught?" The scholar replied, "It is not difficult. I have heard that rice, which is the essence of the earth and sky, can suppress hidden treasures. If you throw it through the window, and it hits its body, it will not be able to escape."

    The woman thought that the children were playing in the courtyard and estimated that the point was about a zhang (about 3 meters) from the window. She thought that throwing the rice might not hit the right spot. So, she cut a bamboo tube, put the rice inside, and used chopsticks to roll and send it. She practiced her technique until it was very skillful and tried again at the window. The two children came forward. The woman immediately shot the rice from the tube at them. It hit, and the two children fell down. They were suddenly caught and became stiff and rigid in her hands. She called her mother-in-law to light a candle. They looked like wooden carvings, with eyebrows and eyes as beautiful as paintings, and had a very fragrant smell.

    The mother-in-law and daughter-in-law discussed and decided to steam them in an iron pot while cooking rice. After one steaming, they became slightly soft. After five or six steamings, they became fragrant and soft enough to eat. The mother-in-law and daughter-in-law each ate one. They found them extremely delicious and felt very full, not wanting to eat or drink for the rest of the day.

    534

    夜眠至次日，皆不能起身矣，卓午门不开，邻姥疑有故，逾垣窥之，见姑妇皆仰卧于床头，面及身俱肿，目开口张，不能言语。邻姥倩人走报，其父子归，不解何由，亦不识何疾，急邀知医之戚诊视。笑曰：“非疾也，日前母所说成形首乌，我曾说以捕法，谅必捕而食之。未识九蒸九晒之制，又不知避忌，误犯铁器，是以有毒。”试以解毒开通之药灌之。至七日，肿消人醒，问之果如医言。起后，强健逾前，累月不思食。其姑年已周甲，发白再黑，齿落重生，枯绉肌肤皆皮脱，而润泽似二十许人，复生子。其妇年近四旬，转而为二八好女子，连举子女十余。后皆寿一百五六十岁，无疾而终。

    Explanation

    534

    From sleeping at night until the next day, all could not get up; at noon, the door was not opened. A neighborly old woman suspected something was wrong and climbed over the wall to peek. She saw the grandmother and her daughter-in-law lying face up at the head of the bed, both their faces and bodies swollen, their eyes wide open, and unable to speak. The neighborly old woman asked someone to run and report. The father and son returned, not understanding the cause and not knowing what disease it was, and urgently invited a relative who knew medicine to diagnose and examine. The doctor smiled and said, "It is not a disease. The mother mentioned a certain herb called Polygonum multiflorum (首乌) a few days ago. I had previously mentioned the method of preparing it, and I believe they must have prepared and eaten it. They did not know the proper method of nine steaming and nine drying, nor did they know the taboos, and mistakenly used iron tools, which is why it was toxic." The doctor tried administering a detoxifying and promoting circulation medicine. By the seventh day, the swelling subsided, and the person woke up. When asked, they confirmed it was as the doctor had said. After waking up, they were stronger than before and did not want to eat for months. The grandmother, who was already 60 years old, had her white hair turn black again, her lost teeth regrow, her dry, wrinkled skin shed, and become smooth and moist like a person in their twenties, and gave birth to a child. The wife, who was nearing 40 years old, transformed into a beautiful woman in her twenties and gave birth to more than ten children. Later, all of them lived for 150-160 years and died without illness.

    535

    芗厈曰：惜乎！得灵药而不知服食之法，仅以延龄，无乃有负仙品。或问制食之法，余日曾考诸文献，当以柳木作桶，承以砂甑，用桑木，火先武后文，九蒸九晒，以竹刀剖作九顿食，可以绝粒入山矣。或笑曰：能得者不能制，能制者必不能得，徒虚语耳。
    　　
    　　补明武宗遗事三则
    　　
    　　正德二年丁卯秋，大比之年，士子云集。星卜之流皆在前门营趁。有相士自命许（名负）、管（名辂）之能，凡朝官微服而来，一见即能指其现居品级，系何出身，历试无误，是以就相者众。帝知之，亦微服在人丛中观听。时八月初八日午刻，突一士人吼奔而来，扭结相士曰：“汝误我功名，当与汝性命相搏。”众为排解不开。帝使卫士分之，入问何如此急。相士曰：“是人前数日来问科名，我以今科解元许，彼自不入场，反赖我误之，真书呆也。”士人曰：“因汝以元许我，亲朋预作贺筵，饮入醉乡，家人唤醒入场，门已闭矣。

    Explanation

    535

    Xiang'an said: Alas! Obtaining the elixir but not knowing the method of taking it, one can only extend one's lifespan, which might be a disservice to one's divine status. Someone asked about the method of preparing the elixir, and I have studied various texts, stating that one should use a willow wood bucket, supported by a sand steamer, with mulberry wood, fire first fierce then gentle, nine steaming and nine sun-drying cycles, and then cut into nine portions with a bamboo knife, which can lead to abstaining from food and entering the mountains. Others laughed and said: Those who can obtain it cannot prepare it, and those who can prepare it cannot obtain it; it is just empty words.

    Supplement to the Anecdotes of Emperor Wu of Ming

    In the second year of the Zhengde era, during the autumn of the Dingmao year, the year of the imperial examinations, scholars gathered in large numbers. Fortune-tellers and astrologers were all present at the front gate. There was a fortune-teller who claimed to have the abilities of Xu (Fub) and Guan (Lü), who could, when court officials came in plain clothes, immediately point out their current rank, their background, and had never been wrong in repeated tests, hence many came to consult him. The emperor knew of this and also came in plain clothes among the crowd to observe. On the eighth day of the eighth month at noon, suddenly a scholar ran in shouting and grabbed the fortune-teller, saying, "You ruined my career; I should fight you to the death." The crowd tried to mediate but could not resolve the conflict. The emperor ordered the guards to separate them and asked why they were so upset. The fortune-teller said, "This person came several days ago to ask about his success in the exams, and I promised him he would be the top scorer in this batch. He didn't even enter the examination hall, and instead blamed me for his failure; he is truly a bookworm." The scholar said, "Because you promised me I would be the top scorer, my friends and relatives prepared a celebratory feast, and I drank myself into a drunken stupor. When my family woke me up to enter the examination hall, the door was already closed."

    536
