    岂有场外解元哉？非汝误我而谁？”二人争执不休，帝曰：“毋哗，使有人送渠入场，不得发解，汝认何罚？”相士曰：“挖我眸子去。”帝曰：“言出无悔。”即取案上纸笔书条，钤以身佩小玺，命卫士送去。帝谓相士曰：“半月后来验汝言也。”相士知机，唯唯而避。当卫士送士人至场外，传鼓称圣旨降，门官揭封去锁，内即传点开门，偕士人直入。至公堂，知贡举各官跪接，卫士面南宣读毕，即去复命。御史捡卷恭送士人入号。内外哄传皇帝送朋友进场，主考亦留心阅卷，见其文亦尚稳妥，与各官公议皆曰：“此公既为当今识拔，不可作第二人以拂上意。”竟中解元。试官复命，力奖其文才出众，贺帝得人。帝大笑曰：“命也，命也。”盖帝本欲使入场而不取，以难相士。因豹房新得美人，恋色而忘后命。今竟发解，益觉相士可畏。着锦衣卫追寻，杳不知其所之矣。

    Explanation

    536

    How could there be a top scorer outside the examination hall? Who else could have misled me but you?" The two men argued endlessly. The emperor said, "Do not make a commotion. Have someone escort him into the hall. If he does not pass, what penalty do you accept?" The fortune-teller said, "Remove my eyes." The emperor said, "Once spoken, there is no regret." He immediately took paper and a pen from the table, wrote a note, stamped it with his small seal, and ordered the guards to deliver it. The emperor told the fortune-teller, "Return in half a month to verify your words." The fortune-teller, understanding the situation, nodded in agreement and then withdrew. As the guards escorted the scholar to the examination hall, the drum was beaten and the imperial edict was announced. The door official unsealed and unlocked the doors. Inside, the doors were opened by announcement, and the scholar was accompanied into the hall. Upon reaching the examination hall, the officials responsible for the examination prostrated themselves to greet. The guards faced south and read the edict aloud, then returned to report. The censor checked the examination papers and respectfully escorted the scholar into the examination room. Inside and outside, people were spreading that the emperor had sent a friend into the examination. The chief examiner also paid close attention to the examination papers, seeing that his writing was quite solid. Discussing with the officials, they all said, "This person has been recognized and promoted by the current emperor. We cannot rank him second, which would go against the emperor's wishes." He ultimately became the top scorer. The examiners reported back, strongly commending his exceptional talent and congratulating the emperor on finding a worthy person. The emperor laughed and said, "It is fate, it is fate." The emperor originally intended to let him enter but not pass, to make it difficult for the fortune-teller. Because the emperor recently acquired a new concubine, he became infatuated and forgot his later command. Now he has actually passed, it becomes even more frightening to think about the fortune-teller. Ordering the Imperial Guards to search for him, he is nowhere to be found.

    537

    戊辰春夜，帝由豹房微行还朝，途遇迎娶者，卤簿之盛，彩舆之华，光耀夺目。停车道旁观之，见彩舆前一黑大汉高二丈余，面目如漆，怪状奇形，衣甲执杵，昂昂而来。见帝注目，似有退意，忽超跃在前。帝异之，命车随至娶妇家。门庭喧赫，大族也。帝下车入中堂，黑汉先彩舆进院，见帝在，掩面而遁。帝始悟非人也。因念凶煞相犯之日，何得迎娶？择日者误人不浅矣。欲问其人，故留观。新人交拜后送入洞房，外即开筵。主人来让客，见帝相貌非常，即推首座。堂中十余席以次告坐。主人因不识帝，故来陪坐。觉凛凛威严，不敢骤问姓氏。

    Explanation

    537

    On the spring night of the third day of the third month, the emperor secretly traveled from the leopard house and returned to the palace. Along the way, he encountered a grand wedding procession, with its splendid ceremonial entourage and colorful, ornate sedan chairs, shining brightly and dazzling. He stopped by the roadside to watch. Seeing a black giant in front of the colorful sedan chairs, who was over two zhang tall, with a face like ink, a strange and unusual appearance, wearing armor and holding a mallet, striding forward proudly. Seeing the emperor looking intently, the giant seemed to have the intention to retreat, but suddenly leaped ahead. The emperor was surprised by it and ordered the carriage to follow to the bride's family. The entrance was noisy and bustling, indicating a prominent family. The emperor got out of the carriage and entered the main hall. The black giant went ahead of the colorful sedan chairs into the courtyard, saw the emperor, and covered his face and fled. The emperor then realized that it was not human. He thought that on a day of inauspiciousness, how could one get married? The person who chose the date had greatly misled others. He wanted to ask the person, so he stayed to observe. After the newlyweds bowed to each other and were sent into the bridal chamber, a banquet was immediately set up outside. The host came to invite guests, seeing the emperor's unusual appearance, and ushered him to the main seat. In the hall, more than ten tables were arranged in order for seating. The host, not recognizing the emperor, came to sit with him. Feeling a stern and majestic aura, he dared not ask about his name and surname immediately.

    538

    帝问择日之人何在？主人指次席一老叟曰：“此钦天监致仕堂官也。术甚精，凡有大事之家，皆请此公诹吉，百无一失。”帝命主人唤来曰：“我闻汝能择日，何得以黑道误人？”叟对曰：“不敢，今日寅时虽有黑煞神一名元首宿者降临，但有紫微临凡可解，逢凶化吉，不但不为殃，且主后福。”帝无以难之，曰：“我有一联，汝能确对，免汝巧辩之愆。”曰：“元首宿逢元首主。”叟辞衰迈恚昏，可否邀新郎代对？帝日可。乃招新郎来，请问上联，即对曰：“紫微星照紫微郎。”帝不觉首肯，曰：“此翰苑才也。”叟命新郎伏地谢恩。帝知机泄，逡巡登车而去。新郎本孝廉也，旋中进士。引见，帝识之，特命入词垣，笑谕曰：“我曾叨汝喜筵，以此补贺礼也。”

    Explanation

    538

    The emperor asked about the whereabouts of the person who selects auspicious dates. The host pointed to an old man seated next to him and said, "This is a retired official from the Bureau of Astronomy and Calendar, who is highly skilled. For any important household, they always invite this gentleman to choose auspicious days, and there has never been a single mistake." The emperor ordered the host to bring the old man forward and said, "I have heard that you are good at selecting auspicious days, but how could you have chosen an inauspicious day that has harmed people?" The old man replied, "I dare not. Today at the hour of Yin, although there is the arrival of the Black Demon named Yuan Shou Xiu, there is also the Purple Emperor descending to the mortal world, which can resolve it. It will turn misfortune into good fortune, not only not bringing harm, but also bringing future blessings." The emperor had no further questions and said, "I have a couplet. If you can match it correctly, you will be exempt from any clever arguments." The old man replied, "I am too old and my memory is not as sharp as it used to be. Can I invite the new groom to match it for me?" The emperor said, "That is acceptable." He then called the new groom over and asked for the upper part of the couplet, which was "Yuan Shou Xiu meets Yuan Shou Host." The new groom immediately responded with "Purple Emperor shines on Purple Emperor's Son." The emperor nodded in approval and said, "This is the talent of a scholar." The old man ordered the new groom to kneel and thank the emperor. The emperor realized that the old man had revealed too much and quickly got up and left. The new groom was originally a scholar who had passed the imperial examinations and soon became a Jinshi (imperial scholar). When he was introduced to the emperor, the emperor recognized him and specially appointed him to the Hanlin Academy, smiling and saying, "I once enjoyed your wedding feast, so this is my way of returning the favor."

    539

    武会试之年，帝亦摺巾佩刀作武士装，游观于市。时已黄昏，见一老提灯踽踽而来，一壮役撞灭其灯，老者曰：“我与君前本乡邻，亦曾周济。今我年迈无能，君见必欺我，是何意见？”役曰：“韩老，我恶汝动称周济，何得扫我颜面？”韩曰：“我非妄言也。十年前饭君几次，资助若干。君今充役大班，我本不期报，君反以为怨，世有如此负心人哉！”役愈怒，摔韩老于地，伏而殴之。环观之人拉劝甚杂。帝怒，由人丛中抽佩刀刺入役腹而去。役转身仰跌旋毙，不知刀从何来。

    Explanation

    539

    In the year of the military examination, the emperor also wore a folded headband and a sword, dressed in warrior attire, and strolled through the market. It was already dusk when he saw an elderly man carrying a lantern, slowly making his way. A strong servant suddenly extinguished the old man's lantern. The old man said, "I was once your neighbor and have helped you before. Now that I am old and incapable, you will surely deceive me. What kind of person are you?" The servant replied, "Old Han, I hate that you always claim to have helped me. How can you disgrace my honor?" The old man said, "I am not lying. Ten years ago, I fed you several times and provided you with some assistance. You are now in a high-ranking position, and I did not expect any return, but you now hold a grudge against me. Is there such a disloyal person in the world?" The servant grew angrier, threw the old man to the ground, and fell upon him, beating him. The people surrounding them pulled and tried to persuade him, but the scene was chaotic. The emperor, enraged, drew his sword from the crowd and stabbed it into the servant's abdomen before leaving. The servant turned over, fell backward, and died instantly. No one knew where the knife had come from.

    540

    卡兵缚韩老报司坊，送入刑部承审。官以韩老锻炼成狱拟抵。秋谳时，帝阅至是狱，命提凶犯廷讯。韩老呼冤，帝笑曰：“汝果大冤。”问谁承审此狱者，尚书以某郎中对。帝君之来，命卫士脱韩老刑具以着郎官曰：“杀人者我也。汝屈人问抵，狱中冤滥者不知多少矣。今即以汝作抵，我为众冤魂稍舒其忿。”郎官以凶刀出自韩老，并非无据为辩，帝命取鞘来，纳之适符，遂伏辜。乃释韩老，赏以百金。曰：“偿汝所费。”且命是年停勾，择御史之能者名曰恤刑，分赴各省提狱，覆按而后报可，果多所平反也。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：观上三则，游戏中确有主裁，但好行小慧，为儒尚且不可，况九五之尊耶？今之读史者直以帝比之桀纣，无乃过甚。当初谥曰武宗毅皇帝，毅者果决之谓，可见遇事实能决断，非尽阿谀可知矣。

    Explanation

    540

    A military officer bound Han Lao and reported to the prefecture, then sent him to the Ministry of Justice for trial. The official fabricated a case against Han Lao and proposed a sentence of execution. During the autumn review, the emperor read up to this case and ordered the criminal to be brought to court for questioning. Han Lao cried out for injustice, and the emperor smiled and said, "Indeed, this is a great wrong." The emperor asked who had presided over this trial, and the Minister of Justice replied with a certain Langzhong (a junior official). The emperor came and ordered the guards to remove Han Lao's restraints and put them on the junior official, saying, "I am the one who killed. You wronged the innocent and proposed a sentence, and there are countless innocent people in the prison. Now I will use you as a substitute, to slightly ease the anger of the many wronged spirits." The junior official argued that the knife used to commit the crime came from Han Lao and it is not without basis. The emperor ordered to bring the sheath, and when it was fitted perfectly, the junior official confessed. Han Lao was then released and rewarded with a hundred gold pieces. The emperor said, "To compensate you for your expenses." He also ordered to stop the executions this year, selected capable censors, called them "compassionate judges," sent them to various provinces to preside over trials, reviewed and then reported back, and indeed many were exonerated.

    Xiang Yan said, "Considering the three cases above, there is indeed a referee in the game, but he likes to show off his cleverness. Even as a Confucian scholar, it is not acceptable, how much more so for one in the highest position?" Those who read history now directly compare the emperor to Jie and Zhou, is this not excessive? At first, he was posthumously titled Emperor Wu of the Resolute, resolute means decisive. It is evident that he could make decisions when faced with facts, it is not all flattery.

    541

    通州吏目
    　　
    　　乾隆纯皇帝东巡，直隶各官照例备供给。光天化日之中，诸凡顺遂，惟此差最难均平。盖随驾官员皆循情理，独太监一项，或重支，或冒支，或以好作歹，挠搅不休。意在饮食之外，其内廷供奉有执事监名二十八他他者，俱在御前行走，皆得用人也。其时支应局总理系通州剌史，协理者即其吏目，发放一切酒筵及例支饭食，皆按照旧章，井井有条。内监亦照例支讫矣。忽有十余人云自他他处来，索食甚侈。刺史以冒滥斥之，诸人去，围随一首领太监蜂拥而来，曰：“咱家随驾出巡，不给饭食，使枵腹从事。有此不近情理之官耶？”刺史以照例业经发讫，恐从者昧没重支为辩。首领曰：“凡人出行，尚许多带一二仆从，岂有主子不许多带几人，而敢以旧章拒格耶？”即举手掴刺史脸，刺史惧而逃。

    Explanation

    541

    Magistrate of Tongzhou

    During the Eastern Tour of Emperor Qianlong, the officials of Zhili Province prepared the necessary supplies as per custom. Everything went smoothly under the bright sun, except for this particular task, which was the hardest to manage fairly. All the officials accompanying the emperor followed reason and propriety, but the eunuchs were a different matter. They sometimes demanded excessive payments, sometimes claimed them fraudulently, and sometimes did bad things under the guise of doing good, troubling endlessly. Their intentions went beyond mere food and drink. Among the imperial court attendants, there were twenty-eight named supervisors who walked in front of the emperor, and they were all entitled to bring people with them. At the time, the head of the supply office was the magistrate of Tongzhou, and his deputy was the magistrate of Tongzhou. They distributed all the banquets and standard meals in perfect order, following the established rules. The eunuchs had also received their payments as usual. Suddenly, more than ten people came claiming to be from the twenty-eight supervisors, demanding a lavish meal. The magistrate dismissed them as fraudulent and sent them away. However, a group of eunuchs surrounded and followed the chief eunuch, who came forward and said, "We are accompanying the emperor on his tour, and you are not providing us with food. How can you expect us to work on an empty stomach? Is this reasonable?" The magistrate argued that the payments had already been made according to the established rules and that he feared the eunuchs' followers might have embezzled the extra payments. The chief eunuch replied, "When people travel, they often bring one or two servants. How can the master not bring a few people with him, and how dare you refuse us based on old rules?" He then slapped the magistrate's face with his hand, and the magistrate fled out of fear.

    542

    吏目迎谓曰：“我等可与争而不可避。避则示弱。倘被接掠一空，明日以何物支应，获咎不更重耶？职愿舍一官对之。”刺史摇手曰：“我无能为矣，足下善处之。”吏目乃唤齐人役，谕曰：“此去遇监，如我喝打，汝等即擒而行刑。有我在，罪不及汝，毋畏葸不前，事定后有赏。”众皆踊跃从事。首领见剌史潜避，率人闯入帐房搜取银物。吏自突前喝问。首领见来官戴花金顶者，即作威福曰：“大老爷在此，汝小官何问为？”吏目曰：“入我帐房，乱我货财，必是匪徒，敢冒内监？”喝皂役拘执行刑。时监少役多，不能抵拒，竟将首领摔地，去裤杖责二十，逐出局外，号哭而去。访明官职姓名，在御前泣诉。

    Explanation

    542

    The clerk greeted him and said, "We can confront this matter but must not avoid it. To avoid would show weakness. If we are completely looted, what shall we use tomorrow to respond? Will not the blame be even heavier? I am willing to sacrifice my position to face them." The governor shook his head and said, "I am powerless. You handle it well." The clerk then gathered the people and officials and instructed them, "If you meet the supervisor, and I shout, you must seize and execute. As long as I am here, the blame will not fall on you. Do not be timid and hesitate. There will be rewards after the matter is settled." All of them eagerly set about their task. The leader saw the governor hiding and led people to break into the account office to seize silver and goods. The clerk suddenly stepped forward and shouted a question. Seeing the official who had arrived wearing a flowered golden cap, the leader assumed authority and said, "The great master is here. Why are you, a lowly official, questioning?" The clerk replied, "To enter my account office and disturb my goods must make you a bandit—dare you pretend to be an imperial official?" He ordered the constables to arrest and execute him. At that time, the guards were few and the officials were many, so they could not resist. They finally threw the leader to the ground, stripped him, beat him twenty times with a rod, and expelled him from the hall. He wept and left. They investigated and determined his official title and name, then wept and reported before the emperor.

    543

    帝谓左右大臣召通州吏目。大臣领旨出宣，制军承命而行，将吏目械系宫门外。吏部派带领司官出问：“吏目何在？”制军指拘囚犯官对，司官笑曰：“上命召见，并无革职拿问之说，何得如是？”立命易衣冠，带领入见。帝曰：“杖内监者，汝耶？”吏目伏奏曰：“缉捕盗贼，臣之职也。此人闯入帐房，擅搜财物，必非正人，臣是以捕而责之。不知其为内监，且窃盗已行，而不得财厥罪应笞。圣朝断不肯因一监而废大清律也。故臣谨遵行。”帝曰善。问吏部近日令缺，尚书按籍以对。帝曰：“某省知县缺出，即着通州吏目某补授。”诏曰：“汝此去作县令，宜始终强项以庇百姓，切勿为上官屈也。”顿首谢训，领凭赴任。

    Explanation

    543

    The emperor instructed his ministers to summon the magistrate of Tongzhou. The ministers carried out the command, and the military official restrained the magistrate and placed him outside the palace gates. The Ministry of Personnel sent an official to ask, "Where is the magistrate?" The military official pointed to the restrained official and replied. The official smiled and said, "The emperor's command was to summon you for an audience, and there was no mention of revoking your position or questioning you. Why were you treated this way?" He immediately ordered the magistrate to change into proper attire and led him to see the emperor. The emperor asked, "Was it you who beat the eunuch?" The magistrate bowed and replied, "Arresting thieves is my duty. This person broke into the office and searched for property, clearly an unscrupulous individual. I arrested and punished him. I did not know he was a eunuch, and since no property was stolen, the punishment should be a light beating. Your Majesty's dynasty would not abandon the laws of the Qing dynasty because of one eunuch. Therefore, I acted according to the law." The emperor said, "Very good." He then asked the Ministry of Personnel about recent vacancies. The Minister of Personnel consulted the records and replied. The emperor said, "The position of magistrate in a certain province is vacant; let the magistrate of Tongzhou, [name], be appointed to it." The emperor decreed, "When you go to serve as magistrate, you should always remain steadfast to protect the people, and do not yield to superiors." The magistrate bowed in gratitude for the instruction and took the official seal to assume his new post.

    544

    芗厈曰：此之谓天王明圣，量有鉴于前明大珰之肆横，故能以抑之者宠之。不仅因吏目之奏对得体也。
    　　
    　　金山寺医僧
    　　
    　　浙右某孝廉约伴入都会试，舟至姑苏，孝廉病矣。同伴唤舆送至名医叶天士家诊治。叶诊之良久，曰：“君疾系感冒风寒，一药即愈。第将何往？”孝廉以赴礼闱对。叶曰：“先生休矣，此去舍舟登陆，必患消渴症，无药可救，寿不过一月耳。脉象已现，速归，后事尚及料理也。”遂开方与之，谕门徒登诸医案。孝廉回舟，惶然泣下，辞伴欲归。同伴曰：“此医家吓人生财之道也。况叶不过时医，决非神仙，何必介意。”次日，孝廉服药果愈。同伴益怂恿之，遂北上，然心甚戚戚。

    Explanation

    544

    Xiang Yan said: This is what is meant by a wise and virtuous ruler, who, by reflecting on the excessive tyranny of the eunuchs during the Ming dynasty, is able to honor those who are suppressed. It is not only because the official's response was appropriate.

    Mountainside Temple Doctor

    A certain scholar-official from the Zhejiang region traveled with companions to the capital for the metropolitan examination. When their boat arrived in Suzhou, the scholar-official fell ill. His companions called for a carriage to take him to the famous physician Ye Tian Shi's house for treatment. Ye examined him for a long time and said, "Your illness is due to a cold and wind, and one medicine will cure it. But where are you going?" The scholar-official replied that he was going to the examination hall. Ye said, "Sir, you should rest. If you leave the boat and go ashore, you will surely suffer from diabetes, which cannot be cured, and your life will not last more than a month. The pulse pattern is already evident. Return quickly, there is still time to arrange the funeral." He then prescribed a medicine and instructed his disciples to record it in the medical records. The scholar-official returned to the boat, in a state of fear and tears, and decided to leave his companions and return home. His companions said, "This is just a doctor's way of scaring people to make money. And besides, Ye is only a contemporary doctor, not a god, so why worry about it?" The next day, the scholar-official took the medicine and indeed recovered. His companions encouraged him even more, and he finally went north, but he was still very anxious.

    545

    舟抵江口，风逆不得渡，同人约游金山寺。山门前有医僧牌，孝廉访禅室，僧为诊视曰：“居士将何之？”以应试对。僧蹙额曰：“恐来不及矣。此去登陆，消渴即发，寿不过月，奈何远行耶？”孝廉泣下曰：“诚如叶天士言矣。”僧曰：“天土云何？”孝廉曰：“无药可救。”僧曰：“谬哉，药如不能救病，圣贤何必留此一道？”孝廉觉其语有因，跽而请救。僧援之曰：“君登陆时，王家营所有者秋梨也。以后车满载，渴即以梨代茶，饥则蒸梨作膳。约至都食过百觔，即无恙焉。得云无药可救，误人性命耶。”孝廉再拜而退。行抵清河，舍舟登车，果渴病大作矣。如僧言，饮食必以梨，至都平服如故。入闱不售，感僧活命恩，回至金山，以二十金及都中方物为谢。僧收物而却其金曰：“居士过苏城时，再见叶君，令其诊视。如云无疾，即以前言质之。彼如问治疗之人，即以老僧告之，胜于厚惠也。”

    Explanation

    545

    When the boat reached the river mouth, the wind was against us, and we could not cross. My companions and I agreed to visit Jinshan Temple. There was a sign for a medical monk at the temple gate. The scholar-official visited the meditation room, and the monk examined him and said, "Where are you going, sir?" He replied that he was going to take the examination. The monk frowned and said, "I fear it will be too late. If you disembark from here, the symptoms of diabetes will soon manifest, and your lifespan will not exceed a month. How can you then travel far?" The scholar-official wept and said, "It is indeed as Dr. Ye Tian Shi said." The monk asked, "What did Dr. Ye say?" The scholar-official replied, "There is no medicine that can save me." The monk said, "How mistaken! If medicine cannot cure illness, why would sages and virtuous men leave it as a path?" Feeling that the monk's words had a reason, he knelt and begged for help. The monk helped him and said, "When you disembark, all that is available at Wangjia Camp is pears. Load the carriage full of pears, use them as tea when thirsty, and cook them when hungry. By the time you reach the capital, having eaten over a hundred pounds of pears, you will be fine. How could he say there is no medicine to save you, misleading people's lives?" The scholar-official bowed twice and left. We arrived at Qinghe and disembarked from the boat onto a carriage. The symptoms of diabetes indeed became severe. As the monk said, we ate and drank only pears. By the time we reached the capital, I was as healthy as before. I failed in the examination. Grateful for the monk's life-saving kindness, I returned to Jinshan Temple and offered twenty taels of silver and souvenirs from the capital as thanks. The monk accepted the gifts but returned the silver and said, "When you pass through Suzhou City, see Dr. Ye again and ask him to examine you. If he says you are free of disease, then confront him with my earlier words. If he asks about the person who treated you, tell him it was this old monk. It is better than a large gift."

    546

    孝廉如言往见天士，复使诊视曰：“君无疾何治？”孝廉以前言质之，天士命徒查案，相符，曰：“异哉，君其遇仙乎？”孝廉曰：“是佛，非仙。”以老僧言告之。天士曰：“我知之矣。先生请行，我将停业以请益。”遂摘牌散徒，更姓名，衣佣保服，轻舟往投老僧，求役门墙，以习医术。僧许之，日侍左右，见其治过百余人，道亦不相上下。告僧曰：“余亦有所悟矣。请代为立方可乎？”僧曰可。天士作方呈览，僧曰：“汝学已与始苏叶天士相类，何不各树一帜，而依老僧乎？”天士曰：“弟子恐如叶之误人性命，必须精益求精，万无一失，方可救人耳。”僧曰：“善哉，此言胜于叶君矣。”

    Explanation

    546

    The Scholar-official, as instructed, went to see Ye Tianshi and asked him to diagnose, saying, "What need is there for treatment if there is no illness?" The Scholar-official challenged Ye Tianshi with the previous statement. Ye Tianshi ordered his disciples to check the records, which matched, and said, "Strange indeed, are you meeting a god?" The Scholar-official replied, "It is a Buddha, not a god." He told Ye Tianshi about the old monk's words. Ye Tianshi said, "I know it. Please go, I will stop my practice to seek further learning." He then removed his sign, dismissed his disciples, changed his name, and dressed in the clothes of a servant or caretaker. He took a light boat to visit the old monk, seeking to serve at his side to learn medical skills. The old monk agreed, and Ye Tianshi served him daily, seeing him treat more than a hundred people, whose methods were also not far apart. He told the old monk, "I have also gained some insight. May I ask you to prescribe a formula?" The old monk said yes. Ye Tianshi wrote a prescription and presented it for the old monk to review. The old monk said, "Your learning is now similar to that of the original Ye Tianshi. Why not each establish your own school and rely on the old monk?" Ye Tianshi replied, "I fear to mislead people as Ye Tianshi did. I must strive for excellence, ensuring no mistakes, before I can truly help others." The old monk said, "Well said, your words surpass those of Mr. Ye."

    547

    一日，有舁一垂毙之人至，其腹如孕，来人曰：“是人腹痛数年，而今更甚。”僧诊讫，命天士复诊，开方首用白信三分。僧笑曰：“妙哉，汝所以不及我者，谨慎太过。此方须用砒霜一钱，起死回生，永除痰根矣。”天士骇然曰：“此人患虫蛊，以信三分，死其虫足矣。多则人何能堪？”僧曰：“汝既知虫，不知虫之大小乎？此虫已长二十寸余矣，试以三分，不过暂困，后必复作。再投以信，避而不受，则无药可救矣。用一钱，俾虫毙，随矢出，永绝后患，不更妙耶？”天士惑甚。僧立命侍者出白丸纳病人口中，以汤下之，谓来人曰：“速舁回寓，晚必遗矢出虫，俾吾徒观之。”来人唯唯，舁病人去。至夜，果如所言，挑一赤虫来，长二尺余。病人已苏，饥而索食，僧命以参苓作糜进之，旬日痊可。天士心悦诚服，告以真姓名而求益。僧念其虚心向往，与一册而遣之。自是天士学益进，无棘手之症矣。

    Explanation

    547

    One day, a stretcher-borne man who was on the brink of death arrived. His abdomen was swollen like pregnancy. The visitor said, "This man has had abdominal pain for several years, and it has become even worse now." The monk finished his diagnosis and ordered Wu Youke to conduct a second examination. The initial prescription was to use three parts of white medicine. The monk smiled and said, "Wonderful! The reason you cannot match me is that you are too cautious. This formula requires one coin of arsenic to revive the dying, permanently eliminate the root cause of phlegm-related illness." Wu Youke was shocked and said, "This man suffers from a parasitic infection. Using three parts of the medicine would be enough to kill the worms. How could the patient bear a larger dose?" The monk replied, "Since you know about the worms, do you not know their size? These worms have grown to over twenty inches long. If you try three parts, it will only provide temporary relief, and the condition will surely recur. If you give the medicine again, the worms will avoid it and not accept it, leaving no medicine to save him. Use one coin to make the worms die and be expelled with feces, permanently eliminating future problems. Isn't that even better?" Wu Youke was greatly puzzled. The monk immediately ordered the attendant to take a white pill and put it in the patient's mouth, administering it with soup. He told the visitor, "Quickly take the patient back to his residence. By evening, he will certainly defecate and expel the worms, allowing my disciples to observe it." The visitor agreed and took the patient away. That night, as predicted, a red worm over two feet long was picked up. The patient had already recovered and was hungry, asking for food. The monk ordered to make a porridge with ginseng and poria and gave it to him. After ten days, the patient was completely recovered. Wu Youke was heartily satisfied and truly convinced. He told the monk his real name and asked for further knowledge. The monk, considering his humble and aspiring attitude, gave him a book and sent him away. From then on, Wu Youke's studies advanced further, and he had no more difficult cases.

    548

    芗厈曰：医道至叶天士，已成名手，犹耻不及人而精益求精。彼后生小子，不过读得《脉诀》、《本草》，居然吾道在是，大胆行医，人命其何堪哉？

    Explanation

    548

    Xiang Yan said: When the medical art reached Ye Tianshi, it had already produced a master, yet he still felt ashamed of not being as good as others and thus pursued perfection relentlessly. Those young and inexperienced practitioners, who have no more than read Mai Jue and Ben Cao, yet presume that our art lies entirely in their hands, boldly practicing medicine—how could human lives possibly endure such recklessness?

    549

    张廉访
    　　
    　　津门档子班主，以钱十余缗买张姓童子，貌俊而性敏。教之歌，不数月，观听者莫不叫绝。但张故旧家子，父母俱没，为堂兄所略，卖身习败业，郁郁不甘。然年仅十二，不能逋逃也。随班至正定郡，寓土地祠，夜梦锦衣绣裳天使降，从者呼土地接天符，见殿内老翁伏地听命。使者曰：“帝命布疫，授一册一囊，命散药河井，以收数内之人。”翁叩首曰：“小神庙内寓张臬使不应遭殃，何以安顿？”天使曰：“速引之出，而后从事可也。”言毕冉冉乘云去。翁回首曰：“贵人在此耶，速回登厕。小神引道西行，自有善士相遇。班主疫数内人也，性命不保，何能追捕？放心前去。”张喜极而觉，潜起出探。果见老翁执拂前引，随之逾墙，复逾城，甚轻捷。翁指命西行，张力奔至曙，入一城，问人曰灵寿县也。力竭不能前，卧于巨室大门下。

    Explanation

    549

    Zhang the Censor

    The head of a local theatrical troupe in Tianjin bought a boy named Zhang with over ten strings of coins. The boy was handsome and intelligent. After teaching him to sing, he became an excellent performer within a few months, and no one who heard him could help but applaud. However, Zhang came from a noble family, and his parents had both passed away. He was sold by his elder brother into a life of debauchery, which left him deeply dissatisfied. Despite being only twelve years old, he could not escape. He traveled with the troupe to Zhengding Prefecture and stayed at a temple dedicated to the earth god. One night, he dreamed of a celestial being dressed in fine clothes and carrying a heavenly decree, with attendants summoning the earth god to receive it. The divine being said, "The emperor has commanded me to spread a plague. I have been given a book and a bag and instructed to distribute medicine in rivers and wells to save those within the specified numbers." The earth god knelt and asked, "The noble official Zhang, who resides in your temple, should not suffer. How should he be protected?" The celestial being replied, "Quickly guide him out, and then proceed with your task." After saying this, the celestial being ascended into the clouds. The earth god turned back and said, "The noble person is here? Return quickly to the temple and use the restroom. I will lead you westward, and you will meet kind people. The troupe leader is among those who will die from the plague and cannot be pursued. Go with peace of mind." Zhang woke up joyfully and secretly left the temple to explore. Indeed, he saw the earth god leading the way, and they climbed over walls and walls again, moving very swiftly. The earth god pointed westward, and Zhang ran until dawn, entering a city where he learned it was Ling County. Exhausted, he fell asleep at the gate of a large house.

    550

    未几，一翁出，见童子倦眠，貌甚清俊，心爱怜之。唤醒叩其所自，张跪而啼曰：“小人张姓，天津人。父母俱殁，被堂兄欲卖充档班，畏惧行丐至此。如长者见怜，仆役自甘也。”翁曰：“儿与老夫同姓，既有志不愿作档班，岂可为僮仆？随老夫来，有以处子。”呼其子出，曰：“此儿有志向上，后必有为。汝其子之。”其子曰：“此人来历不明，且已行丐，儿不愿有此下贱子也。”翁怒曰：“汝不欲耶，我子之，汝其弟之。”乃引入室，见其爱妾曰：“汝素日以无子为戚，今为汝得一佳儿矣。”命张拜为亲母，妾乐而抚之。翁使入学读书，既勤且敏，五年而学成。

    Explanation

    550

    Not long after, an old man emerged and saw the young boy sleeping tiredly, with a very clear and handsome appearance, and felt affectionate and pitiful towards him. He woke the boy up and asked about his origins. The boy knelt and wept, saying, "I am Zhang, from Tianjin. Both my parents have passed away, and my cousin wanted to sell me to join a band. I fled from this fate and came here as a beggar. If the elder shows mercy, I am willing to be a servant." The old man said, "You share the same surname as I, and you have the resolve not to join the band. How could you become a servant? Come with me, and I will find a way to take care of you." He called his son out and said, "This boy has the ambition to rise, and he will surely achieve something in the future. You should adopt him as your son." The son replied, "This person's background is unclear, and he has already been a beggar. I do not want to have such a low-born child." The old man became angry and said, "If you do not want to, I will adopt him, and you will be his brother." He then brought the boy into the house and introduced him to his beloved concubine, saying, "You have been distressed by not having a child. Now I have found a fine son for you." He ordered Zhang to kneel and call the concubine his mother. The concubine was pleased and took care of him. The old man sent him to school to study, and the boy was both diligent and quick-witted. After five years, he completed his studies.

    551

    文笔清灵，一试入泮，年仅十七。翁为之娶妇分家，亲子得其六，螟蛉得其四。乃兄敢怒而不敢言。未几翁卒，乃兄以异姓乱宗告逐。幸父党及张之同学诸生，辩非异姓，系其父爱继。官怒其兄曰：“汝父尸骨未寒，即欲逐其爱子，以不孝论。”欲重责之。张为哀求，官以张情，断令各安其业。张知日久必不见容，随携受分之物，奉嗣母入都。时前明中叶，大珰王振用事，张以赂结。珰谕学使以张名贡入成均肄业，期满选得中州某县丞，益媚事珰，不十年官至廉访使。值土木之变，王振死，失所依，且忆土地张臬使之言，知官止于是矣。引疾归，置业津门，优游林下，又十余年而卒。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：惜乎，张廉访之为人，虽脱其身，究不能脱档子气，故媚事大珰，以奔竟功名。若有贤父兄教之，得为正人君子，既命应廉访终，必至此地位，益为后人所景仰，岂仅为档子出色已耶？

    Explanation

    551

    Zhang's writing was clear and lively, and at the age of seventeen, he passed the imperial examination and entered the imperial academy. His father arranged for him to marry and split the family property, giving six parts to the biological children and four parts to the adopted children. His elder brother was angry but dared not speak out. Not long after, the father died, and the elder brother reported him for being of a different surname and disrupting the clan, leading to his expulsion. Fortunately, his father's relatives and his fellow students argued that he was not of a different surname, as he was the father's beloved adopted son. The official angrily said to his elder brother, "Your father's bones have not yet turned cold, and you want to expel his beloved son, which is considered filial impiety." He wanted to severely punish him. Zhang pleaded on his behalf, and the official, considering Zhang's plea, ruled that everyone should settle into their respective professions. Zhang knew that he would not be tolerated for long, so he took the property he had received and accompanied his adopted mother to the capital. At the time of the mid-Ming dynasty, the powerful eunuch Wang Zhen held power, and Zhang bribed him. The eunuch instructed the provincial examiner to recommend Zhang for admission to the Imperial Academy. After completing his studies, he was selected as the magistrate of a county in Henan and further flattered the eunuch, rising to the position of censor within ten years. During the Tumu Crisis, when Wang Zhen died, Zhang lost his support and remembered the words of the land surveyor Zhang, realizing that his official career was limited to this point. He then feigned illness and returned home, bought property in Tianjin, lived a leisurely life, and died after more than ten years.

    Xiang Yan said: Alas, Zhang the censor's character, although he escaped his physical bonds, could not escape the influence of his adoptive father. Therefore, he flattered the eunuch to pursue fame and fortune. If he had been taught by virtuous father and elder brother, he could have become a virtuous and upright man, and his position as a censor would have been even more admirable, not just as an adopted son.

    552

    第六卷
    　　
    　　某少君
    　　
    　　少君某，年十七，能诗文而未遇，翩翩美少年也。父以科甲铨得四川县令，少君随任。行至羊肠阪，马逸，颠落崖下，身糜而魂出，随风飘荡，瞬息数千里，求止不得。忽堕于山东历城县村。落间初死男尸壳中，大叫曰：“跌死我也。”见妇与童围绕身旁者，皆止哭曰：“苏矣。”一老媪曰：“气绝逾日，如何得醒？”随有二三父老近身细审曰：“气暖身和，复生无疑。”一家庆幸，频闻欢乐声。媪前抚之曰：“我儿何云跌死，曷为我言之。”少君睁目曰：“汝何人，敢儿我？”父老笑曰：“渠虽少苏，神尚未完。此汝母，如何不识？”又指一丑妇曰：“此汝妻。”指一村童曰：“此汝子，皆识否？”少君起坐曰：“谬甚！谬甚！我某公子，随父莅任。行蜀道上，堕马被风吹至此。

    Explanation

    552

    Volume Six
    A Young Gentleman Named "Someone"

    A young gentleman named "Someone," seventeen years old, was skilled in poetry and prose yet had not yet found his fortune. He was a graceful and handsome young man. His father, having passed the imperial examinations and been appointed magistrate of a county in Sichuan, took his son with him on his official post. Traveling to the winding mountain pass, the horse bolted, and the young man fell from the cliff, his body shattered and his soul departing. It followed the wind, drifting for thousands of miles in an instant, unable to find a resting place. Suddenly, it fell into a village in Licheng County, Shandong Province. It entered the body of a recently deceased man, and cried out, "I have fallen to my death!" Seeing the woman and children gathered around him, they all stopped crying and said, "He has revived!" An elderly woman said, "His breath had ceased for more than a day—how could he possibly awaken?" Then, two or three elderly men approached him closely and examined him, saying, "His breath is warm, his body is harmonious—his revival is certain." The whole family rejoiced, and the sound of joy was frequently heard. The old woman approached and gently touched him, saying, "My son, why do you say you fell to your death? Why do you speak to me?" The young man opened his eyes and said, "Who are you, and how dare you call me your son?" The elders laughed and said, "Though he has slightly revived, his spirit is not yet whole. This is your mother—how could you not recognize her?" Then, pointing to an unattractive woman, they said, "This is your wife." Pointing to a village child, they said, "This is your son—do you recognize them?" The young man sat up and exclaimed, "How absurd! How absurd! I am the son of someone, accompanying my father on his official duties. While traveling along the Sichuan road, I fell from my horse and was blown by the wind to this place."

    553

    并未娶妻，焉得有子？且我母乃诰命孺人，村妪何得冒认？”父老曰：“休呓语，汝不信，可以镜自照。”少君对镜，四十余岁之麻胡也。不禁扑镜大哭曰：“还我本来面目，我愿死不愿生矣。”父老粲然皆笑，老媪曰：“谅我儿初醒，神尚模糊，诸公勿扰之，俾静养数日自然复原。”众散去，少君拥衾垂首丧气，无如饥肠作辘辘声，丑妇以半规糠饼饲之，粗粝难堪，勉强吞咽，泪涔涔下。丑妇曰：“我与阿姑守君十余日，已绝粮三四日，仅食槐皮野菜耳。以君初复需调养，忍耻向邻人乞得此饼，亦大人情。君犹以为不足耶？”少君大声叱之出。目睹败屋三椽，土炕上所拥者，破衾败絮蓝缕衣裤一堆，厨灶亦在房中，气息秽不可耐。回思居厦屋，役奴仆，衣罗绮，食膏粱，判若天渊。怦怦懊恼，求死不得。至晚妻儿皆来就宿，少君又大叱之，闻老媪唤其妇与孙去。

    Explanation

    553

    If he has not married, how can he have a son? Moreover, my mother is a titled lady, how can a village woman dare to claim kinship?" The elders said, "Stop your nonsense, if you don't believe, look in the mirror yourself." The young master looked in the mirror and saw a hunchbacked old man in his forties. He could not help but fall to the ground and weep, saying, "Restore my true self, I would rather die than live like this." The elders laughed heartily. The old woman said, "My son is just waking up, his mind is still hazy. Please do not disturb him, let him rest and recover for a few days, and he will return to normal." The crowd dispersed, and the young master lay in bed, head bowed, dispirited. But his stomach growled with hunger, and the unattractive woman fed him a half-circle of bran cake, which was coarse and unpleasant. He swallowed it勉强, tears streaming down his face. The unattractive woman said, "I and my mother-in-law have been taking care of you for over ten days, and we have been without food for three or four days, surviving on wild vegetables and bark. Since you are just waking up and need to recover, I have humbly begged the neighbors for this cake, which is a token of kindness. Do you still think it is not enough?" The young master shouted at her to leave. Looking around, he saw a dilapidated three-room house, a dirt bed covered with torn quilts and rags, and a kitchen in the same room, filled with an unbearable stench. He recalled his past life, living in a grand mansion, served by servants, wearing fine clothes, and eating delicacies, feeling as if he had fallen into a different world. He was filled with regret and a desire to die, but could not find a way to end his life. By evening, his wife and children came to sleep with him, and he shouted at them to leave. He heard the old woman call her daughter-in-law and granddaughter away.

    554

    次日，邻翁来殷勤问候曰：“吾与君至交也，闻君病小痊，性情大变，亲母妻子视若寇仇，恐乡党不能容此不孝不义之人也。将来亲戚不齿，邻里不顾，君又贫困，何以仰事俯育，以终乃身乎？用敢奉劝。”少君泣曰：“承翁美意，请辨我语言是足下好友之音乎？”翁曰：“人是音非，吾固知君借尸返魂也。今既为某人矣，得不为某人之事乎？譬如仕宦本督抚也，降为杂职，能不安杂职之分而从其政乎？君即舍此而就尊公，面目既非，纵怜而育之，他人必不相容。”少君思其言中理，曰：“翁所教良是。

    Explanation

    554

    The next day, the neighboring old man came to sincerely greet and ask, "I am a close friend of yours. I heard that your illness has slightly improved, but your personality has changed greatly. You treat your mother, wife, and children as enemies. I fear that the village cannot tolerate such an unfilial and unrighteous person. In the future, your relatives will not acknowledge you, and your neighbors will not care for you. You are also poor. How will you support your parents and raise your children, to end your life? Dare I offer advice?" The young man wept and said, "I accept the old man's good intentions. Please clarify if what I say is the voice of your good friend?" The old man replied, "The person is right, but the sound is wrong. I know that you have reincarnated in another body. Now that you are someone else, should you not act as that person would? For example, a high-ranking official, if reduced to a minor position, can one not adhere to the duties of a minor position and govern accordingly? If you leave this and return to your father, your face is no longer the same. Even if they pity and raise you, others will not tolerate you." The young man thought his words were reasonable and said, "The old man's teachings are indeed sound."

    555

    后将若何？”翁曰：“母其母子其子，仍营趁以自食其力，承此一家以了此躯而已。”少君曰：“我前生读书作文曾应童试，营趁之事一无所能，奈何？”翁曰：“能如是乎。请为君游扬闾里，以训童蒙。亦自食其力之一道也。”少君起谢之。翁为布告乡党，人素知某为佣保，目不识丁，忽闻一病而能诗文，远近好奇之士成来共语。少君引经据典，侃侃而谈，众皆悦服，从游者甚众。所得修脯以养一家，绰有余裕。但少君自授徒后借居古庙，竟不归家。母来谈论，格格不入，妻子更不顾而问矣。然得温饱，皆乐而安之。

    Explanation

    555

    After the transformation, what will happen?" The old man said, "The mother should be like a mother, and the son should be like a son. Continue to work and support yourselves, to care for this family and fulfill your lives." The young man said, "In my past life, I studied and wrote essays, and I was once a candidate in the imperial examination. I am completely incapable of any practical work. What should I do?" The old man said, "Is it possible to do so? I will help you gain recognition in the local community, to teach young children. It is also a way to support yourself." The young man got up and thanked him. The old man announced this in the village. People who knew the young man as a servant and laborer, who were illiterate, suddenly heard that he could write poetry and prose. People from far and near came to discuss with him. The young man cited classics and authoritative texts, spoke confidently and fluently, and everyone was pleased and convinced. Many people followed him as a teacher. The income from teaching was more than enough to support his family. However, after starting to teach, the young man lived temporarily in an ancient temple and did not return home. His mother came to talk to him, but they were completely incompatible, and his wife and children no longer even asked about him. Yet, with their basic needs met, all were happy and content.

    556

    未几，赴试，旋入学为名诸生。时有客入蜀，少君作禀告乃翁。大令奇其事，寄资作札招之去。少君前生本行兰，因其聪敏俊秀，父母偏爱之。上有两兄，皆不得意。后闻其堕马死，父母衰恸而两兄窃喜。今又闻其来也皆惧。及相晤，其貌不扬，父甚狐疑，两兄直叱为妄冒，母亦不认。少君历举幼时游戏事及父母秘密之言，委曲道达。父虽垂怜，而母与两兄决意逐之。父知必不见容，私与千金遣归山左，家以小康。谈者曰此人现在，后不知作何结局矣。
    　　
    　　或曰：“异哉，少君之一跌，既未入幽冥，又不经神判，以翩翩佳公子顿变而为窭人，何异于膴仕之投荒者。造化弄人至此极矣。”芗厈曰：“此不弟之显罚也。观其后之不见容于乃兄，即可见前之乃兄受侮不少。若使再世得报，人皆不知。直以现身作法，以示鉴于人伦。谚云，一失足成千古恨，再回头已百年身。为少君诵矣。”

    Explanation

    556

    Not long after, the young man took the imperial examination and soon enrolled as a prominent student. At that time, a guest arrived in Sichuan, and the young man wrote a letter to inform his father. The magistrate found the matter strange and sent money and a letter inviting him to leave. The young man's previous life had been that of a commoner, but because of his intelligence and good looks, his parents favored him. He had two elder brothers who were both unsuccessful. Later, they heard that he had died from falling off a horse, and his parents mourned deeply while his brothers secretly rejoiced. Now, when they heard that he had returned, they were all afraid. When they met, he was not attractive in appearance, and his father was in doubt. His two brothers openly accused him of falsely claiming to be someone else, and his mother also did not recognize him. The young man listed in detail childhood games and secret words of his parents, explaining them thoroughly. Although his father felt pity, his mother and brothers decided to drive him away. Knowing that he would not be accepted, the father privately gave him a thousand gold pieces and sent him back to the eastern mountains, where his family became moderately wealthy. People say he is still alive now, but it is unknown what his future holds.

    Some said, "Strange indeed, the young man's fall did not lead him to the underworld nor did he face divine judgment. How could a handsome young nobleman suddenly become a poor man? It is no different from a high-ranking official being exiled to a remote place. Fate plays tricks on people to this extreme." The author said, "This is a clear punishment for his disrespect. By seeing that he is not accepted by his elder brothers, one can see that his elder brothers suffered much humiliation in the past. If he were to seek revenge in another life, people would not know. He serves as an example to show a lesson in human relationships. A proverb says, 'One misstep can lead to eternal regret, and turning back would be like starting over after a hundred years.' Let this be a lesson for the young man."

    557

    许宗伯
    　　
    　　吾乡许大宗伯，讳汝霖。幼失怙恃，终鲜兄弟，室如悬罄，地无立锥。依寺僧训蒙度日，每闻僧作法事归，必聚论人家字画。有云堂幅好者，有云单条佳者，有云横披更妙者，有云楹联出色者，争论不一。间有戚友延僧，宅中实无字画，而论者如故。公大疑，访诸其徒。徒哂曰：“彼所论非真字画，凡请持经之家，妇女不避。我等得以纵观，归而各诩目力以隐语评之。所云堂幅者，其家正妻，横披者妾，单条者女，楹联者婢也。我不敢在师前隐讳，然师亦不可为外人道也。”公深恨之而无如何。嗣应试，屡冠童子军，苦不得售。年将而立，无与论婚者。

    Explanation

    557

    Xu the Grand Censor

    　　Xu Rulin, the Grand Censor of my hometown, was orphaned at a young age and had no brothers or sisters. His home was as empty as a suspended bell, and he had no land to stand a cone. He depended on a temple monk to teach and support himself, and every time the monk returned from performing rituals, they would gather to discuss people's paintings and calligraphy. Some would say the large hanging scrolls were good, others would praise the vertical scrolls, and still others would claim the horizontal scrolls were even better, while some would say the couplets were outstanding. They debated endlessly. Occasionally, relatives or friends would invite the monk to their homes, where there were no paintings or calligraphy, yet they still discussed as before. Xu was greatly puzzled and asked his disciple about it. The disciple laughed and said, "What they were discussing were not the actual paintings and calligraphy. In any household that invites us to hold scriptures, the women do not hide. We get to see them all, and upon returning, we boast of our keen eyesight and evaluate them in coded language. The large hanging scrolls refer to the principal wife, the horizontal scrolls to the concubine, the vertical scrolls to the daughter, and the couplets to the maid. I dare not hide this from my teacher, but you must not tell outsiders either." Xu was deeply resentful but could do nothing about it. Later, he took the exams and often ranked first in the preliminary exams, but he struggled to pass. By the time he was nearing thirty, there was no one to discuss marriage with him.

    558

    乡有葭莩亲，以耕读为业，时全家患疫，惟一女无恙。戚翁静卧床中，夜闻众鬼议曰：“明日许宗伯来视疾，我曹避何处？”一鬼曰：“翁床下大瓮，聚处其中，亦可暂避一时耳。”皆应曰可。是日，公闻戚病，果来探视。翁见公大悦，使其女奉以馔，甚丰。公不安欲去。翁跃起曰：“有事烦君，看老夫薄面万不可辞。”公食毕，请所事，翁曰：“请以纸硃书‘大宗伯封’四字，封老夫床下瓮口，举而投诸河，即感盛德矣。”公不解其意，笑从之，为弃瓮。归，病者果痊愈。翁益喜，遣媒以女订婚。年齿皆长，急须聘娶。翁为公作大布衣冠，草草成礼。公无力备花烛，代以二油灯。其夫人恒曰：“妾每见取妇家，必彩舆、鼓乐、鸣炮，君今一事不备，岂非妾命累君耶？”公曰：“卿今羡此不得，恐异日闻之生厌耳。”夫人曰：“妾乐此不疲，何厌之有？”

    Explanation

    558

    A Village with Distant Relatives, Engaged in Farming and Reading

    In a village, there were distant relatives who made their living through farming and reading. One day, the entire family fell ill with an epidemic, but only one daughter remained unharmed. An elderly relative, Uncle, lay quietly in bed. That night, he overheard the ghosts discussing among themselves: "Tomorrow, Xu Zongbo, the respected official, will come to visit and check on the illness. Where should we hide?" One ghost suggested, "Under Uncle's bed, there is a large jar. We can gather in it and temporarily hide for a while." All agreed, saying it was a good idea. That day, the official heard about the relative's illness and indeed came to visit. Uncle was very pleased to see the official and had his daughter serve him a very abundant meal. The official felt uneasy and wanted to leave. Uncle jumped up and said, "There is something I need your help with. For the sake of my old face, you must not refuse." After finishing the meal, the official asked what the matter was. Uncle said, "Please write the four characters '大宗伯封' on paper with vermilion ink, seal the mouth of the large jar under my bed, lift it, and throw it into the river. This will be a great act of kindness." The official did not understand the meaning but laughed and agreed, throwing away the jar. When he returned, the sick person indeed recovered. Uncle became even happier and sent a matchmaker to arrange a marriage for his daughter. They were both of marriageable age and urgently needed to get married. Uncle made simple clothes and a hat for the official, and the wedding ceremony was hastily conducted. The official could not afford the wedding candles and instead used two oil lamps. His wife often said, "I always see the bride's family with colorful sedan chairs, music, and firecrackers. You have nothing prepared today. Isn't this because of my bad luck?" The official replied, "You can't envy this now, but I fear you will grow tired of hearing about it in the future." His wife said, "I enjoy it without tiring. Why should I be tired of it?"

    559

    未几，公入泮，将应秋闱，无力买舟，惟趁夜航之便，行百里不过数十钱。然无卧具者，倒不准趁船。公谋诸僧，曰：“盍以敝毡裹两破蒲团，用充卧具，谁敢拒之。”公如法趁船赴省。停泊时，舟子举其具掷岸上树间。公亦不顾，入茶肆，旋有驿卒乘骑至，亦系马树下。马饥，见破毡内有草，蹄而食之。群哗曰：“许爷行李被马吃矣。”公亦大笑而去。

    Explanation

    559

    Not long after, the gentleman entered the local school, preparing to take the autumn examination. Lacking the means to hire a boat, he took advantage of the night ferry service, traveling a hundred miles for no more than a few coins. However, those without sleeping gear were not allowed to board the boat. The gentleman consulted the monks and said, "Why not wrap two broken pútuán in a worn felt mat to serve as sleeping gear? Who would dare to refuse?" Following this plan, he boarded the boat and made his way to the provincial capital. When the boat was moored, the boatman took his sleeping gear and tossed it among the trees on the shore. The gentleman paid no heed and entered a tea house. Soon after, a postal official arrived on horseback and tied the horse beneath a tree. The horse, hungry, saw grass inside the broken felt mat and stamped on it with its hoof before eating it. The crowd exclaimed, "Master Xu's luggage has been eaten by the horse!" The gentleman merely laughed and departed.

    560

    后举孝廉，捷南宫，入词林，转春坊，由卿贰外转方伯，旋晋中丞。中丞官廨限于地，横而长，上房距鼓亭炮台密迩，早晚鼓吹鸣炮，夫人不得安眠，谓公曰：“日日鼓乐，未免喧阗，盍命稍停乎？”公笑曰：“卿昔求之不得，今果厌烦耶？我所以日日为此者，非自鸣得意，所以补卿昔日之不足耳。卿既足矣，从此收声，不仅稍停而已。”果内召为大宗伯，妻封一品夫人。公卒时遗教子孙，永不许延僧作佛事，违者以不孝论，实有憾于字画之说也。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：许公未达时，可谓君子固穷。吾乡人至今称物之不堪者，曰“许老爷铺盖。”又曰：“许老爷鼓吹，补补你。”竟成谚语。至其文集，为国初名家政绩，足以上动圣明。不但能言之者少，即知之者亦稀，何风俗之陋耶？

    Explanation

    560

    He was later selected as a filial and honest official, passed the imperial examinations, entered the literary community, transferred to the Spring Palace, and rose from a lower-ranking official to a regional governor. Soon after, he was promoted to the position of Zhongcheng. The Zhongcheng's official residence was limited in space, being long and narrow, with the main building close to the drum tower and artillery platform. The daily rituals of drumming, music, and cannon fire made his wife unable to sleep. She told him, "Every day there is such noise, it's unavoidable. Why don't you ask for it to stop for a while?" He smiled and said, "Didn't you once seek this but couldn't get it? Now you find it annoying? I do this every day not to show off, but to make up for what you lacked in the past. Now that you are satisfied, let's stop completely, not just pause for a while." Indeed, he was later summoned to a high-ranking position in the imperial court, and his wife was granted the title of First-Rank Lady. When he died, he left a will instructing his descendants never to invite monks for Buddhist rituals, and those who violated this would be considered disrespectful, as he had reservations about the practice of calligraphy or painting.

    Xiang Yan said: "Before Xu Gong attained his high position, he could be called a man of virtue in poverty. Even today, people in my hometown refer to things of poor quality as 'Xu Lao Ye's bedding.' They also say, 'Xu Lao Ye's drumming and music, to make up for you.' These have become common sayings. As for his literary works, they are the important records of a prominent statesman in the early years of the dynasty, valuable enough to move even the most enlightened rulers. Not only are they spoken of by few, but even those who know of them are rare, how backward are our customs!"

    561

    巧令三则
    　　
    　　有幕客攫其居停误征已免钱粮印簿讹控者，状已上达，簿未呈堂。宪司咸以此人铁据在握，难以理断，其能者不过私与往还，问其所欲，图赎其簿销之而已。此人索数千金，不得丝毫缺。或云，即予千金，倘抱赃出首，中人亦连累无涯矣。皆窘于计。宪司会议时，有巧令在侧微笑。诘之，令曰：“若交职，三日办矣。”宪欣然委之，令携卷回，不动声色。至第三日，上下皆曰：“限期已到，尚未佥票，得毋误乎？”令曰：“余几忘之。”乃出，升公座，唤三班役来前曰：“有善斗殴者否？”众皆骇，莫敢应，一强项者出曰：“役能之。”令喜曰：“汝能，必知我意。今某处有幕客某寓，汝往生事激之斗。但许自伤，勿许伤人，受伤即来鸣冤，汝知之乎？”役曰唯。即赴客寓，向其人大呼曰：“汝从某县来，我载汝至此，今已逾月，所欠车价若干，速给我，不能再待矣。

    Explanation

    561

    Three Clever Strategies

    There was a guest at a host's residence who mistakenly claimed exemption from taxes based on an incorrect document. The complaint had already been reported to a higher authority, but the document had not yet been presented in court. The officials all believed that the complainant had solid evidence and that it was difficult to resolve the case. The capable ones would only privately negotiate with the complainant, asking what he wanted and trying to buy back the document to resolve the matter. The complainant demanded several thousand gold pieces and would not accept any less. Some said that even if they were given a thousand gold pieces, if the complainant were to turn in the stolen goods, others involved would also be implicated. Everyone was at a loss for what to do. During a meeting of the officials, a clever official was present and smiled. When questioned, the official said, "If I take on this case, I can resolve it within three days." The officials readily entrusted him with the case. The official took the documents and returned without showing any emotion. By the third day, everyone said, "The deadline has passed, but no action has been taken. Is there a mistake?" The official said, "I almost forgot about it." He then stepped out, ascended to the public seat, and called three groups of officials to the front, saying, "Is there anyone skilled in fighting?" Everyone was startled and no one dared to respond. A bold individual stepped forward and said, "I can do it." The official was pleased and said, "You can, you must understand my intentions. Now, there is a guest at a certain place who has a residence. You go there and provoke a fight. Allow only self-inflicted injuries, do not allow harm to others. If someone is injured, report it to the authorities. Do you understand?" The official replied, "Yes." He immediately went to the guest's residence and loudly accused him, saying, "You came from a certain county. I brought you here, and now it has been over a month. The amount you owe for transportation is substantial. Give it to me quickly, as I cannot wait any longer."

    562

    ”客大怒曰：“我来时仆夫非汝，何来恶棍，敢肆讹诈！”役詈不已，客推之出，役即自伤其首回，县令未退堂，即呼冤入跪。正验伤，客亦衣冠至，以棍徒凭空讹诈具禀。役与争辩，令曰：“无哗，此易辨耳。汝既载客来，客行李若干，汝必知之。”令逐—报明核对，役不能知，妄报数物。客大笑，称令贤明。令问之，曰：“所报全非，自愿书单呈验。”令即饬书，带役数人往客寓，将行李捡来当堂查验，与所书单无异。于行箧中搜得印簿，曰：“此系官文，何得私自携取？本应治罪，念汝尚属斯文，姑全颜面。”命取火焚之。客争曰：“案已上控，簿不可焚。”时人多手快，已成灰烬。令大笑曰：“汝肯讹人，无怪人来讹汝。天道好还，汝知之乎？第我治下不容奸险之徒，即备文递回原籍可也。”客知据已毁，无能为，隐忍吞声而去。令即缴卷销案，宪司优奖之。

    Explanation

    562

    The guest became greatly angry and said, "The servant who accompanied me was not you—what kind of rascal are you, daring to extort me fraudulently?" The official kept cursing, and the guest pushed him out. The official immediately injured his own head and returned. The county magistrate had not yet left the court, but he called the accused to kneel and plead guilty. While the magistrate was examining the injury, the guest arrived in full attire and submitted a formal report accusing the rascal of extorting him without cause. The official disputed this, but the magistrate said, "Be quiet—this is easy to resolve. Since you brought the guest here, how many items of his luggage did you have? You must know." The magistrate had him report each item one by one for verification, but the official could not name them accurately and falsely claimed several items. The guest laughed and praised the magistrate as wise. The magistrate asked him, "What you reported was entirely wrong—why not voluntarily write down a list for verification?" The magistrate immediately ordered a document to be prepared and took several officials with him to the guest’s residence, where they brought the luggage in for inspection. The items matched exactly what the official had written. While searching through the guest’s belongings, they discovered an official register. The magistrate said, "This is an official document—how could you have taken it privately? You should have been punished, but since you are still a member of the literati, I will spare your dignity." He ordered fire to be brought and had the document burned. The guest protested, "The case has already been reported to higher authorities—the register cannot be burned!" But before he could act, many people quickly snatched it and reduced it to ashes. The magistrate laughed and said, "You were willing to defraud others—no wonder others came to defraud you. Do you not know that the way of heaven favors return? However, in my jurisdiction, I cannot tolerate deceitful and dangerous people. I will prepare a document and send you back to your native place." Knowing that the evidence had been destroyed and that he could do nothing, the official left in silence, swallowing his anger. The magistrate then submitted the case file and closed the case, and the provincial administration commended him highly.

    563

    有刘姓者，孤独少年，入赘李老家。李以其稚弱无能，虐之。刘不堪，潜投仕宦为仆，得王宠眷。数年积金四百余，辞归，与其妻谋置产业。妻乃炫述于父母，李老生心，欣然设宴为婿洗尘。誉而醉之，且曰：“汝妻年幼，交以多金恐不胜任。况汝须外出谋事，以少妇居守，得无穿窬之虑乎？盍交老夫权为收藏，可以无虑。”刘唯唯，出金点交，八宝十六件也。次日，刘酒醒而悔，亟向李老索银。李曰：“汝贫如丐，寄食我家，邻里咸知，焉得多金寄顿？不思为汝育妻恩，反肆讹耶？”其女助婿争论，李老大怒曰：“女生外向，真不可与处矣。”逐其夫妇出诸大门之外。刘冤忿兴讼，以妻为证。县令曰：“汝物无凭，妻不可以为证。汝妻父曰女生外向，此言诚然。我不能直，汝毋干犯，义之责也。”挥之退。刘素稔巧令名，往陈其苦，令曰：“隔境无能为力。”刘曰：“夭下贤使君惟有阔下，若不肯治理，则无官能明此狱矣。

    Explanation

    563

    There was a man surnamed Liu, a lonely young man who entered into a marriage alliance with Li Lao's family. Li Lao mistreated him because he was young and weak. Liu could not bear it, secretly became a servant in the service of a lord, and gained the lord's favor. After several years, he accumulated more than four hundred pieces of gold, and when he returned home, he discussed with his wife about acquiring property. His wife then boasted to her parents, which made Li Lao jealous, and he happily held a banquet to welcome his son-in-law. He praised him and got him drunk, saying, "Your wife is young, and entrusting her with a large sum of money might be too much for her to handle. Besides, you need to go out to make a living, leaving a young woman alone at home, there might be worries about theft. Why don't you let me keep it temporarily, so you won't have to worry?" Liu nodded, handed over the gold, which amounted to eight treasures in sixteen pieces. The next day, after Liu sobered up, he regretted it and hurriedly asked Li Lao for his money back. Li Lao said, "You are as poor as a beggar, living off my family, and everyone in the neighborhood knows it. How could you have so much money to deposit? Don't you think of the kindness of raising your wife? Are you trying to defraud me?" His daughter helped her husband argue, and Li Lao became very angry, saying, "Women favor their husbands, truly, I cannot live with you." He then chased the couple out of the front gate. Liu, filled with resentment, filed a lawsuit, using his wife as a witness. The county magistrate said, "You have no proof of your claim, and your wife cannot be a witness. Your father-in-law said that women favor their husbands, which is true. I cannot rule in your favor, you should not provoke, it is your duty to uphold justice." He dismissed them. Liu, who was familiar with the magistrate's reputation for being clever, went to present his grievances, but the magistrate said, "I have no power over cases from other jurisdictions." Liu replied, "The only virtuous official in the world is the one in Kuai, if he does not handle this case, no official can clarify it."

    564

    ”哀之切，令笑曰：“若必欲余明此讼，须暂禁囹圄，汝愿之否？”刘曰：“果能明此，虽刀杖加身亦甘承受，况暂禁耶？”令即梏收之。乃移文县令曰：“日者获大盗张三，据供劫得某事主家银四百余两，若干锭件，寄顿贵县某村大窝主李老家，希即委员带捕，查起赃银，连窝主李老解质”云云。县令见系盗劫重情，即身自查抄，人赃并获，解交此。令乃涂刘面以墨，衣囚衣，械系于堂。呼李老诘之曰：“此囚供在某家劫银四百余两，八宝十六件，寄汝家，今所起赃数相符。

    Explanation

    564

    The magistrate, moved by deep sorrow, laughed and said, "If you insist on me clarifying this case, you must temporarily imprison him in prison. Do you agree?" Liu replied, "If you can indeed clarify this, even if I am beaten with sticks and rods, I will willingly endure it. How much more so for temporary imprisonment?" The magistrate immediately put him in shackles and took him away. Then he issued an official document to the county magistrate saying, "The other day, we captured a notorious thief named Zhang San. According to his confession, he robbed a certain household of over four hundred taels of silver, several ingots and pieces, and hid them at the home of a major criminal named Li Lao in a certain village in your county. I hope you will send officials to arrest him immediately to investigate and recover the stolen silver, and to arrest the major criminal Li Lao along with the stolen goods." The county magistrate, seeing the gravity of the theft, personally conducted the search and found the thief and the stolen goods, and handed them over to the magistrate. The magistrate then smeared Liu's face with ink, dressed him in prison clothes, and shackled him in the hall. He called Li Lao and questioned him, saying, "This prisoner confessed that he robbed over four hundred taels of silver and sixteen pieces of treasure from a certain household, and hid them at your home. The amount of stolen goods we have recovered matches his confession."

    565

    汝为盗窝，罪十枭首，据实陈明，勿自庸三木也。”李老呼冤曰：“此银实系小人之婿刘某寄存者，闻其得自随官，是否属实，请拘刘某与张三质之，以明小人之冤。”令笑曰：“若见刘某，汝又将图赖矣。”李老曰：“与其冤诛，莫若明心。召刘某与张三质对，可见小人不知情，庶望一线生路，奚肯贪财舍命耶？”令曰：“若然，则刘某在是矣。”乃释其桎梏，使渍面易服相见。李大惭无词。令乃给还刘银，而薄责李曰：“余为留翁婿情也。”刘感激涕零而去，李亦从此悔过矣。

    Explanation

    565

    The magistrate said, "You are a den of thieves, guilty of a crime punishable by decapitation ten times over. State the facts clearly, and do not avoid the consequences of your actions with the three wooden instruments." The old man Li cried out in his innocence, saying, "This silver was indeed entrusted to me by my son-in-law Liu, who heard that it was obtained from a government official. Is this true? Please bring Liu and Zhang San to testify, so that my innocence may be proven." The magistrate smiled and said, "If you see Liu, you will try to shift the blame onto him again." Li replied, "It is better to prove my innocence than to be unjustly executed. By calling Liu and Zhang San to testify, it can be shown that I am unaware of the crime, and I may hope for a glimmer of hope for survival. How could I possibly be so greedy as to risk my life for money?" The magistrate said, "If so, then Liu is here." He then released Li from his bonds, made him stain his face and change his clothes, and had them meet. Li was ashamed and speechless. The magistrate returned Liu's silver and lightly scolded Li, saying, "I am sparing you for the sake of the relationship between father-in-law and son-in-law." Liu was deeply grateful and teary-eyed as he left, and Li also repented from that point onward.

    566

    某县尉与其大令有隙，面和而心违。一日在令帐房闲话，见几上有入钱簿，内书某季收陋规若干，某役手，某案收钱若干，不一而足。尉俟令回首时，潜藏而退。令送客回，不见此簿，知入尉手矣。所载赃私累累，必受其讹，惶恐之至。函致巧令商之，复书曰：“慎勿言，见尉时谈笑如恒，若无事然，饬库书取银数百两，备文批解杂税，封贮帐房柜内。签差翌日起解，夜于墙上钻穴而入取回内署。次日即呼尉带捕来验，云是夜被窃杂税银若干，公文一角，入钱总簿一本。申明宪司，渠敢自露乎？”令如法行之，旋即通报，自请处分。尉知其心而不敢言，恐干重咎，将所藏之私簿暗毁矣。

    Explanation

    566

    A county magistrate and a county official had a falling out, though they appeared to be on good terms. One day, while chatting in the magistrate's office, the official noticed a ledger on the table, which recorded various unofficial fees collected during different seasons, the names of the collectors, and the amounts received in various cases. When the magistrate turned his back, the official secretly took the ledger and left. Later, when the magistrate returned from sending off guests, he found the ledger missing and realized it was in the hands of the official. The ledger contained records of numerous illicit funds, and the official would surely accuse him of wrongdoing, causing the magistrate great fear. He wrote a letter to the official, suggesting a plan. The official replied: "Do not mention it. When you see me, act as usual, laughing and talking as if nothing happened. Order the treasury to take out several hundred taels of silver, prepare a document to transfer miscellaneous taxes, and seal and store it in the accounting room's cabinet. Appoint someone to escort the silver the next day, and at night, drill a hole in the wall to retrieve it back to the inner office. The next day, call me to bring the captives for inspection, claiming that the silver was stolen during the night, along with a document and the total ledger. Report this to the higher authority; how dare he reveal the truth?" The magistrate followed the plan, immediately reported the incident, and requested punishment for himself. The official, knowing the magistrate's intentions, dared not speak up, fearing severe consequences, and secretly destroyed the private ledger he had hidden.

    567

    芗厈曰：彼诈而我诈之，强中更有强中手。书曰：“诪张为幻。”至此，令人不可测而可笑。盖人情欺愚而畏智，是故惧讼者，讼即随之。若烛其奸，又操其胜具，将不战而胜矣。
    　　
    　　正梦
    　　
    　　凡人之寤也，至人无梦，愚人无梦。然孔子梦周公，非至人之梦征耶？惟愚人无梦，信然。吾家有一婶、一婢、一仆，皆曰：“睡则与死无异，何有梦耶？世之言梦者莫非妄也。”终身不信梦，此其神昏耳。盖梦者，人之神识为之。

    Explanation

    567

    Xiang Yan said: If they deceive, I will deceive them back; there's always someone stronger than the strongest. The Book of Documents says: "To deceive by pretense." To this extent, it makes one both unpredictable and laughable. For human nature tends to deceive the foolish and fear the wise, hence those who fear lawsuits will find themselves in lawsuits. If one exposes their deceit and possesses the means to win, one can achieve victory without fighting.

    On Dreams

    In general, when people wake, sages do not dream, and fools do not dream. Yet Confucius dreamed of the Duke of Zhou, is this not a sign of sages dreaming? Only fools do not dream, and this is true. My family has a matron, a maid, and a servant, all of whom say: "Sleeping is no different from death, so how could there be dreams? Those who talk about dreams are all delusional." They never believe in dreams, which is due to their mental confusion. Dreams are the product of the mind's activity.

    568

    有幻梦者，如遇水火刀兵之厄，此五脏病情也；有噩梦者，如得富贵神仙之乐，此生平妄念也；有正梦者，如逢过去未来之事，虽隔数十年之久，莫不符合，此则有默契之灵也。
    　　
    　　昔者吾发王君未达时，梦胖大和尚执佛，招之登一高楼，飞檐重拱，金碧辉煌，四面窗棂洞启，一片空明。大河环绕，水云飘渺间，荡心旷目，诗兴勃然。正吟哦时，和尚曰：“曷不观此对乎？”举目果见长联，共一百七十二字，其词曰：“五百里滇池奔来眼底，喜茫茫空阔无边。看东骧神骏，西翥灵仪，北走蜿蜒，南翔缟素。高人韵士，何妨选胜登临，尽蝦激螺舟，梳裹就风鬟雾鬓，任蘋天苇地，点缀些翠羽丹霞。莫辜负四围香稻，万顷晴渡，九夏芙蓉，三春杨柳。”下联曰：“数千年往事注到心头，叹滚滚英雄何在，昔汉习楼船，唐标铁柱，宋挥玉斧，元跨革囊。

    Explanation

    568

    Those who have dreamt of illusions, such as encountering the calamities of water, fire, and weapons, these are the symptoms of internal organ diseases; those who have had nightmares, such as experiencing the joy of wealth, nobility, and immortality, these are the delusions of one's lifetime; those who have had true dreams, such as encountering past and future events, even though they are separated by many decades, there is no discrepancy, this is due to the subtle connection of the spirit.

    In the past, when I first met Wang Jun, he was not yet successful. He dreamt of a fat, large monk holding a Buddha statue, inviting him to climb a high building with flying eaves, heavy brackets, and golden splendor, windows on all sides wide open, a clear and bright space. Surrounded by a large river, in the misty waters and clouds, stirring the heart and expanding the view, poetic inspiration surges. Just as he was reciting, the monk said, "Why not look at this couplet?" Upon looking up, he indeed saw a long couplet, comprising 172 characters, its words saying: "The 500-mile Dianchi Lake rushes into view, pleased with the boundless expanse. Looking at the majestic horses in the east, the divine instruments in the west, the winding paths in the north, the white garments flying in the south. Elegant scholars, why not choose the best place to ascend and enjoy the view, all the boats with shrimp and snail decorations, groomed into hair like wind and mist, leaving the reed-filled sky and marshes, adorned with emerald feathers and crimson clouds. Do not miss the fragrant rice around, ten thousand acres of sunny waters, lotuses in the ninth summer, willows in the third spring." The lower couplet says: "Thousands of years of past events pour into the heart, mourning the passing of countless heroes. In the past, the Han dynasty practiced naval warfare, the Tang dynasty erected iron pillars, the Song dynasty wielded jade axes, the Yuan dynasty crossed rivers in leather bags."

    569

    伟烈丰功，费尽移山心力，总朱帘画栋，卷不起暮雨朝云，即断碣残碑，都付与荒烟夕照。只赢得几杵疏钟，半江渔水，数行秋雁，一枕清霜。”反复吟诵，藻畅襟灵，不忍弃去。忽闻大声叱曰：“此大观楼古人名句，俗吏何知？在此窃视。着黄巾力士驱逐。”见金甲伟人执鞭而来，方惊皇失措，和尚护之曰：“此我父母官也，不得无礼。”豁然醒，联句未忘，因录与众共赏。皆曰：“世间无此长对，其仙境乎？”或曰：“明指滇南，阁下功名显达，将来临莅此方，必出入将相而后归隐，以应其语。”或曰：“大观楼稗乘有之，无此一对，将如曹雪芹之身入贾府，藉阁下补此联乎。故茫茫大士接引君也。”王曰：“唯，否，姑存以俟验可也。”

    Explanation

    569

    Great heroic achievements, exhausting all one's strength and effort, the red curtains and painted beams cannot be swept away by the morning mist and evening clouds, even broken stones and crumbling steles are left to be swallowed by the mist and sunset. Only a few distant temple bells, half the river lit by fishing fires, a few lines of autumn geese, and a pillow of clear frost. Repeatedly reciting, expanding one's thoughts and feelings, unable to discard. Suddenly hearing a loud rebuke: "These are famous verses from the ancient Daguang Tower, what do vulgar officials know? Peering here secretly. Order the Yellow Turban soldiers to drive them away." Seeing a golden-armored giant approaching with a whip, just then in a state of panic, the monk protected him saying, "This is my local official, you must not be disrespectful." Suddenly awakening, the linked verses were not forgotten, thus recording them to share with others. All said, "There is no such long couplet in the world, could it be from the realm of immortals?" Some said, "It clearly points to southern Yunnan, your Excellency will achieve fame and success, in the future when you arrive in this region, you will surely enter and exit as a minister before retiring to seclusion, to match these words." Others said, "The Daguang Tower's minor historical records have it, but not this couplet, will it be like Cao Xueqin entering the Jia family estate, using your Excellency to complete this couplet? Therefore, the vast sage is guiding you." The king said, "Yes, no, let us temporarily retain it and wait for verification."

    570

    未几，登贤书，屡赴礼阚不第，大挑一等，分发滇南。至彼，果有大观接，其景其联一一与梦符。后孙弥勒令，终于任所，胖大和尚其弥勒佛之现相乎？此之谓正梦。或问曰：“此梦以人就联乎？以联就人乎？若使以人就联，则吾乡距滇五六千里之遥，生魂一夜往反万余里，得毋遇风吹散乎？以联就人，联句雕于木，亦有神灵移之乎？”

    Explanation

    570

    Not long after, he passed the imperial examination and was listed in the registry of scholars. He took the imperial examinations multiple times but did not pass. However, he ranked first in a special examination and was assigned to work in Yunnan. Upon arriving there, he found that there was indeed a grand view that matched his expectations—the scenery and the couplets were exactly as he had dreamed. Later, a man named Sun Mi'er became an official and died in office. Was the fat monk a manifestation of the Bodhisattva Maitreya? This is what is called a true dream. Someone asked, "Did the person adapt to the couplets, or did the couplets adapt to the person? If it were the case that the person adapted to the couplets, then my hometown is over five or six thousand miles from Yunnan. Could not the living soul, traveling back and forth over ten thousand miles in one night, not be blown away by the wind? If it were the case that the couplets adapted to the person, were the couplets carved into wood, and were they moved by divine powers?"

    571

    芗厈曰：此以人就联也。胖大和尚可证。所谓佛法无边，引道生魂，何虑风霜道近，此弥勒种因，而王君结果也。其然乎？
    　　
    　　圆谎先生
    　　
    　　有封翁家居浙江之畔，贵而且富。生平爱打诳语，且好与人谈，率荒诞不经，闻者皆非笑之，翁不顾也。其子耻之，几谏不从，乃与知交密议，或曰：“太翁性喜纵谈，难以力阻，无已，请聘一善解嘲者为太翁伴，能反虚为实，则人无尤矣。”其子曰：“善，焉得此人，能周旋我父者，虽岁修数百金勿吝。”说者曰：“重聘之下，岂无能人？”其言一出，即有淳于之流来应。翁与谈而乐之，目之曰圆谎先生。朝岁勿离左右。

    Explanation

    571

    Xiang Tan said: This is about aligning people with a group. The fat, large monk can serve as evidence. So-called "the Buddha's teachings are boundless," guiding the soul along the path—why fear the wind and frost on the road? This is the cause for Maitreya's cultivation, and the result for Lord Wang. Could it be so?

    Mr. Who Rounds Up the Lies

    There was a retired official living by the shores of Zhejiang, wealthy and prosperous. Throughout his life, he loved to tell lies and enjoyed conversing with others, often speaking in absurd and unfounded ways. Those who heard him would all mock and laugh at him, but the old man paid no heed. His son was ashamed of him and repeatedly tried to advise him, but the old man would not listen. So the son consulted with close friends in secret. One said: "Grandfather has a fondness for unrestrained talk, and it is difficult to stop him by force. If there is no other way, perhaps we should hire someone skilled in defusing awkwardness to be his companion—one who can turn falsehood into truth, and thus no one will blame us." The son said: "That is good. How can we find such a person? One who can manage my father, even if it costs several hundred gold annually, I will not hesitate." The speaker said: "With such a generous offer, could there not be someone capable?" No sooner had these words been spoken than someone of the style of Chunyu came forward to apply. The old man was delighted by their conversation and called him "Mr. Who Rounds Up the Lies." From then on, he was never to be separated from his side, day or night.

    572

    一日，翁偕先生徘徊江畔，邻翁来接谈笑，共问翁曰：“近日有新闻否？”翁曰：“有之，昨与先生游此，对江有人持数十觔铁斧斫树，脱落江中，浮而过，视之无柄。非异事乎？”邻翁曰：“无柄巨斧，奚能浮江？或戏具中木斧耳。”翁曰：“老夫亦诧，辨之实镔铁所为者。”群笑其谬。先生曰：“非谬也，我昨同观，缘斧头吃入树枝，树本折，连枝叶浮而过此。”邻翁无词以诘。或曰：“昨夜风甚大，真有偃禾拔木之势。”翁曰：“不仅此也，敝园有井，竟被风吹出墙外。”众皆大哗。先生曰：“毋哗，主人园中，墙不一类，有砖石筑者，有紫竹结者，有荆条编者，总而言之曰墙。

    Explanation

    572

    One day, the old man and the scholar strolled along the riverside. A neighbor joined them in conversation and laughter, asking the old man, "Have there been any new events recently?" The old man replied, "Yes, yesterday while I was with the scholar here, across the river, someone was chopping a tree with a large iron axe weighing dozens of jin. The axe head fell into the river and floated past us, and upon inspection, it had no handle. Wasn't that a strange occurrence?" The neighbor said, "How could a large axe without a handle float on the river? Perhaps it was a wooden axe from a toy set." The old man said, "I was also surprised, but I can confirm that it was made of steel." The group laughed at his mistake. The scholar said, "It's not a mistake; I saw it yesterday too. The axe head got stuck in a tree branch, causing the tree to fall and float downstream with its branches and leaves." The neighbor had no further arguments. Someone else remarked, "Last night, the wind was very strong, truly capable of uprooting trees and bending grain." The old man said, "It was even more than that; my garden well was blown out of the wall." Everyone was surprised and made a commotion. The scholar said, "Please don't make noise. In the owner's garden, the walls are not all the same; some are built with bricks and stones, some are woven with purple bamboo, and some are made of thorn branches. In summary, they are all called walls."

    573

    此墙以竹为之，俗所谓篱笆墙者。上附生红白蔷薇花甚茂，循墙有眢井，昨为狂飙所逼，墙竟逾井内移，井反在外，翁晨起以为异，为我言之，我实亲见如是。”众亦无词而退。翁知先生之善圆谎也，更撰妄言，谓人曰：“我昨与先生闲坐小园，忽墙外飞一肥鸭来，烹已大熟，嗅之甚馨。我与先生共饱啖之，至今回昧甘美。”众皆大笑曰：“此睡梦中事耳。”先生曰：“否，否，实有之。小园墙东有一妇馋甚，家畜肥鸭，其夫命留为中秋宴客之物。偶出，妇窃而烹之。方欲食，夫归，见之怒，隔墙弃置。适我与翁在墙下接而食之，无甚异也。”众无言。翁笑曰：“可见吾言不谬。但食此鸭已周时矣，矢溺全无，何故？”先生曰：“此寿征也。”

    Explanation

    573

    This wall is made of bamboo, commonly known as a fence wall. Above it grows abundant red and white rose flowers. Along the wall, there is an abandoned well. Yesterday, due to a strong wind, the wall was pushed over the well and moved inward, leaving the well outside. The old man woke up in the morning and thought it was strange, and he told me about it. I actually saw it like that. The people left without words. The old man knew that Mr. so-and-so was good at making up stories, and he concocted another tale, saying to others, "Yesterday, I was sitting idly in the small garden with Mr. so-and-so, and suddenly, a fat duck flew in from outside the wall. It was cooked and very fragrant. We ate it together, and it tastes sweet and delicious even now." Everyone laughed and said, "This is just a dream." Mr. so-and-so replied, "No, no, it really happened. To the east of the small garden's wall, there was a woman who was very greedy. She kept a fat duck at home, and her husband ordered it to be reserved for a banquet during the Mid-Autumn Festival. She happened to be out, and the woman secretly cooked it. Just as she was about to eat it, her husband returned, saw it, and became angry. He threw it over the wall. Just then, I and the old man were under the wall and picked it up to eat it, and there was nothing unusual about it." The people had no response. The old man laughed and said, "You can see that what I said is not wrong. But it has been an hour since we ate the duck, and there's no sign of excrement or urine, why is that?" Mr. so-and-so replied, "This is a sign of longevity."

    574

    翁又游江畔，家犬随之。人曰：“太颇壮大，其神獒乎？”翁曰：“此犬能作祸，前日浮江过一家，窃其肉值千金。人追至我家索偿，老夫受累无已。”众曰：“太窃肉事有之，但千金则豕当百余，犬虽大，能食万觔肉乎？”共非笑之。先生曰：“勿哂，翁未陈明，此犬所食者实人肉也。尚未及其半，乌得万觔？”众大哗曰：“更无此理。”先生曰：“请毕其词，此犬善浮水，昨过对岸某家，其人昔富今贫，曾以二千金纳五品衔，不意饿而毙在败屋中，无可闭关。其子外出募棺归，见此犬窃食父尸，忿甚，以小舟追至此。日犬已食其父之半体，向翁讹诈不已。计其父有二千金职在身，犬果食其半，非价值千金乎？”人乃无言。

    Explanation

    574

    The old man was walking along the riverbank, and his dog followed him. Someone said, "This dog is quite large, could it be a divine mastiff?" The old man replied, "This dog can cause trouble. The day before, it swam across the river to a house and stole something worth a thousand gold coins. People chased it to my home to demand compensation, and I have been troubled endlessly." Others said, "It is true that the dog stole meat, but if it was worth a thousand gold coins, it would take a hundred pigs to be worth that much. Even though the dog is large, could it really eat ten thousand pounds of meat?" They all laughed at the absurdity. The scholar said, "Do not laugh. The old man did not explain clearly, but the dog actually ate human flesh. It had not yet eaten half of it, so how could it have eaten ten thousand pounds?" Everyone exclaimed, "That is impossible." The scholar continued, "Let me finish my explanation. This dog is good at swimming. Yesterday, it crossed to the other side of the river to a certain house. The man who lived there was once wealthy but is now poor. He had paid two thousand gold coins for a fifth-rank official position, but he died of starvation in a ruined house. There was no one to close the gate. His son went out to raise money for a coffin and returned to find the dog eating his father's body. He was furious and chased the dog to this place. The dog had already eaten half of its father's body, and it continued to demand compensation from the old man. Considering that the father had two thousand gold coins in his official position, if the dog really ate half of it, isn't that worth a thousand gold coins?" Everyone was speechless.

    575

    一日，翁督家僮饭一牛甚肥，先生顾之，啧啧称羡。翁正色曰：“此老夫家之宝万里牛也。方乘之游爪哇国而回，故需亲视。奴辈以大辽人参十觔饲之。”先生见无人诘问，诺而退。未几，翁抱恙已笃，其子侍之。翁忽自掴其口骂曰：“老悖，汝终身无实言，荒谬至此，死奚辞耶？”其子惶恐，请先生入解之。先生曰：“我非良医，无法处此。无已请翁乘食辽参之万里牛，遁入爪哇国中，或免此难。我亦附尾行矣。”不辞而去。

    Explanation

    575

    One day, the old man supervised his household boys in feeding a very fat ox. The master glanced at it and exclaimed with admiration. The old man, with a serious expression, said, "This is the treasure of my family, the 'Ten Thousand Li Ox.' I just rode it on a trip to Java and returned, so I need to inspect it myself. The servants fed it ten pounds of ginseng from the Great Liao region." Seeing no one questioning him, the master nodded and withdrew. Soon after, the old man fell gravely ill, and his son attended to him. Suddenly, the old man slapped his mouth and cursed, "Old fool, you have never spoken the truth, so absurd, what excuse will you have when you die?" His son was terrified and asked the master to come and explain. The master replied, "I am not an excellent doctor and cannot handle this. If there is no other way, please have the old man ride the ox that ate ginseng from the Great Liao region, escape to Java, and perhaps avoid this trouble. I will follow behind."
