He then left without saying goodbye.

    576

    芗厈曰：天下事竞有不能实言者，若言言诚实，亦成笑柄。昔有道学先生以诚敬自矢，每言行皆载籍。有友入其斋，先生不在，阅籍见有某夜与老妻敦伦一次语，友嫌其亵，以笔点之而去。先生归，查籍记事，见敦伦处俱加一点，不觉忿然曰：“何人敢与我妻行此事，亦登我籍中。其信然耶？其欺我耶？”遂贻笑千古，或曰：“然则如何而后可？”我对曰：“有如圆谎先生，事理通达，环转无穷，妄而不谬，可入言语一科。”

    Explanation

    576

    Xiang Yan said: There are things in the world that cannot be spoken truly; if one always speaks the truth, it will also become a laughing matter. Once upon a time, there was a scholar of the Way who swore by his honesty and respect, and every word and deed was recorded. A friend entered his study, but the master was not there. The friend read the records and found a statement about a certain night when he engaged in marital relations with his old wife. The friend found it indecent and marked it with a pen before leaving. The master returned, checked the records, and saw that all the places mentioning marital relations were marked with a dot. Unconsciously, he said angrily, "Who dared to do this with my wife, and also recorded it in my records? Is it really true? Or is it a lie against me?" Thus, he became a laughing stock for a thousand years. Someone asked, "Then what should be done?" I replied, "There is a man like a liar, who understands things and principles, turns around endlessly, is false but not mistaken, and can be included in the category of speech."

    577

    鬼孝子
    　　
    　　孝子滇人也，谈者忘其姓氏，平日为人负载营生，家仅老母，为巫而愚者也。凡为巫之道，先不惜小费，贿通大家婢仆，刺探隐事，及有事登场，假鬼神微露其意，则人皆畏惧，不惜重金求解。孝子之母蠢然一妇，不能为此，偶为人呼去，或被哂逐而回。日无进项，惟赖子肩度日矣。其子得钱供母，惟恐不甘旨，每饭必俚歌以侑食，俾其母欢乐而饱，方敢自食。有时得钱多，则为母备寒衣，己甚蓝褛不顾也。故母食贫处贱，而饱暖逸居。不幸其子忽患寒疾，不治而亡。母痛欲自裁者屡矣，皆为邻姥救免。

    Explanation

    577

    The Ghostly Filial Son

    The filial son was from Dian. Those who spoke of him forgot his surname and given name. By day, he earned a living by carrying burdens for others. His family consisted only of his elderly mother, a foolish woman who practiced witchcraft. In the way of witchcraft, one must first be willing to spend small sums of money to bribe the maids and servants of wealthy families, probing into their secrets. When an opportunity arose, the witch would appear on stage, feigning the presence of ghosts and spirits, subtly revealing her intentions, and thus causing people to fear her, spending large sums of money to seek her help. The mother of the filial son was a dull-witted woman who could not do this. Occasionally, she was called away by people, or she was laughed at and driven back. She had no income each day, relying solely on her son’s shoulders to support her. When her son earned money to provide for her, he feared she would not be content, so at every meal he would sing crude songs to accompany his food, making his mother happy and full before he himself would dare to eat. At times, when he earned more money, he would prepare warm winter clothes for his mother, despite his own ragged and worn-out appearance. Thus, though the mother lived in poverty and low status, she was well-fed, warm, and lived in comfort. Unfortunately, her son suddenly fell ill with a cold disease, which went untreated and he died. His mother, overcome with grief, attempted to commit suicide many times, but each time she was saved by a neighboring old woman.

    578

    一日思子嚎啕，午夜忽见其子痛哭投怀曰：“母毋畏，儿心故未常死也。儿本孽鬼，故生无福寿。今生以事母，故鬼神欢喜，得无罪。游行冥途，闻母悲声，求假来奉侍，且愿蠲儿来生福以福母亲。神皆许之，此后不患饥寒矣。”母曰：“虽汝一片诚心，其奈阴阳途隔，尚能以力求食乎？”孝子曰：“是不能，将藉母之力以营生。”母曰：“奈何？”孝子曰：“母故巫者也。昔巫而假，故见哂于人。今巫而真，当门庭如市矣。”母又曰：“奈何？”孝子曰：“儿请广布城乡曰某妇得神术，知过去未来事。能奉之者，消灾救难，且不勒索于人，随人布施，人有不愿者乎？”母曰：“我素拙于言，恐无益也。”孝子曰：“儿鬼也，母能见儿，他人无睹。有求教者，请以儿言告之。儿于过去事，一见其人，即悉未来事。能知一年休咎，已足动人听闻。母无虑焉。儿今去示梦于好事者，使之传布，母请高抬声价，坐享于家。”母曰诺。孝子乃隐。

    Explanation

    578

    One day, thinking of her son, the mother wailed. At midnight, she suddenly saw her son crying bitterly and throwing himself into her arms, saying, "Mother, do not fear, my heart has never truly died. I am originally a demon, so I had no good fortune in life. In this life, by serving my mother, I have pleased the gods and spirits, and have been absolved of sin. Wandering in the underworld, hearing my mother's sorrowful cries, I asked for permission to come and serve her, and I wish to dedicate my future good fortune to bring happiness to my mother. The gods have granted my wish, and from now on, we will not suffer from hunger or cold." The mother said, "Although you have a sincere heart, how can you overcome the separation between the living and the dead, and still be able to find food through effort?" The filial son replied, "It cannot be done, but I will rely on your strength to earn a living." The mother said, "How?" The son said, "Mother, you are a witch. In the past, being a fake witch, you were laughed at by people. Now, being a true witch, your home will be as busy as a market." The mother said again, "How?" The son said, "I will spread throughout the towns and villages that a certain woman has divine skills and can know past and future events. Those who seek her will be able to avoid disasters and difficulties, and she will not demand payment, but will accept voluntary offerings. Is there anyone who would not want that?" The mother said, "I am usually poor at speaking, I fear it will not be helpful." The son said, "I am a ghost, and only you can see me, others cannot. If anyone seeks advice, please tell them what I say. I can know future events by seeing a person once. Being able to know the good and bad of a year is enough to capture people's attention. Mother, do not worry. I will now go and show dreams to those who are interested, to spread the word. Mother, please raise your price, and enjoy the benefits at home." The mother said yes. The filial son then disappeared.

    579

    次日，十余男妇以斗米眉豚，叩门而拜。母惶恐不自安，曰：“何为乎？”来者众皆稽首而告曰：“昨梦城隍神以姥已成神巫，凡小巫，帝命管束。尔等若不往奉事，刻即降祸，故不敢不来耳。求姥收录，而今而后，我等皆姥之徒矣。”母无以应，忽见其子冠金蟒玉侍侧，请母高坐，而诲之曰：“汝等既知感戴，此后惟吾言是听，当福汝。勿效某等不信神言，汝看今日必有报应。”众但见母言，未常睹孝子形状，佥唯叩首去。母谓孝子曰：“汝何来，何得此盛服？”孝子曰：“儿假诸本邑城隍神者。”母曰：“触犯正神，将无得罪乎？”孝子曰：“凡神皆忠孝之人为之，其神已归天曹，惟正月间一至人间查考善恶，不与凡人溷处。其平日守位者，皆儿辈耳。故可假用衣冠，神知之亦不责也。”

    Explanation

    579

    The next day, more than ten men and women arrived with a basket of rice and a pig, knocking on the door and bowing. The mother felt anxious and uneasy, asking, "What is the matter?" The visitors all bowed deeply and said, "We had a dream last night where the city god told us that you have become a shaman. All lesser shamans are under the command of the emperor. If you do not accept us and we do not serve you, disaster will befall us immediately. That is why we dare not come. We ask you to accept us. From now on, we will all be your disciples." The mother did not know what to say, and suddenly she saw her son wearing a splendid robe adorned with gold and jade, standing by her side. He asked her to sit high and lectured her, saying, "Since you know to be grateful, from now on, you must only listen to me. I will bless you. Do not follow the example of others who do not believe in the words of the gods. You will see that there will be consequences today." The visitors only heard the mother's words and never saw the form of her filial son. They all knelt and bowed before leaving. The mother asked her son, "Where did you come from? How did you get such fine clothes?" The son replied, "I borrowed them from the city god of our town." The mother said, "Will you not offend the supreme deity by doing this?" The son said, "All deities are served by those who are loyal and filial. That deity has already returned to the celestial bureaucracy and only comes down to earth once a year during the lunar new year to investigate good and evil. He does not mingle with ordinary people. Those who hold positions on a daily basis are just like me. Therefore, I can borrow the attire, and the deity knows but will not blame me."

    580

    是日，宦家有召巫者，即不信神言之某某，正表扬主家隐事。众方环观耸听，忽自掴其口，伏地诉曰：“神责我不应贿通某媪，生事诳言。着自击面一百。”两手自责，口告饶命。又曰：“小人就至神巫家请罪。”狂奔而去。主家大笑，诘媪，不敢隐，亦自服其辜。遂撤其供献，及应给之财帛，亦至母家投纳。母正默坐时，忽见狰狞鬼卒押一人，头脸红肿，至母前跪拜请罪。正皇惑间，又见某绅率家人妇女盛送礼物，且为某巫跪求忏悔。

    Explanation

    580

    On that day, a court official summoned a witch, who was a person who did not believe in divine words, and was praising the host family's hidden matters. The crowd was watching and listening intently when the witch suddenly slapped her own mouth, fell to the ground, and confessed, "The gods are blaming me for bribing a certain old woman, causing trouble and lying. I should strike my face a hundred times." She struck herself with both hands and pleaded for mercy. Then she said, "I, a lowly person, will go to the witch's house to confess my sins," and ran away wildly. The host family laughed loudly, questioned the old woman, who dared not hide anything, and she also admitted her guilt. As a result, the offerings were removed, and the appropriate financial and material gifts were also submitted to the mother's family. The mother was sitting in silence when she suddenly saw fierce ghost soldiers escorting one person with a swollen face and head, who knelt before her and begged for forgiveness. While in a state of confusion, she also saw a certain gentleman leading his family and women to bring generous gifts, and he knelt to seek repentance for a certain witch.

    581

    母殊不安，即见其子出曰：“公等请起，某巫自犯正神，无与公等事。但此辈帝命老妇统辖，不信者自取其祸。公等至诚，不但无祸，请看某月日，公子得捷报进士第几名矣。”后果如其言。是以奉神巫者举国若狂。祈福祷灾，踵门不绝。母若倦时，派其徒代应，亦无不验，有欲延请至家者，母高自位置，咸命其徒代之，有得则给与十之四五，较平日所进向多。故其徒感戴，尊奉不敢稍懈。母故丰衣足食，市田宅，役奴婢，居然大家。夜间无事，孝子率鬼党搬演杂剧，唱阴间善恶报应故事，胜于世剧。母顾而乐之，往往夜半不肯眠，孝子再三谆劝而后息。

    Explanation

    581

    The mother was extremely uneasy and immediately saw her son come out and said, "Gentlemen, please stand up. This witch has offended the true god, and it has nothing to do with you. But these people are under the command of the old woman by imperial decree. Those who do not believe will bring disaster upon themselves. You are sincere, so not only will you have no harm, but you will see on a certain day that the young master will receive news of his success and his rank among the Jinshi (imperial scholars)." Later, as she said, it turned out to be true. Therefore, those who worship the god and the witch are all excited throughout the country. They pray for blessings and ward off disasters, coming one after another to the door. When the mother is tired, she sends her disciples to respond, and none of them fail to come true. Those who want to invite her to their homes, she sets herself in a high position and orders her disciples to do it instead. If there is any gain, she gives them one or two out of five, compared to what she usually receives, it is more. Therefore, her disciples are grateful and respect her, and they respect and follow her without any slack. The mother thus enjoys abundant clothing and food, buying land and houses, employing servants and maids, and becomes a wealthy and influential person. At night when there is nothing to do, the filial son leads the ghost party to perform various plays, singing stories about good and evil retribution in the afterlife, better than the plays of the world. The mother looks at them and is pleased, often she does not sleep until midnight. The filial son repeatedly advises her gently until she rests.

    582

    孝子家本盛族，以读书出仕者不乏人，因其子母业鄙，宗人不甚往来。今母如是之盛，且身分自居，并未一出，而入争奉之。故其等夷及卑幼辈皆来趋奉，亦有所觊觎耳。睹其景象光昌，竟分日问安侍膳。母于富贵者礼之，贫贱者济之，举族悦服。族长欲为择继，因立贤立爱，争执未定。孝子知之，谓其母曰：“儿奉母又十余年矣。天下无不散之筵席，今母年八旬，既寿且康，奈儿假满不得再留人间。盍先为母刨基业于地下，然后奉母西归，不亦乐乎？但冥间重者诰命，而先人亦无承祀。儿阴择得族弟诸生某，长厚而有福泽，可为父后。已密遣赴都谋功名，不日事备。请母于热闹场中归去，受冥途清福何如？”母喜曰：“善。”

    Explanation

    582

    The Filial Son came from a once-prosperous family, where many had risen to officialdom through studying. However, due to the decline of their family's fortunes, relatives did not visit them often. Now that the mother had regained such prosperity and had elevated herself, people competed to serve her. Thus, those of her own rank and the younger generation all came to serve, also having some ulterior motives. Seeing her prosperous scene, they even took turns to inquire about her well-being and serve her meals. The mother treated the wealthy with courtesy and helped the poor, and the whole family was pleased and submissive. The clan leader wanted to choose a successor for her, but because of the conflict between choosing the virtuous or the beloved, the decision was still undecided. The Filial Son knew this and told his mother, "I have served you for more than ten years. All good things must come to an end; now that you are eighty years old, you are both long-lived and healthy, but I am about to complete my term and cannot stay in the human world any longer. Why not first establish a foundation for you in the underworld, and then take you to the West, wouldn't that be delightful? But in the afterlife, the most important thing is the imperial decree, and the ancestors have no one to continue the ancestral rites. I have secretly chosen a cousin who is a scholar, mature and kind, with blessings, and can be your successor. I have secretly sent him to the capital to seek fame and glory, and soon everything will be ready. Please allow me to take you to the bustling scene and return to the afterlife, to enjoy the clear blessings of the underworld." The mother happily said, "Good."

    583

    当是时，族长正在宗祠，会族老公议其事，忽有人报曰：“大官府来矣。”族长出迎，见一人四品衣冠，前后护从，俨然宪司气概，近前则族孙某也。昂然升堂，从者为燃烛炬焚香拜祖毕，揖族长而言曰：“我奉母命赴都报捐候铨，今得督理苏、安两省十府粮储道，兼辖卫备弁兵。引见后，奉命莅任。为父母加级，请得二品封典，请假上寿。此皆母自定计，恐预言之，族中倘起争端，未免有伤敦睦之道。故命先默为之，今我与嗣父母已名列帝廷，无可更改矣。敬请传齐族众，在列祖位前公立继约，诹吉率妻子往母家承祀。”族长唯唯。未几，老少咸集，见事已不可移，乃趋炎而附承之。公议是日唤优伶具盛席送某生入继。

    Explanation

    583

    At that time, the clan leader was at the ancestral hall, convening with the elders of the clan to discuss the matter. Suddenly, someone reported, "The high officials have arrived." The clan leader went out to greet them and saw a man in the attire of a fourth-rank official, surrounded by attendants, exuding the imposing demeanor of a censor. Approaching closer, he realized it was a descendant of the clan. With majestic bearing, he ascended the hall. His attendants lit candles and incense and performed ancestral worship. Bowing to the clan leader, he said, "I have come to the capital on my mother's orders to report and await appointment. I have been appointed to oversee the grain storage of ten prefectures in Sichuan and Anhui provinces, and also supervise the garrison and militia. After being introduced, I was ordered to take up my post. To elevate the ranks of my parents, I request a second-rank honorific title and to take leave to celebrate their longevity. This is all according to my mother's plan. She feared that predicting it might cause disputes among the clan, which would inevitably harm the way of harmony. Therefore, she instructed me to act discreetly. Now that I and my adoptive parents are listed in the imperial court, there is no way to change it. I respectfully request that you gather all the clan members and establish a public agreement in front of the ancestral tablets. Choose a favorable day to lead my wife and children to my mother's home to continue the family line." The clan leader nodded in assent. Soon after, the old and young gathered together, seeing that the matter could not be changed, they followed the trend and complied. The public discussion on that day decided to hire entertainers and prepare a grand feast to send the adopted son into the family.

    584

    至日，母家已走启门庭，结彩棚十余座，张各种洋灯，辉煌夺目。广招亲友，远近偕来，值提塘武弁赍送诰命亦至。地方官及绅耆不期而会者数百人。幸宅宇宽广，前知而有备，百余筵咄嗟立办，故从容不迫，井井有条。内外演剧，百戏佥来。其执事自大门排入厅事，鼓吹若雷，内外传呼之声不绝。中堂设龙亭，内供诰授通奉大夫布政使司某暨夫人某氏诰轴一通。紫缎金书，煌煌天语。于是笙箫叠奏，小优吹《朝天乐》《步虚声》前引，太夫人衣蟒腰玉，仙鹤补服，霞披朝珠而出，婢仆抠衣，儒生鸣赞，九顿首拜。受毕，移位正坐。继子妇率孙儿女，朝服叩首侍立。然后官弁亲友、宗人百执事以次拜贺。邻里乡党之来观者潮涌，不得入，延颈引领，登屋而望，几至颠堕。幸孝子默护之，故无伤。母左顾右盼，锦绣在前，珠玉在后。不觉眩晕，命子妇支持宾客，登榻凭几，垂眸少息，见孝子以八座相迎，冥然而卒。

    Explanation

    584

    On the day, the mother's family had already opened the gates, set up over ten decorated pavilions, and displayed various foreign lamps, which were magnificent and eye-catching. They extensively invited relatives and friends, and people from far and near came together. Coincidentally, the military officer delivering the imperial edict also arrived. Local officials and respected elders unexpectedly gathered in hundreds. Fortunately, the house was spacious and well-prepared in advance, and over a hundred banquets were quickly prepared, so everything proceeded calmly and orderly. Performances were staged inside and out, and various entertainers came. The attendants lined up from the main gate to the hall, the music and drums were like thunder, and the calls and announcements inside and out never ceased. A dragon pavilion was set up in the central hall, inside which an imperial edict granting the title of Tongfeng Daifu to a certain Governor of a certain province and his wife was displayed. Written on purple silk with gold ink, the words of the emperor were resplendent. Flutes and pipes played in succession, and young performers played 'Chao Tian Le' and 'Bu Xu Sheng' as a prelude. The lady-in-waiting, dressed in a robe with a dragon pattern and a belt of jade, wearing a phoenix patch and a robe with a peacock feather, and a silk scarf with morning pearls, emerged. Servants held their robes, and scholars announced the rituals. She bowed nine times, received the edict, and moved to the main seat. The son-in-law and daughter-in-law, along with grandchildren and children, dressed in court attire, knelt and stood respectfully. Then officials, relatives, and various attendants paid their respects in order. Neighbors and fellow villagers came in waves to watch, unable to enter, they stretched their necks and climbed onto roofs to look, almost falling off the roof. Fortunately, the filial son silently protected them, so no one was hurt. The lady looked left and right, with brocade before her and jewels behind, and felt dizzy. She asked her daughter-in-law to support the guests, then lay down on the bed and rested her eyes, seeing the son-in-law welcoming her with eight seats, and passed away quietly.

    585

    论者曰：“孝莫大于尊亲，亦莫厚于众养。以担夫而致父母受二品封，使衿民备十方供，孝之至也。嗟乎！孝子何寥落于生前，而赫喧于身后耶？”芗厈曰：“此资格限之也。古者乡举里选，先德行后文章。若孝子者早应策名天府，位列公卿间矣。无奈问内才不识诗文之体，问外财亦无升斗之储，何所藉而奋发于今之世耶？惟阴曹无资格，任孝子金冠玉带以奉母，乃得大展其才华。呜呼！与其郁郁生，何如堂堂死耶？”

    Explanation

    585

    The commentator says: "The greatest form of filial piety is showing respect to one's parents, and the most substantial form of filial piety is providing for them. By being a porter, one can have their parents receive a second-class imperial title, and make the common people prepare offerings from all directions. This is the utmost expression of filial piety. Alas! Why are filial sons so overlooked in life but celebrated after death?"

    Xiang Yan says: "This is due to the limitations imposed by social qualifications. In ancient times, people were selected based on virtue and conduct before their literary skills. If these filial sons had been chosen early, they would have been among the high-ranking officials. However, they lack the internal talent to understand literary forms and the external means to accumulate wealth. What can they rely on to strive in the current world? Only in the underworld, where there are no such qualifications, can filial sons wear golden crowns and jade belts to serve their mothers, thereby fully displaying their talents. Alas! Is it better to die with dignity than to live in misery?"

    586

    荆茅
    　　
    　　楚诸生荆茅，字贡苞，训蒙为业。在前明嘉靖间，是邑大旱，赤地数百里，人心惶惶。有司竭尽求雨之法不得，乃示召能致甘霖者酬百金。向无此例，所以市里喧传。荆知之，与其妻戏述云：“惜无法以致此金，亦名利两全之事也。”其妻曰：“是亦何难。子速为有司言能三日致雨。使之洁净坛坫，子衣冠坐，诵圣经，宜必得之。”荆曰：“天道难知，岂可戏有司取咎耶？”其妻曰：“子试为之，得雨则受酬，不得雨，不过讪笑，何罪之有？”荆从其言，昧昧晋谒。有司如其法使祷，未及二日，大雨滂沱，通邑霑足，上下欢腾。有司钦佩，于酬仪外加以币帛，鼓吹送之。

    Explanation

    586

    Jing Mao and the Rain-Making Ritual

    Jing Mao, a scholar named Gongbao, taught children and beginners in classical Chinese studies. During the Jiajing era of the Ming dynasty, a severe drought struck the region, leaving the land parched over hundreds of miles and causing widespread panic among the people. The local government officials tried every known method to invoke rain but failed. In desperation, they announced a reward of a hundred gold pieces to anyone who could bring forth timely rain. There was no precedent for such a reward, and the news spread quickly through the town.

    Jing Mao heard about the reward and discussed it with his wife. "I regret that there is no way to obtain this money," he said. "But it would be a wonderful opportunity to achieve both fame and profit." His wife replied, "What difficulty is there in this? You should tell the authorities that you can bring rain in three days. Have them prepare a clean altar, and you sit in proper attire, reciting the sacred texts. You will surely succeed."

    Jing Mao hesitated, saying, "The will of heaven is hard to fathom. How can I play a trick on the authorities and invite blame?" His wife reassured him, "You should try it. If it rains, you will receive the reward. If it does not, it will just result in ridicule. What harm could there be in that?"

    Persuaded by his wife, Jing Mao approached the authorities respectfully and secretly. The officials followed his instructions, preparing a clean altar and having Jing Mao sit in proper attire while he recited the sacred texts. Within two days, a heavy rain began to fall, drenching the entire town. The people were overjoyed, and the officials were impressed. They not only gave Jing Mao the promised reward but also added gifts of silk and cloth, sending him off with music and fanfare.

    587

    未几，省垣需雨孔急，有司以荆生致雨事上达。大府檄召。荆恐，怼其妻曰：“我本无能，汝促我为戏，竟为宪召，何术以应？昔也德汝，今则怨汝矣。”妻曰：“子自无能，怨妾何为？妾之所知，非有异术，因厨悬咸鱼于今三载，凡二三日内雨至，先必落水。验之屡矣。子述告示之日，适咸鱼落水之时，故信之确。今亦不难，子持此鱼至省，悬于卧内。见大府时，以先贤董仲舒五龙祈雨之法，铺张陈设。若鱼干无水，总以坛不如式，器用不全，频使改作，以延时日。若一得鱼水，即登坛诵经，未有不获者。何怨之有？”荆无法，不能不用妇言。及赴省会，则鱼已汗淋。急谒大府，朝登坛而夕如注矣。得重酬回，喜出望外。

    Explanation

    587

    Not long after, the provincial capital urgently needed rain, and the officials reported to the higher authorities that Jing had caused the rain. The provincial governor issued an edict to summon Jing. Jing was terrified and angrily confronted his wife, saying, "I am inherently incapable. You pushed me into this prank, and now I am summoned by the law. What method can I use to respond? I once praised you, but now I resent you." His wife replied, "You are inherently incapable, so why resent me? What I know is not a special technique, but rather that the salted fish hanging in the kitchen has been there for three years. Whenever it rains within two or three days, the fish will first fall into the water. I have tested this many times. When you reported the edict, it coincided with the fish falling into the water, so I believed it was true. It is not difficult now. Take this fish to the province and hang it in your room. When you see the provincial governor, use the method of the ancient scholar Dong Zhongshu for praying for rain with five dragons, setting up the altar elaborately. If the fish is dry and no water, always claim the altar is not properly set up, the instruments are incomplete, and frequently require changes to delay time. If the fish is in water, immediately ascend the altar and recite scriptures, and no one fails to get what they want. What reason to resent?" Jing had no choice but to follow his wife's advice. When he arrived at the provincial capital, the fish was already wet. He hurriedly went to see the provincial governor, ascended the altar in the morning, and it rained heavily by evening. He received a generous reward and was extremely happy and beyond expectation.

    588

    此大府乃严相分宜门下者，知嘉靖帝好道，密告分宜以荆生进。特旨召见，荆乃携妻入都，帝问道原，荆进诚意正心之说曰：“至诚之道可以前知。诚者，明之根本也。修齐治平不外乎此。”帝曰：“粹然儒者之言，宜与方士辈异。”命为金马门待诏。嗣亦因求雨验，迁钦天监卿，日近御前。于是都下趋之者众，渐致富矣。
    　　
    　　忽大内九玺失其一，追求甚急，拟召荆推问。内监之盗用者惶惧，夤夜赍金帛叩门哀之。荆乃命以玺藏尚宝处壁间，以尘土掩之，我自有说。帝果召问，荆曰：“玺非人盗，乃某月日用时，为小竖误遗于尘土之中。现在本处东壁下。”帝使人求之，果获。赏赐无算，遂有荆仙之名。奉之者益狂，为御史海忠介之徒劾奏，略曰“荆茅者，本无学术。肆其狂妄，妖言惑众，罪不容诛”云云。帝曰：“方士中惟此人近儒道，专以诚明立说。卿非读书人耶，何不容儒士？”御史曰：“其诚明之说，正藉以行其诈也。

    Explanation

    588

    This official was a follower of Yan Xiang Fen Yi, knowing that Emperor Jiajing was fond of the Dao, he secretly informed Yan Xiang Fen Yi to recommend Jing Sheng. The emperor specially summoned Jing Sheng to meet, and Jing Sheng then brought his wife to the capital. The emperor asked about the origin of the Dao, and Jing Sheng presented the theory of sincerity and rectifying the heart, saying, "The way of utmost sincerity can foresee the future. Sincerity is the foundation of clarity. The principles of self-cultivation, family management, state governance, and world peace are all contained within this." The emperor said, "These are purely Confucian words, and should be different from the Daoist practitioners." He appointed Jing Sheng as a scholar at the Golden Horse Gate. Later, because of successful rain prayers, he was promoted to the position of Chief Astronomer and became close to the emperor. As a result, many people in the capital flocked to him, and he gradually became wealthy.

    Suddenly, one of the nine seals in the inner palace went missing, and the search was very urgent, with plans to summon Jing Sheng for questioning. The eunuch who had stolen the seal was terrified, and at night he brought gold and silk to Jing Sheng's door to beg for mercy. Jing Sheng ordered the seal to be hidden behind the wall in the place where the imperial treasures were kept, covered with dust, and he had his own explanation. The emperor indeed summoned Jing Sheng to question him, and Jing Sheng said, "The seal was not stolen by a person, but during a certain month and day when it was used, it was accidentally left in the dust by a small eunuch. It is now under the eastern wall here." The emperor sent someone to look for it, and indeed found it. The emperor bestowed countless rewards upon him, and Jing Sheng came to be known as the "Jing Immortal." Those who revered him became increasingly fervent, and he was impeached by officials of Hai Zhongjie, who said, "Jing Sheng, originally without academic credentials, indulged in his madness, spreading false doctrines to deceive the masses, and his crimes are unforgivable." The emperor said, "Among the Daoist practitioners, only this person is close to Confucianism, specializing in the theory of sincerity and clarity. Are you not a scholar, and why cannot you tolerate a Confucian scholar?" The censor said, "His theory of sincerity and clarity is actually used to deceive."

    589

    乞皇上藏物于匣，当臣面召问之。果能指明确，方敢以至诚许之。否则请置奸邪于法，毋任蛊惑圣聪。”帝如言召荆于便殿，案陈宝椟，使推测之。荆惶悚伏地，叹曰：“荆茅今日死矣。”御座远，闻未亲切，曰：“是何言也？”适盗玺之监在侧，跪奏曰：“据所言椟中似是金猫。”帝笑谓御史曰：“卿意其诚明为诈，今竟何如？”开椟示之，果一金铸卧猫镇纸。御史无词可执，顿首谢罪而退。荆归，其妻曰：“子以一寒士位四品而富巨万，异数可屡邀耶？若不知足，祸必不远。”荆大悟，引疾致仕而去。

    Explanation

    589

    The official requested the emperor to hide something in a box and summon Jing in his presence to question him. If Jing could clearly identify the item, the official would dare to promise it with utmost sincerity. Otherwise, he requested that the deceitful and evil ones be punished according to the law, and that they not be allowed to deceive the wise ears of the emperor. The emperor followed the official's request and summoned Jing to the private audience hall, where he placed a box containing valuable items on the table and asked Jing to speculate on its contents. Jing was terrified and prostrated himself on the ground, exclaiming, "Jing is doomed today." The emperor's seat was far away, so he could not hear clearly and asked, "What do you mean by that?" The eunuch who had stolen the seal was present and knelt to report, "According to what Jing said, the box likely contains a golden cat." The emperor laughed and said to the censor, "Did you truly believe it was genuine or a fraud? How is it now?" The box was opened, and it indeed contained a golden cat-shaped paperweight. The censor had no further argument and knelt to apologize before leaving. Jing returned home, and his wife said, "You rose from a lowly scholar to a fourth-rank official and amassed great wealth. Can such extraordinary favors be sought repeatedly? If you are not content, disaster is not far off." Jing realized this and resigned on grounds of illness, retiring from office.

    590

    芗厈曰：果有是耶，何其巧也？其为客之不得意而造言欤？谓实学不如虚巧易于见功，发寒士之忿懑，益增才士之欷歔，悲夫。
    　　
    　　第七卷
    　　
    　　义猫
    　　
    　　武林金氏，望族也，代有闻人。有某翁者，救死恤生，利人爱物，至诚恻怛，人皆仰之。然厄于命，年逾强仕，家中落。夏日纳凉院中，见饥猫倾侧将毙，翁睹之惨然，自起饲之。从此猫不他往，日恋恋依翁侧。翁每饭必食以腥，即外出，必嘱家人尽心爱养。由是猫渐肥健，能捕鼠而粮无耗失。是年秋涝，粒米无收。翁家乏食，借贷无门，典质已尽，搔首踟躇，牛衣对泣而已。猫更无从得食，嗷嗷于侧，小女子责之曰：“人尚无食，汝欲食耶？主人穷困至此，心烦意乱，汝不念平日养育恩勤，何以报德，而反嗷嗷取憎耶。”猫呦然似诺，一跃登屋去。人皆异之，翁亦破涕为笑。未几，猫衔一物掷翁怀中。视之，妇女旧抹额也。上缀东珠廿余，光明圆正，大如芡实，值千金。

    Explanation

    590

    Xiang Yan said: "Is it really so? How ingenious! Is it perhaps the words of a guest who is discontented and fabricating tales? He says that practical learning is less effective than superficial cleverness in achieving results, expresses the frustration of poor scholars, and increases the sighs of talented scholars. Alas!"

    Volume Seven

    The Righteous Cat

    The Jin Family of Wulin is a prominent clan, having produced notable figures over generations. There was an old man who saved lives, comforted the living, and benefited people and things, deeply sincere and compassionate, respected by all. However, he was unfortunate due to fate, having passed the age of forty, and his family had fallen into decline. During summer, he was taking a cool in the courtyard when he saw a starving cat about to die. The old man looked at it with a sad expression and personally fed it. From then on, the cat no longer went elsewhere and daily clung to the old man's side. The old man always fed it meat, and when he went out, he would instruct his family to take good care of it. Gradually, the cat became fat and healthy, able to catch mice without any loss of food. That year, there was a flood in autumn, and there was no grain harvest. The old man's family lacked food, had nowhere to borrow money, and all collateral had been exhausted. He scratched his head and hesitated, weeping while wearing a coarse garment. The cat had no way to get food and cried out on the side. A young woman scolded it, saying, "People still have no food, do you want to eat? The master is so poor and distressed that he is upset and confused. You do not remember the kindness and hard work of your daily upbringing, how can you repay the virtue, and instead cry out to gain hatred?" The cat nodded in agreement and leaped onto the roof and left. Everyone found it strange, and the old man also stopped crying and smiled. Shortly after, the cat carried something in its mouth and threw it into the old man's lap. Upon looking at it, it was an old woman's headband, adorned with more than twenty pearls, bright, round, and large like lotus seeds, worth a thousand gold pieces.

    591

    翁惊讶失色，一喜一惧，曰：“猫虽通灵，奈窃取之物，不但污我品行，且恐失物之家冤及婢仆，性命攸关，奈何？”其妻女曰：“翁言虽是，但井上之李，岂无主者。廉士尚且取之，所谓饥不择食也。况此物自至，必天神怜翁，假物以济，岂尽狸奴力耶？无已，姑先质贷度岁，暗访物主，明告其故，而归以质券，似亦无伤。”翁不得已，姑从之。次年遍访，无失物家。或曰：“此巨家殉葬物，年久家贫，墓崩棺坏，则猫取之矣。”或曰：“有心计妇嫁浮荡子，藏此物于复壁承尘中，为子女谋。未及交代，猝病而亡。猫故取之无碍。”皆似也，要之以神天赏善之说为正。翁闻人所议近理，乃赎而货之。缘此起家，子孙发科甲，世承祖训，爱蓄猫，食必以腥。有仕至宪司者，署中猫且数十头，出入随从，专有饲猫之人，至今不衰。

    Explanation

    591

    The old man was astonished and lost color, feeling both joy and fear, and said, "Though the cat is spiritually aware, the stolen object not only tarnishes my character but also causes the family who lost it to blame their maidservants and servants, which concerns life and death. What can I do?" His wife and daughters replied, "Your words are true, but the plums on the well, are they without an owner? Even a righteous person would take them, as hunger knows no choice of food. Moreover, this object came to us, surely the gods pity us and lend it to help us. Is it all due to the cat's effort? Unwillingly, we will follow this plan. Temporarily borrow to get through the year, secretly search for the owner, clearly explain the reason, and return with the pledge document. This seems harmless." The old man, having no choice, reluctantly followed their advice. The next year, they searched everywhere but found no family who had lost the object. Some said, "This was a funerary object from a wealthy family. After many years, the family became poor, and the tomb collapsed, the coffin broke, so the cat took it." Others said, "A cunning woman married a dissolute man, hid this object in the double walls and ceiling for the sake of her children. She did not have time to hand it over before suddenly falling ill and dying. The cat took it without hindrance." All these explanations seem plausible, but ultimately, the belief in divine reward for goodness is correct. Hearing what people say is close to reason, the old man then redeemed and sold it. Thus, he established a family, and his descendants passed the imperial exams, continuing the ancestral teachings, loving to keep cats, and feeding them with meat. There was one who rose to the position of a high-ranking official, with dozens of cats in his office, accompanied when going out and coming in, with dedicated people to feed the cats, and this tradition remains strong to this day.

    592

    芗厈闻而叹曰：人生世上，财可忽乎哉。不但饮食起居以之自奉，即庭帏行孝，棣萼联情，莫不藉此，甚至爵可得而鬻焉，刑可得而赎焉。以之救济，仁名顿起；以之施与，传为美谈。信乎？金圣叹曰：“名以银成，无别术也。”彼猫乌知之，亦以此取义，且永锡尔类，岂不异哉。
    　　
    　　李老
    　　
    　　恒山李老，农家者流。有地数顷，称小康。中年生一子，名曰壹。稍长附学读书，督课极严。壹时年十二，游戏误学，畏父师训责，窃资逃去。李老夫妇情急，悬金以购，搜索无所不至，迄无影响。其母哀痛迫切，几至轻生。李老犹以年齿正强，可望生育慰之。＿然妇已思子成疾，屡劝置妾延嗣，李老不忍。光阴迅速，瞬逾十年。年将古稀，仍无所出。宗族之贫者咸思争继，哓哓不休，益厌苦之。自度精力尚强，且值旱涝不匀之岁，闻韩魏间售子女者，直甚廉。李老携百金往投人牙，以清钱五十贯择得端庄少女，大称心怀。

    Explanation

    592

    Xiang Yan heard this and sighed: "In life, can wealth be neglected? Not only does it provide for daily needs, but it also supports filial piety within the family and strengthens familial bonds. Even positions of power can be bought, and punishments can be redeemed. Using wealth for charity and generosity brings fame and becomes a beautiful story. Indeed, as Jin Shengtan said: 'Fame is achieved through silver, there is no other way.' Even a cat understands this, and this understanding is passed on to others, how extraordinary is that?"

    Li Lao, a farmer from Hengshan, had several acres of land and was considered moderately well-off. In his middle age, he had a son named Yi. When Yi grew older, he was sent to school with strict supervision. At the age of twelve, Yi played and neglected his studies, fearing his father's and teacher's punishment, he stole money and fled. Li Lao and his wife were very anxious, offering a reward for his return, searching everywhere with no success. His wife fell ill due to missing her son and repeatedly suggested taking a concubine to have more children, but Li Lao refused. Time passed quickly, and ten years went by. Li Lao was approaching seventy years old and still had no children. Poor relatives of Li Lao were vying for inheritance, causing distress. Feeling his energy was still strong, and during a year of uneven drought and floods when prices were low, Li Lao heard that in the regions of Han and Wei, children were sold at very low prices. He took a hundred gold pieces to buy a daughter, selecting a modest and well-behaved girl for fifty strings of cash, which greatly pleased him.

    593

    女叩翁姓名籍贯，实告之。讶曰：“妾乃与翁同姓同乡，异哉！”老曰：“同姓或有之，乡则路隔五百里，难言同也。”女曰：“幼闻吾父言之凿凿，云系李姓名壹，恒山人。因逃学出，为人义子。亲父母在乎否乎，念诵涕泣。妾与母时慰劝之。”李老亦讶曰：“据汝言，确是吾子矣。汝当为吾孙女，幸言之早，速赴尔家验之。虽相隔十余年，声音笑貌应不改也。”遂偕女至村，呼其父出，果李老子也。哭述所由，云逃出后惘惘南奔，资用告绝，乞食此村，有老父同姓，畜为义子，为我娶妻，连生四子二女。义父母相继殁，逢此俭岁，故卖女度日耳。李老大悦，命子贷其家具，携其子孙男女八人归。其妻孤苦伶仃抱病，而闻夫妇忽然子孙满堂，不觉跃然而起。

    Explanation

    593

    The girl respectfully inquired about the old man's name and hometown, and he truthfully told her. She was surprised and said, "I am from the same surname and hometown as you, how strange!" The old man replied, "Having the same surname might be possible, but we are separated by five hundred miles, so it cannot be said that we are from the same place." The girl said, "I heard my father say clearly that I am named Li Yi and am from Hengshan. Because he ran away from school, he became an adopted son. Are your biological parents still alive? Sometimes I recite and cry. My mother sometimes comforts and advises me." The old man was surprised and said, "According to what you say, it is certainly my son. You should be my granddaughter. Fortunately you mentioned it early. Quickly go to your home to verify it. Even though separated for more than ten years, the voice and appearance should not have changed." He then accompanied the girl to the village and called her father out, who turned out to be the old man's son. They cried and recounted the story, saying that after running away, he wandered aimlessly south, ran out of money, and begged in this village. An elderly man of the same surname took him in as an adopted son, married him, and he had four sons and two daughters. After the adoptive parents passed away, he sold his daughter to make ends meet during these lean years. The old man was very happy, ordered his son to lend him furniture, and took his eight children (sons and daughters) back home. His wife, who had been suffering from illness and feeling lonely, was suddenly overwhelmed with joy when she heard that her husband had suddenly become a grandfather with many grandchildren, and she unconsciously jumped up and stood up.

    594

    陈伯
    　　
    　　陈氏，海昌世家，多藏书。伯以词林壮年告休，足不出户，手不释卷，亦无著作，惟终身读书而已。其仲以进士司铎府学，髦而致仕归。见兄日孜孜，进而规之曰：“兄既不求闻达，守此残篇已属无益，况年逾八旬，当养精神以时休息，何必如后生小子之勤奋乎？”伯笑曰：“予非博髦而好学之名，予耻不识字也。”仲曰：“何至于是？”伯曰：“弟识几字？”仲对曰：“多或不能，数千字可识。

    Explanation

    594

    Tchen Bo

    　　The Chen family, a distinguished lineage from Hai Chang, is known for its extensive collection of books. Tchen Bo retired from his distinguished position in his prime, never leaving his house and never putting down a book, yet he did not produce any writings, dedicating his life solely to reading. His younger brother, a scholar who had passed the highest level of the imperial examinations, served as a teacher in the official school of the government and retired at an advanced age, returning home. Seeing his elder brother's diligent efforts, he advised him more seriously, saying, "Since you do not seek fame or recognition, continuing to read these outdated texts is of no benefit. Moreover, at your age, you should conserve your energy and take rest when needed. Why should you work as hard as young scholars?" Tchen Bo smiled and replied, "I do not seek the reputation of being an elderly scholar who loves learning. I am ashamed not to know characters." The younger brother asked, "Why should it come to this?" Tchen Bo responded, "How many characters do you know?" The younger brother answered, "I might not know many, but I can recognize several thousand characters."

    595

    ”伯曰：“试问汝识天字否？”仲曰：“三岁幼子尚识之，兄何渺弟如是？”伯曰：“非敢轻汝，字实难识，凡字有五声，有八法，有实读，有虚读。如实读始于何书，自十三经，一十七史，以及数千种说部子集内共有若干。虚读亦然，再将五声辩之，何方读阳平，何方读阴平，何方读上去入声。又以八法书之，不但真草隶篆，即变体亦无所不知，方可曰我识此字。即以天字论，实读则为上天，虚读则一日曰一天。见于经史子集者共有若干。又国号如葛天、刑天，人姓如汉令天高之类。

    Explanation

    595

    The elder brother said, "Let me ask if you know the character for 'heaven'?" The middle brother replied, "Even a three-year-old child knows it; why do you think I am so ignorant?" The elder brother said, "I am not belittling you; the character is truly difficult to recognize. All characters have five tones, eight strokes, actual readings, and virtual readings. Actual readings begin in which book? From the Thirteen Classics, the Seventeen Histories, and thousands of other literary works, how many are found? Virtual readings are also the same; further distinguish by the five tones, which read as level, which as rising, which as departing, and which as entering. Write by the eight strokes, not only regular, cursive, clerical, and seal scripts, but also variant forms must be known without exception, before one can say they know this character. For example, the character '天' in actual reading means 'heaven,' and in virtual reading, it means 'one day' or 'a day.' How many times is it found in the classics, histories, philosophical works, and literary collections? State names like Ge Tian and Xing Tian, and family names like Han Ling Tian Gao, are also examples."

    596

    星名天根、天枢、天棓之类。山名天台、天方、天山之类。禽名天知，兽名天马之类。草木名景天、烛天、天门冬之类。皆当实读，缘实有其人、其地、其物故也。至漏天、木天、谈天之类，则应附虚读。华人读天字，音虽皆在阴阳二平之内，何以五方不同，至西夷变声，则上去入皆有，一字翻作两三音，如祁连普剌统格落之类，在于何国？若书法，作口口口口口口（天之异体字，1形类“蒢”2“上口下大”3“上一下光”4“上兀下兀”5形类“蔸”6“上王下人”），见于何典，何人所造，弟知之乎？此不过言其大略。

    Explanation

    596

    Star names such as Tian Gen, Tian Shu, and Tian Bai. Mountain names like Tian Tai, Tian Fang, and Tian Shan. Animal names like Tian Zhi (a mythical bird) and Tian Ma (a mythical horse). Plant names such as Jing Tian, Zhu Tian, and Tian Men Dong. All these should be read literally because there are actual people, places, and things associated with them. However, names like Lou Tian, Mu Tian, and Tan Tian should be read figuratively. The Chinese character "tian" is pronounced with tones that are all within the two level tones (yin and yang). Why do they differ in the five cardinal directions, and why do Western barbarians have rising, falling, and entering tones, making one character pronounced in two or three different ways, such as Qilian, Pulai, Tongge, and Luoluo? Which country do these variations belong to? If we consider the calligraphy, the character "tian" has various forms: 1) resembling "蒢," 2) "上口下大," 3) "上一下光," 4) "上兀下兀," 5) resembling "蔸," 6) "上王下人." Which classic do these forms appear in, and who created them? Do you know? This is just a general overview.

    597

    若数典而备陈之，终日不能毕一字。”仲曰：“如此方为识字？人寿几何，即逾百龄亦不及也。”伯曰：“不如是，冒称识字是无耻矣。汝知识字之义乎？如相识者，必知其父祖是何名号，母妻何氏，兄弟几人，子孙何名何字，作何事业，一一能详述之，方不愧为相识。若仅能辨其本身姓名声容笑貌，不过泛交耳，识云乎哉！识字不外此理也。”仲曰：“弟实不能，兄好为之。”顿首服膺而退。

    Explanation

    597

    If one were to enumerate and fully detail everything, one could not complete even one character in a whole day. "Only in this way can one truly be said to know how to read?" the younger brother asked. "How long is a person's life? Even if one lived beyond a hundred years, it would still not be enough." the elder brother replied. "If one does not do this, claiming to know how to read is a disgrace. Do you understand the meaning of knowing how to read? To truly know someone, one must know the names and titles of their ancestors, the surnames of their mother and wife, how many brothers they have, the names and characters of their descendants, and what kind of career or achievements they have. One must be able to detail each one, only then can one truly be called acquainted. If one can only recognize their own name, appearance, and mannerisms, it is merely a superficial acquaintance. How can it be called knowing? Knowing how to read is no different from this principle." the elder brother said. "I truly cannot, please do it for me, brother," the younger brother replied. He bowed respectfully and left, having fully accepted the advice.

    598

    芗厈曰：此考据家也。以渊博为事，然学问之道无穷。有道学家，自太极两仪推而至周程张朱之理，无所不达；有名法家，自黄老翻变放而至申韩萧曹之律，无所不通；有术数家，河图洛书，天文星度，奇门禽遁，无不演贯者；有兵法家，韬略阴府，五花八门，攻守技艺，无不精练者，此皆有鬼神莫测之机。为王者师，有益于天下后世者也。通才不一，惟圣人能兼之。至于文章诗歌，润色太平，足以黼黻皇猷，固代有名家。着夫医卜星相，小道可观，然无大造于国家，卑卑不足法矣。

    Explanation

    598

    Xiang Yan said: This is a scholar of textual research. Taking extensive knowledge as their pursuit, yet the path of learning is boundless. There are scholars of the Way, who from the Taiji and the two instruments (Yin and Yang) trace the principles of Zhou, Cheng, Zhang, and Zhu, reaching everywhere; there are scholars of names and laws, who from the transformations of Huang-Lao extend to the laws of Shen, Han, Xiao, and Cao, understanding everything; there are scholars of techniques and numbers, who study the River Map, the Luo Book, celestial phenomena, stellar measurements, and the mysteries of Qi Men Dun Jia, mastering all; there are scholars of military strategy, who specialize in tactics, strategies, and secret methods, excelling in all techniques of attack and defense, all possessing the mysterious and unpredictable mechanisms of ghosts and gods. To serve as a teacher to kings, beneficial to the world and future generations. Talented individuals are not limited to one field, only sages can encompass all of them. As for literature and poetry, to embellish the peaceful times, sufficient to adorn the imperial policies, indeed there have been renowned masters in every generation. As for medicine, divination, astrology, and physiognomy, these are minor paths but worthy of observation, yet they do not make great contributions to the state, they are lowly and not worthy of emulation.

    599

    蒋三官
    　　
    　　壬戌之秋，予应试武林。寓室之前，隔河有染坊，每晨数十人在河干练色。杵声与俚歌相答，讻讻惊睡，作而询，主人对曰：“是蒋氏，三十年前不过落寞小肆。蒋翁人甚忠厚，生三子一女。女已嫁，长次子继父业，三官尚幼，随父兄学习。未几，二子俱完姻，食指日繁。本既不多，息亦无几，出入不敷，蒋翁以郁卒。三官无人管束，终日游荡。兄训之不悛，亦置度外矣。姊怜之，其夫业贸丝，行中客俱嘉湖粤闽富商，须人晋接，姊于是呼三官去日，‘汝游手好闲，何能自食？今我肆中客多，我为汝姊夫言之，可充陪宾，不过饮食谈笑，无他难也。且可得数两劳金。’三官善谈论，意甚惬。其姊夫试使见客，訚訚如也，词多诌媚，人皆悦之。游山观剧，罔不与共者。

    Explanation

    599

    Pao San-guan

    In the autumn of the year Ren Xu, I took the examination in Wulin. My lodging was across the river from a dyeing workshop. Every morning, dozens of people gathered by the riverbank to dye silk. The sound of pounding and folk songs echoed back and forth, creating a noisy disturbance that woke me from my sleep. I got up and inquired about it, and the landlord replied, "This is the Jiang family. Thirty years ago, they were just a small and unremarkable shop. Mr. Jiang was very honest and kind, and he had three sons and one daughter. The daughter is married, and the eldest and second sons carry on the father's business. The third son, San-guan, was still young and followed his father and elder brother in learning. Not long after, both sons got married, and the number of dependents increased daily. With not much capital and very little interest, their income did not cover expenses, and Mr. Jiang died of depression. San-guan, with no one to discipline him, wandered all day. His elder brother scolded him, but he did not reform, and he was also ignored. His elder sister pitied him. Her husband was engaged in the silk trade, and the travellers were all wealthy merchants from Jiangxi, Huzhou, Guangdong, and Fujian provinces. They needed someone to entertain guests, so the sister called San-guan and said, 'You loaf around, how can you support yourself? Now there are many guests in my shop. I will tell my brother-in-law, and you can serve as a companion to guests. It's just eating and chatting, nothing else is difficult. And you can get a few taels of labor money.' San-guan was good at talking, and he was very pleased. His sister-in-law tried to make him meet guests, and he was polite and composed. His words were flattering, and everyone was pleased with him. Visiting mountains and watching plays, he always went along with them."

    600

    “有高客非三官不欢，竟成莫逆交。无事时，客聚博，高拉三官入局，以无钱辞。高代出资同博。三官连胜，得采二百余金。高为收藏，其姊劝之日，‘闻汝博有起色，但无本之事，依人为活，一朝失手，何以对人。藉此经营本业，或有起家之日。即为恒产，岂不妙哉。’三官唯唯，与高客言，欣然与银如数。三官赴泖河，投行家置靛。时有靛客因父暴疾，急欲脱货归去，贬价甚廉，尚无人过而问也。三官至，主人喜引视。靛盈一屋，色甚鲜华，但不肯零星折兑。一价值千金，三官仅携银二百，自叹无成。以再商含糊复之。

    Explanation

    600

    There was a high-ranking guest who would not be happy without the three officials, and they eventually became close friends. When there was nothing to do, the guests gathered to gamble, and the high-ranking guest invited the three officials to join the game. They refused due to lack of money. The high-ranking guest then put up the money and joined in the gambling. The three officials won several times in a row, earning over two hundred gold pieces. The high-ranking guest kept the winnings for safekeeping. His sister advised him, saying, "I hear that your gambling has been going well, but gambling without capital, relying on others for a living, if you lose one day, how will you face others? Use this to manage your business. There might be a day when you can build a family. If it becomes a permanent property, wouldn't that be wonderful?" The three officials agreed and happily gave the silver as requested. They went to Maohu to deal with a merchant and buy indigo. At the time, an indigo merchant, whose father had suddenly fallen ill, urgently wanted to sell his goods and return home, so he lowered the price significantly, but no one had come to ask about it. The three officials arrived, and the owner was delighted to show them around. The indigo filled an entire room, and the color was very vibrant, but he refused to sell in small quantities. One batch was worth a thousand gold pieces. The three officials only had two hundred gold pieces on hand and sighed at their failure. After further negotiation, they responded ambiguously.

    601

    客父病益深，迫不及待，愿减价作八百金，主人怂恿之。三官以实告，客无奈，愿先得二百金作归计，余银立券事后取偿。主人作中，互相交易，各执契券。客先行，嘱三官归取银，乃向其姊告贷。姊夫日。‘安得如许银，不必存此妄想。姑舍是仍旧贯可也。况高客望汝甚切，速往晤之。’高相见大悦，约游西子湖，过酒肆哄饮。三官怀抱不开，静坐极闷郁。高日，‘吾与三兄半月别耳。昔之豪兴安在，何竞疏阔若是？’三官直陈心事。高笑日。‘六百金小事，何必为难，归即贷汝。’于是鼓兴畅谈，各得其乐。高偕三官回，立予银。其姊夫阻之，高日，‘与不与在我，还不还听渠，与尔无干。毋预他人事也。’其姊夫愧谢而退。

    Explanation

    601

    The guest's father's illness is worsening, and he is impatient, wishing to reduce the price to eight hundred gold coins, and the host encourages him. San Guan tells the truth, and the guest has no choice but to wish to receive two hundred gold coins first for his return, with the remaining silver to be settled with a contract after the matter is completed. The host acts as a mediator, and they engage in mutual transactions, each holding a contract. The guest leaves first, instructing San Guan to return and collect the silver, then goes to his sister to borrow money. His sister asks, "How can such a large amount of silver be obtained? There is no need to harbor such unrealistic hopes. Perhaps it is best to set aside this matter and continue as before." She adds, "Moreover, the high guest is eagerly awaiting you. Go quickly to meet him." Upon meeting, they are very pleased to see each other, agree to visit West Lake, and pass by a wine shop, drinking merrily. San Guan, however, is not in a good mood and sits quietly, extremely bored and depressed. The high guest says, "I have been separated from my dear friend San Guan for half a month. Where has your former enthusiasm gone? Why are you so distant now?" San Guan frankly reveals his heart's concerns. The high guest smiles and says, "Six hundred gold coins is a small matter. There is no need to make it difficult. Upon returning, I will lend you the money." Thus, they talk enthusiastically and freely, each finding joy in it. The high guest accompanies San Guan back and immediately gives him the money. His sister-in-law objects, but the high guest says, "Whether to give or not is up to me. Whether to repay or not is up to him. It has nothing to do with you. Do not interfere in other people's affairs." His sister-in-law apologizes and withdraws.

    602

    “时大早，河湖并涸，靛价日增。客侍父病痊而来，三官运银同日至，交清前欠，不能改议。逾三月，靛绝，价顿长十倍。主人复怂恿先售一成，得千金归。还高客本与子金，不受，酬以土宜。乃与两兄谋，倩夫策骑往驮，运而至武林城内，靛绝价徒增，而货不来，各染肆购者纷纷。三官货其半，得数千金。置别色，留其半自用。凡来染者，减价加色，四方争趋之。不十年，财至巨万。三官娶妻生子，为两侄纳粟得官，貤封五品。舆马出入，居然大家矣。”
    　　
    　　予过其肆，金碧辉煌。染夫百余，皆五彩。其手缘一色，有数夫专管，无混杂者。故色倍鲜明，与他肆不同云。

    Explanation

    602

    During a severe drought, rivers and lakes dried up, causing the price of indigo to rise daily. A guest returned after his father's illness was cured, and three officials arrived with silver on the same day to settle all previous debts, with no changes to the agreement. After three months, indigo production ceased, and the price suddenly increased tenfold. The owner again encouraged the sale of one part, and the guest obtained a thousand gold pieces and returned. He refused to accept the guest's capital and the son's money, repaying instead with local specialties. He then consulted with his two brothers and hired men to ride horses and carry the goods, transporting them to Wulin City. Indigo production ceased, but prices continued to rise, and buyers from various dye shops came in large numbers. The three officials sold half of their goods, obtaining several thousand gold pieces. They set aside other colors and kept half for personal use. For those who came to dye, they reduced prices and added colors, attracting people from all directions. Within ten years, their wealth accumulated to a huge amount. The three officials married and had children, and they had two nephews admitted to official positions through the grain system, being enfeoffed as fifth-rank officials. They entered and exited in carriages and horses, becoming a prominent family.

    I passed by their shop, which was splendidly decorated. There were over a hundred dyers, all wearing colorful clothes. Each hand specialized in one color, with several men managing each, ensuring no mixing. Thus, the colors were twice as bright, different from other shops.

    603

    飞车
    　　
    　　机巧之法，盛于西夷。缘彼处以能创新法取士，欲官者争造法器。穷工极巧，超出逾奇，不第供耳目玩，且有切于实用者。如火轮船，以薪煮水，以管束烟，断其机轮，递相催转而行，奇矣。然以小物喻之，不过如我国孩童所作走马灯法耳。悟其理，为之不难。惟飞车御风而行，能渡弱水三千。闻诸古而未见，于今乃竟有目睹者。魏地山明府语予曰：丙午谒选在都，九月上旬偶出厚载门，鼓楼前见通衢无数人咸翘首跂足仰望，哄诧异事。予因随众所指处瞩目，见半天一物，如舟无楫，如车无轮，长约三四丈，宽丈余，蓬蓬然四围如有旗帜。距地数十丈，看不甚明，由东北来，盘旋若鸢翔。忽坠下洋银十余，人争拾之。未几，往西南迅逝，小如一叶，又如一星，转瞬不见。说者曰此飞车也，泰西人所制，车中人以千里镜窥觇下方，城郭人民历历在目矣。

    Explanation

    603

    Flying Vehicle

    The ingenious methods flourish in the West. In that place, new methods for selecting officials are created, and those who wish to become officials compete to create mechanical devices. They exhaust all efforts in craftsmanship and ingenuity, surpassing and exceeding in novelty, not just for the entertainment of the eyes and ears, but also with practical applications. For example, the steamship, which boils water with firewood, channels the steam through pipes, cuts it off to make it turn the wheels, and thus propels the ship forward—strange indeed. However, if we compare it to a small object, it is no more than the method used by our country's children to make a spinning horse lantern. Understanding the principle, it is not difficult to create. The flying vehicle, however, travels on the wind and can cross vast distances. It has been heard about in ancient times but never seen; today, I actually got to see it. Governor Wei Di Shan told me: In the year Bingwu, I went to the capital to be selected for an official position. In early September, I happened to leave the Houzai Gate. In front of the Drum Tower, I saw countless people stretching their necks and standing on tiptoe, looking up, making a commotion about a strange event. I followed the crowd's gaze and looked in the direction they were pointing. I saw an object in the sky, like a boat without oars, like a carriage without wheels, about three or four zhang long, more than one zhang wide, with a tufted appearance, surrounded by something like flags. Several zhang above the ground, not very clear to see, coming from the northeast, circling like a kite flying. Suddenly, it dropped down ten or so foreign silver coins, and people scrambled to pick them up. Soon after, it quickly vanished to the southwest, as small as a leaf, or a star, disappearing in an instant. People said this is the flying vehicle, made by people from the Far West. People inside the vehicle used a telescope to observe below, and the city walls and people were clearly visible.

    604

    或曰：“他国有如是奇器，恐其以数千辆，载精卒数千人，飞入都邑，将不能御，亦不及防，城郭守具皆无用矣，岂不殆哉。”芗厈曰：否，否，此物藉风而起，顺风而行，如我国之纸鸢。有大至丈余者，非大风不能起，风微即落。夫纸竹至轻之物，尚不能收藏自如，况重笨如车耶？起即非易，收亦甚难，风力辅偏，即不能如意起落。况我军亦有轰天炮等火器，足以仰攻耶。君毋作杞人忧也。

    Explanation

    604

    Someone said, "If another country has such a strange device, they might use thousands of vehicles to carry elite soldiers, fly into our capital city, and we will not be able to resist or defend against it, making all city defenses useless. How dangerous!"

    Xiang Yan replied, "No, no. This device relies on the wind to take off and moves with the wind, similar to our country's kites. Some are as large as several feet, but they cannot take off without strong winds and will fall if the wind is weak. Even paper and bamboo, being very light materials, cannot be stored or controlled easily. How much more so for heavy and cumbersome vehicles? Taking off is not easy, and retrieving it is also very difficult. If the wind force is uneven, it will not be able to take off or land as desired. Moreover, our army also has artillery and other weapons, which are sufficient for attacking from above. You need not worry like the man from Qi."

    605

    丁养虚
    　　
    　　吾师丁养虚先生，奇伟人也。学围棋于施湘霞，传瑶琴于郭去非，皆国手也。先生入其堂奥，且精于奇门禽遁之学，能以拳石筑小山，为桥梁亭榭，栽径寸松柏，郁郁茸茸，有天然之致。山颠悬瀑布一道，穿桥曲折泻落。承以磁盆，水流循环，昼夜不绝。有欲窃其机巧者，拔起观之不得，仍置盆内，水止不流。经先生拨弄，依然洋溢。殆按八门生死法耳。好事者愿重价购之，不肯售，问其故，曰：“入他手不过旬日，水法不灵矣。人必以我为欺，我不愿贻人口实也。”

    Explanation

    605

    My teacher, Mr. Ding Yangxu, is a remarkable and extraordinary person. He learned Go from Shi Xiangxia and the guqin from Guo Qufei, both of whom are national masters. Mr. Ding has mastered the essence of their teachings and is also skilled in the study of the "Eight Mansions" and techniques of evasion. He is able to build a small mountain with fist-sized stones, creating bridges, pavilions, and planting miniature pines and cypresses, which are lush and dense, exuding natural charm. At the summit of the mountain, there is a waterfall that cascades through a bridge. The water flows continuously through a magnetic basin, day and night without ceasing. If someone tries to tamper with the mechanism, the water stops flowing. However, with Mr. Ding's adjustments, the water flows freely again, likely due to his deep understanding of the "Eight Mansions" principles of life and death. Those who admire his work offer to buy it at a high price, but he refuses to sell. When asked why, he says, "In the hands of others, it would not last more than ten days, and the water mechanism would no longer function. People would surely think I am deceiving them, and I do not wish to leave room for gossip or criticism."

    606

    一日谓家人曰：“盗将入我室，宜慎防之。”至晚，以椅凳十余，纵横排列院中，遥望之似有烟雾弥漫。先生嘱闭门，许在窗隙默窥。夜深人静，见一壮者持械越墙下，潜入凳内，钻爬逾跃，费尽伎俩，现诸身段，家人不禁大笑。盗似侧耳欲遁，至天明不能出。先生去其一椅，突从缺处逃，为众擒缚，先生曰：“休矣，彼一夜辛勤，未得一物，而供我玩戏，勿复苦之。”乃释缚，先生呼食啖之。问其故，曰：“吾侪三人，小人技最精。故先入，见宅门内房屋壮丽，跃入四面皆墙，或穴之，或逾之，愈进墙愈多，但闻人声，难觅出路。正惶恐间，忽见门开，冲突被擒。小人知罪矣。”问彼二人何不入，对曰：“闻宅内嗤嗤不绝，知有备，先遁矣。”先生笑曰：“归语尔曹，勿再至。我家恐墙多，一夜爬不尽也。”盗不解其故，唯唯谢去。

    Explanation

    606

    One day, the master told his family, "A thief will enter my house; you should be cautious and guard against it." By evening, he arranged more than ten chairs and stools in a disorderly manner in the courtyard, making it appear as if there was a smoky haze. The master instructed them to close the door and allowed them to secretly watch through the window gap. Deep into the night, they saw a strong man with weapons jump over the wall and sneak into the area between the chairs and stools. He crawled, climbed, and leaped, using all his tricks, displaying various postures, which made the family burst out laughing. The thief seemed to be listening and wanting to escape, but until dawn, he could not get out. The master removed one chair, allowing the thief to suddenly escape through the gap and be caught by the crowd. The master said, "Enough, he worked hard all night and got nothing, yet he provided us with entertainment; do not trouble him further." He then released the thief and invited him to eat. When asked why, the thief replied, "We three, the least skilled among us, are the most skilled. So we went in first and saw that inside the house, the buildings were magnificent. We jumped in, only to find walls on all sides, either digging through or climbing over, the more we went in, the more walls there were. We could only hear voices but could not find an exit. In our panic, we suddenly saw the door open and rushed out, only to be caught. We know we have committed a crime." When asked why the other two did not enter, he answered, "We heard laughter inside the house without end, knowing they were prepared, so we fled first." The master laughed and said, "Go back and tell your companions, do not come again. My house has too many walls, one night is not enough to climb them all." The thief did not understand the reason but bowed and left.

    607

    时二子皆冠，尚未婚娶，计非千金不能毕事。无已，乃藉新春设酒肆，用法择吉。凡奇门家验正时到，必天地人三才皆应，斯无谬误。先生择某月日寅时，应天微雪，地色白，先有一人青衣红带，持壶沽饮，后有文武二星官过门，即刻开张大吉。至日五鼓，起呼家人备祀神物。先生率二子悬灯开炉，果微风飘雪，有皂隶着色服，持锡壶叩关入曰：“冷甚，幸汝店早开，藉得御寒甚善。”先生询其夜深应差故，役曰：“都督阅边，舟抵马头，从本官往迎耳。”问知总制系文殿撰出身，所随中军参将系武状元，始悟即二星官也。役去未几，仪卫八驺，呼拥而过。先生即陈祭礼，鸣爆竹开市。从此沽饮者虽倍于前，千金究难骤致也。因自开烧锅，价廉而酒美，贩客厚获赢余，远近争趋之。每晨停于门者，肩挑百十。

    Explanation

    607

    At that time, both sons had come of age but had not yet married. It was calculated that without a thousand gold pieces, the matter could not be completed. With no other option, they took advantage of the New Year to set up a wine shop and used the Qimen method to choose a propitious day. When the time determined by the Qimen method arrived, it was necessary for the three talents of heaven, earth, and man to align, thus avoiding any error. The master chose a certain day and hour of the tiger. The sky responded with a light snowfall, and the ground was white. First, there was a man in blue clothes and a red sash, carrying a flask to buy wine. Later, two celestial officials, one civil and one military, passed by the gate. Immediately, the business opened with great auspiciousness. On the day at the fifth drumbeat (dawn), he got up and called the family to prepare offerings for the gods. The master led his two sons to hang the lights and open the furnace. Indeed, there was a light wind and snow. There was a clerk in colorful clothing, carrying a tin flask, who knocked on the gate and said, "It's very cold. Fortunately, your shop opened early, which is very good for warmth." The master asked why he was on duty so late at night. The clerk replied, "The governor was inspecting the border, and his boat reached the ferry landing. He went to meet him." He learned that the general was a scholar-official from the Dianzhuan (a literary examination), and the accompanying mid-level military officer was a military champion. He then realized they were the two celestial officials. The clerk left shortly. The ceremonial guards and eight attendants passed by loudly. The master immediately set up the sacrificial rites and set off firecrackers to open the market. From then on, the number of wine buyers doubled compared to before, but a thousand gold pieces were still difficult to obtain quickly. Therefore, he decided to open his own distillery. The price was low, and the wine was excellent. Merchants made substantial profits, and people from far and wide competed to come. Every morning, there were dozens of people carrying goods waiting outside the door.

    608

    期月得利千余金，势不能止。风闻入邑宰耳。当是时，烧锅之禁甚严。将搜索酿具而罪之。幸旗人为吏，得信急为之备。其蒸甑，所制铜壶式甚高大，重百余觔，或谋埋之，或谋毁之。先生曰：“否，否，势不及，必致败露。”率家人共举其壶，置厅事众目共睹之地。妻孥方疑讶间，宰已入，携先生进内宅，叱役穷搜，甚至掘地发墙，毫无踪影，宰出坐厅事，侍从吏役百余。壶明明在案间，无一睹者。宰去，集家人从容捶碎，熔化灭迹。或问：“何以官不能见？”先生曰：“搜远不搜近，偶然耳。”予知先生不肯泄机，必用六戊藏形法也。遂为子毕姻，改酒坊为钱局，俾二子掌之。先生以琴棋自娱，不与俗事矣。

    Explanation

    608

    Within a month, the scholar had made a profit of over a thousand gold, and the momentum could not be stopped. Rumors reached the ears of the local magistrate. At that time, the prohibition against distilling alcohol was very strict. They were planning to search for and confiscate the distilling equipment and punish the owner. Fortunately, the person in charge was a military officer, who acted quickly to prepare. The large and heavy copper still, weighing over a hundred jin, was either considered for burial or destruction. The scholar said, "No, no, we cannot escape in time, it will surely be exposed." He led his family to place the still in a visible location, where everyone could see it. While the family was still in disbelief, the magistrate had already entered, taking the scholar into his private quarters. He ordered a thorough search, even digging up the ground and tearing down walls, but found nothing. The magistrate then sat in the main hall, surrounded by over a hundred officials and servants. The still was clearly on the table, yet no one had seen it. After the magistrate left, the scholar calmly gathered his family and broke the still, melting it to destroy the evidence. Someone asked, "Why couldn't the magistrate see the still?" The scholar replied, "He searched far but not near, it was just a coincidence." I knew the scholar would not reveal the secret, and he must have used a specific method to hide the still. He then completed his son's marriage and changed the distillery into a mint, entrusting it to his two sons. The scholar then enjoyed himself with music and chess, no longer involved in worldly affairs.

    609

    其妻父朱氏，为邑名医，子四人，或继业，或设肆，因此起家。无赖之徒觊其有，肆欺讹诈。四子苦累，教子读书应试，凡入庠者，可支门户，盼望綦切。时朱翁考终将葬，舅以葬期谋于先生曰：“姊夫明晰阴阳，能为人福，如使吾子侄一人入泮，举家感甚。”先生敬诺，择冬月某日未时，应天微雨，二狗衔花戏墓侧，一男子戴铁帽，一孝妇索取石炭，此正时也。举棺封壤，孙必游庠。诸舅皆哗曰：“冬月惟有梅花，品甚贵重，狗乌能得？铁帽惟军中有之，太平之世谁敢戴此？恐未必如所算。”先生正容曰：“我亦不能预知，不过依书为断。我尽心为岳翁推选，恐未至大谬。”至日果小雨，舅已奇其言，舆榇入茔，停待正时安穴。见两小犬争芦花一枝来墓间，有农夫买得铁锅戴于首，以代雨具，孝妇亦至。诸舅大悦，推棺闭冢。次年一孙入泮。

    Explanation

    609

    The father-in-law Zhu was a renowned physician in the county, with four sons. Some continued the family's medical practice, while others set up shops, thus establishing their family's prosperity. However, idle and unscrupulous individuals coveted their wealth and engaged in fraud and extortion, burdening the four sons. To alleviate their plight, they taught their sons to read and prepare for the imperial examinations. Those who entered the academy could support the family, and the family hoped very much for this outcome. At the time, Zhu was nearing the end of his life and preparing for his burial. The father-in-law consulted the husband about the burial date, saying, "Your brother-in-law is knowledgeable in yin and yang and can bring good fortune. If one of my nephews or cousins enters the academy, the whole family would be very grateful." The husband respectfully agreed and chose a certain day in the winter month at the hour of the horse, when there was light rain on a clear sky. Two dogs were seen playing with flowers near the tomb, a man wearing a metal hat, and a virtuous woman seeking charcoal. This was considered the auspicious time. The coffin was lifted and buried, and it was predicted that the grandson would definitely enter the academy. All the uncles exclaimed, "In winter, only plum blossoms are present, and they are very valuable. How could dogs get them? Metal hats are only found in the military. In peaceful times, who would dare to wear such a thing? Perhaps it will not be as calculated." The husband responded seriously, "I cannot predict it either, but I will follow the book. I have done my best to select for my father-in-law, and it is unlikely to be a major mistake." On that day, it indeed rained lightly, and the father-in-law was surprised by the prediction. The coffin was carried into the grave and placed at the right time. Two small dogs were seen fighting over a branch of reed near the tomb, an old farmer wearing a metal pot on his head as a rain hat, and the virtuous woman also arrived. All the uncles were delighted and placed the coffin in the tomb. The following year, one of the grandsons entered the academy.

    610

    芗厈曰：先生以琴棋名噪一对，当道富室重聘争迎不应，高士也。其术数之学，老益韬晦。有求之者，曰：“我亦由夫人耳，从宪书指与一黄道日而已。”予从学琴，见案头奇门，谨请教。先生卜曰：“孺子尚可。”随指示奇壬布局法，不以术告也。叩之，曰：“此在自己研究，久能悟得，非师可授也。况以此眩人，恐有杀身之祸。何苦乃尔。”予不敢渎。今先生逝矣，惜秘学失传，谅下愚无德以承受之耳。

    Explanation

    610

    Master Xiang An said: The Master was famed for his skills in the zither and go, and though high officials and wealthy families competed to invite him, he did not respond, truly a recluse. His knowledge of divination and numerology became even more concealed with age. When someone sought his advice, he said, “I simply follow the almanac and point to a favorable day.” I studied the zither under him and saw Qi Men Dun Jia on his table, so I respectfully asked for instruction. The Master divined and said, “You are still capable.” He then showed me the method of arranging the qi and ren but did not explain the technique. When I pressed further, he said, “This requires self-study, and with time, one can understand it. It cannot be taught by a teacher. Using this to dazzle others could lead to one’s death. Why would you want to do such a thing?” I dared not press further. Now that the Master has passed away, it is a pity that his secret learning has been lost to posterity. It is likely that I, being foolish and unworthy, could not bear it.

    611

    阮封翁
    　　
    　　翁江南仪征人，性恻隐好义。壮为鹾伙，岁入百余金，往往賙恤亲友，一挥数十金，贫不能赡妻子，泊如也。一日，至广陵转运鹾务，市不戒于火，被焚千余家。赤贫者无力赁屋，男妇老幼皆露处。忽澍雨滂沱，立泥淖中，相向而泣。翁触目伤心，计极贫人不过百十，费无多，思有以授之。亟趋至商家，谋所以安置之法。商人以翁人微言轻，志大妄举，相与讪笑之，无肯为筹画者。翁忿且愧曰：“诸君既不顾乡谊，我虽非郡人，亦当独任。

    Explanation

    611

    Mr. Ruan, the respected elder from Yizheng in Jiangnan, was known for his compassionate and righteous nature. In his youth, he worked as a salt merchant, earning over a hundred gold pieces annually. He often helped his relatives and friends financially, sometimes donating tens of gold pieces at a time. Despite his poverty, which left him unable to support his family, he remained unperturbed. One day, he traveled to Guangling to manage salt transportation when a fire broke out unexpectedly in the city, burning down over a thousand households. The extremely poor, unable to afford rented houses, were left to live outdoors. Men, women, and children stood in the mud, weeping together. A sudden heavy downpour followed, drenching them in the mire. Witnessing this scene, Mr. Ruan was deeply moved and thought that the number of extremely poor people was no more than a hundred, with the cost of helping them being relatively small. He decided to find a way to assist them. Urgently, he rushed to the merchants to seek ways to provide shelter. However, the merchants, seeing him as a man of low social status with little influence, dismissed his ambitious plans, mocking him and refusing to help. Filled with anger and shame, Mr. Ruan said, "Since you all disregard our hometown ties, although I am not from this area, I will take on this responsibility alone."

    612

    ”咸笑曰：“汝大才有力，愿好为之，勿累我等。此功德无量也。”翁去，向居停作秦庭之哭，求预候薪俸数十金，呼匠为席棚百十间，俾贫民避风雨，欢呼感激，祈天为善士降祥。是年，翁应得劳金俱预支费讫，徒手归，几不能卒岁。幸其子为诸生，肄业书院，聪明才智为当道所器重，均有赠遗，藉以苦度。未几，乡会俱捷，入词林，大试皆前列，简在帝心，典试学差，旋俾以封疆重任，巡抚浙江。翁受二品封而殁。服阕后，节制两粤，入赞黄扉。晋翁太傅。天道福善，有以也夫。

    Explanation

    612

    All laughed and said, "You have great talent and strength. Please do it well, and do not burden us. This is an immense merit." The old man then went to the inn and made a weeping like that of Qin's court, asking for a prepayment of salary amounting to dozens of gold pieces. He called for craftsmen to build a hundred or so shelters, to allow the poor to shelter from wind and rain. They cheered and were grateful, praying to heaven to bestow good fortune on the virtuous. That year, the old man's due wages were all prepaid, and he returned empty-handed, nearly unable to survive the year. Fortunately, his son was a scholar, studying in a school. His intelligence and talent were highly valued by the authorities, and all had gifts, which helped the family survive through hardship. Soon, he passed both the provincial and metropolitan examinations, entered the Hanlin Academy, and ranked at the top in major exams. He was chosen by the emperor's heart, served as an examiner, and soon appointed to a high-ranking position, becoming the governor of Zhejiang. The old man received a second-rank official title before passing away. After mourning, he governed two Guang provinces and advised the emperor. He was promoted to the rank of Grand Tutor. Heaven rewards virtue, indeed it does.

    613

    或曰：“吾子持福善祸淫之说，每遇封翁，无不啧啧称叹，似矣。然如颜子渊，德行首列四科，宜其身膺显爵，子孙昌盛。何以生前屡空且夭殁，又无贤裔。是天道不可问久矣。”芗厈曰：“否，否，圣贤虽不可以常理论，然颜子独善其身，不屑兼善天下故也。尝读《韩诗外传》，孔子谓回曰：家贫居卑，胡不仕乎？对曰：回有郭外之田五十亩，足以给饘粥。郭内之田四十亩，足以为丝麻。鼓琴足以自娱，学于夫子者足以自乐。回不愿仕也。由此观之，品则高矣，而无益于苍生。天故报以配享圣殿，俎豆千秋，又世袭博士以奉之，正所以酬其德也。吾屡验天道，总以泽及于人者速得美报。所述诸封翁，德未必如颜子，而膏泽下于民，更觉难能而可贵也。

    Explanation

    613

    Someone said, "Your belief in the principle that virtue is rewarded and wickedness punished seems sound, as you often express admiration for ennobled men. However, consider Yan Hui, whose moral integrity was ranked first among the four categories of Confucian virtues. He should have been honored with a high-ranking official position and had a prosperous lineage. Why, then, was he frequently in poverty and died prematurely, with no worthy descendants? This suggests that the ways of heaven are beyond questioning."

    Xiang Yan replied, "No, no. While sages and virtuous men may not be judged by ordinary standards, Yan Hui focused solely on cultivating himself and did not concern himself with benefiting the world. I once read The Outer Transmission of the Han Poetry, where Confucius said to Hui, 'Your home is poor and your status low. Why not seek a position in government?' Hui replied, 'I have fifty acres of land outside the city, which is enough for my daily sustenance. Forty acres inside the city are sufficient for silk and hemp. Playing the lute brings me joy, and learning from you fills me with happiness. I have no desire to serve in office.' From this, it is clear that his moral character was high, but he did not benefit the people. Heaven rewarded him by honoring him in the same temple as the sage, ensuring his memory through rituals for a thousand generations, and granting him a hereditary position of a scholar-official to preserve his teachings. This was precisely how his virtue was repaid. I have often observed the ways of heaven, and they always reward those who benefit others with swift and favorable outcomes. The ennobled men I have mentioned may not have had the same moral integrity as Yan Hui, but their benevolence and blessings to the people make them even more remarkable and valuable."

    614

    赵甲
    　　
    　　晋人李某，在京师佐典商，岁入俸饯三百缗。有同乡赵甲者与相识，无事业，谋欲设杂贷肆而无资。商于李，慨然以百贯付之，曰：“姑以此试，得意后与吾合业可也。”一言为约，并无文券，亦无人知。未几李以疾卒，典主呼其子来，扶榇而反。适赵甲置货他出，归后知李已卒，为位哭奠。由此兴旺，不十年业隆数万。李子家中落，衣食不充，亲党咸劝出外营谋，卜之大吉。适有入都者，相伴而去。至旧典主处，与父执求引荐，有人知赵与李父故交，今赵业大兴，往求之，谅可录用，浼友往说。

    Explanation

    614

    Zhao Jia

    Jin person Li, in the capital city assisting a merchant, had an annual salary of three hundred strings of cash. There was a fellow townsman named Zhao Jia who knew him, but had no business and planned to set up a shop for miscellaneous loans but had no capital. He discussed with Li, who generously gave him a hundred strings of cash, saying, "Let's try this for now; after success, we can join the business together." They made an agreement with a single word, without any written documents, and no one else knew about it. Not long after, Li died of illness. The merchant called his son to come and carry the coffin back home. Zhao Jia happened to be out of town when he heard that Li had died, and upon returning, he set up a position to mourn and offer sacrifices. From this, Zhao Jia's business prospered, and within ten years, it grew to a significant size. Li's son fell into poverty, with insufficient clothing and food. Family and friends all advised him to go out and seek opportunities, which divined to be greatly auspicious. Happened to meet someone going to the capital, and they went together. They went to the old merchant's place, and sought recommendation from his elderly friend or mentor. Someone knew that Zhao and Li were old friends, and now Zhao's business was thriving, so they went to seek employment, and it was likely that they would be hired. They asked a friend to go and speak.

    615

    赵闻李子来，欣然款接曰：“我因尔父得有今日，我觅子久矣，奈无音耗，今自来投，若有神使。”纳为主帐而不议劳金。李子安于初学，亦不计较，尽心极力随同营运。赵见其辛勤刻苦，出入无私，顾而乐之曰：“子已逾冠，能自成家，应议婚娶为嗣续计。”李子曰：“侄依伯父为生，尚无进益，何敢娶妇自取累乎？”赵曰：“姑缓亦是，但尔既为主帐，应将我所有总计之，现存若干。”李子唯唯，数日查毕，现资并货物合计六万余。赵曰：“我与子剖之。子应得其半。”李子骇曰：“伯父何出戏言？侄在此数年，伯父周给衣食，感无既矣。矧在小郎之列，本不应得劳金，纵伯父怜而恤之，不过年例数十贯钱而已。

    Explanation

    615

    Sui warmly received Li Zi and said, "I owe my current position to your father. I have been looking for you for a long time, but there was no news. Now you have come to me as if guided by a divine hand." He took him on as the main accountant without discussing wages. Li Zi was content with his initial learning and did not dwell on the lack of wages, working diligently and with great effort alongside the operations. Sui looked at him with pleasure and said, "You are already over twenty, able to establish your own household. You should consider marriage for the continuation of the family line." Li Zi replied, "Nephew relies on his uncle for a living and has not yet made any progress. How dare I marry and burden myself?" Sui said, "Delaying is also acceptable, but since you are the main accountant, you should calculate all my assets and tell me how much we have now." Li Zi agreed and spent several days calculating, finding that the total assets and goods amounted to over 60,000 units. Sui said, "I will divide this with you. You should get half." Li Zi was startled and said, "Uncle, why are you joking? Nephew has been with you for several years, and you have provided for my food and clothing. I am deeply grateful. Especially since I am among the junior staff, I should not expect wages. Even if you show compassion and give me an annual allowance of several dozen strings of cash, that would be enough."

    616

    何若是之多耶？伯父子孙振振，非无人承受者，侄何敢越分？敬辞。”赵笑曰：“子毋却也。我自有法。”乃设盛筵，邀其荐主及乡里长者咸集。李子亦在坐，三杯后赵谓众曰：“某昔落魄京师，人皆白眼。李故友与我，非素交亦无瓜葛，一言之下，慨然助以百千，不立契券，是诚信我也。我由此起家，而李兄已逝。当时有与吾合业之说，既有此语，获利理应均分。我初晤李侄时，本欲表白，恐少不更事，入手挥霍去也。今见其勤俭能自经营，我何敢负李兄于地下乎？”乃出孝子所开单目曰：“请诸公作主匀分。”众顾李子曰：“赵伯世所难得，君有福哉。一窭人顿成富室，吾等借酒以贺。

    Explanation

    616

    "Why is there so much of it?" The descendants of the elder brother are numerous and thriving, yet there is no one to inherit it. How dare I overstep my bounds?" With respect. Zhao smiled and said, "Do not refuse. I have my own method." He then prepared a grand feast and invited the host of the recommendation and the elders of the village to gather. Li's son was also present. After three cups of wine, Zhao said to the crowd, "I was once penniless in the capital, and everyone treated me with contempt. Li, an old friend of mine, was not a close acquaintance nor did we have any family ties, but with a single word, he generously offered a hundred thousand without signing a contract. This is a testament to his trust in me. I thus established my family, but Li's elder brother has passed away. At that time, there was talk of joining businesses together. Since such words were spoken, the profits should be equally divided. When I first met Li's nephew, I originally intended to explain, fearing that he was inexperienced and might squander it. Now I see that he is diligent and thrifty, capable of managing his own affairs. How dare I betray Li's elder brother in the afterlife?" He then produced the list opened by the filial son and said, "I ask you all to decide on an equal division." The crowd looked at Li's son and said, "Uncle Zhao is rare in the world. You are fortunate indeed. A poor man suddenly became wealthy. We will toast to celebrate."

    617

    ”李子曰：“诸公且止，听我一言。赵伯所云并无凭证，是欲为义士耳。侄虽年幼，亦不敢取非义财。即亡父果存百千钱，以远年债一本一利，取二百贯足矣。多即非义，何敢自污？”赵笑而入，命群仆以三万数百金出曰：“今日交清，卸我重肩，惟子所欲。”李子取其百金而出，追之，遁矣。赵乃邀众作证，呈报城坊，求访恩主。有司异其事，行文山右，唤李子至，质明判给。李子曰：“吾侪小人，实无功德，不劳而得多金，暴富不祥，故不敢纳也。”官曰：“无已，今某庙久圯，汝其葺之，非功德乎？”二人叩谢去，争出布施，庙貌焕然一新。官易庙号曰“双义”，而颁赵甲以额，曰“重义轻财：“

    Explanation

    617

    "Li Zi said, 'Gentlemen, please stop and listen to me. What Lord Zhao said lacks evidence; he wishes to act as a righteous man. Although my nephew is young, he dares not take unjust wealth. If my father indeed had a hundred thousand coins, taking the principal and interest of old debts, two hundred strings of cash would be enough. Too much is unjust; how dare I defile myself?' Zhao smiled and entered, ordering his servants to bring out three hundred thousand strings of cash, saying, 'Today, settle everything and lift my heavy burden; do as you wish.' Li Zi took a hundred strings of cash and left, and Zhao chased him, but he fled. Zhao then assembled witnesses, reported to the city officials, and sought the benefactor. The officials found the matter unusual, issued a document to Shan You, and called Li Zi to come, making the judgment at dawn.
