Li Zi said, 'We common folk actually have no merit; receiving a lot of money without effort is unlucky, so we dare not accept it.' The official said, 'If there is no other way, now a certain temple has been in ruins for a long time; you should repair it, is it not a merit?' The two bowed and left, competing to donate, and the temple's appearance was completely renewed. The official changed the temple's name to 'Double Righteousness' and awarded Zhao Jia with a title, calling him 'Valuing Righteousness Over Wealth.'"

    618

    芗厈曰：晚近之世，至亲分家不均，甚至争讼。从未闻让财而逃，官访恩主者。不意市井小民，竟超乎世家之上。忆成案，载雍正六年。豫民崔世有拾秦太遗银一百七十两，访归原主，毫不受谢。制军田文镜入奏，奉旨予七品顶戴，赏银百两，以旌其善。赵甲之事在后，当事者宜援此例官之，以昭盛世之隆，而示天下法，惜乎未及此也。

    Explanation

    618

    Xiang Yan said: In recent times, the improper division of property among close relatives has led to disputes, even to the point of litigation. It is rarely heard of that someone would renounce wealth and flee, and that officials would visit the benefactor. Unexpectedly, common people from the marketplace have surpassed the gentry in their conduct. I recall a precedent from the sixth year of the Yongzheng era. A resident of Henan named Cui Shiyou found one hundred and seventy taels of silver left behind by Qin Tai, returned it to the original owner, and refused any thanks. The governor-general Tian Wenjing presented a report to the emperor, who issued an edict granting Cui Shiyou a seventh-rank hat and a hundred taels of silver as a reward, to commend his good deeds. The case of Zhao Jia occurred later, and officials should follow this precedent and reward him, to demonstrate the prosperity of the era and set an example for the entire country. Unfortunately, this did not happen.

    619

    崇伯鲧
    　　
    　　昔张太史江在馆修撰，夜梦冕旒王者曰：“朕崇伯鲧也，为有夏禹王之父。朕天性悻直，出言好争，不避君前，虽唐帝谓朕嚣讼，犹为四岳所举。治水无功则有之，虞帝改封朕于羽山，抚有东夷。朕由是励精图治，使贪狠夷风亦皆丕变。汉太史司马迁著于《本纪》，宋学士苏轼又表之于《志林》，皆彰彰可考者。何至如野乘所载，以朕盗帝息壤殛死羽渊，神化黄熊之说，荒诞不经，被朕以三千余年之诬妄。今剖析于子，若果穷凶极恶，误国殃民，唐尧圣主也，肯涵容乎？四岳贤相也，肯举荐乎？稷契、皋陶、伯益，非具臣也，肯包荒而与为同列乎？即虞帝登庸时，不过以绩用勿戚，知朕非治水才，降为子爵，迁谪东方。迂儒不解息壤之义，姑妄言之。抑知壤者，堤也，息者，安也。

    Explanation

    619

    Cao Bo Gun

    In ancient times, Zhang Taishi Jiang, while serving as a compiler at the imperial court, had a dream in which a king wearing a ceremonial crown and flowing train of pearls said, "I am Cao Bo Gun, the father of Yu the Great of the Xia Dynasty. My nature is straightforward and unyielding; I speak boldly and do not shy away from confronting the ruler. Although Emperor Yao called me contentious and litigious, I was still recommended by the Four Peaks. It is true that I failed in flood control, and Emperor Shun reassigned me to Yu Mountain, where I governed the eastern tribes. From then on, I strove with vigor to improve governance, bringing about significant changes in the fierce and barbaric customs of the eastern tribes. The Grand Historian of the Han Dynasty, Sima Qian, recorded this in the 'Chronicles' section of his 'Records of the Grand Historian,' and the scholar of the Song Dynasty, Su Shi, also documented it in his 'Essays on the Records.' These accounts are all verifiable. How could it be that folk histories claim I stole the divine soil from the emperor, was killed by divine punishment in Yu Mountain, and transformed into a yellow bear? Such stories are absurd and unreasonable, and I have been falsely accused for over three thousand years. Now, I will analyze this with you. If I were truly extremely wicked and harmful to the country and its people, would Emperor Yao, the sage ruler, have tolerated me? Would the Four Peaks, the wise ministers, have recommended me? Would capable officials like Ji, Xi, Gao Yao, and Boyi have tolerated me and placed me among them? When Emperor Shun appointed Yu the Great, it was because I was ineffective and caused worry, and he knew I was not suited for flood control, so he demoted me to a marquis and exiled me to the east. Pedantic scholars do not understand the meaning of the divine soil and speak without serious intent. But do they know that 'soil' refers to a dike, and 'peace' refers to stability?

    620

    当其时，洪水横流，帝都筑长堤以阻遏，名曰息壤，犹今所谓太平堤耳。及朕奉命治水，见壤内积雨成河，高于壤外，势极厄迫，不及陈奏，开去息壤以达之于壶口，俾帝都奠安。此亦一定不移之势，故朕子继朕之绩，既载壶口。朕惟不先陈明，加之曰盗，犹可言也。乃妄谓息壤有国重宝，帝以镇冀州者，朕盗至豫州，虞舜以是入罪，诛于羽渊，化为三足鳖。此等怪诞之词，欺后世而诬朕躬。其甘心乎？息骧果为国宝，朕冒不韪之名以救百姓，二帝将褒奖之不暇，而罪之乎？因盲左以虞帝分封四族于四裔，诬之曰投。又配以四兽，恶名遂至，贻为口实。果如盲左所诋凶德，天道昭明，肯予以圣子神孙乎？子为盛世史官，其为朕正之。”太史唯唯曰：“臣力不足以纂易古史，为流布人间可也。”崇伯喜，以袖拂之而觉。

    Explanation

    620

    When that time came, floods were raging out of control, and the imperial capital constructed a long embankment to block them, calling it Xirang, which is akin to today's so-called Peace Embankment. Upon being ordered to manage the water, I saw that the water inside the embankment had accumulated into a river, higher than the outside, creating an extremely urgent and pressing situation. I did not have time to report it, so I opened up Xirang to connect it to the Hukou, ensuring the stability and safety of the imperial capital. This was also an unchangeable situation, hence my son will continue my achievements, as already recorded in Hukou. I only failed to explain beforehand, and was called a thief, which is still understandable. However, they falsely claimed that Xirang was a national treasure, used by the emperor to stabilize the state of Jizhou, and I stole it to the state of Yuzhou, leading Emperor Shun to charge me with a crime, execute me in Yuyuan, and transform me into a three-legged turtle. Such absurd words deceive future generations and accuse me personally. Are they content with it? If Xixiang is indeed a national treasure, I risked the name of wrongdoing to save the people, and the two emperors would not have time to praise me, yet they would punish me? Because Blind Zuo recorded Emperor Yu dividing four clans among the four borders and accused him of being exiled, pairing him with four beasts, the bad reputation followed, leaving it as a pretext for criticism. If it is as Blind Zuo accused of evil deeds, the will of heaven is clear, would they grant me the status of a sage's son and a divine descendant? You are a historian of a prosperous age, please correct it for me. The historian nodded and said, "My ability is insufficient to rewrite ancient history, but spreading it among people is acceptable." Chongbo was pleased, and with a wave of his sleeve, he became aware.

    621

    芗厈曰：祟伯鲧不过若后世恃才傲物人耳。信如左氏所云，世济其凶，增其恶名，以至于尧舜不能去。唐尧之治，贤奸混杂，尚成其为圣世乎？况鲧为轩辕元孙，伊者族叔，如何加以世凶之名。盖左氏浮夸，不过为季文子表功，图行文绚烂起见，不足为据。至稗官野史，肆其诋诬，甚有幽尧逼让，敢加虞帝以篡弑名，其谬妄为何如！崇伯事又乌足辩，惟息壤之说，剖析分明，足解千古疑惑，洵确凿可据也。
    　　
    　　象棋子
    　　
    　　吾乡陈体斋中丞，讳用敷。抚皖时，有吏年仅三十四五岁，老成练达，谨慎小心，为中丞所倚重。然甚贫，恒周济之，而蓝缕如故。中丞诲之曰：“凡人立身，以品行为第一。汝虽下吏，亦有进阶，窘困若是，谅有不端所致。”吏跪诉曰：“大人平日不以吏为愚拙，公事任之且屡沐恩波，垂青逾格。吏有人心，何敢荡检越闲，作非分事？惟有勤慎自守，此在天鉴之中。其所以贫困至此，实为子累。

    Explanation

    621

    Xiang Yan said: Gun, the Lord of Yu, is no more than a person from later generations who relies on his talent and looks down on others. If it is indeed as the Zuo Commentary says, that the evil of previous generations continued in him, increasing his bad reputation, to the point where Yao and Shun could not remove him. The reign of Emperor Yao, with its mixture of the virtuous and the wicked, can still be considered a sage era? Moreover, Gun was the grandson of the Yellow Emperor and the nephew of Yi, how could he be given the name of a generation of evil? The Zuo Commentary is exaggerated, merely to show off the achievements of Ji Wenzi, to make the writing splendid, and is not sufficient as evidence. As for minor officials and folk histories, they indulge in slander and defamation, even going so far as to claim that Yao was deceived and forced to abdicate, and that Shun was given the name of usurpation and murder. How absurd is this! The story of Gun is not worth debating, but the explanation of the magical soil (息壤) is clearly analyzed, sufficient to resolve doubts for thousands of years, indeed solid and reliable.

    As for the chess pieces:

    During the time when I was governor of Anhui, there was an official who was only in his mid-thirties, mature and experienced, careful and cautious, and relied upon by the governor. Despite my constant help, he remained in tattered clothes. The governor instructed him: "The way a person establishes themselves, character is the first priority. Although you are a low-ranking official, you also have opportunities for advancement. In such a state of distress, surely due to some improper behavior." The official knelt and said: "You usually do not think of me as stupid or incompetent, entrust me with official duties and often bathe me in your grace, favoring me beyond the usual. I have a human heart, how dare I violate rules and cross boundaries, do things beyond my duty? Only by being diligent and cautious can I maintain myself, this is within the view of heaven. The reason for my poverty up to this point, is actually due to my son."

    622

    ”中丞曰：“汝有几子，各为何业？”吏曰：“自十六岁娶妻，夫妇几年，年必双产，皆男。至三十三岁，已有三十二子矣。俱成立无夭者。人目为象棋子。虽布衣蔬食，岁需数百金。吏守法循分，岁入不过百余金，故窘若此。”中丞诧异久之，曰：“此熙朝人瑞也，悉以而子见我。”吏唯而出，未几抱者，携者，随者，鱼贯入拜堂下者，皆貌似其父。中丞顾而怜之，人赐帛一匹，银一定，领谢而去。中丞谓人曰：“小为赐与，费已百余金，无怪乃父之窘若是也。”
    　　
    　　或曰：“子以多为贵，中必有佳者。此吏窘于前，必裕于后。”芗厈曰：“否，否，好子不在多，多则贤愚不等。古今来多子者莫如周文主，虽有武，周之圣，而管、蔡播乱于骨肉之间。故尧曰：多男多累，子仅九人，尚有傲慢朋淫之丹朱，况多至百男，虽圣人亦训诲不及。凡庶之家，教育奚能周？至若有一败辕之犊，必牵率老夫至于绝地。多子之为害，岂浅鲜哉？”

    Explanation

    622

    The Chancellor said, "How many sons do you have, and what are their occupations?" The official replied, "Since I was sixteen and married, my wife and I have had two sons each year, all of whom are boys. By the time I was thirty-three, I had already had thirty-two sons. All of them have grown up and none have died prematurely. People call them 'chess pieces.' Although I live a simple life with a vegetable diet, it costs hundreds of gold pieces annually. I adhere to the law and follow my duties, earning no more than a hundred gold pieces per year, which is why I am in such a difficult situation." The Chancellor was astonished for a long time and said, "This is a blessing of the prosperous dynasty; bring all your sons to see me." The official nodded and left. Shortly after, he returned with his sons, who entered in a line and bowed in the hall, all resembling their father. The Chancellor looked at them with pity and gave each one a piece of silk and a hundred gold pieces, after which they thanked him and left. The Chancellor said to someone, "This small gift costs over a hundred gold pieces, so it is not surprising that the father is in such a difficult situation."

    Some said, "Children are valued for their number; among them, there must be some who are excellent. This official may be in difficulty now, but he will surely be wealthy in the future." The Xiangyan said, "No, no, good children are not determined by their number; having many means that their talents and intelligence vary. Throughout history, no one has had more children than King Wen of Zhou, who had ninety-nine sons. Although he had King Wu, the sage of Zhou, his brothers Duke Guan and Duke Cai caused chaos among the family. Therefore, Emperor Yao said, 'Having many sons brings many responsibilities; I have only nine sons, yet one of them, Dan Zhu, was arrogant and promiscuous. How much more so if one has a hundred sons? Even a sage cannot educate them all. For common families, education cannot cover all children. If one misbehaving child is like a calf that leads an old ox to a dead end, the harm of having many children is not slight.'

    623

    斗鹑
    　　
    　　禽之善斗者，有鸡，有画眉，有鹌鹑。斗鸡，古法也。而今亡矣。南人好斗画眉，北人好斗鹌鹑，惟山右为甚。富室贵胄，遇有俊物，不惜重价购之。是以捕鹑为业者众，而招客开圈以分其采者，所在多有。示日开圈，鹑客麋集，挟资千万。佥凭主人配鹑之优绌与采之多寡议定。二客以鹑入圈合斗，飞跃而出即以败论，胜者获采，主抽加一。规矩整肃，严同军令，无弗凛遵者。畜鹑之法，择俊者喂以黍，贮以囊，于午夜手握之。时其饮食而简节之，不使肥。俾鹑得人之精气，则骨力尤劲。

    Explanation

    623

    Partridge Fighting

    Among birds known for their fighting prowess, there are chickens, orioles, and partridges. Partridge fighting is an ancient practice, but it no longer exists today. People from the south enjoy fighting orioles, while those from the north prefer partridges, with the people of Shanxi being particularly enthusiastic. Wealthy families and nobles, upon encountering fine partridges, would not hesitate to pay high prices for them. As a result, many people make a living by catching partridges, and those who invite guests to fight partridges and share the winnings are numerous. On the day of the match, partridge enthusiasts gather, each bringing substantial sums of money. They all rely on the owner to match the partridges based on their quality and the amount of money they bring. Two guests bring their partridges into the arena to fight; if a partridge flies out, it is considered a loss; the winner gets the winnings, and the host takes an additional share. The rules are strict, as if military orders, and everyone strictly follows them. The method of raising partridges involves selecting the best ones, feeding them millet, and keeping them in bags, which are held in the hand at midnight. They control their diet and keep them lean, so that the partridges absorb the essence of human energy, making their bones and strength particularly strong.

    624

    有富室某翁，幼好鹑，老而弥笃。偶得一鹑，貌亦如恒，惟神清骨俊，有矫矫不群之概。翁视为至宝，以重聘延善鹑客专抚此鹑。客亦不惜辛勤，昼夜把持。半载，鹑益健，翁喜曰：“可以一战矣。”凡鹑当之，无不靡者。适有百里外圈主招翁，翁将应命，因有事阻，使客囊鹑与金先往。主人不知其为翁使也，知其多金，意谓好斗之客，延接甚优，次日开圈，主谓客曰：“今有某公子以大采战者，客有意乎？”客思此鹑一时无敌，盍私与斗，倘得采亦善策，允之。

    Explanation

    624

    A wealthy man from a certain family, who had been fond of pheasants since his youth, grew even more passionate about them as he aged. Once, he obtained a pheasant that looked ordinary, but it had a clear spirit and elegant appearance, with a distinctive and outstanding demeanor. The man regarded it as a precious treasure and hired a skilled pheasant trainer with a high salary to care for it exclusively. The trainer was also diligent, tending to the pheasant day and night. After half a year, the pheasant grew stronger, and the man was pleased, saying, "It is now ready for a fight." Whenever it faced other pheasants, none could defeat it. Just then, a pheasant owner from a hundred miles away invited the man to a match. The man intended to accept the invitation but was prevented by some unforeseen circumstances. He sent the trainer with the pheasant and money to go first. The host did not know that the trainer was acting on behalf of the man and, knowing that the trainer had a lot of money, thought he was a competitive person. The host treated him very well. The next day, the pheasant fighting arena was opened, and the host spoke to the trainer: "There is a young nobleman who wants to fight with a valuable pheasant. Are you interested?" The trainer thought that this pheasant was unbeatable at the moment and considered secretly fighting it. If he won, it would be a good strategy. He agreed.

    625

    至圈，所见公子，翩翩美少，华彩功人。出鹑洁白如鹤，赤其嘴足，英英独立。知与不知，莫不叫绝，客自视其鹑，不如远甚。逡巡欲退，主前阻之曰：“客已许之，不能中止，犯规有罚。”客不得已，请以百金试。乃纵鹑入圈，白鹑即奔啄之。客鹑绕圈避，一跃而出，幸勿伤也。公子哗曰：“客鹑败矣。”攫其百金。客思丧金无以对翁，不觉泪下，主曰：“客金多矣。以豪华士吝此微资，何必来我家耶？”客以实对。公子曰：“既如是，尔姑讳之。俟翁来，请以千金赛，尔怂恿以成其事。当归尔金。”客喜诺，主亦从之。

    Explanation

    625

    Upon arriving at the pen, the guest saw a young man who was graceful and handsome, a skilled and accomplished individual. The cock he presented was as white as a crane, with red beak and feet, standing out prominently. Both those who understood the game and those who did not were amazed by its appearance. The guest, looking at his own cock, found it far inferior. He hesitated and wanted to back out, but the host stopped him and said, "You have already agreed to the bet, and you cannot stop now. Breaking the rules will result in a penalty." The guest, having no choice, asked to bet a hundred gold coins. The cocks were then released into the pen. The white cock immediately charged and pecked at the other cock. The guest's cock circled around to avoid the attack and jumped out, luckily without getting hurt. The young man loudly declared, "The guest's cock has lost." He took the hundred gold coins. The guest, thinking he would lose money and have nothing to show his father, could not help but cry. The host said, "You have a lot of money. As a wealthy person, you should not be stingy with such a small amount of money. Why come to my house?" The guest told the truth. The young man said, "If it is like this, you might as well keep it a secret. Wait for your father to come, and I will bet a thousand gold coins. You will encourage him to do it. I will give you back your money." The guest happily agreed, and the host also agreed.

    626

    翌日翁来，与公子晤。各道向慕之诚，主人特为开圈，翁先出其鹑。公子赞叹，为赏其俊健，曰：“非我玉鹑莫与对。然非赌千金不可。”客旁哂曰：“哈公子何视千金意重耶？吾鹑即万金何畏？”翁欣然允诺，各以千金交主人。公子始以白鹑入圈。翁视之有惧色，然已无可奈何，姑听之，白鹑见翁鹑，怒逐之，翁鹑复绕圈而奔。公子洋洋自得，视千金为囊中物矣，曰：“闻翁鹑无与对者，今何怯耶？”翁被讪甚赧，曰：“鹑乎，汝好为之。”翁鹑忽翻身仰卧，白鹑突啄其胸，翁鹑以两足指抉白鹑二目，腾跃而起，连啄其脑，脑裂血飞，白鹑竟毙。公子咋舌曰：“何前怯而后猛耶？”翁曰：“高明柔克，公子之鹑失之骄耳。”主人乃以二千金归翁，大设筵宴以贺胜。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：胜者所用，即败者之兵。可知人各有命，不能强也。彼阴谋诡计者，不值一笑矣。

    Explanation

    626

    The next day, the old man arrived and met with the young lord. Each expressed their deep admiration and sincerity. The host specially opened the pen, and the old man first brought out his quail. The young lord praised it, admiring its strength and vigor, and said, "Only my jade quail can match it. But it cannot be done without betting a thousand gold." A guest nearby sneered and said, "Young lord, why do you treat a thousand gold as so significant? Even if my quail is worth ten thousand gold, I have no fear!" The old man happily agreed, and each handed over a thousand gold to the host. The young lord then brought his white quail into the pen. The old man looked at it with a fearful expression, but having no choice, he let it go. The white quail saw the old man's quail and angrily chased it, while the old man's quail ran around the circle. The young lord was very pleased with himself, treating the thousand gold as if it were in his pocket, and said, "I've heard that your quail has no match, yet why is it so timid now?" The old man, feeling deeply humiliated, said, "Quail, do well!" Suddenly, the old man's quail flipped over and lay on its back. The white quail suddenly pecked at its chest, and the old man's quail used its two feet to gouge out the white quail's eyes, leaping up and pecking at its brain. The white quail's brain split open, blood flew, and it died. The young lord gasped and said, "Why was it timid before and fierce afterward?" The old man replied, "The wise and gentle can overcome. Your quail lost because of arrogance." The host then gave the two thousand gold to the old man and held a grand banquet to celebrate the victory.

    Xiang Yan said: The victor uses what the defeated used. It shows that each person has their own fate and cannot be forced. Those who use underhanded tricks are not worth a laugh.

    627

    禄命
    　　
    　　唐太宗命太常博士吕才刊定阴阳杂书，其叙禄命曰：“长平坑卒，未闻共犯三刑；南阳贵人，何必惧当六合？今有同年同禄而贵贱殊，共命共胎而夭寿异，此皆禄命不验之证也。”
    　　
    　　近有赵姓推测子平，精极一时。自推其命应得四品，因思索未读书，难求科甲，纳贤尚可求名，奈家无储畜。仗其术云游四方，欲争名于朝，遂至京师，无如依命直谈，鲜所许可，致绝顾问。见人皆以谄誉获利，不胜忿懑。出都回至扬州，其道盛行。乃赁夏屋，延衣冠中人作支宾。自居楼上，悬铃缒筐以取。来求者先以一金登号，支宾始置八字于筐，鸣铃而上。非大富贵人，罕谋其面，名噪一时。本郡太守遗仆就问，赵见八字咸与己同，不胜诧异，飞一条以询曰：“生于南方，忝在同行。生于北方，四品黄堂。”来人曰：“是旗籍也。”推之皆验，人验而己不验，何也？

    Explanation

    627

    1. 禄命

      • Translation: Fate and Fortune

    2. 唐太宗命太常博士吕才刊定阴阳杂书，其叙禄命曰：“长平坑卒，未闻共犯三刑；南阳贵人，何必惧当六合？今有同年同禄而贵贱殊，共命共胎而夭寿异，此皆禄命不验之证也。”

      • Translation: Emperor Taizong of Tang ordered the Grand Master of Rites, Doctor Lu Cai, to compile and standardize various books on yin-yang and fortune-telling. In his introduction to fate and fortune, he said: "The soldiers buried at Changping did not hear of their shared involvement in the three calamities; the nobleman from Nanyang, why should he fear the alignment of the six directions? Today, there are people born in the same year and holding the same rank who have vastly different statuses, and those with the same fate and birth who have different lifespans. All these are evidence that fortune-telling is unreliable."

    3. 近有赵姓推测子平，精极一时。自推其命应得四品，因思索未读书，难求科甲，纳贤尚可求名，奈家无储畜。仗其术云游四方，欲争名于朝，遂至京师，无如依命直谈，鲜所许可，致绝顾问。

      • Translation: Recently, there was a man surnamed Zhao who specialized in Zi Ping fortune-telling and was extremely skilled for his time. He predicted that his own fate would make him a fourth-rank official. However, he thought that without a good education, it would be difficult to pass the imperial examinations. He believed that seeking talented people could still bring fame, but he had no savings at home. He traveled around the world, hoping to gain fame in the court, and eventually arrived in the capital. But because he spoke directly based on fate, he was rarely accepted, and thus cut off from inquiries.

    4. 见人皆以谄誉获利，不胜忿懑。出都回至扬州，其道盛行。乃赁夏屋，延衣冠中人作支宾。自居楼上，悬铃缒筐以取。来求者先以一金登号，支宾始置八字于筐，鸣铃而上。非大富贵人，罕谋其面，名噪一时。

      • Translation: Seeing that others gained profit through flattery, he could not help but feel angry. He left the capital and returned to Yangzhou, where his method became popular. He rented a summer house and invited middle-class gentlemen as assistants. He lived on the upper floor, hanging a bell and lowering a basket to receive offerings. Those who came first paid one gold coin to register, and the assistant then placed the eight characters (birth details) in the basket and rang the bell to signal. He rarely met with people of great wealth and status, but his name became famous for a time.

    5. 本郡太守遗仆就问，赵见八字咸与己同，不胜诧异，飞一条以询曰：“生于南方，忝在同行。生于北方，四品黄堂。”来人曰：“是旗籍也。”推之皆验，人验而己不验，何也？

      • Translation: The governor of the prefecture sent a servant to ask. Zhao saw that the eight characters were all the same as his own and could not help but be astonished. He sent a message to inquire: "Born in the south, shamelessly in the same profession. Born in the north, a fourth-rank official." The servant replied: "It is from the Manchu banner." All predictions were accurate, but why did others predict correctly while he did not?

    Explanation of Key Translations

    1. 禄命: Translated as "Fate and Fortune" to capture the essence of the practice of predicting one's destiny.
    2. 长平坑卒: Translated as "The soldiers buried at Changping" to maintain the historical reference.
    3. 南阳贵人: Translated as "the nobleman from Nanyang" to preserve the regional and social context.
    4. 三刑: Translated as "the three calamities" to convey the astrological concept.
    5. 六合: Translated as "the alignment of the six directions" to maintain the astrological meaning.
    6. 同年同禄而贵贱殊: Translated as "people born in the same year and holding the same rank who have vastly different statuses" to clarify the contrast.
    7. 共命共胎而夭寿异: Translated as "those with the same fate and birth who have different lifespans
    628

    又有浙抚亦旗人也。其夫人舟至镇江，午夜欲产。闻停泊处人声嘈杂，有市腐者，妇亦将产。干仆以重金夺其收生姥至。则夫人生儿，肆中亦生儿。同时闻啼声，夫人疑此儿之庸也。后抚故，公子以荫得官，洊升浙抚。太夫人道出停泊处，访同产之儿，亦已成人，仍业腐也。是二人者，或母有南北，时有正初，地有水陆之分。至《消夏录》载纪晓岚学士之侄，与奴子刘云鹏同生。其侄十六岁而夭，奴子健在。产时只隔一窗，两儿并落蓐，非惟地同时同，乃至分秒亦同。一尊一卑，一夭一寿，又何说也？可见吕太常之驳论，千古不移矣。
    　　
    　　芗厈曰：天下广大，每日万生万死，帝皇夭寿之日，岂无同者？昔明太祖密谕各布政，确搜与同八字之人。乃进三人，一僧，一丐，一市侩。帝以问刘青田，亦无以对。故曰，命之理微，圣人罕言之。

    Explanation

    628

    There was also a governor of Zhejiang who was a Manchu. His wife arrived by boat in Zhenjiang and wanted to give birth at midnight. She heard that the place where the boats were moored was noisy, and there was a butcher there who was also about to give birth. The servant bribed someone with a large sum of money to bring a midwife. The lady gave birth to a son, and a son was also born in the shop. At the same time, they heard the cry of the baby, and the lady suspected that this child was common. After the governor died, his son inherited his position through imperial favor and was later promoted to governor of Zhejiang. The grandmother passed by the place where the boats were moored and visited the child who was born at the same time. The child had grown up and was still working as a butcher. These two children, one mother was from the north, the other from the south, the time was the same, and the place was different, either on water or land. In the Xiao Xia Lu (Record of Summer), it is recorded that the nephew of Academician Ji Xiaolan and the servant Liu Yunpeng were born at the same time. His nephew died at the age of sixteen, but the servant Liu Yunpeng was still alive. At the time of birth, they were separated by only one window, and both children were born at the same time. Not only was the place and time the same, but even the seconds were the same. One was noble, the other was lowly; one died young, the other lived long. What can be said about this? This shows that the refutation of Lü Taichang is unshakable for all time.

    Xiang Yan said: The world is vast, and every day thousands of people are born and die. Could it be that the day a monarch dies young or lives long is not the same as someone else's? Once, Emperor Zhu Yuanzhang of the Ming dynasty secretly ordered the provincial governors to search for people with the same birth year, month, day, and hour. They found three people: a monk, a beggar, and a hawker. The emperor asked Liu Qingtian, but he had no answer. Therefore, it is said that the principle of fate is subtle, and even sages rarely speak of it.

    629

    第八卷
    　　
    　　疑难杂案五则
    　　
    　　燕赵间有乡农，中年续娶。妇年十五，性好淫。其夫酒徒也，日在醉乡，不能满其愿。妇逾年举一子。其夫外出佣工，时往时还。更历十六年，子已长成，贫无二室，母子同卧起。其母不顾名义，强与子奸而交恶其夫。醉而毙之，分其尸埋炕内。虽无人知，皆心惊不自安。值秋涝，母子随流民出关，至沈阳为人佣，阅三四年，以勤俭积东钱百十贯。适市有杂货肆，主西人，欲贱售还乡。子得之，仍携其母同居，竟称夫妇，连举二子。又阅五六年，业日隆盛，俾奸生子附学读书。其年又值内歉外丰，妇之胞兄亦逃出口，适至此求宿，有老人指令投乡亲，引至肆，母子相见愕然。其兄以遇妹与甥，喜出意外。问妹夫何在？则支离其词。

    Explanation

    629

    In the eighth volume,
    Five Cases of Complex Legal Issues

    Between the states of Yan and Zhao, there lived a rural dweller who remarried in middle age. His new wife was fifteen years old and had a tendency towards promiscuity. His husband was a habitual drunkard, spending his days in a state of intoxication, unable to satisfy his wife's desires. After a year, she gave birth to a son. The husband went out to work as a laborer, coming and going irregularly. After sixteen years, the son had grown up, and they were poor, without a separate room, sleeping and waking together. The mother disregarded social norms, forcing herself on the son and hating her husband. One day, she killed him in a drunken state, dividing his body and burying it in the kang (heating stove). Although no one knew about it, everyone was shocked and uneasy. During the autumn floods, the mother and son followed the refugees out of the pass, arriving in Shenyang as laborers. After three or four years, they accumulated a hundred or so strings of copper coins through diligence and frugality. They happened to be in a market with a miscellaneous goods store, the owner of which was a Westerner, wanting to sell cheaply and return home. The son acquired the store and still took his mother to live with him, eventually calling themselves husband and wife and having two more children. After another five or six years, their business grew increasingly prosperous, allowing the illegitimate son to attend school and study. That year, there was an internal shortage and external abundance, and the wife's brother also escaped to the frontier, arriving here to seek shelter. An old man instructed him to seek out his fellow villagers, leading him to the store. The mother and son met in surprise. The brother was joyfully surprised to meet his sister and nephew. When he asked where his sister's husband was, she evaded the question.

    630

    未几，见二子自塾归，母其妹而父其甥，虽不解其故，亦虑不及此也。妇不能不留兄宿，惧泄机关，明日赠千钱遣之曰：“此地官不准留外人，毋累我也。”其兄不肯行，则挥逐之。复遇前老人曰：“既遇至亲，何不多留几日？”其兄以妹母子无情告。老人曰：“是夫妇也，街坊咸知。生子时，我妻为之收产，何得谓母子？”其兄惊悟报官，拘讯得实，曰：“光天化日之下，岂可容此恶兽？然无律可援，亦不敢上污圣聪。”缚母子四人于狱，绝其食毙之。焚尸于野，投骨于河，以家业断给其兄作奖赏完结。或曰：“若使上谳，母子佥拟凌迟，罪所应得。其二子杀之乎？放之乎？穷于拟议矣。”予曰：“应请比照叛逆案内犯该凌迟者，其子孙解交内务府，阉割发塞外，给官兵为奴。既不戕无知之命，又不留悖逆之裔。老法家以为然否？”

    Explanation

    630

    Shortly after, she saw two sons returning from the school. She treated the mother as her sister and the father as her uncle, though she did not understand the reason, nor did she consider it. The wife could not help but keep the brother to stay, fearing the revelation of secrets. The next day, she gave him a thousand coins and sent him away, saying, "The local officials do not allow outsiders to stay; do not trouble me." When the brother refused to leave, she hit him and drove him away. Later, she met the previous old man and said, "Since you have encountered a close relative, why not stay for a few more days?" The brother reported that his sister and her son were heartless. The old man said, "They are a married couple, and everyone in the neighborhood knows. When the child was born, my wife helped with the delivery. How can they be called mother and son?" The brother was shocked and reported to the authorities. After interrogation, the truth was revealed. They said, "Under the bright sun, how can such a beast be tolerated? However, there is no law to support this, and I dare not tarnish the wisdom of the emperor." They tied the mother and her three children in prison and starved them to death. They burned the bodies in the wild and threw the bones into the river. They divided the family property and gave it to the brother as a reward to conclude the matter. Someone said, "If it were to be judged by a higher court, the mother and son were both sentenced to be executed by slicing, which is the punishment they deserve. Should the two sons be killed? Or released? It is difficult to decide." I say, "It should be requested to compare with cases of rebellion that were sentenced to be executed by slicing. Their descendants should be handed over to the Imperial Household Department, castrated, and sent to the frontier to serve as slaves to the soldiers. Neither harming innocent lives nor leaving behind traitorous offspring. Do the old legal scholars agree with this?"

    631

    有小家子年十二，父母以疫相继亡。胞伯父母年老无子，嗣为子，甚爱之。老夫妇皆好静，子甚顽劣，另居一室。一日，过午不起，呼之不应，穴窗而窥，悬梁自尽矣。夫妇惊泣，呼邻里掇门入室，室中仅有土炕，无椅桌之类。自炕至所悬之梁，相距九尺余。童子何能跃而上？奇矣。邻人取梯解尸下，系四尺余裤带自缢者，而梁木围三尺，项上仅有尺余，何以先给扣而后入颈？则更奇矣。里胥报官，其伯拦验，未经通报。因案属奇异，官不得不往验尸身。脑后八字不交，确系自缢，并无他故。研鞠其子，生前为伯父母所钟爱，众证确凿。讯其家业，则织布营生，并无恒产，可知非谋财故杀。童子有何哀痛迫切，竟至忿激轻生耶？

    Explanation

    631

    A Child from a Poor Family

    There was a child from a poor family who was twelve years old. His parents died one after another due to an epidemic. His uncle and aunt, who were old and childless, adopted him as their son and loved him very much. The old couple enjoyed peace and quiet, but the child was very mischievous and unruly, so he lived separately in another room. One day, past noon, he did not get up, and when called, he did not respond. They peeped through a hole in the window and saw that he had hanged himself. The couple were shocked and wept, calling the neighbors to remove the door and enter the room. Inside, there was only a simple bed made of earth and no chairs or tables. The distance from the bed to the beam where he was hanging was more than nine feet. How could the child, being only twelve years old, jump up to hang himself? This was strange. The neighbors took a ladder to lower the body. The child had tied a belt more than four feet long around his neck, but the beam had a circumference of three feet, and the belt was only more than a foot long on his neck. How could it first be tied around his neck and then go into it? This was even more strange. The village official reported the incident to the authorities, and his uncle stopped to examine the body without reporting. Because the case was unusual, the official had to come to examine the body. The back of the neck showed an 'eight' shape without crossing, confirming that it was definitely self-hanging, with no other cause. The investigation revealed that the child had been loved by his adoptive parents before his death, and the evidence was clear. When inquired about his family's livelihood, it was found that he made a living by weaving and had no permanent property. It can be seen that it was not a murder for money. What sorrow or urgency could have led the child to commit suicide out of anger?

    632

    乐亭县有民妇赵杨氏者，年三十余。其夫在口外贸易，家称小康。无子，以姊女名银姑者为继女，年十三，同居过度。赵氏身患下疳，卧炕不起。呼其母杨王氏来作伴，年六十余矣。又有赵氏之姨甥女张王氏，年二十二岁，来视其姨母疾，亦止宿。一日担水者送水至，唤门不应。至晚仍不启，知有故。纠集邻里宗亲，肩门入视。则三妇一女俱自缢于房内，赵氏以绳系窗棂，拥被而坐缢于炕。杨氏以炕几竖立，仰墙而缢于几足。张氏与银姑以柳木椅移置中间，用一带二人分扣于颈，相背系于椅档上，如天平然，坐地而死。绳带俱不甚紧切，四人皆衣新衣，幼者涂粉画眉，簪花易履如将作客。

    Explanation

    632

    In the county of Yutian, there was a woman named Zhao Yangshi, who was in her thirties. Her husband was engaged in trade outside the borders, and they lived a moderately comfortable life. They had no son, so they adopted their sister's daughter, named Yin Gu, who was thirteen years old, and they lived together in a manner that was considered excessive. Zhao Yangshi suffered from a skin ulcer known as xiaguan and was bedridden. She called her mother, Yang Wangshi, who was over sixty years old, to keep her company. Additionally, Zhao Yangshi's cousin, Zhang Wangshi, who was twenty-two years old, came to visit her aunt, also sick, and stayed overnight. One day, a water carrier delivered water, but no one answered the door. By evening, the door was still not opened, and they knew something was wrong. Neighbors and relatives gathered, shouldered the door to enter, and found that the three women and one girl had all hanged themselves in the room. Zhao Yangshi had tied a rope to the window frame and hanged herself while sitting on the kang, wrapped in a blanket. Yang Wangshi stood a table upright, hanged herself against the wall by the legs of the table. Zhang Wangshi and Yin Gu moved a willow wood chair to the middle, tied a belt around both their necks, and hung themselves back to back on the chair's armrests, like a balance scale, sitting on the ground and dying. The ropes and belts were not very tight, and all four were wearing new clothes. The younger ones had applied powder and drawn eyebrows, wore flowers, and changed their shoes as if preparing to go out.

    633

    官验四尸，身俱无伤，则非杀；室无男迹，则非奸；一物不失，则非盗；从容妆饰，则非忿。四人何以同死，证见无人，何从究其故？赵氏之夫宗圣，杨氏之子锷，怀疑京控。提省会审。司谳者以赵杨氏因病难过自缢身死，其母杨王氏痛女，银姑痛母，张王氏亦痛其姨母死于非命，一时同缢。此揣度之词耳，虽刑部不驳，究非实在情由。予意前两案皆祟也。刑律无遇祟之条，不能声说，然兵部则例内，有兵丁遇祟自尽照病故例，一体赏恤之语。则刑律，虽无他例，不可援以为证耶？如他例不准用以断刑名，何以妇女羞忿自尽，准用礼例请旌耶？均为大清定例，司谳何甘心扭捏，而不敢比引耶？

    Explanation

    633

    The officials examined the four bodies, and since there were no injuries, it was not a case of murder; there were no signs of a man in the room, so it was not a case of adultery; nothing was missing, so it was not a case of theft; they were calmly dressed, so it was not a case of anger or conflict. How did the four die together? There were no witnesses, so how can the cause be investigated? Zhao's husband, Zong Sheng, and Yang's son, Keo, suspected and filed a lawsuit in the capital. The case was brought up for joint review. The judge ruled that Zhao and Yang died by suicide due to illness. Their mother, Yang Wang, Silver Girl, and Zhang Wang also mourned the death of their aunt, who died an unnatural death. They all hanged themselves at the same time. This is merely a guess, even though the Ministry of Punishment did not object, it was not the true cause. I believe the previous two cases were due to supernatural forces. There is no law in the penal code for dealing with supernatural forces, so it cannot be officially stated. However, the military regulations state that if a soldier dies by suicide due to supernatural forces, they should be treated as if they died of illness and receive the same rewards and condolences. Therefore, even though the penal code does not have other examples, can it not be used as evidence? If other examples are not allowed to be used to determine criminal cases, why is it permissible to use ritual examples to request commendation for women who commit suicide out of shame and anger? Both are established rules of the Great Qing, so why is the judge unwilling to mention it and不敢引用 it?

    634

    有六十九岁老妇，其夫瞽而耄。悦二十余岁之雇工人雄壮，以财物诱与成奸。为子妇所遇，勒毙之以灭口，以目缢报官。验实讯供确凿，而司谳者不信，驳提，致奸夫奸妇皆瘐毙。或曰：“男子八八而阳道衰，女子七七而天癸绝，皆无好色之心，医经可证。上司之驳，宜也。”予曰：“否，否，姑无论世有八十余岁之老翁得子，七十余岁之老妇嫁夫。请征诸古诗，称文王则百斯男，凯风之母七子。试思既有百男，则七八十岁尚应生子。有子七人且能咏歌规劝，谅皆成立。则母年可知，尤不能安于其室。若以常理论，是《诗经》亦不足信也，何必读书。”

    Explanation

    634

    A woman of sixty-nine years, whose husband was both blind and elderly, was attracted to a young hired worker who was strong and robust. She seduced him with material goods and they engaged in adultery. Her daughter-in-law discovered their affair and killed the man to silence him, reporting his death as a suicide by hanging to the authorities. The investigation confirmed the facts and the evidence was solid, but the official in charge doubted the case and ordered a retrial, resulting in the death of both the adulterer and the adulteress in prison. Some argued, "According to medical texts, men's sexual vigor declines at sixty-four and women's fertility ends at forty-nine, so they no longer have desires for sex. The higher authority's decision to doubt the case was appropriate." I replied, "No, no. Let us not consider that there are exceptions, such as an elderly man over eighty who still has children, or a woman in her seventies who remarries. Let us look to ancient poetry: the Book of Songs speaks of King Wen having a hundred sons and a mother with seven sons in the Kai Feng poem. If someone has had so many children, it is reasonable to believe they could still have more at an advanced age. If she has seven sons who can compose poetry and offer advice, they must be grown and capable. Therefore, she is unlikely to be content staying at home. If we apply common sense, the Book of Songs itself cannot be trusted, so why should we read it?"

    635

    有七十余岁老翁，爱邻女幼慧，保抱提携，胜于己出。父母知翁诚实，使女拜翁为义父，往来无间。其父母因奔亲戚之丧，女命犯忌不能偕往，呼邻翁代为掌家抚女，翁遂居于内室。对女年十岁，因父母不在，过邻舍听姊姒闲谈。皆青年妇女，聚论阴阳交合之事。其中有幼妇年十三，为养媳，与其夫苟合者。或问其始事何能忍受。妇曰：“夫初与交对，本不能成。夫云以纸作捻穿鼻，得嚏可立入。试之果然。后乐而失防，为姑所遇，得完姻也。”女闻而慕之，归与义父同榻，潜抚其阳具求合。翁辞曰：“汝年幼稚，决不能成事。”女曰：“我得方矣，邻妇教我以得嚏则入，乐不可支也。

    Explanation

    635

    There was an elderly man in his seventies who loved his neighbor's young and intelligent daughter, caring for her as if she were his own child, even better. The girl's parents knew the elderly man was honest and asked her to call him her adoptive father, and they had close and frequent interactions. The girl's parents were away due to a family funeral, and she was unable to go due to some taboo or restriction. They asked the elderly man to take care of the household and the girl, and he lived inside the house. When the girl was ten years old, her parents were away, and she went to her neighbor's house to listen to her sisters-in-law gossip. They were all young and married women, and they discussed sexual matters. One of them, a young woman who was thirteen years old and a daughter-in-law, was asked how she could tolerate the beginning of sexual relations. She said, "My husband initially could not penetrate me. He said to use a paper wick inserted through the nose to cause a sneeze, which would allow penetration. I tried it, and it worked. I enjoyed it so much that I lost my guard, and my mother-in-law discovered us, leading to a complete marriage." The girl heard this and was envious. She returned and slept with the elderly man in the same bed, secretly touching his penis and trying to have sexual relations. The elderly man responded, "You are too young to succeed." The girl said, "I have learned a method from a neighbor's wife that involves causing a sneeze to facilitate penetration, and I will be very happy."

    636

    ”翁久鳏，闻言心动，姑令横陈，以纸捻试之，一嚏而入其半。女疼极几毙，翁不敢讫事，慰之曰：“我知不可，汝必欲为此，受伤奈何？但汝父母归，断不可告也。”女唯唯。次日父母归，见女行彳亍，怪而验之，究得实。鸣诸官，依奸幼女者，虽和同强，律拟大辟。或曰：“此老究为人误，与实在光棍立意诱奸者大相径庭。有法挽回乎？”予曰：“强奸幼女已成者，斩。法无可贷。第当初何不辨作以指试探，因喊即止，依强奸十二岁以下幼女未成例，改发烟瘴充军。即年逾七十，不准收赎。庶情法两得其平。当先开导其父母，为此女留名节地步，将来择配，不至为人所弃，谅无不乐从也。”

    Explanation

    636

    The old man had been a bachelor for a long time. Upon hearing the news, his heart moved, and he ordered the girl to lie down, trying it with a paper wick. He sneezed and inserted half of himself, causing her extreme pain that nearly killed her. The man dared not finish the act, comforting her by saying, "I know it is wrong, but you insist on this. What will you do if you get hurt? But when your parents return, you must not tell them." The girl agreed. The next day, the parents returned and noticed the girl walking awkwardly, which made them suspicious. Upon investigation, they discovered the truth. They reported it to the authorities, who, according to the law for defiling a young girl, sentenced him to death, even if the act was consensual. Someone asked, "This old man was misled, and his actions are very different from a real rascal who deliberately seduces a girl. Is there any way to mitigate this?" I replied, "Rape of a young girl, once completed, is punishable by death. There is no leniency. However, if he had only used his finger to test and stopped upon her cry, it could be treated as an attempted rape of a girl under twelve years old, resulting in a milder punishment of being sent to a remote and unhealthy place as a military exile. Even if he is over seventy years old, redemption is not allowed. This way, both sentiment and law can be balanced fairly. One should first counsel the parents, preserving the girl's reputation and honor, so that she can find a suitable match in the future without being rejected. Surely they will be willing to follow."

    637

    转女为男二则
    　　
    　　吾乡史苕楣明经，壮年博学，功名未遂，改业钱款，为袁简斋明府所识拔，因入其幕。终任后在八闽为历任方伯所倚重。修脯丰厚，慷慨好施，济贫拔蹇，极一时之雄豪。坐上食客常满。及其老也，望孙念切。子妇怀孕，未卜男女，时有精歧黄之道不屑以医名者，苕楣延诊。其人曰：“脉主得女，然吾翁之善可回天意。请竭吾术，使转为男以报知己。第阳茎须移一肢改造，得男必缺一肢，翁愿之否？”苕楣诧曰：“先生之学素所敬佩，不意竟能化女作男。无已，请移其足指为之，无碍观瞻更妙。”其人曰：“是不能也，上可移下，下不能上。再三筹度，惟两手小指无用，可以挪借。”苕楣欣然诺谢。

    Explanation

    637

    A Tale of Transforming a Girl into a Boy

    In my hometown, there was a scholar named Shi Tao Mei, who was well-versed in the classics during his youth but failed to achieve fame and success. He later changed his career to deal with money and was recognized by Yuan Jianzhi, a high-ranking official, who then invited him to join his staff. After serving his term, Shi Tao Mei was highly trusted and relied upon by provincial governors in Fujian. He was generously paid, generous and charitable, helping the poor and assisting those in distress, and was considered a hero of his time. His home was always filled with guests. As he grew older, his longing for a grandson became intense. His wife was pregnant, but the gender of the child was unknown. At that time, there was a skilled practitioner of traditional Chinese medicine who did not seek recognition as a doctor. Shi Tao Mei invited him to diagnose the pregnancy. The practitioner said, "The pulse indicates a girl, but your virtue can change fate. Please allow me to use my skills to transform the fetus into a boy to repay your friendship. However, the penis must be moved and modified by one limb, and getting a boy will result in losing one limb. Would you agree?" Shi Tao Mei was surprised and said, "I have always admired your learning and did not expect you to be able to transform a girl into a boy. If there is no other way, please move the little fingers of the hands to do it. It would be even better if it does not affect appearance." The practitioner replied, "That is not possible. Limbs can be moved downward, but not upward. After careful consideration, only the little fingers of the hands are useless and can be used." Shi Tao Mei gladly agreed and thanked him.

    638

    遂设炉炼药，佩服兼行。及期果产男孩，手仅八指，见客腼腆，宛若闺阔中人，及长，羞涩更甚，有欲验其指者，大啼而藏匿，为同人所噱。昔苕楣与吾师之父王公交相善，方其归也，王谓之曰：“阁下与当世名公友而仅诸生，修资至巨万而仍寒士，依然故我，徒劳无功，窃为君不取也。”苕楣大笑曰：“予之出也，一肩行李，笔砚之外无他物，不数年出入公卿间，推贤让能。有为予引拔置身青云者，有赖予仰事俯育衣食充裕者，各若干人。畴不企史善人之号，亦可以自豪矣。足下欲予坐拥万镒，纳资为郎，方谓吐寒士之气耶？此守钱虏所为，卑卑者何足道？”其胸襟之阔达如是，宜其遇转女为男之奇士，此其中盖有天道也。

    Explanation

    638

    He then set up a furnace to refine medicine and took it simultaneously. By the due time, he actually gave birth to a boy who had only eight fingers. The boy was shy when meeting guests, as if he were a lady from the inner chamber. When he grew up, his shyness became even more pronounced. Those who wanted to check his fingers cried loudly and hid, and he was laughed at by his peers. In the past, Tao Mei was on good terms with my teacher's father, Mr. Wang. When he was returning, Mr. Wang told him, "You befriended prominent figures of the time but remained a student, accumulated wealth up to millions but still a poor scholar. You remained the same, and your efforts were in vain. I think it is not worth it for you." Tao Mei laughed and said, "When I went out, I had just one shoulder of luggage, nothing except for writing tools. In a few years, I moved among high officials, recommended talented people and yielded positions. Some were recommended by me to high positions, and some depended on me for their livelihood and were well-fed. Several people each. Who would not aspire to be called a good person in history, and can also be proud of it. Do you want me to sit with millions of cash, pay money to become an official, and call it getting rid of the poor scholar's air? This is what a money-grubber does, and such a lowly person is not worth mentioning." His broad-mindedness was like this, and it is appropriate for him to meet a strange man who can transform a woman into a man. There must be some heavenly principle involved.

    639

    姑苏有老翁富而无嗣，仅生一女，及笄病笃，医皆束手。翁不措重资，聘名医叶天士诊视，笑曰：“是非病也，肯以若女为我女，且从我游。百日后还阁下以壮健者，非复娇弱之态矣。如迟疑不决，是翁自杀之，死非正命，良可哀也。”翁诧曰：“诚如是，愿以千金送膝下。”天士携归，另洁密室，选婢之美而艳者使伴女宿，嘱曰：“此汝姑也，终身依倚在是，顺姑无违，稍有拂逆，致增其病，惟汝是问。”于是日给药饵，恒往瞷之，见女体渐壮，颜渐舒，与婢情好日密，形影相随。

    Explanation

    639

    In Suzhou, there was an old man who was wealthy but had no heir, having only one daughter. When she reached the age of 15, she fell seriously ill, and all the doctors were at a loss. The old man did not spend a lot of money but sought out the famous physician Ye Tianshi for a diagnosis. Ye Tianshi smiled and said, "This is not an illness. If you agree to let your daughter be my daughter and accompany me on my travels, I will return her to you in a healthy and robust state after a hundred days, no longer in a frail and delicate state. If you hesitate or refuse, it will be as if you are causing your own death, which would be a tragic end." The old man was surprised and said, "If it is truly as you say, I am willing to pay a thousand gold coins to you."

    Ye Tianshi took the girl back to his home, prepared a clean and private room, and chose a beautiful and attractive maid to accompany her. He instructed the maid, "This is your aunt, and you will depend on her for life. Obey her without disobedience. If there is any slight disobedience, it will worsen her condition, and I will hold you accountable." From then on, he provided medicine daily and frequently visited to check on her. He saw the girl's body gradually becoming stronger, her face gradually becoming more relaxed, and her relationship with the maid becoming increasingly close, to the point where they were inseparable.

    640

    知事已遂，遽入其室，迫喝婢曰：“汝与姑所作何事？我窥觇洞彻，必尽言之，如敢隐讳，将以刑求，毋自苦也。”婢视女而泣。女忸怩曰：“婢之伴我，翁之严命，如违应责，顺何罪耶？”婢因曰：“是主陷奴耳，以郎君伪称义姑，而使奴同衾枕，违既不敢，从又获咎，使奴置身何地？”天士大笑曰：“已顺从姑夫耶，方为汝喜，岂汝责耶？”速女改装，去发而辫之，以药展其弓足，衣冠履舄，居然美男子。延其父至，告曰：“阁下以子为女，伪疾诳我，误使义女伴之，今为所乱，将如之何？”翁愕然，不解所谓。乃使小夫妇出拜翁，顾而大乐，愿以婢为儿妇，与天士结为姻娅，往来无间。

    Explanation

    640

    Once the matter was resolved, he hastily entered his room and forcefully shouted at the maid, "What did you and my aunt do? I have observed everything clearly, so you must tell me everything. If you dare to hide anything, I will use punishment to extract the truth, and do not make yourself suffer unnecessarily." The maid looked at the woman and cried. The woman, embarrassed, said, "The maid accompanied me because of my husband's strict order. If I disobey, I should be punished, but what fault is there in obeying?" The maid then said, "This master has trapped me. He pretended to be an aunt and made me share bedding and pillows with him. If I disobey, I dare not, and if I obey, I suffer blame. Where should I place myself?" Tian Shi laughed loudly and said, "Have you already obeyed my husband? Only then will I be happy for you. How can you be blamed?" He quickly instructed the woman to change her clothes, remove her hair, and braid it. He used medicine to stretch her bound feet and dressed her in clothes, hats, shoes, and sandals, making her appear as a beautiful man. He invited her father and said, "You treated your son as a daughter, pretended to be ill, and deceived me. You mistakenly made the adopted daughter accompany him. Now, he has caused chaos. What should we do?" The father-in-law was startled and did not understand what was meant. Tian Shi then made the young couple come out and bow to the father-in-law, who was greatly pleased. He wished to have the maid as his daughter-in-law and formed an alliance with Tian Shi, maintaining close relations.

    641

    芗厈曰：变女为男之法见于《医经》，史以盛德而遇良医，理所应得，无足怪者。惟叶所治之女，其《医经》所载之五不男耶，名曰天、捷、妒、变、半。任冲不盛，宗筋不成，曰天。值男即女，值女即男，曰捷。男根不满，似有似无，曰妒。半月能男，半月能女，曰变。虽有男根，不能交媾，曰半。此五等人状貌血气本具男形，惟任冲二脉不足，似男而不成其为男，为父母者误认作女，年至十六，气足神旺，阳事兴矣，郁不得发，是以病笃。幸遇名医，充以妙药，诱以所欲，自然阳茎突出不复女矣。吾意五不男中除天阉外，皆可以药救也，故见于《医经》。奈世鲜精其技者，叶天士医学名家，固其宜也。而史公所遇之人，其名不亚于叶，名逸不传，惜哉！由是推之，天下之抱绝技而隐没者，不知凡几，岂仅医学为然哉。

    Explanation

    641

    Xiang Yan said: The method of transforming a woman into a man is described in the Medical Canon. The historian encountered a skilled physician due to his great virtue, which is what one should expect, nothing to be surprised about. The woman treated by Ye Tianshi, was she one of the five types of "half-men" described in the Medical Canon, known as Heaven, Swift, Jealous, Change, and Half? Those with insufficient energy in the Conception and Thrusting Vessels and underdeveloped genitals are called Heaven. Those who change gender based on the sex of the partner are called Swift. Those with an underdeveloped or ambiguous penis are called Jealous. Those who alternate between male and female characteristics every half month are called Change. Those who have a penis but are unable to engage in sexual intercourse are called Half. These five types appear male in form and blood, but lack sufficient energy in the Conception and Thrusting Vessels, making them not fully male. Parents mistakenly identify them as girls, but by age 16, their energy and vitality increase, leading to the development of male characteristics, which causes frustration and illness. Fortunately, they encountered a skilled physician who used effective medicine and persuasion, resulting in the development of male characteristics. I believe that all five types of "half-men" except for Heaven (true impotence) can be treated with medicine, which is why it is recorded in the Medical Canon. However, few people in the world are skilled in this technique, but Ye Tianshi, a renowned medical expert, is an exception. The name of the person the historian encountered is not as well-known as Ye Tianshi's, and their identity is lost to history. From this, we can infer that there are many people with extraordinary skills who remain unknown, not just in medicine.

    642

    程翁
    　　
    　　江西程翁世以营运起家，富至百万。年四十无子，喟然叹曰：“吾闻君子之泽，五世而斩。自吾高祖创业，今其时矣，若不广修德业，理将斩绝。与其遗产业于不知谁何之人，何如散与乡里之为得也。”修桥铺路，施药送棺，凡遇善事勇往争先，善士之名日著。时前明遗逆未净。军饷甚急，又值荐饥，赋无所出。有司不敢请蠲，追呼尤迫，民间卖子鬻妻，不敷完纳。翁虽目击心伤，不过施予邻里乡党，焉能博济，日夜殷忧而已。当是时，县宰仁慈，催科政拙，上司委员守提钱粮十万，予限半月，县官不忍敲扑，惟思自尽。或请与程善人谋，当有以济。官无奈，具盛馔延翁与商，翁慨然曰：“十万金无难，只须遍示居民，免本年正赋，生当如数代纳。”遂发帖谕免，鸠形鹄面者尽释，即以翁银上解。宪司以为能，擢县令为司马。翁大悦，谓得遂所愿矣。后连举三子，俱以科甲官至督抚。翁寿九十余，膺一品封而卒。

    Explanation

    642

    程翁

    　　The elder man from Jiangxi, whose family had risen to wealth through business, became a millionaire. At the age of forty, he had no son, and he sighed deeply, saying, "I have heard that the virtue of a gentleman's influence diminishes after five generations. Since my great-grandfather founded our fortune, this is the time for it to come to an end. If we do not widely cultivate virtue and career, it will surely be cut off. It is better to leave our fortune to unknown people than to scatter it among our fellow villagers." He built bridges, laid roads, distributed medicine, and provided burial services. Whenever he encountered good deeds, he led the way, and his reputation as a virtuous person grew daily. At that time, remnants of the Ming Dynasty were still active, and the military supply was urgent, compounded by widespread famine and the inability to collect taxes. The officials dared not request exemptions, and the demands were particularly pressing, leading to the sale of wives and children in the countryside. Despite witnessing and feeling heartbroken, the elder man could only help his neighbors and fellow villagers; he could not provide widespread relief, and he was deeply worried day and night. At that time, the county magistrate was kind but ineffective in tax collection. The superior authorities sent a commissioner to collect ten million in taxes, with a deadline of half a month. The county official, unwilling to resort to harsh measures, only thought of taking his own life. Someone suggested consulting the virtuous Mr. Cheng, who might be able to help. The official, having no other choice, prepared a lavish meal to invite the elder man to discuss the matter. The elder man said resolutely, "Ten million in gold is not difficult. I just need to inform all the residents to exempt the current year's taxes, and I will pay them as usual." He issued a notice to exempt the taxes, and those who were starving and emaciated were all relieved. The taxes were then paid with the elder man's money. The commissioner praised his ability and promoted the county magistrate to the position of司马 (a high-ranking official). The elder man was very pleased, thinking that his wish had been fulfilled. Later, he had three sons, all of whom passed the imperial examinations and rose to the position of督抚 (high-ranking officials). The elder man lived to be over ninety and received a first-rank posthumous title before he died.

    643

    芗厈曰：圣贤仙佛，俎豆千秋，以及富贵寿考，皆人之所欲也，是不难。自考功业能如周程张朱乎？即入圣庙，能如释迦老君乎？即登天界，能如封翁之居心利济乎？即福寿并臻，苟自问—无所可，徒羡人之显荣，岂非空过一世？孟子曰：人皆可以为尧舜，何不为也？予故广搜诸封翁作善降祥之事，以为劝。
    　　
    　　马氏
    　　
    　　吾乡居海滨，有时秋潮汛发，漫过石塘，居民顿遭陷溺。马氏者，家有八旬老病父母，一妻二子，务农为活。是年，秋汛甚大，人皆凛凛。一夜，澎湃之声将近，邻里悲号。马氏夫妇议弃二子，各负父母奔高阜，及归，屋与子俱无存，悲不自胜。此年孪生二子，面目与前无异，触动悲思。二子曰：“儿等又来，且为司命易富骨，何伤也？”闻初生儿发言，莫不骇然，后问之不能答矣。及长，果善经营，致巨富。

    Explanation

    643

    Xiang Yan said: Sages, virtuous men, immortals, and Buddhas, honored for eternity, as well as wealth, longevity, and prosperity, are all things that people desire, and achieving them is not difficult. However, can one's achievements rival those of Zhou, Cheng, Zhang, and Zhu? Can one enter the temple of sages as revered as Sakyamuni and Laozi? Can one ascend to heaven as beneficially as the esteemed old gentleman? Can one achieve wealth and longevity, yet when self-reflecting, find nothing to be proud of, and merely envy others' prominence and honor, is this not a wasted life? Mencius said: All people can become Yao and Shun, why not? Therefore, I broadly search for stories of the esteemed old gentlemen who performed good deeds and brought blessings, to encourage others.

    Ma Shi

    My hometown is located by the sea. Sometimes during the autumn tide, the sea level rises and overflows the stone embankment, causing residents to be suddenly submerged. The Ma family had an elderly couple in their eighties who were suffering from illness, a wife, and two sons who were engaged in farming for a living. That year, the autumn tide was particularly large, and everyone was frightened. One night, the sound of waves crashing was approaching, and the neighbors were wailing in fear. The Ma family couple discussed abandoning their two sons and each carried their elderly parents to a high ground. Upon returning, their house and sons were both gone, and they were overwhelmed with grief. That year, twins were born, looking no different from before, which triggered their sorrow. The two sons said, "We have come again, and we will be given the fortunate bone by the deity of fate, what harm is there?" Hearing the newborn twins speak, everyone was startled, and later, when asked, they could not answer. As they grew up, they indeed became skilled at managing and became very wealthy.

    644

    土神显应
    　　
    　　昔史苕楣在闽，济拔单寒，人皆仰之。有王姓者，一事无成，郁郁失志，遂投史公。奈王去而史已归，落魄闽垣，寓土神祠内。已无生机，幸乡人集腋，助洋银六圆，藉为用度。王欲归不敷，欲留不得，进退维谷，踌躇神殿间。忽见衣冠来者，以一单呈神前，焚香致祝而祈签语。王初不以为意，后见其频来，迎谓之曰：“再三渎，渎则不告，何吾子之不惮烦也。”其人笑曰：“先生异乡人，不知吾乡风俗，所持者名花单，有三十二数目。开花会者所传，藏一款于匣中，使人以银钱猜。压中则获二十九倍之利，不中连本去矣。

    Explanation

    644

    The Earth God's Manifestation

    In the past, Mr. Shi Tiaomei was in Fujian, where he rescued the poor and needy, and people all looked up to him. There was a man surnamed Wang who achieved nothing, feeling depressed and disheartened, he then turned to Mr. Shi. Unfortunately, Wang had left and Mr. Shi had returned, Wang was destitute in Fujian, staying in the temple of the earth god. Having no hope, fortunately, the fellow townspeople pooled their resources, helping him with six foreign silver dollars to use as living expenses. Wanting to return home but lacking the money, wanting to stay but unable to, being in a difficult situation, hesitating in the temple. Suddenly, he saw a well-dressed person come, presenting a lottery ticket before the god, burning incense and praying for a sign. Wang initially did not pay attention, but later seeing him come frequently, greeted him and said, "Repeatedly offending, offending leads to no response, why are you so persistent?" The person smiled and said, "Sir, you are a stranger here, not knowing our local customs, what you hold is called a lottery ticket, with thirty-two numbers. Passed down by those who organize the gambling game, hiding one number in a box, letting people guess with silver coins. If you hit, you gain twenty-nine times the stake, if not, the stake is lost."

    645

    夫宝仅四门，尚难猜度，今三十二门，何从意计？是以卜于土神，求得签语，有一字与单内同者压之，每有显应，故恒卜之。”王曰：“局在何处，以若干为本？”其人曰：“局设人迹罕到之区，其本以千万计。自一文钱至百千，不论彩之多寡，按数以酬，从无错误。”王曰：“局既隐藏，能无诳耶？”其人曰：“事属犯禁，不得不密，然设局不止一家，而压者男妇以亿计。稍有诈伪，则不能兴矣。何以数十年如一日？”王曰：“既难到局，焉知开示之得失耶？”其人曰：“市有肩布而货者，手执一竿，悬五色布缕若旗者，即会中送单接彩人也。虽万金可托，按门书条与之。开日即来报信，中则本利全交，不中亦得回报。闺阁中寄压者甚众。”

    Explanation

    645

    In ancient times, the treasure had only four doors, and it was still difficult to guess or predict the outcome. Now, with thirty-two doors, how can one guess or plan? Therefore, they divined with the earth deity and obtained an oracle slip. If there is a character that matches the one in the slip, it is suppressed. Each time there is a clear response, it is always divined. The king asked, "Where is the game located, and what is the base amount?" The person replied, "The game is set up in a place rarely visited by people, and the base amount is in the tens of thousands. Bets range from one coin to ten thousand, regardless of the quantity of the prize, and are paid according to the amount, with no errors." The king said, "If the game is hidden, how can there be no deception?" The person replied, "The matter belongs to a violation of law, and it must be kept secret. However, the game is not set up by just one family, and those who suppress or control it are in the tens of millions. Slight deception would not allow it to flourish. Why has it remained stable for several decades?" The king asked, "If it is difficult to reach the game, how can one know the gains and losses of the outcome?" The person replied, "In the market, there are those who carry shoulder cloths and sell, holding a pole with five-colored threads hanging like a flag. These are the people in the meeting who send the slips and receive the prizes. Even ten thousand gold coins can be entrusted, and a note is written according to the door and given to them. On the day of opening, they come to report. If winning, the principal and interest are fully handed over; if losing, reimbursement is also obtained. Women in the women's quarters who entrust their suppression or control are very many."

    646

    王闻而艳羡，即觅得布客花单，于土神前亦祈一签，有“寿山福海永绵绵”句。核单中竟有“寿山”二字，王以洋银一元，书条交去。不数日，客送洋银三十，另以一单至。王大乐，又卜得字同者以二十圆压之。未几客送六百圆来。王思事属偶然，断难屡中，沉迷其间，反丧厥本。遂留百圆作归计，祷于神曰：“事不过三，弟子欲以五百洋银为本，若再中，愿新庙貌，且施榇百具。”因庙中有施棺会，故计及此。签得“许君一著劝仁人，是后无烦再渎神”语。是日，单集四书内有“仁者人也”句，王竟以五百圆，书条封交。布客一去弥月，王盼望切，见客率十余人，肩洋银万五千来。王重酬之，乃践修庙施棺之愿，拥厚资而归。

    Explanation

    646

    The king, upon hearing of it, admired and coveted the outcome. He promptly sought out a fortune-teller and obtained a divination slip, which he drew before the earth god. The slip bore the phrase, "May longevity and happiness last forever." Upon checking the slip, he found the words "Shou Shan" (寿山), and he wagered one foreign silver dollar, writing a note and handing it over. Within a few days, the fortune-teller returned with thirty foreign silver dollars and another slip. The king was greatly pleased and, drawing another slip with the same characters, wagered twenty more foreign silver dollars. Soon after, the fortune-teller brought six hundred foreign silver dollars. The king thought the matter was purely coincidental and unlikely to continue, but he became so engrossed that he lost his initial stake. He then set aside one hundred foreign silver dollars as a reserve, and prayed to the god, saying, "Things don't go right more than three times. I wish to use five hundred foreign silver dollars as capital. If I win again, I will renovate the temple and donate a hundred coffins." Since there was a coffin donation society in the temple, he made this vow. The slip he drew this time read, "One move is enough to advise a benevolent person, no need to trouble the gods again." On that day, the slip he drew from the Four Books contained the phrase, "A benevolent person is a human being." The king wagered five hundred foreign silver dollars, wrote a note, sealed it, and handed it over. The fortune-teller left, and the king anxiously awaited his return. Soon, the fortune-teller arrived with over ten people, carrying fifteen thousand foreign silver dollars. The king generously rewarded him and fulfilled his vow to renovate the temple and donate coffins, returning home with a substantial fortune.

    647

    芗厈曰：能如是乎，人人可以舍业赴闽中花会去矣。何以寓是省者，往往输至一无所有，流为饿殍而后已。可见王君存心本善，土神亦偶示其灵耳。昔人有词云：“跋阘一声鸣，掉头来五万金。忙将三万来行运，三千赠人，三千造坟，四千小小园林景。余万金，名花美酒，到处品佳人。”是因财而造孽矣。何如施衣舍食，到处济贫人乎。
    　　
    　　拐带
    　　
    　　凡为父母者，顾复为难，是以《诗》有“昊天罔极”之歌。彼富贵之家，出入相随，尚恃婢仆。中人以下，其父或顾经营，或勤读书，保抱提携惟仗一母。有子数人，往往顾此失彼，是以拐带之害兴也。

    Explanation

    647

    Xiang Yan said: Can one be like this? Everyone can abandon their business and go to the flower fair in Fujian. Why do those who stay in this province often lose everything, becoming starving corpses and stopping there. It can be seen that Mr. Wang's intentions are originally good, and the earth god occasionally shows his divinity. An ancient person said in a poem: "A sudden sound of a horse, turning around to bring five thousand gold. Quickly using three thousand to run the business, giving three thousand to others, three thousand to build a tomb, and four thousand for a small garden. The remaining ten thousand, famous flowers and fine wine, everywhere tasting beautiful women." This is creating misfortune because of wealth. How about giving clothes and food, helping the poor everywhere?

    Kidnapping

    For all parents, taking care is difficult, so the Book of Songs has the song "The boundless sky." Those in wealthy families, going out and coming in together, still rely on servants. For those of moderate means, the father may be busy with business or diligent in reading, and the mother is the only one who holds and carries the children. With several children, they often look after this and miss that, which is why the harm of kidnapping arises.

    648

    乾隆初年，有浙江乌程县人富子文，及妻富沈氏，子富大者，合陈大、俞九龄、鲍二、谢世荣、富大金，每年五月五日，共驾冒头小艇，远拐幼孩。赴太湖杀之，以祭邪神。于六月初一日，富子文南向跪拜，书符咒水以洒孩童，着体即迷。曾于乾隆五年，富子文偕陈大、俞九龄、富大金，拐得幼孩赴太湖，杀以祭神，共食其肉。以骨煅炼为丸，可架大刑，可去私胎，可治劳瘵，获利甚厚。十年三月，在江南娄县迷拐九岁幼女卜三姑，陈大缚其足，富大以剪去其趾，火煅铁针插入，使其母富沈氏煮石灰水浸令肿烂，作为废疾。四月初一日，舟至南浔，迷抱幼孩，当即格毙，食肉炼骨。五月十三日，见邻近幼孩蒋二，迷拐入船，亦以火煅铁针插入足踝，以断其筋，旋将盲目之药，令卜三姑，蒋二同饮，即成瞽废，转卖丐船行乞。七年四月，在苏州齐门外迷得无名幼童，至吴江北栅，恶其叫号，杀而食之，并粉其骨。

    Explanation

    648

    During the early years of the Qianlong reign, there was a man named Fu Ziwén from Wucheng County in Zhejiang Province, along with his wife Fu Shén, their son Fu Dà, and accomplices Chen Dà, Yu Jiúlín, Bǎo Èr, Xie Shìróng, and Fu Dàjīn. Every May 5th, they would travel in small boats to kidnap young children. They would take the children to Lake Taihu, kill them, and sacrifice them to a demon god. On June 1st, Fu Ziwén would kneel facing south, write charms, and sprinkle water on the children, causing them to become dazed upon contact. In the fifth year of the Qianlong reign, Fu Ziwén, accompanied by Chen Dà, Yu Jiúlín, and Fu Dàjīn, kidnapped a child, took it to Lake Taihu, killed it, sacrificed it, and ate its meat. They boiled the bones into pills, which were claimed to be able to withstand severe punishment, remove unwanted pregnancies, treat chronic illness, and bring significant profit. In March of the tenth year, they kidnapped a nine-year-old girl named Bu Sangu from Lucheng County in southern Jiangnan. Chen Dà tied her feet, and Fu Dà cut off her toes, inserting a heated iron needle. Her mother, Fu Shén, boiled slaked lime water to make her feet swell and rot, rendering her crippled. On April 1st, they arrived in Nanxun, kidnapped a young child, killed it, and consumed its meat and bones. On May 13th, they saw a nearby child named Jiang Er, kidnapped him, mutilated his foot, and cut his tendons. They then gave them blindness medicine to drink, making them blind and crippled, and sold them to beg on beggar ships. In April of the seventh year, they kidnapped an unnamed child in Suzhou, killed it, and consumed its meat and ground its bones.

    649

    又历年，叠拐男女幼童不计其数，俊者卖之远方，蠢者杀食其肉，灸骨为丸。与其党鲍陆氏伪以行医算命为名，曾卖孩骨丸与濮院之尼，乍浦之妇，以去私孕。经嘉善县陈令以刚破获其案，供证凿凿。听审时，衿民公忿，有杨秀章、姚泰晋、赵凝周，将富大共殴至死。其父富子文在监惊毙。有司虽照采生折割律，挫尸枭示，但元恶富子文父子未经生受寸磔，明正典刑而伸童蒙之怨气，惜哉！其余伙党皆依为从律斩决，即恶妇富、鲍二氏亦同正法。惟据各供，江浙间冒头船只一百七十余号之多，后仅破其徒黄殿侯诱拐年甫入龄之孙大宝出卖，又顾景文活煮徐姓弥月之孩为药，又蒋运生迷拐幼童汪田业，刀截其足以行丐，及孙文韬、丁昌成、汤开元、孙彦生、王胥氏、蔡天章、王俊生辈，男妇十数，俱系驾冒头船之人，或用死孩合药，或随从贷卖，均按律治以应得之罪。奈实繁有徒，冒头船仅破十分之一，流毒尚未已也。有子者可不恐惧保护哉。

    Explanation

    649

    After many years, countless children were kidnapped, with the attractive ones sold to distant lands and the less desirable ones killed, their flesh eaten, and their bones roasted into pills. The criminals, led by Bao Lushi, pretended to be doctors or fortune-tellers, once selling child bone pills to a nun in Puyuan and a woman in Zhapu to terminate unwanted pregnancies. The case was uncovered by Magistrate Chen Yigang of Jiasan County, and the evidence was solid. When the case was heard, the officials and common people were collectively furious. Yang Xiuzhang, Yao Taijin, and Zhao Ningzhou together beat Fu Da to death. His father, Fu Ziwen, died of shock in prison. Although the authorities punished the criminals according to the law against kidnapping and mutilation, mutilating and displaying their bodies publicly, the main culprits, Fu Ziwen and his father, did not receive the full punishment of dismemberment while alive, and the law was not fully enforced to appease the resentment of the innocent children. Alas! The rest of the accomplices were executed according to the law for being accomplices, and the wicked women Fu and Bao Er were also executed according to the law. According to the testimonies, over 170 boats were involved in the area of Jiangsu and Zhejiang, but only one of their followers, Huang Dianhou, was caught for luring and selling a boy named Sun Dabao who had just turned a year old. Gu Jingwen boiled a newborn child of the Xu family alive to make medicine, and Jiang Yunsong lured and abducted a young child named Wang Tianye, cutting off his feet to make him beg. Sun Wentao, Ding Changcheng, Tang Kaixuan, Sun Yansheng, Wang Xushi, Cai Tianzhang, and Wang Junsheng, a dozen men and women, were all involved in operating the boats. Some used dead children to make medicine, while others followed along to sell them. All were punished according to the law for their crimes. However, there are many more like them, and only one-tenth of the boats were broken up, and the evil influence is not yet over. Those with children should not fear but protect them.

    650

    芗厈曰：曾闻明有异人在闯贼营行医，以人脑骨为膏，立起金疮之危笃者。闯神之，使军中叠千百几案，架台数十丈之高，令射手关弓迫之，驱之使登极颠，环拜而封之曰老神仙。其人受封而下，惊悸死。乱世有之，不意光天化日之下，亦有以人为粮，以骨为丸者，地方聚此妖孽，失察之咎奚辞。然亦由童之父母，疏于防范故耳。
    　　
    　　爵大元戎
    　　
    　　大元戎某公者，生而壮健，习为走跳，数仞之墙，—跃而上。其在浙时，尝失业，入富室行窃，为火枪所伤，堕卧寡妇某氏院中。妇奇其貌曰：“若个好男儿，何不入营为伍，而甘心为匪耶？”公感泣曰：“某异乡人，落魄于此，无力回籍，图腰缠耳，岂情愿哉？故有五不偷之约，孤寡一也，老疾二也，事忙三也，中等之家四也，失于防范五也。”妇韪其言，赠百金。公归至黔中，费尽，又因窃被获，发县尉宋君笞责，递回原籍。

    Explanation

    650

    Xiang Yan said: I have heard that during the Ming dynasty, there was a mysterious healer in the camp of Li Zicheng's rebels who used human skull bones as medicine to instantly revive those with severe and critical injuries. Li Zicheng was impressed by this and had thousands of tables stacked up, building a platform dozens of feet high. He ordered archers to draw their bows and force the healer to climb to the top of the platform, where they surrounded him and hailed him as an old immortal. The healer, honored and then descending, died from shock. Such things happen in times of chaos, but it is unexpected that even in broad daylight, there are those who use humans as food and bones as medicine. The local authorities gathered this evil, and their failure to detect it is inexcusable. However, it is also due to the negligence of the child's parents in failing to protect him.

    The Great General

    The Great General, a certain official, was born strong and healthy, skilled in jumping and running, able to leap over walls several feet high in a single bound. When he was in Zhejiang, he once lost his job and entered a wealthy household to steal, but was injured by gunfire and fell into the courtyard of a widow named Ms. Zhang. The widow found his appearance remarkable and said, "What a fine young man! Why not join the army and serve as a soldier, rather than willingly becoming a bandit?" The official wept and replied, "I am a stranger in a strange land, destitute here, unable to return home, and seeking only to make a living. How could I willingly do such a thing? Therefore, I have a rule of not stealing from five groups: orphans and widows, the elderly and sick, those who are busy, middle-class families, and those who are careless in their precautions." The widow approved of his words and gave him a hundred gold pieces. He returned to Guizhou, spent the money, and was caught stealing again. He was punished by County Magistrate Song Jun with corporal punishment and sent back to his hometown.

    651

    时齐二寡妇以谋逆败，困守山中。其山三面陡峭壁立，无他径可上，一面稍坦，严设三关，守以千军，有百二之固。官弁无策可施，任受督责而已。有县令刘清者，多心计，思攻齐后寨，使善捕致诸偷考其技，公为最。余三四人亦能登高，必需人接其力。清携以见帅曰：“请疾攻前关，不遗余力，清愿率壮健偷劫其寨后。”帅许之。清乃使公首登，得老树息足，以绳悬四人上踞树间。公三登至颠，引四人，先盗贼衣，改装一色，俾难辨别，共入后寨粮草所纵火，大呼曰：“官兵从后入矣。”贼皆顾后，五人开关，将士俱登，齐二寡妇不能拒，滚崖死。公以军功得官，使以千金报杭妇德。妇因嫁之。

    Explanation

    651

    At the time when the two widows of Qi were defeated for plotting rebellion, they were trapped and defending themselves in the mountains. The mountain was steep and sheer on three sides, with no other way up, while one side was slightly flat, where they had set up three strict checkpoints, guarded by a thousand soldiers, making it as secure as a city with a hundred defenses. The officials had no strategy to employ, and were only left to be reprimanded.

    There was a county magistrate named Liu Qing who was cunning and thought of attacking the rear camp of Qi, having skilled hunters test the thieves' abilities, and he was the best among them. The other two or three people could also climb high, but needed someone to assist them. Liu Qing took him to see the general and said, "Please attack the front checkpoint quickly, with all your might, and I will lead the strong and healthy to raid the rear camp." The general agreed to it.

    Liu Qing then made the thief climb first, find an old tree to rest his feet, and use a rope to pull the other four up to sit among the branches. The thief climbed three times to the top, led the four, changed into the thieves' clothes, making them indistinguishable, and together they entered the rear camp's grain storage and set it on fire, shouting, "The soldiers are coming from behind!" The thieves all looked behind, the five opened the gate, and the soldiers all climbed up, the two widows of Qi could not resist, and fell off the cliff to die.

    The thief gained an official position through military merit and made a thousand gold coins to repay the virtue of the Hang woman. The woman then married him.

    652

    后累功至总戎，拜爵五等，为大将军。率兵出云贵间，宋尉尚在任，伏迎不敢仰视，九顿首谢过。公笑曰：“尉何前倨而后卑也。各尽其职，毋恐。”某制军以公出身微贱，渺视之。公曰：“始为盗而终为官，由能报效朝廷。第恐显为官而隐为盗，何以抚绥百姓？”讲道学者，至今诵其言为确凿不移。
    　　
    　　某富翁
    　　
    　　明季夭崇间，阉宦用事，士民解体，盗贼蜂起蚁屯，水乡益甚。吾浙嘉、湖二郡间，有大水墩为巨寇窃据，四出摽掠，得千万金。欲图大举，埋金于墩，号召匪徒，同谋不宄。入我朝，为大军扑灭，除其巢穴，仅存荒墩，为丐舟停泊处。某翁于兵燹后失业，身患疯癫，与妻子以一破舟行乞。

    Explanation

    652

    After accumulating merit, he rose to the rank of general, was bestowed with a noble title of the fifth rank, and became a Grand General. Leading troops through the Yunnan and Guizhou regions, when the Prefect Song was still in office, he knelt to greet him and dared not look up, apologizing nine times with prostrations. The general smiled and said, "Why were you so arrogant before and so humble now? Each perform your duties and do not fear." A certain military governor looked down on him because of his humble origins. The general replied, "I began as a thief but ended up as an official, because I could serve the court. However, I fear that if I appear as an official but secretly act as a thief, how can I pacify and comfort the common people?" Scholars of Daoism still recite his words as solid and unchangeable to this day.

    A Certain Wealthy Man

    During the late Ming period under Emperor Chongzhen, eunuchs were in power, and scholars and common people lost cohesion. Bandits rose up and gathered in large numbers, especially in flood-prone areas. In the Zhejiang region between the Jia and Hu prefectures, a large water-damaged mound was seized by a notorious bandit, who raided and plundered all over, amassing millions in gold. Intending to launch a major attack, he buried the gold in the mound, called upon bandits, and plotted together without causing trouble. When we came to power, the army put them down, eliminated their base, and only a desolate mound remained, serving as an anchorage for begging boats. A certain old man became unemployed after the war, suffered from madness, and rowed a broken boat with his wife and children to beg.

    653

    夏夜，翁独赴墩上乘凉，土忽坟起，异而拨之，赫然朱提现。归于妻子谍，筑室墩之正中，旋加增广，四面起垣，发得银无数。患宵小觊觎，乃于墙外起群室，使同丐者居之，授以田，俾业佃，而环卫有人矣。翁乃立条教：一，子孙延明师读书明理，虽得科甲，不许出仕；一，永远不许分家。公举谨厚者为家长，一切听其主裁；一，不许衣绫罗，惟有事应酬，及出官者得衣之；一，祭祀惟虔，宴客惟诚，菜不过八簋。平日不许私藏饮食。男女分班，每日两餐。在内外公所聚食。三八日用猪肉，余日惟菜；一，子孙不肖，小者听家长训责，大过逐出，不许容留；一，亲族婚丧，官司劝赈，及施棺药棉衣诸善事争先襄助，毋吝惜。数百年聚族而居，规矩整肃。浙之数富室者至今称首云。

    Explanation

    653

    On a summer night, an old man went to a mound to cool off. Suddenly, the earth rose up, and finding it unusual, he dug it up and discovered a bright red treasure. Returning to his wife and children, he built a house in the center of the mound, then expanded it and surrounded it with walls, uncovering countless silver pieces. Worried that thieves might covet the treasure, he built additional rooms outside the wall and assigned them to fellow beggars, giving them land to farm, thus providing security around the area. The old man then established rules: First, descendants should invite learned teachers to study and understand principles; even if they pass the imperial examinations, they are not allowed to serve in government. Second, the family shall never be divided. Third, someone cautious and honest shall be elected as the head of the family, and all matters shall be decided by him. Fourth, they are not allowed to wear fine silk, except for formal occasions or those who have served in government. Fifth, offerings must be sincere, guests must be treated with sincerity, and there should not be more than eight dishes. Sixth, not allowed to secretly store food and drink. Seventh, men and women eat separately, and meals are taken twice a day. Eighth, gather to eat at public places inside and outside the family compound. Ninth, on the third and eighth days of the month, pork is used, otherwise, only vegetables. Tenth, if descendants are unworthy, minor offenses are handled by the head of the family, but serious offenses result in expulsion. Eleventh, in matters of family weddings and funerals, government relief efforts, and charitable acts like providing coffins, medicine, and clothing, one should take the lead and not be stingy. For several hundred years, the family lived together under strict rules. Among the wealthy families in Zhejiang, this family is still highly regarded today.

    654

    芗厈曰：盗也而爵居专阃，丐也而富甲一省，直驾陶朱、淮阴之上。忆吴之甘宁，起于锦帆贼；唐之李勣，起于亡赖贼，皆名将也。史未尝以其出身微贱而贬之。至某翁之立条教，大设施，何等胸襟。天之富贵，惟与善人。奈何以非盗非丐之身，大有可为而不为，吾未如之何也已矣。

    Explanation

    654

    Xiang Yan said: A thief who holds a high military rank, a beggar who is the wealthiest in the province, surpassing even Tao Zhu and Han Xin. Recalling Gan Ning of Wu, who rose from a pirate; Li Ji of Tang, who rose from a ruffian—both were renowned generals. Historians have never belittled them because of their humble origins. As for the elder's establishment of regulations and great achievements, what a noble heart he possessed. Heaven's wealth and honor are bestowed only on good people. How can someone who is neither a thief nor a beggar, with so much potential, fail to act? I am at a loss as to what to do about it.

    655

    百岁老人
    　　
    　　乾隆间，纯皇帝八十万寿，开千叟宴，诏天下督抚，举八十以上老人能远行者，无论缙绅士庶，邮送来京与宴。惟释道只问如何延年，不与焉。蜀有峨嵋山僧，年一百四十余岁，独居静室，不与世接见。人亦不言不笑，有司往问之，以偈对曰：“人生七十古来稀，剩有僧人历更稀。若问延年何法术，一生淡泊养天机。”此僧寿算已为世所罕见，乃更有大年者。
    　　
    　　时千叟寿数各已册报皇帝，欲得一百龄外士人，堪庸爵赏者冠其班。浙抚所举，天台岁贡生齐世南，年一百六十一岁，曾铨授教职，不赴，名登部籍，确实可稽。惟适子亲孙俱殁，只有曾玄十余，择诸生二人扶掖，进京召见。世南奏对称旨，赐国子监祭酒。命莅任三日，以作实授。宴期，朝衣朝冠，赐坐陛下，为千叟领班。庞眉皓首，舞蹈彤廷，举觥下寿，天颜大悦。礼成，睦辞日锡之御制诗文、几杖、金帛有差。且命缓缓乘传回籍。诚熙朝人瑞也。

    Explanation

    655

    A Hundred-Year-Old Elder

    During the reign of Emperor Qianlong, on the occasion of the emperor's eightieth birthday, a Thousand Elders Banquet was held. The emperor decreed that provincial governors and military commanders should recommend elderly people aged eighty or older who were capable of traveling long distances, regardless of whether they were officials, scholars, or commoners, and send them to the capital to attend the banquet. Buddhist and Daoist monks were only asked about their methods for prolonging life and were not included in the banquet. In the mountains of Emei, Sichuan, there was a monk who was over one hundred and forty years old. He lived alone in a quiet room and did not interact with the world. He neither spoke nor smiled. When officials went to ask him, he responded with a verse: "Living to seventy is rare even in ancient times, but even more rare are monks who have lived longer. If asked what method prolongs life, a life of simplicity and detachment nurtures the natural essence." This monk's lifespan was already rare in the world, and there was even an even older one.

    At the time, the ages of the thousand elders had already been recorded and reported to the emperor. The emperor wished to find a scholar over one hundred years old who was worthy of an official title and reward and place him at the head of the group. The governor of Zhejiang recommended Qi Shinan, a scholar from TianTai who had been appointed to a teaching position but did not accept it. His name was recorded in the official register and could be verified. However, his immediate family members, including his son and grandson, had all passed away.
